   SEPTARI D
   OLIVIA M
AE UNIV LPPM RIAU (UYLP-Non-standard)
GA 2020305766
AB    NOVELTY - Material in the form of a geopolymer composition product from waste palm oil fuel ash or palm oil fuel ash for construction in the peat environment.
   USE - The material is used in the field of civil engineering, preferably for construction in the peat environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly material utilizes waste as the main ingredient, reduces environmental pollution, reduces carbon dioxide emissions resulting from Portland cement production which causes global warming, and is resistant and stable to the peat environment for a long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use of palm ash or palm oil fuel ash and an alkaline solution of sodium hydroxide and sodium silicate activator as the main material for the preparation of geopolymer ecosystems.

The wearable cooling device is connected with the temperature sensor that is provided with a temperature measuring probe.

PT P
PN CN110102310-A; CN110102310-B
TI Preparation of tar lightening catalyst based on waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst involves taking waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst as active metal source, adding nitric acid and hydrofluoric acid mixed acid, and digesting.
AU YAN L
   WANG M
   LV P
   REN L
   LIU Y
   WANG Y
   BAO W
   LI F
   GAO M
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019707494
AB    NOVELTY - A tar lightening catalyst based on waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst is prepared by taking waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst as active metal source, adding nitric acid and hydrofluoric acid mixed acid, digesting at 150-250 degrees C to obtain digestion solution containing active metal, impregnating with carbon material to obtain precursor, and calcining at 650-800 degrees C under mixed atmosphere of carbon dioxide gas and/or water and inert gas.
   USE - A method for preparing tar lightening catalyst based on waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The product can condense aromatic hydrocarbons in tar, realizes catalytic cracking of oxygen-containing heterocyclic ring to form benzene, and light aromatic hydrocarbons including toluene, and improves tar quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The tar lightening catalyst based on waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst is prepared by taking 1-20 g of 2-17 wt.% waste fluid catalytic cracking catalyst as active metal source, adding 1 L 2-8:1 volume ratio of 30-68 wt.% nitric acid and 15-40 wt.% hydrofluoric acid mixed acid, microwave heating digesting at 150-250 degrees C for 2-6 hours. to obtain digestion solution containing active metal, impregnating with carbon material comprising raw coal or pyrolysis coke, coal gangue, coal activated carbon, biomass activated carbon, pitch coke or biomass pyrolysis coke to obtain precursor, drying at 60-80 degrees C, and calcining at 650-800 degrees C under 20% mixed atmosphere of carbon dioxide gas and/or water and inert gas for 1-3 hours.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-B02; H04-F02B; H04-F04; H04-F05; H04-F06
IP B01J-023/847; B01J-037/02; B01J-037/06; B01J-037/08; C10G-011/05

PT P
PN CN109943912-A
TI Plant-based cling film comprises tartary buckwheat seed extract, polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution, nano-silica dispersion, polylactic acid, crosslinker, and balance is water.
AU SONG J
   WANG D
   HUANG X
   DAN D
   WAN R
   CHANG H
AE ANHUI MINZHEN ACTIVE PACKING MATERIAL CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201958407X
AB    NOVELTY - Plant-based cling film comprises 1-5 wt.% tartary buckwheat seed extract, 4-8 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution, 3-8 wt.% nano-silica dispersion, 10-16 wt.% polylactic acid, 0.6-1.2 wt.% crosslinker, and balance is water.
   USE - Plant-based cling film.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant-based cling film is environmentally friendly, has high water vapor transmission and suitable oxygen and carbon dioxide transmission rates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a plant-based wrap film, which involves:
   (A) weighing tartary buckwheat seed extract, polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution, nano silica dispersion, the polylactic acid, crosslinking agent and water according to the ratio to obtain a mixed liquid; and
   (B) spinning by the electrospinning machine to obtain plant-based wrap film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The tartary buckwheat seed extract is prepared by instantaneously steaming bitter buckwheat seed to obtain the mature bitter buckwheat seed, and adding mature bitter buckwheat seed to the water of 8-16 times the mass to 50-60 degrees C, then adding high temperature -amylase, ultrasonic stirring for 1.5-3 hours, heat inactivating to obtain the enzymatic hydrolyzate, and then concentrating and drying at low temperature to obtain the tartary buckwheat seed extract. The high-pressure instantaneous steaming is performed by subjecting high-temperature steam passing through 106-124 degrees C to high-temperature instantaneous steaming of the Kuding leaves, and the treatment time is 10-20 seconds. Preferred Conditions: The high temperature resistant alpha-amylase is added in an amount of from 0.08-0.15% by weight of the tartary buckwheat seed. The polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution is prepared by weighing 6-12 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol and 88-94 pts. wt. water according to the parts by weight, and mixing and stirring to obtain an aqueous solution of polyvinyl alcohol. The nano silica dispersion is preparde by weighing 10-16 pts. wt. nano silica, 1-4 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose, 0.3-0.7 pts. wt. sodium metaaluminate and 60-70 pts. wt. water, then uniformly mixing and cooling to room temperature to prepare a nano-silica dispersion. The crosslinking agent is calcium citrate or calcium lactate. The spinning condition includes spinning voltage is 14-17 Kilo Volt, the spinning flow rate is 0.3-0.7 ml/minute, the spinning receiving distance is 8-12 cm, and the time is 10-13 hours.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F04 (Braiding, knitting - including trimmings and non-woven fabrics (D04).)
MC A03-A04; A05-E02B; A07-A04D; A08-C09; A08-D05; A08-M; A08-R06A; A10-E09B; A11-A03; A11-B15; A12-P01A; A12-S05L; F01-C08; F01-D; F01-D04; F01-D08; F01-E01
IP D01F-008/10; D01F-008/14; D01F-001/10; D04H-001/4382; D04H-001/728; D01D-005/00

PN CN109734725-A; CN109734725-B
TI Preparation of piperacillin acid by adding N-ethyl-dioxo-piperazine, trimethyl chlorosilane, pyridine and 4-dimethylaminopyridine into organic solvent, controlling temperature, adding triphosgene, preserving temperature, and post-processing.
AU SUN Z
   FAN C
   SUN Y
   LI Y
AE QILU TIANHE HUISHI LELING PHARM CO LTD (QLPH-C)
   QILU TIANHE PHARM CO LTD (QLPH-C)
   SHANDONG ANSHUN PHARM CO LTD (QLPH-C)
   SHANDONG ANXIN PHARM CO LTD (QLPH-C)
GA 201945174B
AB    NOVELTY - A piperacillin acid is prepared by adding N-ethyl-dioxo-piperazine, trimethyl chlorosilane, pyridine and 4-dimethylaminopyridine into organic solvent, controlling temperature at - 35 to - 15 degrees C, adding triphosgene, preserving temperature and reacting, post-processing to obtain N-ethyl-di-piperazine acylchloride, adding ampicillin and binary weak alkali to mixed solvent of water and ethyl acetate, controlling temperature at 25-45 degrees C, adding N-ethyl-di-piperazine acyl chloride solution, and post-processing.
   USE - The method is used for piperacillin acid preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases condensation reaction yield by 5%, replaces weak alkali sodium bicarbonate with binary weak base including calcium carbonate, and reduces degradation and production of non-condensable gas carbon dioxide by 50% and has environment protection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of 4-dimethylaminopyridine and N-ethyl-dioxo-piperazine is 1:500-1:3000. Preferred Components: The binary weak alkali is calcium carbonate or manganese carbonate.

PT P
PN CN109675632-A
TI Preparing carbon-based zeolitic imidazolate            framework composite catalyst, comprises purifying, acid            treating of carbon material, adding, stirring and            carbonizing.
AU HU C
   MU Y
   QIU J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019407874
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-based zeolitic imidazolate                framework composite catalyst, comprises (i)                purifying and acid treating of the carbon material;                (ii) adding the pretreated carbon material and a                binder to a methanol solution of a transition metal                salt, zinc nitrate and 2-methylimidazole, and                stirring to obtain precursor; and (iii) subjecting                the precursor to high temperature carbonization                under an inert atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                carbon-based zeolitic imidazolate framework                composite catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has outstanding performance. The                method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) carbon-based zeolitic imidazolate                framework composite catalyst prepared by the                production method; and
   (2) use of the carbon-based zeolitic                imidazolate framework composite catalyst in the                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  carbon material is graphene oxide, carbon                  nanotubes or conductive carbon black. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), pre-treating for                  hydrochloric acid pickling of graphene oxide for                  carbon nanotubes and conductive carbon black,                  mixing acid, refluxing, and washing the                  pretreated carbon material with deionized water                  to near neutrality. In the step (ii), the binder                  is polyvinylpyrrolidone or polyethyleneimine. In                  the step (ii), the transition metal salt is                  ferrous sulfate, nickel nitrate, and cobalt                  nitrate. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of the                  transition metal salt to the zinc nitrate is 1:                  (10-30). In the step (ii), the stirring is                  carried out at 25-35 degrees C for 12-24 hours.                  In the step (iii), the carbonization temperature                  is 850-1050 degrees C, and the time is 1-2 hour.                  The electrocatalytic electrolyte is a potassium                  hydrogen carbonate solution having a                  concentration of 0.1-0.5 M.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC J04-E01; J04-E04A; J04-E04D; J04-E11; A10-E05B; A12-W11K

PT P
PN TW201907783-A
TI Automatic disinsection and sterilization non-toxic technology for grain silos a technical approach capable of preserving non-toxicity of grains, grain powder and agricultural products.
AU CHEN W
AE ENRICHFLOW GROUP CO LTD (ENRI-Non-standard)
GA 201949016W
AB    NOVELTY - An automatic disinsection and sterilization technology for grain silos is mainly a vector prevention and control technology applied to grain silos, which mainly comprises introducing a mixed gas into a storage space of a grain silo, and sealing for 1 to 24 hours so as to result in a suffocating disinsection of the vectors in the storage space, then introducing a mixed gas formed by mixing nitrogen and carbon dioxide through a gas inlet of the grain silo to blow off powder body and insect body on the wall of the storage space of the grain silo during the spraying process, thereby achieving a technical approach that is capable of preserving non-toxicity of grains, grain powder and agricultural products with high safety.

PN DE102017007317-A1
TI Digital vehicle sticker for classifying current pollutant class based on emission measurements, has sensors for detecting hydrocarbons, noise emissions and exhaust gas flow in exhaust stream of internal combustion engine.
AU MERKEL B J
   MERKEL T C
AE MERKEL B J (MERK-Individual)
   MERKEL C (MERK-Individual)
GA 2019135911
AB    NOVELTY - The sticker has sensors for detecting carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxide, particle mass, particle number, hydrocarbons, noise emissions and exhaust gas flow in an exhaust stream of an internal combustion engine. A windscreen is digitally and visually arranged with the sticker by radio data transmission module i.e. GPRS module.
   USE - Digital vehicle sticker for classifying current pollutant class based on emission measurements.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensors detects the hydrocarbons, the noise emissions and the exhaust gas flow in the exhaust stream of the internal combustion engine, so that environment can be protected from being polluted, and driving behavior-based approval of the sticker can be achieved.

PT P
PN CN109294979-A
TI Separating and cultivating umbilical cord and placental stem cells comprises taking materials safely, carrying out cold chain transportation, monitoring whole process, maintaining safe transportation and processing tissue.
AU INVENTOR U
AE CHINA ORIGIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHOR-Non-standard)
GA 201914652F
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and cultivating umbilical cord and placental stem cells comprises taking materials safely, (2) carrying out cold chain transportation, monitoring safe transportation, (3) processing tissue, (4) dispersing tissue in digestion liquid (enzyme) to obtain cells, (5) screening and filtering the cells, purifying cells by density gradient centrifugation and retaining tissue blocks, (5) incubating with mesenchymal stem cells (MSC) medium supplemented with placental tissue fluid, incubating at 37 degrees C using 5% carbon dioxide incubator, (6) carrying out cryopreservation of cells, and (7) maintaining quality and safety, testing biological efficacy and ensuring culture safety, cell purity, proliferation and differentiation.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and cultivating umbilical cord and placental stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), taking umbilical cord and placenta (discarded after natural birth or caesarean section), where the placenta must undergo physical examination, with no genetic disease, infectious disease or carrier, and other diseases, strict aseptic operation, and must be fresh (within 0-2 hours). The step (2), process comprises preliminarily cleaning blood stain on surface of umbilical cord and placenta using washing liquid comprised of physiological saline containing streptomycin and miconazole, transporting entire umbilical cord and placenta in cold chain, monitoring safe transportation, adding placenta and umbilical cord into the placenta preservation solution, carrying low temperature cold chain monitoring, quickly transporting to laboratory, processing immediately (0-72 hours) to improve safety level. The step (3), process comprises washing umbilical cord and placental tissue with phosphate buffer saline solution containing streptomycin and miconazole, washing away residual preservation solution and blood stains, and washing with pure phosphate buffer saline, peeling off the tissue block, ensuring that cells we cultivate are safer and more energetic, cutting tissue block into size of 1-3 mm3 and infiltrating in phosphate buffer saline solution free of antibiotics and fetal bovine serum to avoid residue, shaking the digestion liquid (enzyme) at 37 degrees C, maintaining room temperature, placing statically for cold digestion at 2-8 degrees C. The digestion liquid (enzyme)refers trypsin, collagenase II, collagenase IV, dispase, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA), which is more efficient than single enzyme digestion. The digestion liquid (enzyme) is 0.05-0.25 wt.% trypsin, 0.05-0.3 wt.% collagenase II, 0.05-0.3 wt.% collagenase IV, 0.1-2.4 mu /ml dispase and 0.005-0.1 wt.% ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA). The step (5), process comprises filtering enzyme through sieve, washing with phosphate buffer saline, removing cells and selecting single cells, carrying out purification by performing hydroxyethyl starch centrifugation or Ficoll centrifugation, resuspending digested tissue pieces which were not ground in phosphate buffer saline, and centrifuging together to prevent the grinding from causing greater damage to cells. The step (6), process comprises supplementing serum-free medium with placental tissue fluid, the placental tissue fluid containing plurality of factors, which are the original growth environment of placental cells, dividing cells and tissue blocks into bottles at 37 degrees C, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator to shorten the incubation time. In the step (7), taking the cloning cell bottle group, carrying out trypsin digestion, resurfacing certain density or subculturing, carrying out tissue block group clone digestion treatment, half-replacing and re-applying remaining tissue block. In the step (8), subjecting cells to biosafety, carrying out biological efficacy and phenotypic detection, and archiving data from all source to ensure to safe and organized method.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

The                tubular steam reformed portion of hydrocarbon feed                stock to tubular steam reformed gas. The                autothermal reformed in autothermal reformer                tubular steam reformed gas with portion of oxygen                containing stream obtained by electrolysis of water                and/or steam to autothermal reformed gas stream                which includes hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide. The portion of separate hydrogen                containing stream is introduced into autothermal                reformed gas stream and withdrawed synthesis                gas.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves separating air into separate stream                  containing oxygen and into separate stream                  containing nitrogen. The portion of separate                  stream containing oxygen is introduced into                  autothermal reformer. The portion of hydrocarbon                  feed stock is by passed tubular steam reforming                  and introduced to autothermal reformer. The                  electrolysis of water and/or steam is powered in                  portion by renewable energy. The separating of                  air is powered in portion by renewable energy.                  The substantially pure carbon dioxide is                  introduced in upstream, and/or downstream. The synthesis gas                  withdrawn is converted to methanol product.                  Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon feed stock                  involves natural gas, methane, liquified natural                  gas, naphthalene or mixtures of either as such or                  pre-reformed and/or desulfurized.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing calcium carbonate particles in cubic form comprises e.g. mixing first additive containing first crystal form controlling agent with water to obtain first solution.
AU ZHEN X
   XIE Z
   PAN W
   CAI J
AE ENPING YANYI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (ENPI-Non-standard)
GA 2019114012
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate particles in cubic form comprises mixing the first additive containing the first crystal form controlling agent with water to obtain first solution; mixing second additive containing second crystal form controlling agent with water to obtain second solution; adding the first solution to calcium hydroxide solution, performing one stirring, adding the second solution to calcium hydroxide solution, and performing second stirring; introducing carbon dioxide into solution after the second stirring until the solution is neutral or acidic, and filtering to obtain calcium carbonate particles in cubic form.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium carbonate particles in cubic form.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate particles: has uniform particles, good dispersibility and particle diameter of 60-80 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first crystal form controlling agent and the second crystal form controlling agent is sodium pyrophosphate, zinc sulfate, citric acid, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, barium carbonate, caramel, glucose, aluminum sulfate, sulfuric acid, hydrogen dioxide, sodium bicarbonate and/or ammonium chloride. The mass ratio of the first crystal form controlling agent in the first additive is 0.15-1 wt.%. The mass ratio of the second crystal form controlling agent in the second additive is 0.15-1 wt.%. The first additive has mass concentration of 1-10 wt.% in the first solution. The second additive has a mass concentration of 1-10 wt.% in the second solution. The mass concentration of calcium hydroxide solution is 5-12 wt.%. The the volume concentration of the carbon dioxide is greater than or equal to 32 vol.%. Preferred Method: In the method, the time of one stirring is 30 minutes, and time of second stirring is 30 minutes. In the method, the initial temperature at which carbon dioxide is introduced into the solution after the second stirring is 25-35 degrees C.

GA 201864034Y

PT P
PN KR2018069432-A; KR1898236-B1
TI Producing lithium carbonate, comprises e.g. naturally evaporating brine to precipitate sodium chloride, removing magnesium ions by adding calcium hydroxide/calcium oxide, and adding sodium carbonate to precipitate calcium carbonate.
AU KIM K H
   LEE E J
   CHOI J H
   SEO C W
   LEE S M
   LEE J H
   KIM K Y
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 201852256Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lithium carbonate, comprises (a) naturally evaporating brine to precipitate sodium chloride, (b) removing magnesium ions by adding calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide to the brine, (c) adding sodium carbonate to the brine from which magnesium ions are removed to precipitate calcium carbonate, and removing calcium ions, (d) reacting precipitated sodium chloride prepared in the step (a) and precipitated calcium carbonate prepared in the step (c) to obtain sodium carbonate and calcium chloride, and (e) reacting the calcium ion-removed brine with carbon dioxide to form lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and utilizes minimum amount of raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (c) is carried out by adding the sodium carbonate to remove calcium ions, where the sodium carbonate is produced in the step (d). The step (a) is carried out by adding calcium chloride to precipitate sodium chloride, where the calcium chloride is produced in the step (d). The step (a) is carried out until the concentration of lithium in the brine reaches greater than or equal to 2000 mg/l. The step (b) is carried out by adding 1.1-1.8 equivalents of calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide equivalent to the magnesium ion in the brine at -15 degrees C to 40 degrees C with stirring. In the step (c), the calcium ions are removed at -15 degrees C to 40 degrees C with stirring, where the amount of sodium carbonate added is 1.1-1.5 equivalents relative to calcium ions in the brine. The step (e) is carried out in carbonation reactor having internal pressure of 2-3 bar. The method further comprises (f) re-introducing the remaining brine to the natural evaporation process after the step (e).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC E33-G
IP C01D-015/08; C01D-003/06; C01F-011/08; C01F-011/24

PN CN107969476-A
TI Packing honey peaches in modified atmosphere comprises cleaning honey peaches, soaking in sodium hypochlorite solution, adding preservative, drying, soaking in electrolytic brine, drying, adding into the bag and sealing.
AU JI J
   HE X
AE ZHEJIANG RUNWU NETWORK TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018357781
AB    NOVELTY - Packing honey peaches in modified atmosphere comprises (i) picking seven to eight mature honey peaches, (ii) cleaning honey peaches, soaking in 1% sodium hypochlorite solution for 5-10 minutes, adding preservative solution, and continue soaking for 30-50 minutes, (iii) drying, (iv) soaking honey peaches in electrolytic brine for 5-15 minutes, taking out and drying, and (v) adding dried honey peaches into compound preservative bag containing ethylene absorbent, storing bag is filled with gas barrier and sealing, the gas barrier is a mixture of nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and helium gas mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for packing honey peaches in modified atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains the delicious taste of the peaches, suitable for mass production, is simple and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas barrier is mixture of nitrogen, helium gas, and carbon dioxide, and the volume ratio is 5:1:1. The preservative solution comprises 2 pts. wt. citric acid, 10 pts. wt. calcium chloride, 7 pts. wt. lignin, 5 pts. wt. honey, 5 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 4 pts. wt. glucose, 1 pts. wt. mannose, 3 pts. wt. sodium glutamate, and 40 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: The process of drying comprises subjecting honey peaches to hot air treatment at 45-60 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, and cooling to room temperature. The peaches are sterilized using ozone before bagging and packing. The packed bag is placed in an ultraviolet ray at a wavelength of 254 nm for secondary sterilization for 10 minutes, and then stored at 12-25 degrees C. The temperature is maintained at 0-5 degrees C during the soaking process of preservative liquid.

   TREUBRODT U
AE ENGIT RES UG (ENGI-Non-standard)
   ENGIT RES GMBH (ENGI-Non-standard)
GA 201827760L
AB    NOVELTY - The generator has multiple electromagnets arranged on a rotor and a stator. The electromagnets are mounted on the stator to control an actual drive axle. Permanent magnets are mounted on the rotor, where a resulting magnetic field repels the permanent magnets. An electronic control unit is provided for central control of the generator and comprises a programmed chip set.
   USE - Magnetic generator.
   ADVANTAGE - The design of the generator eliminates the need to shield the magnetic fields, and omits even the mechanical devices to overcome the zero point. The digitally controlled control technology ensures permanent power generation with higher output of energy. The magnet generator consumes no resources other than the power needed for control and is completely carbon-dioxide free.

AU HE Y

   HANGZHOU YUANDA BIOPHARM CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING PAIJIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022647356
AB    NOVELTY - Uricase preparation comprises polyethylene glycol modified uricase as active ingredient, and buffer reagent as adjuvant, where the buffer reagent includes phosphate and/or hydrochloride.
   USE - The uricase preparation or medicine composition is useful in preparing medicine (in the form of liquid, semi-solid, solid) for treating or preventing hyperuricemia or its related disease (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The uricase can catalyze the oxidation of allantoin by uric acid and release carbon dioxide in the presence of oxygen, does not cause severe allergic reactions, which can be used for long-term treatment, ensure its enzymatic activity, stability and storage time when it is prepared into medicine preparations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a medicine composition comprising the uricase preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the uricase has at least 11 amino acid sites for PEG modification including T1, K3, K4, K30, K35, K76, K79, K97, K112, K116, K120, K152, K179, K222, K231, K266, K272, K285, K291 and/or K293, and the amino acid site mapping comprises a fully defined sequence of 298 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 1), as given in the specification. At least one, at least two, at least three or four of the following four amino acid sites in the uricase have PEG modifications: K 30, K 35, K 222, and K 231. The uricase has an amino acid sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NOs: 1-7) or a polypeptide having at least 70%, 75%, 80%, 85%, 90%, 95%, 99% identity compared to (SEQ ID NOs: 1-7); or a polypeptide obtained by substituting, adding and/or deleting at least one amino acids to (SEQ ID NOs: 1-7), e.g. comprises a fully defined sequence of 298 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 1), as given in the specification, a fully defined sequence of 304 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 2), as given in the specification, a fully defined sequence of 297 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 3), as given in the specification, a fully defined sequence of 304 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 4), as given in the specification, a fully defined sequence of 304 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 5), as given in the specification, a fully defined sequence of 304 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 6), as given in the specification, and a fully defined sequence of 304 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 7), as given in the specification. The uricase has an amino acid sequence as shown in (SEQ ID NOs: 1-4). Preferred Method: The pH of the uricase preparation is 7-9, preferably 7.4-8.2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The polyethylene glycol has monomethoxy group or hydroxy group. The polyethylene glycol is a linear or branched structure. The polyethylene glycol and the uricase are coupled via an amide bond. The polyethylene glycol is a modified polyethylene glycol, and the modifying group of the modified polyethylene glycol is at least one of N-hydroxysuccinimide, N-hydroxysuccinimide carbonate, N-hydroxysuccinimide acetate, N-hydroxysuccinimide propionate, N-hydroxysuccinimide butyrate, N-hydroxysuccinimide succinate, and/or p-nitrophenyl carbonate. The modifying group of the modified polyethylene glycol is N-hydroxysuccinimide propionate. The buffer reagent comprises disodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, and/or sodium chloride. The mass ratio of the polyethylene glycol modified uricase to the buffer agent is (5-6):(6-37), preferably 6:10. The mass ratio of the polyethylene glycol modified uricase to the phosphate is (5-6):(1-7), where the phosphate is disodium hydrogen phosphate and/or disodium hydrogen phosphate. The mass ratio of the polyethylene glycol modified uricase to the sodium chloride is (5-6):(5-30). The mass ratio of the phosphate to the sodium chloride is (1-7): (5-30). The preparation is in the form of a single dosage form, and each preparation contains 6 mg uricase. The medicine composition further comprises other medicines for treating or preventing hyperuricemia or its related diseases.

PN IN202021000473-A
TI Identifying active site involves soaking apo PcaC crystals in aqueous solution for predetermined time, collecting multiple data sets based on different soaking time, refined data sets against apostructure, and analyzing difference maps.
AU PANJIKAR S
   ANAND R
   BHASKAR S
AE IITB-MONASH RES ACAD (IITB-Non-standard)
GA 2022217743
AB    NOVELTY - Identifying active site involves soaking apo PcaC crystals in an aqueous solution for a predetermined time. The multiple data sets is collected based on different soaking time. The data sets is refined against the apostructure. The difference fourier maps is analyzed. A strong triangular positive difference electron density is located. The density models as bicarbonate followed by locating elongated positive difference electron density in the vicinity of bicarbonate and modeling density as carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for identify active site in decarboxylase enzyme.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables identifying active site in decarboxylase enzyme solves the crystal structure of gamma -carboxymuconolactone decarboxylase from the catabolic pathway, and catalyzes the removal of carboxylic group from the substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous solution comprises of sodium bicarbonate. The concentration of sodium bicarbonate is 10 20 mM. The multiple data sets are collected at 5-15 minutes. The bicarbonate binding site helps in identifying the active site and the carbon dioxide binding site maps the carbon dioxide exit pathway in decarboxylase enzymes.

PN CN113461565-A
TI Preparing cyclohexanone oxime used for producing high-quality caprolactam product by Beckman rearrangement reaction, involves adding hydrogen peroxide, ammonia and cyclohexanone to aqueous solution in presence of titanium silicon catalyst.
AU ZHAO Y
   XIAO Y
   ZHAO C
   XIAO Z
AE ZHAO Y (ZHAO-Individual)
   XIAO Y (XIAO-Individual)
   XIAO Z (XIAO-Individual)
   ZHAO C (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 2021B7719S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclohexanone oxime involves adding hydrogen peroxide, ammonia and cyclohexanone to aqueous solution in presence of titanium silicon catalyst and ammonium carbonate, passing in carbon dioxide to obtain a direct ammoximation reaction system to prepare cyclohexanone oxime.
   USE - Method for preparing cyclohexanone oxime used for producing high-quality caprolactam product by Beckman rearrangement reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared cyclohexanone oxime has cost-effective, eco-friendly and due to the slowing down of the hydroxylamine disproportionation reaction during the reaction process, the nitrous oxide emission from the direct ammoximation process of cyclohexanone is significantly reduced, which only reached 1/3-1/5 of the nitrogen oxide reduction method and high performance organization (HPO) method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The amount of cyclohexanone is 2-20 wt.% based on the total reaction system. The method for preparing cyclohexanone oxime further involves finishing direct ammoximation reaction, using cyclohexane for extraction to transfer the generated cyclohexanone oxime out of the reaction system, returning the remaining aqueous catalyst solution of the extraction to the reactor for recycling. Preferred Conditions: The pH value of the reaction system is maintained at 7-10 during the reaction process by introducing carbon dioxide. The reaction system comprises 0.5-10 wt.% titanium-silicon catalyst and 0.1-3 wt.% ammonium carbonate. The molar ratio of cyclohexanone, ammonia, and hydrogen peroxide is 1:1.02-1.05:1.05-1.08. The temperature of the reaction system is 60-100degrees Celsius and the pressure of the reaction system is 0.1-0.8 megapascal.

AU DAURELIO R
AE CHARMSTAR CAMBRIDGE LTD (CHAR-Non-standard)
GA 202117105Q
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of a selective synthetic polymeric                adsorbent involves forming an emulsion comprising a                monomer comprising functional groups having an                affinity for a desired target molecule, where the                monomer is dissolved in a solvent to form a                solvent/monomer solution which acts as dispersed                phase in an oil-based continuous phase to form the                emulsion, polymerizing the monomer, removing the                oil-based continuous phase by extraction with an                extraction liquid comprising isopropanol, and                having no known aquatic toxicity and/or known                reproductive toxicity.
   USE - Manufacture of selective synthetic polymeric                adsorbent for capturing gases such as carbon                dioxide, for chemical separations, catalysis, and                molecular sensors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of selective                synthetic polymeric adsorbent with excellent                binding properties, and absorption                properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The removal of oil-based                  continuous phase involves contacting the                  extraction liquid with the polymer, agitating,                  then separating the extraction liquid from the                  polymer, and repeating the process one or more                  times using fresh extraction liquid to wash the                  polymer several times. The method further                  involves adding an initiator and a crosslinker.                  The method does not involve additional washing                  with another substance.Preferred Component: The polymerization                  process produces a molecularly imprinted polymer                  (MIP) or non-imprinted polymer (NIP).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The continuous phase                  comprises mineral oil. The extraction liquid                  further comprises 1-5C alcohol, preferably an                  alkanol or 3-5C ketone, preferably an alkanone.                  The extraction liquid comprises methanol,                  ethanol, butanol, or propanone, preferably                  isopropanol. The initiator is chosen from                  2,2'-azoobis(2-methylpropionitrile), ammonium                  persulfate (APS) and di(4-tert-butylcyclohexyl)                  peroxydicarbonate. The crosslinker is chosen from                  ethylene glycol dimethacrylate (EGDMA),                  tetraethylene glycol dimethacrylate (TEGDMA),                  triethanolamine trimethacrylate (TEAMA), and                  N,N'-methylenebis (acrylamide). The monomer is                  chosen from acrylamide, bis-acrylamide,                  diallylamine, allylamine, methacrylamide,                  4-vinylaniline, triallyamine, phenylenediamine,                  N-isopropylacrylamide, N,N-cystaminebis                  (acrylamide), N-(3(dimethylamino                  propyl)methacrylamide, and N,N-dimethylaminoethyl                  methacrylate. The emulsion further comprises a                  template molecule.

PN CN112299701-A
TI Preparing rock mineral wool by putting raw materials into kiln, melting, drawing and making into rock mineral wool, where raw materials comprises hot melting slag, alkali-free glass waste silk and basalt.
AU TANG Y
   HUANG J
   LI W
   XIA J
AE NANJING FIBERGLASS RES & DESIGN INST CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202113702Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing rock mineral wool involves putting raw materials into a kiln, melting, drawing and making into rock mineral wool, where the raw materials comprises 50-90 wt.% hot melting slag, 5-30 wt.% alkali-free glass waste silk and 2-30 wt.% basalt.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rock mineral wool.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the large amount of alkali-free glass fiber waste silk solid waste and the problem of using the hot melting slag to produce the mineral slag wool ingredients, reduces the amount of carbon dioxide exhaust emissions, fuel consumption and heat energy consumption, adopts less reaction volume and increases heat utilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hot melting slag comprises 30-45 %mass silicon dioxide, 5-20 %mass aluminum oxide, 25-40 %mass calcium oxide, 4-16 %mass magnesium oxide and 4-8 %mass first other component. The alkali-free glass waste silk comprises 50-70 %mass silicon dioxide, 8-25 %mass aluminum oxide, 9-25 %mass calcium oxide, 0-10 %mass magnesium oxide and 5-9 %mass second other component. The basalt comprises 40-60 %mass silicon dioxide, 12-20 %mass aluminum oxide, 6-12 %mass calcium oxide, 4-12 %mass magnesium oxide and 15-20 %mass third other composition. Preferred Method: The method involves preparing the rock mineral wool involving (1) crushing and drying the alkali-free glass waste silk and the basalt to form a crushed material and (2) putting the hot melting slag into a furnace kiln for melting and putting the crushed material into the kiln until the crushed material is completely melted to form a wire drawing melt and conducting wire drawing, where after crushing, the fiber length of the alkali-free glass waste silk is 2-50 mm and the particle size of basalt is greater than or equal to 20 mm. The method involves adding the crushed material when the temperature of the hot melting slag is 1000-1300 degrees C, stirring, adjusting the temperature to 1400-1500 degrees C, preserving heat, adjusting the mixture to 1350-1450 degrees C for homogenization to form the wire drawing melt after the alkali-free glass waste wire and the basalt particles are completely melted and performing the wire drawing. The method involves putting the hot melting slag into the kiln for melting, heating and melting the alkali-free glass waste wire and the basalt respectively to form a glass melt and a basalt melt, respectively introducing the glass melt and the basalt melt into the kiln for mixing with the hot melting slag, heating again to form a wire drawing melt and drawing the wire, where the temperature of the hot melting slag is 1000-1300 degrees C, the temperature of the glass melt is 1250-1350 degrees C, the temperature of the basalt melt is 1250-1350 degrees C and the temperature of the drawing melt is 1350-1450 degrees C.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).)
MC L01-C01; L01-F03; L01-F04

PN JP2021008385-A
TI Manufacture of zinc carbonate for e.g. pharmaceutical, involves forming zinc-containing aqueous solution containing zinc component in electric furnace dust or secondary dust as raw material and separating zinc component.
AU ANSAKI M
   MORI S
AE KINOTECH SOLAR ENERGY CORP (KINO-Non-standard)
GA 202110319U
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of zinc carbonate (21) involves forming (101) zinc-containing aqueous solution containing a zinc component in a raw material by using electric furnace dust or secondary dust generated when the electric furnace dust is reduced in a reduction furnace as a raw material and separating (102) the zinc component in the zinc-containing aqueous solution as zinc carbonate.
   USE - Manufacture of zinc carbonate used for industrial products, pharmaceuticals, and feeds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables stable manufacture of zinc carbonate with excellent yield, purity, and stability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the manufacture of zinc carbonate. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Zinc carbonate (21)
   Filtrate (22)
   Carbon dioxide (23)
   Zinc extraction process (101)
   Zinc carbonate separation process (102)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of zinc carbonate involves forming zinc-containing aqueous solution by selectively extracting the zinc component from the raw material, performing pre-stage zinc carbonate separation by separating the zinc component in the zinc-containing aqueous solution produced in the zinc extraction step as zinc carbonate, bringing an aqueous solution of hydrochloric acid into contact with the zinc component of the zinc carbonate separated in the pre-stage zinc carbonate separation step to produce a zinc chloride aqueous solution which is an aqueous solution of zinc chloride, performing deironing and manganese demineralization step by contacting an oxidizing agent with the zinc-containing aqueous solution and separating the iron component and the manganese component in the zinc-containing aqueous solution, contacting metallic zinc with the zinc-containing aqueous solution that is carried out the iron removal and manganese removal step, and performing substitution process by reducing and precipitating a metal impurity component nobler than zinc in the zinc chloride aqueous solution. The manufacture of zinc carbonate involves forming zinc-containing aqueous solution by bringing chlorine gas into contact with the raw material and evaporating to selectively chlorinate the zinc component to produce zinc chloride, dissolving the zinc chloride in water to produce a zinc chloride aqueous solution as the zinc-containing aqueous solution, contacting the zinc chloride aqueous solution with an alkaline agent to separate the iron component in the zinc chloride aqueous solution, reducing and precipitating a metal impurity component nobler than zinc in the zinc chloride aqueous solution from which the iron component is separated, and bringing oxidizing agent into contact with the zinc chloride aqueous solution to separate the manganese component in the zinc chloride aqueous solution, and separating the zinc component in the zinc chloride aqueous solution.

PN US2020398254-A1
TI Making sorbent involves providing sorbent feedstock and which is selected from activated carbon, reactivated carbon, and combination, oxidizing sorbent feedstock to form oxidized sorbent feedstock, adding nitrogen containing precursor.
AU MAZZOCCOLI J
   TRAMPOSCH W G
   WALKER R
   VAUGHN R H
AE CALGON CARBON CORP (KURS-C)
GA 2020C8348K
AB    NOVELTY - Making sorbent involves providing sorbent feedstock and which is selected from activated carbon, reactivated carbon, and combination, oxidizing sorbent feedstock to form oxidized sorbent feedstock, adding nitrogen containing precursor to oxidized sorbent feedstock. The nitrogen containing precursor is reduced nitrogen compound. The oxidized sorbent feedstock is heated to temperature of 400 degrees C under inert atmosphere to form calcined sorbent feedstock. The sorbent feedstock, oxidized sorbent feedstock, or calcined sorbent feedstock are not subsequently activated with one or more of water, carbon dioxide, oxygen, or any combination.
   USE - Method for making sorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to make sorbent that improves catalytic properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a filter assembly, which comprises sorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen containing precursor is selected from ammonia, ammonium salts, ammonium carbonate and bicarbonate, ammonium thiocyanate, azodicarbonamide, diammonium phosphate, dicyandiamide, guanidine hydrochloride, guanidine thiocyanate, guanine, melamine, thiourea, urea, and combinations. The sorbent feedstock is activated carbon and the activated carbon is formed from bituminous coal, sub-bituminous coal, lignite coal, anthracite coal, peat, nut shells, pits, coconut shell, babassu nut, macadamia nut, dende nut, peach pit, cherry pit, olive pit, walnut shell, wood, lignin, polymers, nitrogen-containing polymers, resins, petroleum pitches, rice hulls, bagasse, corn stalks, wheat hulls, graphenes, carbon nanotubes, and combinations. The CDN is based on the destruction of monochloramine. The activated carbon or the reactivated carbon is formed from one or more of bituminous coal, lignite coal, anthracite coal, peat, nut shells, pits, coconut shell, babassu nut, macadamia nut, dende nut, peach pit, cherry pit, olive pit, walnut shell, wood, lignin, polymers, resins, petroleum pitches, bagasse, rice hulls, corn husks, wheat hulls and chaff, graphenes, and carbon nanotubes. Preferred Conditions: The nitrogen containing precursor has oxidation state of ( )3. The sorbent feedstock has mean particle diameter of 1 micrometer-4 millimeter. The formed sorbent has nitrogen edge concentration of 0.20 at.%. The oxidizing the sorbent feedstock is performed in dry atmosphere. Preferred Process: The method involves adding nitrogen containing precursor. The heating are each individually performed from one to six (1-6) times. The formed sorbent has chloramine destruction number (CDN) of 4.0, CDN is absolute value of the first order linear kinetic fit, multiplied by 1000 that is applied to natural log of a concentration of chloramine in water versus time. The initial concentration of chloramine is decreased over period of 150 minutes. The formed sorbent has CDN of 10.0-30.0. The formed sorbent has chlorine destruction number (C1-DN) of 80.0. The C1-DN is the absolute value of the first order linear kinetic fit, multiplied by 1000 that is applied to natural log of a concentration of chlorine in water versus time. The initial concentration of chlorine is decreased over a period of 150 minutes. The C1-DN value is 80.0-100.0. The method involves adding sorbent feedstock which has amount of 5-95 wt.% based on the total amount of the formed sorbent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN112089666-A
TI Antioxidant composition useful in preparation of cosmetics preferably shampoo, comprises white pool flower seed oil, shaddock peel extract, and birch leaf extract.
AU LI R
   HU G
   WU Z
   CHEN L
   WAN Y
AE GUANGZHOU UNIASIA COSMETICS TECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C91575
AB    NOVELTY - Antioxidant composition comprises white pool flower seed oil and shaddock peel extract.
   USE - The antioxidant composition is useful in preparation of cosmetics having mass percentage 0.3-30%, preferably cosmetics include shampoo (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: can directly act on skin of head to adjust pH of scalp, relieves symptoms e.g. scalp sensitivity, hair loss and broken hair, ensures raw materials are not damaged due to high temperature, and has simple operation, and energy-saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing antioxidant composition comprising using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and vacuum extracting separation to respectively extracting white pool flower seed and shaddock peel, obtaining white pool flower seed oil and shaddock peel extract, mixing white pool flower seed oil and shaddock peel extract to obtain antioxidant composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antioxidant composition further comprises birch leaf extract. Preferred Composition: The antioxidation composition comprises 0.1-30 pts. wt., preferably 0.1-15 pts. wt. white pool flower seed oil, 0.1-30 pts. wt., preferably 0.1-15 pts. wt. shaddock peel extract and 0.1-30 pts. wt., preferably 0.1-15 pts. wt. birch leaf extract. Preferred Method: The pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 25-40 MPa, the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction temperature is 30-50 degrees C, the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction time is 2-5 hours, the flow rate of carbon dioxide in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 10-15 l/hours, the flow rate of the inclusion agent in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 5-10 ml/minutes, the inclusion agent comprises a mass fraction of 60-80% ethanol solution, the mass fraction is 60% ethanol solution, the pressure of the vacuum extracting and separating is 8-10 MPa, the vacuum extracting and separating temperature is 25-35 degrees C, the supercritical carbon dioxide before extraction further comprises the step of preprocessing the raw material. The preparation of white flower seed oil comprises adding white pond flower seed into supercritical extraction kettle for extracting and separating, obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing white pond flower seed extract, vacuum extracting and separating obtained supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing white pond flower seed extract, removing carbon dioxide and obtaining white pond flower seed oil. The preparation of shaddock peel extract comprises cutting shaddock peel, removing white bag in the skin, obtaining pure shaddock peel, vacuum freezing and drying purified shaddock peel to obtain freeze-dried shaddock peel, crushing freeze-dried shaddock peel, adding supercritical extraction kettle for extracting and separating, obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing shaddock peel extract, vacuum extracting and separating obtained supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing shaddock peel extract, removing carbon dioxide to obtain shaddock peel extract, the thickness of the pure shaddock peel is 2-3 mm, the vacuum freezing and drying time is 20-28 hours, preferably 24 hours, the granularity of the freeze-dried shaddock peel after crushing is 70-90 meshes, preferably 80 meshes. The preparation method further comprises using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and vacuum extracting and separating birch leaf for extracting, preparing birch leaf extract, preferably, the preparation method of the birch leaf extract comprises drying birch leaf and crushing to obtain the birch leaf dry powder, adding supercritical extraction kettle for extracting and separating to obtain the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing birch leaf extract, vacuum extracting and separating obtained supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing birch leaf extract, removing carbon dioxide, obtaining birch leaf extract, the granularity of the birch leaf dry powder is 80-200 meshes. The method specifically comprises (a) using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and vacuum extracting and separating respectively the white pool flower seed, shaddock peel and birch leaf to extract, obtaining white pool flower seed oil, shaddock peel extract and birch leaf extract, where the white pool flower seed oil is prepared by the following method: adding white pond flower seed into supercritical extraction kettle at 35-40 MPa, extracting and separating for 4-5 hours at 45-50 degrees C, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10-15 l/hours, the flow rate of 60-80% ethanol solution is 5-10 ml/minutes, obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing white pond flower seed extract, vacuum extracting and separating the obtained supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing white pond flower seed extract at 9-10 MPa, at 25-30 degrees C, removing carbon dioxide, obtaining white pool flower seed oil, the shaddock peel extract is prepared by cutting shaddock skin, the thickness is 2-3 mm, removing white capsule in the skin, obtaining pure shaddock peel, vacuum freezing and drying obtained pure shaddock peel for 20-28 hours to obtain freeze-dried shaddock peel, crushing obtained freeze-dried shaddock peel to 70-90 meshes, adding freeze-dried shaddock peel into the supercritical extraction kettle at 25-30 MPa, extracting and separating for 3-4 hours at 35-40 degrees C, the flow speed of the carbon dioxide is 10-15 l/hours, the flow speed of the 60-80% ethanol solution is 5-10 ml/minutes, obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing shaddock peel extract, vacuum extracting and separating the obtained supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing shaddock peel extract at 8-9 MPa, at 30-35 degrees C, removing carbon dioxide to obtain shaddock peel extract, the birch leaf extract is prepared by white birch leaf, drying and crushing to 80-200 meshes to obtain birch leaf dry powder, adding birch leaf dry powder into the supercritical extraction kettle at 30-35 MPa, extracting and separating for 2-3 hours at 40-45 degrees C, the flow speed of the carbon dioxide is 10-15 l/hours, the flow speed of the 60-80% ethanol solution is 5-10 ml/minutes, obtaining the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing birch leaf extract, then vacuum extracting and separating obtained supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing birch leaf extract at 9-10 MPa at 30-35 degrees C, removing carbon dioxide, obtaining birch leaf extract, and (b) mixing obtained white pool flower seed oil, shaddock peel extract and birch leaf extract to obtain antioxidant composition.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

PN WO2020166676-A1; TW202037706-A; CN113439326-A; KR2021125997-A; JP2020572318-X; EP3926663-A1; US2022010206-A1; SG11202108704-A1
TI Process liquid used for etching ruthenium for            manufacturing semiconductor element, comprises            hypochlorous acid ion, pH buffer, and tetraalkyl            ammonium ion.
AU SHIMODA T
   KIKKAWA Y
   NEGISHI T
   TOUNO S
   SATO T
   TONO S
   KITKAWA Y
   NEKISHI T
AE TOKUYAMA CORP (TOKU-C)
   TOKUYAMA CORP (TOKU-C)
   TOKUYAMA CORP (TOKU-C)
GA 202079745A
AB    NOVELTY - A process liquid comprises hypochlorous acid                ion, pH buffer, and tetraalkyl ammonium ion                (I).
   USE - Process liquid used for etching ruthenium                (claimed) for manufacturing semiconductor                element.
   ADVANTAGE - The process liquid increases the etching rate                of ruthenium, suppresses the change of the etching                rate by pH change accompanying the etching reaction                and carbon-dioxide-gas absorption of ruthenium,                maintains constant etching rate at the time of use,                and enables stable precision processing of several                nm level.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A process liquid comprises hypochlorous acid                ion, pH buffer, and tetraalkyl ammonium ion of                formula: (I).
   R1-R4 = 1-20C alkyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) etching method;
   (2) ruthenium-containing gas generation                inhibitor;
   (3) suppressing generation of                ruthenium-containing gas;
   (4) processing agent of ruthenium-containing                waste liquid; and
   (5) processing ruthenium-containing waste                liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The comprises                  ruthenium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH buffers is at                  least one chosen from carbonic acid, boric acid,                  phosphate, trishydroxy methyl amino                  tris(methane), ammonia, pyrophosphoric acid,                  p-phenolsulfonic acid, diethanolamine,                  ethanolamine, triethanolamine,                  5,5-diethylbarbituric acid, glycine,                  glycylglycine, imidazole, N,N-bis                  (2-hydroxyethyl)-2-aminoethane sulfonic acid,                  3-morpholinopropanesulfonate,                  N-tris(hydroxymethyl) methyl-2-aminoethane                  sulfonic acid,                  2-(4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazinyl)                  ethanesulfonic acid,                  4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazine propanesulfonic                  acid, tricine, N,N-di(2-hydroxyethyl) glycine,                  2-cyclohexyl aminoethane sulfonic acid,                  hydroxyproline, phenol, and EDTA, preferably                  carbonic acid, boric acid, and phosphate.                  Preferred composition: The process liquid                  comprises 0.05-20.0 %mass hypochlorous acid ion,                  0.0001-10 %mass pH buffer, and 0.0001-50 %mass                  tetraalkyl ammonium ion. Preferred Property: The                  process liquid has pH of 7 or more, less than 14                  at 25 degrees C.

PN CN111234290-A; CN111234290-B
TI Preparing bone tissue engineering scaffold by            using supercritical fluid foaming technology involves            adding iron oxide particles to the polymer raw material            as a nucleating agent in the foaming process in the            supercritical foaming process.
AU JIAO Z
   ZHANG Y
   SUN J
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2020518779
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bone tissue engineering scaffold by                using supercritical fluid foaming technology                involves adding iron oxide particles to the polymer                raw material as a nucleating agent in the foaming                process in the supercritical carbon dioxide foaming                process, where the added amount of the iron oxide                particles is 5-25 wt.% of the mass of the polymer                raw material.
   USE - Method for preparing bone tissue engineering                scaffold by using supercritical fluid foaming                technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare bone tissue                engineering scaffold by using supercritical fluid                foaming technology improves the magnetic iron oxide                particle to obtain connectivity and porosity of the                bracket, and accelerates guiding tissue from the                broken defective portion regeneration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The particle size of                  the iron oxide particles is 10-500 nanometer.                  Preferred Components: The polymer material is a                  polyester polymer, including polylactic acid,                  polyglycolic acid, poly mixture of caprolactone                  or poly(lactic-glycolic acid) copolymer. The                  additive is a nucleating agent or the additive                  includes a nucleating agent and other auxiliary                  agent, where the auxiliary agent is one of                  hydroxyapatite, tricalcium phosphate, potassium                  phosphate, sodium phosphate, and bone                  growth-promoting biologically active polypeptide.                  The amount of the auxiliary is 5-25% of the mass                  of the polymer raw material. The die is                  cylindrical or elongated. The pressure release                  rate is 0.5-20 MPa/minutes. Preferred Process:                  The method involves mixing the formulated amount                  of polymer powder and additives uniformly and                  press to form a tablet, placing the die in the                  autoclave, pass carbon dioxide and making reach                  the supercritical state, and keep the pressure at                  0.5-3 hours at a temperature of 35-70 degrees C                  and a pressure of 8-50 megaPascal to make the                  supercritical carbon dioxide solvent and die is                  in full contact, and the carbon dioxide is                  dissolved and saturated in the polymer matrix.                  The pressure is released after the reaction to                  obtain a porous tissue engineering                  scaffold.

PN CN111139425-A
TI Performing nitrocarburizing process involves putting workpiece into nitriding furnace for pre-oxidation treatment, so as to produce active oxide film on surface of workpiece, and nitrogen gas is introduced into nitriding furnace.
AU LI C
AE JIANGSU FENGDONG THERMAL TECHNOLOGY CO L (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202042442E
AB    NOVELTY - Performing nitrocarburizing process involves putting the workpiece into the nitriding furnace for pre-oxidation treatment, so as to produce an active oxide film on the surface of the workpiece. The nitrogen gas is introduced into the nitriding furnace for replacement, so that the nitriding furnace is filled with nitrogen gas. The ammonia gas is introduced into the nitriding furnace for nitridation, while carbon dioxide is introduced for carbonization. The workpiece is pulled out of the nitriding furnace.
   USE - Method for performing nitrocarburizing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform nitrocarburizing process which ensures that the workpiece is uniformly nitrided and improves the corrosion resistance of the workpiece.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pre-oxidation treatment includes hoisting the workpiece into the nitriding furnace. The temperature of the nitriding furnace was increased to 350 degrees C and held for 30 minutes. The nitrocarburizing method further involves increasing the temperature of the nitriding furnace to 350 degrees C and maintaining it for 30 minutes, hoisting the work piece out of the nitriding furnace, after 2-3 minutes. The work piece was hoisted into the nitriding furnace. The ammonia gas is introduced into the nitriding furnace for nitriding and carbon dioxide for carbonization, after the temperature of the nitriding furnace is increased to 330 degrees C, ammonia gas is introduced, after the temperature of the nitriding furnace is increased to 520 degrees C, ammonia gas and carbon dioxide are simultaneously introduced. The temperature of the nitriding furnace is raised to 520 degrees C. The furnace pressure of the nitriding furnace is increased to 20 kilo Pascal by adjusting the exhaust gas exhaust valve.

PN CN111097390-A
TI Blood purification adsorbent useful in blood perfusion device and for treating endotoxemia, sepsis, and cytokine storms caused by severe infection.
AU ZHENG F
   ZHOU P
   LIU L
   XING X
   CHEN R
AE SUZHOU TONGKANG MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202041615D
AB    NOVELTY - Blood purification adsorbent is claimed. The blood purification adsorbent is porous polyurethane aerogel.
   USE - The blood purification adsorbent is useful in blood perfusion device (claimed); and for treating endotoxemia, sepsis, and cytokine storms caused by severe infection.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: utilizes porous polyurethane aerogel material which has excellent mechanical properties, controllable pore size and distribution, high specific surface area, and large adsorption capacity; has wide adsorption range and simultaneously adsorb bacterial endotoxin, tumor necrosis factor, interleukin 6 and other cytokines in plasma.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing blood purification adsorbent comprising taking methylene diphenyl diisocyanate trimer and polyethylene glycol/polypropylene glycol as raw materials, drying supercritical carbon dioxide by sol-gel method; and
   (2) blood perfusion device comprising blood purification adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adding methylene diphenyl diisocyanate into acetone and dissolving by shaking to obtain first solution, (ii) adding polyethylene glycol or polypropylene glycol into acetone and dissolving by shaking to obtain second solution, (iii) mixing the obtained first solution with second solution, stirring and dissolving, adding catalyst to obtain third solution, and (iv) pouring the obtained third solution into mold, and allowing to stand, gelling, and then taking out gel, soaking and washing with acetone, and then drying supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain porous polyurethane aerogel bulk material. The method comprises adding 1 mol methylene diphenyl diisocyanate trimer into 5 l acetone and dissolving by shaking to obtain first solution, (ii) adding 1.5 mol polyethylene glycol or polypropylene glycol into 5 l acetone and dissolving by shaking to obtain second solution, (iii) mixing the obtained first solution with second solution, dissolving with stirring, and adding 5 ml catalyst to obtain third solution, and (iv) pouring the obtained third solution into mold, allowing to stand at room temperature, gelling after 12 hours, taking out after gelling, soaking and washing 4 times using acetone, and then supercritical drying under carbon dioxide conditions to obtain porous polyurethane aerogel block material as blood purification adsorbent. Preferred Components: The polyethylene glycol is polyethylene glycol 200, polyethylene glycol 400, polyethylene glycol 600 and/or polyethylene glycol 800. The polypropylene glycol is polypropylene glycol 400 or polypropylene glycol 1000. The catalyst is dibutyltin dilaurate.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Immunosuppressive.

   WANG Y
   ZHANG Y

AE UNIV SHANDONG TECHNOLOGY (SDUT-C)
GA 202023497G
AB    NOVELTY - Deep oxidation treatment for tert-butanol-containing wastewater, comprises using inorganic salt and water to prepare inorganic molten salt hydrate, uniformly mixing the inorganic molten salt hydrate and the homogeneous catalyst, heating, adding tert-butanol-containing wastewater and oxidant for first oxidation reaction to obtain small molecular organic compounds, performing second oxidation reaction on small molecule organics, oxidants and solid catalysts to obtain water and carbon dioxide; the inorganic salt comprises calcium chloride and/or calcium nitrate.
   USE - The method is useful for deep oxidation treatment for tert-butanol-containing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realizes reaction at normal pressure or low pressure; and has mild reaction conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of water to inorganic salt is 1-6:1. The solid catalyst is niobium-cerium/titanium dioxide. Preferred Method: In the method, the reaction temperature of first oxidation reaction is 80-85 degrees C. In the method, reaction time of the first oxidation reaction is 5-20 minutes. In the method, the reaction temperature of the second oxidation reaction is 150-250 degrees C. In the method, the reaction time of the second oxidation reaction is 5-60 seconds.

TI Preparation of planographic printing plate involves developing printing plate precursor containing image recording layer comprising infrared ray absorbent material, and binder polymer containing structural unit having acidic radical.
AU KAYAKI K
   KOYAMA I
AE FUJI FILM CORP (FUJF-C)
GA 202019449T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of planographic printing plate involves developing a positive planographic printing plate precursor containing an image recording layer comprising a binder polymer comprising a structural unit (s1) having an acidic radical and a structural unit (s2) having a basic group, in a molecule, and an infrared ray absorbent material, using a developing solution having a pH of less than 7.
   USE - Preparation of planographic printing plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of planographic printing plate having excellent image-forming property, stability, printing resistance, sensitivity and durability. The planographic printing plate enables formation of print with suppressed generation of image development dregs and carbon dioxide gas exhaustion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Component: The printing plate preferably comprises an upper layer and a lower layer, formed on a support component. The upper layer and/or the lower layer are the image recording layer. Preferred Process: The method further involves subjecting the positive planographic printing plate precursor to image-exposing process before the image development process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: Structural unit (s1) and/or structural unit (s2) are connected with a principal chain through a coupling group. The chain length between the acidic radical or basic group and the principal chain is 3 or more atoms. The acidic radical is carboxyl group. The basic group is tertiary amino group or pyridyl group. The binder polymer is an acrylic resin. Structural unit (s1) is of formula (I) or (II). Structural unit (s2) is chosen from structural units of formula (III)-(V). The binder polymer is a polycondensation resin or a polyaddition resin. The basic group is optionally contained in the principal chain. The image recording layer further comprises a resin (r1) having a urea bond, a urethane bond, or amide bond in a principal chain. Resin (r1) is one or more chosen from phenol resin, styrene-acrylonitrile copolymer, acrylic resin, and acetal resin.R1,R2=H or methyl;X1,X2=single bond, ester bond, or amide bond;L1-L4=single bond or bivalent coupling group;A=acidic radical;B'=basic group;andor L3+L4,A2+B',L2+B'=ring.Preferred Properties: The acid dissociation constant of the acid conjugated with respect to basic group is 4-9. The weight average molecular weight of the binder polymer is 8000-20000.

PN KR2019115700-A; KR2101285-B1
TI Producing porous polymer used to produce porous polymer particles, film and monolith, comprises e.g. polymerizing precursor aqueous solution containing monomer including amine and aldehyde group to obtain resin, and curing obtained resin.
AU KIM S
   KANG I
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 201988031M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing porous polymer, comprises (a) polymerizing precursor aqueous solution containing monomer including amine group and aldehyde group to obtain resin, and (b) curing the obtained resin using aprotic solvent to obtain porous polymer.
   USE - The method is useful for producing porous polymer particles, film and monolith (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method easily produces porous polymer of desired shape and having high surface area and uniform medium pore size; and uses adsorbent having excellent adsorption capacity for carbon dioxide or metal ions or catalyst support for improving catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for porous polymer prepared by the above method; and #porous polymer particles produced by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aprotic solvent includes dimethylacetamide, dimethylformamide, 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinone, or dimethyl sulfoxide. The first organic solvent and second organic solvent are each the same as or different from each other includes cyclohexane, hexane, heptane, octane, benzene, toluene, xylene, mesitylene or ethylbenzene. The precursor aqueous solution further includes acetoguanamine 6-methyl-1,3,5-triazine-2,4-diamine, urea, diaminodiphenyl sulfone, triaminotriphenylphosphineoxide or polyacrylamide aqueous solution. Preferred Method: The step of preparing suspension comprises further adding second organic solvent. The reverse phase suspension polymerization takes place in a layered micelle comprising the first organic solvent-the precursor aqueous solution-the second organic solvent. The method further comprises preparing suspension by adding the precursor aqueous solution to a first organic solvent, polymerizing the precursor in the suspension by a reverse phase suspension polymerization, and removing the first organic solvent to obtain particles of the resin, and curing the particles of the resin obtained by the using the aprotic solvent to obtain the particles of the porous polymer. The step (c) further comprises adding a second organic solvent, so that the reverse phase suspension polymerization occurs in a micelle of a layer structure including the first organic solvent-the precursor aqueous solution-the second organic solvent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The monomer containing amine group is melamine-based compound. The precursor aqueous solution is polymerized into particles, films, or monoliths to obtain the resin. The porous polymer is carbon dioxide adsorbent, metal ion adsorbent, catalyst support, or precursor of porous carbon structure. The porous polymer particle is carbon dioxide adsorbent, metal ion adsorbent, catalyst support, or precursor of a porous carbon structure
   EXAMPLE - 19.2 ml Cyclohexane and 0.072 g suspension stabilizer Span 80 (RTM: Nonionic surfactant) were added and dissolved in a 50 ml three-necked round flask at 60 degrees C. The precursor mixture was added into the cyclohexane solution and polymerized by stirring at 60 degrees C at 1200 revolutions/minute for 3 hours, and filtered to obtain porous polymer.

PN CN110203948-A; CN110203948-B
TI Preparing lithium iodide trihydrate involves dissolving lithium hydroxide monohydrate into aqueous solution, slowly adding iodine under stirring to form lithium iodide and lithium iodate, and stirring well, until reaction is complete.
AU YANG F
AE XINJIANG JUNQIANG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
   XINJIANG JUNQIANG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201978531F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium iodide trihydrate involves dissolving lithium hydroxide monohydrate into aqueous solution, slowly adding iodine under stirring to form lithium iodide and lithium iodate, and stirring well until reaction is complete, at which time solution is brown, adding formic acid under stirring until brown color of solution completely fades, boiling to remove carbon dioxide formed by reaction, adjusting pH, adding activated carbon, stirring, suction filtration, and heating filtrate to lithium iodide crystal to stop heating immediately, and naturally cooling, keeping, centrifugal dehydration, to obtain lithium iodide trihydrate.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium iodide trihydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare lithium iodide trihydrate that prevents corrosion of iodide of the evaporation vessel and avoids evaporating vessel pollution of lithium iodide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the aqueous solution of lithium hydroxide in single water is 8-12%. The molar ratio of lithium hydroxide in monohydrogen to iodine is 2:1. The mass of activated carbon is 0.296-0.893% of mass of iodine. The filtrate obtained by centrifugal dehydration is used for configuration of aqueous lithium hydroxide monohydrate solution.

AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
