PN CN110002947-A; CN110002947-B
TI Preparing monofluoroalkane by gas phase catalytic fluorination comprises using alkyl ether R1-O-R2 as a raw material, and carrying out gas phase fluorination reaction with carbonyl fluoride.
AU ZHANG C
   YANG G
   GUO Z
   QUAN H
AE BEIJING YUJI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   QUANZHOU YUJI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (QUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201963226P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monofluoroalkane by gas phase catalytic fluorination comprises using alkyl ether R1-O-R2 as a raw material, and carrying out gas phase fluorination reaction with carbonyl fluoride in the presence of a fluorination catalyst to obtain monofluoroalkanes R1-F and R2-F, the product stream of the fluorination reaction has alkyl ethers R1-O-R2, carbonyl fluoride, R1-F and R2-F and carbon dioxide and rectifying to obtain R1-F and R2-F, where R1=CnH2n-1, R2=CmH2m-1, n and m are all positive integers, n less than or equal to m.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monofluoroalkane by gas phase catalytic fluorination.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and provides high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluorination catalyst is aluminum fluoride, chromium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, zinc fluoride, calcium fluoride, iron fluoride, cobalt fluoride and/or nickel fluoride. The fluorination catalyst is chromium oxide, fluorinated chromium oxide, fluorinated alumina, chromium oxide supported on activated carbon or aluminum fluoride or magnesium fluoride, and chromium oxide containing various metals of zinc, cobalt and nickel. The alkyl ether is dimethyl ether and methyl ethyl ether or diethyl ether. Preferred Method: The fluorination catalyst is prepared by calcining precursor at a high temperature or by charging a precursor on a porous support at a high temperature, where the precursor is oxide or hydroxide of aluminum, chromium, magnesium, zinc, calcium, iron, cobalt and nickel or fluorinated oxide. The preparation of fluorination catalyst comprises press-molding precursor or press-molding precursor supported on the porous support to obtain catalyst precursor, calcining the obtained catalyst precursor at 300-500 degrees C for 6-15 hours under nitrogen atmosphere, further at 60-450 degrees C introducing mixed gas atmosphere of hydrogen fluoride and nitrogen at a molar ratio of 1:10 and activating for 6 to 15 hours. The gas phase catalytic fluorination reaction condition is: a reaction pressure of 0.1-1.5 MPa, molar ratio of alkyl ether to carbonyl fluoride of 1:1-10, preferably 1:2-5, the reaction temperature of 200-450 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C and the contact time is 0.5-50 seconds, preferably 5-30 seconds. In the rectification process, when the alkyl ether is dimethyl ether, the method comprises (a.i) when the top components of the first distillation column are carbonyl fluoride, carbon dioxide and monofluoromethane, the components of the column are dimethyl ether, recycling the components of the column to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor to continue the reaction, and recycling the top component to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor to continue the reaction, and (a.ii) continuously reacting the top component of the first distillation column by continuously circulating to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor until the carbonyl fluoride content in the top component of the first distillation column is less than 1%, passing the top component to a carbon dioxide absorption tower containing a 14-31.6% aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide, controlling the amount of the substance absorbing carbon dioxide to 50% of the amount of the sodium hydroxide substance, and the component of the column after the absorption of the carbon dioxide is an aqueous solution of sodium carbonate, and the composition of the top of the column is monofluoromethane. In the rectification process, when the alkyl ether is CH3-O-R2, where R2=CmH2m-1, m is a positive integer, and 2 less than or equal to m, the method comprises (b.i) when the top components of the first distillation column is carbonyl fluoride, carbon dioxide and monofluoromethane, the components of the column are CH3-O-R2 and R2-F, distilling the components of the column in the second stage and the distillation column continuously, and recycling the top component to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor to continue the reaction, (b.ii) reacting the top component of the first distillation column continuously by continuously circulating to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor until the carbonyl fluoride content in the top component of the first distillation column is less than 1%, passing the top component into a carbon dioxide absorption tower containing a 14-31.6% aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, and controlling the amount of the carbon dioxide-absorbing substance to 50% of the amount of the sodium hydroxide substance, the tower component after the carbon dioxide is absorbed is an aqueous solution of sodium carbonate, and the top component is monofluoromethane, and (b.iii) distilling the top component of the second distillation column is R2-F, and the composition of the column is methyl ethyl ether for second time. In the rectification process, when the alkyl ether is R1-O-R2, where R1=CnH2n-1, R2=CmH2m-1, n and m are both positive integers, and 2 less than or equal to n less than or equal to m, the rectification process comprises (c.i) when the top component of the first distillation column is carbonyl fluoride and carbon dioxide, and the components of the column are a mixture of alkyl ethers R1-O-R2, R1-F and R2-F, and recycling the top component to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor to continue the reaction, and separating the column component into the second distillation column for separation, (c.ii) continuously reacting the top component of the first distillation column by continuously circulating to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor until the carbonyl fluoride content in the top component of the first distillation column is less than 1%, passing the top component to a carbon dioxide absorption tower containing a 14-31.6% aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide, controlling the amount of the substance absorbing carbon dioxide to be 50% of the amount of the sodium hydroxide substance, and the component of the tower after the carbon dioxide is absorbed is an aqueous solution of sodium carbonate, (c.iii) when the top component of the second distillation column is a mixture of R1-F and R2-F, the components of the column are alkyl ethers R1-O-R2, and recycling the components of the second distillation column to the gas phase catalytic fluorination reactor to continue the reaction, and (c.iv) if R1 and R2 are not the same, the top components R1-F and R2-F of the second distillation column enter the third distillation column to continue the distillation, if R1 and R2 are the same, the top component of the second distillation column has R1-F or R2-F, and the distillation operation of the third distillation column is not required, the top component of the third distillation column is R1-F having a lower boiling point, and the composition of the bottom of the third distillation column is R2-F having a higher boiling point, if R1 and R2 are the same, the top component of the second distillation column has obtained R1-F or R2-F, and the distillation operation of the third distillation column is not required.

AU LEE J

AE UNIV LUNGHWA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLU-Non-standard)
GA 201956640S
AB    NOVELTY - The invention discloses a novel method for extracting catechins and a detergent containing catechins. The method comprises the following steps of: placing newly-harvested fresh leaves into a super-pure water solution with a temperature of more than 70 DEG C and below 100 DEG C produced by water quality purification technology using an ion exchange principle for 1 minute; oscillating the fresh leaf ultra-pure water mixed solution at the frequency of more than 50 Hz and the frequency of 250 Hz for 10 minutes to obtain a fresh leaf mixed solution; heating the fresh leaf mixed solution in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology at the temperature of 280 DEG C and the pressure of 0.05 million pa (mpa) processing so as to obtain an extracting solution.

AB    NOVELTY - A barrier raw material has an oxygen                permeability of 10 mL/m2.day.atm or less at a                relative humidity of 0% and at 23 degrees C, and an                overall biomass degree of at least 50% or                more.
   USE - Barrier raw material is used in production of                bag, paper container, carton box, cup, and normal                or flexible packaging material (all claimed) for                foodstuffs. Can also be used as industrial                materials, such as wall paper, paper tube,                corrosion-prevention paper, and for                construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The barrier material has excellent biomass                degree, gas and water vapor-barrier performance,                biodegradability, and low carbon dioxide discharge                amount thus contributing to global warming                prevention.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The raw material                  comprises a resin layer comprising biodegradable                  resin, preferably polylactic acid or polybutylene                  succinates, and a biological-origin resin as a                  main component on at least one surface of the                  base material, and a coating layer on at least 1                  surface of the base material, preferably paper                  base material comprising a water vapor barrier                  layer and a gas barrier layer on a base paper.                  The raw material comprises mice. The gas barrier                  layer comprises surfactant and water-soluble                  polymer. The water vapor barrier layer contains                  water vapor barrier resin and a water repellent.                  Preferred Properties: The raw material has a                  water vapor permeability at 40 plus minus -0.5                  degrees C and relative humidity of 90 plus minus                  -2% of 100 g/m2.day or less, preferably 3.5                  g/m2.day or less, and a preferably oxygen                  permeability of 3 mL/m2.day.atm or less at a                  relative humidity of 85%. The basic weight of the                  paper base material is 30-150 g/m2.

TI Production of zirconium tetrachloride by one-step reaction involves reacting zirconium silicate, zirconium carbide, and chlorine gas.
AU CHENG G
   JIA G
   LIU Y
   WANG X
AE INNER MONGOLIA AUTONOMOUS REGION (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2019310544
AB    NOVELTY - Production of zirconium tetrachloride involves reacting 1 mol part zirconium silicate, 2-4 mol parts zirconium carbide and 8-12 mol parts chlorine gas to obtained product with silicon chloride and carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide as by-products at 900-1300 degrees C.
   USE - Production of zirconium tetrachloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction is continuous, exothermic, can be spontaneously maintained, does not requires external heating source to maintain the reaction, and has high production efficiency. The solid materials have similar densities, are not easy to be layered in the boiling furnace, and are uniformly dispersed, which is favorable for the reaction, and most of the chlorine used in the reaction is converted into zirconium tetrachloride. The capacity of zirconium tetrachloride per unit cross-sectional area of the reactor is high.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The zirconium silicate has particle size 100-400 mesh, preferably 150-200 mesh, and the zirconium carbide has particle size of 100-400 mesh, preferably 150-200 mesh. Preferred Process: The chlorine gas is treated and reused during the reaction. The reactor is a boiling furnace.

GA 2019234634
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises 30-50 pts. wt.                licorice, 20-40 pts. wt. radix sophorae                flavescentis and 20-40 pts. wt. radix                scutellariae.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the composition, which involves                mixing the above-mentioned raw materials to obtain                a mixture, subjecting the mixture to super-critical                carbon dioxide extraction to obtain an extract,                separating the extract to obtain an isolated                product, subjecting the isolate to flash extraction                to obtain an extract, filtering the extract to                obtain a crude extract, and subjecting the crude                extract to nanofiltration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises 30-50 pts. wt. 1,3-butanediol.                  Preferred Method: The method further involves                  crushing licorice, radix sophorae flavescentis                  and radix scutellariae to a size of 60-100 mesh                  before mixing the materials, and concentrating                  the crude extract by nanofiltration using a                  dalton nanofiltration membrane having a pore size                  of 500-1000 mesh before concentrating to 40-50                  %mass crude extract by nanofiltration, and adding                  1,3-butanediol to the composition. The flash                  extraction on the isolate is performed by adding                  purified water 8-10 times based on the volume of                  the isolate to the isolate and carrying out flash                  extraction for 30-60 seconds. Preferred                  Conditions: The super-critical carbon dioxide                  extraction is carried out at 20-30 MPa, and at                  35-45 degrees C for 15-30 minutes. The separation                  process is carried out at less than or equal to 8                  MPa. The filtration is carried out using a                  ceramic membrane having a pore size of 0.22 mu                  m.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.

PN CN109399675-A; CN109399675-B
TI Mineralizing carbondioxide using magnesium resources in serpentine comprises e.g. subjecting pretreated serpentine and sulfuric acid to cross-flow leaching, subjecting leaching liquid to crystallization treatment and returning filtrate.
AU ZHU P
   LI Z
   QIAN G
   WANG Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 2019227039
AB    NOVELTY - Mineralizing carbondioxide using magnesium resources in serpentine comprises (a) subjecting pretreated serpentine and sulfuric acid to cross-flow leaching, subjecting filtered leaching liquid to crystallization treatment to obtain magnesium sulfate crystals and collecting the residue after filtration, returning the filtrate and leaching the serpentine reaction system solution; and (b) dissolving crystal of magnesium sulfate heptahydrate in water to obtain a magnesium sulfate solution, adding serpentine to the magnesium sulfate solution, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the magnesium sulfate solution to prepare hydrogen carbonate and heating magnesium solution to obtain magnesium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for mineralizing carbondioxide using magnesium resources in serpentine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can achieve storage of greenhouse gases and enrichment of metal nickel and iron.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: in the step (a) crushing serpentine, subjecting to ball-milling treatment and sieving the size of the particles through a sieve of 100-200 mesh to obtain a ball-milled pre-processed serpentine powder or crushing and subjecting the serpentine to ball milling treatment, sieving size of the particles through a sieve of 100-200 mesh to obtain serpentine powder, calcining serpentine powder at 500-700 degrees C for 15-180 minutes t o obtain calcined pre-activated treated serpentine powder. In the step (a) the temperature is 50-100 degrees C, the time is 10-250 minutes, and the solid-liquid mass-to-volume ratio of the pretreated serpentine and sulfuric acid is 0.1:10-1:1 (g/ml), the mixing speed of the pretreated serpentine and sulfuric acid mixture is 100-500 revolutions/minute and the mass concentration of sulfuric acid is 20-70 wt.%, the leaching solution after the leaching is concentrated and crystallized, and the heating temperature is 50-100 degrees C for 15-200 minutes, and then cooling to obtain a crystal of magnesium octahydrate. In the step (a) the number of cross-flow leaching is 3-10 times, and each of the cross-flow leaching solutions is crystallized and the filtered liquid is returned to the pre-treated serpentine in the first leaching process to obtain mixed solution of sulfuric acid. In the step (a) the pH of the leaching solution after the respective cross-flow leaching is controlled to be not more than 6. In the step (b) the filtered magnesium sulfate heptahydrate crystal is dissolved in water, and the serpentine ore powder is added, and the serpentine ore powder is prepared by using the pretreated serpentine ore prepared in the step (a) and carbon dioxide was bubbled into the magnesium sulfate solution to control the equilibrium pressure of carbon dioxide above the surface of the magnesium sulfate solution to be 0.1-100 MPa and the temperature of the magnesium sulfate solution was controlled at 10-80 degrees C. In the step (b) the magnesium sulfate and serpentine mixture system is ball-milled to accelerate the neutralization of the acid produced by the carbon dioxide mineralization reaction and the ball milling speed is controlled at 100-500 revolutions/minute. In the step (b) the magnesium hydrogencarbonate solution obtained by the mineralization reaction is subjected to heat treatment to obtain magnesium carbonate precipitation at a heating temperature of 50-100 degrees C and a heating time of 15-200 minutes. In the step (b) the pH of the solution in the preparation of the magnesium carbonate is controlled to be 8-10.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-B02

PN DE102017007109-A1
TI Nitrogen-free compressor-less internal combustion engines or turbines for motor vehicles or an aircraft, comprises a built-in pressure tank for oxygen as a replacement for the air compression.
AU ZELTER B
AE ZELTER B (ZELT-Individual)
GA 201911673L
AB    NOVELTY - The nitrogen-free compressor-less internal combustion engines or turbines comprises a built-in pressure tank for oxygen as a replacement for the air compression.
   USE - Nitrogen-free compressor-less internal combustion engines or turbines for motor vehicles or an aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the compression of the combustion air is spatially separated from the expansion, the power required for compaction is eliminated. Depending on the engine used, the power required for compression amounts to approximately 50 percent to 65 percent of the useful power. This separation reduces fuel consumption by more than 50 percent, and also reduces carbon dioxide emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of a natural gas as fuel. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN109197986-A
TI Preserving chilled largehead hairtail fish, by mixing e.g. oxygen and nitrogen to get fresh gas, immersing fish in preservative obtained by mixing e.g. citric acid, glycine and water, placing in closed box, air-drying and vacuum-packaging.
AU JIANG W
   LIU Y
   HU S
   YAN X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
GA 201907676T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving chilled largehead hairtail fish, comprises (i) mixing carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen to obtain a fresh gas, (ii) mixing sodium lactate, citric acid, glycine, trehalose, chitosan, pomegranate seed extract and water to obtain a preservative, and (iii) immersing the chilled fish in the preservative, then placing in a closed box filled with fresh gas at 0 degrees C, taking out after 20 minutes, air-drying at 0 degrees C and vacuum-packaging in a bag.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving chilled largehead hairtail fish.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively ensures the quality of the chilled fish and has excellent preservation effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen in step (i) is 2:1:2. The preservative comprises 0.5-1 wt.% sodium lactate, 2-4 wt.% citric acid, 3-4 wt.% glycine, 2-3 wt.% trehalose, 1-3 wt.% chitosan, 1-2 wt.% pomegranate seed extract and remaining water, where the sum of the all the components is 100 wt.%. Preferred Method: The pomegranate seed extract is prepared by air-drying the fresh pomegranate seeds, pulverizing to 40 mesh to obtain pomegranate seed powder, adding the seed powder to petroleum ether in a weight ratio of 1:12, heating at 55 degrees C, then degreasing for 9 hours, taking out to obtain a defatted pomegranate seed powder, adding the defatted powder to a 60 vol.% aqueous ethanol solution in a weight ratio of 1:18, heating at 55 degrees C, then extracting by shaking in a water bath for 1.5 hours to obtain an extract, centrifuging the extract at 3000 rpm for 30 minutes to obtain a supernatant, rotationally evaporating and concentrating the extract at 30 degrees C and drying the concentrated extract.

TI Preparing bioengineered plastic comprises adding polylactic acid resin, polycarbonate, chitosan, and impact modifier into mixer for mixing, and extruding the mixed material in twin-screw extruder.
AU LIU C
AE HUAIAN BINGQING CONSTR ENG MANAGEMENT CO (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 201908071C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bioengineered plastic comprises adding 50-85 pts. wt. polylactic acid resin, 50-75 pts. wt. polycarbonate, 3-8 pts. wt. chitosan, and 5-20 pts. wt. impact modifier into mixer for mixing, and extruding the mixed material in twin-screw extruder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bioengineered plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The bioengineered plastic: contains easily available raw material; has low cost production method, stable quality, good heat resistance and mechanical properties; contains impact-modifying auxiliaries, which improves impact resistance; contains polylactic acid resin, which reduces the amount of petroleum-based polymer in the material, and amount of carbon dioxide waste emitted to the atmosphere during production, thus achieves environmentally friendly; and suitable for industrial continuous production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The impact modifier is ACR impact modifier or an EVA impact modifier. Preferred Method: In the method, the mixing conditions are, the mixing speed is 60-80 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 10-15 minutes. In the method, the extrusion conditions are, the temperature control parameters are, feeding section is 153-155 degrees C, compressed section is 168-170 degrees C, the head temperature is 188-192 degrees C, and the die temperature is 187-189 degrees C. The polylactic acid resin preparation comprises (a) extracting the raw material, adding pure lactic acid and calcium carbonate to ferment under the action of the biocatalyst to obtain fermentation liquid, (b) adding sulfuric acid to the fermentation liquid, stirring, filtering, concentrating the filtrate by evaporation, and filtering again to obtain lactic acid, and (c) refining the lactic acid, dehydrating and oligomerizing, pyrolyzing to form cyclic dimer lactide, and then cyclically polymerizing into polylactic acid resin, where the raw material extracted in the step (a) is corn, wheat, and cassava, the biocatalyst is maltase and a saccharification enzyme, and the volume percentage of sulfuric acid in the step (2) is 20-30 vol.%.

AE JIANGSU HANBON SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201908081E
AB    NOVELTY - The method for separating and purifying omeprazole involves (1) dissolving crude omeprazole using methanol, and filtering using 0.45 mu m microporous membrane, (2) configuring an entrainer, (3) separating and purifying the filtered crude product by a supercritical fluid chromatography system, using a silica matrix filler as a stationary phase, and a supercritical carbon dioxide with entrainer as a mobile phase to obtain filtered omeprazole crude product, and (4) post-processing received liquid fraction using a rotary evaporator, and the obtained white powder is the target product.
   USE - Method for separating and purifying omeprazole.

PN IN201831039706-A; IN408498-B
TI Cereal based carbonated beverage comprises storage            stable synergistic blend of pre-treated roasted            cereal/s and edible carbonation material as beverage            mix.
AU MISHRA H N
   PANDEY P
   THAKUR A
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY KHARAGPUR (IIKH-C)
   INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY KHARAGPUR (IIKH-C)
GA 2018A2775D
AB    NOVELTY - Cereal based carbonated beverage comprises                storage stable synergistic blend of pre-treated                roasted cereal/s and edible carbonation material as                beverage mix.
   USE - Cereal based carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The cereal based carbonated beverage replaces                unhealthy carbonated drink (soda) which poses lots                of harmful effects related to health, and is very                much nutritious (16% protein) and easily                digestible, and can be easily stored in sachets or                sealed containers with maximum flexibility of                transportation for 2 months.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing cereal based carbonated beverage,                which involves providing storage stable synergistic                blend of pre-treated roasted cereal/s as beverage                premix together with edible carbonation materials                and obtaining beverage mix adapted for slow release                of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The cereal based                  carbonated beverage comprises dry storage stable                  beverage powder and formulations including                  synergistic blend of pre-treated roasted cereal                  flours and edible carbonation powder adapted for                  slow release of carbon dioxide when reconstituted                  with water. The synergistic blend comprises                  pre-treated roasted select cereal flours with                  moisture content at (5-7 % wet basis) level with                  edible carbonation powder present in the levels                  of 2-8% (upto 0.8 g per 10 g) of the beverage                  mix. The synergistic blend involving edible                  carbonation powder in amounts includes 45-50%                  sodium bicarbonate and 50-55% food grade                  acidulant (leavening acid) preferably citric acid                  in beverage mix favoring enhancement of                  acceptability and organoleptic properties with                  regard to sensory evaluation with overall                  acceptability of 7.7 and is free from any bitter                  soapy off taste due to the incorporation of                  sodium bicarbonate. The cereal based carbonated                  beverge is protein rich and includes 14-16%                  protein, minerals such as 10-12 mg/100g iron,                  36-38 mg/100g calcium, 86-88 mg/100g magnesium                  respectively and is stable for 2 months storage                  free of additives and artificial sweeteners. The                  pretreated roasted cereal flours comprises maize,                  bengal gram, finger millet flours selectively                  roasted and is present in combination with pea                  protein isolate, sugar and is gluten free,                  optionally including flavors including black salt                  and cumin powder. The maize, bengal gram, finger                  millet are selectively roasted at select                  conditions including for maize at 175-180 degrees                  C for 10-15 minutes, bengal gram at 175-180                  degrees C for 25-30 minutes and finger millet at                  100-120 degrees C for 30-35 minutes. The selected                  amounts of grains involves taking 30-40 wt.%                  maize, 30-35 wt.% bengal gram, 8-12 wt.% finger                  millet that is gluten free together with 20-25                  wt.% sugar and 10-15 wt.% pea protein isolate for                  preparation of 100 g of the beverage mix. The                  effective carbonation is attainable for upto 8%                  carbonation powder concentration in the powder                  beverage mix reconstituted with water in the                  ratio of 1:4. Preferred Process: The method for                  preparing cereal based carbonated beverage, which                  involves performing grain pre-treatment by                  limiting the moisture content within level,                  roasting grains at select conditions followed by                  grinding and sieving to provide for select                  particle size of 250 micron, mixing the                  pre-treated roasted grain flour mix with water in                  the ratio of 1:2 and colloidal milling to obtain                  colloidal paste, freeze drying colloidal paste at                  select conditions of (-)40 degrees C, pressure of                  10-3 millibar for 12 hours followed by grinding                  and sieving freeze dried paste to obtain select                  particle size of 150 micron to provide for                  beverage premix, adding carbonation powder                  (0.8g/10 g sample), and obtaining carbonated                  beverage mix preferably as powder. The method                  optionally includes adding flavors including                  black salt and cumin powder to pretreated roasted                  grain flour prior to the step of colloidal                  milling.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108822094-A
TI Extracting silymarin comprises e.g. dissolving silybum marianum seed, shelling, washing, drying the silybum marianum seed by ultraviolet lamp sterilizing, adding the enzymolysis liquid and concentrating.
AU WANG F
AE XIAN REJOY ESSENCE PHARM CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201891909U
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting silymarin comprises (i) dissolving the silybum marianum seed, shelling to obtain the silybum marianum seed and washing the silybum marianum seed repeatedly, drying in the ventilated environment, drying the silybum marianum seed by ultraviolet lamp sterilizing, (ii) adding the silybum marianum seed into the sealing tank, and adding the enzymolysis liquid for enzymolysis reaction for 30-60 minutes, (iii) adding anhydrous ethanol to the enzymatic reaction product for 2-3 times to obtain an extract, (iv) adding the extract into the carbon dioxide fluid device for extraction, (v) concentrating the extract to obtain the concentrate, and (vi) drying the concentrate at low temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting silymarin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the enzymatic hydrolysate is 20 wt.% lactic acid, 30 wt.% papainase, 20 wt.% L-cysteine, 10 wt.% ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium salt, and 20wt.% cellulase. In the step (iv), the carbon dioxide fluid flow rate is 400-500 l/hour, the fluid extraction pressure is 40-50 MPA, and the extraction temperature is 45-55 degrees C. In the step (v), the concentration temperature is 40-60 degrees C. In the step (vi), the drying temperature is 45-65 degrees C and the time is 120-180 minutes.

TI New cationic amino acid-containing ionic liquid containing anion and cation useful for catalysis synthesis of cyclic carbonate by epoxy compound.
AU YUE S
   HAO X
   LI J
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 2018927279
AB    NOVELTY - Cationic amino acid-containing ionic liquid containing anion (I) and cation (II) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for catalysis synthesis of cyclic carbonate by epoxy compound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high yield, strong activity, mild reaction condition, and high stability; does not requires other catalyst and solvents; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cationic amino acid-containing ionic liquid containing anion of formula (I) and cation of formula (II) is new.
   R = -CH2COOH, or (CH2)2COOH; and
   R1 = CH3 or C4H9.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use method comprising reacting catalyst, carbon dioxide and epoxy compound at a pressure of 0.5-3 MP and temperature of 100-150 degrees C for 10-15 hours to obtain cyclic carbonate, where the catalyst is (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises mixing 3-bromopropylamine hydrobromide with n-butylimidazole or N-methylimidazole under the protection of nitrogen, dissolving in absolute ethanol, refluxing at 85-95 degrees C for 20-25 hours, distilling the ethanol off under reduced pressure, and adding the organic solvent, stirring, and filtering, distilling off the filtrate under reduced pressure to remove the organic solvent, and drying in vacuo to obtain ionic liquid, passing the 717 type anion exchange resin, reacting the exchanged ionic liquid and amino acid in deionized water at a molar ratio of 1:1.1, and stirring, and filtering to remove excess amino acids, concentrating the filtrate by distillation under reduced pressure on a rotary evaporator, and suction filtering and drying to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The mole ratio between 3-bromopropylamine hydrobromide and n-butylimidazole or N-methylimidazole is 1:1.1. The organic solvent is toluene, acetonitrile, ethyl acetate, acetone or dichloromethane. The amino acid is aspartic acid or glutamic acid. The addition amount of catalyst is 0.1-0.3% by mole based on the epoxy compound. The epoxy compound is epichlorohydrin, propylene oxide, 1,2-epoxyethylbenzene, 1,2-epoxyhexane or 1,2-epoxyoctane.

TI Electrolytic process for manufacturing hydrogen involves step of electrolyzing a composition containing water and hydrazine with hydrazine.
AU TANBERK O
   VON LIECHTENSTEIN T
   LIECHTENSTEIN T V
AE TEC ADVANCED ANSTALT (TECA-Non-standard)
   TEC ADVANCED ANSTALT (TECA-Non-standard)
   TEC ADVANCED ANSTALT (TECA-Non-standard)
   TEC ADVANCED ANSTALT (TECA-Non-standard)
GA 201885376A
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolytic process for manufacturing hydrogen involves step of electrolyzing a composition containing water and hydrazine with greater than or equal to 98 wt.% hydrazine.
   USE - An electrolytic process for manufacturing hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Method has improved efficiency and improved combustion and avoids carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) electrolytic cell comprising housing, cathode, anode, electrolytic composition and optionally a diaphragm in which electrolytic composition contains water and greater than or equal to 98 wt.% hydrazine;
   (2) device comprising the electrolytic cell device chosen from motor vehicles, aircrafts, trains, heating system, compressor systems, and generator systems;
   (3) use of hydrazine as electrolyte; and
   (4) use of composition as electrolyte composition containing 2-99.8 wt.% water and 0.2-98 wt.% hydrazine, or as additive to inertnal combustion engines.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Composition contains water and 0.2-98 wt.%, preferably 0.5-50 wt.% hydrazine. Preferred Conditions: Process has voltage of 2-480 V, preferably 12-240 V; and/or temperature of 0-100 degrees C, preferably 20-40 degrees C. Composition has pH greater than or equal to 7, preferably 7-12.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Cathode material is selected from titanium and its alloys or plated with titanium and its alloys, and anode is selected from titanium and its alloys or plated with titanium and its alloys. TI Preparation of composite shell for reducing blind hole investment casting involves brushing mold base coating layer, pouring gypsum slurry in cavity, scraping, solidifying, and removing plaster mold core.
AU LIU K
   WANG L
   SUN T
AE ANHUI XINHONG MACHINERY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018492857

PN CN107963963-A
TI Preparing o-hydroxybenzoic acid comprises e.g. reacting phenol and sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, vacuum extracting and dehydrating, adding iso-octanol into sodium phenolate fluid, carrying out pressure carboxylation and acidifying.
AU LIU Y
   XIAO Y
   YE Z
   WAN G
AE JIANGXI LONGNAN PHARM & CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018347954
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing o-hydroxybenzoic acid comprises (i) reacting phenol and sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to obtain sodium phenolate, vacuum extracting and dehydrating to obtain sodium phenate fluid; (ii) adding iso-octanol into the sodium phenolate fluid and removing the water under reduced pressure for a second time to obtain mixture I; (iii) feeding mixture I into carbon dioxide for carrying out pressure carboxylation to obtain mixture II; and (iv) acidifying mixture II.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing o-hydroxybenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of danger of using phenol as a solvent, defects of the gas-solid reaction and equipment pipeline blockage and uses mild reaction condition, is safe and controls operating environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of water content in sodium phenate fluid in step (i) is 15-25%, preferably 20%. The volume ratio of the sodium phenate fluid and added isooctanol in step (ii) is 1: 1-4, preferably1:1.5-2. The volume ratio of the distillate and isooctanol in step (ii) is 0.5-0.8:1, preferably 0.5-0.6:1. The amount of water content in mixture I in step (ii) is less than 0.3%. Preferred Method: The pressure of the second depressurization and dehydrating in step (ii) is carried out at 0.05-0.09 MPa, preferably at 0.08 MPa. The second depressurization and dehydrating in step (ii) is carried out at 70-130 degrees C. The carboxylation reaction in step (iii) is carried out at 120-180 degrees C, preferably at 155-165 degrees C at 0.5-0.7 MPa for 6-12 hours.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)
MC E10-C03; E11-F02B; E11-F11; E11-W

TI Tea extract used for preparing cosmetic composition e.g. skin cream, for e.g. providing anti-skin aging and antioxidant effects, comprises liquid or solid components extracted from purple bamboo shoots using e.g. organic solvent.
AU HUO G
   MA P
   FANG T
   ZHANG L
AE OSMUN BIOLOGICAL CO LTD (OSMU-Non-standard)
GA 201833092J
AB    NOVELTY - Tea extract comprises liquid or solid components extracted from purple bamboo shoots using water, organic solvent or supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The tea extract is useful for preparing cosmetic composition e.g. facial cleanser, lotion, skin cream, essence or mask, which is used providing anti-skin aging and antioxidant effects, inhibiting key receptors in synthesis and transfer pathway of melanin and metastasis, and promoting proliferation of HaCaT cells and HSF cells (all claimed). The tea extract is also used for beautifying and whitening skin and promoting proliferation of human fibroblasts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cosmetic composition, which comprises tea extract and auxiliary material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The cosmetic composition is in form cream, emulsion, solution, film, aerosol or spray form. The cosmetic composition is chosen from facial cleanser, shower gel, lotion, skin care gel, skin care emulsion, skin cream, essence, eye cream or mask. The cosmetic composition comprises 0.001-100 wt.%, preferably 0.01-50 wt.%, more preferably 0.5-20 wt.% purple bamboo shoot tea extract. Preferred Components: The organic solvent is chosen from ethanol, butanediol, and acetone. The auxiliary material is chosen from solvent, solubilizer, preservative, antioxidant, pH regulator, penetrating agent, liposome, humectant, thickening agent, chelating agent, skin-feel regulator, surfactant, emulsifier, essence, pigment, and other additive.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Melanin synthesis inhibitor; Tyrosinase inhibitor; Tyrosine-related protein 2 inhibitor; Melanocortin receptor 1 inhibitor; Protease activated receptor 2 inhibitor; Hyaluronan synthase 1 expression promoter.

PN WO2018069702-A1; TW201829366-A; CA3041025-A1; KR2019061085-A; SG11201903249-A1; BR112019007526-A2; IN201917016253-A; EP3526191-A1; CN110382454-A; JP2019532073-W; US2019382332-A1; ID201906017-A; US10683255-B2; MX2019004301-A1; SG11201903249-B; IN378039-B; TW736690-B1; JP7058267-B2; KR2464444-B1
TI Ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid ester            production involves reacting formaldehyde or its            suitable source with carboxylic acid ester in presence            of basic metal methyl carbonate co-reactant to produce            another basic metal salt.
AU EASTHAM G R
   IGGO J A
   BEAUMONT M
AE LUCITE INT UK LTD (LUCI-C)
   LUCITE INT UK LTD (LUCI-C)
   LUCITE INT UK LTD (LUCI-C)
   LUCITE INT UK LTD (LUCI-C)
   MITSUBISHI CHEM UK LTD (MITU-C)
GA 201830866W
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid                ester production involves reacting formaldehyde or                its suitable source with a carboxylic acid ester in                presence of a basic metal methyl carbonate                co-reactant to produce another basic metal salt.                The produced basic metal salt is contacted with                carbon dioxide and optionally methanol or dimethyl                carbonate to regenerate the basic metal methyl                carbonate co-reactant.
   USE - Method for producing ethylenically unsaturated                carboxylic acid ester, preferably beta                -ethylenically unsaturated carboxylic acid                ester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces ethylenically unsaturated                carboxylic acid ester in a simple and                cost-effective manner with remarkably high                conversions and selectivities for the reaction                products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation                depicting conversion of cesium propionate versus                temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is                  by-product carbon dioxide. The basic metal salt                  with carbon dioxide is carried out in presence of                  methanol. The methanol is by-product methanol.                  The process is a continuous process. The reaction                  takes place with reactants and co-reactant                  dissolved in the liquid phase. The basic metal                  methyl carbonate co-reactant is a group I or a                  group II metal methyl carbonate. The basic metal                  methyl carbonate co-reactant is cesium methyl                  carbonate. The latter basic metal salt is a metal                  carboxylate of general formula R3-CH2-COOM. The                  latter basic metal salt is a group I or group II                  metal propionate or methacrylate, preferably                  cesium propionate or cesium methacrylate.M=a metal of basic metal methyl carbonate                  co-reactant; andR3 and R4=hydrogen or 1-4C-alkyl group,                  preferably a methyl group or is a metal                  carboxylate of formula CH2=C(R4)(COOM).The suitable source of formaldehyde is                  selected from formalin (formaldehyde, methanol or                  water), low molecular weight polyformaldehyde                  (para-formaldehyde), gaseous formaldehyde,                  formaldehyde hemiacetal (alcoform), trioxane or                  anhydrous formaldehyde, or formaldehyde from a                  distillative drying process, or other sources of                  formaldehyde. The process also comprises one or                  multiple solvents. The solvent is wholly or                  substantially aprotic. The solvent is selected                  from dimethyl formamide, diethyl formamide,                  dimethylacetamide, diethylacetamide,                  1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinone, hexamethylphosphoric                  triamide, pyridine, tetramethyl urea,                  dimethylsulfoxide, acetonitrile, propionitrile,                  benzonitrile, acetone, 2-butanone, 3-pentanone,                  acetophenone, nitromethane, nitrobenzene,                  tetrahydrothiophene 1,1-dioxide (sulfolane),                  diethyl ether, diisopropyl ether, 1,4-dioxane,                  tetrahydrofuran, 1,2-dimethoxyethane, diglyme,                  benzene, cyclohexane, xylene or toluene. The                  carboxylic acid ester is non-cyclic. The former                  basic metal salt co-reactant is selected from                  potassium oxide, cesium oxide, sodium oxide,                  rubidium oxide, barium oxide, potassium                  hydroxide, cesium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                  rubidium hydroxide, barium hydroxide, potassium                  phosphate, cesium phosphate, sodium phosphate,                  rubidium phosphate, barium phosphate, sodium                  methoxide, potassium methoxide, rubidium                  methoxide, sodium tert.-butoxide, potassium                  tert.-butoxide, rubidium tert.-butoxide, cesium                  tert.-butoxide, sodium fluoride, potassium                  fluoride, rubidium fluoride, cesium fluoride,                  potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate, sodium                  carbonate, rubidium carbonate, barium carbonate,                  potassium hydrogen carbonate, sodium hydrogen                  carbonate, rubidium hydrogen carbonate, cesium                  hydrogen carbonate, or barium hydrogen                  carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107913689-A
TI Regeneration of solid waste active carbon and hazardous waste active carbon by mixing solid waste active carbon and hazardous waste active carbon, drying mixture, activating, and sending activated mixture into three-stage cyclone separator.
AU YU W
AE YU W (YUWW-Individual)
GA 201831521F
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration of solid waste active carbon and hazardous waste active carbon is carried out by uniformly mixing solid waste active carbon and hazardous waste active carbon, and drying resulting mixture in flash evaporation drying device to obtain dry active carbon mixture; activating the dry active carbon mixture in physical activation furnace using activating medium comprising mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide; sending the activated active carbon mixture and gas in physical activation furnace into a three-stage cyclone separator; and collecting activated carbon products from first-stage cyclone separation and second-stage cyclone separation and storing.
   USE - Method for regeneration of solid waste active carbon and hazardous waste active carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Drying in the flash evaporation drying device is carried out for 0.5-10 hours at air inlet temperature of 160-190 degrees C and air outlet temperature of 90-100 degrees C. The physical activation furnace is provided with a low temperature zone for drying the activated carbon mixture at 400-600?Oc, an intermediate temperature zone for activation and carbonization of activated carbon mixture at 650-750 degrees C, and a high temperature zone for deep activation of the activated carbon mixture at 1000-1200 degrees C. The activated carbon products are packed in plastic woven bag or cloth bag and are stored at controlled environment humidity of less than 30% and temperature of lower than 15 degrees C.

AU DAN H
   LI H
   DAN S
   HAI D
   JIANG G
   LI X
AE HENAN SANHE PHARM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201815602C
AB    NOVELTY - Low residual sulfur processing of traditional Chinese medicine by fumigating with sulfur comprises (1) placing the Chinese medicinal materials in the fumigating room, taking (2-4) g/m3 sulfur as fumigating for 1-6 hours, (2) soaking the traditional Chinese medicine in the solution for 4-6 hours, (3) placing the soaked Chinese medicinal materials into calcium hydroxide solution, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas for 1-2 hours, and (4) washing the Chinese medicinal materials, and drying to obtain low-residual Chinese medicinal materials.
   USE - The method is useful for low residual sulfur processing of traditional Chinese medicine by fumigating with sulfur.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can preserves Chinese herbal medicines; ensures the health of the human body; and reduces side effects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (1) introducing sulfur dioxide gas in the fumigation room into salt water after the low-sulfur fumigation is completed. In the step (2) solution is salt aqueous solution, the mass ratio of sodium chloride and water is 1: (10-20). In the step (2), solution immersion process in ultrasonic strengthening treatment. The ultrasonic power of the ultrasonic strengthening treatment is 300-500w. The each ultrasonic time is 5-10 seconds, the interval time of two adjacent ultrasonic treatment is 30-60 seconds. In the step (3) the calcium hydroxide solution is saturated solution. The carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide with mol ratio of 1:(2-5). The carbon dioxide in the process of performing ultrasonic treatment. The time of each time of ultrasonic treatment is 5-10 seconds, the interval time of two adjacent ultrasonic treatment is 60-120 seconds.

   USE - Method for processing black water produced by coal gasification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces hard desalination, increases processing device, improves water recovery rate, does not affect process and layout for coal gasification process, and solves gray water scale formation, blockage and corrosion.

   (1) coal gasification method; and
   (2) coal gasification system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: Removing solid is done to reduced solid content by more than 60%. Contacting is done at 10-50 degrees C for 30-90 minutes. Micro-filtering is done at 10-40 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. Ultrafiltration is done at 10-40 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. Reverse osmosis is done at 10-40 degrees C for 10-30 minutes.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flocculating agent is inorganic flocculating agent and/or organic flocculating agent. The inorganic flocculating agent is polymerized aluminum chloride, polymerized aluminum sulfate, polymeric ferric sulfate and polymeric ferric chloride. The quantity of carbon dioxide is 0.7-2 L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic flocculating agent is polyacrylamide, sodium polyacrylate, polyvinyl pyridine salt and polyethylene imine. The dosage of flocculating agent is 2-4 g.

PT P
PN EP3868467-A1
TI Extracting carbon dioxide from liquid without using electrode involves extraction cycle by adsorbing carbon dioxide dissolved in liquid to porous material, and releasing adsorbed carbon dioxide from porous material as gaseous carbon dioxide.
AU JOOS L H S
AE JOOS L H S (JOOS-Individual)
GA 2021989205
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting carbon dioxide from a liquid, comprises an extraction cycle that comprises: (i) a sorption step by adsorbing carbon dioxide dissolved in the liquid to a porous material, where the porous material contacts the liquid; and (ii) a desorption step by releasing the adsorbed carbon dioxide obtained at the end of the sorption step from the porous material as gaseous carbon dioxide. The method does not comprise the use of an electrode.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting carbon dioxide from a liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not utilize the electrode; provides extraction of carbon dioxide at higher rates; involves at least two extraction cycles which do not employ the same porous material; utilizes the porous material which is hydrophilic in the gas phase that selectively absorbs carbon dioxide in water; requires less energy, to apply a temperature in the desorption step, hence it is energetically efficient; does not increase the mortality of aquatic organisms by more than 10% or more than 5%; and does not utilize any form of electricity and utilizes renewable energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for extracting carbon dioxide from the liquid, comprising the porous material, where the apparatus does not comprise a functioning electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises at least two extraction cycles. The desorption step comprises reducing pressure, heating the porous material, and/or contacting the porous material with steam. Preferred Components: The liquid is water. The porous material: is a selective carbon dioxide adsorber in the liquid; comprises zeolites, porous carbons, porous aluminosilicate polymorphs, porous polymer networks, porous inorganic oxides, metal-organic frameworks, zeolitic imidazolate frameworks or polyoligosiloxysilicones, preferably carbon black, activated carbons, silicalite-1, H-ZSM-5, faujasite, mordenite or zeolite; and comprises an acid site.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus: has a fixed-bed layout; and comprises a sealable compartment.
   EXAMPLE - 100 ml Carbonated water was poured into a glass, and 3 g carbon black (A8040605 SF) was added to it. The pH went up to 5.9. Then again 1.5 g carbon black was added, the pH further increased to 6.44. Small gas bubbles were formed at the carbon black pallets. A plastic bell was held with a carbon dioxide analyzer over the glass that showed that the carbon dioxide level on top of the water was going up, indicating that some carbon dioxide was leaving the water. The adsorbent was sieved, to remove most of the water. Then, the adsorbent was heated in a pan on a stove, at low power. A plastic bell with an opening on the side was placed on top of the pan. First, water vapor was formed followed by the formation of carbon dioxide.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

Content of a carbon element actually burned by a coal receiving base is obtained. A furnace flame excess air coefficient is determined based on the content of the CO and CO2 in the in-oxidative flue in the main burner zone. The hearth flue is determined according to the volume of CO in the flue generated by deep air classification combustion.
   USE - Method for determining hearth flame excess air coefficient of a hearth burner of a coal-fired boiler. Can also be used for a gas-fired burner.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining the furnace flame excess air coefficient according to the content of the carbon element actually burned by the coal receiving base and the theoretical amount dry air or, based on content of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the under-oxygen flue gas generated by the main combustion zone, thus controlling the boiler in a better manner.

PT P
PN WO2021133304-A1
TI Producing boric acid from colemanite by feeding water and colemanite mineral into pressurized reactor, feeding pressurized carbon dioxide gas, concentrating boric acid-containing main solution, crystallizing, and separating product.
AU GONEN M
AE GONEN M (GONE-Individual)
GA 2021741797
AB    NOVELTY - Boric acid is produced from colemanite by feeding water and colemanite mineral as solid-liquid suspension into pressurized reactor; pressurizing carbon dioxide (CO2) gas with compressor and feeding CO2 gas into pressurized reactor; producing boric acid and by-product calcium carbonate in the reaction; separating precipitated calcium carbonate and unreacted solids (dolomite, clay) from aqueous phase by filtering in the filtration unit; concentrating boric acid-containing main solution under vacuum (0.01-0.2 atm) at 60-80 degrees C; subjecting concentrated solution to crystallization in crystallization unit; separating solid product from aqueous phase with decanter and recycling boric acid-containing solution into reactor; and drying product (boric acid containing 8-11% water) in a drying unit.
   USE - Production of boric acid from colemanite.
   ADVANTAGE - The mass transfer resistances are reduced with the circulation of the carbon dioxide gas in the pressurized reactor by using the compressor during the reaction period. The method reduces water consumption in separation processes and reduces the number of impurities in the product because the solubility of calcium carbonate (calcite) formed in the reaction between colemanite mineral and carbonic acid in the pressure reactor in water is very low. The remaining solid (dolomite, clay, etc.) is washed with 50-80 degrees C water so as to remove the boric acid, and the diluted solution is fed back into the process. At the end of the reaction, the unreacted carbon dioxide gas is recycled to the carbon dioxide tank for reuse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Mixture is fed to the pressure reactor in a manner such that colemanite mineral:water weight ratio is 10:20. CO2 gas is fed at 35-70 degrees C with 60-90 atm pressure into the pressurized reactor. Solids are washed with water at 50-80 degrees C to remove remaining boric acid and recycling the diluted solution into the reactor. Concentrated solution is cooled to 30-40 degrees C and subjected to crystallization. The product (boric acid containing 8-11% water) is dried with air at 100-120 degrees C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

The data is sent to the monitoring host, and the monitoring host records all measurement data in real time. The monitoring host analyzes and processes the received data, displays and stores the received data, records the curve of the current carbon dioxide concentration over time, and then analyzes the data through the analysis software to determine whether the received data is qualified.

PN CN112844486-A; CN112844486-B
TI Catalyst composite material used for carbon            dioxide chemical fixation during cycloaddition reaction            of carbon dioxide and epoxide, comprises spindle-shaped            cerium dioxide, and ZIF-8 particles loaded on outer            surface of cerium dioxide.
AU HU L
   XU W
   QU J
   DING L
   WANG X
AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 2021616610
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst ZIF-8/cerium dioxide composite                material comprises spindle-shaped cerium dioxide,                and ZIF-8 particles uniformly loaded on the outer                surface of cerium dioxide, and has a micro-nano                structure, and a multi-level pore structure. The                ZIF-8 particles have a microporous structure. The                cerium dioxide has a mesoporous structure.
   USE - The catalyst composite material is useful for                carbon dioxide chemical fixation during                cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and                epoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst composite material has high                stability, specific surface area, and porosity, no                decrease in catalytic activity when using the fifth                time, and high repeated use performance than that                of ZIF-8 material. The product yield of the                catalyzed carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction can                reach more than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the catalyst composite material,                which involves: (1) dissolving cerium nitrate                hexahydrate and urea in deionized water, stirring                for 30-60 minutes to form a homogeneous mixed                liquid, transferring to stainless steel reactor                with PTFE liner, crystallizing under hydrothermal                conditions, heating the reactor, cooling to room                temperature, centrifuging, ultrasonically washing,                drying to obtain white powder, and calcining in a                muffle furnace to obtain light yellow powder of                spindle-shaped cerium dioxide; and (2) dispersing                the cerium dioxide powder in methanol solution,                then adding inorganic zinc salt, stirring at room                temperature for 1-2 hours to obtain a dispersion,                slowly adding methanol solution of                2-methylimidazole to the dispersion, continuously                stirring for 5-90 minutes, reacting, centrifuging                the reaction solution, ultrasonically washing, and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The uniform particle                  size of ZIF-8 particles is 200-300 nm. The                  spindle-shaped cerium dioxide has a diameter of                  1-2 mu m, and a length of 8-12 mu m. Preferred                  Conditions: The crystallization step is carried                  out at 120 degrees C for 8 hours. The drying step                  in step (1) is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for                  12-36 hours. The calcination step is carried out                  600 degrees C for 5 hours. The drying step in                  step (2) is carried out at 80-110 degrees C for                  12-18 hours. Preferred Components: The                  concentration of cerium nitrate hexahydrate is 30                  mmol/l. The concentration of urea aqueous                  solution 80 mmol/l. The concentration of cerium                  dioxide in the dispersion is 1-2 wt.%, and the                  usage amount of methanol is 2-10 ml. The                  inorganic zinc salt is zinc nitrate hexahydrate,                  zinc acetate dihydrate and/or zinc chloride.                  Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  inorganic zinc salt to cerium dioxide is                  (0.1-3):1. The molar ratio of the inorganic zinc                  salt to 2-methylimidazole is 1:(4-8).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The usage amount of                  methanol in the methanol solution of                  2-methylimidazole is 2-10 ml.

PN JP2021023840-A
TI Metal oxide-containing composite used as catalyst            for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises particles of            metal oxide containing metals and carrier supporting            particles of metal oxide which forms perovskite-type or            spinel-type crystal structure.
AU TSUJI E
   FUJITA Y
   MATSUMOTO N
   SUGANUMA M
   KATADA N
AE UNIV TOTTORI (TTOR-C)
GA 202118005S
AB    NOVELTY - A metal oxide-containing composite comprises                particles of a metal oxide containing two or more                metals classified into elements of groups 6-12 in                the periodic table and a carrier supporting the                particles of the metal oxide. The metal oxide forms                the crystal structure of a spinel-type or a                perovskite-type, and has a average particle size of                5 nm or less.
   USE - Metal oxide-containing composite used as                catalyst for reducing carbon dioxide (all claimed)                on fossil resources such as petroleum and                coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite provides catalyst which                effectively photoreduces carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of the composite, which                involves (i) mixing a solution (S1) comprising an                emulsion containing metal ions, surfactant, water,                and a hydrophobic organic solvent and containing                water-in-oil micelles, and a solution (S2)                comprising an emulsion containing hydroxide ions, a                surfactant, water, and a hydrophobic organic                solvent and containing water-in-oil micelle, (ii)                adding a carrier to the mixed solution obtained in                the step (i), (iii) adding a water-soluble organic                solvent to the mixed solution obtained in the step                (ii), (iv) taking out an insoluble material                containing a carrier from the mixed solution                obtained in the step (iii), and (v) calcining the                insoluble material obtained in the step (iv);
   (2) a catalyst, which comprises the composite;                and
   (3) reduction of carbon dioxide, which                involves photoreducing the carbon dioxide in the                presence of the metal oxide-containing composite,                light absorber and sacrificial agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide forms                  preferably spinel-type crystal structure. The                  metal oxide comprises two or more chosen from                  copper, cobalt, manganese, iron, nickel and zinc,                  preferably copper cobaltite, manganese cobaltite                  or iron cobaltite. The carrier comprises titanium                  oxide, tin oxide, aluminum oxide, lead oxide, or                  zinc oxide particles. Preferred Composition: The                  composite comprises 0.001-30 wt.% metal oxide.                  The carbon dioxide reduction method is carried                  out using 0.001-100 g with respect to 1 L of the                  solvent. Preferred Process: In the step (ii), the                  carrier dispersion containing the carrier and the                  hydrophobic organic solvent is added to the mixed                  solution obtained in the step (i). The                  water-in-oil micelle in the step (iii) is cleaved                  by adding a water-soluble organic solvent. The                  carbon dioxide is photoreduced in the liquid                  phase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The light absorber is                  ruthenium bipyridine. The sacrificial agent is                  triethanolamine.

PN US10483558-B1
TI System for generating electrical power by combustion of fuel including carbonaceous materials, has scrubber which receives flue gas of furnace after gas passed over blades of wind turbine and scrubber isolates carbon dioxide from flue gas.
AU PAREHK V
   PAREHK A
AE PAREHK V (PARE-Individual)
   PAREHK A (PARE-Individual)
GA 2019970394
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a furnace (10) that produces heat and flue gas from the combustion of fuel. A wind turbine (22) has blades and a shaft is connected to blades. The blades are located in the flow path of flue gas that is produced from furnace. The flue gas has sufficient velocity to cause the blades of turbine to rotate shaft at an angular velocity that is greater than a predetermined minimum angular velocity. A generator (24) is coupled to shaft of wind turbine and produces electrical power in response to the angular movement of shaft. A scrubber (26) receives the flue gas of furnace after gas has passed over the blades of wind turbine. The scrubber isolates carbon dioxide (CO2) from flue gas. A vessel (30) for receiving the CO2 is isolated by scrubber. The vessel reacts CO2 with water under conditions to create carbonic acid. The carbonic acid is added to a galvanic cell to generate electrical power from galvanic cell.
   USE - System for generating electrical power by combustion of fuel including carbonaceous materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reliably and inexpensively capture carbon dioxide and use it to augment the net electrical power produced at a generating station that is supplied by coal or other carbonaceous fuels. The system also captures and decomposes carbon dioxide released from such combustion processes to reduce carbon dioxide emissions from those sources.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for secondary power generation.
   Furnace (10)
   Wind turbine (22)
   Generator (24)
   Scrubber (26)
   Vessel (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The voltaic cell has an anode side that produces aluminum hydroxide. The voltaic cell has a cathode side that produces sodium carbonate. The aluminum anode electrode is wetted with aluminum sulfate. The platinum cathode electrode is wetted with carbonic acid solution.

TI Preparing carbamate ester, comprising e.g. adding            dimethyl sulfoxide ylide compound, amine compound and            solvent into pressure-resistant reactor, then adding            catalyst, alkali, ligand and additive, introducing            carbon dioxide and separating.
AU JIANG H
   ZHANG H
   XIONG W
   QI C
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2019393825
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbamate ester i.e. 2-oxoethyl                carbamate compound (III) using dimethyl sulfoxide                ylide compound (I), amine compound (II) and carbon                dioxide, comprising adding dimethyl sulfoxide ylide                compound (I), amine compound (II) and solvent into                pressure-resistant reactor, then adding catalyst,                alkali, ligand and additives, introducing carbon                dioxide and stirring the reaction at 50-120 degrees                C for 2-24 hours, separating and purifying the                reaction product to obtain final product, where the                (II) is cycloheximide or morpholine.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbamate                ester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes easily available raw                materials, and is simple and safe to operate and is                suitable for industrial production; has excellent                adaptability to functional groups.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbamate ester i.e. 2-oxoethyl                carbamate compound of formula (III) using dimethyl                sulfoxide ylide compound of formula (I), amine                compound of formula (II) and carbon dioxide,                comprising adding dimethyl sulfoxide ylide compound                of formula (I), amine compound of formula (II) and                solvent into pressure-resistant reactor, then                adding catalyst, alkali, ligand and additives,                introducing carbon dioxide and stirring the                reaction at 50-120 degrees C for 2-24 hours,                separating and purifying the reaction product to                obtain final product, where the (II) is                cycloheximide or morpholine.
   R1 = phenyl, P-tolyl, p-methoxyphenyl ,                p-bromophenyl, p-chlorophenyl, p-fluorophenyl                group, p-trifluoromethylphenyl,                p-trifluoromethoxyphenyl, p-tert-butylphenyl,                m-methylphenyl, m-bromophenyl, m-chlorophenyl,                3,4-dimethoxyphenyl, 2-naphthyl, 2-thienyl, styryl,                cyclopentyl or cyclohexane; and
   R2 and R3 = ethyl, propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl,                benzyl or allyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio                  between (II) and (I) is 1-7:1. The solvent is                  acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, toluene, tert-butanol,                  dichloromethane and/or dichloroethane. The                  catalyst is chloro(1,5-cyclooctadiene)iridium(I)                  dimer or (1,5-cyclooctadiene)(methoxy)iridium(I)                  dimer, and the molar ratio between added amount                  of catalyst and (I) is 0.05-0.2:1. The alkali is                  1,4-Diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane,                  tetramethylguanidine, and/or                  1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene; and and the                  molar ratio between added amount of alkali and                  (I) is 1-4:1. The ligand is triphenylphosphine,                  1,10-phenanthroline,                  2,9-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline,                  2,9-dimethyl-4,7-diphenyl-1,10-phenanthroline,                  and/or 4,7-diphenyl-1,10-phenanthroline, and the                  molar ratio between ligand and (I) is 0.1-0.5:1.                  The additive is silver acetate, silver phosphate,                  silver bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide, silver                  hexafluoroantimonate, silver                  trifluoromethanesulfonate, silver carbonate,                  silver nitrate, and/oe silver oxide; and the                  molar ratio between additive and (I) is 0.1-2:1.                  Preferred Method: The pressure of introducing                  carbon dioxide is 1-6 MPa. The separating and                  purifying the reaction product comprises cooling                  to room temperature after the reaction is                  completed, releasing carbon dioxide to normal                  pressure, washing the reaction liquid with water                  and extracting with ethyl acetate, and drying the                  organic phase over anhydrous sodium sulfate and                  filtering, and distilling off solvent under                  reduced pressure to give crude product, and                  purifying crude product by column chromatography                  to obtain (III). The column chromatography                  purification is carried out by column                  chromatography using a mixed solvent of petroleum                  ether and ethyl acetate as an eluent, and the                  volume ratio between petroleum ether and ethyl                  acetate is 5-20:1.

TI Reactor useful for biological methanation of e.g. dihydrogen, comprises enclosure having low longitudinal end and high opposite longitudinal end, and material support not consumable by methanation reaction to form bed of methanogenic flora.
AU FORTIN S
   KARA Y
   CAPELA S
   MAHEUT M
AE ENGIE (GAZD-C)
   ENGIE (GAZD-C)
GA 201917972D
AB    NOVELTY - Reactor (100) comprises: an enclosure (105) having a so-called low longitudinal end (107) and a so-called high opposite longitudinal end (106), where the enclosure comprises, (i) close to the low end, a primary water inlet (110), an inlet of dihydrogen or of a gas rich in hydrogen, and an inlet of carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-rich gas, and (ii) near the high end, an outlet for synthetic methane or for a gas rich in synthetic methane, and at least one outlet for water; and a material support not consumable by the methanation reaction to form a bed of methanogenic flora, having a density greater than the density of water, configured to receive a methanogenic flora.
   USE - The reactor is useful for the biological methanation of dihydrogen or of a gas rich in hydrogen and of carbon dioxide or of a gas rich in carbon dioxide with a fluidized bed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor: increases the reaction capacity per unit volume, while reducing the footprint of the reactor; provides residuals that are very low in reagents in the product gas; produces efficient transfer of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the aqueous phase provided by the mixture inherent in the fluidization hydrodynamics; conserves the pressure loss; and ensures controlled support and elimination of bacterial sludge by the phenomenon of surface attrition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the reactor.
   Reactor (100)
   Enclosure (105)
   High opposite longitudinal end (106)
   Low longitudinal end (107)
   Primary water inlet (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reactor comprises: means for measuring the water level in the chamber and a discharge of water from the reactor, where the opening of the evacuation is controlled according to the measured water level, and the recirculated water is injected into a flow comprising at least dihydrogen and/or carbon dioxide, upstream of the inlet for dihydrogen and/or the entry for carbon dioxide in the enclosure; upstream of the entry confounded with water, dihydrogen or gas rich in hydrogen and carbon dioxide or gas rich in carbon dioxide, and means for dissolving carbon dioxide and dihydrogen in water; and means for dissolving carbon dioxide in water on the one hand and means for dissolving dihydrogen in water on the other hand, each dissolution means is supplied with water by recirculated water, where the flow of water recirculation to each dissolution means is controlled independently. The enclosure comprises an inlet for additional nutrients and/or reagents and/or water, where the additional nutrients and/or reagents are injected into the injection line in recirculated water. The inlet for dihydrogen or for a gas rich in hydrogen, where the entry for carbon dioxide or for gas rich in dioxide of carbon and the primary water inlet are merged.
