TI Hybrid pipe for carbon dioxide supplying system that is utilized in greenhouse, has inner pipe for emitting fluid of second fluid path to first fluid path, and opening/closing valve for opening and closing spout section.
AU CHANG C K
   HOON I Y
   WON Y S
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2019111262
AB    NOVELTY - The pipe (100) has an outer pipe (110) formed with a first fluid path. An inner pipe (120) is arranged in an inner side of the outer pipe and formed with a second fluid path. The outer pipe gushes out fluid to an outer side, and is connected with a first spout section. The inner pipe emits fluid of the second fluid path to the first fluid path, and is formed with a second spout section. An opening/closing valve opens and closes the second spout section. A supporting part is arranged in the first fluid path. The outer pipe is provided with a transparent dye sensitive battery unit that is arranged in an exterior predetermined portion.
   USE - Hybrid pipe for a carbon dioxide supplying system (claimed) that is utilized in a greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The opening/closing valve opens and closes the second spout section so as to simplify fertilization and cooling of the carbon dioxide, reduce installation cost of the greenhouse and improve use convenience and workability of the pipe. The pipe is provided with a transparent dye sensitive battery unit so as to avoid efficiency drop due to deterioration of a dye sensitive solar battery, so that solar energy intensity can be increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide supplying system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a hybrid pipe.
   Vaporizing apparatus (34)
   Atmospheric carbon dioxide supply line (35)
   Hybrid pipe (100)
   Outer pipe (110)
   Inner pipe (120)

A                blower section (120) comprises a blower (121). The                blower section is configured to receive mixed air                from the mixing chamber, where the mixed air from                the mixing chamber is received through a mixing                damper (118). A relief damper (112) is connected to                the mixing chamber and the blower section through a                relief ducting (114). The relief damper allows                release of the fresh air, the exhaust, and the                mixed air to an ambient environment.

   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces temperature to minimize                potential condensation so as to ensure desired                temperature for delivery into an agriculture                facility.

The proportion of coarse                synthetic gas is formed by the coarse synthetic gas                after the synthetic gas purification device removes                the impurities in the rough synthetic gas, where                the coarse synthetic gas leads to the external                user.

PN CN114441707-A
TI Fast analysis of trisodium phosphate involves            dissolving weighed sample in carbon dioxide-free water,            adding acid, removing carbon dioxide, adding methyl            orange indicator into solution, titrating with base            until solution turns yellow, measuring values, adding            indicator, titrating with base.
AU DONG S
   YANG P
   ZHAO E
   WANG C
   DONG Z
   LI H
   WANG T
   HE S
AE SHANXI CHANGQING ENERGY CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202266824L
AB    NOVELTY - Rapid analysis method involves: 1) dissolving                weighed sample in carbon dioxide-free water; 2)                adding 50ml hydrochloric acid standard titration                solution into the formed solution, mixing, slowly                boiling in electric furnace, removing carbon                dioxide and cooling; 3) adding a methyl orange                indicator solution into the cooled solution to turn                red, titrating by using a sodium hydroxide standard                titration solution until the solution turns yellow,                recording volume of consumed sodium hydroxide                standard titration solution as V and calculating                the volume of consumed hydrochloric acid standard                titration solution as V1; 4) adding phenolphthalein                indicator, preventing carbon dioxide from getting                absorbed in the solution, continuously titrating                with sodium hydroxide standard titration solution                until solution turns red, followed by recording                volume of the consumed sodium hydroxide standard                titration solution as V2; 5) calculating the                content of the industrial trisodium phosphate in                the sample.
   USE - The rapid analysis method is used for                determination of the content of trisodium phosphate                used as scale inhibitor in coal chemical industry,                and also used for detecting the content of                trisodium phosphate in raw materials at purchasing                end before starting the process (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The analysis and detection method is a quick,                simple and easy process which enables to reduce the                content determination time of industrial trisodium                phosphate from 6 hours to 20 minutes. The method                enables to provide real and accurate numerical                value, and enables to measure the main content of                the industrial trisodium phosphate, in the steam                drum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step 3 of the rapid                  analysis method, after adding methyl orange to                  the cooled solution, the excess hydrochloric acid                  is left to make the solution acidic so that the                  indicator turns acid red. The methyl orange color                  change range is 3.1-3.8-4.4.The phenolphthalein indicator is added in                  step 4, and titration with sodium hydroxide                  standard titration solution is continued until                  the solution becomes red to the titration end                  point, at which the solution becomes alkaline and                  becomes red, and the phenolphthalein                  discoloration range is 8.0-8.6-9.6.Preferred Method: The rapid analysis method                  further involves: 1) taking 5g sample, accurately                  measuring to 0.0002g, placing in a conical flask                  and adding 50ml carbon dioxide-free water for                  dissolving; 2) adding hydrochloric acid standard                  titration solution into the formed solution using                  a pipette; 3) adding 2-3 drops of methyl orange                  indicator solution into the cooled solution                  during titration; 4) adding 2-3 drops of                  phenolphthalein indicator.

PT P
PN CN112479223-A
TI Low-silicon nano-sheet silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve useful in producing low-carbon olefins by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
AU KANG J
   YU G
   LIU Z
   ZHANG Q
   WANG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202130154M
AB    NOVELTY - Low-silicon nano-sheet silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve is claimed. The low-silicon nano-sheet silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve has both low silicon content and small-size nano-sheet morphology. The molecular sieve contains 0.002-0.02 silicon (silicon/(silicon+aluminum+phosphorus)). The molecular sieve has average particle size of 50-200 nm, and thickness of 10-50 nm.
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful in producing low-carbon olefins by carbon dioxide hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve: exhibits excellent catalytic performance in the reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to low-carbon olefins; and 2-4C light olefins selectivity reaches 77.4%, carbon dioxide conversion rate reaches 25%, has low carbon monoxide selectivity i.e. 35%, and significantly improved stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing low-silicon nano-sheet silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve, comprising (i) mixing and stirring silicon source, aluminum source, phosphorus source, template agent, hydrogen fluoride and deionized water at a certain temperature to obtain initial gel, and continuously stirring and aging to obtain molecular sieve precursor having with low silicon content, (ii) continuously stirring the obtained molecular sieve precursor, and evaporating the water at elevated temperature to obtain dry glue, (iii) adding certain amount of deionized water into the obtained dry glue, stirring and dissolving it thoroughly, placing it in a hydrothermal kettle, and fixing the hydrothermal kettle in a homogeneous reactor for low-temperature crystallization, and (iv) performing high-temperature crystallization, rapidly cooling the hydrothermal kettle, washing, centrifuging, drying and calcining to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of the molecular sieve for producing low-carbon olefins by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the certain temperature is 20-30 degrees C, and the time of stirring and aging is 12-48 hours. In step (ii), the heating temperature is 70-110 degrees C. In step (iii), the speed of the homogeneous reactor is 5-30 revolutions/minute, the crystallization temperature is 70-120 degrees C, and the crystallization time is 12-24 hours. In step (iv), the high-temperature crystallization is performed at 160-200 degrees C for 24-48 hours; the drying process is performed at 70-150 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours, and the calcination is performed at 300-650 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours. The use method comprises taking the formed silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve and metal oxides, mixing it with particles to obtain composite catalyst, and loading it into a fixed-bed high-pressure micro-reactor for reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to low-carbon olefins, where the reaction pressure is 1-5 MPa, the space velocity of the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1000-10000/hour, the reaction temperature is 300-500 degrees C, and the reaction time is more than 10 hours. Preferred Components: In step (i), the silicon source is silica sol, tetraethyl orthosilicate, sodium silicate, fuming silica and/or active silica; the aluminum source is aluminum oxide, pseudo-boehmite, aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum hydroxide, and aluminum alkoxide; and the molar ratio between silicon source and aluminum source is 0.006-0.2:1. In step (i), the template agent is tetraethylammonium bromide, dimethyloctadecyl(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)ammonium chloride, dimethylhexadecyl(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)ammonium chloride, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, diethylamine, triethylamine, morpholine and/or tetraethylammonium hydroxide; and the phosphorus source is phosphoric acid, phosphate, phosphite and/or tributyl phosphate. In step (iii), the mass ratio between deionized water and dry glue is 2-10:1.

TI Visible light catalyst useful for catalytic conversion reaction of carbon dioxide to synthesize benzoazepine heterocycles comprises organic ligand and metal oxide.
AU ZHENG H
   YANG S
AE UNIV HANGZHOU NORMAL (UYNL-C)
GA 2020A96098
AB    NOVELTY - Visible light catalyst comprises organic ligand and metal oxide. The organic ligand is 3-(N,N-dimethylalkylammonium)propane sulfonate or 3-(N,N-dimethylalkylammonium)propane carbonate. The alkyl group is 1-12C alkyl. The metal oxide is iron(II,III) oxide nano-particles.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic conversion reaction of carbon dioxide to synthesize benzoazepine heterocycles (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The use method: utilizes the visible light catalyst under mild reaction conditions under visible light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the visible light catalyst, comprising (i) adding iron salt solution and sodium lauryl sulfate into toluene, heating and stirring under the protection of nitrogen to obtain iron salt emulsion, (ii) adding 0.3-0.4 g/ml sodium hydroxide solution dropwise into the iron salt emulsion, stirring and reacting for 40-80 minutes, adding absolute ethanol dropwise, continue stirring for 5-10 minutes to obtain suspension of iron(II,III) oxide nano-particles, adding organic ligand, reacting for 0.5-1.5 hours, cooling, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of the visible light catalyst for catalytic conversion reaction of carbon dioxide to synthesize benzoazepine heterocycles. The visible light catalyst can convert carbon dioxide to the benzo nitrogen-containing heterocyclic intermediate body under visible light. The conversion method comprises dissolving the raw materials in a mixed solution of tetrahydrofuran and dimethylformamide, adding visible light catalyst, introducing in carbon dioxide, reacting under sunlight irradiation, and separating to obtain benzonitrogen-containing heterocyclic intermediate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between iron(II,III) oxide nano-particle and organic ligand is 1:0.5-2. The mass-volume ratio between sodium dodecyl sulfate, iron salt solution and toluene is 0.3-1.2 g:10 ml:90-150 ml. The iron salt solution is mixed aqueous solution of iron(II) sulfate heptahydrate and iron(III) chloride hexahydrate, the concentration of iron(II) sulfate in the mixed aqueous solution is 0.01-0.0500 mol/l, and the concentration of iron(III) chloride in the mixed aqueous solution is 0.02-0.1 mol/l. In step (ii), the volume ratio between iron salt solution, sodium hydroxide solution and absolute ethanol is 1:0.5-1.5:0.5-1.5. In use method, the mass molar ratio between visible light catalyst and raw material is 0.1-1 g/mol; the concentration of the raw material in the mixed solution is 0.01-0.3 mol/l; and the volume ratio of the tetrahydrofuran and dimethylformamide is 1:0.5-1.5. Preferred Method: In step (i), the heating and stirring process is performed at 75-85 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. In step (ii), the reaction temperature is 75-85 degrees C. In the use method, the reaction is performed at 30-80 degrees C for 3-5 hours.

PT P
PN CN111349484-A; CN111349484-B
TI Preparing low-sulfur diesel fuel modifier, by adding unsaturated diamine into high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, stirring, adding tung oil biodiesel, reacting, recovering carbon dioxide, and distilling under vacuum.
AU LI L
   LI X
   CAO C
   WANG Y
   CHENG J
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202063390W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a low-sulfur diesel fuel modifier, involves (a) adding unsaturated diamine into a high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor, and making the carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, turning on the stirring, gradually adding a tung oil biodiesel, and continuously reacting for a while after the addition, and (b) recovering carbon dioxide, and distilling under vacuum to obtain low-sulfur diesel fuel modifier.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a low-sulfur diesel fuel modifier which is used for improving the lubricity of low-sulfur diesel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses unsaturated diamine to modify tung oil biodiesel under supercritical conditions. The prepared modifier has high lubricity, low freezing point, low dosage and the efficacy of detergent. The low-sulfur diesel meets the national V lubricity standard and freezing point requirements after blending. The modifier has function of diesel detergent, and synergistic effect, and reduces fuel consumption and smoke per hundred kilometers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a low-sulfur diesel fuel modifier prepared by the above method; and
   (2) use of the low-sulfur diesel fuel modifier for improving the lubricity of low-sulfur diesel and has the function of diesel detergent, when the dosage is 400-600 ppm, the corrected wear scar diameter of low-sulfur diesel is not greater than 460 mu m, where the fuel consumption and smoke per hundred kilometers are reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of unsaturated diamine to tung oil biodiesel is controlled to be (0.5:1)-(3:1), preferably (0.8:1)-(2:1). In step (a), introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor and making the carbon dioxide in a supercritical state, and the reaction temperature is controlled to be 32-60 degrees C and the pressure is 7.5-13.5 MPa. The method includes gradually adding the tung oil biodiesel to the unsaturated diamine, and dripping the tung oil biodiesel within 1-3 hours. In gradually addition of tung oil biodiesel, the feeding speed is fast and slow, so that the time for adding tung oil biodiesel to 1/2 does not exceed 1/3 of the total feeding time. The pressure of the vacuum distillation is 30-150 Pa, preferably 65-120 Pa, and the temperature is 180-220 degrees C, preferably 195-205 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The low-sulfur diesel refers to diesel with a sulfur content of less than 10 ppm and a wear scar diameter of more than 580 mu m. Preferred Components: The unsaturated diamine has a carbon number of not less than 4, preferably an unsaturated aliphatic diamine with a carbon number of 4-6. The unsaturated diamine is at least one of diaminobutene, diaminopentene, and diaminohexene. The unsaturated diamine is at least one of 1,4-diamino-2-butene, 1,4-diamino-1-butene, 1,5-diamino-1-pentene, 1,5-diamino-2-pentene, 1,6-diamino-1-hexene, 1,6-diamino-2-hexene, 1,6-diamino-3-hexene, 1, 3-diamino-2-methyl-1-propene, 1,4-diamino-2-methyl-2-butene and 1,5-diamino-4-methyl-2-pentene. The tung oil biodiesel is a fatty acid lower alcohol ester produced by the transesterification reaction of tung oil and lower alcohol, preferably at least one of tung oil methyl ester, tung oil ethyl ester, tung oil propyl ester, and tung oil butyl ester.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN WO2020058852-A1; EP3853289-A1; US2021309801-A1; US2022033556-A1; CN114269806-A; IN202137015092-A; EP4059981-A1; IN202134014733-A; CN115109241-A
TI Formation of degradable polymer used for forming            modified biomolecule, involves contacting ethylene            oxide monomer with carbon dioxide or cyclic ester using            alkyl borane to form poly(ethylene oxide) containing            degradable carbonate linkages.
AU FENG X
   VARGHESE J
   GNANOU Y
   HADJICHRISTIDIS N
   JIA M
   AUGUSTINE D
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 2020237221
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of degradable polymer (105) involves                contacting (101) an ethylene oxide monomer (102)                with carbon dioxide or cyclic ester (103) in the                presence of an alkyl borane and an initiator (104)                to form poly(ethylene oxide) comprising degradable                carbonate linkages or degradable ester linkages                incorporated into a polymer backbone.
   USE - Formation of degradable polymer used for                forming modified biomolecule (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables formation of degradable                polymer having improved chemical stability,                hydrophilicity and biocompatibility, without                modifying intrinsic property of polymer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) modified biomolecule, which comprises a                bioactive molecule conjugated with the degradable                polyether;
   (2) degradable polyether, which comprises 10%                or less degradable ester linkages and/or degradable                carbonate linkages incorporated into poly(ethylene                oxide) backbone;
   (3) formation of degradable polyether star,                which involves contacting a diepoxide monomer with                carbon dioxide or cyclic ester in the presence of                initiator and alkyl borane in an amount (a1) to                form a multifunctional core comprising degradable                carbonate linkages or degradable ester linkages,                and contacting the multifunctional core with                ethylene oxide monomer in the presence of alkyl                borane in an amount (a2) to form arms of a                polyether attached to the multifunctional core;                and
   (4) degradable polyether star, which comprises                the arms of a polyether attached to a                multifunctional polycarbonate core.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                formation of degradable polymer.
   Contact process of ethylene oxide monomer                with carbon dioxide or cyclic ester (101)
   Ethylene oxide monomer (102)
   Carbon dioxide or cyclic ester (103)
   Alkyl borane and initiator (104)
   Degradable polymer (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The bioactive                  molecule is protein, peptide, enzyme, medicinal                  chemical and/or organic moiety.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethylene oxide                  monomer is chosen from 18 monomers e.g. monomers                  of formulae (I-1)-(I-5). The cyclic ester is                  lactide, trimethylene carbonate, glycolide, beta                  -butyrolactone, delta -valerolactone, gamma                  -butyrolactone, gamma -valerolactone,                  4-methyldihydro-2(3H)-furanone, alpha -methyl-                  gamma -butyrolactone, epsilon -caprolactone,                  1,3-dioxolan-2-one, propylene carbonate,                  4-methyl-1,3-dioxan-2-one, 1,3-doxepan-2-one, or                  5-1-4C alkoxy-1,3-dioxan-2-one. The alkyl borane                  is triethylborane, triphenylborane,                  tributylborane, trimethylborane, and/or                  triisobutylborane. The initiator is etherate                  compound of formula: Y'+RO-, carboxylate compound                  of formula: Y'+RCOO-, azide compound of formula:                  X+N3-, and/or chloride compound of formula:                  X+Cl-. Each epoxide ring of the diepoxide monomer                  ring opens and copolymerizes with the carbon                  dioxide or cyclic ester. At least one epoxide                  ring of the diepoxide monomer copolymerizes with                  carbon dioxide to form degradable carbonate                  linkages. At least one epoxide ring of the                  diepoxide monomer copolymerizes with the cyclic                  ester to form degradable ester linkages. The                  diepoxide monomer is vinyl cyclohexene dioxide,                  butadiene dioxide, poly(ethylene glycol)                  diglycidyl ether, 1,2,3,4-diepoxybutane,                  1,2,7,8-diepoxyoctane,                  1,2,5,6-diepoxycyclooctane, dicyclopentadiene                  diepoxide, or diglycidyl ether of                  1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,6-hexandiol,                  cyclohexane-1,4-diol, cyclohexane-1,1-dimethanol,                  cyclohexane-1,2-dimethanol,                  cyclohexane-1,3-dimethanol,                  cyclohexane-1,4-dimethanol, diethylene glycol,                  hydroquinone, resorcinol,                  4,4-isopropylidenebisphenol, or naphthalene diol.                  The molar ratio of the diepoxide monomer to                  initiator is 10 or less.Y+ = K+, t-BuP4+, or t-BuP6+;X+ = NBu4+, PBu4+, NOct4+, or PPN+;RO- = group of formula (i) or (ii),                  CH3O(CH2)2O(CH2)2O-, or H2C=CHCH2O-;andRCOO- = aliphatic or aromatic                  carboxylate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The degradable polyether                  and degradable polymer star are formed under                  metal-free conditions. Preferred Composition: The                  degradable ester linkage and degradable carbonate                  linkage comprise 10 or more adjacent ester units                  or carbonate units. The degradable polyether star                  comprises a multifunctional core comprising 80%                  or more degradable carbonate linkages or ester                  linkages. Preferred Property: The number average                  molecular weight of the polyether arms is 4                  kg/mol or more.

PN CN110630535-A; CN211039087-U
TI Direct-drive two-stage supercritical carbon dioxide compressor has first stage compressor whose inlet is being used as compression system air inlet, and two-stage compressor whose air outlet is being used as compression system air outlet.
AU WANG Y
   ZHOU D
   WEN X
   LI Y
   ZHANG H
   SUN D
   HOU C
   LI X
   ZHANG Y
   GONG Y
   YIN X
   LIU H
   DENG D
AE CHONGQING JIANGZENG SHIPBUILDING HEAVY (CSHI-C)
GA 202002512X
AB    NOVELTY - The direct-drive two-stage supercritical carbon dioxide compressor has a high-speed motor which includes a motor casing and a stator, a rotor, and an air bearing system provided in the motor casing. A first-stage compressor and a second-stage compressor are provided at both ends of the high-speed motor, and the impellers of the first-stage compressor and the second-stage compressor are both mounted on a rotor of the high-speed motor. The first stage compressor and the second stage compressor are connected in series to form a compression system, and the air inlet of the first stage compressor is used as the air inlet of the compression system. The air outlet of the two-stage compressor is used as the air outlet of the compression system.
   USE - Direct-drive two-stage supercritical carbon dioxide compressor.
   ADVANTAGE - The motor, the compressor impeller and the sealing structure are coaxially connected, eliminates the traditional way of connecting the gear box and the motor through a coupling, and has the advantages of compact and simple structure, and high power density. The gas is introduced into the airflow of the compressor's inlet to achieve zero leakage. The compressor has no oil or air in the entire experimental prototype, and is suitable for the compression of micro-sized supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the direct-drive two-stage supercritical carbon dioxide compressor.
   First-stage compressor (11a)
   One-stage compressed impeller (12a)
   Bearing (31)
   Stator (32)
   Pure thrust bearing (33)

   ADVANTAGE - The concrete surface sealing system is environmentally friendly, and efficiently seals concrete using sealant having excellent stain resistance, and UV-protective property, and has reduced toxicity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for sealing of concrete surface using the sealing system, which involves applying, using the carbon dioxide as propellant, the sealant composition (S1) to cover an area of the concrete surface, and applying, using the carbon dioxide as propellant, the sealant composition (S2) to cover the area of the concrete surface covered by the sealant composition (S1).

AE JEONG Y H (JEON-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - The autonomous inflatable life jacket (1) has a waterproof case (200) accommodating a portable terminal. An expansion cartridge (300) is coupled to an upper portion of the waterproof case, and configured to expand a carbon dioxide cylinder (310) by injecting a manual lever connected to a neck (110) and expand a built-in life tube (320), and restricts movement of the neck by a neck locking unit (360). The manual lever moves to a piston, when the neck is pulled, and the carbon dioxide cylinder is triggered, or the carbon dioxide cylinder is triggered when a manual drilling button (340) directly coupled to the piston is pressed. Multiple pulleys are configured for smoothing the movement path of the neckline.
   USE - Autonomous inflatable life jacket for preventing drowning accidents when watering in aquatic environments such as valleys and seas.
   ADVANTAGE - A reliable operation is ensured by having a neck lock unit that prevents malfunction. The life-saving tube is inflated by using the manual cylinder drilling button as a double safety device, and by using the cylinder manual drilling button, thus ensuring double safety.

TI Ventilating air conditioning apparatus for            removing contaminants such as carbon dioxide, has            honeycomb rotor that moves from re-generation or            desorption zone to processing zone by rotation of            honeycomb rotor.
AU OKANO H
AE SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
   SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
   NISHIBU GIKEN KK (NISH-Non-standard)
   SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
GA 201952885T
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a heat exchanger that is                provided at an entrance of re-generation or                desorption zone. The air to be processed is passed                through processing zone to remove carbon dioxide                and contaminants and produce supply air. The water                is directly sprayed on or dropped into heat                exchanger provided at the entrance of re-generation                or desorption zone, to form a water film on the                heat exchanger to evaporate the water film with                heat from the heat exchanger, and to generate                saturated steam. The saturated steam is introduced                to the re-generation or desorption zone to desorb                the carbon dioxide and contaminants and produce a                discharge stream that is vented outdoors. The                honeycomb rotor (1) is in a wet state due to                moisture absorption and water condensation, when                honeycomb rotor moves from the re-generation or                desorption zone to the processing zone by rotation                of the honeycomb rotor.
   USE - Ventilating air conditioning apparatus for                removing contaminants such as carbon dioxide,                volatile organic compound (VOC), and odor.
   ADVANTAGE - The stability of the ion exchange resin is                increased in the hydrated state, and oxidative                deterioration is prevented and durability is                improved. The desorption re-generation is performed                by saturated steam generated by using the exhaust                heat, energy saving performance is improved and                contaminants such as carbon dioxide and VOC in the                room are reduced by the small amount of                ventilation, and discharged and energy loss caused                by ventilation is prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                proposed ventilation air conditioning                apparatus.
   Honeycomb rotor (1)

PN US2019160418-A1; JP2019098874-A; CN109910565-A; JP6709773-B2; US11110386-B2; CN109910565-B
TI Air purification device for vehicle, has            controller that controls three-way valve, blower and            heater to execute purification mode in which air in            vehicle cabin is sent into sorption device at            purification temperature.
AU MATSUMOTO S
   KAKIZAKI S
   MATSUMOTO T
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 201946782G
AB    NOVELTY - The air purification device (10) has a                sorption device (12) that is provided with a                sorbent that sorbs the moisture and carbon dioxide                in air at a purification temperature and releases                the sorbed moisture and carbon dioxide at a first                regeneration temperature higher than the                purification temperature. A controller is                configured to control a three-way valve, a blower                and a heater to selectively execute a purification                mode in which air in the vehicle cabin is sent into                the sorption device at the purification                temperature, and purified air from which the                moisture and the carbon dioxide are removed by the                sorbent is blown into the vehicle cabin, and a                first regeneration mode in which air heated to the                first regeneration temperature is sent into the                sorption device and exhaust air containing moisture                and carbon dioxide released from the sorbent is                discharged to the outside of the vehicle                cabin.
   USE - Air purification device for vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purification device suppresses an                increase in the carbon dioxide concentration                continuously during travel of the vehicle and                suppresses fogging of the window glass of the                vehicle. By starting the purification mode subject                to a condition that the air conditioner executes                the inside air recirculation mode, saturation of                the adsorbent is suppressed, and the energy                consumption for executing the regeneration mode is                reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air                purification device.
   Air purification device (10)
   Sorption device (12)
   Air intake duct (14)
   Common air intake duct (18)
   Temperature sensor (27)

PT P
PN CN109251944-A; CN109251944-B
TI Preparing sugar ester catalyzed by immobilized enzyme under a supercritical carbon dioxide process involves dissolving bromopropionic acid in acetonitrile, adding an imidazole compound, stirring the reaction for the first time.
AU ZOU B
   ZHANG L
   XIA J
   CHU Y
   HUO S
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201910423A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sugar ester catalyzed by immobilized enzyme under a supercritical carbon dioxide process involves dissolving bromopropionic acid in acetonitrile, adding an imidazole compound, stirring the reaction for the first time, and extracting the obtained reaction product with diethyl ether to obtain a lower oily substance, which is dried in vaccum to obtain modifier. The modifier is mixed with carbonyldiimidazole, added to anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide, and stirred magnetically for a second time, and reacted at room temperature to obtain an active body. The active body is added to the lipase solution, and the third magnetic stirring reaction is performed, and dialysis is performed to obtain an acylated lipase solution. Monomer acrylamide is added to the acylated lipase solution, stirred and dissolved, nitrogen is passed, ammonium persulfate and tetramethylethylenediamine is added, polymerization is conducted, dialysed, and lyophilized to obtain immobilized lipase.
   USE - Method for preparing sugar ester catalyzed by immobilized enzyme under a supercritical carbon dioxide process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sugar ester catalyzed by immobilized enzyme under a supercritical carbon dioxide has mild reaction, the operation is simple, the conversion rate is high, and the enzyme catalyst is reused, and the environmental pollution is small, and industrial production is expected.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sugar is mixed with the fatty acid to obtain a reactant, and the immobilized lipase is added, placed in a reaction vessel, a carbon dioxide medium is added, stirred, and filtered to obtain a sugar ester.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the bromopropionic acid to the imidazole compound is 10:1.1. The imidazole compound is vinylimidazole or propenyl imidazole. The temperature of the first magnetic stirring reaction is 60-100 degrees C, and the time of the first magnetic stirring reaction is 5-10 hour. The temperature of the third magnetic stirring reaction is 0-4 degrees C, and the time of the third magnetic stirring reaction is 8-24 hour. The molar ratio of the modifier to carbonyldiimidazole is 1:1, the reaction time of the room temperature is 2-8 hour, the volume ratio of the active body to the lipase solution is 90 mimiliter: 5 mimiliter, and the lipase solution has a concentration of 150-350 mimiliter. The ratio of the monomer acrylamide, the acylated lipase solution and the ammonium persulfate is 250 miligram: 5 mililiter: 15 miligram, and the concentration of the lipase in the acylated lipase solution is 40-100 miligram/mililiter. The ratio of the ammonium persulfate to the tetramethylethylenediamine is 15 miligram: 12 microgram. The stirring and dissolving temperature is 30-60 degrees C, the nitrogen passing time is 20-45 minutes. The polymerization temperature is 30-70 degrees C, and the polymerization reaction time is 12-24 hours. The amount of immobilized lipase is 0.5-12 wt.% of the total amount of the reactant, the temperature of the stirring reaction is 20-90 degrees C, the pressure of the stirring reaction is 5-40 megapascal, and the stirring reaction time is 2-30 hours. Preferred Components: The lipase in the lipase solution is Aspergillus oryzae lipase, Aspergillus niger lipase, Rhizomucor pneumoniae lipase, Pseudomonas cepacii lipase, Thermophilic lipase, Antarctic fake Saccharomyces cerevisiae lipase, Candida rugosa lipase or porcine pancreatic lipase. The sugar is a monosaccharide or a disaccharide, a mixture of one or more of seed oil, soybean oil, peanut oil, corn oil, castor oil, cottonseed oil, tung oil, ebony oil, fish oil and lard, and the sugar ester is a sucrose ester, a fructose ester or a glucose ester.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC D05-A01A; D05-A01B3; D05-C; E07-A02A; E07-A02D; E07-A02H; E11-H01

   USE - Electrode used as negative electrode for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (all                claimed), and used in renewable energy                storage.

The molar ratio of tin to the                organic additive is 1: 1-30: 1, preferably 4: 1-10:                1. The molar ratio of sodium fluoride to tin is 1:                20-40: 1, preferably 1: 5-20: 1.

TI Removal of alkali from red mud involves            precipitating to-be-treated red mud, introducing carbon            dioxide into supernatant, mixing bottom mud with            carbonated water, mixing carbonized red mud with            gypsum, and separating calcified red mud.
AU LI P
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202260165R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing red mud alkali comprises (i)                precipitating red mud to be treated to obtain                supernatant and sediment, (ii) introducing carbon                dioxide into the supernatant, reacting to obtain                carbonated water, (iii) mixing the bottom mud and                the carbonate water to obtain a carbonated red mud,                (iv) mixing carbonized red mud with gypsum,                reacting, obtaining a calcified red mud and (v)                separating the solid and the liquid of the                calcified red mud, where the obtained solid is                dealkaline red mud.
   USE - The method is useful for removing alkali from                red mud, where the red mud is industrial solid                waste produced in alumina industrial production                process, recycling valuable metal such as iron,                titanium and rare earth element scandium, and                gallium, producing building material such as glass,                cement, brick, roadbed material, and environment                repairing material, such as industrial catalyst,                waste water adsorbent and soil modifying                agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully uses carbon dioxide, gypsum                and other wastes, the red mud is combined to reduce                alkali, and the heavy metal in red mud, fluoride                and arsenic and other harmful substances for                complex curing, realizes the harmless and recycling                of red mud. The method has long-term stability,                laying a good foundation for later resource product                development. The device operation is simple,                facility intensive utilization, large-scale                processing red mud and easy to popularize and                realize industrialization and scale. The economic                benefit is obvious. The red mud alkali reducing and                product development raw material is mainly                industrial waste, waste for treating waste, raw                material that is easy to obtain, low product cost,                strong market competitiveness, comprehensive cost                can be controlled at 50 yuan/ton.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) an                alkali-removing red mud; (2) a gel material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the red mud                dealkalide process. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

TI Amine grafted agricultural waste based aerogel            with in-situ hydrophobicity for use in carbon dioxide            capture, comprises homogeneous sol-gel of oleic            acid-(3-aminopropyl) triethoxysilane, and cellulose            fibres-glyoxal-polyvinyl alcohol.
AU DORA T K D
   KUMAR G
   REDDY S D
AE DORA T K D (DORA-Individual)
   KUMAR G (KUMA-Individual)
   REDDY S D (REDD-Individual)
GA 202214608M
AB    NOVELTY - Amine grafted agricultural waste based aerogel                with in-situ hydrophobicity comprises a homogeneous                sol-gel of oleic acid-(3-aminopropyl)                triethoxysilane (OA-APTES) sol-gel, and a                functionalized cellulose fibers-glyoxal-polyvinyl                alcohol (FCF-GX-PVA) sol-gel, where the                functionalized cellulose fibers are derived from                agricultural waste, and the FCF-GX-PVA sol-gel has                GX crosslinked between the polyvinyl alcohol and                functionalized cellulose fibers.
   USE - Amine grafted agricultural waste based aerogel                with in-situ hydrophobicity for use in carbon                dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The Amine grafted agricultural waste based                aerogel has a carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of                0.4 mmol/g at a temperature of 278 K and has a                thermal conductivity in the range of 0.028-0.049                W/m K.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing of amine grafted agricultural waste                based aerogel with in-situ hydrophobicity, which                involves mixing (3-aminopropyl) triethoxysilane                (APTES) to oleic acid (OA) forming equi-mole                mixture of OA-APTES, dissolving OA-APTES mixture in                ethanol at 60 degrees C through continuous stirring                for 1 hour, mixing 4 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol (PVA)                in distilled water with functionalized cellulose                fibers by stirring for 30 minutes to obtain a                sol-gel, mixing 40 wt.% glyoxal (GX) to the sol-gel                obtained, mixing hydrochloric acid (HCl) to the                sol-gel obtained to maintain the pH at 3, mixing                obtained solutions by continuously stirring for a                time period of 1 hour to obtain a homogeneous                sol-gel, aging obtained sol-gel in an oven at 90                degrees C for 6 hours, freezing sol-gel at 20                degrees C and sublimating into amine grafted                agricultural waste based aerogel in a lyophilizer,                where the FCF is derived from the addition of GX                causes crosslinking between the polyvinyl alcohol                and the functionalized cellulose fibres.

TI New dimethylammonium-containing zinc-organic            framework compound for e.g. adsorption and separation            of acetylene, has three-dimensional framework            structure, and preset porosity, Langmuir specific            surface area and pore volume.
AU WANG Y
   HOU L
   SU R
   LI Y
   SHI W
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 2021D1789A
AB    NOVELTY - A dimethylammonium-containing zinc-organic                framework compound, is new. The framework compound                has three-dimensional framework structure,                one-dimensional square pores, porosity of 43.1%,                Langmuir specific surface area of 632.1 m2/g and                pore volume of 0.40 cm3/g.
   USE - New dimethylammonium cation-containing                zinc-organic framework compound for adsorption and                separation of acetylene from acetylene-methane                mixture, and acetylene-carbon dioxide mixtures (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The zinc-organic framework compound has                uniform pore hole diameter distribution and hole                surface containing fluorine atom and excellent                separation effect to acetylene, methane and carbon                dioxide. The preparation method is simple and easy,                and produces product with low device                requirement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A dimethylamonium-containing zinc-organic                framework compound of formula:                ((CH3)2NH2)0.5(Zn(BDC)0.5F1.5).DMF.H2O, is new. The                framework compound has three-dimensional framework                structure, one-dimensional square pores, porosity                of 43.1%, Langmuir specific surface area of 632.1                m2/g and pore volume of 0.40 cm3/g.
   BDC = deprotonated terephthalic acid; DMF =                N,N-dimethylformamide; and (CH3)2NH2 =                dimethylammonium cation.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the zinc-organic framework                compound. The preparation involves adding zinc                sulfate heptahydrate and terephthalic acid to the                mixed solvent of N,N-dimethylformamide, methanol                and hydrofluoric acid, adding to reactor, reacting                at 115-120degrees Celsius for 70-80 hours to obtain                intermediate product, cooling to room temperature                at rate of 4-6degrees Celsius/hour to obtain                colorless crystals as zinc-organic framework                compounds, and exchanging the zinc-organic                framework compounds in dichloromethane for 65-75                hours, and activating at 150degrees Celsius under                vacuum condition for 3-5 hours; and
   (2) use of the zinc-organic framework compound                in adsorption and separation of acetylene,                acetylene-methane mixture, and acetylene-carbon                dioxide mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The hole size of                  one-dimensional square pores is 8.1x8.1 Angstrom.                  The zinc-organic framework compound belongs to                  the tetragonal crystal system and has space group                  of I4/mmmm , and unit cell parameter alpha of                  90degrees, beta of 90degrees, gamma of 90degrees,                  a value of 18.7279(4) Angstrom, b value of                  18.7279(4) Angstrom, c value of 11.8716(6)                  Angstrom and unit cell volume of 4163.8(3)                  Angstrom3. The adsorption amount of zinc-organic                  framework compound at 298 K to acetylene is 79.5                  cm3/g, methane is 8.5 cm3/g, and carbon dioxide                  is 23.6 cm3/g.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  zinc sulfate heptahydrate to terephthalic acid is                  1:1. The volume ratio of N,N-dimethylformamide,                  methanol and hydrofluoric acid is 7:3:0.05. The                  amount of mixed solvent added is 300-320 times                  the mass of zinc sulfate heptahydrate.

AB    NOVELTY - The air purifier has a lower housing having an                open upper surface and accommodating a culture                medium in which microalgae grow. A light source unit                (111) is provided in the lower and upper housings                to provide light to the culture medium. A top fan                (113) is mounted on the upper surface of the upper                housing to generate an intake airflow for                introducing outside air into the inner space of a                body or an exhaust airflow for discharging the                inside air of the body to the outside. An electric                shock unit (121) is fixed and located below the                upper fan. A control unit (127) controls operation                of the upper fan and the side fan so that the                generated oxygen is discharged to the outside, and                controls the top fan to generate the descending                airflow as the intake airflow.

The                pleasant environment for various subjects such as                examinees and newborns is provided.

A gas output port of the heat exchanger is connected with an expansion valve (10). An output port of a cold storage tank (11) is provided with a gas input port and a water input port. A gas input port of the cold storage tank is connected with an output port of a data center machine room (4).
   USE - Data center system integrated with refrigeration and heat supply near pump station.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes water pump station self-lifting water potential energy and high potential energy water impacts the water turbine to drive the compressor to apply work. The oxygen generated by the photosynthesis of the microalgae white day can be used for breathing at night. The carbon dioxide generated at night can be used as the reactant of the daytime photosynthesis. The system stores carbon dioxide generated by the microalgae at night, as the radiating working medium of the data center machine room. The system can effectively use the heat generated by the data center to supply heat for the user. The low-temperature exhaust gas after expansion can be used for cooling the user, and the step utilization of the waste heat is realized.

TI Composite article used for a device e.g., gas            separation device comprises a porous substrate and a            membrane containing layers having multiple polymer            chains comprising silicon-oxygen groups and            silicon-carbon groups.
AU ZHU L
   HUANG L
   SWIHART M
   LIN H
AE UNIV NEW YORK STATE RES FOUND (UNYS-C)
GA 202183151B
AB    NOVELTY - A composite article comprises a porous                substrate and a membrane, where the membrane                comprises a first layer and a second layer, where                greater than or equal to 1 portion of porous                substrate has first layer and greater than or equal                to 1 portion of first layer has second layer and                the first layer comprises multiple polymer chains                comprising silicon-oxygen groups and silicon-carbon                groups and the first layer has a silicon to oxygen                ratio of 4:1-1:1.25; a silicon to carbon ratio of                1:2-1:10; and the second layer comprises polymer                chains comprising multiple silicon-oxygen groups                and silicon-carbon groups and the second layer has                a silicon to oxygen ratio of 1:1-1:2; a silicon to                carbon ratio of 2:1-10:1; and greater than or equal                to 1 portion of the polymer chains of the second                layer are crosslinked.
   USE - A composite article used for a device e.g.,                gas separation device for separating helium gas                and/or hydrogen gas from hydrocarbon gas(es), or a                liquid separation device, such as nanoseparation                device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite article exhibits mechanical                properties and desirable properties, such as gas                permeability. The composite article has high                temperature hydrogen/carbon dioxide gas                separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas                separation method which involves contacting a gas                sample comprising (i) hydrogen and/or helium; and                (ii) carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and/or                greater than or equal to 1 hydrocarbon(s), with a                composite article or a device comprising the                composite article; where greater than or equal to 1                portion or all of the hydrogen and/or helium is                separated from greater than or equal to 1 portion                or all of the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                and/or greater than or equal to 1                hydrocarbon(s).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Parameters: The second layer has a                  thickness of 0.1-50 nm. The membrane has an area                  of greater than or equal to 1 cm2. The first                  layer has a thickness of 100 nm-5 mu m. The                  porous substrate has a thickness of 10-200 mu m.                  The composite article has a hydrogen and/or                  helium permeance of 50-2000 GPU. The method is                  carried out at - 20 to 100 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The polymer chains comprising                  silicon-oxygen groups and silicon-carbon groups                  of the first layer are chosen from silicon                  (Si)-containing polymers, Si-containing polymer                  blends, and/or polymers doped by Si-containing                  chemicals. The Si-containing polymers are chosen                  from polysiloxanes, polysilanes, polysilazanes,                  polycarbosilanes, and/or polysilphenylenes. The                  first layer comprises polydimethylsiloxane,                  polydiethylsiloxane, polyethylmethylsiloxane,                  polyoctylmethylsiloxane,                  polytetradecylmethylsiloxane,                  poly(methylhydrosiloxane),                  poly(methylphenylsiloxane),                  poly(dimethylsiloxane-co-alkylmethylsiloxane),                  and/or                  poly(dimethylsiloxane-co-diphenylsiloxane). The                  porous substrate is chosen from polysulfones,                  polyether sulfones, polyamides, polyimides,                  polyetherimides, polyesters, polycarbonates,                  copolycarbonate esters, polyethers,                  polyetherketones, polyvinylidene fluoride,                  polybenzimidazoles, polybenzoxazoles, cellulosic                  derivatives, polyazoaromatics,                  poly(2,6-dimethylphenylene oxide), polyarylene                  oxide, polyureas, polyurethanes, polyhydrazides,                  polyazomethines, cellulose acetates, cellulose                  nitrates, ethyl cellulose, brominated                  poly(xylylene oxide), sulfonated poly(xylylene                  oxide), polyguinoxaline, polyamideimides,                  polyamide esters, blends, copolymers, substituted                  materials/derivatives/analogues, and/or zeolites.                  The membrane is disposed on greater than or equal                  to 1 portion of an exterior surface of a hollow                  fiber or flat sheet membrane. The composite                  article has a hydrogen to other gas(es)                  permeability ratio of 10:1-100:1; and/or helium                  to other gas(es) permeability ratio of                  10:1-200:1.

PN CN110893008-A
TI Electronic cigarette liquid useful for e.g. dispelling effects of alcohol, removing toxicity, protecting liver, clearing away heat and nourishing yin, comprises liquid base, compound extract and extraction solvent.
AU NING J
   WANG J
   MO Z
   SHAO L
   QIANG Y
   WANG H
   FENG W
   ZHAO H
   PAN W
AE SHENZHEN BOTON FLAVORS & FRAGRANCES CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
   CHINA TOBACCO HEBEI IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 202023895G
AB    NOVELTY - Electronic cigarette liquid comprises a liquid base comprising nicotine, megastigmatrienone, damascenone and beta-ionone, a compound extract comprising radix puerariae, Ampelopsis grossedentata and lalang grass rhizome, and an extraction solvent.
   USE - The cigarette liquid is useful for dispelling the effects of alcohol, removing toxicity, protecting liver, clearing away heat, nourishing yin, removing pathogenic factor, alleviating hangover, refreshing the spleen and stomach, and promoting saliva production.
   ADVANTAGE - The cigarette liquid utilizes the compound extract with the base liquid that contain antialcoholic and liver-protecting ingredients to increase the characteristic aroma and improve the taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the electronic cigarette liquid, comprising (i) taking 4-12 pts. wt. A. grossedentata, crushing to 80-120 mesh, and extracting the crushed A. grossedentata material by using supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain an A. grossedentata supercritical carbon dioxide extract, (ii) taking 2-10 pts. wt. radix puerariae, 3-9 pts. wt. lalang grass rhizome, crushing to 50-80 mesh, soaking and boiling the crushed radix puerariae and lalang grass rhizome material to obtain an extracting solution, concentrating the filtrate to a density of 1.1 g/ml, adding 3 times the volume of 95% ethanol alcohol, precipitating, filtering, and concentrating the supernatant to obtain a clear paste, and (iii) mixing the A. grossedentata supercritical carbon dioxide extract with the clear paste to obtain a compound extract with antialcoholic and liver-protecting ingredients, uniformly mixing 0.5-2.2 pts. wt. nicotine, 0.2-1.2 pts. wt. megastigmatrienone, 0.3-0.8 pts. wt. damascenone, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. beta-ionone, then making up to 100 pts. wt. by using propylene glycol, uniformly stirring to obtain electronic cigarette liquid base, adding propylene glycol, glycerol and deionized water to the compound extract and electronic cigarette liquid base, and uniformly stirring and mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The liquid base comprises 0.5-2.2 pts. wt. nicotine, 0.2-1.2 pts. wt. megastigmatrienone, 0.3-0.8 pts. wt. damascenone and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. beta-ionone. The compound extract comprises 2-10 pts. wt. radix puerariae, 4-12 pts. wt. A. grossedentata and 3-9 pts. wt. lalang grass rhizome. The cigarette liquid comprises 5-12 pts. wt. liquid base, and 3-10 pts. wt. compound extract. Preferred Components: The extraction solvent comprises 50-80 pts. wt. propanediol, 10-20 pts. wt. propanetriol and/or 2-8 pts. wt. de-ionized water. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the compound extract is extracted at an extraction pressure is 40-60 MPa at 30-55 degrees C with a carbon dioxide flow rate of 200-600 l/hour for 1-3 hours. In the step (ii), the compound extract is prepared by soaking radix puerariae and lalang grass rhizome and boiling to obtain an extract, where the first extraction is performed by immersing for 6-9 times in water for 2 hours, then immersing at 85 degrees C for 3 hours, and performing second extraction, adding water with a volume of 6-9 times, boiling at 85 degrees C for 2 hours, and combining the two extracts. In the method specifically comprises adding 50-80 pts. wt. propylene glycol, 10-20 pts. wt. glycerol and 2-8 pts. wt. deionized water to the compound extract and the base liquid, stirring and mixing to obtain a detoxifying compound.
   ACTIVITY - Antialcoholic; Hepatotropic; Antidote; Antipyretic; Gastrointestinal-Gen.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN IN201911001856-A
TI Organic precipitate tailing fertilizer includes carbon precipitates obtained from thermal plant waste, leaf tailing powder, protein solution of D-amino acids, and protein solution of L-amino acids, carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen gas.
AU SINGH M M
AE SINGH M M (SING-Individual)
GA 2020185444
AB    NOVELTY - An organic precipitate tailing fertilizer comprises carbon precipitates obtained from thermal plant waste, leaf tailing powder prepared by the leaves of vegetation, protein solution of D-amino acids prepared by the seeds of jatropha and Mucuna pruriens, protein solution of L-amino acids prepared by the body parts (nails, horns and bones) of the dead animals, carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen gas.
   USE - Organic precipitate tailing fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The fertilizer is chemical free and non-toxic, has low cost, and is easy to prepare from natural products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing an organic precipitate tailing fertilizer comprising: (A) preparing the carbon precipitates from the ash collected from the waste generated by the thermal plant; (B) collecting and preparing the leaf tailing powder from the leaves of vegetation; (C) preparing the protein solution I containing D-amino acids from the seeds of jatropha and Mucuna pruriens; (D) preparing the protein solution II containing L-amino acids from the body portions (nails, horns and bones) of dead animals; (E) heating the carbon precipitates at 110-120 degrees C in the heating chamber; (F) mixing carbon precipitates and leaf tailing powder in a ratio of 3:2 in the mixing chamber; (G) sprinkling of the 10% protein solution I into the mixture of carbon precipitates and leaf tailing powder in the presence of carbon dioxide and oxygen gas (in a ratio of 6.8:100) using the sprinkler; (H) cooling the mixture with 10% protein solution II in the presence of nitrogen gas (6.8:100) in the separate chamber; and (I) obtaining the fertilizer in granular form containing 63% carbon, 17.66% nitrate, 3% sulfur, 2% enzymes, 7% phosphorus, 0.1% carbohydrates, 0.24% calcium, 6% proteins and 1% other minerals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Carbon precipitates, leaf tailing powder and 20% protein solutions are taken in a ratio of 3:2:1. The source of carbon precipitates are ash discharged from thermal plant. Preferred Method: The method of preparing the leaf tailing powder comprises: (a) collecting and storing leaves leaves of vegetation which have fallen from the trees; (b) drowning the collected leaves in water for greater than or equal to 24 hours; (c) grinding the leaves in wet state; (d) mixing the fresh water with the ground leaves as in step (c) in a ratio of 1:1; (e) heating the contents at step (d) for 10-15 minutes at 100 degrees C; (f) separating ground leaves from water using filter; (g) drying the leaves; and (h) grinding the dried leaves into fine tailing powder. The method of preparing the protein solution 1 comprises: (a) collecting the seeds of jatropha and Mucuna pruriens and storing; (b) crushing the jatropha seeds and removal of oil content; (c) drying the leftover residues; mixing the jatropha seeds and Mucuna pruriens in the ratio of 10:1; (e) grinding the mixture into fine powder; (f) mixing water and the powder in the ratio of 10:1; (g) heating the mixture at 100 degrees C for 45-60 minutes; and (h) obtaining the resultant solution as protein solution (I). The method of preparing the protein solution II comprises: (a) collecting and storing the nails, horns and bones of dead animal; (b) mixing the body portion in the ratio of 1:1:10; (c) grinding the mixture into fine powder; (d) mixing water with the powder in the ratio of 10:1 and leaving untouched for 72-90 hours; (e) heating the mixture in step (d) at 100 degrees C till the solution remained one fourth of the initial amount and develops different chemical properties; and (f) obtaining the final solution as protein solution II.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - A fertilizer was prepared from 60% carbon precipitate, 40% leaf tailings, protein solutions containing D-amino acid and tailing powder in a weight ratio of 1:10, protein solutions containing L-amino acid and tailing powder in a weight ratio of 1:10, oxygen and wet protein solution I in a weight ratio of 6.8:100, carbon dioxide and wet protein solution I in a weight ratio of 6.8:100, and nitrogen and wet protein solution II in a weight ratio of 6.8:100. The final fertilizer product contained 63% carbon, 17.66% nitrate, 3% sulfur, 2% enzyme, 7% phosphorus, 0.1% carbohydrate, 0.24% calcium, 6% protein, and 1% other additive.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); P81 (Optics (G02).)
MC C04-D01; C04-D02; C04-L01; C04-N01; C04-N02; C05-A01B; C05-B02A4; C05-C02; C05-C05; C05-C06; C12-M11D; C14-S18; C14-T03; C14-T04; C14-Z; D04-A01J; F03-C; F03-E01; F04-G01; P81-A09
IP C02F-001/72; C05F-005/00; C09D-007/40; D06M-013/513; G02B-006/30

PN CN110711567-A
TI Preparation of adsorbent involves dissolving melamine and arylamine derivatives in water, adding aqueous ammonia and aldehyde, stirring, carbonizing under inert atmosphere, mixing with activator, activating at different temperatures.
AU LV R
   SONG C
   LIU M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202009760G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of adsorbent involves dissolving melamine and arylamine derivatives in water at 10-50 degrees C, adding aqueous ammonia, stirring well, adding aldehyde, stirring to obtain polymer nano microspheres, carbonizing polymer nano microspheres under inert atmosphere at 500-800 degrees C to obtain nitrogen-containing porous carbon, mixing nitrogen-containing porous carbon with activator, and transferring into inert atmosphere for activation at different temperatures or drying polymer nano microspheres, carbonizing in inert atmosphere, changing to carbon dioxide atmosphere when temperature is 600 degrees C, and increasing temperature to 700-800 degrees C. The aromatic amine derivative is chosen from 2,4-diaminobenzenesulfonic acid, p-aminoanisole, p-aminobenzonitrile, p-aminophenol, anthranilic acid, m-aminobenzoic acid, p-aminobenzoic acid, o-phenylenediamine, m-phenylenediamine, p-phenylenediamine, p-aminophenylthiophenol, p-nitroaniline, aminophenylboronic acid and p-aminophenol phosphate monoester.
   USE - Preparation of adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, environmentally-friendly, and has mild conditions and short preparation cycle. The nitrogen-containing porous carbon dioxide adsorbent prepared has low energy consumption, high adsorption capacity, high selectivity, and excellent recycling performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of adsorbent involves dissolving melamine and arylamine derivatives in water at 10-50 degrees C, adding aqueous ammonia, stirring well, adding aldehyde, stirring to obtain polymer nano microspheres, carbonizing polymer nano microspheres under inert atmosphere at 500-800 degrees C to obtain nitrogen-containing porous carbon, mixing nitrogen-containing porous carbon with activator and transferring into inert atmosphere for activation at different temperatures or drying polymer nano microspheres, carbonizing in inert atmosphere, changing to carbon dioxide atmosphere when temperature is 600 degrees C, and increasing temperature to 700-800 degrees C. The aromatic amine derivative is chosen from 2,4-diaminobenzenesulfonic acid, anthranilic acid, m-aminobenzenesulfonic acid, p-aminobenzenesulfonic acid, m-phenylenediamine disulfonic acid, 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid, p-aminochlorobenzene, p-aminoanisole, p-aminobenzonitrile, p-aminophenol, anthranilic acid, m-aminobenzoic acid, p-aminobenzoic acid, aniline, o-phenylenediamine, m-phenylenediamine, p-phenylenediamine, p-aminophenylthiophenol, p-nitroaniline, aminophenylboronic acid and p-aminophenol phosphate monoester.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The activator comprises potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, potassium oxalate, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, lithium hydroxide, phosphoric acid, and zinc chloride. Preferred Composition: The nitrogen-containing porous carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises 1-28% nitrogen. Preferred Property: The energy consumption for carbon dioxide adsorption is 15-35 kJ/mmol. The adsorption capacity of adsorbent at room temperature is 2.4-3.72 mmol/g (1 bar), and the adsorption capacity at 0 degrees C is 3-6.19 mmol/g (1 bar). The selectivity of adsorbent at room temperature and atmospheric pressure is 15-129 and the selectivity at 0 degrees C under normal pressure is 17-220. Preferred Condition: The activation process is carried out at 600-800 degrees C. The reaction to obtain polymer nano microspheres is carried out at 1-4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of melamine and arylamine derivatives is (1:1)-(5:1). Preferred Component: The aldehyde comprises formaldehyde or fatty dialdehyde. The fatty dialdehyde comprises glyoxal, malonaldehyde, succinaldehyde, glutaraldehyde, and adipaldehyde.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN US2019320938-A1; WO2019207584-A1; CA3096456-A1; CN112020325-A; EP3784127-A1
TI Capnography system for measuring respiratory            carbon dioxide of patient to provide metabolic            condition of patient, has communication unit that            transfers data to processor based on indication            received from communication component.
AU NARKISS N
AE ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
GA 201988114C
AB    NOVELTY - The capnography system (100) comprises a                carbon dioxide (CO2) sensing system (104). A CO2                sensor is configured to measure a CO2 concentration                in exhaled breath of a subject. A processor is                configured to derive several breath related                parameters based on the measured CO2 concentration.                A tubing system (112) is configured to enable flow                of respiratory gasses. The tubing system comprises                a connector that is configured to connect the                tubing system to the CO2 sensing system. A                communication component (102) is configured to                provide an indication of a type of the tubing                system to a communication unit (108), when the                tubing system is connected to the CO2 sensing                system. The communication unit is configured to                transfer data to the processor based on the                indication received from the communication                component. The processor is configured to change or                suggest a change of an operation mode of the CO2                sensing system based on the data.
   USE - Capnography system for measuring respiratory                carbon dioxide of subject for providing significant                indication of metabolic condition of patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The pump in capnography modules is designed to                work around an optimal point, in order to reduce                the pump size or minimize acoustic noise generated                by the pump. The efficiency of utilizing the                communication component for indicating use of the                capnography system during emergency settings (EMS)                is assessed, in order to reduce time for                verification of correct intubations. The operation                mode of the CO2 sensing module is enabled to                trigger the increasing of the pumping speed until a                first reading is obtained, which significantly                decreases the response time and thus the time                required for verification of correct                intubation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for operating a capnography system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a functional block-diagram                of a capnography system.
   Capnography system (100)
   Communication component (102)
   CO2 sensing system (104)
   Communication unit (108)
   Tubing system (112)

PN US2019276943-A1; US10844501-B2
TI Making catalyst comprises e.g. adding catalytic metal in its metallic form to molten alkaline metal, atomically dispersing catalytic metal in metal, forming alkaline metal-catalytic metal solid and converting portion of alkaline metal.
AU LIU D
   XU T
   REBOLLAR D
   XU H
AE UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
   UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
   UNIV NORTHERN ILLINOIS (UYNI-Non-standard)
GA 201979223Y
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing (s1) a catalyst comprises: adding a catalytic metal in its metallic form to molten alkaline metal; atomically dispersing the catalytic metal in the molten alkaline metal; forming an alkaline metal-catalytic metal solid; converting a portion of alkaline metal in the alkaline metal-catalytic metal solid to alkaline metal hydroxide forming a metal-alkaline metal hydroxide solid; mixing the metal-alkaline metal hydroxide solid with a conductive support material to form a mixture; removing alkaline metal hydroxide from the mixture leaving a mixture of metal in the conductive support; and drying the mixture of metal and the conductive support to produce the catalyst containing the catalytic metal atomically dispersed over the conductive support.
   USE - The methods are useful for synthesizing the catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: provides the catalytic material which has a catalytic selectivity of at least 95% and Faradaic efficiency of at least 93% in converting of carbon dioxide to ethanol, and a catalytic selectivity of 100% and Faradaic efficiency of higher than 97% in converting carbon dioxide to acetone; and provides the catalyst with high selectivity, high Faradic efficiency and low overpotential toward to the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to fuels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing (s2) a catalyst comprising forming an dispersion of multiple metals in a molten alkaline metal, forming an alkaline metal-multiple metal solid, converting a portion of alkaline metal in the alkaline metal-first catalytic metal-second catalytic metal solid to an alkaline metal hydroxide forming an alkaline metal hydroxide-multiple metal solid, mixing the alkaline metal hydroxide-multiple metal solid with a conductive support material, removing alkaline metal hydroxide from the mixture of the alkaline metal hydroxide-multiple metal solid and a conductive support, and drying the multiple metal and conductive support mixture to produce the catalyst containing the multiple metals atomically dispersed over the conductive support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of converting the portion of the lithium-catalytic metal to metal-lithium hydroxide solid in the method (m1) comprises reacting lithium in the metal-lithium solid with the moist air. The method (m1) further comprises mixing the metal-lithium hydroxide solid with a conductive support material using mechanical method such as ball milling. The catalytic metal before adding to molten lithium is in metallic state and in the form of power, ingot, wire and shredded pieces, is a transition metal, and is a platinum group metal. The catalytic metal is dispersed in a single atom form. The step of removing the metal hydroxide in the method (m1) comprises washing the metal-lithium hydroxide with water for removing lithium. The washing step in the method (m1) comprises forming an alkaline water solution and modifying the carbonaceous support with the oxygenated species serving as anchoring sites for the catalytic metal. The step of forming the dispersion of multiple metals in the method (s2) comprises adding a first catalytic metal in its metallic form to the molten alkaline metal, and adding a second catalytic metal in its metallic form to the molten alkaline metal. The step of forming the dispersion of multiple metals in the method (s2) comprises adding a multi-metallic catalytic compound to the molten alkaline metal. The conductive support material is a carbonaceous material having porous network and further catalytic metal is decorated through the porous network of carbonaceous material. The step of removing the metal hydroxide in the method (s2) comprises forming an alkaline water solution and modifying the carbonaceous support with the oxygenated species serving as anchoring sites for the catalytic metal. Preferred Components: The molten alkaline metal is lithium. The conductive support material is a carbonaceous material which is a porous network and further catalytic metal is decorated through the porous network of carbonaceous material. The catalytic metal is a bimetallic compound.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
