PN US2019247785-A1; US10981107-B2
TI Method for re-circulating clean air in atmosphere of enclosure, involves light returning fraction stream to atmosphere of enclosure, and controlling level of carbon dioxide in atmosphere of enclosure by selecting number of coiled ducts.
AU GENTRY G J
   SON C H
   CLARK R W
AE BOEING CO (BOEI-C)
GA 2019697218
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directing an input air stream from an atmosphere of an enclosure through a number of coiled ducts at a rate sufficient to stratify the input air stream within each coiled duct according to a molecular weight of gaseous components of the input air stream and to form a heavy fraction stream and a light fraction stream (102), where the heavy fraction stream is relatively enriched in carbon dioxide as compared to the light fraction stream. The heavy fraction stream is withdrawn (104) from the number of coiled ducts. The light fraction stream is returned (106) from the number of coiled ducts to the atmosphere of the enclosure. A level of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of the enclosure is controlled by selecting the number of coiled ducts.
   USE - Method for re-circulating clean air in an atmosphere of an enclosure in enclosed environments. Uses include but are not limited to spacecraft, space habitat, submarine, underground mine, aircraft, armored vehicle, and pressurized rover.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables using centrifugal air separators and the coiled ducts to produce a pressure differential between an entrance port and an exit port or between a duct entrance port and the duct exit port of less than 100 kPa, less than 60 kPa or less than 30 kPa when gas flows with a mass flow rate of 2.5 g/s from the entrance port.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for separating gas by molecular weight.
   Method for separating gas by molecular weight (100)
   Step for directing input air stream from atmosphere of enclosure through number of coiled ducts (102)
   Step for heavy fraction stream from number of coiled ducts (104)
   Step for light fraction stream from number of coiled ducts to atmosphere of enclosure (106)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110102300-A; CN110102300-B
TI Flexible carbon-based support supported metal            monoatomic catalyst useful for electrocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises metal monoatomic            content, graphene, carbon-based carrier, and hetero            atom.
AU ZHANG H
   WANG J
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA (UYZB-C)
GA 201970749D
AB    NOVELTY - Flexible carbon-based support supported metal                monoatomic catalyst comprises 0.001-4 wt.% metal                monoatomic content, 6-34.999 wt.% graphene, 50-80                wt.% carbon-based carrier, and 5-20 wt.% hetero                atom, where the catalyst is anchored to graphene by                coordination of metal monoatoms and heteroatoms,                and the graphene is supported on flexible                carbon-based support.
   USE - The flexible carbon-based support supported                metal monoatomic catalyst is useful for                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has simple preparation method,                high metal monoatomic content, uniform monoatomic                dispersion, metal monoatoms anchored on the carrier                by coordination with heteroatoms; ensures stable                physicochemical structure; has excellent carbon                monoxide selectivity, high gas Faraday efficiency,                and has good catalyst cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) mixing metal                precursor, and nitrogen-containing precursor with                graphene powder in a solvent to prepare homogeneous                mixed slurry, (ii) uniformly loading the mixed                slurry prepared in step (i) on flexible                carbon-based carrier, and drying to obtain a                nitrogen-containing precursor of metal precursor                i.e. flexible and graphene-type carbon-based                carrier, (iii) subjecting the graphene-type                carbon-based carrier to multi process and slow                high-temperature heat treatment to obtain the                flexible carbon-based carrier, where the metal                precursor and nitrogen-containing precursor                complexation reaction occurs under high temperature                condition, and (iv) washing with acid, and then                using de-ionized water to repeatedly washing to                neutral, and then drying; and #use of the flexible                carbon-based supported metal monoatomic catalyst                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (CO2), the catalyst is used directly as an                electrode, and no need to add adhesive to the                conductive carbon paper or glassy carbon electrode,                reduced electrode contact resistance,the catalyst                is directly used as a working electrode for CO2                reduction, the reference electrode is a saturated                calomel electrode, the counter electrode is                platinum sheet or a platinum wire, the electrolyte                is a 0.1 mol/l CO2 saturated potassium hydrogen                carbonate aqueous solution or ionic liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  metal precursor comprises cobalt phthalocyanine,                  iron phthalocyanine, copper phthalocyanine, zinc                  phthalocyanine, nickel phthalocyanine, iron                  porphyrin, copper porphyrin, zinc porphyrin,                  cobalt porphyrin, vitamin B12, dicyclopentadienyl                  iron, ferritin, superoxide dismutase, nitrogen                  fixation enzyme or carboxypeptidase, the                  nitrogen-containing precursor comprises                  dicyandiamide, cysteine, glutamic acid, lysine,                  melamine, ethylene diamine, urea, aniline or                  polyethylene imine, where the disperse system is:                  5-9.9 wt.% metal precursor, 80-90 wt.%                  nitrogen-containing precursor and 0.1-15 wt.%                  graphite alkene. In the step (II), the flexible                  carbon-based carrier comprises carbon fiber                  cloth, cellulose cloth, nylon cloth, bamboo fiber                  cloth, melamine foam or graphite film. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the solvent comprises                  water, methanol, ethanol, acetone, sodium                  hydroxide aqueous solution or isopropyl alcohol,                  where the mixing method comprises stirring and                  mixing or ultrasonic mixing, the stirring and                  mixing comprises magnetic stirring or mechanical                  mixing, the mixing time is 3-10 hours, the                  stirring temperature is 25-40 degrees C, the                  ultrasonic mixing of the ultrasonic frequency is                  100-1200 W, ultrasonic time is 1-4 hours, and the                  ultrasonic temperature is 20-50 degrees C. In the                  step (i), the load method comprises soaking                  method, spin-coating method or a blade coating                  method, where the infiltration method is: the                  solid content of the mixed slurry is 5-30%,                  soaking the carbon-based carrier in the mixed                  slurry for 30-60 minutes, taking out and drying                  in vacuum drying box, the drying temperature is                  60-100 degrees C and the time is 2-5 hours, and                  then repeatedly dipping into the mixed slurry,                  soaking and drying for 10-50 times circularly,                  the spin coating method is: solid content of the                  mixed slurry is 40-60 wt.%, the spin coating                  speed is 100-1000 rpm and the time is 1-5 hours,                  the scraping a solid content coating method is:                  using 65-80 wt.% mixing slurry, and using scraper                  to the mixture uniformly coating two surfaces of                  blade in the flexible carbon-based carrier, and                  scraping the thickness of coating is 20-60 mm. In                  then step (ii), the drying method comprises                  naturally drying at room temperature, drying in                  vacuum, when the blast drying or freezing or                  drying under vacuum drying i.e. the temperature                  is 50-80 degrees C, the time is 5-10 hours, the                  pressure is -0.8 to -1 Mpa, blast drying: the                  temperature is 80-120 degrees C and the time is                  8-20 hours, freezing and drying: -30 to -50                  degrees C to freeze for 6-10 hours, and then                  drying for 12-36 hours at 0.0-10 Mpa of vacuum                  environment at the temperature. In the step (ii),                  the multi-step high temperature heat treatment                  comprises (iii-a) heating from room temperature                  to t1 is 150-200 degrees C, the time is 1.5-2.5                  hours, the second stage is: heating t1 to t2,                  where t2 is 300-450 degrees C, the time is                  1.5-2.5 hours, the third stage: heating t2 to t3,                  where t3 is 750-850 degrees C, the time is                  1.5-2.5 hours, the fourth stage is: from t3                  cooling to the room temperature, heating speed is                  2-5 degrees C/min, cooling speed is 5-10 degrees                  C/min, and whole process under the protection of                  the high pure argon gas or high pure nitrogen. In                  the step (iv), the acid aqueous solution                  containing hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid                  aqueous solution in the concentration of acid is                  0.2-1.5 mol/l, the acid washing temperature is                  50-90 degrees C, and the time is 10-15                  hours.

PN CN109759074-A
TI Methane carbon dioxide dry reforming nickel-based            catalyst useful for catalytic dry reforming reaction,            comprises a catalyst carrier, an active complex and a            synergistic complex, where the synergistic complex            comprises yttrium oxide.
AU JIANG H
   CAI Y
   CHEN Y
   JI J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201946884R
AB    NOVELTY - Methane carbon dioxide dry reforming                nickel-based catalyst comprises 50-92 wt.% catalyst                carrier, 5-30 wt.% active complex and 3-20 wt.%                synergistic complex supported on the catalyst                carrier, where the active complex is an elemental                nickel, the synergistic complex is yttrium oxide,                and the catalyst carrier is gamma -aluminum                oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic dry                reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the catalytic activity and                stability of the catalyst, and disperses metallic                nickel particles on gamma -aluminum oxide by                utilizing cerium oxide. The catalyst has good                resistance to carbon deposition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising (i)                adding gamma -aluminum oxide into a muffle furnace,                heating to 750-850 degrees C for 3-5 hours, and                naturally cooling at room temperature to obtain                pretreated gamma -aluminum oxide, (ii) adding                nickel salt and yttrium salt into deionized water,                stirring and dissolving until transparent, and                ultrasonically treating for 20-40 minutes to obtain                solution A, (iii) slowly dropping solution A into                pretreated gamma -aluminum oxide at a rate of 0.5-1                ml/minute under stirring condition, mixing and                stirring to obtain a paste, (iv) ultrasonically                treating the paste for 20-40 minutes, dipping at                room temperature for 12-24 hours, drying at 100-110                degrees C for 10-14 hours, calcining at 550-650                degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain gamma -aluminum                oxide loaded with nickel oxide and yttrium oxide                catalyst precursor, and (v) placing the catalyst                precursor in a tubular furnace, calcining in a                hydrogen atmosphere at 750-850 degrees C for                0.8-1.2 hours, and reducing nickel oxide to nickel                elementary substance to obtain catalyst of - gamma                -aluminum oxide-loaded nickel and yttrium oxide;                and
   (2) use of methane carbon dioxide dry                reforming nickel-based catalyst in catalytic dry                reforming reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst                  comprises 69.3-86.3 wt.% catalyst carrier, 10.7                  wt.% active complex and 3-20 wt.% synergistic                  complex. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  heating is carried out at 750-850 degrees C with                  a heating rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute. The                  reaction is carried out in a fixed bed reactor at                  800-900 degrees C, where the gas space velocity                  is 96000 ml/(h/g), the feed volume ratio of                  methane to carbon dioxide is 1:1 and the reaction                  pressure is 0.1 MPa. Preferred Components: In the                  step (ii), the nickel salt is nickel nitrate, and                  the yttrium salt is yttrium nitrate.

PN CN109599583-A; CN109599583-B
TI Electricity-gas combined production solid oxide fuel cell stack structure, has sealing cover provided with fuel inlet pipes and tail gas outlet pipes, carbon bin formed with exhaust gas reaction cavity through carbon supplement cavity.
AU KONG W
   HAN Z
   HUANG H
   ZHOU S
AE UNIV JIANGSU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJG-C)
GA 201935207E
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a sealing cover (102) covered around a tubular solid oxide fuel cell battery group (103). Two ends of a solid oxide fuel cell single battery in the tubular solid oxide fuel cell battery group extends into the sealing cover. An exhaust gas reaction cavity is vertically formed in the tubular solid oxide fuel cell battery group and provided with two filtering screens, a ventilating pipe and a multi-stage filtering piece. The multi-stage filtering piece is mounted between adjacent ventilating pipes. Two sides of the sealing cover at a top part of the tubular solid oxide fuel cell battery group are respectively provided with multiple fuel inlet pipes (101) and multiple tail gas outlet pipes (112). A top part of the exhaust gas reaction cavity is communicated with a carbon bin through a carbon supplement cavity (108).
   USE - Electricity-gas combined production solid oxide fuel cell stack structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure designs multiple layers of exhaust gas reaction cavities in fuel flowing direction, increases filtering screen distances, and reacts product water and carbon dioxide with solid carbon so as to reduce concentration differences at fuel inlet and outlet. The structure provides a carbon bin between multiple tail gas outlet pipes to convert residual water and carbon dioxide in tail gas into carbon monoxide and hydrogen by reaction, realizes electricity-gas combined production, and improves device efficiency. The structure can be reused by an electric pile, and avoids battery performance influence caused by product water and carbon dioxide dilution in a solid oxide fuel cell electric pile.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an electricity-gas combined production solid oxide fuel cell stack structure.
   Fuel inlet pipes (101)
   Sealing cover (102)
   Tubular solid oxide fuel cell battery group (103)
   Carbon supplement cavity (108)
   Tail gas outlet pipes (112)
DC X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN CN109232239-A
TI Extraction of shikimic acid by washing star anise, crushing, sieving, adding ether, extracting in acetone, sonicating, soaking, filtering, soaking residue in extraction solvent, sonicating, soaking, filtering, and soaking residue in acid.
AU LI Y
   LU H
   LI M
AE NAPO KANGZHENG NATURAL PLANT EXTRACTION (NAPO-Non-standard)
GA 2019133236
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of shikimic acid includes washing star anise, crushing, sieving, adding ether, extracting in acetone, sonicating, soaking, filtering to obtain residue and filtrate, soaking residue in extraction solvent, sonicating, soaking, filtering to obtain residue and filtrate, soaking residue in ethanol-containing hydrochloric acid or citric acid extraction agent, sonicating, soaking, and filtering to obtain residue and filtrate; and adding residue to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting with chloroform, soaking extract to solvent comprising water, ethanol, methanol and acetic acid, filtering, concentrating filtrate through reverse membrane device, decolorizing, and crystallizing.
   USE - Method for extraction of shikimic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The shikimic acid has high purity. The method has high extracting rate of up to more than 12%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of shikimic acid comprises washing star anise, crushing, sieving through 80 mesh sieve, adding ether at mass ratio of 1:1, extracting in acetone, sonicating for 40-50 minutes, soaking at 60-65 degrees C for 1-3 hours, filtering to obtain residue and filtrate, soaking residue in extraction solvent comprising 5 wt.% acetic acid, 5 wt.% phenol and balance water, sonicating for 40-50 minutes, soaking at 90-100 degrees C for 4-5 hours, filtering to obtain residue and filtrate, soaking residue in 70-80% ethanol-containing hydrochloric acid or citric acid extraction agent, sonicating for 40-50 minutes, soaking for 4 hours, and filtering to obtain residue and filtrate; and adding residue to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting with chloroform at 40-50 degrees C, 30-35 MPa and at carbon dioxide flow rate of 15 L/min for 30-40 minutes to obtain volatile oil and extract, soaking extract to solvent comprising water, ethanol, methanol and acetic acid at mass ratio of 100:20:8:8 at room temperature for 3-5 minutes, filtering, concentrating filtrate through reverse membrane device, decolorizing, and crystallizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The decolorization is done by adding 5-10 wt.% activated carbon to concentrated filtrate, stirring to 20-35 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, and filtering. The method further comprises evaporating filtrate to 90-100 degrees C to obtain paste, and freeze-drying to obtain octagonal crude polysaccharide; or filtering filtrates through 20-25 mu m and 5-410 mu m filters respectively, super membrane filtering at 30-35 degrees C and at 1.5 kg/cm2, and vacuum freeze-drying to obtain octagonal crude protein; or extracting filtrates with anhydrous ethanol, and adding to skin care product containing skin moisturizing factor. The obtained volatile oil is used in feed and daily chemicals.

PN CN108653675-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicine composition for            reducing weight and reducing fat, comprises bunge            cherry seed, ebony, Gardenia, Cassia seed, turmeric and            lotus leaf.
AU HE X
   WANG J
   YU J
   LI H
   SHEN L
AE NINGBO NO 2 HOSPITAL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018837436
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicine composition                comprises 5-50 pts. wt. bunge cherry seed, 5-50                pts. wt. ebony, 5-50 pts. wt. Gardenia, 5-50 pts.                wt. Cassia seed, 5-50 pts. wt. turmeric and 5-50                pts. wt. lotus leaf.
   USE - Traditional Chinese medicine composition for                reducing weight and reducing fat (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The traditional Chinese medicine composition                has good weight control effect, improved insulin                resistance, low adverse reaction, low cost,                extracting and separating efficiency, strong                operability, low production cost, and is suitable                for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing traditional Chinese                medicine composition, which involves weighing bunge                cherry seed, ebony, Gardenia, Cassia seed, turmeric                and lotus leaf, cutting lotus leaf to blocks,                arranging weighed lotus leaves in pre-prepared                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction                kettle for extraction to obtain extract, crushing                turmeric and Cassia seed to coarse powder to form                mixture, adding mixture to prepared supercritical                carbondioxide fluid extracting kettle to extract to                obtain extracting liquid, taking mixture after                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of medicine                slag, adding proper ethanol solution, refluxing,                extracting, combining, refluxing, reducing                pressure, concentrating until there is no alcohol                taste to obtain extracting solution, crushing                ebony, bunge cherry seed and Gardenia to fine                powder to form mixture, subjecting to supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid extracting kettle to extract                to obtain extraction liquid, mixing extraction                liquid obtained for multiple times to form mixed                liquid, concentrating mixed liquid and performing                spray drying to obtain the desired                traditionalChinese medicine composition;
   (2) a method for preparing oral liquid, which                involves taking extract of each raw material,                adding distilled water to dissolve, filtering raw                materials, adding syrup and adjusting pH to obtain                the desired oral liquid; and
   (3) a method for preparing tablet, which                involves taking extract of each raw material,                adding lactose or corn starch, adding lubricant                magnesium stearate, if necessary, mixing uniformly,                granulating, and pressing to obtain the desired                tablet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: During lotus leaf                  extraction, concentration of 50-90% ethanol is                  added as an entraining agent, and ratio of carbon                  dioxide to entrainer flow rate is used, which is                  2:1-2:2. Preferred Conditions: The lotus leaf                  block size is 1-2 cm, extracting pressure is                  32-35 megaPascals, extracting temperature is                  40-45 degrees C, extracting time is for 2-4                  hours, separating pressure is 4.5-5.5 megaPascals                  and separating temperature is 30-35 degrees C.                  The extracting pressure for mixture of extraction                  is 12-15 megaPascals, extracting temperature is                  25-30 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate is 1-2                  l/minutes, extracting time is firstly statically                  extracting for 30-60 minutes, then dynamically                  extracting for 1.5-2 hours, separating pressure                  is 4.0-4.5 megaPascals, and separating                  temperature is 30-35 degrees C. The solvent of                  ethanol solution is added to 5-8 times of dregs,                  where concentration of ethanol solution is                  50-80%, and number of times of reflux extraction                  is 1-2 times, each time 1-3 hours. The extract of                  each raw material is added to pharmaceutically                  acceptable carrier to prepare oral liquid,                  granule, capsule, tablet or injection                  medicine.
   ACTIVITY - Anorectic; Antilipemic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN CN107854540-A; CN107854540-B
TI Traditional Chinese medicine oil used for treating hemorrhoids comprises peach essential oil, safflower essential oil, creeper essential oil and menthol.
AU MIAO M
   ZHANG D
   MIAO Y
   FANG X
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
   UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 2018264928
AB    NOVELTY - A traditional Chinese medicine oil comprises peach essential oil, safflower essential oil, creeper essential oil and menthol at ratio of 1.8-2.2:0.8-1.2:2.5-3.5:0.8-1.2.
   USE - Traditional Chinese medicine oil used for treating hemorrhoids (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine oil is convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A traditional Chinese medicine oil comprises peach essential oil, safflower essential oil, creeper essential oil and menthol at ratio of 1.8-2.2:0.8-1.2:2.5-3.5:0.8-1.2. The peach essential oil is prepared by crushing peach into 10-20 mesh coarse powder, adding 0.5-1.5 times its weight ethanol, ethyl acetate and/or cyclohexane, soaking for 2-4 minutes, taking out, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at extraction temperature of 42-50 degrees C and pressure of 20-25 MPa and at separation temperature of 50-60 degrees C and pressure of 4.5-5.5 MPa for 1.5-2.5 hours. The safflower essential oil is prepared by crushing safflower into 10-20 mesh coarse powder, adding 0.5-1.5 times its weight ethanol, ethyl acetate or cyclohexane, soaking for 2-4 minutes, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at extraction temperature of 42-50 degrees C and pressure of 20-25 MPa and at separation temperature of 50-60 degrees C and pressure of 4.5-5.5 MPa for 1.5-2.5 hours. The creeper essential oil is prepared by crushing creeper into 10-20 mesh coarse powder, adding 0.5-1.5 times its weight ethanol, ethyl acetate or cyclohexane, soaking for 2-4 minutes, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at extraction temperature of 42-50 degrees C and pressure of 20-25 MPa and at separation temperature of 50-60 degrees C and pressure of 4.5-5.5 MPa for 1.5-2.5 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the oil by mixing raw materials, and stirring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The peach essential oil, safflower essential oil, creeper essential oil and menthol have ratio of 2:1:3:1, preferably 2.1:0.9:3.4:0.9.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic.

PN EP3339253-A1; WO2018115121-A1; CA3047503-A1; AU2017384504-A1; CN110214128-A; SG11201905636-A1; BR112019012665-A2; US2020017383-A1; MX2019007242-A1; JP2020514019-W; HK40009408-A0; SG11201905636-B; RU2019119878-A
TI Treatment of filtrate from anaerobic membrane bioreactor for obtaining purified water and/or nutrients for plants involves reducing pH of filtrate from bioreactor and subjecting filtrate to reverse osmosis or nanofiltration treatment.
AU HEFFERNAN B
   FRANKLIN R J
   FRANKIN R J
   FRANKIN R Y
AE VEOLIA WATER SOLUTIONS & TECHNOLOGIES SU (VEOL-C)
   VEOLIA WATER SOLUTIONS & TECHNOLOGIES SU (VEOL-C)
   VEOLIA WATER SOLUTIONS & TECHNOLOGIES SU (VEOL-C)
GA 2018498751
AB    NOVELTY - Filtrate from anaerobic membrane bioreactor is treated by reducing pH of filtrate from anaerobic membrane bioreactor and subjecting filtrate having reduced pH to reverse osmosis treatment or nanofiltration treatment, forming reverse osmosis or nanofiltration permeate and reverse osmosis or nanofiltration concentrate while keeping filtrate from the anaerobic membrane bioreactor essentially anaerobic.
   USE - Treatment of filtrate from anaerobic membrane bioreactor for obtaining purified water and/or nutrients for plants or animals (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces tendency of fouling and/or scaling and improves treatment capacity of filtrate (volume of filtrate that can be treated per period of time, e.g. per year) from anaerobic membrane bioreactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) installation for treatment of filtrate comprising anaerobic membrane bioreactor having digester and membrane filtration unit of which filtrate outlet is connected to reverse osmosis or nanofiltration unit via gas-liquid mixing unit for introducing carbon dioxide into filtrate, in which reverse osmosis or nanofiltration unit has reverse osmosis or nanofiltration permeate outlet and reverse osmosis or nanofiltration concentrate outlet; carbon dioxide recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide from permeate, having inlet connected to permeate outlet, in which carbon dioxide recovery unit further has outlet for liquid, in particular water, from which carbon dioxide is recovered and outlet for recovered carbon dioxide, in which the outlet is connected to inlet of mixing unit to introduce carbon dioxide into mixing unit; carbon dioxide recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide from concentrate, having inlet connected to concentrate outlet, in which carbon dioxide recovery unit further has concentrate outlet for concentrate from which carbon dioxide is recovered and outlet for recovered carbon dioxide, in which outlet for recovered carbon dioxide is connected to inlet of mixing unit to introduce carbon dioxide into mixing unit; and carbon dioxide recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide from biogas produced in digester, in which carbon dioxide recovery unit has inlet for introducing biogas, outlet for biogas from which carbon dioxide is recovered and outlet for recovered carbon dioxide which is connected to inlet of mixing unit to introduce carbon dioxide in mixing unit, and the installation is configured to maintain essentially anaerobic conditions in the digester, membrane filtration unit and reverse osmosis or nanofiltration unit; and
   (2) obtaining purified water and/or nutrients for plants or animals which involves performing treatment of aqueous waste stream comprising biodegradable organic substance and inorganic matter in installation or performing treatment of aqueous waste stream in anaerobic membrane reactor from which filtrate is obtained and which filtrate is treated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Processes: The pH is reduced by introducing carbon dioxide into filtrate. Preferred Compositions: Filtrate from anaerobic membrane bioreactor to be subjected to reverse osmosis or nanofiltration contains greater than or equal to 20 ppm, preferably greater than or equal to 50 ppm phosphate, and ammonia in molar ratio ammonia to phosphate of greater than or equal to 1.5, preferably greater than or equal to 2.5.

PT P
PN WO2021261410-A1
TI Immobilizing carbon dioxide by passing seawater through nanofiltration membrane, precipitating and recovering crystal comprising calcium sulfate from nanofiltration membrane concentrate, and reacting carbon dioxide with alkaline earth metal oxide prepared from nanofiltration membrane concentrate.
AU TAKAO N
   MYERS C A
   SHO F
   YOSHIHIKO K
   SATORU H
   KAZUHIKO I
   MOTOYORI H
   ERI S
   YOSHIAKI M
AE UNIV WASEDA (UYWS-C)
   JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
   SASAKURA ENG CO LTD (SASB-C)
GA 2021F09440
AB    NOVELTY - Method for immobilizing carbon dioxide involves (a) passing seawater or brine through nanofiltration membrane to produce nanofiltration membrane concentrate, which is concentrated without permeating nanofiltration membrane, (b) precipitating and recovering at least one crystal comprising calcium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium chloride and sodium sulfate from obtained nanofiltration membrane concentrate, (c) preparing alkaline earth metal from obtained nanofiltration membrane concentrate, and (d) reacting carbon dioxide with alkaline earth metal oxide and immobilizing carbon dioxide as carbonate.
   USE - The method is used for immobilizing carbon dioxide and for waste liquid treatment. The magnesium carbonate on which carbon dioxide is immobilized is used as building material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the anthropogenic carbon dioxide emissions to zero as evaluation model, enhances the carbon dioxide reduction capacity while considering the amount of carbon dioxide emissions, is environmentally-friendly, and contributes to reduction of waste liquid treatment cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves performing first concentrated crystallization process in which calcium sulfate crystals are precipitated and recovered by adding calcium sulfate as seed crystals to nanofiltration membrane concentrated solution, evaporating and concentrating the solution. In first concentrated crystallization process, the recovered calcium sulfate crystal is used as seed crystal. The step (b) involves performing second concentrated crystallization process for precipitating and recovering sodium chloride crystals by further evaporating and concentrating nanofiltration membrane concentrate after recovering calcium sulfate crystals. The step (b) involves performing cooling crystallization process for recovering precipitated crystals by cooling crystallization of nanofiltration membrane concentrate. The cooling crystallization process involves performing first cooling crystallization process for recovering potassium chloride crystals precipitated by cooling crystallization of nanofiltration membrane concentrate, and performing second cooling crystallization process for recovering sodium sulfate crystals precipitated by cooling crystallization of nanofiltration membrane concentrate which has undergone first cooling crystallization process at temperature less than cooling crystallization temperature of first cooling crystallization process. The step (a) involves performing pH adjustment process for adjusting pH by adding acid to generated nanofiltration membrane concentrate. The step (a) involves performing salt-production process for concentrating nanofiltration membrane permeate that has permeated the nanofiltration membrane and recovering the precipitated sodium chloride crystals. The step (a) involves performing merging process for merging generated nanofiltration membrane concentrate with blow liquid after sodium chloride crystals have been recovered in salt-production process. The step (a) involves performing pH adjustment process in which acid solution obtained by electrodialyzing the solution of sodium chloride crystal produced in salt production process, which is added to nanofiltration membrane concentrated solution produced for adjusting pH value. Preferred Component: The alkaline earth metal oxide obtained in step (c) comprises magnesium oxide.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN KR2022141267-A; KR2462123-B1
TI Chemical roofing water decomposition method used            for various purposes i.e. carbon dioxide capture during            combustion and hydrogen production, comprises reacting            water decomposing material with hydrogen and reduced            water decomposing material.
AU JANG Y S
   KIM U
   HYUN L J
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 2022D19450
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical roofing water decomposition method                comprises reducing the water decomposing material                by reacting the water decomposing material with                hydrogen and reacting the reduced water decomposing                material with water to regenerate the water                decomposing material. The water decomposition                material comprises 50-90 wt.% iron oxide-based                active material and 10-50 wt.% support having a                spinel structure, where the support includes                magnesium aluminate. The iron oxide-based active                material has average size of less than 17                &#956; m, and includes needle-shaped ferric                trioxide hydrate having a specific surface area of                40-200 m2/g. The water decomposition material is                reduced by a reduction reaction (950&#176; C,                30 minutes) with a mixed gas (flow rate 100 l/min)                containing hydrogen and nitrogen in volume ratio of                1:9 to reduce the water decomposition                material.
   USE - The method is used for various purposes i.e.                carbon dioxide capture during combustion and                hydrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides excellent thermal                stability, shelf life and long-term performance to                product, can prevents performance degradation                during repeated redox reactions at high                temperatures, and provides excellent reactivity                with water and hydrogen production                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical roofing water decomposition method                comprises reducing the water decomposing material                by reacting the water decomposing material with                hydrogen and reacting the reduced water decomposing                material with water to regenerate the water                decomposing material. The water decomposition                material includes 50-90 wt.% iron oxide-based                active material and 10-50 wt.% support having a                spinel structure, and the support includes                magnesium aluminate. The iron oxide-based active                material has an average size of less than 17                &#956; m, and includes needle-shaped ferric                trioxide hydrate having a specific surface area of                40-200 m2/g. The water decomposition material is                reduced by a reduction reaction (950&#176; C,                30 minutes) with a mixed gas (flow rate 100 l/min)                containing hydrogen and nitrogen in volume ratio of                1:9 to reduce the water decomposition material. The                reduced water decomposition material is subjected                to an oxidation reaction (950&#176; C, 20                minutes) with a mixed gas (flow rate 100 l/min)                containing water and nitrogen in volume ratio of                1:9 to decompose the water to produce hydrogen, and                an oxidation-reduction reaction in which one cycle                of oxidizing and regenerating the reduced water                decomposition material is performed. The chemical                roofing water decomposition method has an oxidation                amount of 16-20 wt.% after 20 cycles measured by                equation-1 i.e. oxidation amount (wt.%) is equal to                [(W2-W1)/W1]x 100, where W1 is the initial mass of                the water-decomposing material, and W2 is the mass                of the water-decomposing material measured after 20                cycles.
TF EXAMPLE - The example provide details of preparation of                water decomposition material that do not support                the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC E31-A02C; J03-B; J04-E01; J04-E11; N02-A01; N06-E01; N07-J
IP B01J-021/10; B01J-023/00; B01J-023/745; B01J-035/02; B01J-035/10; B01J-037/00; B01J-037/04; B01J-037/08; C25B-001/04
PD KR2022141267-A   19 Oct 2022   B01J-023/745   202289   Pages: 14   
   KR2462123-B1   04 Nov 2022   B01J-023/745   202290

TI Supercritical enzymolysis compound extraction            method of essential oil dried orange peel extract            involves spraying enzymolysis solution on pulverized            orange peel material, performing enzyme inactivation,            steam-distilling obtained enzymolysis product, and            dehydrating obtained crude volatile oil.
AU WAN Q
   HE Y
   LUO P
   ZHAO H
   LI R
   CUI J
AE YUNNAN ZHONGBAOLONG NATURAL FLAVORS CO (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B04869
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical enzymolysis compound extraction                method of essential oil dried orange peel extract                involves pulverizing raw materials of dried orange                peel, dripping phosphate buffer to first                enzymolysis solution, spraying on pulverized orange                peel raw material, placing dried orange peel raw                material in supercritical kettle, introducing                carbon dioxide and performing enzyme inactivation                treatment, putting obtained first enzymolysis                product into steam distillation kettle, collecting                second distillate, separating second distillate by                oil-water separator, dehydrating obtained crude                volatile oil to obtain refined volatile oil and                drying third distilled slag, dripping sodium                hydroxide to second enzymolysis solution, spraying                on third slag and performing enzyme inactivation                treatment, reacting obtained second enzymolysis                product with organic solvent, concentrating                obtained fourth extracting solution, and mixing the                refined volatile oil with obtained concentrated                solution and homogenizing.
   USE - Supercritical enzymolysis compound extraction                method of essential oil dried orange peel                extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can release aroma substances and                transform aroma precursors, for improving richness                of aroma substances in the raw materials of dried                orange peel. The aroma substances in the raw                materials of orange peel can be extracted in                layers, for improving quality and yield of the                orange peel extract.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical enzymolysis compound extraction                method of essential oil dried orange peel extract,                involves drying and pulverizing raw materials of                dried orange peel, dripping phosphate buffer to                first enzymolysis solution, adjusting pH to 5-8 and                spraying on the pulverized orange peel raw                material, placing dried orange peel raw material in                supercritical kettle, introducing carbon dioxide                for enzymolysis and performing enzyme inactivation                treatment after enzymolysis to obtain first                enzymolysis product after drying, putting the first                enzymolysis product into steam distillation kettle,                feeding lower end of steam distillation kettle with                water vapor, connecting upper end with condenser to                collect second distillate, separating second                distillate by oil-water separator to obtain crude                volatile oil, dehydrating the crude volatile oil to                obtain refined volatile oil and drying third                distilled slag, dripping sodium hydroxide aqueous                solution to second enzymolysis solution, adjusting                pH to 8-9, spraying on the third slag, placing in a                closed enzymolysis tank for enzymolysis and                performing enzyme inactivation treatment to obtain                second enzymolysis product, placing the second                enzymolysis product in a reflux extraction tank,                adding 5-10 times the mass of an organic solvent,                soaking for 3-5 hours and heating under reflux for                2-3 hours to obtain fourth extracting solution,                sending fourth extracting solution into                concentration tank to be concentrated under reduced                pressure to obtain fifth concentrated solution, and                mixing the refined volatile oil with fifth                concentrated solution in a vacuum homogenizer                emulsifier at a mass ratio of 0.5-2:0-8 and                homogenizing.

The second                  enzymolysis solution is prepared by adding water                  with biological enzyme, where the biological                  enzyme is one or more of xanthine oxidase,                  dioxygenase, and lipoxygenase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  one of ethanol, ethyl acetate and petroleum                  ether.

PN CN114634265-A
TI Treating refractory organic wastewater treatment            by using optical Fenton process involves lifting or            self-flowing raw water to the adjustment tank, and            adjusting value, and then entering the high-efficiency            mixing tank, and mixing chemicals and wastewater to            improve the mass transfer efficiency.
AU ZHANG X
   PU X
   LUO Z
   HE Y
   ZHANG H
   CAI X
AE BEIJING ONYX ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022843181
AB    NOVELTY - Treating refractory organic wastewater                treatment by using optical Fenton process involves                lifting or self-flowing raw water to the adjustment                tank, and adjusting the pH value to 3-5, and then                entering the high-efficiency mixing tank. The                chemicals and wastewater are fully mixed to improve                the mass transfer efficiency in the high-efficiency                mixing tank, under the action of swirling aeration                and stirring, and then entered the oxidation                reaction zone. The multi-point dosing method is                used to uniformly mix the agent. The chain reaction                between ferric ions (Fe2+) and hydrogen peroxide                (H2O2) in the oxidation reaction zone catalyzes the                formation of hydronium ion (OH). The chain of                organic pollutants in wastewater is cut off, and                ring-opening to form small organic matter or direct                mineralization to carbon dioxide (CO2) and water                (H2O) to reduce organic matter content. The                divalent iron ions (Fe2+) have been converted into                ferric iron ions Fe3+.
   USE - Method for treating refractory organic                wastewater treatment by using optical Fenton                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces chemical sludge with iron                hydroxide as the main component by reusing iron                ions, and amount of waste is reduced by 60-80%,                which greatly reduces the cost of chemicals and                sludge treatment and disposal costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating refractory organic wastewater                treatment by using optical Fenton process involves                lifting or self-flowing raw water to the adjustment                tank, and adjusting the pH value to 3-5, and then                entering the high-efficiency mixing tank. The                chemicals and wastewater are fully mixed to improve                the mass transfer efficiency in the high-efficiency                mixing tank, under the action of swirling aeration                and stirring, and then entered the oxidation                reaction zone. The multi-point dosing method is                used to uniformly mix the agent. The chain reaction                between ferric ions (Fe2+) and hydrogen peroxide                (H2O2) in the oxidation reaction zone catalyzes the                formation of hydronium ion (OH). The chain of                organic pollutants in wastewater is cut off, and                ring-opening to form small organic matter or direct                mineralization to carbon dioxide (CO2) and water                (H2O) to reduce organic matter content. The                divalent iron ions (Fe2+) have been converted into                ferric iron ions Fe3+, which passed through the                reflux system and flow through the UV                photocatalytic reactor. The ferric ion (Fe) in the                UV photocatalytic reactor is re-converted into the                ferrous ion (Fe) under the excitation of the UV                high-intensity light quantum of the UV                photocatalytic reactor, and then returned to                previous step for reuse. The wastewater is                neutralized by the wastewater neutralization zone                and then enters the high-density sedimentation tank                for solid-liquid separation, and finally the                effluent is discharged up to the standard.

PN CN112778662-A
TI Improving performance of polyvinyl chloride rain boots with calcium carbonate whiskers, comprises e.g. adding dolomite to resistance furnace for high-temperature calcination to obtain calcined dolomite, and grinding calcined dolomite.
AU SHEN Q
   SHEN H
   HUANG Z
AE JIESHOU XUSHENG PLASTIC PROD CO LTD (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 202154919F
AB    NOVELTY - Improving performance of polyvinyl chloride (PVC) rain boots with calcium carbonate whiskers, comprises e.g. (i) adding dolomite to a resistance furnace for high-temperature calcination to obtain calcined dolomite, grinding the calcined dolomite into powder, adding to the ammonium salt solution, stirring and reacting for 40 minutes, filtering to remove the filter residue to obtain a reaction liquid, heating the reaction solution to 70-78 degrees C for 15 minutes, then pouring excess carbon dioxide into the reaction solution, reacting for 2 hours, filtering, washing, and drying to constant weight, (ii) adding the white carbon black to grinder, then adding diatomaceous earth and ethanol, evenly stirring, grinding for 40-50 minutes, and adding the compound bromide 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, continuously grinding for 1 hour, and recovering ethanol to obtain white carbon black composite bromide 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, and (iii) adding calcium carbonate whiskers.
   USE - The method is useful for improving performance of polyvinyl chloride rain boots with calcium carbonate whiskers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the hardness of the rain boots; improves the hardness of rain boots through the synergistic promotion effect of calcium carbonate whiskers and white carbon black compound 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole bromide; improves the wear resistance of rain boots; simply adds when calcium carbonate whiskers or white carbon black; reduces the hardness of rain boots, which is due to the agglomeration effect that occurs when calcium carbonate whiskers or white carbon black are directly added; and leads to uneven dispersion, which affects the performance of rain boots.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving performance of polyvinyl chloride (PVC) rain boots with calcium carbonate whiskers, comprises (i) adding dolomite to a resistance furnace for high-temperature calcination to obtain calcined dolomite, grinding the calcined dolomite into powder, adding to the ammonium salt solution, stirring and reacting for 40 minutes, filtering to remove the filter residue to obtain a reaction liquid, heating the reaction solution to 70-78 degrees C for 15 minutes, then pouring excess carbon dioxide into the reaction solution, reacting for 2 hours, filtering, washing, and drying to constant weight, (ii) adding the white carbon black to grinder, then adding diatomaceous earth and ethanol, evenly stirring, grinding for 40-50 minutes, and adding the compound bromide 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, continuously grinding for 1 hour, and recovering ethanol to obtain white carbon black composite bromide 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, (iii) adding calcium carbonate whiskers, white carbon black composite bromide 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, and PVC resin in sequence to twin-screw extruder, and extruding and granulating to obtain masterbatch, and (iv) adding the masterbatch obtained above to an injection molding machine, installing rain boot mold into mold cavity of an injection molding machine, performing injection molding, and demolding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the twin-screw extruder, the temperature in the first zone is 150 degrees C, the temperature in the second zone is 155 degrees C, the temperature in the third zone is 160 degrees C, the temperature in the fourth zone is 170 degrees C, the temperature in the fifth zone is 175 degrees C, and the mold temperature is 170 degrees C, the screw speed is 15 revolutions/minute (rpm), and the injection pressure of the injection molding machine is 105-108 Bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The high-temperature calcination is carried out at 780-810 degrees C for 45 minutes. Preferred Components: The ammonium salt solution is an ammonium sulfate solution with a mass fraction of 10 wt.%. The mixing ratio of the calcined dolomite and the ammonium sulfate solution is 150 g: 400 ml. The mixing ratio of the white carbon black, diatomaceous earth and ethanol is 120 g: 18-20 g: 150 ml. The ethanol is absolute ethanol. The mass ratio of the silica and 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole bromide is 15:1. The mixing weight ratio of the calcium carbonate whiskers, white carbon black composite bromide 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, and PVC resin is 30-35:4-6:98-105.

PN KR2247610-B1
TI Decomposing organic matter using microorganisms involves performing composting process by decomposing waste organic matter using microorganisms, and injecting the waste organic matter and microorganisms to be composted into a stirring tank.
AU KI K H
AE KI K H (KIKH-Individual)
GA 202143401X
AB    NOVELTY - Decomposing organic matter using microorganisms involves performing composting process by decomposing waste organic matter using microorganisms. The organic matter decomposition method using the microorganism. The waste organic matter and microorganisms are injected to be composted into a stirring tank provided in the organic matter treatment apparatus. The internal temperature of the stirring tank are maintained at 50 degrees C, and rotated the stirring blades provided in the stirring tank in a forward or reverse direction at predetermined time intervals while supplying oxygen into the stirring tank to stir for 10-24 hours. The carbon dioxide, water vapor, and unripe residue generated by the decomposition activity of waste organic microorganisms are discharged during the stirring process. The microorganisms includes Bacillus, protein, yeast, lactic acid bacteria and natto bacteria.
   USE - Method for decomposing organic matter using microorganisms.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves recyclability, and reduces generated odors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Decomposing organic matter using microorganisms involves performing composting process by decomposing waste organic matter using microorganisms. The organic matter decomposition method using the microorganism. The waste organic matter and microorganisms are injected to be composted into a stirring tank provided in the organic matter treatment apparatus. The internal temperature of the stirring tank are maintained at 50 degrees C, and rotated the stirring blades provided in the stirring tank in a forward or reverse direction at predetermined time intervals while supplying oxygen into the stirring tank to stir for 10-24 hours. The carbon dioxide, water vapor, and unripe residue generated by the decomposition activity of waste organic microorganisms are discharged during the stirring process. The microorganisms includes Bacillus, protein, yeast, lactic acid bacteria and natto bacteria. The waste organic matter and microorganisms to be composted are injected into a stirring tank provided in the organic matter treatment apparatus. The waste organic material and the microorganism are added in a weight ratio of 1:1. The organic matter treatment device has an installation space. A main body having an inlet port for introducing microorganisms and waste organic substances into the stirring tank on an upper surface, and an intake hole formed at one edge of the upper surface for sucking outside air. A microorganisms and waste organic matter are accommodated, the main outlet is formed on the side and the auxiliary outlet is formed on the front. The outside air sucked through the intake hole. An oxygen supply unit for supplying to the stirring tank. A deodorizing unit in which a composition for removing odor is stored to supply the composition for removing odor into the stirring tank. A protruding jaw formed to protrude from the bottom surface of the installation groove and into which an inlet pipe connected to the deodorizing portion is inserted and installed. A support frame having a spray hole for spraying water.

PN CN112279227-A
TI Chemical demagnesium for collophanite without tailings comprises e.g. mixing powdered rock phosphate with magnesium removing agent to carry out magnesium removing reaction, finishing reaction, and separating solid and liquid.
AU PAN Z
   CHEN B
   FAN X
   WU H
   AI X
   ZHANG H
   CHEN X
   YUAN L
AE HUBEI EZHONG ECOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202115585A
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical demagnesium for collophanite without tailings comprises (i) mixing powdered rock phosphate with magnesium removing agent to carry out magnesium removing reaction, finishing reaction, separating solid and liquid to obtain phosphorus concentrate and magnesium removing liquid, the magnesium removing agent is hydrochloric acid, and obtaining the recycled hydrochloric acid, (ii) introducing the carbon dioxide into sodium hydroxide solution to obtain sodium carbonate solution, (iii) reacting the calcium sulfate with sodium carbonate solution, finishing reaction, and solid-liquid separating to obtain sodium sulfate solution and light calcium carbonate, and (iv) detecting the mol ratio of calcium ions (Ca2+) ion and sulfate ion in the demagnesium liquid obtained in step (i), if it reaches 3: 2, executing the step (vii), or executing the step (v).
   USE - The method is useful for chemical demagnesium for collophanite without tailings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical demagnesium for collophanite without tailings comprises (i) mixing powdered rock phosphate with magnesium removing agent to carry out magnesium removing reaction, finishing reaction, separating solid and liquid to obtain phosphorus concentrate and magnesium removing liquid, the magnesium removing agent is hydrochloric acid, and obtaining the recycled hydrochloric acid, (ii) introducing the carbon dioxide into sodium hydroxide solution to obtain sodium carbonate solution, (iii) reacting the calcium sulfate with sodium carbonate solution, finishing reaction, and solid-liquid separating to obtain sodium sulfate solution and light calcium carbonate, (iv) detecting the mol ratio of calcium ions (Ca2+) ion and sulfate ion in the demagnesium liquid obtained in step (i), if it reaches 3: 2, executing the step (vii), or executing the step (v), (v) adding concentrated sulfuric acid in the demagnesium liquid obtained in the step (i), finishing the reaction, separating solid and liquid to obtain calcium diluted hydrochloric acid solution and calcium sulfate, (vii) mixing the calcium diluted hydrochloric acid solution with the concentrated hydrochloric acid to obtain recycled hydrochloric acid, and returning to the step (i), (vii) reacting the magnesium liquid and sodium sulfate solution, finishing reaction, and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain calcium sulfate and calcium chloride solution, (viii) adding ammonium phosphate magnesium seed crystal, adding ammonia to adjust the pH to 5-6, and solid-liquid separating to obtain nitrogen magnesium phosphate fertilizer, and separating phosphorus solution, (ix) separated phosphorus solution with sodium carbonate solution, finishing the reaction, separating solid and liquid to obtain magnesium carbonate and sodium chloride solution, where the sodium carbonate solution is from step (ii), and (x) concentrating the sodium chloride solution, and crystallizing to obtain sodium chloride.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for chemical demagnesium for collophanite without tailings (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), crushing the phosphorite is to the 80-100 powder to obtain powdered rock phosphate, dripping the magnesium removing agent in the powdered rock phosphate, the reaction time is 10-20 minutes, the concentration of the hydrogen chloride in the magnesium removing agent is 3-6 mol/L, the mass ratio of the magnesium removing agent and phosphorus ore powder is 1-5: 1. In the step (ii), the reaction temperature is 25-40 degrees C, and the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 20-40 wt.%. In the step (iii), the molar ratio of sodium carbonate to calcium sulfate is 0.8-1.2:1, and the reaction time is 90-120 minutes. In the step (v), the molar amount of the concentrated sulfuric acid is 0.9-1.1 times the molar amount of calcium ion ions in the demagging solution, and the reaction time is 10-20 minutes. In the step (vii), the concentration of the sodium sulfate solution is 2.5-5.35 mol/L, the molar amount of sulfate ions in the sodium sulfate solution is equal to the molar amount of calcium ions in the magnesium removal solution. In the step (viii), the seed crystal is magnesium ammonium phosphate synthesized by hot water method, adjusting the pH value with aqueous ammonia, the concentration of the aqueous ammonia is 15-25 wt.%, the reaction time is 10-20 minutes, and the reaction temperature is 10-20 degrees C In the step (ix), the molar amount of carbonate ion in the sodium carbonate solution is 0.9-1.1 times the molar amount of magnesium ions in the phosphorus evolution solution, the reaction temperature is 0-30 degrees C, and the reaction time is 10-30 minutes. In the step (x), detecting the concentration of sodium chloride in the sodium chloride solution, if it is greater than or equal to 30 wt.%, it is concentrated to 30-50% of the original volume and crystallized to obtain sodium chloride, if it is not less than 30 wt.%, and used as solvent to dissolve sodium hydroxide to obtain sodium hydroxide solution for use in step (ii). The method specifically comprises (1) grinding the phosphate rock to 80-100 mesh to obtain phosphate rock powder, mixing the phosphate rock powder with the demaggaging agent to carry out the demagnesia reaction, the reaction time is 10-20 minutes, and solid-liquid separation to obtain phosphate concentrate and demagnesia to obtain recycled hydrochloric acid, the concentration of hydrogen chloride in the magnesium removal agent is 3-6 mol/L, the mass ratio of the magnesium removal agent to phosphate rock powder is 1-5:1, (2) passing the carbon dioxide into sodium hydroxide solution, the reaction temperature is 25-40 degrees C to obtain sodium carbonate solution, the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 20-40 wt.%, (3) reacting calcium sulfate with sodium carbonate solution, the reaction time is 90-120 minutes, and solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium sulfate solution and light calcium carbonate, the molar ratio of the sodium carbonate to calcium sulfate is 0.8-1.2:1, the sodium carbonate solution is from step (ii), and the calcium sulfate is step (v) and step (vii), (4) detecting the molar ratio of calcium ions to sulfate ions in the demagnesium solution, if it reaches 3:2, performing step (7), or performing step (5), (5) adding concentrated sulfuric acid into the demagnesium solution obtained in step (1), the reaction time is 10-20 minutes, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain calcium precipitation dilute hydrochloric acid solution and calcium sulfate, the molar amount of the concentrated sulfuric acid is 0.9-1.1 times the molar amount of calcium ions in the magnesium removal solution, (6) mixing the dilute calcium precipitation hydrochloric acid solution with concentrated hydrochloric acid to obtain recycled hydrochloric acid and return to step (1), (7) reacting the magnesium removal solution with the sodium sulfate solution, completing the reaction, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain calcium sulfate and calcium precipitation solution, the sodium sulfate solution has concentration of 2.5-5.35 mol/L, the molar amount of sulfate ions in the sodium sulfate solution is equal to the molar amount of calcium ions in the magnesium removal solution, (8) adding seed crystals of magnesium ammonium phosphate synthesized by hot water method to calcium evolution solution, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH to 5-6, the reaction time is 10-20 minutes, and the reaction temperature is 10-20 degrees C, and performing olid-liquid separation to obtain nitrogen-magnesium phosphate fertilizer and phosphorus precipitation solution, the concentration of aqueous ammonia is 15-25 wt.%, (9) reacting the phosphorus precipitation solution with sodium carbonate solution, the reaction temperature is 0-30 degrees C, the reaction time is 10-30 minutes, and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain magnesium carbonate and sodium chloride solution, the molar amount of carbonate ion in the sodium carbonate solution is 0.9-1.1 times the molar amount of magnesium ions in the phosphorus evolution solution, and (10) detecting step (9) to obtain the concentration of sodium chloride in the sodium chloride solution, if it is greater than or equal to 30 wt.%, concentrating to 30-50% of the original volume, and crystallizing to obtain sodium chloride, if it is not less than 30 wt.%, using as solvent to dissolve sodium hydroxide to obtain sodium hydroxide solution for use in step (2).

PN CN111304671-A; CN111304671-B
TI Preparing strontium-doped barium titanate/zinc telluride photocathode material comprises e.g. adopting barium acetate, strontium acetate and tetrabutyl titanate as raw materials, and dissolving in acetic acid, ethanol and water solution.
AU XIONG X
   ZHANG X
   WANG Y
   WU C
   LI J
   LI T
   FU S
AE UNIV TAIZHOU (UYTZ-C)
GA 202058050D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing strontium-doped barium titanate/zinc telluride photocathode material comprises (1) adopting barium acetate, strontium acetate and tetrabutyl titanate as raw materials, dissolving in a mixed solution of acetic acid, ethanol and water, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone with a mass fraction of 10-30% and stirring for 24 hours to obtain a spinning solution, (2) adding the spinning solution to a 10ml syringe and set certain electrospinning process parameters, receiving the cured complex fiber on the fluorine-doped tin oxide (FTO) glass fixed on the roller, drying in an oven at 150 degrees C overnight, drying, performing calcination in a muffle furnace for a certain period of time, and carrying out thermal decomposition to obtain strontium-doped barium titanate thin film electrode, and (3) preparing a concentration of mixed water of zinc nitrate, sodium tellurium and sodium borohydride, dissolving, stirring uniformly, and transferring to 50 ml hydrothermal reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing strontium-doped barium titanate/zinc telluride photocathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can induce the selective separation of zinc telluride photogenerated charges, enhance the aggregation of photogenerated electrons at the catalyst/solution interface, and improve the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction activity of zinc telluride. The material has low manufacturing cost, high activity for carbon dioxide reduction, and good application prospect in the field of photoelectric catalysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing strontium-doped barium titanate/zinc telluride photocathode material comprises (1) adopting barium acetate, strontium acetate and tetrabutyl titanate as raw materials, dissolving in a mixed solution of acetic acid, ethanol and water, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone with a mass fraction of 10-30% and stirring for 24 hours to obtain a spinning solution, (2) adding the spinning solution to a 10ml syringe and set certain electrospinning process parameters, receiving the cured complex fiber on the Fluorine-doped tin oxide (FTO) glass fixed on the roller, drying in an oven at 150 degrees C overnight, drying, performing calcination in a muffle furnace for a certain period of time, and carrying out thermal decomposition to obtain strontium-doped barium titanate thin film electrode, (3) preparing a concentration of mixed water of zinc nitrate, sodium tellurium and sodium borohydride, dissolving, stirring uniformly, and then transferring to a 50 ml hydrothermal reactor, adding the strontium-doped barium titanate thin film electrode into the reaction kettle with the FTO conductive surface facing down, sealing and placing in a constant temperature drying box, and hydrothermally reacting for a certain time, taking out the FTO electrode, washing 3 times with water, and drying under vacuum at 60 degrees C for 8 hours, placing the film in a tube furnace, and performing calcination under nitrogen protection for a certain period of time strontium-doped barium titanate/zinc telluride photoelectrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the concentration of barium acetate is 5-100 mmol/l, the concentration of strontium acetate is 5-100 ummol/l, and the concentration of tetrabutyl titanate is consistent with that of barium acetate. The volume ratio of acetic acid, ethanol and water is 1:3:3-1:10:10. In the step (3), the concentration ratio of zinc nitrate, sodium tellurium and sodium borohydride is 10:10:1-1:1:1. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the electrospinning process parameters are the advancement speed of the syringe is 1-5 mm/h, the spinning voltage is 10-30 kV, the receiving distance is 5-10 cm, and the drum speed is 200-500 revolutions/minute. The calcination temperature in the muffle furnace is 400-700 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-4 hours. In the step (3), The hydrothermal temperature is 150-220 degrees C, and the hydrothermal time is 2-12 hours. The calcination temperature under nitrogen protection is 200-400 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-6 hours.

TI Recovering potassium and sodium salt from lithium            mica after lithium precipitation using ethanol            comprises roasting lithium mica ore with e.g. sodium            sulfate, adding e.g. potassium sulfate crystals,            evaporating, centrifuging and packaging.
AU WANG W
   MAO S
   ZHOU L
   ZHANG H
   LU C
   XUE L
   ZHANG J
   NAN J
   LIU J
   HUANG D
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
   JIANGXI NANSHI LITHIUM NEW MATERIAL CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202025879Q
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering potassium and sodium salt from                lithium mica after lithium precipitation using                ethanol comprises (1) roasting lithium mica ore                with e.g. sodium sulfate, impregnating in water,                purifying and adding carbon dioxide or sodium                carbonate to obtain lithium sink solution, (2)                introducing lithium-sinking liquid into settling                tank, adding ethanol and potassium sulfate                crystals, allowing to stand and filtering to get                crystal slurry I and filtrate A, (3) adding ethanol                to filtrate A, stirring and filtering to obtain the                crystal slurry II and alcohol-water mixture, (4)                introducing mixture into mechanical vapor                recompression (MVR) evaporation system, storing in                storage tank and completing water immersion and                purification process, (5) transferring crystal                slurry I and II to centrifuge to obtain crude                potassium sulfate and crude potassium sodium                sulfate and returning to step (2) and (6) drying                crude potassium sulfate, inspecting, packaging for                sale and drying and returning to step (1).
   USE - The method is useful for recovering potassium                and sodium salt from lithium mica after lithium                precipitation using ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes salting-out effect of                ethanol to cause potassium sulfate and potassium                sulfate sodium salt to supersaturate and                precipitate; has low energy consumption and easy                industrialization; and is environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering potassium and sodium salt from                lithium mica after lithium precipitation using                ethanol comprises (1) roasting lithium mica ore                with sodium sulfate, potassium sulfate and calcium                carbonate, impregnating in water, purifying to get                pre-liquid lithium and adding carbon dioxide or                sodium carbonate to precipitate lithium carbonate                to obtain lithium sink solution, (2) introducing                the lithium-sinking liquid into settling tank,                adding 14-16% ethanol after lithium precipitation                and 0.05-0.1% potassium sulfate crystals after                lithium precipitation below 20 degrees C, allowing                to stand for 28-35 minutes and filtering to get                crystal slurry I and filtrate A, (3) adding 18-20%                ethanol to the filtrate A, stirring evenly,                allowing to stand for at least 30 minutes and                filtering to obtain the crystal slurry II and                alcohol-water mixture, (4) introducing the                alcohol-water mixture into a mechanical vapor                recompression (MVR) evaporation system to evaporate                ethanol at 78-80 degrees C, storing in the storage                tank and completing the water immersion and                purification process after the liquid returns to                the water tank, (5) transferring crystal slurry I                and crystal slurry II to centrifuge for separation                to obtain crude potassium sulfate and crude                potassium sodium sulfate and returning the                supernatant discharged from the centrifuge to step                (2) and (6) drying the crude potassium sulfate,                inspecting, packaging for sale and drying and                returning the crude potassium sulfate and sodium                sulfate to the mixing and roasting process in step                (1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid after                  lithium deposition comprises 60-90 g/l,                  preferably 7-80 g/l sodium, 30-40 g/l, preferably                  30-35 g/l potassium and 2-4 g/l, preferably 3-3.5                  g/l lithium. The crude potassium sulfate                  comprises 14.5 wt.% sodium sulfate and 2 wt.%                  lithium sulfate. The crude potassium sodium                  sulfate comprises potassium sulfate and sodium                  sulfate in a mass ratio 26-27.5:68-70 and the                  amount of lithium sulfate in the crude potassium                  sodium sulfate is 4.5% or less. The crude                  potassium sulfate comprises 85.4 wt.% potassium                  sulfate, 12.8 wt.% sodium sulfate and 1.8 wt.%                  lithium sulfate and potassium sulfate sodium salt                  comprises 26 wt.% potassium sulfate, 69.8 wt.%                  sodium sulfate and 4.2 wt.% lithium                  sulfate.
