PT P
PN CN110550824-A
TI Biological treatment of industrial wastewater, by adding alkaline substance and anaerobic bacteria to sewage, fermenting, hydrolyzing, performing chemical oxygen demand detection, adding phosphorus-containing bacteria, disinfecting sewage.
AU TANG Y
AE OURUNJI ECOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ZHEJIANG CO LTD (OURU-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5942H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for biological treatment of industrial wastewater, involves (i) injecting industrial sewage to an anaerobic acidification reaction chamber, adding an alkaline substance to the sewage, controlling the pH of the sewage to 9-10, adding a fermentation anaerobic bacteria to the reaction chamber, fermenting in an oxygen-free environment for 7-14 days, hydrolyzing organic matters in the sewage, and realizing acidification of the sewage to obtain a large amount of acetic acid, (ii) removing pressure in the reaction chamber, introducing sewage into a methane fermentation chamber, adding anaerobic granular sludge, nutrient salt and methane bacteria to the sludge, continuously carrying out anaerobic fermentation in a methane fermentation chamber to generate methane and carbon dioxide gas, and collecting methane mixed gas, and (iii) continuously fermenting sewage, discharging the sewage, and filtering the fermentation precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for biological treatment of industrial wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method generates natural energy, improves the utilization rate of organic matter in sewage, and reduces the cost of sewage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for biological treatment of industrial wastewater, involves (i) injecting industrial sewage to an anaerobic acidification reaction chamber, adding an alkaline substance to the sewage, controlling the pH of the sewage to 9-10, adding a fermentation anaerobic bacteria to the reaction chamber, fermenting in an oxygen-free environment for 7-14 days, hydrolyzing organic matters in the sewage, and realizing acidification of the sewage to obtain a large amount of acetic acid, (ii) removing pressure in the reaction chamber, introducing sewage into a methane fermentation chamber, adding anaerobic granular sludge, nutrient salt and methane bacteria to the sludge, continuously carrying out anaerobic fermentation in a methane fermentation chamber for 7-14 days to generate methane and carbon dioxide gas, and collecting methane mixed gas through a professional device, (iii) continuously fermenting sewage in the methane fermentation chamber for 8-12 days, where the release amount of the methane is close to zero, discharging the sewage, filtering the fermentation precipitate of the sewage at the bottom of the methane fermentation chamber, and keeping the precipitate in the methane fermentation chamber for later use in sewage fermentation, (iv) subjecting the fermented sewage to chemical oxygen demand detection, introducing the sewage into an aeration tank, improving the oxygen content of the sewage, and promoting the propagation of aerobic bacteria, (v) introducing the aerated sewage into a phosphating tank, adding the phosphorus-containing bacteria and the phosphorus-accumulating bacteria to the phosphating tank, and synthesizing phosphate in the sewage under the environment of sufficient oxygen and storing the phosphate, and (vi) introducing sewage into a disinfection tank, adding grade sodium hypochlorite to the disinfection tank, adding activated carbon to the sewage, adsorbing and disinfecting the sewage, and discharging the sewage through secondary filtration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: In step (i), 30-40 kg alkaline substance is added to each ton of sewage, and the sewage is acidified at 40-46 degrees C. In the step (ii), the anaerobic granular sludge is added in an amount of 3-5%, and the temperature in the methane fermentation chamber is controlled at 37-40 degrees C. In step (iii), the sewage is continuously fermented 3 times to clear the bottom of the methane fermentation chamber, and a non-hydrolysate screening is performed on the retained precipitate to remove non-hydrolysable substances from the plastic. In step (vi), the sewage is left still in the disinfection tank for 1-2 days, and the sewage to be discharged is subjected to the chemical oxygen demand test.

PN CN110016547-A; CN110016547-B
TI Comprehensive utilization of yellow natrojarosite involves using sodium hydroxide, hydrochloric acid, ammonium chloride, sulfur dioxide to obtain industrially pure zinc oxide, adding extractant to obtain battery grade iron phosphate.
AU WANG K
AE WANG K (WANG-Individual)
   WANG K (WANG-Individual)
GA 201963758R
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of yellow natrojarosite involves adding yellow sodium jarosite to sodium hydroxide, calcining, adding pure water and stirring and washing to obtain wash water (I) and wash residue, adding washing residue to hydrochloric acid and ammonium chloride, obtaining filtrate and filter residue, introducing washing water and sulfur dioxide, obtaining arsenic trioxide and zinc-containing filtrate, obtaining arsenic trioxide and zinc-containing filtrate, passing zinc-containing filtrate into carbon dioxide, filtering to obtain industrial pure zinc oxide, adding filtrate to methyl isobutyl ketone extractant and back-extraction of phosphate solution to obtain iron phosphate, washing to obtain battery grade iron phosphate, reacting raffinate with ammonium sulfite to obtain cuprous chloride precipitate and cobalt-containing nickel solution, and raffinate through concentrating and crystallizing to obtain ammonium chloride crystal returning leaching with hydrochloric acid.
   USE - Comprehensive utilization method using yellow natrojarosite to produce industrially pure zinc oxide and battery grade iron phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The resource utilization process is simple, and short.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilization of yellow sodium jarosite involves adding yellow sodium jarosite to sodium hydroxide, calcining at 350-550 degrees C in a rotary kiln, obtaining a calcined material, cooling the calcined material and taking out, performing mechanical crushing, adding pure water and stirring and washing to obtain wash water (I) and wash residue (I), adding washing residue (I) to hydrochloric acid and ammonium chloride, reacting at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, filtering, obtaining filtrate (II) and filter residue (II), stirring washing water (I) for 30-60 minutes, introducing sulfur dioxide at a temperature of 130-200 degrees C and a pressure of 3-4 atm, and cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain arsenic trioxide and zinc-containing filtrate, passing the zinc-containing filtrate into the carbon dioxide, adjusting the pH of the solution to 7.5-8.5, filtering to obtain zinc precipitate and sodium-containing filtrate then calcining to obtain industrial pure zinc oxide, concentrating the obtained sodium-containing filtrate and crystallizing to obtain a mixed crystal of sodium sulfate and sodium carbonate, removing iron, adding filtrate (II) to the methyl isobutyl ketone extractant, extracting 3-5 times using 1-2 grade pure water wash and 2-3 grade phosphate solution back extraction to gives iron phosphate, washing to obtain battery grade iron phosphate, adding ammonium sulfite to raffinate, reacting at a temperature of 40-60 degrees C, obtaining a cuprous chloride precipitate and a solution containing cobalt nickel, extracting the cobalt-containing nickel solution into a P507 extraction line to obtain a cobalt salt solution and a nickel salt solution concentrating the raffinate and crystallizing to obtain ammonium chloride crystals which are leaching together with hydrochloric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the yellow sodium iron strontium to sodium hydroxide is 1:0.1-0.2. The molar ratio of copper ions to ammonium sulfite in the raffinate is 1:0.55-0.65. Preferred Condition: The calcination time is 1-2 hours, and air is introduced into the calcination process. The calcined material is pulverized through a 100 mesh sieve, and the mass ratio of the pulverized material to the pure water is 1:3-4. The mass ratio of the first washing slag to hydrochloric acid and ammonium chloride is 1:3-4:1-1.5. Preferred Property: The concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 3-4 mol/L. Preferred Process: In the zinc-containing precipitation calcination process, involves calcining at 700-850 degrees C for 1-2 hours, crushing and sieving, obtaining industrial grade zinc oxide. The pH of the phosphate solution is 2-3. The concentration of phosphate in the hydrochloride solution is 0.3-0.5 mol/L. In P507 extraction line extraction process, using aqueous ammonia for saponification. In the phosphate solution stripping process, stirring mixing time is 3-5 minutes, clarification time is 15-25 minutes, filtering and washing clarified aqueous phase. The conductivity of the washing to the washing water is 30 mu S/cm or less, and spray-drying washed washing slag to obtain a battery-grade iron phosphate. In the preparation process, adding the filter residue (II) is with sulfuric acid, passing sulfur dioxide, reacting at a temperature of 50-70 degrees C for 1-2 hour, adjusting the pH of the solution to 3-4, filtering, concentrating, and crystallizing to obtain industrial grade manganese sulfate crystals.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The components of the methyl isobutyl ketone extractant comprises 20-30 vol.% methyl isobutyl ketone and 70-80 vol.% octanol. The volumetric flow ratio of the filtrate (II), methyl isobutyl ketone extractant, pure water and phosphate solution is 3-5:1:0.1-0.2:0.2-0.5.

PN CN109805226-A
TI Preparation of healthcare drink used e.g. for delaying senility involves processing seepweed stem leaf and Suaeda salsa seeds, adding e.g. processed red date with water and honey, coarse filtering, ultrafiltering, and aseptic filling.
AU LI X
   LI H
AE PANJIN HONGHAI SUAEDA FOOD CO LTD (PANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201949441Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of healthcare drink comprises collecting stems and leaves of the fresh saline-alkali land seepweed stem leaf and purifying; cutting; adding deionized water, mixing, and pulping; high-precision wet superfine pulverizing; ultrasonic dynamic countercurrent circulation extracting with deionized water, adding cellulase and vitamin C, and enzymatic hydrolyzing; coarse filtering and ultrafiltering; collecting alkali-salted Suaeda salsa seeds, removing impurities, gravel, seed shell and dust, and magnetic separating; vacuum drying; pulverizing; supercritical carbon dioxide extracting; collecting separated matter, evaporating ethanol, and vacuum drying; centrifuging and mixing; mixing processing raw materials; taking red dates, coix seed, donkey-hide gelatin, lily, white fungus, and Aloe vera, removing impurities, crushing, mixing, adding water to cook, filtering, and taking supernatant; and mixing mixture and extract with water and honey, filtering, and filling.
   USE - Method for preparing healthcare drink is used for delaying senility, beautifying (claimed), delaying skin aging.
   ADVANTAGE - The drink smoothness and beautifies skin, removes human generated free radical, inhibits decomposition of collagen, has anti-wrinkle function, contains linoleic acid, selenium and flavone compound nutrient elements, and has comprehensive and balanced nutrition and obvious healthcare effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of healthcare drink comprises: (A) collecting stems and leaves of the fresh saline-alkali land seepweed stem leaf and purifying for 1-3 times to remove impurities and pesticide residues and kill bacteria; (B) cutting into 1-2 cm each; (C) adding deionized water at ratio of 1:0.5-1:2, mixing, and pulping; (D) high-precision wet superfine pulverizing; (E) ultrasonic dynamic countercurrent circulation extracting with deionized water at ratio 1:20-1:50, adding 2-4 wt.% cellulase and 0.05-0.08 wt.% vitamin C, and enzymatic hydrolyzing for 20-30 minutes at 30-50 degrees C and 50-200 W; (F) coarse filtering and ultrafiltering; (G) collecting alkali-salted Suaeda salsa seeds, removing impurities, gravel, seed shell and dust, and magnetic separating; (H) vacuum drying at 40-60 degrees C and 10-200 Pa for 3-6 hours; pulverizing; (I) supercritical carbon dioxide extracting at 8-10 MPa and 55 degrees C and 5-8 MPa and 35 degrees C for 2.5-4.5 hours; (J) collecting separated matter, evaporating ethanol; and vacuum drying at 40 degrees C; (K) centrifuging for 20 minutes and mixing; (L) mixing processing raw materials; (M) taking 35 wt.% red dates, 22 wt.% coix seed, 8 wt.% donkey-hide gelatin, 15 wt.% lily, 10 wt.% white fungus, and 10 wt.% Aloe vera, removing impurities, crushing, mixing, adding water to cook for 2-3 hours, filtering, and taking supernatant; and (N) mixing mixture and extract with water and honey, coarse filtering, ultrafiltering, and aseptic filling.
TF ACTIVITY - Nootropic; Dermatological.

PN CN109627734-A; CN109627734-B
TI Preparing lightweight, corrosion-resistant plastic comprises e.g. dispersing the titanium phosphate powder in deionized water, wet ball milling, adding nano-calcium carbonate powder, continue ball milling, spray-drying dispersion.
AU JIANG T
AE CHENGDU QIQIXIAOSHU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI NASU IND TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019385879
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lightweight, corrosion-resistant plastic comprises (i) dispersing the titanium phosphate powder in deionized water, wet ball milling, adding nano-calcium carbonate powder, continue ball milling, spray-drying dispersion, (ii) heating at a high temperature to discharge carbon dioxide generated by decomposition of calcium carbonate, (iii) adding deionized water, heating, adding p-toluenesulfonic acid, ultrasonically dispersing, adding oleic acid ethanol, reacting solution, filtering, washing and vacuum drying, (iv) dissolving in tetrahydrofuran, adding rare earth chloride, stirring and dispersing, heating to boiling under stirring, and refluxing, finishing the reaction, continuing heating to volatilize the solvent, and washing and drying, (v) mixing the oleic acid-modified calcium-doped porous titanium phosphate powder, rare earth chloride-modified polycarbonate, polycarbonate matrix resin, and antioxidant, and carrying out extrusion granulation.
   USE - The plastic is useful in pesticide spray drone casing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plastic: meet the requirements of light and pesticide corrosion resistance of the pesticide spraying drone shell material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lightweight, corrosion-resistant plastic comprises (i) dispersing the titanium phosphate powder in deionized water, wet ball milling for 2-3 hours, make the surface of the powder produce defects under stress, improving the reactivity, adding nano-calcium carbonate powder, continue ball milling for 20-40 minutes, spray-drying dispersion to obtain active titanium phosphate/calcium carbonate complex powder, (ii) heating the complex powder obtained in the step (i) at a high temperature to discharge carbon dioxide generated by decomposition of calcium carbonate to obtain calcium-doped active titanium phosphate powder having a porous structure, (iii) adding the calcium-doped active titanium phosphate powder obtained in the step (ii) to deionized water, heating to 70-80 degrees C, adding p-toluenesulfonic acid as a catalyst, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-30 minutes, and adding oleic acid ethanol, reacting solution for 3~4 hours, filtering, washing and vacuum drying to obtain oleic acid-modified calcium-doped porous titanium phosphate powder, (iv) dissolving the polycarbonate in tetrahydrofuran, adding rare earth chloride, stirring and dispersing for 10-20 minutes, heating to boiling under stirring, and refluxing for 4-5 hours, finishing the reaction, continuing heating to volatilize the solvent, and washing with deionized water and drying in vacuum to obtain rare earth chloride modified polycarbonate, (v) mixing the oleic acid-modified calcium-doped porous titanium phosphate powder, rare earth chloride-modified polycarbonate, polycarbonate matrix resin, antioxidant, and ultraviolet shielding agent obtained in the step (iii), and carrying out extrusion granulation by a twin-screw extruder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for lightweight, corrosion-resistant plastic prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Preferred Components: The raw materials in the step (i) are 25-30 pts. wt. titanium phosphate powder, 65-72 pts. wt. deionized water, and 3-5 pts. wt. calcium carbonate powder. The raw materials in the step (iii) are 20-25 pts. wt. calcium-doped active titanium phosphate powder, 44.5-58.8 pts. wt. deionized water, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. p-toluenesulfonic acid, and 6-10 pts. wt. oleic acid, and 15-20 pts. wt. ethanol. The rare earth chloride in the step (iv) comprises cerium chloride and lanthanum chloride. The raw materials in the step (iv) are 20-30 pts. wt. polycarbonate, 55-70 pts. wt. tetrahydrofuran, and 10-15 pts. wt. rare earth chloride. The antioxidant in the step (v) is tetrakis( beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acid)pentaerythritol ester, beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4- At least one of hydroxyphenyl) propionic acid n-octadecyl alcohol. The ultraviolet shielding agent comprises titanium dioxide, talc, and clay powder. The raw material in the step (v) is 10-15 pts. wt. oleic acid modified calcium-doped porous titanium phosphate powder, 15-20 pts. wt. rare earth chloride modified polycarbonate, and 60.2-72.5 pts. wt. polycarbonate matrix resin, 0.5-0.8 pts. wt. antioxidant, and 2-4 pts. wt. ultraviolet shielding agent. Preferred Method: The spray drying in the step (i), is carried out at 18000-25000 revolutions/minute (rpm), the diameter of the spray disk is 50-120 mm, the inlet temperature is 200-220 degrees C, and the outlet temperature is 85-88 degrees C. The high temperature heating in the step (ii) is carried out at 900-920 degrees C for 30-60 minutes.

PN BR102017015717-A2
TI Preparation of liquid/pasty coffee liquor involves grinding roasted green coffee beans using inert gas cooling mill, mixing finely milled coffee powder with vegetable oil, and storing resultant liquor in dry and ventilated environment.
AU DA CRUZ ABAURRE F
AE DA CRUZ A F (DCRU-Individual)
GA 2019331686
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of liquid/pasty coffee liquor involves roasting green coffee beans in a toaster equipped with a steam chamber pressurized with superheated steam at 180-240 degrees C for 7-15 minutes, cooling the roasted coffee beans in a fluidized bed sieve by introducing inert gas, such as argon, nitrogen or carbon dioxide, grinding the resultant coffee beans using an inert gas cooling mill at -20 to -100 degrees C, obtaining a finely milled coffee powder of 1-10 mu m, preferably 5 mu m, mixing the coffee powder with vegetable oil in a horizontal continuous turbo mixer, to obtain a pasty coffee liquor containing 10-90% vegetable oil, and 90-10% finely ground coffee powder of 5 mu m, storing in a hot water jacket and a mechanical stirrer storage tank at 24-32 degrees C, and volumetrically dosing the resultant liquid/pasty coffee liquor in a polyethylene pail using a doser and storing at 8-28 degrees C in a dry and ventilated environment.
   USE - Preparation of liquid/pasty coffee liquor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides liquid/pasty coffee liquor having excellent long-term fragrance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of liquid/pasty coffee liquor involves roasting green coffee beans in a toaster equipped with a steam chamber pressurized with superheated steam at 180-240 degrees C for 7-15 minutes, cooling the roasted coffee beans in a fluidized bed sieve by introducing inert gas, such as argon, nitrogen or carbon dioxide, preferably nitrogen, at -50 to -10 degrees C, storing the roasted coffee beans in a sealed storage bin in an inert gas atmosphere, leaving still at 5-25 degrees C, preferably at 15 degrees C, grinding the resultant coffee beans using an inert gas cooling mill at -20 to -100 degrees C, preferably at -30 degrees C, using mill, such as micronizer or hammer, obtaining a finely milled coffee powder of 1-10 mu m, preferably 5 mu m, storing the ground coffee powder in a sealed storage bin provided with an inert gas cooling system at 10-25 degrees C, preferably 15 degrees C, mixing the coffee powder with vegetable oil stored in a storage tank at 26-32 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C, in a horizontal continuous turbo mixer, to obtain a coffee liquor paste containing 10-90% vegetable oil, and 90-10% finely ground coffee powder of 5 mu m, storing in a hot water jacket and a mechanical stirrer storage tank at 24-32 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C, and volumetrically dosing the resultant liquid/pasty coffee liquor in a polyethylene pail using a doser and storing at 8-28 degrees C, preferably 18 degrees C in a dry and ventilated environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method further involves adding an aqueous solution of 40-90%, preferably 70% water, and 60-5%, preferably 30% polymer, and 3-20%, preferably 5% food additives. Preferred Component: The vegetable oil is one of palm oil, palm kernel oil, coconut oil, cottonseed oil, sunflower oil, corn oil or soybean oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polymer is one of dextrin, sugar, starch, gum or gelatin.

PN CN109381680-A
TI Preparation of zedoary turmeric oil spray by cleaning curcuma zedoary, draining water, drying, preparing into sheet, freeze drying, putting in low-temperature crushing device, crushing, and putting into extracting kettle.
AU ZHAO L
   WU H
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Y
AE TIBET FUTURE BIOLOGICAL MEDICINE CO LTD (TIBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019208502
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zedoary turmeric oil spray includes cleaning curcuma zedoary, draining water, drying, preparing into sheet with thickness of 0.5-1.0 mm, freeze drying until water content is 5-8%, putting in low-temperature crushing device at - 70 to - 60 degrees C, crushing to fineness of 300-500 mesh to obtain rhizoma zedoariae freeze-dried powder, putting into extracting kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction device, continuously extracting for 3 times, where first extraction is conducted at 25-30 MPa and 48.0 plus minus 0.3 degrees C for 60-65 minutes, second extraction is conducted at 25-30 MPa and 50.0 plus minus 0.3 degrees C for 60-65 minutes and third extraction is conducted at 25-30 MPa and 52.0 plus minus 0.3 degrees C for 90-100 minutes, collecting extract, mixing with polysorbate and injection water at 10:80-90:1.5-2.5 mass ratio, stirring uniformly, putting in high pressure homogenizer, and homogenizing at 20-25 MPa to obtain homogeneous liquid.
   USE - Preparation of zedoary turmeric oil spray.
   ADVANTAGE - The zedoary turmeric oil spray is convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of zedoary turmeric oil spray comprises cleaning curcuma zedoary, draining water, drying, preparing into sheet with thickness of 0.5-1.0 mm, freeze drying until water content is 5-8%, putting in low-temperature crushing device at - 70 to - 60 degrees C, crushing to fineness of 300-500 mesh to obtain rhizoma zedoariae freeze-dried powder, putting into extracting kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction device, continuously extracting for 3 times, where first extraction is conducted at 25-30 MPa and 48.0 plus minus 0.3 degrees C for 60-65 minutes, second extraction is conducted at 25-30 MPa and 50.0 plus minus 0.3 degrees C for 60-65 minutes and third extraction is conducted at 25-30 MPa and 52.0 plus minus 0.3 degrees C for 90-100 minutes, collecting extract, mixing with polysorbate and injection water at 10:80-90:1.5-2.5 mass ratio, stirring uniformly, putting in high pressure homogenizer, homogenizing at 20-25 MPa to obtain homogeneous liquid, mixing with water for injection at 1:2-4 mass ratio, stirring uniformly, putting in high pressure homogenizer, homogenizing at 30-35 MPa to obtain he second homogeneous liquid, mixing with water for injection at 1:2-3 mass ratio, stirring uniformly, putting in high pressure homogenizer, homogenizing at 40-45 MPa to obtain third homogeneous liquid, performing aseptic filtration and spray filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The freeze drying step is conducted at - 50 to - 40 degrees C and - 0.09 to - 0.10 MPa.

PN CN108998205-A
TI Sumac seed nucleolus oil refining method comprises e.g. collecting fresh and mold-free sumac seed, freezing, raking, screening, adding solvent, de-solvating and centrifuging.
AU WANG Z
AE WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
GA 2018A3558H
AB    NOVELTY - Sumac seed nucleolus oil refining method comprises e.g. collecting fresh and mold-free sumac seed, and then freezing at -30 degrees C to -10 degrees C for 1-15 hours, raking, screening through vibrating screen, collecting the lacquered husk skin layer and nucleolus separately, adding sumac seed nucleolus into the press for oil extraction to obtain sumac seed nucleolus virgin oil and sumac seed kernel cake, and collecting the virgin sumac seed nucleolus oil 1, further extracting the sumac nucleolus oil by adding solvent, de-solvating, and the desolvation and heating time is 25-50 seconds, subjecting the desolvation product to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, the extraction pressure is 20-30 MPa, centrifuging the extracted product at 3000-5000 revolutions per minute for 5-10 minutes, and taking the upper layer of oil to obtain sumac seed nucleolus oil 2, and mixing virgin sumac seed nucleolus oil 1 and sumac seed nucleolus oil 2.
   USE - The method is useful for sumac seed nucleolus oil refining.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, high purity; avoids denaturation of free urushiol and thus excellent color, quality and grease performance; reduces production cost; reduces the refining loss rate of the lacquer seed kernel oil; and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sumac seed nucleolus oil refining method comprises (i) collecting fresh and mold-free sumac seed, and then freezing at -30 degrees C to -10 degrees C for 1-15 hours, (ii) raking the sumac seed at a pressure of 1-8 kg for 5-15 minutes and a rotation speed of 20-200 revolutions per minute, (iii) screening through vibrating screen, collecting the lacquered husk skin layer and nucleolus separately, (iv) adding the sumac seed nucleolus into the press for oil extraction to obtain sumac seed nucleolus virgin oil and sumac seed kernel cake, and collecting the virgin sumac seed nucleolus oil 1, (v) further extracting the sumac nucleolus oil by adding solvent according to the ratio of solid to liquid ratio of the pressed eucalyptus seed cake by weight ratio of 1:5-20, (vi) de-solvating the solution after leaching in a high vacuum environment, the degree of vacuum is 10-150 Pa, the desolvation temperature is 70-90 degrees C, and the desolvation and heating time is 25-50 seconds, (vi) subjecting the desolvation product to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, the extraction pressure is 20-30 MPa, the extraction temperature is 40-45 degrees C, and the extraction time is 1-2 hours, (viii) centrifuging the extracted product at 3000-5000 revolutions per minute for 5-10 minutes, and taking the upper layer of oil to obtain sumac seed nucleolus oil 2, and (ix) mixing virgin sumac seed nucleolus oil 1 and sumac seed nucleolus oil 2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The desolvation step is performed by high vacuum evaporation. The high vacuum film evaporation method is implemented by a thin film evaporator. Preferred Components: The solvent in the step (v) is ethanol or two kinds of solvents mixed.

PN CN108384287-A
TI Manufacture of pigment carbon black using waste tire, by ball milling crushed waste tire, carrying out pyrolysis, adding sodium hydroxide, ultrasonic oscillating, freeze-drying, heating with e.g. dimethylformamide, spray-drying and milling.
AU LEI C
   SHI Y
AE LEI C (LEIC-Individual)
GA 201864250C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of pigment carbon black using waste tire comprises crushing waste tire, sieving, mixing sieve particle and auxiliary agent, ball milling, adding acetone, ball milling and collecting product; placing milled product into reactor, discharging gas in reactor using carbon dioxide, carrying out pyrolysis, cooling to room temperature, collecting pyrolysis mixture and standing mixture; adding pyrolysis mixture with sodium hydroxide solution, ultrasonic oscillating, placing into container under nitrogen atmosphere, adding glacial acetic acid and stirring; collecting mixture, freeze-drying and collecting dried product; heating dimethylformamide, dried product and tri-sulfo-tetra-phenyl porphyrin in reaction kettle, adding methanol and additive, continuously heating, cooling, adding tyrosine and tannin and insulating; and preserving heat, hot discharging, spray-drying, washing, putting into container, filling oxygen, pressurizing, stirring, collecting and milling to obtain product.
   USE - Method for manufacturing pigment carbon black using waste tire.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully utilizes waste tire and prevents environmental pollution, generation of waste resources and reduced oxygen content on surface of carbon black during high-temperature oxidation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of pigment carbon black using waste tire comprises crushing waste tire, sieving, collecting sieved particle, mixing sieve particle and auxiliary agent in mass ratio of 7:1-3, ball milling at 300 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes, adding acetone 15-20% by mass of sieve particles, ball milling at 400 revolutions/minute for 45-50 minutes and collecting product; placing ball milled product into reactor, discharging gas in reactor using carbon dioxide, heating to 756-763 degrees C for pyrolysis, cooling to room temperature, collecting pyrolysis mixture and standing mixture at 10-15 degrees C; adding pyrolysis mixture with sodium hydroxide solution in mass ratio of 1:4, ultrasonic oscillating, placing into container under nitrogen atmosphere, adding glacial acetic acid 40-45% by volume of sodium hydroxide solution and stirring at 80-90 degrees C; collecting mixture, freeze-drying, collecting dried product and preparing 90-150 pts. wt. dimethylformamide, 40-45 pts. wt. dried product, 23-27 pts. wt. tri-sulfo-tetra-phenyl porphyrin, 20-25 pts. wt. methanol, 18-22 pts. wt. tyrosine, 7-13 pts. wt. additive, 2-4 pts. wt. salicylic acid and 1-3 pts. wt. tannin; placing dimethylformamide, dried product and tri-sulfo-tetra-phenyl porphyrin into reaction kettle, heating at 145-155 degrees C for 1-2 hours, adding methanol and additive, continuously heating for 3-5 hours, cooling to 40-45 degrees C, adding tyrosine and tannin and insulating for 2-5 hours; and preserving heat, hot discharging, spray-drying, collecting spray-dried product, washing, putting into container, filling oxygen, pressurizing to 1.5-1.7 MPa, stirring at 70-80 degrees C for 4-6 hours, collecting and milling to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent comprises sodium carbonate and iron sulfate, in mass ratio of 4:1-3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive comprises nitrobenzene and boric acid, in mass ratio of 7:1.

PN CN108311692-A
TI Preparation of antioxidation copper iron clad by stirring copper ammonia solution, thermal coke, glyoxylic acid, tartrate, polyurethane and sodium carbonate in deionized water, heating, adding reduced iron powder, reducing and drying.
AU SHEN C
   HE W
   ZHANG J
AE FOSHAN JIUMO TECHNOLOGY INFORMATION CONSULTING CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201861231T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of antioxidation copper iron clad involves adding waste etching liquid of circuit board with deionized water and ammonia water to get copper ammonia solution; soaking iron powder in acetone, washing with water, placing in reaction kettle, adding deionized water and acrylate, reacting, filtering, drying, crushing and sieving to get reduction iron powder; adding cotton-pulp black liquor into kettle, sealing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and heating to get reaction material; mixing reaction material with calcium oxide, heating, digesting, filtering, placing residue in oven, and drying to get hydrothermal coke; stirring copper ammonia solution, thermal coke, glyoxylic acid, tartrate, polyurethane and sodium carbonate in deionized water to get chemical copper plating solution; and adding chemical copper plating solution into beaker, heating, putting reduced iron powder, reacting, filtering, placing precipitate in oven, putting dried ammonia, reducing and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing antioxidation copper iron clad.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves growth of particles, corrosion resistance and oxidation resistance of copper plating layer, and improves inorganic strong acid and alkali.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of antioxidation copper iron clad comprises:
   (A) taking 200-300 ml waste etching liquid of circuit board, and adding deionized water, adding ammonia water into waste etching liquid, and stirring to obtain copper ammonia solution;
   (B) taking 100-150 g iron powder, soaking into acetone, washing using deionized water for 2-3 times, placing in reaction kettle with stirrer, adding 20-25 mL deionized water into reaction kettle, adding 30-45 g acrylate, heating, reacting, cooling to room temperature, filtering, removing filtrate, separating, drying wet powder, putting into crusher, crushing and sieving to obtain reduction iron powder;
   (C) adding 200-250 mL cotton-pulp black liquor into gas valve of reaction kettle, sealing reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction kettle through gas valve, closing vent valve after aeration, heating, and keeping temperature for reaction to obtain reaction material;
   (D) mixing reaction material with calcium oxide, heating, digesting to obtain caustic slurry, filtering, separating and collecting filter residue, placing filter residue in oven, and drying to obtain hydrothermal coke;
   (E) adding 40-50 pts. wt. copper ammonia solution, 20-30 pts. wt. thermal coke, 15-20 pts. wt. glyoxylic acid, 60-70 pts. wt. tartrate, 40-50 pts. wt. polyurethane and 20-25 pts. wt. sodium carbonate into 400-500 pts. wt. deionized water, mixing and stirring to obtain chemical copper plating solution; and
   (F) adding 400-500 mL chemical copper plating solution into beaker, heating, putting 40-50 g reduced iron powder in beaker, reacting, filtering and separating to obtain precipitate, placing precipitate into oven, putting dried ammonia into decomposition hydrogen furnace, continuously reducing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of copper ions in waste etching solution is 0.3-0.5 mol/L. Preferred Method: The soaking in second step is done for 2-3 hours. The heating is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 30-35 minutes. The reduction step is done at 70-80 degrees C for 40-50 minutes.

PT P
PN CN108277639-A; CN108277639-B
TI Preparing quaternary silica sol doped silk comprises mixing e.g. ethyl orthosilicate and anhydrous ethanol, ultrasonically processing under irradiation, reacting, adding e.g. nano zinc borate, impregnating silk in obtained sol and pressing.
AU LAN T
AE YIZHOU ZHUANGZHIDU SILK HOME TEXTILES CO LTD (YIZH-Non-standard)
   YIZHOU ZHUANGZHIDU SILK HOME TEXTILES CO (YIZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018566489
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing quaternary silica sol doped silk comprises adding ethyl orthosilicate, anhydrous ethanol and purified water into a flask, adjusting the pH to 4.8-6.5, stirring at a speed of 200-500 revolutions/minute, ultrasonically processing at a power of 1200-2000 W, subjecting to infrared irradiation and reacting for 4-6 hours to obtain modified silica sol, adding silica sol and sodium chloride, placing into a high pressure container, heating to 50-63 degrees C, starting a stirrer, stirring, introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into the high pressure vessel, where the system pressure of the reaction container is 4-8 MPa, stirring, adding disodium hydrogen phosphate at a speed of 2 g/minute, adding nano zinc borate at a speed of 1.5 g/minute, continuously reacting for 2-3 hours to obtain quaternary silica sol, adding silk broadcloth into quaternary silica sol, impregnating, heating, ultrasonically processing, pressing by a small roller by double dipping pressure and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing quaternary silica sol doped silk.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the true silk wire limiting oxygen index, to improve the flame resistance of wire silk.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing quaternary silica sol doped silk comprises (a) preparing pure silica sol by adding 10 parts ethyl orthosilicate, 50-70 parts anhydrous ethanol and 200-300 parts purified water into a flask, adjusting the pH to 4.8-6.5, stirring at a speed of 200-500 revolutions/minute, ultrasonically processing at a power of 1200-2000 W, subjecting to infrared irradiation at a power of 500-800 W and reacting for 4-6 hours to obtain modified silica sol, (b) adding 100 parts silica sol and 0.1 parts sodium chloride, placing into a high pressure container, heating to 50-63 degrees C, starting a stirrer, stirring at a speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute, introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into the high pressure vessel, where the system pressure of the reaction container is 4-8 MPa, stirring, adding 4-8 parts disodium hydrogen phosphate at a speed of 2 g/minute, adding nano zinc borate at a speed of 1.5 g/minute, continuously reacting for 2-3 hours to obtain quaternary silica sol, and (c) adding 10 parts silk broadcloth into 500-700 parts quaternary silica sol, impregnating, heating to 40-48 degrees C, ultrasonically processing at a power of 800-1500 W for 10-20 minutes, pressing by a small roller by double impregnation pressure and drying to obtain doped silk.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas containing carbon dioxide comprises carbon dioxide, nitrogen and chlorine in a volume ratio of 10-20:3:0.1. The particle diameter of the silica sol in the steps (a) or (b) is 20-100 nm. The sodium chloride in the step (b) can be replaced by sodium bicarbonate. The drying process in the step (c) comprises drying at 45 degrees C for 25-36 minutes, and then drying for 10-15 minutes at 75 degrees C and finally drying for 2 minutes at 110 degrees C. In the step (c): the double rolling three impregnation pressure is 75-85%.

PN KR2018014909-A; KR1869448-B1
TI Cement burning facility useful in fluorinated gas treatment, comprises e.g. preheater system having precalciner to increase decarbonation reaction and preheat cement raw material feed from raw material feeder and rotary kiln to form flame.
AU KIM H S
   LEE S D
   LEE J Y
AE SUNG SHIN CEMENT IND CO LTD (SUNG-Non-standard)
GA 201813824K
AB    NOVELTY - Cement burning facility comprises a preheater system having a precalciner for increasing the decarbonation reaction in which carbon dioxide in the raw material is liberated and preheating the cement raw material supplied from a raw material feeder, a rotary kiln for generating flame in the direction of an inlet port from a burner provided at a position opposite to the inlet port for burning, and a cooler having an air flow partition plate and a partition wall for guiding the air flow to be sucked into the outlet port of the kiln and cooling the clinker by multiple air injection units.
   USE - The facility is useful in fluorinated gas treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The facility enables treatment of fluorine gas in large capacity and simultaneously produces cement additives, and is environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cement burning facility comprises a preheater system (30) having a precalciner (31) for increasing the decarbonation reaction in which carbon dioxide in the raw material is liberated and preheating the cement raw material supplied from a raw material feeder, a rotary kiln (40) for generating flame in the direction of an inlet port from a burner (50) provided at a position opposite to the inlet port for burning, and a cooler (60) having an air flow partition plate and a partition wall for guiding the air flow to be sucked into the outlet port of the kiln and cooling the clinker by multiple air injection units. The raw material passed through the preheater system is introduced into the inlet port, fired in a clinker and taken out by an outlet port. The preheater system, rotary kiln or the cooler have a supply part for supplying hydrogen source and fluorinated gas. The preheater system and the rotary kiln are sealingly connected and the rotary kiln and the cooler are sealed. The fluorinated gas contains perfluorocarbon, hydrofluorocarbon and/or chlorofluorocarbon. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating fluoride gas, comprising (a) preheating the cement raw materials supplied from the raw material supplier into the preheater system having the precalciner, (b) charging and discharging the raw materials passed through the preheater system in the rotary kiln having the burner into an inlet port and burning the clinker with combustion heat of the burner to carry to the outlet port, and (c) cooling the clinker supplied from the kiln according to the moving step. The steps (a), (b) and (c) are carried out by supplying source of hydrogen and a source of fluorine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the facility.
   Preheater system (30)
   Precalciner (31)
   Rotary kiln (40)
   Burner (50)
   Cooler (60)

PN US10106753-B1
TI Coal gasification process and carbon dioxide conversion process comprises e.g. producing raw syngas from gasifier heating coal producing syngas product, removing carbon dioxide and recovering syngas carbon neutral heat energy.
AU GRAHAM U M
   DAVIS B H
AE GRAHAM U M (GRAH-Individual)
   DAVIS B H (DAVI-Individual)
GA 2018829524
AB    NOVELTY - Coal gasification process and carbon dioxide conversion process comprises: producing a raw syngas from gasifier heating coal producing a syngas product having a temperature of 800-1200 degrees C; purifying the syngas product containing the carbon dioxide with a purification unit fluidly coupled to the gasifier; removing the carbon dioxide from the syngas product with a carbon dioxide separation/recovery unit fluidly coupled to the purification unit; compressing the carbon dioxide with a compressor fluidly coupled to the carbon dioxide separation/recovery unit forming a concentrated carbon dioxide product forming a compressed carbon dioxide product; cooling and reducing the temperature of the syngas product with a heat exchanger fluidly coupled to the gasifier; and recovering syngas carbon neutral heat energy removed from the syngas product during cooling with the heat exchanger.
   USE - The process is useful for coal gasification process and carbon dioxide conversion process (claimed) for fuels production.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides oxygen from carbon dioxide produced in gasifier and feeds the oxygen back into the gasification process eliminating carbon dioxide along the way and making the gas-to-liquids process less carbon dioxide intensive; and directs the oxygen back to the gasifier to minimize or eliminate the need for an oxygen plant, while the carbon by-product (granular carbon) will be used for a variety of adsorbents for environmental applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coal gasification process and carbon dioxide conversion process comprises: producing a raw syngas from gasifier heating coal producing a syngas product having a temperature of 800-1200 degrees C, where the gasification process forms syngas product containing a carbon dioxide where a gasifier for use in the process is operated at 15-1500 psi and 750-1200 degrees C; purifying the syngas product containing the carbon dioxide with a purification unit fluidly coupled to the gasifier; removing the carbon dioxide from the syngas product with a carbon dioxide separation/recovery unit fluidly coupled to the purification unit; compressing the carbon dioxide with a compressor fluidly coupled to the carbon dioxide separation/recovery unit forming a concentrated carbon dioxide product forming a compressed carbon dioxide product; cooling and reducing the temperature of the syngas product with a heat exchanger fluidly coupled to the gasifier; and recovering syngas carbon neutral heat energy removed from the syngas product during cooling with the heat exchanger and using the syngas carbon neutral heat energy to drive a conversion/disassociation of the carbon dioxide product into a carbon product and an oxygen product by circulating at least a portion of the syngas carbon neutral heat energy through a molten salt reactor fluidly coupled to the heat exchanger unit and the gasifier by heating the molten salt reactor to a selected temperature utilizing the carbon neutral heat energy generated from the heat exchanger from cooling a least a portion of the syngas product, the carbon neutral heat energy producing at least a portion of the heat necessary to operate the molten salt reactor, feeding the compressed carbon dioxide product to the molten salt reactor, converting and disassociating the compressed carbon dioxide product with the molten salt reactor producing carbon by-product collecting on an anode of the molten salt reactor and a pure oxygen product released at a cathode of the molten salt reactor, feeding the pure oxygen product produced in the molten salt reactor to the gasifier and removing the carbon by-product produced in the molten salt reactor.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2021072173-A1; US11340182-B2
TI Breathing apparatus has tube which is connected to breathing mask and first flow control valve and second flow control valve are controlled by processing module in response to concentration of carbon dioxide determined by processing module.
AU SPENGLER C
   SCHAER C
   STUTZER D
   MAURER D
   SALLAZ A
   DILL D H
   MAURBERGER M
   SCHOLZ A
AE IDIAG AG (IDIA-Non-standard)
GA 202127921C
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a tube (101) with a proximal end (102) connected to a breathing mask and a distal end (103) connected to a splitter. A first branch with a proximal end connected to the splitter and an open distal end. A first sensor is operatively connected to a processing module (120). A first flow control valve is arranged in the first branch and operatively connected to the processing module. A second branch with a proximal end connected to the splitter and a distal end connected to an inflatable reservoir. A second flow control valve is arranged in the second branch and operatively connected to the processing module. The first sensor is a gas sensor enabling the processing module to determine a concentration of carbon dioxide. The first flow control valve and the second flow control valve are controlled by the processing module in response to the concentration of carbon dioxide determined by the processing module.
   USE - Breathing apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal conductivity sensing elements are small and arranged close together, e.g. within an area of 4 mm 2 to reduce power consumption and improve dynamic behavior. Exhaled air is thus trapped between the entry flap and the exit flap and can be accurately analyzed without having to account for the influence of air movement on the power required to maintain a particular sensing element's temperature. Reduced power consumption is particularly advantageous when the sensor is used in battery-powered, mobile devices. Primarily, the multi-element sensor has better dynamic behavior, avoiding the delay that is inherent to a single-element sensor which requires time for heating and cooling the sensing element to different temperatures.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the respiratory gas sensing system.
   Tube (101)
   Proximal end (102)
   Distal end (103)
   Processing module (120)
   Processor (130)

PN US2021053871-A1; WO2021035106-A1; CA3152252-A1
TI Producing separated ammonia and hydrochloric acid            from ammonium chloride, comprises e.g. reacting            ammonium chloride with zinc oxide to form zinc            chloride, ammonia and water, and reacting zinc chloride            with sulfuric acid to form zinc sulfate.
AU NOVEK E J
AE INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
   INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 202120759J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) separated ammonia and                hydrochloric acid from ammonium chloride,                comprises: reacting ammonium chloride with zinc                oxide to form a zinc chloride, ammonia, and water;                reacting the zinc chloride with sulfuric acid to                form a zinc sulfate and hydrochloric acid; and                thermally decomposing the zinc sulfate to produce                zinc oxide.
   USE - The process is useful for: producing separated                ammonia and hydrochloric acid from ammonium                chloride; producing sodium bicarbonate or sodium                carbonate from sodium chloride (all claimed);                preparing zinc oxide and other substances;                producing calcium oxide during the production of                phosphoric acid from calcium phosphate; producing                ammonia from ammonium chloride; and producing other                carbonate or bicarbonate salts.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: converts ammonium chloride and/or                ammonium sulfate, which may be waste products or                low cost, into valuable free ammonia (e.g. ammonium                hydroxide solution or gaseous ammonia or anhydrous                ammonia); produces value from excess or low-cost                natural gas or flare gases using the heat from                flaring to produce valuable ammonia and                hydrochloric acid; may consume less energy in their                endothermic reaction steps than is required during                the production of ammonia from natural gas; does                not require an air separation unit or                post-combustion carbon dioxide capture to produce                pure carbon dioxide; employs to regenerate calcium                oxide from calcium carbonate or similar carbonate                or bicarbonate molecules; and enables the                production of calcium oxide without the calcining                of calcium carbonate while producing valuable                phosphoric acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing (P2) sodium bicarbonate or sodium                carbonate from sodium chloride, comprising:                reacting sodium chloride with ammonia, carbon                dioxide, and water to form sodium bicarbonate and                ammonium chloride; reacting ammonium chloride with                zinc oxide to form zinc chloride, ammonia, and                water; reacting the zinc chloride with sulfuric                acid to form zinc sulfate and hydrochloric acid;                and decomposing the zinc sulfate to produce zinc                oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process (P1): the                  step of thermal decomposing zinc sulfate further                  produces sulfur dioxide, or diatomic oxygen,                  and/or sulfur trioxide; the step of thermal                  decomposing zinc sulfate further produces sulfur                  dioxide, where the process further comprises                  reacting sulfur dioxide with diatomic oxygen to                  produce sulfur trioxide; at least a portion of                  the formed zinc sulfate and/or the formed zinc                  chloride are dissolved in the hydrochloric acid,                  where the process further comprises separating at                  least a portion of (1) the dissolved zinc sulfate                  or (2) the dissolved zinc chloride, or (3) the                  combination of (1) and (2) from the hydrochloric                  acid; the step of separating comprises employing                  a membrane based process, distillation,                  electrodialysis, ion exchange, crystallization,                  precipitation, salting-out and/or common-ion                  effect; the step of reacting ammonium chloride                  with zinc oxide is conducted at a temperature of                  greater than 200degrees Celsius, where both the                  ammonium chloride and zinc oxide are in solid                  form; the step of reacting ammonium chloride with                  zinc oxide is conducted at a temperature of                  greater than 200degrees Celsius or greater than                  or equal to 338degrees Celsius, where the                  ammonium chloride comprises a gaseous mixture of                  ammonia and hydrochloric acid; the step of                  reacting ammonium chloride with zinc oxide is                  conducted in a low diatomic oxygen environment;                  and the ammonium chloride is sourced from a                  process for producing sodium bicarbonate or                  sodium carbonate. The process (P1) further                  comprises: reacting the sulfur dioxide, diatomic                  oxygen and/or sulfur trioxide with water to form                  sulfuric acid; reacting the formed sulfuric acid                  with zinc chloride; condensing the gaseous water                  vapor; condensing the gaseous ammonia and the                  gaseous water vapor; separating at least a                  portion of zinc sulfate from at least a portion                  of hydrochloric acid; and producing sodium                  bicarbonate or sodium carbonate using a process                  employing the ammonia and/or water. In the                  process (P2), the step of decomposing zinc                  sulfate further comprises forming sulfur dioxide,                  diatomic oxygen, and/or sulfur trioxide. The                  process (P2) further comprises: reacting the                  formed sulfur dioxide, formed diatomic oxygen,                  and/or formed sulfur trioxide with water to                  produce sulfuric acid; reacting the produced                  sulfuric acid with zinc chloride to form zinc                  sulfate and hydrochloric acid; decomposing the                  formed sodium bicarbonate to form carbon dioxide,                  and sodium carbonate and/or sodium                  sesquicarbonate; reacting the formed carbon                  dioxide with sodium chloride, ammonia, and water                  to form sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride;                  recycling the formed gaseous ammonia and/or                  gaseous water vapor; dissolving the formed sodium                  bicarbonate in the ocean; dissolving the formed                  sodium carbonate and/or formed sodium                  sesquicarbonate in the ocean; and employing                  carbon dioxide captured from at least one carbon                  dioxide emission source and/or from air.Preferred Components: In the process (P1):                  the ammonia comprises gaseous ammonia and the                  water comprises gaseous water vapor; at least a                  portion of the zinc chloride and/or the zinc                  sulfate are in a solid phase; at least a portion                  of sulfuric acid and/or hydrochloric acid are in                  a liquid phase; and the low diatomic oxygen                  environment comprises less than 1 vol.% diatomic                  oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The total power generation efficiency of the system reaches more than 60%.

PT P
PN CN109912078-A
TI Air-alkaline ammonia nitrogen stripping device comprises stripping tower, where upper portion of stripping tower is provided with ammonia nitrogen sewage inlet pipe.
AU JIANG Z
AE UNIV FUJIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYFI-C)
GA 2019572809
AB    NOVELTY - Air-alkaline ammonia nitrogen stripping device comprises stripping tower, where upper portion of the stripping tower is provided with an ammonia nitrogen sewage inlet pipe, and an air intake pipe and a drain pipe are arranged at the bottom of the blowing tower and air outlet is provided at the top of the tower. The intake pipe at the bottom of the stripping tower is connected with an alkali liquid tank, and the alkali liquid tank is connected with an air pump for introducing air. The lye tank is also connected with a lye tube, the lye tube is connected with the ammonia nitrogen sewage inlet pipe, and the lye tube is provided with an lye pump. The lye tank is filled with a sodium hydroxide solution. The upper portion of the stripping tower is provided with a uniform water distributor, the middle portion of the stripping tower is provided with a packing, and the lower portion of the stripping tower is provided with a water collecting device.
   USE - Air-alkaline ammonia nitrogen stripping device.
   ADVANTAGE - The air-alkaline ammonia nitrogen stripping device avoids scale generation during the soda lime pH adjustment process, reduces carbon dioxide concentration in the stripping tower, reduces dosage of alkali to reduce the running cost, and improves blowing efficiency, and lowering cost of ammonia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for stripping air-alkaline ammonia nitrogen, which involves:
   (A) charging air into the alkali solution, filtering out carbon dioxide, mixing alkali solution into the ammonia nitrogen sewage and mixing into the stripping tower;
   (B) entering air that filters out carbon dioxide to stripping tower from the bottom; and
   (C) blowing off air from ammonia nitrogen sewage, and blowing out ammonia gas from the top of the stripping tower into the exhaust gas collecting device, and absorbing by the water in the exhaust gas collecting device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of air-alkaline ammonia nitrogen stripping device.
   Alkali adding pump (4)
   Inlet pipe (5)
   Air inlet tube (6)
   Stripping tower (7)
   Distributor (701)
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

PN CN109618809-A; CN109618809-B
TI New Hypholoma fasciculare E140855 strain useful for preventing fungi and protecting forest, inhibiting growth of multiple species of Armillaria.
AU HU H
   XIE Y
   LIU Y
   MO W
   LIANG X
AE GUANGDONG MICROBIOLOGY GUANGDONG DETECTI (GDIN-C)
   GUANGDONG YUEWEI EDIBLE MUSHROOM TECHNOL (GDIN-C)
GA 201937750K
AB    NOVELTY - Hypholoma fasciculare E140855 strain having preservation number of CCTCC No M2018292, is new.
   USE - The strain is useful for preventing fungi, preferably Armillaria and protecting forest (claimed), inhibiting growth of multiple species of Armillaria.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for artificial cultivating of clustering silkworm species, comprising (i) inoculating mycelium culture into the production of mycelium species in clusters into the culture bag, culturing at 25 degrees C, shading and culturing having humidity of 50-60%, maintaining the carbon dioxide concentration below 4000 ppm until the mycelium is full of cultivation bags, where the cultivation bag is prepared by loading artificial domestication culture base into a polypropylene strain bag, where the composition of the artificial domestication medium includes 30-31 wt.% cottonseed hull, 57-58 wt.% wood chips, 10 wt.% bran, 1-2 wt.% calcium carbonate, and where the moisture content of the artificial domesticated medium is 60-65%, (ii) when the matured mycelium is overgrown with the cultivation bag, placing in the blackout at 25 degrees C for 15-25 days for post-ripening treatment, (iii) opening the cap and removing the aging mycelium layer on the surface layer by 0.3-0.5 cm, (iv) carrying out primordial formation management, placing the product obtained in the step (iii) on the cultivation bag, covering cap, reposition at 20-25 degrees C, maintaining the carbon dioxide content to below 1%, adjusting the relative humidity of the air to over 90%, removing the cap after 5-7 days, placing the cultivation bags in vertical rows, when the hyphae begin to kink and form pale yellow rice bran-shaped primordium, (v) after the primordium grows to 0.5 cm, controllling temperature to 20-22 degrees C, where the relative humidity of the air is 80-90%, and increasing the ventilation and maintaining the carbon dioxide concentration at 350-1500 ppm, using 9 hours of light per day, light intensity is 300-500 lx, when the clusters grow into a mushroom along the silk umbrella until the size of the child entity is unchanged, and harvesting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), in the primordium formation management, maintaining the primordium in relative humidity, and water is not directly spraying onto the primordium. The clustered species along the silkworm is prepared by inoculating of the tufts along the silkworm production mother seed into the production medium, when inoculation, ensuring that the parent material block, burying in the production medium and placing in a dark culture at 25 degrees C, when the hyphae is full, producing the tufts along the silk umbrella, where the composition of the production medium includes 98-99 wt.% grain Sorghum and 1-2 wt.% calcium carbonate. The tufting along the silk umbrella production mother is prepared by planting isolated and purified tufts along the species of the silkworm into the production mother medium, placing in dark culture at 25 degrees C, when the hyphae grows over the slope, producing the tufts along the silk umbrella, and production mother medium is enriched integrated PDA medium. The isolated and purified tufted species is obtained by tearing the collected wild tufts along the surface of the silk umbrella fruit body, connecting fungus tissue to the isolated parental medium and placing in a dark culture at 25 degrees C, when the hyphae are overgrown, transferring into the purified parental medium and dark culturing at 25 degrees C, picking the tip hyphae, when the mycelium grows and the bacteria have not grown, picking tip hyphae to obtain isolated and purified clusters. The isolated parental culture medium is integrated PDA. The purified parental medium is Bengal red culture medium.
   ACTIVITY - Fungicide.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN109264686-A; CN109264686-B
TI Enriching phosphorus concentrate with medium-low            grade phosphate rock or phosphorus tailings while            recovering calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide            includes calcining medium and low grade phosphate rock,            mixing, reacting and drying.
AU ZHANG Q
   ZHANG F
   LU Y
   CEN Z
   ZHANG L
   HAN Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG X
   LI R
   DENG Y
AE GUIZHOU SHENGYUAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU CHEM IND RES INST (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU SHENGYUAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201912028R
AB    NOVELTY - Enriching phosphorus concentrate with                medium-low grade phosphate rock or phosphorus                tailings while recovering calcium carbonate and                magnesium hydroxide comprises calcining medium and                low grade phosphate rock, adding calcined slag and                ammonium nitrate solution to the reaction tank,                mixing, reacting to obtain ammonia gas, filtering                to obtain leachate A and leaching residue A,                mixing, reacting leachate A by introducing ammonia                and carbon dioxide, filtering to obtain leachate B,                drying, adding leaching slag A and ammonium nitrate                solution to the reaction tank, mixing, reacting,                separating to obtain leaching liquid C, filtering                leaching slag C and ammonia gas, enriching leaching                slag C with phosphorus concentrate, adding ammonia                or aqueous ammonia to leachate C, mixing, reacting,                separating to obtain leachate D, repulping,                washing, separating, and drying.
   USE - Method of enriching phosphorus concentrate                with medium-low grade phosphate rock or phosphorus                tailings while recovering calcium carbonate and                magnesium hydroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles calcium and magnesium and                does not produce new tailings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enriching phosphorus concentrate with                medium-low grade phosphate rock or phosphorus                tailings while recovering calcium carbonate and                magnesium hydroxide comprises calcining medium and                low grade phosphate rock at 700-1200 degrees C to                obtain calcined slag and carbon dioxide, adding                calcined slag and ammonium nitrate solution to the                reaction tank, mixing, reacting to obtain ammonia                gas, filtering to obtain leachate A and leaching                residue A, mixing, reacting leachate A by                introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide, filtering                to obtain leachate B, drying the leaching residue B                to obtain calcium carbonate product, adding                leaching slag A and ammonium nitrate solution to                the reaction tank, mixing, reacting, separating to                obtain leaching liquid C, filtering leaching slag C                and ammonia gas, enriching leaching slag C with                phosphorus concentrate by drying, adding ammonia or                aqueous ammonia to leachate C, mixing, reacting,                separating to obtain leachate D by filtration,                repulping leaching residue D, washing, separating,                and drying. The leachate B is ammonium nitrate                solution. The leachate D is ammonium nitrate                solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The calcination                  temperature is 700-1200 degrees C. The                  calcination time is 1-2 hours. Preferred                  Components: The ammonium nitrate solution has                  concentration of 5-50%. The carbon dioxide has                  concentration of 15-35%. The aqueous ammonia                  solution has concentration of 5-25%.

PT P
PN CN109010250-A
TI Halloysite-silica composite aerogel material has            nano and micro pores, and is obtained by adding            methacryloyloxydodecyltrimethylammonium chloride to            halloysite-silica composite gel, and supercritically            drying using carbon dioxide.
AU LI H
   SONG L
   LIU H
   ZHOU J
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2018A5446R
AB    NOVELTY - A halloysite-silica composite aerogel material                has average specific surface area of 600-608 m2/g,                and nano and micro pores. The nano pores have                average size of 10-12 nm. The micro pores have                average size of 2-4 mu m. The halloysite-silica                composite aerogel material is obtained by adding                0.05-2 pts. wt.                methacryloyloxydodecyltrimethylammonium chloride to                halloysite-silica composite wet gel, maintaining,                placing resultant product in carbon dioxide                supercritical high pressure extraction unit, and                supercritically drying with carbon dioxide as                medium at 30-60 degrees C under pressure of 5-15                MPa. Polydivinylsiloxane has number average                molecular weight of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000,                and vinyl content of 0.1-5%, and is chosen from                amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane or                amino double-terminated                polydimethyldivinylsiloxane.
   USE - Halloysite-silica composite aerogel                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The halloysite-silica composite wet gel is                obtained by adding 0.5-20 pts. wt. halloysite                nanotubes to mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water                and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing,                obtaining halloysite dispersion, adding 0.5-20 pts.                wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.05-5 pts. wt.                polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. initiator                and 0.05-15 pts. wt. dexamethasone to mixture of 50                pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol,                stirring, adding obtained solution to halloysite                dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuumizing,                maintaining at vacuum condition, returning to                normal pressure, repeatedly vacuumizing, washing                resultant product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt.                water, increasing the temperature of the water bath                to 60-120 degrees C, initiating polymerization for                48 hours or more, obtaining product (I), washing,                dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, adding 70-160                pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 0.05-2 pts. wt. 12                mol/L hydrochloric acid, 0.01-2 pts. wt. initiator                and 0.05-20 pts. wt. lovastatin, stirring                uniformly, heating the water bath to 30-75 degrees                C, initiating polymerization, adding 0.01-0.5 pt.                wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring until it is                completely dissolved and polymerizing for 48 hours                or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The initiator is                  dibenzoyl peroxide or                  azobisisobutyronitrile.

PN KR1861934-B1; CN108203094-A; CN108203094-B
TI Method and system useful recovering carbonate from steelmaking slag, comprises e.g. mixing steelmaking slag and acidic solvent, passing via first filter to separate into second mixture containing metal ions and residue, and adding ligand.
AU YOU J Y
   KOO J H
   SHIN K H
   KIM Y S
   YOU J
   KONG J
   SHI K
   JIN Y
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 2018425032
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbonate from steelmaking slag, comprises (a) mixing steelmaking slag and an acidic solvent to prepare mixture containing metal ions, (b) passing the mixture through a first filter to separate into a second mixture containing metal ions and residue, (c) adding ligand to the second mixture and mixing mixture to prepare a third mixture comprising complex prepared by reacting metal ion with ligand, (d) separating the complex by an electrochemical method, (e) reacting the complex with carbon dioxide to produce carbonate, and (f) separating the produced carbonate.
   USE - The method and system are useful for recovering carbonate from steelmaking slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system utilize carbonates without recycling of steelmaking slag and releasing carbon dioxide gas into the atmosphere, increase extraction efficiency of carbonate mineral and reduce amount of waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recovery system comprising a metal ion extracting unit for mixing steelmaking slag and acidic solvent to produce a first mixture containing metal ions, a first separator for separating the first mixture into a second mixture containing metal ions and a residue, a complex compound production unit for adding ligand to the second mixture and mixing the metal ions and the ligand to produce a complex, a complex separation unit for separating the product obtained from the complex compound production unit by an electrochemical method, a complex ligand, a chargeable ligand and unreacted metal ions, a carbonation unit for producing a carbonate by reacting the complex separated in the complex separation unit with carbon dioxide, a carbonate separator for separating the carbonates produced in the carbonation unit, a first recovering unit for recovering unreacted metal ions separated from the complex compound separating unit and supplying the unreacted metal ions to the metal ion extracting unit, and a second recovery unit for recovering the ligand separated from the complex separation unit and supplying the ligand to the complex compound production unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (d), uncharged complexes, charged unreacted ligands and unreacted metal ions are separated by an electrochemical method using a single-system desalination apparatus, and the unreacted ligand and the unreacted metal ion are supplied to the step (a), and the unreacted metal ion and the unreacted ligand are coupled to a pair of electrodes of the single-electrode desalination apparatus by action of the acidic solvent to flow through the single-electrode desalination apparatus and the potential of the electrode is adjusted to move the ligand and the unreacted metal ions toward the acidic solvent and the acidic solvent containing the ligand and the unreacted metal ion is supplied to the step (a). In the step (d), an uncharged complex is separated from an unreacted ligand and an unreacted metal ion having charge by an electrochemical method using a multi-stage electrochemical desalination apparatus, where the unreacted metal ions are recovered and supplied to the step (a) and the unreacted ligand is recovered and supplied to the step (c). The unreacted ligand is bound to the anode of the multi-stage electrochemical desalination apparatus, due acidic solvent is flowed into the multi-stage electrochemical desalination apparatus, the anode is changed to a cathode to move the ligand toward the acidic solvent, and the acidic solvent containing the recovered ligand is supplied into step (c). The unreacted metal ions are coupled to a cathode of the multi-stage electrochemical desalination apparatus, in which an acidic solvent is flowed into the multi-stage electrochemical desalination apparatus, the unreacted metal ions are changed to an acidic solvent by changing the cathode to anode, and acidic solvent containing the recovered unreacted metal ion is fed to the step (a). Preferred Components: The metal ion is calcium (II). The ligand includes acetic acid, nitrilotriacetic acid, nitrobenzene, iminodiacetic acid, citrate3+, glutamate+ and/or oxalic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic solvent includes nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrobromic acid, hydroiodic acid, perchloric acid and/or phosphoric acid. The carbonate is calcium carbonate. The first filter comprises pores having diameter of 30-50 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
