PN WO2022191499-A1; KR2022126094-A
TI Recovering lithium precursor by preparing positive            electrode powder from positive electrode of lithium            secondary battery, preparing positive electrode active            material mixture by mixing calcium compound with            positive electrode powder, and forming preliminary            precursor mixture by reducing mixture.
AU SEO Y B
   PARK J Y
   SON S R
   LEE S I
   HONG S J
   KIM J M
   SOHN S
   HONGSEOKJOON
   KIM J
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 2022B6710M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering a lithium precursor from                a lithium secondary battery, involves (a) preparing                a positive electrode powder from the positive                electrode of a lithium secondary battery, (b)                preparing a positive electrode active material                mixture by mixing a calcium compound with the                positive electrode powder, (c) forming a                preliminary precursor mixture by reducing the                positive electrode active material mixture, and (c)                recovering a lithium precursor from the preliminary                precursor mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering a lithium                precursor from a lithium secondary battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains the lithium precursor with                high purity without a complicated leaching process                and an additional process resulting from a                wet-based process of an acid solution, and                impurities such as hydrogen fluoride and carbon                dioxide generated in the dry reduction process by                mixing the calcium compound with a waste positive                active material react with calcium before reacting                with lithium, and improves the yield of the lithium                precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The positive electrode                  comprises a scrap-derived waste positive                  electrode, a current collector and a positive                  electrode active material layer formed on the                  current collector and including a binder and the                  positive electrode active material, where the                  positive electrode powder comprises the positive                  electrode active material and a component derived                  from the binder. Preferred Method: The preparing                  of the positive electrode powder involves dry                  grinding the positive electrode of the lithium                  secondary battery. The step of preparing the                  positive electrode active material mixture or the                  step of forming the preliminary precursor mixture                  involves at least partially removing a component                  derived from the binder by reacting with the                  calcium compound comprising calcium oxide, where                  the component derived from the binder comprises a                  fluorine component and a carbon component. The                  reaction of the calcium compound with the                  positive electrode powder involves mixing a                  calcium compound containing 0.5-1.5 times the                  calcium element compared to the element fluorine                  contained in the positive electrode powder. The                  preparing of the positive active material mixture                  involves heating the positive electrode powder                  and calcium compound together at                  300-600&#176; C. The reduction treatment                  involves performing dry reduction using hydrogen                  gas or a carbon-based material, where the                  reduction treatment temperature is                  400-600&#176; C. The step of recovering the                  lithium precursor from the preliminary precursor                  mixture involves washing the preliminary                  precursor mixture with water to obtain a lithium                  precursor hydrate. Preferred Conditions: The                  selectivity of lithium hydroxide in the lithium                  precursor hydrate is 97% or more.

TI Membrane composite comprises layer of polymer, and            macrocycle homogeneously distributed within polymer            layer, where polymer comprises polar moiety, and            macrocycle comprise another polar moiety to form            electrostatic interaction with polar moiety of            polymer.
AU LI B
   WU J
   JAPIP S
   WANG K Y
   CHUNG T
AE UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 2022A6584Y
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane composite comprises (a) a layer of                polymer, and (b) a macrocycle homogeneously                distributed within the polymer layer, where the                polymer comprises a first polar moiety, and the                macrocycle comprises a second polar moiety to form                an electrostatic interaction with the first polar                moiety of the polymer, the first polar moiety is                chosen from acyl, amino, acylamino, aminoacyl,                and/or their combinations, and the macrocycle is                calix(n)arene or a derivative, where n is                5-8.
   USE - The membrane composite is used in gas                separation, where the gas is a mixture of hydrogen,                oxygen, nitrogen, methane, and carbon dioxide. The                membrane composite is used in organic solvent                filtration and in organic solvent                nanofiltration.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane composite uses macrocycles that                are advantageous in acting as pore regulators in                membrane composites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of fabricating the membrane composite having a                macrocycle homogeneously distributed within a layer                of polymer, comprising incubating the layer of                polymer in a macrocycle solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first polar moiety                  is chosen from cyano, acyl, oxyacyl, acyloxy,                  amino, acylamino, aminoacyl, amido oximyl,                  oximyl, hydrazonyl, iminyl, keto oximyl, aceto                  oximyl, hydroxyl, pyrrolyl, oxazolyl, imidazolyl,                  pyrazolyl, triazolyl, and/or their combinations.                  The upper rim is functionalized with at least one                  moiety independently chosen from sulfonyl,                  phosphoryl, amino, carboxyl, oxyalkyl and/or                  their a combinations. The lower rim is                  functionalized with at least one moiety                  independently chosen from optionally substituted                  acyloxy, optionally substituted acyl, optionally                  substituted alkyl, optionally substituted                  alkenyl, optionally substituted alkoxy, and                  optionally substituted alkenyloxy. The macrocycle                  solution comprises a protic solvent chosen from                  methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, n-propanol,                  water, and/or their combinations. The crosslinker                  solution has a crosslinker concentration of 5                  wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The first polar moiety                  resides in a polymer backbone, as a side group,                  or their combinations. The macrocycle is further                  covalently bonded to the layer of polymer. The                  polymer in the layer of polymer is chosen from a                  polymer with intrinsic microporosity-1 (PIM-1),                  polyamide, polyamide-imide, polybenzimidazole,                  polylactic acid (PLA), polycarboxylic acid (PCA),                  polyethylenimine (PEI), polyarylamine,                  polyalkylamine, polyallylamine, poly(vinyl amine)                  or their combinations. The polymer layer of                  polymer is crosslinked. The electrostatic                  interaction is chosen from hydrogen-bond, proton                  transfer, ionic interaction or their                  combinations. The layer of polymer is crosslinked                  with a crosslinker having at least 2 pendant                  groups chosen from aminoalkyl, haloalkyl, and/or                  their combinations. The polymer is chosen from                  polyamide-imide or polybenzimidazole. The                  membrane composite is formed as a hollow fiber                  membrane. The macrocycle or its derivative is                  chosen from cyclodextrin, calixarene,                  cucurbituril, resorcinarene, pillararene, and/or                  their combinations. The macrocycle or its                  derivative is a calixarene or its derivative,                  chosen from sulfocalixarene (or                  sulfonylcalixarene), sulfothiacalixarene,                  carboxylatocalixarene, aminocalixrene,                  p-phosphonic acid calixarene, or their                  combinations. The macrocycle or its derivative                  has monomer residues of 4-12. The macrocycle or                  its derivative has an upper rim and a lower rim,                  where the upper rim and lower rim are separated                  by a frustum-shaped cavity, and a diameter of the                  lower rim is at least 3 A. The mole ratio of                  macrocycle to polymer is 0.1-10%. The membrane                  composite comprises 0.1-10 wt.% macrocycle. The                  macrocycle is homogeneous dispersed across the                  thickness of the layer of polymer. The macrocycle                  is 4-sulfocalix(8)arene (SCA8). The macrocycle is                  sulfocalix(n)arene. Preferred Method: The method                  further involves crosslinking the layer of                  polymer with 1,6-hexanediamine (HDA),                  tris(2-aminoethyl)amine (TAEA), &#945;                  ,&#945; '-dibromo-p-xylene or their                  combinations. The step of crosslinking is                  performed by incubating the layer of polymer in a                  crosslinker solution. The method further involves                  a drying step after the incubation step.                  Preferred Conditions: The crosslinking step is                  performed for at least 12 hours. The layer of                  polymer is incubated in a macrocycle solution for                  at least 0.5 hours. Preferred Properties: The                  membrane composite having a thickness of 80-150                  &#956; m, Fourier transform infrared                  spectroscopy (FTIR) spectrum peak of 1040 cm1, a                  sulfur surface composition of at least 1                  atomic.%, a free volume intensity S-parameter of                  at least 0.455, a pore size distribution                  R-parameter of at least 0.465, a Victoria blue B                  rejection of at least 90% when the macrocycle                  loading is 0.1 wt.%, a methylene blue rejection                  of at least 80% when the macrocycle loading is                  0.1 wt.%, a paracetamol rejection of at least 30%                  when the macrocycle loading is 0.1 wt.%, a pure                  methanol permeance of 1-1.8 LMH/bar when the                  macrocycle loading is 0.3 wt.%, a pure                  acetonitrile permeance of 1-0.28 LMH/bar when the                  macrocycle loading is 0.3 wt.%, a pure acetone                  permeance of 0.4-1 LMH/bar when the macrocycle                  loading is 0.3 wt.%, a pure ethanol permeance of                  0.48-0.76 LMH/bar when the macrocycle loading is                  0.3 wt.%, a pure ethyl acetate permeance of                  0.24-0.36 LMH/bar when the macrocycle loading is                  0.3 wt.%, a pure tetrahydrofuran permeance of                  0.12-0.24 LMH/bar when the macrocycle loading is                  0.3 wt.%, a pure toluene permeance of 0.01-0.08                  LMH/bar when the macrocycle loading is 0.3 wt.%,                  a stability in a methylene blue/methanol mixture                  for at least 7 days, and a                  N,N-dimethyl-4-nitroaniline (DMNA)/methylene blue                  (MB) separation factor of 14.5.

TI Low serum concentration medium for culturing            mesenchymal stem cells in vitro, where mesenchymal stem            cells include human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem            cells, human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells,            comprises fetal bovine serum and Morinda officinalis            oligosaccharide in basal medium.
AU LU G
   HE Y
AE GUANGZHOU MEIJIAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A64695
AB    NOVELTY - Low serum concentration medium comprises                10-50&#956;l/ml fetal bovine serum and                1-15&#956;g/ml Morinda                officinalisoligosaccharide in the basal                medium.
   USE - Low serum concentration medium for culturing                mesenchymal stem cells in vitro, where mesenchymal                stem cells include human umbilical cord mesenchymal                stem cells, human bone marrow mesenchymal stem                cells and human adipose stem cells (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The low serum concentration medium satisfies                rapid expansion of cells in vitro at a lower serum                concentration, has stable osteogenic and adipogenic                differentiation potentials, which is a significant                improvement compared with the existing                technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for culturing mesenchymal stem cells in vitro,                which involves separating and obtaining mesenchymal                stem cells, culturing isolated mesenchymal stem                cells using the low serum concentration medium                under the conditions of 37&#176;C, 5% carbon                dioxide, and saturated humidity, changing the                medium every 2-3 days until the cells are fully                confluent and then passaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  the fetal bovine serum is preferably 10-30                  &#956;l/ml. The concentration of the Morinda                  officinalis oligosaccharide is 5-15                  &#956;g/ml. 5-50 &#956;g/ml of sodium                  ascorbyl phosphate and 0.01-0.1 &#956;g/ml of                  Epimedium polysaccharide are also included in                  medium. The concentration of the sodium ascorbyl                  phosphate is preferably 10 to 40 &#181;g/ml.                  The concentration of the Epimedium polysaccharide                  is preferably 0.05-0.1 &#956;g/ml. Preferred                  Component: The basal medium is Dulbecco's                  Modified Eagle Medium or Dulbecco's Modified                  Eagle Medium /F12.

PN GB2602485-A; WO2022144444-A2; WO2022144444-A3
TI Forming bio-gasoline fuel from biomass feedstock            involves providing biomass feedstock, ensuring moisture            content of biomass feedstock, pyrolyzing low moisture            biomass feedstock to obtain mixture of biochar,            hydrocarbon feedstock, non-condensable light gases,            such as hydrogen, carbon monoxide.
AU ATKINS M
AE ABUNDIA BIOMASS-TO-LIQUIDS LTD (ABUN-Non-standard)
GA 2022875707
AB    NOVELTY - Forming bio-gasoline fuel from biomass                feedstock involves providing biomass feedstock,                ensuring moisture content of biomass feedstock is                10 wt.% based on the biomass feedstock, pyrolyzing                the low moisture biomass feedstock at a temperature                of at least 950degrees Celsius to obtain a mixture                of biochar, hydrocarbon feedstock, non-condensable                light gases, such as hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide and methane, and water, separating                the hydrocarbon feedstock from the obtained                mixture, cracking the hydrocarbon feedstock, using                a fluidized catalytic cracking (FCC) process to                obtain a bio-oil, and fractionating the resulting                bio-oil to obtain a bio-derived gasoline fuel                fraction.
   USE - Method for forming bio-gasoline fuel from                biomass feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has eco-friendly, and                cost-effective.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow image of the method                for forming bio-gasoline fuel from biomass                feedstock
   Biomass feedstock stream (12)
   Dryer (14)
   Outlet (16)
   Pyolysis reactor (18)
   Inlet (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Preferred Components:                  The water gas shift reaction further comprises a                  shift catalyst, where the shift catalyst is                  copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst or a chromium or                  copper promoted iron-based catalyst, preferably,                  copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst. The fluidized                  cracking catalyst comprises zeolite or high                  activity crystalline alumina silicate, amorphous                  binder compound or a filler, where the amorphous                  binder compound is silica, alumina, titania,                  zirconia and/or magnesium oxide, and the filler                  is clay, such as kaolin. The zeolite is a large                  pore zeolite, which is natural large-pore                  zeolite, such as gmelinite, chabazite,                  dachiardite, clinoptilolite, faujasite,                  heulandite, analcite, levynite, erionite,                  sodalite, cancrinite, nepheline, azurite,                  scolecite, natrolite, offretite, mesolite,                  mordenite, brewsterite, and ferrierite and/or a                  synthetic large pore zeolite, such as zeolite X,                  Y, A, L ZK-4, 2K-5, B, E, F, H, J, M, Q, T, W, Z,                  alpha and beta, omega, rare earth zeolite (REY)                  and ultra-stable zeolite (USY), preferably,                  faujasite, zeolite Y, USY, and REY. The zeolite                  is a medium pore zeolite, which is MFI type                  zeolite, for e.g. ZSM-5, MFS type zeolite, a MEL                  type zeolites MTW type zeolite, for e.g., ZSM-12,                  MTW type zeolite, an WO type zeolite, a MIT type                  zeolite, a HEU type zeolite, TON type zeolite,                  for e.g. theta-1, and/or a FER type zeolite, for                  e.g. ferrierite, preferably, ZSM-5, ZSM-12,                  ZSM-22, ZSM-23, ZSM-34, ZSM-35, ZSM-38, ZSM-48,                  ZSM-50, silicate, and silicalite 2, more                  preferably, ZSM-5. The catalyst is nickel                  molybdenum sulfide (NiMo5), molybdenum,                  molybdenum disulfide (MoS2) cobalt/molybdenum,                  cobalt molybdenum sulfide (CoMoS) and/or a                  nickel/molybdenum based catalyst, preferably, the                  catalyst is a supported catalyst, such as                  activated carbon, silica, alumina, amorphous                  silica-alumina, a molecular sieve, titania,                  ceria, zirconia, carbon, silicon carbide, zeolite                  Y, zeolite beta, SAPO-11, SAPO-41, and/or a                  zeolite. The catalyst comprises a metal, where                  metal is nickel (Ni), chromium, molybdenum (Mo),                  tungsten, cobalt (Co), platinum, palladium,                  rhodium, ruthenium, iridium, osmium, copper,                  iron, zinc, gallium, indium, and vanadium,                  preferably, CoMo, NiMo, and Ni.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: Preferred Components:                  The biomass feedstock comprises cellulose,                  hemicellulose or lignin-based feedstock.                  Preferred Process: The biomass feedstock is a                  non-food crop biomass feedstock, preferably,                  miscanthus, switchgrass, garden trimmings, straw,                  such as rice straw or wheat straw, cotton gin                  trash, municipal solid waste, palm fronds/empty                  fruit bunches (EFB), palm kernel shell, bagasse,                  wood, such as hickory, pine bark, Virginia pine,                  red oak, white oak, spruce, poplar, and cedar,                  grass hay, mesquite, wood flour, nylon, lint,                  bamboo, paper, and corn stover.Preferred Process: The method for forming                  bio-gasoline fuel from biomass feedstock further                  involves filtering the hydrocarbon feedstock                  product to at least partially remove                  contaminants, such as carbon, graphene,                  polyaromatic compound or tar by contacting the                  hydrocarbon feedstock product with an active                  carbon compound or a crosslinked organic                  hydrocarbon resin, separating orderly the                  hydrocarbon feedstock product from the active                  carbon and crosslinked organic hydrocarbon resin                  compound though filtration.The step of separating the hydrocarbon                  feedstock from the obtained mixture involves                  separating non-condensable light gases from the                  hydrocarbon feedstock product by flash                  distillation or fractional distillation. The                  method for forming bio-gasoline fuel from biomass                  feedstock specifically involves providing a                  bio-derived hydrocarbon feedstock comprising 0.1                  wt.% C8 compound, 1 wt.% C10 compound, 5 wt.% C12                  compound, 5 wt.% C16 compound and 30 wt.% C18                  compound, cracking the hydrocarbon feedstock,                  using a fluidized catalytic cracking (FCC)                  process to produce a bio-oil, and fractionating                  the resulting bio-oil to obtain a bio-derived                  gasoline fuel fraction.Preferred Conditions: The large pore zeolite                  has internal pores having a pore diameter of                  0.62-0.8 nanometer. The medium pore zeolite has                  internal pores having a diameter of from                  0.45-0.62 nanometer. The low moisture biomass                  feedstock is pyrolyzed at temperature of                  1000degrees Celsius, preferably, 1100degrees                  Celsius, the pressure is 850-1000 pascal,                  preferably, 900-950 pascal, and the time is 10                  seconds - 2 hours, preferably, 100 seconds - 10                  minutes. The biomass feedstock is in the form of                  pellet, chip, particulate or a powder, preferably                  the pellets, chips, particulates or powder have a                  diameter of from 5 microm - 10 cm, such as from 5                  microm-25 mm, preferably 100 microm-10 mm. The                  water at least partially separated from the                  hydrocarbon feedstock product is a pyroligneous                  acid. The active carbon compound and crosslinked                  organic hydrocarbon resin is contacted with the                  hydrocarbon feedstock product for at least 15                  minutes before separation, preferably, 25                  minutes. The carbon monoxide present in the                  non-condensable light gas is contacted with steam                  in a water gas shift reaction to produce carbon                  dioxide and a bio-derived hydrogen gas, where the                  water gas shift reaction is performed at a                  temperature of 205-260degrees Celsius. The lift                  gas is supplied to the FCC reactor through an                  inlet at or near the base of the reactor,                  preferably, the lift gas is steam, nitrogen, or                  vaporized oil. The initial moisture content of                  the biomass feedstock is up to 50 wt.% based on                  biomass feedstock, preferably, 30 wt.%.

AB    NOVELTY - Processing treatment agent comprises firstly                adding medicine A into the waste acid water,                adjusting pH to 2-7, preferably 3-6, neutralizing                the waste acid, generating calcium sulfate, ferric                hydroxide, aluminium hydroxide precipitate B, where                the generated gas is carbon dioxide, recycling and                refining to generate product, treating                precipitating B with C agent, separating to                generate calcium sulfate, ferric aluminum sulfate                product, adding waste acid and the generated                filtrate, continuously adding D agent, adjusting                the pH value to be 6-9, filtering to obtain                filtrate residue, adding copper, zinc, manganese                precipitate product, filtrate metal precipitator E                to make the heavy metals meet the standard, and                reusing or discharging wastewater.

   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures resource comprehensive                utilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The medicine A                  comprises calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide,                  carbide slag, magnesium carbonate, preferably                  calcium carbonate. The C agent comprises                  phosphoric acid, hydrochloric acid, and nitric                  acid, preferably sulfuric acid and waste acid.                  The amount of alkaline hydroxide is 0.01-15 wt.%.                  The D agent comprises calcium oxide, calcium                  hydroxide, calcium carbonate, carbide slag,                  magnesium oxide, and copper. The total                  precipitation amount of zinc ions is 0.01-10                  wt.%. The method comprises using                  calcium carbonate to neutralize the waste acid                  and generating carbon dioxide and collecting                  carbon dioxide product. The method comprises                  adding A medicine into the waste acid, ensuring                  the pH reaches 2-7, generating calcium sulfate,                  ferric hydroxide, aluminum hydroxide precipitate                  B, filtering and separating to generate                  precipitate B, then adding agent C to obtain                  gypsum, ferric sulfate aluminum product, adding D                  agent into the filtrate after precipitating iron                  and aluminum, ensuring the pH is 6-9, generating                  copper, zinc, manganese and other hydroxide                  precipitate, filtering to obtain                  zinc-copper-manganese product, adding filtrate                  with heavy metal auxiliary agent E, filtering and                  separating to remove thallium and other heavy                  metal, ensuring the water reaches standard,                  discharging or recycling.

TI Producing oxygen isotope-labeled carbon monoxide by contacting carbon as raw material with water containing isotope of oxygen in higher concentration than natural abundance, and reacting carbon with oxygen gas containing isotope of oxygen in higher concentration than natural abundance.
AU TOKUOKA S
   NAKAYAMA E
AE TAIYO NIPPON SANSO CORP (NIIO-C)
GA 202258620L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing oxygen isotope-labeled carbon monoxide, involves contacting carbon as raw material with water containing isotope of oxygen (17O or 18O) in higher concentration than natural abundance, and reacting the carbon with oxygen gas containing 17O or 18O in higher concentration than natural abundance.
   USE - The method is used for producing oxygen isotope-labeled carbon monoxide, which is used as internal standard for mass spectrometry and as labeling raw material for labeling substances in industrial applications, such as natural science and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces oxygen isotope-labeled carbon monoxide having oxygen isotope concentration of greater than or equal to 99 atomic%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing oxygen isotope-labeled carbon dioxide, involving contacting carbon as raw material with water containing 17O or 18O in higher concentration than natural abundance, and reacting carbon with oxygen gas containing 17O or 18O in higher concentration than natural abundance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves separating and recovering carbon monoxide from reaction mixture gas produced by reacting carbon with oxygen gas containing 17O or 18O in higher concentration than natural abundance ratio. The method involves adsorbing carbon dioxide contained in reaction mixture gas by adsorbent for removing carbon dioxide, separating and recovering the carbon monoxide. The oxygen isotope-labeled carbon dioxide production method involves separating and recovering oxygen isotope-labeled carbon dioxide from reaction mixed gas produced by reacting carbon with oxygen gas containing 17O or 18O in higher concentration than natural abundance ratio. The method involves adsorbing carbon dioxide contained in reaction mixture gas by adsorbent, heating the adsorbent to which carbon dioxide has been adsorbed, and desorbing carbon dioxide from adsorbent.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the general preparation of raw material carbon that does not support the claims.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The graphene oxide is                  prepared by adding graphite powder and phosphoric                  acid into concentrated sulfuric acid, placing it                  in an ice-water bath under stirring, dissolving                  graphite powder and phosphoric acid fully, adding                  pre-ground potassium permanganate powder into the                  system at a constant speed. The reaction solution                  is transferred to a water bath and deionized                  water is slowly added. The solution is                  transferred to a hot water bath and stirred again                  after continuous stirring, where deionized water                  and refrigerated hydrogen peroxide are added to                  the system after the reaction to remove undesired                  water. The fully reacted potassium permanganate                  is used to obtain graphene oxide solution. The                  product is allowed to stand and centrifuge to                  obtain a neutral graphene oxide dispersion, which                  is freeze-dried and then used for drying.Preferred Components: The tertiary amine                  compound is N,N-dimethylethylenediamine,                  N,N-diethylethylenediamine,                  3-dimethylaminopropylamine,                  3-diethylaminopropylamine,                  4-dimethylaminobutylamine,                  4-diethylaminobutylamine. The water phase is                  sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium                  carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, cesium carbonate,                  potassium phosphate, sodium phosphate, potassium                  acetate, and lithium carbonate. The oil phase is                  an organic substance immiscible with water such                  as toluene, alkane or ionic liquid. The inorganic                  base is used as auxiliary agent. The oil phase is                  halogenated benzene or halogenated benzene                  derivative and phenylboronic acid or                  phenylboronic acid derivative. The oil-water                  ratio system also contains tetrakis                  (triphenylphosphine) palladium as a catalyst. The                  halogenated benzene comprises chlorobenzene,                  bromobenzene and iodobenzene. The halogenated                  benzene derivative comprises p-nitro                  bromobenzene, p-methylbromobenzene,                  p-hydroxybromobenzene, 4-methoxyiodobenzene,                  4-formyliodobenzene, 4-methyliodobenzene,                  p-nitroiodobenzene, and p-hydroxyiodobenzene. The                  phenylboronic acid derivatives include                  4-methoxyphenylboronic acid,                  4-formylphenylboronic acid,                  2-methoxyphenylboronic acid,                  2-methylphenylboronic acid, and                  4-methylphenylboronic acid.

PN CN114181976-A
TI Method for pretreatment of multi-component mixed raw materials and high-concentration anaerobic digestion to produce biogas comprises taking manure, straw, kitchen waste and pomace as mixed raw materials, pulverizing, homogenizing, carrying out hydrolysis pretreatment, and anaerobic digestion.
AU WU J
   YAO J
   CHEN X
   LIU W
AE HEFEI COMPREHENSIVE NAT SCI CENT ENERGY (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2022440467
AB    NOVELTY - Method for pretreatment of multi-component mixed raw materials and high-concentration anaerobic digestion to produce biogas comprises (i) taking manure, straw, kitchen waste and pomace as multi-component mixed raw materials, pulverizing the raw materials, and homogenizing to obtain mixed raw material liquid, (ii) carrying out hydrolysis pretreatment to the mixed raw material liquid to obtain the mixed material liquid after the pretreatment, and (iii) anaerobic digestion of the pretreated mixed feed liquid to generate biogas.
   USE - The method is useful for pretreatment of multi-component mixed raw materials and high-concentration anaerobic digestion to produce biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves ratio of methane and carbon dioxide in the fermentation gas to high concentration of 12-17%; has mild conditions, low energy consumption, and is combined with the control scheme of key indicators in the anaerobic fermentation process, can provide a good solution for the efficient and stable operation of large and medium-sized biogas projects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The multi-component mixed raw materials per gram of dry matter, the added amounts of the composite enzymes are: 5-8 U liquefaction enzyme, 100-150 U saccharification enzyme, 60-100 U cellulase, 40-60 U hemicellulase and 30-50 U pectinase.Preferred Method: The hydrolysis pretreatment includes adding formula compound enzymes and trace elements for hydrolysis pretreatment. The anaerobic fermentation includes: in the feed liquid of the anaerobic fermentation, the solid content concentration is 12-17 wt.%, and the fermentation temperature is 35-43degreesC. In the step (iii), the key indicators of regulation and control of the anaerobic digestion and biogas production process mainly include: the ratio of acetic acid and propionic acid in fermentation broth, ammonia nitrogen concentration, pH value, and the ratio of methane and carbon dioxide in gas production. The formula compound enzyme includes liquefaction enzyme, saccharification enzyme, cellulase, hemicellulase and pectinase; the trace elements include Fe2+, Mg2+, Cu2+ and Ni2+; the activity of the described formula compound enzyme is 2000 U/ml liquefaction enzyme, 100000 U/ml saccharification enzyme, 60000 U/ml cellulase, 50000 U/g hemicellulase and 25000 U/g pectinase; the trace elements are derived from the soluble chloride or nitrate of four metal ions of Fe2+, Mg2+, Cu2+ and Ni2+; the soluble chloride salt is magnesium chloride, cupric chloride, nickel chloride, and ferrous chloride; and the nitrates are magnesium nitrate, copper nitrate, nickel nitrate and ferrous nitrate. In the step (ii), the hydrolysis pretreatment process conditions are: the hydrolysis pretreatment temperature is 40-45degreesC, the action time is 2-4 hours, and stirring is performed every 30 minutes during the period. In step (iii), the control ranges of the key indicators of the regulation and control of the anaerobic digestion and the biogas production process are respectively: the mass ratio of acetic acid and propionic acid in the fermentation broth is 2:1-4:1; the ammonia nitrogen concentration in the fermentation broth is 400-3000 mg/l, the pH value is 6.8-7.7, and the ratio of methane to carbon dioxide in the biogas is greater than 1.15; the mass ratio of acetic acid and propionic acid in the fermentation broth is 3:1; the ammonia nitrogen concentration in the fermentation broth is 400-1800 mg/l, and the pH value is 7.3-7.5. The key index adjustment methods for the regulation of anaerobic digestion and gas production process are: when the high concentration of multi-component mixed raw materials is monitored, the concentration ratio of acetic acid and propionic acid in fermentation broth, ammonia nitrogen concentration, when the pH value and the ratio of methane and carbon dioxide in the produced gas deviate from the control range, (i) reduce the organic load rate (OLR) of the feed to below 2.5 kgVS/(L.m3); (ii) and reflux 10-30 vol.% biogas slurry or add 1.5-2 kg/m3 biochar according to the volume of fermentation broth, after 3-5 days, after each control index returns to the control range, then reduce 5-10 wt.% normal feed amount for anaerobic fermentation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal ions according to the addition amount per liter of anaerobic digestion fermentation broth are: 20-45 mg Fe2+, 20-30 mg Mg2+, 30-40 mg Cu2+ and 5-10 mg Ni2+.

   ADVANTAGE - The formulation contains exosomes with high activity and purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Formulation: The formulation comprises 0.007-0.012 %mass, preferably 0.0095 %mass stem cell exosome in terms of protein mass.Preferred Components: The active peptide is glutathione. PN CN113817228-A
TI Modified method for removing iron tailings slag comprises adding iron-removed tailings slag into crucible and sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide into distilled water, separating by filtration, modifying by adding modifiers, introducing precipitant, washing precipitate and calcining.
AU TANG B
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 202206953S
AB    NOVELTY - Modified method for removing iron tailings slag comprises adding iron-removed tailings slag into the crucible, adding certain amount of sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide into distilled water to mix well, adding crucible into a muffle furnace for reaction, adding water after cooling, separating by filtration, drying filter residue at 80degrees Celsius for 3 hours, modifying by adding modifiers and modified by ball milling, which was used as a reinforcing filler for plastics and rubber, adding filtrate with a dispersant, mixing uniformly, introducing precipitant at room temperature for precipitation reaction, allowing to stand for aging, filtering and separating, and using filtrate to recover sodium bicarbonate, washing precipitate with distilled water and calcining to obtain nano-silica, which is sold as a product.
   USE - The method is useful for removing iron tailings slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and clean; provides a new feasible way for the resource utilization of the giant iron tailings and industrial carbon dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tailings slag after iron removal comprises 88.62 wt.% silicon dioxide, 2.85 wt.% aluminum oxide, 2.35 wt.% calcium oxide, 2.08 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.41 wt.% potassium oxide, 0.51 wt.% sodium oxide, 0.04 wt.% manganese oxide, 0.06 wt.% titanium dioxide 0.06 wt.% sulfur and 0.05 wt.% phosphorus and fineness is 100-160 mesh. The alkali fluxes used are sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide, the mass ratio of iron-removing tailing slag to sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide is 1:1-3:1-2 and the amount of distilled water is 20-40% of the mass of the iron-removing tailings slag. In the step (iv), the iron-removing tailings residue was dried at 80 degreesC for 3 hours, and then modified by ball milling with modifiers, where modifiers used were silane coupling agents (Si69, vinyltri(2-methoxyethoxy)silane, KH-560, KH-550, KH-151, Si747 and VTES), titanate coupling agents (KR-TTS, KR-138S and KR-46), aluminate coupling agents (DL-141-A, DL-414 and DL472) and organosilicone (polydimethylsiloxane) in an amount of 0.5-3% of the mass of the iron-removing tailings residue.Preferred Method: The alkali fusion reaction temperature is 400-600degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 2-3 hours, after the reaction, 300ml of distilled water is added to dissolve, and the filter residue (removal of iron tailings residue) and filtrate (sodium silicate solution) are obtained by filtration. The ball milling modification comprises 200-600 revolutions/minute for 1-3 hours, the ratio of ball to material is 5-8:1, the obtained material is used as a reinforcing filler for rubber and plastic, and the amount thereof is 10% of the quality of plastic and rubber 20-40%. In the step (iv), method comprises adding the sodium silicate solution to a dispersing agent, mixing evenly, and where dispersing agent used is a quaternary ammonium salt (dodecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide or cetyl trimethyl ammonium bromide) and the dosage is 1.0-5.0% of the quality of iron-removing tailings slag. In the step (vii), the mixed solution is treated with a precipitant at room temperature, and the used precipitant is various industrial purge gases containing carbon dioxide, the flow rate is 2-5 l/hour, and the time is determined according to the pH value of the end point to 7-8. The precipitate is washed 3-5 times with distilled water, and calcined to obtain nano-silica, where calcination temperature is 300-400degrees Celsius and the calcination time is 2-3 hours.

AE ZHEJIANG YANGHE HEALTH MANAGEMENT TECHNO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)

Apocynum venetum, 8-12 pts. wt. spina date seed and 5-8 pts. wt. fructus rosa davurica.Preferred Method: The method involves adding 3-5 wt.% entrainer during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide flow rate during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 15-20 l/hour. During the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, the extraction pressure is 35-45 MPa, the temperature is 40-60degrees Celsius, and the time is 1-3 hours. During the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, the primary separation pressure is 8.5 MPa, and the secondary separation pressure is 7 MPa. The clear paste is spray dried at an inlet air temperature of 110-150degrees Celsius.
   ACTIVITY - Antiemetic; Anabolic.

PN CN113488169-A
TI Method for evaluating health of human body based on energy metabolism, involves calculating entropy of human body in resting and moving state to evaluate health state of human body according to energy entropy.
AU SONG C
   LI T
   TANG Q
   WANG Y
   OUYANG G
   LUO J
   GAO M
   SONG Z
AE XINYI HEALTHY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XINY-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0810S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting breathing data of a human body in a resting and moving state. An oxygen consumption and carbon dioxide expiration amount in the breathing data are obtained. A model is determined according to a pre-constructed human body energy entropy. An entropy of the human body is calculated in the resting and the moving state to evaluate a health state of human body according to the energy entropy.
   USE - Method for evaluating health of a human body based on energy metabolism. Uses include but are not limited to body surface area, thin tissue, body fat, normal person, overweight, obesity and predicting formula of patient energy needs under various pathological conditions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately reflecting the energy consumption of the human body, and judging and analyzing human health state according to the range of the energy entropy value.

PT P
PN WO2021202479-A1; IN202247061403-A; CN115335530-A
TI Producing polypeptide of interest by submerged            fermentation involves e.g. fermenting microorganism in            fermentation broth, and adding enzyme or co-expressing            enzyme in microorganism and secreting enzyme to reduce            viscosity of broth.
AU CAI X
   MULLIKIN R
   CORNELISSEN S
   HERMANSEN C K
   BREUENER A G
   HANSEN K
   DIANO A
   MCBRAYER B
   BROWN S
   CHANDLER T
   GE H
   RIISGAARD F K
   CASSELLS B
   KRISTENSEN K J
   SEREDA V
   PHAM J
   BREUNER A G
   BRAUN S
   SCHAFFER S
AE NOVOZYMES AS (NOVO-C)
   PHAM J (PHAM-Individual)
GA 2021B56062
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one polypeptide of interest                by submerged fermentation, comprises: (a)                fermenting at least one microorganism that produces                the polypeptide of interest in a fermentation                broth; (b) adding at least one enzyme to the                fermentation broth, or co-expressing the enzyme in                the microorganism and secreting the enzyme into the                fermentation broth to reduce the viscosity of the                fermentation broth, compared to when the enzyme is                not added or co-expressed; and, optionally (c)                recovering the polypeptide of interest.
   USE - The process is useful for producing at least                one polypeptide of interest by submerged                fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: facilitates addition of the                enzyme, preferably comprising a muramidase, a                chitinase, a glucanase or a phosphodiesterase to a                submerged fermentation broth resulted in a reduced                viscosity, as compared to when no enzyme was added;                improves liquid mixing, reduces spatial variation                of important fermentation parameters, improves                oxygen uptake, improves carbon dioxide removal,                provides higher productivity and improves yield;                and allows to keep constant viscosity but decrease                power input and/or oxygen supply.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The enzyme is added as a                  bolus or as continuous addition. The process is a                  fed batch or a continuously fed fermentation                  process, where the enzyme is added in the feed.                  The process comprises: adding up to 20000 mg,                  preferably up to 500 mg/kg of enzyme per kg of                  fermentation broth; and adding the enzyme in an                  activity ranging from below the detection limit                  of the muramidase assay and up to an activity of                  0.2 limit of the muramidase assay when diluted                  4000 times, preferably when diluted 3000 times,                  or up to 0.2 when diluted 2000 times, preferably                  when diluted 100 times. The enzyme is added to                  the fermentation broth by co-cultivating a second                  microorganism in the fermentation broth, where                  the second microorganism produces and secretes                  the enzyme into the fermentation broth. The                  oxygen transfer rate is increased by increasing                  the agitation, stirring speed, mixing or                  oxygenation of the fermentation broth during the                  fermentation. Preferred Components: The                  polypeptide of interest is a single polypeptide                  or a mixture of at least two polypeptides. The                  polypeptide or the mixture of at least two                  polypeptides is enzyme, preferably at least one                  secreted enzyme, or the polypeptide or the                  mixture of at least two polypeptides comprise a                  hydrolase, isomerase, ligase, lyase,                  oxidoreductase, or transferase e.g. an                  aminopeptidase, amylase, carbohydrase,                  carboxypeptidase, catalase, cellobiohydrolase,                  cellulase, chitinase, cutinase, cyclodextrin                  glycosyltransferase, phosphodiesterase,                  endoglucanase, esterase, alpha -galactosidase,                  beta -galactosidase, glucoamylase, alpha                  -glucosidase, beta -glucosidase, invertase,                  laccase, lipase, mannosidase, mutanase, oxidase,                  pectinolytic enzyme, peroxidase, phytase,                  polyphenol oxidase, proteolytic enzyme,                  ribonuclease, transglutaminase, xylanase, and                  beta -xylosidase. The enzyme comprises the                  glycosyl hydrolase families, preferably a                  muramidase, chitinase, glucanase, mannanase,                  nuclease and/or phosphodiesterase, or the enzyme                  is a muramidase, preferably a GH24 or GH25                  muramidase, or the enzyme comprises an amino acid                  sequence at least 60%, preferably at least 99%                  sequence identity, or even 100% sequence identity                  to the amino acid sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 1-453                  (sequences not defined here may be found at                  ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication),                  or their mature version. The enzyme comprises at                  least two enzymes, preferably a glycosyl                  hydrolase and a phosphodiesterase. The                  microorganism is bacterial, preferably                  prokaryotic i.e. Gram positive or Gram negative,                  preferably the microorganism comprising Gram                  positive bacteria including Bacillus,                  Clostridium, Enterococcus, Geobacillus,                  Lactobacillus, Lactococcus, Oceanobacillus,                  Staphylococcus, Streptococcus or Streptomyces, or                  Gram negative bacteria inducing Campylobacter, E.                  coli, Flavobacterium, Fusobacterium,                  Helicobacter, Lysobacter, Neisseria, Pseudomonas,                  Salmonella or Ureaplasma. The microorganism                  comprises a Bacillus species, preferably                  comprising Bacillus alcalophilus, Bacillus                  altitudinis, Bacillus amyloliquefaciens, B.                  amyloliquefaciens subsp. plantarum, Bacillus                  brevis, Bacillus circulans, Bacillus clausii,                  Bacillus coagulans, Bacillus firmus, Bacillus                  lautus, Bacillus lentus, Bacillus licheniformis,                  Bacillus megaterium, Bacillus methylotrophicus,                  Bacillus pumilus, Bacillus safensis, Bacillus                  stearothermophilus, Bacillus subtilis or Bacillus                  thuringiensis. The microorganism is a                  Lactobacillus species, preferably comprising                  Lactobacillus reuteri, Lactobacillus casei,                  Lactobacillus paracasei, L. paracasei subsp.                  paracasei, L. paracasei subsp. tolerans,                  Lactobacillus rhamnosus, Lactobacillus brevis,                  Lactobacillus plantarum, Lactobacillus crispatus                  or Lactobacillus delbrueckii. The microorganism                  is eukaryotic, preferably the microorganism is a                  fungus i.e. a filamentous fungus including                  Acremonium, Aspergillus, Aureobasidium,                  Bjerkandera, Ceriporiopsis, Chrysosporium,                  Coprinus, Coriolus, Cryptococcus, Filobasidium,                  Fusarium, Humicola, Magnaporthe, Mucor,                  Myceliophthora, Neocallimastix, Neurospora,                  Paecilomyces, Penicillium, Phanerochaete,                  Phlebia, Piromyces, Pleurotus, Schizophyllum,                  Talaromyces, Thermoascus, Thielavia,                  Tolypocladium, Trametes, or Trichoderma or a                  yeast including Candida, Hansenula,                  Kluyveromyces, Pichia, Saccharomyces,                  Schizosaccharomyces, Yarrowia, Kluyveromyces                  lactis, Saccharomyces carlsbergensis,                  Saccharomyces cerevisiae, Saccharomyces                  diastaticus, Saccharomyces douglasii,                  Saccharomyces kluyveri, Saccharomyces norbensis,                  Saccharomyces oviformis, or Yarrowia lipolytica.                  The microorganism is an Aspergillus, Penicillium                  or Trichoderma species, preferably comprising                  Aspergillus awamori, Aspergillus foetidus,                  Aspergillus fumigatus, Aspergillus japonicus,                  Aspergillus nidulans, Aspergillus niger,                  Aspergillus oryzae, Penicillium purpurogenum,                  Trichoderma harzianum, Trichoderma koningii,                  Trichoderma longibrachiatum, Trichoderma reesei                  or Trichoderma viride.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of reducing                culture broth viscosity by adding muramidase to a                fermentation for amylase production with                recombinant Bacillus licheniformis that do not                support the claims.

   HISAO O
   YUJI T
   ECHIGO M
   OHNISHI H
   TSUDA Y
AE OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
GA 2021B5221D
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrocarbon manufacturing system (100)                comprises an electrolysis reaction unit (10), a                reverse water-gas shift reaction unit (20), and a                hydrocarbon synthesis reaction unit (30).
   USE - System is used for manufacturing hydrocarbon                using water and carbon dioxide as raw                materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrocarbon manufacturing system has                excellent strength, heat resistance and durability.                The high-performance electrolysis reaction unit is                compact, and has excellent strength and                reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture (M1) of the hydrocarbon                manufacturing system, which involves arranging an                impregnated support obtained by impregnating and                supporting an active metal on a metal oxide support                in the reverse water-gas shift reaction unit and                the hydrocarbon synthesis reaction unit; and
   (2) method (M2) for operating the hydrocarbon                manufacturing system, which involves subjecting the                reverse water-gas shift reaction to reduction                pretreatment to operate the hydrocarbon                manufacturing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                hydrocarbon manufacturing system. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Electrolysis reaction unit (10)
   Reverse water-gas shift reaction unit                (20)
   Hydrocarbon synthesis reaction unit                (30)
   Water separation unit (40)
   Hydrocarbon manufacturing system (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The reverse water-gas                  shift reaction unit comprises a reverse water-gas                  shift catalyst in which an active metal is                  supported on a metal oxide support. The                  hydrocarbon synthesis reaction unit comprises a                  hydrocarbon synthesis catalyst in which an active                  metal is supported on a metal oxide support. The                  electrolysis reaction unit comprises an                  electrolytic cell in which an electrode layer, an                  electrolyte layer and a counter electrode layer                  are formed on a support. The hydrocarbon                  synthesis reaction unit is supported by a                  support, preferably metal. Preferred Process: The                  process (M1) involves arranging the impregnated                  support on portion(s) of support and baking at a                  temperature of 450 degrees C or more to form                  reverse water-gas shift reaction unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the reverse                  water-gas shift catalyst, at least one or both of                  nickel and iron are supported as active metal on                  support comprising ceria-based metal oxide or                  zirconia-based metal oxide as a main component.                  The ceria-based metal oxide is ceria doped with                  gadolinium, samarium and/or yttrium. The                  zirconia-based metal oxide is zirconia stabilized                  with yttria and/or scandia. In the reverse                  water-gas shift catalyst, copper is supported as                  active metal. The active metal of the hydrocarbon                  synthesis catalyst is ruthenium. The support of                  the electrolysis cell is a metal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Electrolysis reaction of                  water is performed in the electrolysis reaction                  unit. Co-electrolysis reaction of water and                  carbon dioxide is performed in the electrolysis                  reaction unit.

PN FR3108253-A1; WO2021191525-A1; CA3176724-A1
TI Platelet lysate foam useful for e.g. promoting            tissue regeneration or osteogenesis, and treating            cornea disorder, comprises transforming growth            factor-beta, epidermal growth factor and insulin-like            growth factor-1 within polymerized fibrin matrix.
AU CAZALBOU S
   CANCEILL T
AE UNIV TOULOUSE III SABATIER PAUL (UTOS-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE CENT HOSPITALIER (UYTO-Non-standard)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE III SABATIER PAUL (UTOS-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE CENT HOSPITALIER (UYTO-Non-standard)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 2021B0259P
AB    NOVELTY - Platelet lysate foam comprises transforming                growth factor (TGF)- beta , epidermal growth factor                (EGF), platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF)-AB,                insulin-like growth factor-1 (IGF-1), vascular                endothelial growth factor (VEGF) and basic                fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), within a                polymerized fibrin matrix.
   USE - The platelet lysate foam is useful for (i)                culturing cell, (ii) promoting tissue regeneration,                osteogenesis, bone regeneration, skin healing, and                regeneration of dermis, (iii) performing cell                therapy, (iv) promoting regeneration of                sub-periodontal tissue, and (v) treating cornea                disorder (all claimed), chronic skin ulcer,                osteoarthritis and Parkinson's disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The platelet lysate foam retains biological                properties of platelet lysate, has improved storage                and mechanical properties, and is easy to                handle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process                for obtaining a platelet lysate foam, which                involves (a) obtaining a hydrogel by performing                polymerization of a platelet lysate, (b) performing                substitution by washing aqueous solvent with a                polar solvent, and (c) drying in a supercritical                carbon dioxide (CO2) atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The platelet lysate                  foam further comprises calcium and/or tranexamic                  acid. The foam has a porosity of 70-95%,                  preferably 80%. Preferred Method: The hydrogel is                  obtained by performing polymerization of a                  platelet lysate, and combining platelet lysate                  with (i) a polymerization initiator chosen from                  calcium chloride (CaCl2), thrombin and genipin,                  (ii) an agent allowing the maintenance of isotony                  and swelling of gel, i.e. sodium chloride (NaCl),                  and (iii) a coagulation stabilizer chosen from                  tranexamic acid, amino-caproic acid and                  fibronectin.
   ACTIVITY - Osteopathic; Vulnerary; Ophthalmological;                Antiulcer; Antiarthritic; Antiparkinsonian. No                biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of obtaining                hydrogel from platelet lysate, that do not support                the claims.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN113387811-A; CN216808659-U
TI Producing dimethyl carbonate comprises reacting propylene or ethylene oxide with carbondioxide to synthesize propylene or ethylene carbonate to remove reaction heat and passing gasified methanol into ester exchange reaction rectifying tower.
AU FANG Y
   SHEN W
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021A86269
AB    NOVELTY - Producing dimethyl carbonate comprises (i) introducing supplemented and recycled the catalyst 1, carbon dioxide and propylene oxide (or ethylene oxide) continuously into the lower part of the tubular reactor for reacting; (ii) flowing out the reaction material in the tubular reactor from the upper part and passing into the evaporation cooler and the reaction material through the tube side; and (iii) passing the liquid phase methanol into the shell side of the evaporation cooler, extracting the gas phase methanol from the upper part of the evaporation cooler and passing into the lower part of the ester exchange reaction rectifying tower.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dimethyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can reduce energy consumption thus saves energy; provides simple flow, economical and optimized production process of transesterification dimethyl carbonate, and reduces steam consumption by more than 10% compared with production devices of the same scale.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the flow chart of the producing dimethyl carbonate. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst 1 is homogeneous catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction material cooled by the cooling evaporator is connected with the pump, where one part of the outlet of the pump enters into the pipe type reactor, the other part into the heat-insulating pipe type reactor from the bottom, the top outlet pipeline of the insulating pipe type reactor is connected with the pressure adjusting valve after the pressure adjusting control, the material enters the upper part of the flash tank is provided with a demister, the evaporated light component is separated from the top gas phase port, entering into the condenser, the non-condensable gas passing into the subsequent device for processing, the liquid phase material enters the upper part of the evaporation separator from the bottom outlet, the inlet of the evaporation separator is connected with the bottom part liquid phase outlet tank through the pipeline, the recovered catalyst 1 is connected with the inlet of the pump through the bottom outlet of the evaporation separator, the catalyst 1 is returned into the bottom of the tubular reactor, the upper part of the reaction rectifying tower is provided with a rectifying section, the liquid phase raw material comprises propylene carbonate or ethylene carbonate and methanol, the catalyst 2 enters from the lower part of the rectifying section, the top part of the reaction rectifying tower is provided with a gas phase outlet is connected with the condenser, the condensed liquid enters into the reflux tank, the back part of the reflux of the pump, the other part enters into the subsequent methanol/methyl carbonate azeotrope separation process, the lower part of the reaction rectifying tower is provided with a reboiler for providing heat and the product propylene glycol or ethylene glycol, the unreacted methanol, and catalyst 2 through the bottom outlet pipeline is connected with the pump and entering into the subsequent separation process. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of the tubular reactor is 130-160 degrees C. The operation pressure of the tubular reactor is 2-6 MPa. The flow of the liquid-phase methanol in the evaporation cooler is controlled by the temperature of the reaction material flowing out of the evaporation cooler.

PN CN113350276-A
TI Preparing octreotide acetate injection comprises e.g. mixing lactic acid, mannitol and water for injection, adjusting pH, adding octreotide acetate and filtering to obtain octreotide acetate injection.
AU ZHU L
   CHEN C
   DING J
AE SPH NO 1 BIOCHEMICAL & PHARM CO LTD (SHPH-C)
GA 2021A7551Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing octreotide acetate injection comprises (i) mixing lactic acid, mannitol and water for injection of 85-90%; (ii) adjusting the pH value of the mixture and adding octreotide acetate; (iii) filtering the mixture to obtain octreotide acetate injection; and (iv) adding up to the total amount of water for injection, where the 1l octreotide acetate injection comprises 3.4-3.8 g lactic acid, 40-45 g mannitol, 0.1 g octreotide acetate and water for injection.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing octreotide acetate injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The can effectively improves the stability of the octreotide acetate injection, improves the stability of the octreotide acetate injection at high temperature and keeps high content of octreotide acetate and low maximum single impurity and total impurity percentage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) encapsulation method of octreotide acetate injection, comprising encapsulating the octreotide acetate injection prepared by the above mentioned method, and filling the protective gas carbon dioxide; and
   (2) encapsulated octreotide acetate injection prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises adding lactic acid into the water for injection at 5-25 degrees C and adding mannitol and stirring. The all operations of the preparation method are carried out at 0-40 degrees C, preferably at 15-25 degrees C. The step (ii) comprises adjusting the pH of the mixture using 5-7% sodium bicarbonate solution to 4-4.4. In the step (iii), the filtration is secondary 0.2 mu m filter element sterilization filtration.

PN CN113215419-A; CN113215419-B
TI Extracting valuable element from waste selective            catalytic reduction (SCR) denitration catalyst includes            mixing vanadium-titanium waste SCR denitration catalyst            powder and sodiumization agent, calcining and adsorbing            tungsten and molybdenum.
AU CAO C
   YUN Z
   MIAO J
   NIE H
   WANG R
   LI Y
   PANG Z
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
   JIAOCHENG JINGHUIZHONG ENVIRONMENTAL PRO (JIAO-Non-standard)
GA 202195518D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting valuable element from waste                selective catalytic reduction (SCR) denitration                catalyst comprises mixing vanadium-titanium waste                SCR denitration catalyst powder and sodiumization                agent, adding water and/or high-salt wastewater,                pressing to form block-shaped mixture, drying the                block-shaped mixture, calcining, cooling to obtain                hot calcined material, then mixing with                second-stage eluate, dissolving sodium tungstate,                sodium molybdate and sodium vanadate in hot                calcined material into aqueous phase to obtain                section of dissolution liquid and dissolution                residue, dissolving residual sodium tungstate,                sodium molybdate and sodium vanadate in a section                of dissolution residue into aqueous phase to obtain                second-stage dissolution liquid and second-stage                dissolution residue, passing carbon dioxide into                eluate, adding hydrochloric acid to                vanadium-removed liquid, and adsorbing tungsten and                molybdenum in solution to obtain                tungsten-molybdenum-loaded resin and high-salt                wastewater.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting valuable                element from waste selective catalytic reduction                (SCR) denitration catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can economically extract valuable                element from selective catalytic reduction                denitration catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vanadium-titanium                  waste SCR denitration catalyst comprises oxygen,                  titanium, tungsten, molybdenum and vanadium. The                  sodiumization agent comprises sodium chloride,                  and sodium carbonate and/or sodium bicarbonate.                  Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (a) mixing vanadium-titanium-based                  waste SCR denitration catalyst powder and                  sodiumization agent, adding water and/or                  high-salt wastewater, mixing uniformly, and                  pressing to form block-shaped mixture, (b)                  placing the block-shaped mixture in a                  multi-temperature zone heating furnace, drying,                  dehydrating, calcining, and cooling to obtain hot                  calcined material, (c) mixing hot calcined                  material with second-stage eluate, dissolving by                  ball milling for dissolving sodium tungstate,                  sodium molybdate and sodium vanadate in hot                  calcined material into aqueous phase, and                  solid-liquid separation to obtain section of                  dissolution liquid and dissolution residue, (d)                  mixing a section of dissolution residue with                  water, dissolving residual sodium tungstate,                  sodium molybdate and sodium vanadate in a section                  of dissolution residue into aqueous phase, and                  solid-liquid separation to obtain second-stage                  dissolution liquid and second-stage dissolution                  residue, (e) passing carbon dioxide into eluate                  for converting sodium carbonate in the solution                  into sodium bicarbonate, crystallizing, and                  solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium                  bicarbonate and crystallization mother liquor,                  (f) contacting crystallization mother liquor with                  weakly basic anion exchange resin for adsorbing                  vanadium in the crystallization mother liquor to                  resin to obtain vanadium-removing liquid and                  vanadium-carrying resin, and (g) adding                  hydrochloric acid to vanadium-removed liquid, and                  using weakly basic ion exchange resin for                  adsorbing tungsten and molybdenum in solution to                  obtain tungsten-molybdenum-loaded resin and                  high-salt wastewater.

PT P
PN RU2751403-C1; WO2022035350-A1
TI METHOD OF LASER-ARC SURFACING WITH A MELTING ELECTRODE IN A PROTECTIVE GAS ENVIRONMENT.
AU IONOV A V
   CHIRKOV A M
   LANEV R A
AE IRS LASER TECHNOL CO LTD (IRSL-Non-standard)
   IONOV A V (IONO-Individual)
GA 202199000N
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a method of hybrid laser-arc surfacing on any three-dimensional surfaces and can be used in various branches of mechanical engineering for the restoration of worn-out parts of machines and mechanisms, tools and the production of new ones. The method includes supplying a surfacing wire to the surfacing zone and exposing it to simultaneously reflected laser radiation and an electric arc. The laser beam and the arc torch are tilted at equal angles relative to the normal to the surface to be welded to ensure that the reflected laser beam is directed to the end of the welding wire. The metal is deposited in a protective environment, and, for example, argon or a mixture of argon with carbon dioxide is used as protective gases.
   USE - Mechanical engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - Due to the simultaneous action of laser radiation and an electric arc on the surfacing wire, the productivity of laser surfacing is increased several times and the cost is reduced by replacing expensive laser energy with cheaper electric arc energy.

AU MATIONS E
   WANG H
   LI W
AE DARTMOUTH COLLEGE (DARC-C)
GA 202165591S
AB    NOVELTY - A nonaqueous electrolyte (14) comprises a polymeric component (10) and a ceramic component (12). The polymeric component comprises a polyester-based polymer and a polyether-based polymer. The ceramic component comprises inorganic materials.
   USE - Nonaqueous electrolyte for energy storage device e.g. sodium-based battery such as sodium metal batteries, sodium sulfur batteries, sodium-ion batteries, sodium-air batteries, sodium oxygen batteries, sodium-carbon dioxide batteries, sodium-sulfur metal batteries, and/or sodium-containing metal batteries (all claimed) for electronics and electric vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolyte has decreased crystallinity, excellent ionic conductivity, sodium ion mobility, chemical stability, and provides storage device having excellent flexibility, throughput, low interfacial impedance, excellent performance, capacity, cycle efficiency, cycle life, stable voltage profiles, utilizes low cost polymers and is manufactured easily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) energy storage device, which comprises an anode, a cathode and the electrolyte; and
   (2) manufacture of the electrolyte.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrolyte.
   Polymeric component (10)
   Ceramic component (12)
   Nonaqueous electrolyte (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The electrolyte comprises 0.1-25 wt.% or 25-50 wt.% ceramic component. The weight ratio of polymeric component and the ceramic component is 50:50, preferably 85:15.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic materials comprise sodium super ionic conductors (NASICON) containing sodium-zirconium-silicon phosphate of formula: Na1+xZr2SixP3-xO12, where x is 0-3 (excluding 0 and 3), preferably Na3Zr2Si2PO12. The electrolyte is in the form of a solid-state electrolyte. The anode is selected from sodium, aluminum, zinc, magnesium, titanium, tin, cadmium, lead, copper, lithium and/or metal alloys, preferably a sodium-based anode. The cathode is selected from sodium, vanadium, phosphor, sulfur, aluminum, zinc, magnesium, titanium, tin, cadmium, lead, copper, lithium and/or metal alloys, preferably sodium-based cathode.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyester-based polymer is selected from polyglycolide, polyglycolic acid (PGA), polylactic acid (PLA), polycaprolactone (PCL), polyhydroxy alkanoate (PHA), polyhydroxy butyrate (PHB), polyethylene adipate (PEA), polybutylene succinate (PBS), polyethylene terephthalate (PET), polybutylene terephthalate (PBT), polytrimethylene terephthalate (PTT), polyethylene naphthalate (PEN), polypropylene carbonate (PPC) and/or polyethylene carbonate (PEC), preferably PPC and/or PEC. The polyether-based polymer is selected from paraformaldehyde, polyethylene glycol (PEG) (preferred), polypropylene glycol (PPG), polytetramethylene glycol (PTMG), polytetramethylene ether glycol (PTMEG), polyoxymethylene (POM), polyacetal, polyformaldehyde, polyethylene oxide (PEO), polyoxyethylene (POE), polypropylene oxide (PPOX), polyoxypropylene (POP) and/or polytetrahydrofuran (PTHF). The polymeric component preferably comprises PEO and PPC. Preferred Composition: The electrolyte comprises 25-50 wt.% or 50-75 wt.% polymeric component.

PN CN112916986-A
TI Resurfacing welding of continuous casting roller comprises e.g. removing residual hard surface and defect of continuous casting roller surface, preheating continuous casting roller, using low carbon nitrogen alloy welding wire to weld layer.
AU KONG N
   WANG Q
AE CHANGZHOU BENSEN WELDING TECHNOLOGY CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202169884X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves (i) removing residual hard surface and defects on the surface of the continuous casting roll; (ii) preheating the continuous casting roll, after the temperature of the continuous casting roll reaches 200-300 degrees C; (iii) surfacing a layer on the above-mentioned roller surface through a gas shielded surfacing welding gun using a low-carbon nitrogen alloy welding wire, where the shielding gas is argon and/or carbon dioxide; (iii) cooling the continuous casting after surfacing to reduce the stress of the surfacing layer; (iv) machining the surfacing layer after slow cooling to remove the margin so that the thickness of the single-sided surfacing layer is 1.5 mm.
   USE - Method for resurfacing welding of continuous casting roller.
   ADVANTAGE - The method only needs to weld a layer on the roll surface of the continuous casting roller to form the low-carbon nitrogen alloy overlaying welding layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical composition of the low carbon nitrogen alloy welding wire comprises 0.02-0.06 wt.%, preferably 0.103-0.139 wt.% carbon, 0.06-0.13 wt.%, preferably 0.073-0.077 wt.% nitrogen, 17.0-19.0 wt.%, preferably 12.48-12.55 wt.% chromium, 0.8-1.5 wt.%, preferably 1.03-1.11 wt.% manganese, 0.10-0.5 wt.%, preferably 0.309-0.322 wt.% silicon, 4.8-5.8 wt.%, preferably 3.66-3.86 wt.% nitrogen, 0.6-1.0 wt.%, preferably 0.850-0.861 wt.% molybdenum, less than or equal to 0.03 wt.%, preferably 0.0062-0.0076 wt.% sulfur and 0.016-0.017 wt.% phosphorus, hardness HRC44-50 and iron (remaining amount).
