PT P
PN CN112920052-A
TI Preparing o/p-nitrophenyl ethyl ether involves adding o/p-nitrochlorobenzene and ethanol to a four-necked flask equipped with heating and reflux equipment, then adding N-methylmorpholine, heating, blowing in air and adding ethanol solution.
AU NIU G
   LIU S
   LI M
   LIU B
AE ZHENGZHOU KEKUANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202170608P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing o/p-nitrophenyl ethyl ether involves adding o/p-nitrochlorobenzene and ethanol to a four-necked flask equipped with heating and reflux equipment, then adding N-methylmorpholine, heating up to 65 degrees C, blowing in air for 5 minutes at 25 minutes intervals at a flow rate of 2L/minutes, adding 15-20% potassium hydroxide ethanol solution dropwise, dripping, heating up to 65-80 degrees C, reacting for 5-8 hours, and monitoring the disappearance of o/p-nitrochlorobenzene in the liquid phase to complete the reaction, colling down to 10 degrees C, filtering out the solid salt potassium chloride, then passing in carbon dioxide to neutralize the unreacted potassium hydroxide to produce potassium carbonate, filtering out potassium carbonate, and recovering ethanol and N-methylmorpholine under reduced pressure to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing o/p-nitrophenyl ethyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles ethanol and N-methyl morpholine to obtain product, which is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethanol is 95% ethanol or anhydrous ethanol. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of the volume of the ethanol to the mass of o/p-nitrochlorobenzene is 0.95-1.2ml:1g. The mass ratio of the N-methylmorpholine to the o/p-nitrochlorobenzene is 0.004-0.006:1. The 15-20% potassium hydroxide ethanol solution is mixed with ortho/p-nitrochlorobenzene in the mass ratio of 3-5:1.

MC E10-G03A; E11-F05

PT P
PN CN112919507-A
TI Extracting lithium salt in aluminum electrolyte            comprises e.g. mixing aluminum electrolyte powder            containing lithium element with sodium hydroxide            solution, alkali leaching, filtering, mixing, adjusting            pH value, separating and reacting.
AU YANG W
   XIAO Y
   CHEN Y
   LI P
   WANG L
   HAN D
   KONG Y
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202169886Q
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium salt in aluminum                electrolyte comprises (1) mixing aluminum                electrolyte powder containing lithium element with                sodium hydroxide solution, performing alkali                leaching, filtering to obtain primary filtrate and                residue; (2) mixing primary filter residue with                sodium hydroxide solution, performing alkali                leaching and filtering to obtain secondary filtrate                and filter residue, mixing with sodium hydroxide                solution, alkali leaching, filtering, mixing the                filter residue with sodium hydroxide solution for                alkali leaching operation until no residue is                generated to obtain final filtrate; (3) mixing all                the filter residue with the acid liquid, performing                acid leaching and filtering to obtain third                filtrate and filter residue; (4) adjusting the pH                value to 10-12 to obtain alkaline mixed solution,                introducing carbon dioxide into the alkaline mixed                solution, carrying out solid-liquid separation to                obtain lithium carbonate; and (5) adjusting pH                value and reacting for 12-24 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lithium                salt in aluminum electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high purity cryolite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the steps (1) and (2),                  the alkali leaching is carried out at 80-100                  degrees C for 120-150 minutes. In the step (3),                  the acid leaching is carried out at 50-90 degrees                  C for 12-24 hours. In the steps (1) and (2), the                  alkaline leaching and the acid leaching are                  carried out under stirring conditions. Preferred                  Components: In step (1) and step (2), the                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is                  2.5-5 mol/l. In the step (1) The liquid-solid                  ratio of the aluminum electrolyte powder                  containing lithium element to the sodium                  hydroxide solution is 4.5-30 ml/g. In the step                  (2), the liquid-solid ratio of the primary filter                  residue and the sodium hydroxide solution and the                  liquid-solid ratio of the secondary filter                  residue and the sodium hydroxide solution are                  independently 5-20 ml/g. In the step (3), the                  acid solution comprises hydrochloric acid,                  sulfuric acid, nitric acid or perchloric acid. In                  the step (3), the concentration of the acid                  solution is 1-4 mol/l. In the step (3), the                  liquid-to-solid ratio of all the filter residues                  to the acid solution is 15-45 ml/g.

PT P
PN JP2021083407-A
TI Culturing sample containing mammalian intestinal bacteria in anaerobic environment by autoclaving medium, substituting gas in medium with mixed gas, dispensing medium, inoculating sample, creating anaerobic environment, and culturing sample.
AU INAGAKI M
AE UNIV GIFU (UYGF-C)
GA 2021599166
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing a sample containing mammalian intestinal bacteria in an anaerobic environment involves autoclaving medium, substituting gas contained in medium with a mixed gas comprising nitrogen and carbon dioxide while lowering temperature of medium to room temperature, dispensing medium into cell culture plate, inoculating a sample containing mammalian gut microbiota into a medium before or after dispensing, creating an anaerobic environment by sealing the cell culture plate from which the medium is dispensed together with the formulation that absorbs oxygen, and performing shaking culture for 18-48 hours while maintaining the environment.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing a sample containing mammalian intestinal bacteria in an anaerobic environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to culture mammalian intestinal bacteria in a small amount of medium i.e. reduces amount of medium required for one sample to one-hundredth of existing amount, allows simultaneous testing of many specimens, has high reproducibility, and verifies small amount of sample in an easy manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The cell culture plate is cultured by shaking at 50-200 revolutions per minute (rpm).

PT P
PN AU2021101348-A4
TI Two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet mixed            matrix membrane used in gas separation comprises            two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet material e.g.,            porous heteroatom doped carbon nanosheets and polymer            matrix e.g., polyether copolyamide.
AU LI X
   DAI Y
   YANG X
   HE G
   ZHENG W
   GUO J
   CHU Z
   JIANG H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202152670Q
AB    NOVELTY - A two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet                mixed matrix membrane comprises a two-dimensional                porous carbon nanosheet material and polymer                matrix. The two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet                material comprises one or mixture of porous                heteroatom doped carbon nanosheets, porous carbon                nanosheet composite materials, porous vertically                arranged carbon nanosheets and embedded transition                metal nanosheets. The polymer matrix comprises                polyether copolyamide, polysulfone, polyether                sulfone, polyimide or ethyl cellulose.
   USE - A two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet                mixed matrix membrane for use in gas                separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The matrix membrane has excellent separation                performance and high mechanical strength. The                permeability of the membrane is improved because                the porous structure of two-dimensional porous                carbon nanomaterials has a good adsorption effect                on carbon dioxide. The incorporation of two                dimensional porous carbon nano-materials also                improves the free volume fraction of the membrane,                thus improving the permeability of the                membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet mixed                matrix membrane which involves blending                two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet material                with polymer matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyether                  copolyamide model is PEBAX (RTM: Thermoplastic                  elastomer) 2533 or PEBAX (RTM: Thermoplastic                  elastomer) 1657. Preferred process: The method                  includes (S1) mixing the two-dimensional porous                  carbon nanosheet with 50-80 mass% ethanol                  solution to obtain a two-dimensional porous                  carbon nanosheet solution with the mass                  concentration of 0.2-10 wt.%, (S2) mixing polymer                  particles with 50-70% ethanol solution according                  to mass ratio of 1-5:99-95, stirring at 75-120                  degrees C for 2-5 hours, naturally cooling,                  centrifuging, and taking supernatant as polymer                  solution, (S3) adding the two-dimensional porous                  carbon nanosheet solution obtained in step (S1)                  into the polymer solution obtained in step (S2)                  according to a certain ratio of 1-10:100, after                  stirring for 1-5 hours, ultrasonic treatment is                  carried out for 3-5 minutes, standing for 1-3                  hours to defoam and make a membrane, and (S4)                  desolventizing the membrane prepared in the step                  (S3) to obtain the two-dimensional porous carbon                  nanosheet mixed matrix membrane. The                  two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet is                  prepared by (1) dissolving poloxamer F127 in                  water at 40-60 degrees C, heating the obtained                  solution to 80-100 degrees C, and adding molten                  stearic acid into hot aqueous solution, stirring                  vigorously for 3-5 hours to obtain stable and                  translucent microemulsion, and curing at 28-48                  degrees C for 24-72 hours, where, the mass volume                  ratio of poloxamer F127 to molten stearic acid to                  water is 0.1-0.5:0.075-0.375:10-50 (g/g/ml), (2)                  dissolving resorcinol and formaldehyde in water,                  adding the solution obtained in step (1), and                  mixing uniformly, adding propylamine, the                  solution turns white within 0.5-2.5 hours, adding                  1.5-3 mol/L ammonia solution into the final                  solution, heating the obtained solution to 80-90                  degrees C and stirring for 4-8 hours, where mass                  volume ratio of resorcinol to propylamine,                  formaldehyde and water is                  0.22-1.1:0.03-0.15:0.292-1.46:380-1900                  (g/g/ml/ml), mixing 1.5-3 mol/L ammonia solution                  with the white solution prepared in step (2)                  according to a volume ratio of 1:380, (3)                  centrifuging the solution obtained in the step                  (2) at a centrifugal speed of 5000-10000 rpm,                  washing with deionized water, discarding                  supernatant, and repeating the step (3) for 5-15                  times until the pH value is 6-7, (4)                  freeze-drying the precipitate obtained in the                  step (3) for 24-48 hours to obtain a dried                  two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet                  precursor, (5) carbonizing the dried                  two-dimensional porous carbon nanosheet precursor                  obtained in the step (4), and keeping at 500-600                  degrees C for 2-5 hours in argon atmosphere, (6)                  mixing the solid obtained in step (5) with                  potassium carbonate (K2CO3) in a mass ratio of                  1:n (n=1, 2, 3, 4, 5), and then keeping the                  mixture at 600-700 degrees C for 3-6 hours in                  argon atmosphere, (7) washing the solid obtained                  in step (6) with 0.1-0.5 mol/L hydrochloric acid                  solution and water until the pH value of filtrate                  is 6-7, (8) drying the solid in step (7) at 50-70                  degrees C for 6-24 hours. The specific process of                  centrifugation in step S2 is that the solution is                  centrifuged at room temperature for 10-30 minutes                  at rotating speed of 5000-9000 rpm. Operation of                  the membrane preparation is pouring the solution                  obtained in step (S3) onto a                  polytetrafluoroethylene plate, and standing at                  65-75 degrees C for 24-48 hours to prepare the                  membrane. The specific operation of desolvation                  is that the prepared membrane is transferred to a                  vacuum oven for desolvation at 65-75 degrees C                  for 24-72 hours.

PN CN112778258-A; CN112778258-B; WO2022147872-A1
TI New amide derivative neutral mitochondrial            fluorescent markers useful in preparing mitochondrial            fluorescent labeling reagent targets the mitochondria            of cancer cells and for cell imaging.
AU GE J
   MA W
   SUN R
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202155006M
AB    NOVELTY - Amide derivative neutral mitochondrial                fluorescent markers (A) (I)-(IV) are new.
   USE - (A) are useful in preparing mitochondrial                fluorescent labeling reagent targets the                mitochondria of cancer cells and for cell imaging                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (A) improves optical properties of fluorescent                dyes through creative modification of its structure                to regulate organelle targeting ability of original                dye, biological properties of dyes have been                significantly improved after being connected with                amide compounds and controls the cost of                dyes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amide derivative neutral mitochondrial                fluorescent markers (A) of formulae (I)-(IV) are                new.
   R1 and R2 = H or 1-6C alkyl group, preferably                methyl;
   M, E1, and E2 = 1-6C alkyl groups, preferably                methyl or ethyl; and
   n = 1-3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (A); and
   (2) use of mitochondrial fluorescent labeling                reagent for cell imaging comprising co-cultivating                the mitochondrial fluorescent labeling reagent and                cells at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for                more than 10 minutes, and then using laser confocal                microscope to perform cell imaging, and collecting                fluorescent signal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (A), comprises reacting                  7-amino-3-(4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolan-2-yl)-2H-chromen-2-one                  compound of formula (VII) with                  4-bromo-N,N-dimethylbenzamide compound of formula                  (V) or 5-bromoindolin-2-one compound of formula                  (VI) in weak alkaline conditions and organic                  solvents to neutral mitochondrial fluorescent                  marker of the amide derivative (I) or (II) or                  reacting                  9-(dimethylamino)-3-(4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolan-2-yl)-5H-benzo(a)phenoxazin-5-one                  compound of formula (VIII) with (V) or (VI) in                  weakly alkaline conditions and organic solvents                  to obtain (III) or (IV). Preferred Method: The                  reactions are all carried out in the presence of                  noble metal salt catalysts. The reaction                  temperature is 85-110 degrees C. When the                  mitochondrial fluorescent labeling reagent                  includes (I) or (II), use 405 nm light source to                  excite, and collect the fluorescent signal in the                  range of 410-500 nm; when the mitochondrial                  fluorescent labeling reagent includes (III) or                  (IV), it is excited by 561 nm light source, and                  the fluorescent signal in the range of 570-750 nm                  is collected. Preferred Components: The noble                  metal salt catalyst includes a palladium salt                  catalyst.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mulberry wine comprises (i) cleaning                and crushing the mulberry fruit and then placing at                a temperature of 25-26 degrees C for 1-2 hours to                obtain the raw fruit for wine making, (ii) adding                the flavoring agent to the wine-making raw                material, and then adding potassium metabisulfite                and pectinase to it to obtain enzymatic                hydrolysing, (iii) placing the enzymatic                hydrolysate obtained in resultant step into the                fermentor, while introducing carbon dioxide gas,                keeping the raw material temperature at 5-10                degrees C and leaching at low temperature for 3-5                days, (iv) raising the temperature to 22-25 degrees                C and carrying out fermentation at a constant                temperature after inoculation with yeast, (v)                carrying out fermentation for 3-5 days at constant                temperature, separating the grape mash from the                skin and residue, and continue the pure juice                fermentation, and adjusting the fermentation                temperature to 18-20 degrees C.

PN CN112646225-A
TI Preparing flexible open-cell polyimide foam comprises adding aromatic dianhydride to organic solvent, carrying out esterification, and reacting, reacting, placing the product in a high-pressure vessel, and vacuuming.
AU YUAN C
   ZHENG Y
AE BAODING KAIBORUI MACHINERY MFG CO LTD (BAOD-Non-standard)
GA 202143602N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flexible open-cell polyimide foam comprises (i) adding the aromatic dianhydride to the organic solvent, adding 2.2-4.2 times the molar amount of the aromatic dianhydride aliphatic alcohol, and adding the solvent, carrying out esterification, and reacting for 1.5-5.5 hours to obtain solution A, (ii) adding specific amount of diamine, catalyst, surfactant and capping agent to the solution A obtained in the step (i), and reacting for 0.5-3.5 hours to obtain solution B, and(iii) placing the product obtained in the step (ii) in a high-pressure vessel, vacuuming and removing air, filling with carbon dioxide, replacing for three times, then heating and pressurizing for constant period of time, cooling, releasing pressure to obtain cross-linked polyimide foam.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing flexible open-cell polyimide foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam has low cost, good flexibility and high open cell rate, excellent thermal stability, excellent flame retardancy, outstanding dielectric properties and good chemical stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the method comprises drying obtained solution B for removing the organic solvent, and then pulverizing to obtain precursor powder, and then placing the precursor powder in a high-pressure container. In the step (i), the ratio of the amount of fatty alcohol to aromatic dianhydride is 2.2-4.2:1, mass ratio of organic solvent and aromatic dianhydride is 100:22-410, esterification temperature is 55-75 degrees C, and the esterification time is 1.5-5.5 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the aromatic dianhydride is mixture of 1,2,4,5-pyromellitic dianhydride (PMDA), 3,3',4,4 diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic acid dianhydride (ODPA), 3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic dianhydride (TDA, bisphenol A type diphenyl ether dianhydride (BPADA), 2,2-bis (3,4-phthalic anhydride) hexafluoropropane (sic) or 1,3-bis (3, 4-phthalic anhydride) hexafluoro propane (sic), 3, 34, 4'-diphenyl sulfide tetracarboxylic dianhydride (sic), 3,34,4-benzene diether tetracarboxylic dianhydride (sic), tetrabromophthalic anhydride, and/or 4,4-(hexafluoroisopropylene) diphthalic anhydride (sic). In the step (i), the organic solvents is mixture of tetrahydrofuran, 2-chloropyridine, 2,6-dichloropyridine, dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, diethylene glycol monomethyl ether, propylene glycol methyl ether or dipropylene glycol dimethyl ether, and N-methylpyrrolidone. In the step (i), the fatty alcohol is mixture of ethanol, methanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, ethylene glycol, butanol, and/or pentanol. In the step (ii), diamines are mixture of 1,4-p-phenylenediamine, 1,3-m-phenylenediamine, 4,4-diaminodiphenyl ether, 3,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether, 4,4'-bis(3-aminophenoxy)benzophenone, 4,4'-bis(3-aminophenoxy)diphenylsulfone and/or 2,2-bis(4-(4-aminophenoxy)phenyl)propan. In the step (ii), the catalyst is mixture of diethanolamine, triethanolamine, dicyclohexylmethanediamine, diphenylmethanediamine, isophordionediamine, 3,5-diethyltoluene diamine, 3,5-dimethylthiotoluene diamine amine, triethylenediamine, stannous octoate, dibutyltin dilaurate, and/or dibutyltin octoate diacetate. The surfactants are mixture of polyether modified siloxane, polyether modified silicone oil, silicones, silicone glycol copolymer, silicone polyether copolymer, 3-trimeric epoxy hexafluoropropane amidopropyl betaine, and/or water-soluble silicone oil. The mass ratio between diamine, catalyst and the capping agent added to the solution A is 100:1.2-22:5.5-155.

PT P
PN CN112211608-A
TI Shale reservoir micro-fracture self-supporting            fracturing involves non-uniform etching treatment to            crack wall surface of clay acid, constructing            low-viscosity soil acid in small micro-scale crack, and            filling adhesive slip water with seam.
AU JIANG T
   WANG H
   ZHONG G
   ZUO L
   BIAN X
   WEI R
   XIAO B
   SU Y
   LI K
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM ENG (SNPC-C)
GA 2021081061
AB    NOVELTY - Shale reservoir micro-fracture self-supporting                fracturing involves pre-fracturing reservoir                characteristic parameter evaluation, fracture                parameter and perforation position optimization,                and fracturing construction parameter optimization.                The acid pretreatment and pre-seaming of                high-viscosity glue is performed. The sticky                slippery water extends the branch cracks and small                micro cracks. Supercritical carbon dioxide is                injected to further extend the small micro cracks.                The clay acid performs non-uniform etching                treatment on the crack wall surface. Low clay acid                in small and micro-scale cracks is constructed. The                slit is filled with sticky and slippery water                carrying proppant.
   USE - Method for shale reservoir micro-fracture                self-supporting fracturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can form different slit width                scale, self-supporting complex crack system                connected with each other, improves the high                brittle shale, deep shale and difficult sand of                complex shale fracture system guide ability and                crack effective change volume.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The acid displacement                  is 1 to 2 m3/minutes, and the acid liquid volume                  is 10-20 m3. The viscosity of the high-viscosity                  glue is 80-200 megaPascal-second. The                  pre-fracture is carried out at 50-70% of the                  optimized maximum displacement, and the amount of                  high-viscosity glue is 15-20% of the total amount                  of fracturing fluid. When the length of the joint                  reaches the length of the designed main crack,                  the medium sticky slippery water is used to                  further extend the branch joints and small cracks                  according to 70-90% of the optimally designed                  maximum displacement. The amount of medium slimy                  water is 60-70% of the total amount of fracturing                  fluid. The viscosity of the medium slimy water is                  6-12 megaPascal-second. The viscosity ratio of                  medium slimy water and high viscosity glue is                  less than 1/6. The supercritical carbon dioxide                  is injected with the optimally designed maximum                  displacement and further uses the secondary                  viscous fingering action to extend the small                  micro-cracks. The amount of supercritical carbon                  dioxide is 10-15% of the total amount of                  fracturing fluid. Three viscous fingering                  constructions in the main crack are carried out                  with a medium clay acid, and the crack wall                  surface is non-uniformly etched. The displacement                  of medium clay acid is 30-50% of the design                  displacement, and the amount of medium clay acid                  is 20-30% of the total amount of clay acid. The                  viscosity of the medium clay acid is 10-15                  megaPascal-second. Low-clay acid is injected                  according to 40-60% of the optimized maximum                  displacement, and four viscous finger                  penetrations are carried out in small and                  micro-scale cracks. The dosage of low clay acid                  is 65-75% of the total amount of clay acid. The                  viscosity of the low clay acid is 1-3                  megaPascal-second. The viscosity ratio of low                  clay acid to medium clay acid is less than 1/6.                  The displacement is increased to the optimized                  maximum displacement. Medium-viscosity and                  slippery water is used to add 3-5 m3 of 50/80                  mesh proppant at a sand-liquid ratio of 6-9%.                  Immediately after replacing 1.2 to 1.5 times the                  volume of the wellbore, the slippery water,                  complete the final filling of the joints, that                  results in completing the entire fracturing                  construction. The volume density of the 50/80                  mesh proppant is lower than 1.2 g/cm3.
DC Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC Q49-A; Q49-V35
IP E21B-043/267; E21B-043/27
PD CN112211608-A   12 Jan 2021   E21B-043/267   202119   Pages: 13   Chinese

PN CN112142089-A
TI Synthesizing azurite mineral pigment, by reacting soluble copper salt with acid-containing substance in water, controlling reaction solution, performing dispersion treatment, separating solid and liquid, washing and drying turbid liquid.
AU QIN Y
   ZHOU G
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021048649
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing azurite mineral pigment, involves (a) reacting soluble copper salt with acid-containing substance in water to obtain reaction solution containing carbon dioxide gas and malachite precipitation, where the acid-containing substance comprises carbonate and bicarbonate, (b) controlling reaction solution in above system, controlling the temperature to 15 degrees C for leaving still and aging, and performing dispersion treatment to obtain first suspension, and (c) sequentially performing sedimentation treatment, solid-liquid separation, water washing and drying process on first turbid liquid to obtain azurite mineral pigment.
   USE - Method for synthesizing azurite mineral pigment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes chemical methods to synthesize azurite mineral pigments, which standardize the production of azurite pigments, and the synthesized azurite mineral pigment has same physical and chemical properties as natural azurite pigments, ensures few impurities and high purity than natural azurite pigments, lays foundation for large-scale production, and meets the social needs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves adding dry ice to reaction solution, leaving still for aging in closed system, and performing undergo dispersion treatment to obtain first suspension. Preferred Composition: The step (a) involves using certain concentration of copper nitrate aqueous solution for reaction, and the concentration of copper nitrate aqueous solution is 0.1-1 mol/l. In step (a), the acid radical-containing substance is calcium carbonate, which has same number of moles as copper nitrate. The method further involves repeating step (a), (b) and (c) using dried precipitate as raw material to obtain refined azurite mineral pigment. Preferred Conditions: The step (a) involves maintaining reactants in reaction system under interval stirring condition until reaction is completed, uniformly stirring at interval of not greater than 30 minutes, preferably interval of 5-10 hours in total. The step (b) involves leaving still and aging at less than 25 degrees C for 24 hours, and ultrasonically dispersing for 1-10 minutes to obtain first suspension. The step (c) involves collecting obtained first precipitate after performing solid-liquid separation, and washing first precipitate with water several times and drying at 60-100 degrees C to obtain azurite mineral pigment. The method involves after performing three repeated optimizations, the particle size of refined azurite mineral pigment is 20-35 mu m.

PT P
PN CN111944042-A
TI Preparing protein-A antigen by breeding male rabbits, injecting rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus into testis of male rabbits, grinding testicles, and freezing-thawing crude rabbit testicular tissue culture liquid.
AU ZHANG Y
AE VANWORLD PHARM RUGAO CO LTD (VANW-Non-standard)
GA 2020B71898
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing protein-A antigen involves (a) selecting male rabbits and breeding the selected male rabbits in isolation, (b) taking out a tube of rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus from the refrigerator, thawing and transferring into sterile room with a thermos for testis inoculation, (c) injecting rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus into each testis of male rabbits, (d) observing the injected male rabbit twice a day, (e) killing the male rabbit with carbon dioxide and transferring into a sterile room to take testicles, (f) rinsing the testicles and freezing the testicles in a refrigerator for preservation, (g) grinding and freezing the crude rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus in refrigerator for preservation, (h) collecting the virus from the ruptured cell and (i) freezing-thawing the crude rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus 3 times, freezing and storing in the refrigerator.
   USE - The method is used for preparing protein-A antigen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves (i) selecting healthy male rabbits weighing more than 2.5 kg and (ii) breeding each male rabbit selected in isolation. The step (b) involves (i) taking out a 15 ml tube of rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus from the refrigerator at -80 degrees C and (ii) thawing and transferring into sterile room with a thermos for testis inoculation. The step (c) involves (i) fixing the male rabbit on the fixed plate in the clean room of the sterile room and transferring into the sterile room from the transfer window, (ii) disinfecting both testicles of male rabbits with 70% ethanol on the workbench, (iii) using a 1 ml disposable syringe to draw rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus from the test tube and (iv) injecting 0.2 ml rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus into each testis of male rabbits. The step (d) involves (i) stocking male rabbits whose testes have been inoculated with rabbit testicular tissue culture containing vaccinia virus into the rabbit house and (ii) observing twice a day to check the color, swelling and hardness of the testicles in order to determine the date of testicular collection. The step (e) involves (i) observing the male rabbits injected with Vaccinia virus for 4-5 days until the testes meet the requirements and (ii) killing the male rabbit with carbon dioxide and sending to the sterile room to take testicles. The step (f) involves (i) washing the collected testes once with 0.85% saline, and then washing twice with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and (ii) adding the washed testicles into a special stainless steel box and immediately freezing in a -80 degrees C refrigerator for preservation. The step (g) involves (i) thawing the testicles out of the refrigerator at -80 degrees C for 1-2 hours at room temperature, (ii) weighing, using sterilized tweezers to put the testicles into the masher, and adding the equal amount of Eagle's medium protection solution and (iii) crushing to obtain a crude product of rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus, adding the crude product into 100 ml infusion bottles, and immediately freezing in a -80 degrees C refrigerator for preservation. The step (h) involves (i) thawing the infusion bottle containing the frozen rabbit testicular tissue culture containing the vaccinia virus in a 37 degrees C water bath, where the neck of the bottle must be exposed to the water, (ii) again freezing the infusion bottle containing the crude rabbit testicular tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus into the refrigerator at -80 degrees C and (iii) repeating the steps (i)-(ii) 3 times. The step (i) involves (i) centrifuging the crude rabbit testis tissue culture containing Vaccinia virus that has been freeze-thawed 3 times at 4 degrees C in a low-temperature centrifuge at 4000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 20 minutes to obtain the supernatant and (ii) aseptically storing the supernatant, titering, performing comprehensive and neutralization test, passaging into a 20 ml test tube with a lid, adding into 100 ml production infusion bottle and storing in the refrigerator at -80 degrees C.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-B04C1; B04-F02; B04-F11A; B04-N03J; B05-B02A3; B05-C04; C04-B04C1; C04-F02; C04-F11A; C04-N03J; C05-B02A3; C05-C04; D05-H07

PT P
PN CN111892030-A; CN111892030-B
TI Synthesizing and purifying phosphorene comprises e. g. preparing dilute sulfuric acid solution, preparing high-purity zinc phosphide crystal powder and adding high-purity zinc phosphide crystal powder into dilute sulfuric acid solution.
AU CHEN G
   GONG S
   CHEN J
AE SPECTRUM MATERIALS CORP LTD (SPEC-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2976T
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing and purifying phosphorene comprises preparing dilute sulfuric acid solution with concentration of 6-40%, preparing high-purity zinc phosphide crystal powder, adding the high-purity zinc phosphide crystal powder into the dilute sulfuric acid solution, fully stirring under the preset air condition and temperature to obtain crude phosphorene, condensing the crude phosphorane at temperature of -50 to -10 degrees C and drying, obtaining crude phosphorane after condensation and drying to multi-stage rectification to obtain gaseous primary pure phosphorene, spraying the hydrogen peroxide containing free radicals in advance in the multi-stage distillation, using molecular sieve to screen the primary pure phosphorane at least one stage, removing the residual moisture, oxygen and carbon dioxide in the primary pure phosphane, and obtaining high-purity phosphane.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing and purifying phosphorene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the purification of phosphane to obtain high-purity phosphane, and abandons the traditional way of preparing phosphorane by hydrolysis of metal phosphides, and has high reaction yield and purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method also includes passing the primary pure phosphane into a chemical adsorption column containing alkaline substances to remove residual sulfide after obtaining the gaseous primary pure phosphorane through multi-stage distillation. The dilute sulfuric acid solution prepared with a concentration of 6-40%, diluting with 96-98% sulfuric acid in pure water, adding pure water in batches to prepare dilute sulfuric acid solution with concentration of 6-40% by mixing. The preparation of the high-purity zinc phosphide crystal powder includes mixing metal zinc powder and red phosphorus powder in a mass ratio of 3.5-4.3:1, adding them into a high-purity graphite boat, adding into a smelting furnace filled with shielding gas to 100-125 KPa after vacuuming, and sealing the smelting furnace, slowly heating at rate of 2-20 degrees C/minute, heating the temperature of the mixed powder from 100-900 degrees C, carrying out the synthesis reaction to obtain massive zinc phosphide and grinding to obtain zinc phosphide crystal powder. The preset air state is a vacuum state, and the preset temperature is 55-78 degrees C. The hydrogen peroxide containing free radicals is prepared by photocatalytic reaction. The method further comprises preparing crude phosphane, obtaining the temperature of the reactor in real time, comparing the current reactor temperature with the preset temperature, determining the current temperature exceeds the preset temperature threshold, and making issue an alarm command. In the process of preparing gaseous primary pure phosphorane, real-time acquisition of phosphorane gas within a preset distance around the reactor, and when it is determined that phosphorane gas is obtained, an alarm command is issued. Preferred Components: The basic substance is basic alumina with pH value of 9-10, and the particle size specification of the chemical adsorption column is 100-150 mesh. The free radicals are carboxy or hydroxy radicals.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E31-K04

PN DE102019206155-A1; WO2020221638-A1; CN114008008-A; EP3962885-A1; IN202117049551-A; BR112021021636-A2; KR2022064935-A; US2022227689-A1; ZA202107693-A; JP2022530558-W
TI Producing vinyl chloride by catalytic thermal            cleavage of 1,2-dichloroethane, comprises supplying            heat required via liquid or condensing heat transfer            medium, and temporarily partially electrically heating            heat transfer medium.
AU BENJE M
   KAMMERHOFER P
   KREJCI K
   BENJI M
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   VINNOLIT GMBH & CO KG (VINN-Non-standard)
   VINNOLIT GMBH & CO KG (VINN-Non-standard)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP CO LTD (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   VINNOLIT GMBH & CO KG (VINN-Non-standard)
   THYSSEN KRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   VINNOLIT GMBH & CO KG (VINN-Non-standard)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
GA 2020A69262
AB    NOVELTY - Producing vinyl chloride by catalytic thermal                cleavage of 1,2-dichloroethane, comprises supplying                the heat required for thermal cleavage via a liquid                or condensing heat transfer medium, and temporarily                at least partially electrically heating the heat                transfer medium.
   USE - The method is useful for producing vinyl                chloride by catalytic thermal cleavage of                1,2-dichloroethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides improved process for                producing vinyl chloride by thermal cleavage of                1,2-dichloroethane in cost efficient manner;                achieves significant reduction in carbon dioxide                emissions; and provides electrical control                power.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant for the producing vinyl chloride by catalytic                thermal cleavage of 1,2-dichloroethane, comprising                at least one reactor in which the thermal cleavage                takes place and at least a first heating device, by                which heat is supplied to the reaction medium in                the reactor by the liquid or condensing heat                transfer medium, and at least one second electrical                heating device for heating the heat transfer                medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The heat required for the                  reaction is at least temporarily made available                  exclusively by electrical heating of the heat                  transfer medium. The 1,2-dichloroethane is                  preheated and/or evaporated and/or overheated by                  the heat transfer medium. The heat transfer                  medium is at least temporarily partially heated                  by burning at least one fuel and partially by                  electrical heating. The heat transfer medium is                  conveyed in a circuit in which a reactor is                  integrated, in which the catalytic thermal                  cleavage of 1,2-dichloroethane is carried out,                  with a heat exchange between the reaction medium                  of the reactor and the heat transfer medium takes                  place. The thermal cleavage of 1,2-dichloroethane                  is carried out in a temperature of 200-400                  degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: At least one first heating                  device operated by combustion of at least one                  fuel and additionally at least one second                  electrically operated heating device are used for                  heating the liquid heat transfer medium. The heat                  transfer medium is conducted in a circuit and the                  first heating device and the second electrically                  operated heating device are integrated into this                  circuit. At least one first heating device and at                  least one second electrically operated heating                  device are connected in series or in parallel in                  a circuit. The heat transfer medium is conveyed                  in a circuit in which, in addition to the reactor                  in which the catalytic thermal cleavage of                  1,2-dichloroethane is carried out, devices for                  preheating and/or evaporation and/or superheating                  are involved, with a heat exchange between the                  reaction medium and the heat transfer medium. The                  heat transfer medium is conveyed in the circuit                  in countercurrent to the flow of the reaction                  medium through the reactor. The second                  electrically operated heating device is operated                  at least temporarily by electrical energy                  obtained from regenerative sources. The second                  electrically operated heating device is operated                  in standby mode.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN211771406-U
TI High-efficiency utilization device of blast furnace gas comprises blast furnace main body, vent pipe, filter box, and storage tank.
AU WANG A
   WANG H
   LIU F
   WANG Y
   ZHANG B
AE HEBEI SHOUWEIJINGTU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTEC (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020A91054
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a blast furnace gas efficient utilization device, comprising a blast furnace main body, a vent pipe, a filter box and a storage tank; the upper end of the blast furnace main body is provided with a vent pipe; the vent pipe is connected with the filter box; the storage tank is connected with the filter box; the storage tank is provided with a recycling processing structure; The utility model relates to blast furnace iron-making technical field, the blast furnace gas efficient utilization device has simple structure, convenient use, by pressurizing and cooling the blast furnace gas, the mixed carbon monoxide is liquefied before carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas, entering the supercritical state, separating the combustible component, and then using, Compared with the existing directly using blast furnace gas as fuel, the combustible gas generated by the combustion temperature is higher, more pure, can be applied to many fields, the generated heat is much higher than the temperature when mixing combustion.

PN CN111748702-A; CN111748702-B
TI Preparing vanadium oxide from vanadium solution and sodium and ammonium circulation comprises removing silicon from vanadium solution, crystallizing, cooling, dissolving in water, and adding ammonium chloride and ammonium bicarbonate.
AU FU Z
   PENG Y
   LI M
   JIANG L
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2020A12387
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vanadium oxide from vanadium solution and sodium and ammonium circulation comprises (a) removing silicon from vanadium solution, obtaining silicon-removed liquid, where silicon-removed liquid is concentrated to a vanadium concentration of 110-160 g/l, adjusting pH value of system to 7-9 through carbon dioxide, and crystallizing at 45-80 degrees C to obtain sodium bicarbonate and a primary crystallization mother liquor, cooling primary crystallization mother liquor to 20-35 degrees C for secondary crystallization to obtain sodium metavanadate and secondary crystallization mother liquor, (b) dissolving sodium metavanadate in water, adding ammonium chloride and ammonium bicarbonate to precipitate vanadium to obtain ammonium metavanadate and vanadium precipitating upper layer liquid, and calcinate ammonium metavanadate to obtain vanadium pentoxide, (c) adding ammonium bicarbonate to vanadium precipitation supernatant obtained and crystallizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vanadium oxide from vanadium solution and sodium and ammonium circulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the consumption of reagents, and reduces the process cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crystallization mother liquor obtained in the step (c) is returned to in the step (b) to dissolve sodium metavanadate, when the crystallization mother liquor is returned to dissolve sodium metavanadate. The number of moles of sodium metavanadate added is controlled to differ by plus minus 5% from the number of moles of ammonium bicarbonate added to the vanadium precipitation supernatant when preparing the crystallization mother liquor; more preferably the same. The sodium bicarbonate obtained in the step (a) and/or step (c) is returned to the leaching process of clinker after the vanadium slag roasting. The mother liquor of the secondary crystallization obtained in the step (a) is returned and mixed with the liquid after silicon removal, and in the step (a) is concentrated and crystallized to separate sodium bicarbonate and sodium metavanadate. In the step (a), sodium aluminate is used for the silicon removal. The silicon removal is carried out at 50-90 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. In the step (b), the added amount of ammonium chloride is ammonium ion/vanadium is 2-4:1 by molar ratio. The added amount of ammonium bicarbonate is bicarbonate-/sodium=1-1.5:1 by molar ratio. In the step (b), the vanadium precipitation temperature is normal temperature to 50 degrees C. In the step (c), the added amount of ammonium bicarbonate differs from the number of moles of sodium metavanadate by plus minus 5%, further, the number of moles is the same. The crystallization is carried out at 5-30 degrees C. In the step (a), the vanadium solution is vanadium slag obtained by calcification and roasting to obtain roasted clinker, which is obtained by leaching the roasted clinker with sodium carbonate. The calcification and roasting step at least one of the following is satisfied. The vanadium slag particle size is -0.125 mm. The amount of the calcium salt to be calcified and roasted is 0-8% of the mass of the vanadium slag as calcium oxide. The calcification roasting is carried out at 800-950 degrees C for 40-200 minutes. The sodium-containing carbonate is sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate; The sodium bicarbonate concentration is 130-220g/l. The solid ratio of the leachate is 1.5-5 ml:1g. The leaching is carried out at 80-100 degrees C for 30-150 minutes.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC E35-N; M25-A01; M25-B02A
IP C01D-007/02; C01G-031/02; C22B-003/44; C22B-034/22

PN CN111647555-A; CN111647555-B
TI Preparing human adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell exosomes by thawing frozen adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, adding basic medium, centrifuging, subculturing, performing ultrafiltration, concentrating, centrifuging and collecting.
AU YANG G
   ZHAO J
   ZHAO Y
AE HARMONY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HARM-Non-standard)
GA 2020914863
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing human adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell exosomes, involves (i) performing resuscitation of adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells by placing frozen adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells in 35-40 degrees C water bath to thaw, adding basic medium, centrifuging, discarding supernatant, and resuspending cell pellet, inoculating cell suspension to culture flask and placing culture flask in carbon dioxide incubator for 72 hours, (ii) subculturing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells resuscitated in step (i) with culture solution made of basal medium, serum substitutes and other additives for 72 hours, and (iii) harvesting of adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell exosomes by transferring culture flask obtained in step (ii) to biological safety cabinet, collecting supernatant, performing ultrafiltration and concentrating to obtain primary concentrate, centrifuging primary concentrate with 30% sucrose density gradient, and collecting concentrate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing human adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell exosomes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the possibility of cells infected by bacteria during the culture process, and promotes the proliferation of stem cells and secrete exosomes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The other additive comprises antibacterial agents, and proliferation adjuvants. Preferred Composition: The inoculation amount of cells in step (i) is 1x 106-1.4x 106 cells/bottle. The inoculation amount of cells in step (ii) is 1.3x 106-1.7x 106 cells/bottle. The antibacterial agent based on the volume of the basal medium comprises 0.02-0.05 vol.%, preferably 0.03-0.04 vol.% fennel extract, 0.01-0.04 vol.%, preferably 0.02-0.03 vol.% clove extract, 0.03-0.06 vol.%, preferably 0.04-0.05 vol.% honeysuckle extract, 0.02-0.05 vol.%, preferably 0.03-0.04 vol.% Houttuynia cordata extract and 0.04-0.09 vol.%, preferably 0.06-0.08 vol.% radix scutellariae extract. The proliferation adjuvant based on the volume of the basal medium comprises 0.02-0.08 vol.%, preferably 0.04-0.06 vol.% Pueraria lobata extract and 0.2-0.7 vol.%, preferably 0.5-0.6 vol.% vitamin E. Preferred Method: The method involves heating the antibacterial agent and/or the proliferation adjuvant by circulating steam at 100 degrees C for 30-45 minutes and adding to the basic medium.

PN WO2020174392-A1; US2022118436-A1
TI Preparing catalyst for the conversion of hydrogen/carbon monoxide mixtures (syngas) to hydrocarbons, involves contacting an alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst with ammonium ions, causing an exchange of alkali metal ions.
AU BAROCHIA J
   KARIM K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202085521B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst involves contacting an alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst with ammonium ions, causing an exchange of alkali metal ions for ammonium ions on the alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst and producing an ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing catalyst used for the conversion of hydrogen/carbon monoxide mixtures (syngas) to hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare catalyst has a water gas shift reaction that produces less than 8% or less than 5% carbon dioxide from the carbon monoxide (CO) feed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst, which comprises catalyst, where the ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst has an ammonium modified surface;
   (2) a method of producing C2+ hydrocarbons, which involves contacting syngas with the ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst producing C2+ hydrocarbons; and
   (3) a composition, which comprise alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst, ammonium source and a solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ammonium ions are produced from an ammonium source selected from ammonia, ammonium carbonate, urea, ammonium chloride, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium tartrate, ammonium nitrate, and ammonium sulfate. The alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst comprises cobalt and manganese. The precipitating agent comprises alkali metal is a carbonate, bicarbonate, or hydroxide. The ammonium modified Co based catalyst comprises the formula CoMnxOy, where the molar ratio of x is from about 0.5-1.5, and the molar ratio of y is a number determined by the valence requirements of cobalt and manganese. The ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst comprises cobalt and manganese, where the ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst has an ammonium modified surface. The ammonium modified Co based catalyst includes CoMnxOy, where the molar ratio of x is from about 0.5-1.5, and the molar ratio of y is a number determined by the valence requirements of cobalt and manganese, where the ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst has an ammonium modified surface. Preferred Conditions: The method involves drying the ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst. The method involves calcining the ammonium modified cobalt based catalyst. The method involves producing the alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst includes mixing cobalt salt and a precipitating agent includes an alkali metal in a solution, producing an alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst precursor, drying and/or calcining the alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst precursor, and producing the alkali metal modified cobalt based catalyst. The method involves mixing cobalt salt, manganese salt, and a precipitating agent includes alkali metal in a solution, and producing the alkali metal cobalt based catalyst precursor. The selectivity of carbon dioxide is less than 8%. The total C2+ hydrocarbons selectivity is 80%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 2020571986
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating organic zinc catalyst                involves introducing a polyalkylene carbonate resin                solution comprising a polyalkylene carbonate resin                and an organic zinc catalyst into a composite                filtration membrane comprising a hydroxy-containing                material and a polyolefin composite material, in                the form of coating, in which the                hydroxy-containing material is coated on both sides                of the polyolefin.
   USE - The method is useful for separating organic                zinc catalysts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high purity polyalkylene                carbonate solution, is economical, separates                product without contamination, easily removes                filter cake formed on surface of filter membrane,                and reuses filter membrane easily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing polyalkylene carbonate resin                involving polymerizing a monomer composition                comprising a carbon dioxide and an epoxide compound                in the presence of an organic zinc catalyst to                obtain a polyalkylene carbonate resin and a                polyalkylene carbonate resin solution comprising                the organic zinc catalyst, and separating the                organic zinc catalyst by the above-mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydroxy-containing                  material comprises cellulose, glass fiber,                  hydroxypropyl cellulose, polyvinyl alcohol,                  silica and/or alumina. The organic zinc catalyst                  comprises a zinc dicarboxylate-based compound.                  The zinc dicarboxylate-based compound comprises a                  zinc salt of 3-2C aliphatic dicarboxylate or a                  zinc salt of 8-40C aromatic dicarboxylate. The                  epoxide compound comprises at least one of 2-20C                  alkylene oxide optionally substituted with halo                  or 1-5C alkyl, 4-20C cycloalkylene optionally                  substituted with halo or a 1-5C alkyl, and 8-20C                  alkylene oxide optionally substituted with halo                  or 1-5C alkyl. Preferred Method: The hydroxy                  group-containing material is coated to a                  thickness of 10 mu m to 10 mm. The step of                  introducing is carried out at a pressure of 1-10                  bar. The method involves dispersing the organic                  zinc catalyst in the polyalkylene carbonate resin                  solution in the form of particles. Preferred                  Composition: The weight ratio of the polyolefin                  and the hydroxy group-containing material is                  (1:0.1)-(1:10). Preferred Properties: The                  composite filtration membrane has an average pore                  size of 0.1-10 mu m. The polyalkylene carbonate                  resin solution has a viscosity of 10-10000                  cP.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyolefin is a                  film or non-woven fabric formed of high density                  polyethylene, low density polyethylene, linear                  low density polyethylene, ultra high molecular                  weight polyethylene, polypropylene, polybutylene,                  polypentene, polypropylene polyethylene                  terephthalate, polybutylene terephthalate,                  polyester, polyacetal, polyamide, polycarbonate,                  polyimide, polyether ether ketone, polyether                  sulfone, polyphenylene oxide, polyphenylene                  sulfide furnace and/or polyethylene                  naphthalene.

PT P
PN CN111266065-A
TI Preparing glycerol tribenzoate ethyl cellulose            sustained release microcapsules comprises e.g. taking            N,N dimethylcyclohexylamine, glycerol tribenzoate,            ethyl cellulose and emulsifier A, stirring and            uniformly mixing to completely dissolving.
AU ZHENG C
   YAN C
   HUANG Z
   ZHANG T
   WANG J
   WU Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202055059D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glycerol tribenzoate-ethyl cellulose                sustained-release microcapsules comprises (i)                taking N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine, glycerol                tribenzoate, ethyl cellulose and emulsifier A,                stirring and uniformly mixing to completely                dissolving to obtain an oil phase, (ii) adding                emulsifier B to deionized water and stirring to                completely dissolving emulsifier B to obtain an                aqueous phase, (iii) mixing the oil phase and the                aqueous phase, stirring and emulsifying to obtain                O/W emulsion, (iv) passing carbon dioxide into the                O/W emulsion, precipitating glycerol tribenzoate                and ethyl cellulose from the solvent to obtain                microcapsules with tribenzoate as the core and                ethyl cellulose as the wall, allowing to stand,                settling, suction filtering, washing and drying the                precipitating to obtain microcapsules, and (v)                adding the nitrogen to the filtrate obtained in the                step (iv) to obtain N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine and                emulsifier aqueous solution and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing glycerol                tribenzoate-ethyl cellulose sustained-release                microcapsules.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and                environmental friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  glycerol tribenzoate and ethyl cellulose is 1:1.                  The emulsifier A comprises Span-80 (RTM: Sorbitan                  monooleate). The emulsifier B comprises Tween-80                  (RTM: Polyoxyethylene-sorbitan-20-monooleate).                  The mass ratio of emulsifier A and emulsifier B                  is 1:3. The total mass of emulsifier A and                  emulsifier B accounts for 2% of the mass fraction                  of the system. The mass ratio of the aqueous                  phase and the oil phase is 4.5:5.5. The mass                  ratio of glycerol tribenzoate and ethyl cellulose                  in the system is 20%. The introduction rate of                  carbon dioxide per liter of O/W emulsion                  comprises 120-150 ml/minute. The flow rate of                  nitrogen per liter of protonated solvent is                  100-120 ml/minute.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN111254496-A; CN111254496-B
TI Preparing single crystal aza-mesoporous carbon material comprises e.g. providing aqueous solution of structure directing agent, adding phenol, aldehyde slow-release agent, catalyst and urea, and performing hydrothermal reaction.
AU ZHANG Z
   SUN N
   WEI W
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 202053642X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing single crystal aza-mesoporous carbon material comprises (i) providing aqueous solution of structure directing agent, (ii) adding phenol, aldehyde slow-release agent, catalyst and urea into the aqueous solution, and performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain single crystal polymer, and (iii) performing carbonization and/or activation of the single crystal polymer to obtain final product.
   USE - The carbon material is useful as an adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material: has complete single crystal morphology, nano size, three-dimensional multi-level pore crossing channels, high specific surface area, and basic nitrogen-containing groups, and thus it has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption performance and separation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) single-crystal aza-mesoporous carbon material prepared by above method;
   (2) preparing single crystal aza-mesoporous polymer comprising removing the single crystal morphology polymer by removing the structure directing agent to obtain final product; and
   (3) single crystal aza-mesoporous polymer prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The aqueous solution of the structure directing agent in the step (i) is prepared by dissolving the structure directing agent in an aqueous solution at 5-30 degrees C. In step (ii), the hydrothermal reaction is performed at 60-120 degrees C for 2-36 hours. The carbonization and/or activation temperature in step (iii) is 400-1200 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (i), the structure-directing agent is nonionic surfactants, cationic surfactants, anionic surfactants and/or amphoteric surfactants. The mass ratio between structure guiding agent, phenol, aldehyde slow-release agent, catalyst and urea is 1:0.2-0.8:0.2-0.8:0.01-1:0.1-0.5. In step (ii), the phenol is phenol, resorcinol, pyrogallol, p-fluorophenol, o-fluorophenol, m-fluorophenol and/or m-chlorophenol. In step (ii), the aldehyde slow-release agent is hexamethylenetetramine. The catalyst in the step (ii) is alkali catalyst, acid catalyst and/or salt catalyst. The alkali catalyst is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, ammonia, urea and/or thiourea. The acid catalyst is hydrochloric acid, carbonic acid, citric acid and/or lysine. The salt catalyst is sodium carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or zinc chloride. The single-crystal aza-mesoporous carbon material has single-crystal morphology with nano size of 200-1000 nm, and the single-crystal morphology is spherical, diamond-shaped dodecahedron, hexagonal prism and/or cube, and the specific surface area is 600-1000 m2/g.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN111058151-A
TI Method for manufacturing environmentally friendly recycled flannel, involves recycling PET, washing and drying the PET for dehumidification, and after PET is sliced impurities are filtered and removed from the PET slice.
AU SHEN B
AE WUJIANG KAIYUE TEXTILE CO LTD (WUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202036612B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves recycling PET, washing and drying the PET for dehumidification. After PET is sliced impurities are filtered and removed from the PET slice. After processing PET chips are sent to the spinning machine for spinning treatment to obtain recycled polyester fiber. The recycled polyester fiber is spun into recycled polyester fiber yarn by spinning process. The recycled polyester fiber yarn is weaving after processing, and a recycled polyester gray cloth is obtained. The recycled polyester gray cloth is dyed, sanded and shaped to obtain an environmentally friendly recycled flannel.
   USE - Method for manufacturing environmentally friendly recycled flannel.
   ADVANTAGE - The recycled PET is used to make environmentally friendly recycled flannel, which saves the use of petroleum and the flow of carbon dioxide emissions, and effectively reduces the impact of petroleum-based polyester production on global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a environmentally friendly recycled flannel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a recycled flannel manufacturing method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

GA 202036697F
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is prepared by using catalyst precursor having volume average particle diameter of less than 80 mu m, and volume particle diameter D (0.97) of less than 160 mu m.
   USE - Catalyst for oxidative dehydrogenation of butene to butadiene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst efficiently produces butadiene products, which can be used in the large-scale industrial production of butadiene by oxidative dehydrogenation of butane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, which involves (1) mixing at least one of iron source, phosphorus source and zinc source, mixing magnesium source, cobalt source or manganese source with at least one halogen element to obtain a catalyst precursor, (2) molding the mixture comprising the catalyst precursor and pore-forming agent, calcining and drying; and
   (2) oxidative dehydrogenation of butane using the catalyst, which involves contacting butene with the catalyst in presence of an oxygen-containing gas. The oxygen-containing gas comprises at least one of air, oxygen, or a mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst comprises metal-iron oxide: M(power)(II)Fe2O4. The catalyst further comprises additives comprising metal oxide of formula: AaB'bC'cOx.M(power)(II) = Zn, Mg, Co or Mn, preferably Zn or Mg;A = Fe;B' = P;C' = halo, preferably Cl or Br;a = 0.01-2, preferably 0.05-0.5;b = 0.01-1, preferably 0.01-0.5;c = 0.001-1, preferably 0.005-0.05;andx = total number of oxygen atoms required to satisfy the valence of each element in the catalyst.Preferred Property: The carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of the catalyst at 400-450 degrees C is greater than 20% of the carbon dioxide saturation adsorption capacity at room temperature. Preferred Condition: The drying process is carried out at 90-150 degrees C for 1-24 hours. The calcining process is performed at 450-850 degrees C for 1-24 hours. The reaction inlet temperature of oxidative dehydrogenation of butene is 300-500 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of butane, oxygen, water vapor in the reactant is 1:0.1-20:1-20. The molar ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide is 1:0.1-100.

PN CN110986576-A; CN110986576-B
TI Cement firing system energy-saving liner comprises refractory layer, and thermal insulation layer consisting of hollow ceramic microbeads, tin oxide, bismuth oxide, silicate, aluminum oxide, silicon carbide, and chromium oxide.
AU XU J
   TONG C
   ZHANG W
   CHEN Y
AE ANHUI WUHU HAILUO BUILDING INSTALLATION (ACCC-C)
GA 2020313312
AB    NOVELTY - A cement firing system energy-saving liner comprises a refractory layer, and a thermal insulation layer. The refractory layer is attached to an inner wall of a cylinder of each component of the cement firing system. The thermal insulation layer is coated on the surface of the refractory layer, and contains hollow ceramic microbeads, tin oxide, bismuth oxide, silicate, aluminum oxide, silicon carbide, and chromium oxide. The refractory layer of a rotary kiln comprises a kiln opening castable, a magnesium spinel brick, a magnesium iron aluminum spinel, a magnesium aluminum spinel brick, a low thermal conductivity anti-stripping brick DDR-50, a low thermal conductivity anti-stripping brick DDR-30 and a kiln tail castable from the kiln opening to the end of the kiln.
   USE - Cement firing system energy-saving liner.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement firing system energy-saving liner can reduce the heat dissipation on the surface of the system, thus saving coal consumption, electricity consumption and reducing the emission of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The components of the cement firing system include a preheater, a decomposition furnace, a kiln hood, a tertiary air duct, a grate cooler, and a rotary kiln.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The average particle diameter of the hollow ceramic microbeads is 20-40 nm. The average particle diameters of the tin dioxide, bismuth oxide, silicate, aluminum oxide, silicon carbide and chromium oxide are each independently 0.1-0.2 mm. The total length of the cement rotary kiln is 74 m. The independent length of each component in the refractory layer from kiln opening to kiln tail direction is 0-0.8 m kiln opening castable, 0.8-2.8 m magnesium spinel brick, 2.8-27 m magnesium iron aluminum spinel, 27-38 m magnesium aluminum spinel brick, 38-45 m low thermal conductivity anti-stripping brick DDR-50, 45-73 m low thermal conductivity anti-stripping brick DDR-30, and 73-74 m kiln tail castable. The thermal conductivity of the thermal insulation layer is less than 0.013 W/mk.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The thermal insulation layer is formed by applying a thermal insulation paint to the refractory layer, drying and calcining. Preferred Components: The thermal insulation paint contains an aqueous acrylic emulsion, a defoamer, a leveling agent, hollow ceramic microbeads, tin dioxide, bismuth oxide, silicate, aluminum oxide, silicon carbide, and chromium oxide in a weight ratio of 50:(1-3):(1.5-2):(4-6):(3-9):(0.8-1.2):(1.6-2.5):(5-9):(4-8):(0.4-0.9). The defoamer is emulsified silicone oil, polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene pentaerythritol ether, polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene amine ether, polyoxypropylene glyceryl ether and polyoxypropylene polyoxyethylene glyceryl ether, and/or polydimethylsiloxane. The leveling agent is polyacrylic acid, carboxymethyl cellulose and/or methyl cellulose.

PN CN110744656-A; CN110744656-B
TI Purification of pulped bamboo sheet surface silicon impurities by preparing alkali-activated leather fixed Bacillus subtilis, and preparing bamboo sheets by pulping bamboo materials and washing to remove impurities and dust.
AU ZHANG Y
   LUO X
   ZHAO M
   HU S
   YUAN C
   LI C
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020117382
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of pulped bamboo sheet surface silicon (Si) impurities by alkali-activated leather fixed Bacillus subtilis sequentially comprises preparing alkali-activated leather fixed B. subtilis, preparing bamboo sheets by pulping bamboo materials and washing to remove impurities and dust, preparing alkali-activated leather fixed B. subtilis purifying processing solution, soaking the pulped bamboo sheets into the purifying processing solution, introducing boiler purified flue gas carbon dioxide (CO2), keeping purification treatment temperature at 30-50 degrees C for 2-4 hours, filtering, washing and removing the purification treatment solution, where the removal rate of the bamboo sheet surface layer Si impurities is 90-95%.
   USE - The method is for purification of pulped bamboo sheet surface Si impurities by alkali-activated leather fixed B. subtilis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves bamboo sheet surface Si impurities removal rate of greater than or equal to 90%, reducing the silicon interference, improving the bamboo pulp yield and quality, and reduces the cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The B. subtilis is B. subtilis CLB (CGMCC 7683).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The alkali-activated leather fixed B. subtilis is prepared by cutting tanning waste material into 0.5x 0.5 cm particles, soaking in 0.075 mol/L sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution at 50 degrees C, hydrolyzing for 4 hours, washing until neutral, drying at 40 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain alkali-activated leather immobilization carrier, taking B. subtilis CLB powder and water at 1:10 weight ratio, activating for 1-2 hours, obtaining bacteria liquid having optical density (OD)600 of 600 to obtain bacterial cells for immobilization, immersing the alkali-activated leather immobilization carrier in the bacterial liquid, fixing at 4 degrees C for 2-4 hours, after the reaction is finished, filtering the immobilized carrier, and obtaining alkali-activated leather immobilized B. subtilis.
