PN US2019390400-A1; CN110629570-A; TW202001038-A
TI Dyeing textile substrate containing residual oligomers, by attaching textile substrate on mounting carrier to constitute substrate-loaded carrier and overlying textile substrate of substrate-loaded carrier with microporous film.
AU WANG J
   HUANG H
   CHEN I
   WANG Z
   HUANG X
   CHEN Y
AE FAR EASTERN NEW CENTURY CORP (FENC-C)
   FAR EASTERN NEW CENTURY CORP (FENC-C)
GA 2019A7389X
AB    NOVELTY - Process for dyeing a textile substrate containing residual oligomers, involves (a) attaching the textile substrate on a mounting carrier to constitute a substrate-loaded carrier which is to be subjected to supercritical dyeing in a dyeing autoclave, and (b) overlying the textile substrate of the substrate-loaded carrier with a microporous film which includes pores having a pore size such that when the textile substrate is dyed in the dyeing autoclave, a dye-containing supercritical fluid is allowed to pass through the microporous film to permit the textile substrate to be dyed while the residual oligomers entrained in the supercritical fluid are retained by the microporous film.
   USE - The process is used for dyeing a textile substrate containing residual oligomers.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enhances the dying degree of the supercritical carbon dioxide-treated polyester textile, and avoids a water washing process after the supercritical fluid dyeing process to remove the residual oligomers from the dyed textile, thus solving the problem of discharging waste water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dye-containing supercritical fluid includes supercritical carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dye-containing supercritical fluid includes a disperse dye.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The microporous film is made from a polymeric material. The polymeric material is chosen from PTFE, polyurethane and polypropylene. Preferred Properties: The pore size of the pores of the microporous film is smaller than a particle size of the residual oligomers. The pore size of the pores of the microporous film is 0.1-1 mu m, preferably 0.2-0.8 mu m. The microporous film has a thickness of 10-90 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: In step (a), the mounting carrier is tubular-shaped, and the textile substrate is wound on the tubular-shaped mounting carrier around an axis. Preferred Components: The textile substrate is made from a polyester material.

   ADVANTAGE - The food dough formulation reduces carbon dioxide pollution by minimizing 70% wasted cogollo, and allows to obtain a dough composition that contains antioxidants and dietary fiber whose properties allow it to be used in the manufacture of tortilla chips.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing food dough formulation, which involves:
   (A) washing fiber with running water in immersion tank at room temperature;
   (B) heat-treating by microwave of the fibers moistened with water in relation 70-80 ml water for every 5 g fiber for 5 minutes at 190 degrees C;
   (C) disinfecting fibers with citric acid by spraying for 5 minutes at room temperature;
   (D) drying the fibers at 50-60 degrees C;
   (E) crushing in a knife mill with sieve until 90% material passes through 80 mesh;
   (F) mixing with cornmeal in a proportion of 88-95 g cornmeal with 5-12 g sugar cane bud and homogenize; and
   (G) hydrating and homogenizing flour for 10 minutes.

PT P
PN CN110591415-A
TI Preparing agglomeration-resistant modified nanocalcium carbonate by calcium hydroxide solution treatment, carbonizing, modification and suspension treatment, and constant-temperature and pressure spraying reaction of dispersion liquid.
AU GU Y
AE CHANGSHU HONGYU CALCIUM COMPOUND CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7575C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing agglomeration-resistant modified nano calcium carbonate involves: (a) calcium hydroxide solution treatment: adding a certain amount of crystal form control agent into the calcium hydroxide solution, uniformly stirring, and then performing ultrasonic dispersion treatment; (b) carbonizing treatment: under the state of slow stirring and constant temperature, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the calcium hydroxide solution, and carbonizing until the pH value of the solution is neutral; (c) modification and suspension treatment: adding a surfactant into the solution, stirring and mixing, and spraying the mixed solution into supercritical ethanol to form a suspension dispersion liquid; and (d) preparing the anti-agglomeration nanocalcium carbonate: adding the suspension dispersion liquid into a spraying device for constant-temperature and constant-pressure spraying reaction, filtering, and then decompressing and cooling to obtain the anti-agglomeration nanocalcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing agglomeration-resistant modified nanocalcium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified nanocalcium carbonate has high activity, good size stability, excellent performance and wide market prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (a), the concentration of the calcium hydroxide solution is 8-15 mass%; the addition amount of the crystal form control agent is 1-3 mass% of the dry mass of the calcium hydroxide. In step (c), the addition amount of the surfactant is 1-2 mass% of the dry mass of the calcium hydroxide in the step (a). Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the ultrasonic dispersion process conditions are as follows: the ultrasonic frequency is 20-30 kHz, room temperature, and the time is 30 minutes. In step (b), the stirring speed is 150-250 revolutions per minute (r/min), and the constant temperature is 30-40 degrees C; the carbon dioxide gas is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and air, where the volume concentration of the carbon dioxide is 50-99%. In step (c), the spraying process conditions are as follows: the temperature is 50-70 degrees C, the spraying speed is 1-3 ml/minute, and the volume ratio of the supercritical ethanol to the mixed solution is 12-15:1. In step (d), the process conditions of the constant-temperature and constant-pressure spraying are as follows: the temperature is 50-70 degrees C, and the pressure is 1-1.5 MPa. Preferred Component: In step (a), the calcium hydroxide solution is a solution obtained by slaking lime.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).)

   HE X
   LI F
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 202000107D
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering sulfur from industrial by-product                gypsum comprises adding catalyst and reducing agent                into industrial by-product gypsum, mixing, reducing                reaction to obtain reaction products, sorting                reaction products to obtain calcium sulfide, adding                water to calcium sulfide, performing hydrolysis                reaction to obtain calcium hydroxide and calcium                sulfide, separating liquid and solid to obtain                calcium hydroxide solid and calcium sulfide                solution, passing carbon dioxide gas into calcium                sulfide solution to obtain calcium carbonate                precipitation and hydrogen sulfide gas, adding                capture agent and pH adjuster in scrubber to absorb                hydrogen sulfide gas to obtain rich liquid,                entering absorbed rich liquid in oxidation tank,                adding surfactant and foaming agent, passing air in                oxidation tank, separating elemental sulfur                generated by oxidation of rich liquid from solution                with the bubbles floating up to obtain elemental                sulfur.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering sulfur                from industrial by-product gypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical, has high production                efficiency and good product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is one of                  hematite, waste battery anode, chemical waste                  catalyst or electroplating slag and added amount                  is 1-10% of the mass of industrial by-product                  gypsum. The reducing agent is one of waste                  plastic, waste rubber and waste engine oil and                  added amount is 1-10% of the quality of                  industrial by-product gypsum. The capturing agent                  is one of manganese citrate, copper sulfate, and                  ferric lactate and added amount is 0.1-5% of the                  mass of industrial by-product gypsum. The pH                  adjusting agent is one of calcium oxide,                  potassium carbonate, or ammonium carbonate, and                  added amount is 0.1-5% of the mass of industrial                  by-product gypsum. The surfactant is one of                  xanthate, ethyl thiocarbamate and black catching                  agent and added amount is 0.01-0.1% of the mass                  of industrial by-product gypsum. The foaming                  agent is one of cresol glyceryl ether, methyl                  isobutyl methanol, methylpentanol, or                  polyethylene glycol ether and amount of the                  foaming agent is 0.01-0.1% of the mass of                  industrial by-product gypsum.

PN CN110563009-A
TI Carbonizing and decomposing preparation of electrode-level lithium carbonate from fly ash by adsorbing coal ash extracted lithium-containing solution by adsorbent, desorbing, concentrating, filtering, adding carbonate and decomposing.
AU SUN S
   XU Z
   YU J
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2019A6677Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonizing and decomposing preparation of electrode-level lithium carbonate from fly ash comprises adsorbing coal ash extracted lithium-containing solution by lithium ion adsorbent at 10-80 degrees C at purifying liquid dosage of 1-30 BV, desorbing at 10-80 degrees C by desorbent at dosage of 1-30 BV to obtain desorption liquid, concentrating, filtering to obtain filtrate with suspended matter content of less than 0.01 g/L, adding carbonate solution at speed of 0.5-5 L/hour with adding amount of 1-3 molar times of theoretical amount of lithium ion, stirring at 40-120 degrees C, reacting, precipitating, filtering, drying to obtain primary lithium carbonate product, mixing with water, infusing carbon dioxide gas, stirring, reacting, filtering to obtain lithium bicarbonate solution, heating and decomposing at 60-150 degrees C, and crushing.
   USE - The method is for carbonizing and decomposing preparation of electrode-level lithium carbonate from fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves economic benefit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium ion adsorbent is manganese ion sieve, titanium ion sieve, niobium ion sieve, zirconium ion sieve, aluminum salt nickel adsorbent, adsorption resin and/or chelate resin. The desorbent is water, lithium chloride solution, diluted hydrochloric acid and/or diluted sulfuric acid. The carbonate solution is sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate with concentration of 5-40 wt.%.

PN CN110476982-A
TI Biological pesticide comprises vegetable oil, bio-lectin, emulsifier and surfactant.
AU LAI Y
AE GUANGDONG WANWUSHENG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A1276N
AB    NOVELTY - Biological pesticide comprises 10-80 wt.% vegetable oil, 1-80 wt.% bio-lectin, 10-30 wt.% emulsifier and surfactant.
   USE - Used as biological pesticide.
   ADVANTAGE - The biological pesticide: makes the insect pests less resistant; does not stimulate other pests to occur and it is not easy to kill natural enemies; contains pure biological source, thus is safe for humans and livestock, and low lethality to environmental organisms; can be decomposed into water and carbon dioxide by microorganisms in short time; has simple preparation method, which is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of bio-lectin as an agricultural adjuvant, mixed with other pesticides during tank mixing spraying; and
   (2) preparing biological pesticide, comprising (1) providing vegetable oil, bio-lectin, emulsifier and surfactant, (2) adding vegetable oil and bio-lectin simultaneously to reaction kettle for heating reaction, forming mixture of vegetable oil and biolectin, (3) adding emulsifier and surfactant to the mixed solution of the vegetable oil and biolectin, through a reaction kettle reaction, forming mixed liquid of vegetable oil, biolectin, emulsifier and surfactant, and (4) stirring the obtained mixed liquid, and high-speed shearing to complete dissolution to obtain biological pesticide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The biological lectin comprises insect lectin and plant lectin, where the insect lectin comprises tobacco sphingomyces lectin, Helicoverpa armigera lectin, Asian corn borer lectin, and Drosophila melanogaster lectin, the plant lectin includes pea lectin, and snow lotus lectin, the vegetable oil is mainly vegetable oil containing unsaturated fatty acids, the unsaturated fatty acid-containing vegetable oil includes soy oil, corn oil, and coconut oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The emulsifier and surfactant include sodium diglycerol laurate and castor oil polyoxyethylene ether. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the temperature in the reaction kettle needs to be controlled at 40-50 degrees C. In the step (4), the stirring time needs to be controlled within 30-45 minutes. In the step (4), needs to be left to cool to room temperature, and then can be stored in a box.
   ACTIVITY - Pesticide.

The ingot or ingot blanking step is performed by forging or continuous casting and rolling, where the heating temperature is 1000-1250 degrees C, the initial forging temperature is 1050-1200 degrees C, the final forging temperature is 900-1050 degrees C, and the solution treatment after forging is 1050 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and water-cooling. The post-rolling heat treatment step comprises steel forging or hot rolling after heat treatment, quenching at 900-1050 degrees C, holding for 0.5-4 hours, water cooling, where the tempering temperature is 500-650 degrees C, the holding time is 1-5 hours, and the air is cooled to room temperature.

PN CN109717348-A
TI Argon-containing modified atmosphere packaging Cordyceps sinensis fresh-keeping method involves soaking Cordyceps sinensis preservative solution, taking out, drying, soaking in coating liquid, bagging, and filling with mixed gas.
AU BI H
   WEI L
   XIAO Y
   DU Y
   YANG H
AE NORTHWEST INST PLATEAU BIOLOGY CHINESE A (CAPB-C)
GA 201942956A
AB    NOVELTY - An argon-containing modified atmosphere packaging Cordyceps sinensis fresh-keeping method comprises soaking Cordyceps sinensis preservative solution comprising 1-3 mmol/L sorbitol, 1-3 mmol/L vitamin C or or iso-vitamin C, 6-9 mu mol/L phytic acid and 0.4-0.8 mg/kg L-cysteine, taking out, drying, soaking in coating liquid comprising 3-100 mg/mL Cordyceps sinensis ethanol extract fermentation liquid, 5-30 mg/L chitosan oligosaccharide and 5-50 mg/L chitosan glucose compound, bagging, filling with mixed gas comprising 85-95:3-8:2-7:0-5 volume ratio of argon gas, oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and refrigerating.
   USE - Argon-containing modified atmosphere packaging Cordyceps sinensis fresh-keeping method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits Cordyceps sinensis weight loss rate, slows down soluble protein content decrease, delays water activity and polyphenol oxidase activity growth, improves preservation time to 75 days, and improves Cordyceps sinensis color and flavor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Methods: The argon-containing modified atmosphere packaging Cordyceps sinensis fresh-keeping method comprises taking Cordyceps sinensis, ultrasonic washing 2-5 times at power of 250-1200 W and frequency of 40-120 kHz, soaking for 2-5 minutes in preservative solution comprising 1-3 mmol/L sorbitol, 1-3 mmol/L vitamin C or or iso-vitamin C, 6-9 mu mol/L phytic acid and 0.4-0.8 mg/kg L-cysteine, taking out, drying, soaking for 30-200 seconds in coating liquid comprising 3-100 mg/mL Cordyceps sinensis ethanol extract fermentation liquid, 5-30 mg/L chitosan oligosaccharide and 5-50 mg/L chitosan glucose compound, bagging, filling with mixed gas comprising 85-95:3-8:2-7:0-5 volume ratio of argon gas, oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and refrigerating at - 10 to 0 degrees C. The Cordyceps sinensis ethanol extract fermentation liquid is prepared by inoculating Cordyceps sinensis to liquid fermentation culture medium comprising potato, soybean, sucrose, glucose, peptone, yeast extract, mineral salt and vitamin, culturing for 6-8 days, separating solid and liquid, taking fermented Cordyceps sinensis liquid, adding ethanol to adjust ethanol concentration to 70-80%, centrifuging, taking supernatant, removing ethanol, and drying. The chitosan glucose compound is prepared by dissolving chitosan and glucose in glacial acetic acid to prepare 0.5-2 wt.% chitosan glacial acetic acid solution and 0.5-2 wt.% glucose glacial acetic acid solution, mixing and reacting under 103.4 KPa for 15-20 minutes at 121.3 degrees C, and freeze drying.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Preparing high performance water retaining turf brick useful for building community, comprises mixing cement, construction waste granules and sorghum straw smash filler with water to obtain a base, and mixing with microporous plastic.
AU LI F
   LIN W
AE GUANGXI HONGFA SPONGE CITY TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201933500X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high performance water retaining turf brick, comprises (i) mixing cement, construction waste granules and sorghum straw smash filler with water to obtain a base a; (ii) dispersing microporous plastic, 45-60% sulfuric acid and mesh-like starch under ultrasonic wave for 3-5 hours, to obtain microporous plastic deposited with mesh-like starch; melting the microporous plastic at high temperature, stirring while introducing carbon dioxide gas, and then mixing with the base material a and stirring for 5-8 minutes to obtain a base b; (iii) adding additive A, additive B, and additive C to the base b prepared in step (ii) for mixing, stirring, subjecting to vibration and pressure molding to release the mold to obtain a brick body; and (iv) coating the brick body obtained with a pigment, and the naturally maintaining.
   USE - The high-performance water-retaining turf brick is useful for building community, and lawn square.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high-performance water-retaining turf brick comprises 15-35 pts. wt. cement, 20-30 pts. wt. construction waste, 10-15 pts. wt. sapwood straw smash, 1-2 pts. wt. microporous plastic, 10-30 pts. wt. sulfuric acid with a concentration of 45-60%, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. mesh starch, 4-6 pts. wt. pigment, 1-2.5 pts. wt. additive A, 3-6 pts. wt. additive B, and 3.5-7.5 pts. wt. additive C. The construction waste has a particle size of 0.4-0.55 mm. The green stalk straw pulverized material has a particle diameter of 0.15-0.45 mm. The pigment is yellow phthalocyanine green. The additive A comprises 5-8 pts. wt. ferrous sulfate, 4-7 pts. wt. aluminum chloride, 8-15 pts. wt. sodium carbonate, and 10-15 pts. wt. porous calcium phosphate. The additive B includes 6-12 pts. wt. 1-methyl-2-propanol, 4-7 pts. wt. potassium rosinate, and 2-6 pts. wt. ethylbenzene. The additive C comprises 1.3-2 pts. wt. PE fiber, 2-3.2 pts. wt. polybutenoic acid, 1.6-2.5 pts. wt. 1-tert-butoxycarbonyl-3-aminocyclobutylamine, 2.4-3.5 pts. wt. 2,2-bis-(t-butylperoxy)propane, and 1-2.5 pts. wt. lauryl polyoxyethylene ether sulfosuccinate disodium. Preferred Method: The pressure of the vibration press molding in the step (ii) is 15 MPa. The natural conservation described in step (iii) is 26 days.

AB    NOVELTY - Low-speed pre-ignition events reduction                involves lubricating crankcase of engine with                lubricating oil composition, and providing                boron-containing additive and molybdenum-containing                additive, where lubricating oil composition                includes 150 parts per million (ppm) molybdenum                based on total weight of the lubricating oil                composition, and 150 ppm boron based on the weight                of the lubricating oil composition.
   USE - Method for reducing low-speed pre-ignition                events in direct-injection spark-ignition internal                combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces low-speed pre-ignition                events in a direct-injection spark-ignition                internal combustion engine in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                improved fuel economy, reduced carbon dioxide                emissions across engine families, simultaneously                maintained performance in horsepower, and provides                higher power densities, and increases boost                pressure by using turbochargers or superchargers to                increase specific output and down-speeding the                engine by using higher transmission gear ratios                allowed by higher torque generation at lower engine                speeds.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lubricating oil                  composition has 150-1500 ppm molybdenum, 150-1500                  ppm boron, calcium detergent with 0.08-0.5 wt.%                  calcium, 0.12 wt.% magnesium and 0.12 wt.%                  phosphorus. The boron-containing additive is                  selected from borated dispersant, a borated                  dispersant viscosity index improver, an alkali                  metal or an alkaline earth metal borate, a                  borated overbased metal detergent, a borated                  epoxide, a borate ester, a sulfurised borate                  ester, or a borate amide. AU AHN T W
   PARK J M
   JANG T S
   CHOI H J
   MI P
   CHOI H
AE HYEHWA EDUCATIONAL INST (HYEH-Non-standard)
GA 201929704W
AB    NOVELTY - Pharmaceutical composition with an anti-atopic effect, comprises natural product complex oil containing 25 wt.% of araliae cordatae radix oil, 30 wt.% of fir leaf oil, 25 wt.% of peppermint oil and 20 wt.% of mori Ramulus oil as an active ingredient.
   USE - Pharmaceutical composition with an anti-atopic effect used as food composition for relieving pain Method for preparing XXX (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The pharmaceutical composition has an excellent anti-atopic effect and less side effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing pharmaceutical composition, which involves:
   (A) performing supercritical fluid extraction using the carbon dioxide as the supercritical fluid to extract the natural product complex oil of the araliae cordatae radix, fir leaf, mentha herb and mori Ramulus to obtain the supercritical fluid extract; and
   (B) mixing the natural product complex oil of the araliae cordatae radix, fir leaf, mentha herb oil and mori Ramulus to obtain the supercritical fluid extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The natural product complex oil, is at least one selected from the fat material, organic solvent, dissolution agent, thickener and gelling agent, the softener, antioxidant, suspending agent, stabilizer, foaming agent, aromatic agent and surfactant, and further comprises at least one selected from the nonion type emulsifier, filler, the scavenger and chelating agent, preserver, vitamin, blocking agent, moisturizer, essential oil, dye, pigment, hydrophilicity or the lipophilic activator, and the lipid vesicle. The food composition comprises the natural product complex oil as an active ingredient. Preferred Composition: The natural product complex oil contains about 0.5-3.0 wt.% of herbal extract based on the total weight of the natural composition. Preferred Conditions: The extraction is performed at 49-54 degrees C under 340-365 bar pressure for 7-9.5 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Analgesic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2019062929-A1; KR2019022277-A; US10697072-B2; KR2132825-B1
TI Photoelectrode for cathode, comprises catalyst retaining layer binding metal catalyst contained in catalyst layer to reduce desorption of metal catalyst from catalyst layer.
AU SHIN B
   KOO B
   BYUN S
   KOO B H
   GI B
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 201919709E
AB    NOVELTY - A photoelectrode comprises an electrode formed on the substrate (110), photoactive layer (130) formed on electrode (120) and configured to receive light and generate electron, catalyst retaining layer (150) formed on the photoactive layer and configured to improve the mobility of electron and catalyst layer (160) formed on the catalyst retaining layer and containing metal catalyst. The catalyst retaining layer binds the metal catalyst contained in catalyst layer to reduce the desorption of metal catalyst from catalyst layer.
   USE - Photoelectrode for cathode of photoelectrochemical cell used for generating hydrogen by splitting water and reducing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photoelectrode including catalyst retaining layer has improved photoelectrochemical conversion efficiency, improved electron mobility, reduced catalyst loss and stability of the photoelectrode for cathode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) photoelectrochemical cell, which comprises photoelectrode for cathode, counter electrode electrically connected with photoelectrode and an electrolyte; and
   (2) method for preparing photoelectrode for cathode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of overall structure of photoelectrode.
   Substrate (110)
   Electrode (120)
   Photoactive layer (130)
   Buffer layer (140)
   Catalyst retaining layer (150)
   Catalyst layer (160)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photoelectrode further comprises buffer layer (140) between photoactive layer and catalyst retaining layer or between electrode and photoactive layer. The catalyst retaining layer contains carbonaceous material which includes reduced graphene oxide or graphene oxide. The catalyst layer includes an element chosen from platinum, cobalt, rhodium, iridium, ruthenium, rhenium, gold, silver, copper, nickel, molybdenum, iron, bismuth, tin, zinc, gallium, lead, titanium, indium, cadmium, tungsten, tantalum and niobium. The catalyst layer is in the form of nanoparticles, thin film, or three-dimensional nanostructure. Preferred Property: The catalyst retaining layer has thickness of 0.3-3 nm. Preferred Process: The photoelectrochemical cell generates hydrogen by splitting water and reduces carbon dioxide. The forming of catalyst retaining layer is performed by spray coating method.

PN CN109350688-A
TI Composition useful for treating gout and decreasing uric acid comprises Cordyceps militaris, burdock, lily and tuckahoe.
AU TANG J
AE JIANGSU GUOZHEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019192892
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 20-40 pts. wt. Cordyceps militaris, 15-35 pts. wt. burdock, 8-15 pts. wt. lily and 12-20 pts. wt. tuckahoe.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating gout, decreasing uric acid and relieving and treating gout symptoms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of the composition, comprising (a) taking Cordyceps militaris fruiting body, adding water to extract, separating solid from liquid, respectively collecting the aqueous extract and a precipitate, collecting the precipitate, supercritical extraction at a pressure of 25-30 Mpa at a 35-40 degrees C under carbon dioxide flow rate of 50-100 l/minute of 1-2 hours with an aqueous solution containing 20-30 wt.% 1-methyl-3-ethylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate as a cosolvent, collecting the extract, combining with the aqueous extract to obtain Cordyceps militaris extract, (b) taking burdock root, lily and tuckahoe, mixing, crushing or mixing after crushing, adding water to decoct, collecting decoction liquid after solid-liquid separation and (c) mixing the Cordyceps militaris extract and the decoction liquid, concentrating, adding accessories to prepare the composition into a clinically acceptable preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 30 pts. wt. Cordyceps militaris, 25 pts. wt. burdock, 16 pts. wt. lily and 12 pts. wt. tuckahoe. The Cordyceps militaris is Cordyceps militaris fruiting body. In the step (a): the amount of the cosolvent is 0.3-0.8 times of the weight of the precipitate; the amount of water is 5-12 times of the weight of the Cordyceps militaris; the temperature of the Cordyceps militaris extraction step is 60-100 degrees C. In the step (b): the amount of the water based on total weight of burdock root, lily and tuckahoe is 8-12 times in the boiling step.
   ACTIVITY - Antigout; Metabolic.

PN CN109354350-A
TI Processing pig manure used for preparing            fertilizer, comprises selecting pig manure from farm,            sedimenting, filtering, separating pig manure and pig            urine, and separately treating pig manure and pig            urine.
AU ZHANG Z
AE YINGSHANG NANZHAO BULIN ZHIMING BREEDING (YING-Non-standard)
GA 201919201Q
AB    NOVELTY - Processing pig manure comprises selecting pig                manure from the farm, sedimenting, filtering,                separating pig manure and pig urine, and separately                treating pig manure and pig urine. The treatment of                pig manure comprises removing debris in the pig                manure; placing in a microwave device and                sterilizing; composting and fermenting; and drying                using a dryer and crushing. The treatment of pig                urine comprises adding pig urine, washing water and                domestic sewage into collecting tank and stirring;                placing into acidification tank, hydrolyzing, and                acidifying; placing to a gas boiler and preheating;                placing into fermentation tank and anaerobically                fermenting; placing into a biogas digester and                processing to obtain biogas; and separating and                purifying methane and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for processing pig manure used for                preparing fertilizer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases fermentation speed of pig                urine, shortens fermentation time of pig urine,                reduces pollution to the environment, labor                consumption, energy consumption, odor emissions and                production of bacteria during the accumulation of                pig manure, and improves conversion of pig manure                into fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sterilization is                  carried out at frequency of 600-700000 MHz for                  20-40 minutes. The composting is carried out by                  adding straw, mushroom residue and fermentation                  agent. The anaerobic fermentation step is carried                  out with addition of absorbent at 30-35 degrees                  C. The preheating is carried out at 31-35 degrees                  C. Preferred Components: The absorbent is                  sepiolite, bentonite and zeolite.

TI Method for evaluating natural degradation ability of pollutants in petroleum chemical industry sites, involves combining current underground and background groundwater DIC concentration, and estimating degradation ability of site pollutant.
AU ZHANG M
   NING Z
   CAI P
AE HYDROLOGY GEOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENTAL GEOLO (CGSU-C)
GA 201915805W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring DIC and hardness of groundwater in petroleum chemical industry sites. Dominant microbial types in the groundwater of a well are analyzed according to relationship between groundwater hardness and DIC. DIC consumption of microbial degradation is calculated in petrochemical contaminated sites. GA 2018927304

TI Method for fabricating semiconductor device,            involves treating barrier layer, and filling opening            with dielectric material, where barrier layer is more            hydrophilic after being treated, and dielectric            material is formed over barrier layer.
AU LIN B
   CHEN H
   BAO T
AE TAIWAN SEMICONDUCTOR MFG CO LTD (TSMC-C)
GA 201883246R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming an opening (100)                in a conductive layer. A barrier layer (110) is                formed partially in the opening. The barrier layer                is treated, where the barrier layer is more                hydrophilic after being treated, the treating                comprises a plasma treatment process, and the                plasma treatment process uses anhydrous ammonia,                oxygen, helium, argon, nitrous oxide, and carbon                dioxide. The opening is filled with a dielectric                material (200), where the dielectric material is                formed over the treated barrier layer. A conductive                via is formed in a portion of the dielectric                material, where the conductive via is formed over a                portion of the conductive layer.
   USE - Method for fabricating a semiconductor                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing portions of a                low-k dielectric material inside the openings to                provide electrical isolation between the conductive                elements and the lower dielectric constant to                enhance remote control performance.

PT P
PN CN108675262-A; CN108675262-B
TI Preparing hydrogen by catalyzing formic acid comprises e.g. adding covalent triazine polymeric porous material into deionized water, adding water-soluble palladium salt solution and formic acid solution, and hydrogenating.
AU XU Z
   CAO W
   SUN J
   HOU J
   DING G
   ZHENG S
   QU X
   WU A
   ZHANG L
   WENG X
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 201884298V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen by catalyzing formic acid comprises (i) synthesis of covalent triazine polymerized porous material (CTF) by ion thermal copolymerization, (ii) adding covalent triazine polymeric porous material prepared in the step (i) into the deionized water, then adding water-soluble palladium salt solution, stirring, adding alkali to adjusting the pH to 9-11, stirring, washing the product with deionized water until neutral and then drying, and reducing under hydrogen and nitrogen gas atmosphere to obtain supported catalyst palladium/CTF, (iii) adding catalyst Pd/CTF prepared in step (ii) into the formic acid solution, and hydrogenating the formic acid under the conditions of 298-328 K.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrogen by catalyzing formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves catalytic activity of the catalyst; changes charge structure of the precious metal surface; eliminates by-product carbon dioxide production; is simple to prepare the catalyst, and easy to operate; and has high catalytic efficiency and recyclable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) grinding anhydrous zinc chloride and 1,4-terephthalonitrile under nitrogen gas atmosphere, mixing, the molar ratio between zinc chloride and 1,4-terephthalonitrile is 1:1-1:5, transferring the obtained powder to quartz tube, (b) sealing the quartz tube under the condition of vacuum degree less than or equal to 10-2 Pa and heating at 300 degrees C for 60 hours, and then heating at 450-650 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (c) crushing the obtained product and washing repeatedly with distilled water and hydrochloric acid solution to remove residual zinc chloride, (d) washing the obtained black powder with distilled water and tetrahydrofuran, and drying under vacuum to obtain triazine polymerized porous material. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the atomic ratio of the N element on the surface of the supported palladium catalyst Pd/CTF prepared is 8.76-9.89%, and the particle diameter of palladium is 1.58-1.87 nm. In step (ii), the baking temperature is 450-550 degrees C. In step (ii), the reduction temperature under hydrogen and nitrogen gas atmosphere is 200-300 degrees C, and the reduction time is 2-3 hours. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the amount of palladium in supported palladium catalyst Pd/CTF precious metal is 1-5 wt.% based on the mass of the catalyst. The water-soluble palladium salt is palladium(II) chloride solution; and the base is sodium hydroxide or sodium carbonate. In step (iii), the amount of the catalyst per 10 ml of the formic acid solution is 10-50 mg. In step (iii), the initial concentration of the formic acid solution is 0.5-3 mol/l.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Adsorbent used for separating lithium in salt lake brine, comprises lithium chloride-aluminum hydroxide hydrate complex, and has preset specific surface area and adsorption of monovalent lithium ion.
AU XI G

AB    NOVELTY - An adsorbent comprises lithium chloride-aluminum hydroxide hydrate complex (I), and has specific surface area of 350-380 m2/g and adsorption of monovalent lithium ion of 6-10 mg/g.
   USE - Adsorbent is used for separating lithium in salt lake brine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has large specific surface area, excellent durability and high lithium absorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of adsorbent, which involves preparing sodium hydroxide solution having concentration of 3-5 mol/L, heating to 70-75 degrees C, adding aluminum hydroxide powder, heating to 95-105 degrees C, dissolving, obtaining sodium-aluminum oxide solution, mixing with deionized water in volume ratio of 1:4-8, ultrasonically processing, purging carbon dioxide, adding lithium hydroxide and titanium oxide powder, mixing, processing at room temperature for 40-80 hours, gelling, filtering, washing with water, drying at reduced pressure, obtaining an adsorbent precursor and activating with hydrochloric acid to remove lithium; and
   (2) method for separating lithium in salt lake brine.

The raw materials are dried to water content of 30-40%, followed by vacuum microwave expansion. The raw materials are crushed into 100 mu m. The supercritical extraction is done at 35-40 degrees C with pressure of 50-150 MPa and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid for 5-12 hours. The flowrate of carbon dioxide is 10-12 L/hour. The high temperature sterilization is done at 121 degrees C for 15 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises: polyester; partially                aromatic polyamide; ionic compatibilizer; and a                cobalt salt, where the ionic compatibilizer is a                co-polyester containing a metal sulfonate                salt.
   USE - The composition is useful for articles which                are containers (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides the container that                exhibits a burst pressure of greater than 8 bar,                greater than 9 bar, exhibits improved haze as                compared to articles of conventional blends,                exhibits modified crystallization (claimed) during                production, thus increasing the window for                container production, and exhibits both active and                passive oxygen barrier and carbon dioxide barrier                properties; and utilizes the modified PET that                reduces crystallization rate of the polyester used                for the containers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a modified resin comprising polyethylene                terephthalate (PET), 2.5-4.5 wt.% of isophthalic                acid (IPA) and 0.4-0.7 wt.%                5-sodiumsulfoisophthalic acid dimethyl ester                (NaSIM); and

Preferred Components: The polyester comprises PET                  modified with isophthalic acid. The modified PET                  further comprises the ionic compatibilizer. The                  partially aromatic polyamide is MXD-6 of a low                  molecular weight grade. The MXD-6 contains a                  co-monomer. The partially aromatic polyamide                  comprises 2-8 (preferably 5) wt.% of the                  composition. PT P
PN CN108410000-A; CN108410000-B
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester foamed material comprises e.g. placing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester plastic in mold, pressing, quenching into sheet-shaped product, and placing in closed container.
AU PANG Y
   ZHENG W
   LIU W
   WU M
   QIN K
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 201865910A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester foamed material comprises (a) placing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester plastic in mold, pressing into thin plate, quenching into sheet-shaped product, (b) placing sheet-shaped product and first blowing agent in closed container, contacting first blowing agent with sheet-shaped product, where first blowing agent is chloroform, acetone, diethyl ether, pentane, and hexafluoroisopropanol, (c) introducing second blowing agent into the sealed container, where second blowing agent is carbon dioxide or nitrogen, maintaining temperature in sealed container at 40-65 degrees C, converting first blowing agent from a liquid state to a gaseous state, maintaining pressure in closed container at 1-6 MPa for 4-15 hours, and (d) removing gas from sealed container, removing sheet-shaped product, placing in foaming medium, and cooling to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester foamed material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester foamed material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester plastic is poly 2,5-furandicarboxylate, poly2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,3-propanediol ester, poly 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,4-butanediol, poly 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-1,6-hexanediol, or poly(1,5-furandicarboxylic acid 1,8-octane diester). The water content in 2,5-furandicarboxyl polyester is less than or equal to 0.05 wt.%. The thickness of the sheet-shaped product is less than or equal to 1.5 mm. In the step (d), the foaming medium is dimethicone, glycerol, and engine oil. The cell size of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid-based polyester foamed material is 5-50 mu m having 2-15 times expansion rate. Preferred Method: The quenching treatment time is 5-10 minutes. The ratio of the sheet-shaped product and first blowing agent is 50:1-5 (g/ml). In the step (d), discharging gas from closed vessel within 30 seconds. In the step (d), the foaming temperature is 100-160 degrees C, and time is 8-25 seconds.
DC A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

TI Highly-activated carbonic anhydrase, where at least one of at least one of the aspartic acid at position 29, glycine at position 233 and aspartic acid at position 29 of carbonic anhydrase of Bacillus clausii are mutated.
AU XU X
   CHEN J
   MA L
   WEI X
   ZHANG K
   XU J
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 201866108U
AB    NOVELTY - Highly-activated carbonic anhydrase, where at least one of at least one of the aspartic acid at position 29, glycine at position 233 and aspartic acid at position 29 of carbonic anhydrase of Bacillus clausii are mutated, is claimed.
   USE - Used as highly-activated carbonic anhydrase.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonic anhydrase has improved carbon dioxide capture and conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for constructing activated carbonic anhydrase, comprising (i) mutating carbonic anhydrase of Bacillus clausii, (ii) obtaining the gene of interest, (iii) obtaining Escherichia coli BL21 containing the gene of interest, (iv) enlarging the culture of the transformed Escherichia coli BL21, adding IPTG to induce expression, collecting the Escherichia coli BL21 and lysing to obtain lysate and precipitate, identifying the fusion protein by SDS-PAGE and western blot, (v) isolating the fusion protein and expressing using a nickel column, carrying out gel filtration, purifying, renaturing the fusion protein with guanidine hydrochloride, and (vi) measuring the carbonic anhydrase enzyme activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The aspartic acid at position 29 is substituted with glutamic acid, serine, methionine, cysteine or threonine. The glycine at position 233 is substituted with histidine, serine, tryptophan, lysine or arginine. The aspartic acid at position 83 is substituted glutamic acid, serine, methionine, cysteine or threonine. In the step (v), the concentration of the guanidine hydrochloride is 0.3-0.6 mol/l. Preferred Method: The carbonic anhydrase is applied to the hydration reversible reaction of carbon dioxide. In the step (i), at least one of the aspartic acid at position 29, glycine at position 233, and aspartic acid at position 83 of the carbonic anhydrase of Bacillus clausii are mutated. In the step (ii), the process after the designing primers based on the gene of interest comprises extracting RNA from the Bacillus clausii cells, carrying out reverse transcription to obtain cDNA, reusing the primers for PCR and agarose gel electrophoresis, amplifying the target gene by amplification. In the step (iii), the process comprises ligating the target gene to a cloning vector, transforming Escherichia coli DH5 alpha , extracting the plasmid and transforming into Escherichia coli BL21, identifying Escherichia coli BL21 containing the gene of interest.

PN CN108103335-A
TI Method for refining magnesium-zinc alloy crystal grain involves heating magnesium-zinc alloy ingot, adding tin ingot into melt, stirring, adding JDMJ type refining agent, stirring, heating, pouring slag into metal mold, and solidifying.
AU HUANG Z
   ZHOU N
   NONG D
   QI W
   YANG L
AE GUANGDONG INST MATERIALS & PROCESSING (GZNN-C)
GA 201844971L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for refining magnesium-zinc alloy crystal grain involves heating magnesium-zinc alloy ingot to 730 degrees C under the protection of carbon dioxide and 0.2 vol.% sulfur hexafluoride gas mixture, adding 0.042-0.083 wt.% industrial pure tin ingot into a melt, stirring the melt twice for 1 hour, adding JDMJ type refining agent to the melt, stirring, heating to 750 degrees C, maintaining for 30 minutes, cooling to 715 degrees C, pouring the slag into a wedge-shaped metal mold at a preheating temperature of 250 degrees C, and solidifying into a cast state.
   USE - Method for refining magnesium-zinc alloy crystal grain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and pollution-free due to the addition of tin, and provides refined magnesium-zinc alloy grain having improved mechanical properties, average grain size of 130-135 mu m, tensile strength of 250-265 MPa, yield strength of 140-182 MPa and elongation at room temperature of 13.0-13.5%.

AU YUE Q
AE HEFEI ZHONGLONG SHENLI ANIMAL PHARM CO L (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201841069Y

   USE - As sodium selenite vitamin C injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The injection is convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the above-mentioned sodium selenite vitamin C injection, which involves injecting 300 ml water for injection into the concentrate tank, saturating the mixture with carbon dioxide, adding 1 g sodium selenite, closing feed port, stirring the mixture at 60 degrees C for 15 minutes, adding vitamin C to the mixture, adding 50 ml ethanol and 100 ml polysorbate-80 solution to the mixture, filtering the mixture through titanium rod filter for 15 minutes, filling concentrated tank with 100 ml water for injection, adding 600 ml water for injection to diluted tank, stirring the mixture for 10 minutes, homogenizing the mixture, circulating the mixture for 15 minutes by fine filter pump and filtering the mixture.

PN CN107904014-A
TI Composite plant essential oil microcapsule comprises caraway essential oil, peppermint oil, beta-cyclodextrin, gum arabic, sodium alginate, sucrose ester, glycerol stearate and water.
AU DAI F
AE DAI F (DAIF-Individual)
GA 2018300707
AB    NOVELTY - A composite plant essential oil microcapsule comprises 5-20 wt.% caraway essential oil, 1-5 wt.% peppermint oil, 20-50 wt.% beta -cyclodextrin, 5-20 wt.% gum arabic, 2-8 wt.% sodium alginate, 0.2-1.5 wt.% sucrose ester, 0.1-1 wt.% glycerol stearate and remaining water.
   USE - Composite plant essential oil microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite plant essential oil microcapsule has high stability, excellent solubility, oxidation resistance and long shelf life, and is prepared by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of composite plant essential oil microcapsule, which involves (1) drying and crushing caraway and peppermint, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction process, (2) using aqueous solution of beta -cyclodextrin and gum arabic as wall material solution, (3) adding mixed emulsifier of sucrose ester and glycerol stearate to wall material solution, and then adding caraway essential oil and peppermint oil, emulsifying at high pressure, (4) spray drying, after homogeneous emulsification and forming composite plant essential oil microcapsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite plant essential oil microcapsule preferably comprises 12 wt.% caraway essential oil, 3 wt.% peppermint oil, 35 wt.% beta -cyclodextrin, 15 wt.% gum arabic, 5 wt.% sodium alginate, 0.5 wt.% sucrose ester, 0.5 wt.% glycerol stearate and remaining water. Preferred Conditions: The step (3) comprises carrying out homogeneous emulsion process at 75-95 degrees C and 10-20 MPa for 3-8 hours. The step (4) comprises spray drying at inlet air temperature of 120-150 degrees C, outlet temperature of 60-80 degrees C, feeding temperature of 50-90 degrees C and atomization pressure of 0.2-0.5 MPa.

AB    NOVELTY - Agent comprises hydroxyapatite solution comprising hydroxyapatite as an active ingredient, where the hydroxyapatite has an average crystallite size of 3-50 nm in a minor axis direction and a major axis direction.
   USE - The agent is useful for treating cell proliferation disorder i.e. inflammation and cancer, preferably leukemia (all claimed), and reducing induction of vaso-occlusion, cerebral infarction and myocardial infarction. The cancer includes chest cancer, lung cancer, thyroid cancer, head or neck cancer, brain cancer, lymphoma, gastrointestinal tract cancer, nasopharynx cancer, urinary bladder cancer, kidney cancer, pancreatic cancer, liver cancer, bone cancer, muscle cancer, and/or skin cancer. The agent is used in form of injection or a perfusion agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The agent is non-toxic and does not cause adverse side effects, and has excellent dispersibility, solubility and stability. The agent is produced in an easier manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing agent for treating cell proliferation disorder, which involves obtaining the hydroxyapatite sol by adding and reacting calcium hydroxide, phosphate or its salt, and carbon dioxide gases or carbonate.

Preferred Component: The hydroxyapatite has an average crystallite size of 5-30 nm in the direction of minor axis and major axis. The hydroxyapatite sol comprises 0.1-5 wt.% carbonate ions. Preferred Method: The method involves obtaining the hydroxyapatite sol at 15 degrees C or less. The method further involves adding physiological saline and/or Ringer's solution to the hydroxyapatite sol.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Vasotropic; Cerebroprotective; Cardiant. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN JP2018039953-A; WO2018047318-A1; JP6434465-B2
TI Manufacture of fuel involves ionizing molecular aggregate of water, charging fuel oil into stirring and mixing tank, and mixing and stirring ionized water, after adding to fuel oil, while circulating fuel oil using mixer.
AU YAMAMOTO Y
AE YAMAMOTO Y (YAMA-Individual)
   ENECO HOLDINGS KK (ENEC-Non-standard)
   ENECO HOLDINGS INC (ENEC-Non-standard)
   ENECO HOLDINGS INC (ENEC-Non-standard)
GA 201821563M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of fuel involves manufacturing fuel in which fuel oil is mixed and diluted with water by subdividing and ionizing (1) a molecular aggregate of water, charging fuel oil into a stirring and mixing tank, mixing and stirring (3) the ionized water charged with an additive, after adding to the fuel oil, while circulating the fuel oil using a mixer, and repeating the stirring and mixing process with respect to the mixture of fuel oil and water in a state maintained at a temperature of 15-80 degrees C and an atmospheric pressure of 1.5-20 Pa.
   USE - Manufacture of fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of fuel with excellent transparency and combustion property, reduced heat emission amount, and suppressed generation of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, while reducing deterioration and corrosion of combustion chamber and exhaust pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart for manufacture of fuel. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Ionization process (1)
   Additive addition process (2)
   Mixing and stirring process (3)
   Fusion process (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The additive is chosen from sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, magnesium chloride, sodium, magnesium, hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution, and enzyme.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ionization process is performed by irradiating an ultrasonic wave of 10-60 kHz with respect to water. The molecular assembly of water is subdivided by irradiating an ultrasonic wave of 200 kHz or more with respect to water, performing shirasu porous glass film emulsification method with respect to water, adding condensation mineral extracted from the natural ore to the water, or producing anion water by mixing a powder and water as raw material. Preferred Composition: The addition amount of enzyme is 0.004-2 wt.% with respect to total volume of fuel oil and water. The addition amount of sodium hydroxide is 0.001-0.1 wt.% with respect to total volume of fuel oil and water. The addition amount of hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution is 0.001-0.1 wt.% with respect to total volume of fuel oil and water.

TI Manufacture of jagged metal nanowires used in            catalyst material used for catalyzing e.g. oxygen            reduction reaction, involves providing noble            metal-sacrificial metal nanowires and subjecting            nanowires to electrochemical dealloying.
AU DUAN X
   HUANG Y
   LI M
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 201819598A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of metal nanowires involves                providing noble metal (M')-sacrificial metal (M")                nanowires, and subjecting the nanowires to                electrochemical dealloying to form jagged metal                (M') nanowires. At least one sacrificial metal (M")                is different from metal (M').
   USE - Manufacture of jagged metal nanowires e.g.                jagged platinum nanowires used as electocatalyst in                catalyst material used for catalyzing oxygen                reduction reaction, hydrogen evolution reaction,                oxygen evolution reaction, carbon dioxide                reduction, carbon monoxide oxidation, nitrogen                reduction, methanol oxidation, and ethanol                oxidation reactions (all claimed) in fuel                cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces ultrafine                jagged metal nanowires with excellent surface                roughness and stability, which provides catalyst                material having high catalytic activity and                efficiency for catalyzing oxygen reduction                reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) catalyst material, which comprises a                catalyst support and the jagged platinum nanowires                affixed to the support; and
   (2) method of catalyzing reactions using the                catalyst material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                a process for manufacturing jagged platinum                nanowires.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal (M') is                  chosen from platinum, ruthenium, silver, rhodium,                  palladium, osmium, iridium, and gold. The metal                  (M") is chosen from nickel, titanium, vanadium,                  iron, copper, cobalt, ruthenium, palladium,                  silver, molybdenum, and tungsten, preferably                  nickel. The nanowires comprise an alloy of metals                  (M') and (M"). Preferred Properties: The jagged                  platinum nanowires have an average diameter of up                  to 5 nm, an electrochemical active surface area                  of at least 80 m2/g platinum, and a mass activity                  of at least 8 A/mg platinum for oxygen reduction                  reaction at 0.9 V and reversible hydrogen                  electrode.

PN CN107720724-A; CN107720724-B
TI Preparation of high-specific surface area nanoporous carbon material used for electrode material of e.g. fuel cell, involves uniformly mixing arylboronic acid and phenolic resin, curing, crosslinking, and carrying out pyrolysis.
AU JING X
   WANG Y
   WANG S
   LI Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201817194B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-specific surface area nanoporous carbon material, involves uniformly mixing arylboronic acid and phenolic resin, then curing and crosslinking, and carrying out pyrolysis to obtain porous material, treating porous material, removing residual boron oxide and obtaining high-specific surface area nanoporous carbon material.
   USE - Preparation of high-specific surface area nanoporous carbon material (claimed) used for electrode material of energy storage devices such as primary batteries, fuel cells and super capacitors, and for use as storage material for gases such as carbon dioxide, hydrogen and natural gas, and for use as catalyst carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-surface-area nano-porous carbon material is obtained in simple and easy manner by one-step high-temperature treatment of cured resin while avoiding external template agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for high- specific surface area nanoporous carbon material, which has an average size of 10-50 nm and specific surface area of 300-1000 m2/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The arylboronic acid is monosubstituted, disubstituted or trisubstituted arylboronic acid. The arylboronic acid is 2-hydroxyphenylboronic acid, 3-hydroxyphenylboronic acid, 4-hydroxyphenylboronic acid, 3-hydroxymethylphenylboronic acid, 4-hydroxymethylphenylboronic acid, 1,4-benzenediboronic acid, 4,4'-biphenyl diboronic acid or 1-naphthalene boronic acid. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of phenolic resin to arylboronic acid is 100:1-200. Preferred Process: The arylboronic acid and phenolic resin are mixed by heating arylboronic acid and phenolic resin and melt blending, or adding arylboronic acid and phenolic resin to organic solvent, carrying out dissolution and removing organic solvent. The residual boron oxide is removed by washing or extraction with water or organic solvent. Preferred Conditions: The curing and crosslinking process is carried out at 50-200 degrees C for 0.5-20 hours. The high-temperature pyrolysis is carried out by placing cured crosslinked resin in an inert gas atmosphere and held at 250-800 degrees C for 2-30 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The phenolic resin is thermosetting phenolic resin and/or thermoplastic phenolic resin.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V01 (Resistors and Capacitors); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN US2022040669-A1
TI Supported carbon dioxide adsorbent used to remove carbon dioxide from gas mixture, comprises porous substrate on and in which is infused active adsorbent for carbon dioxide comprising e.g. poly(allylamine) or guanidylated poly(allylamine).
AU BALI S
   JONES C W
   KHUNSUPAT R
AE GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
GA 2022249149
AB    NOVELTY - Supported carbon dioxide adsorbent (A1) comprises a porous substrate on and in which is infused an active adsorbent for carbon dioxide comprising poly(allylamine), crosslinked poly(allylamine) with epichlorhydrin or guanidylated poly(allylamine).
   USE - The adsorbents are useful for removing carbon dioxide from a gas mixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbents: are readily supportable on a solid substrate; have a high proportion of primary amines and lower sensible heat when heated; and are less susceptible to oxidative deterioration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) a supported carbon dioxide adsorbent (A2) comprising a porous substrate on and in which is infused an active adsorbent for carbon dioxide comprising poly(vinylamine) or crosslinked and branched derivatives of the poly(vinylamine); and
   (b) removing carbon dioxide from a gas mixture, comprising passing the gas mixture through a porous substrate supporting the sorbent for carbon dioxide, and treating the substrate supported sorbent in a closed container, at a temperature of not greater than 120degrees Celsius, to remove and capture the carbon dioxide from the sorbent and regenerate the sorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide adsorbent has an average molecular weight of not greater than 5000 Daltons. The adsorbent is poly(allylamine) having an average molecular weight of not greater than 3000 Daltons. The adsorbent is a crosslinked poly(allylamine) crosslinked with epichlorhydrin. The average molecular weight of the poly(allylamine) material is 500-2200 Daltons. The porous substrate comprises mesoporous silica or mesoporous-gamma-alumina. The mesoporous-gamma-alumina substrate is a macro- and mesoporous alumina in monolithic shape. The substrate is silica mesocellular foam in monolithic shape. The adsorbent comprises poly(allylamine) crosslinked with epichlorhydrin or guanidylated poly(allylamine), having an average molecular weight below 5000 Daltons.Preferred Process: The sorbent is treated with steam at a temperature of not greater than 120degrees Celsius, obtained as process heat.
   EXAMPLE - 25 wt.% Poly(ethylenimine) branched having molecular weight of 800 Da was dissolved in methanol under stirring for 15 minutes while purging the mixture with argon gas, until the polymer dissolved completely. Then, calcined mesoporous gamma-alumina (MCF) was added to the mixture. The resulting mixture was stirred for 16 hours under an argon atmosphere. The mass ratio of methanol: MCF was always maintained constant at 28:1. The resulting final solid was recovered by removal of the solvent under vacuum and drying under vacuum at ambient temperature for 24 hours to form adsorbent.

   USE - The method is used for treating gas by using a                gas processing device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method suppresses the occurrence of                bubbles in an organic substance generation                device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for treating gas involves an adsorption                step for sending a raw material gas containing at                least carbon dioxide and nitrogen through an                adsorption unit accommodating an adsorption                material for adsorbing the carbon dioxide, and                reducing the carbon dioxide concentration in the                raw material gas, a supplying the raw material gas,                whose carbon dioxide concentration has been reduced                via the adsorption step, to an organic substance                generation device for generating an organic                substance, and a monitoring step for measuring the                carbon dioxide concentration and the nitrogen                concentration in the raw material gas before the                gas is sent or after the gas has been sent through                the adsorption unit, or measuring the concentration                of carbon dioxide and the concentration of nitrogen                supplied to the organic substance generation                device. The adsorption step includes an ability                adjustment step for enhancing the ability of the                adsorption unit to reduce the carbon dioxide                concentration in the raw material gas in a case                where the total concentration of the carbon dioxide                concentration and the nitrogen concentration                monitored during the monitoring step has exceeded a                threshold value. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for a gas processing device comprising a gas                processing apparatus for a raw material gas                containing at least carbon dioxide and nitrogen, an                adsorbing section containing an adsorbent for                adsorbing carbon dioxide, an introducing section                for introducing the source gas into the adsorbing                section, and a deriving section for extracting the                source gas from the adsorbing section, an                adsorption device for reducing the concentration of                carbon dioxide in the source gas, a supply device                that supplies the raw material gas having a reduced                concentration of carbon dioxide by the adsorption                device to an organic substance generation device                that generates an organic substance, and a monitor                unit for measuring the concentration of carbon                dioxide and the concentration of nitrogen in the                source gas before or after passing through the                adsorption unit. When the total concentration of                the carbon dioxide and the concentration of                nitrogen measured by the monitor unit exceeds a                threshold, the adsorption unit increases the                ability of the adsorption unit to reduce the                concentration of carbon dioxide in the source gas,                and a control unit that controls the introduction                unit or the derivation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The adsorption unit in the                  adsorption step includes a first adsorption unit                  and a second adsorption unit that alternately                  perform an adsorption process of adsorbing carbon                  dioxide on the adsorbent and a regeneration                  process of releasing carbon dioxide adsorbed on                  the adsorbent, the capacity adjustment step is a                  step in which the first adsorption unit and the                  second adsorption unit alternately perform the                  adsorption process when the total concentration                  of the carbon dioxide and the nitrogen monitored                  in the monitoring process exceeds a threshold,                  and shortening the switching cycle of the suction                  process and the regeneration process to be                  performed alternately by the first suction unit                  and the second suction unit.

PN JP2018110568-A
TI Cultivating plants in carbon dioxide enriched facility, by supplying biogas to gas separation membrane having different permeation rates for carbon dioxide and methane, and supplying enriched carbon dioxide gas to cultivation facility.
AU TANIHARA N
   NAKAMURA T
AE UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
GA 2018564707
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating plants in a carbon dioxide enriched facility involves supplying biogas to gas separation membrane having different permeation rates for carbon dioxide and methane, obtaining and supplying enriched carbon dioxide gas to a plant cultivation facility.
   USE - The method and system are useful for cultivating plants in a carbon dioxide enriched facility (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes photosynthesis of plants and reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant cultivation system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves supplying biogas to the gas separation membrane in a state of 0-80 degrees C. The biogas is obtained by methane-fermenting the organic waste containing plant culture residues. Preferred Components: The gas separation membrane is a gas separation membrane in which the permeation rate of water vapor and carbon dioxide is higher than the permeation rate of methane, where water vapor is enriched in the carbon dioxide enriched gas. The enriched carbon dioxide gas contains methane, where the methane is burnt and the enriched carbon dioxide gas is supplied to the plant cultivation facility. The gas separation membrane comprises polyimides.

PN CN212832791-U; CN113800454-A
TI Fine brewing fresh beer pressure difference type            filling valve, has inflation tube whose free end is            connected with air source, where end part of inflation            tube is provided with charging valve, and exhaust pipe            provided with vacuum valve.
AU ZHANG Y
AE LANGFANG DAHAO PACKAGING MACHINERY CO (LANG-Non-standard)
GA 202139707B
AB    (CN212832791-U)   NOVELTY - The invention claims a precision brewing fresh                beer pressure difference type filling valve. It                comprises a valve body and a flow guide component                and an air guide component set on the valve body;                The invention opens the vacuum valve and the                inflation valve by opening the vacuum valve; after                1 second, closing the vacuum valve; the opening of                the inflation valve makes the air source and the                inflation tube be conducted; filling carbon dioxide                gas into the bottle; realizing the inner part of                the bottle as deoxidation environment, and using                the second control valve to control the flow rate                of the carbon dioxide gas; then filling carbon                dioxide gas into the bottle again, so that the                bottle and the wine cylinder form a pressure                difference; the size of the pressure difference is                adjustable; opening the first control valve on the                connecting pipe, making the fresh beer in the wine                cylinder flow into the connecting pipe, flowing                into the bottle through the guide pipe, and using                the valve body to control the flow of fresh beer,                so as to control the speed of filling; when the                filling is finished, opening the vacuum valve and                the exhaust valve on the exhaust pipe, discharging                the carbon dioxide in the bottle to the external                environment, so that the air pressure in the bottle                is equal to the atmospheric pressure, the filling                process is finished completely.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - Adjusting process- step for placing the                bottle on the bottle support assembly, starting the                bottle support assembly, driving the bottle to move                in the lower direction of the valve body, moving                the bottle to the proper position, closing the                bottle support assembly; (S1)
   Charging process- step for opening the air                guide assembly, vacuumizing the bottle and                conducting the carbon dioxide gas source and                charging side of the air guide assembly, filling                carbon dioxide gas into the bottle, discharging the                gas in the bottle through the exhaust side of the                air guide assembly and making the inner part of the                bottle form a deoxidizing environment and closing                the air guide assembly. (S2)
   Secondary inflating process- step for using                the air guide component to rush carbon dioxide gas                into the bottle, so that the bottle and the wine                cylinder form pressure difference, closing the air                guide component. (S3)
   Filling process- step for opening the guide                assembly, making the fresh beer in the cylinder                flow into the bottle through the guide assembly,                and controlling the speed of filling fresh beer,                until filling to the set position, and closing the                guide assembly. (S4)
   Exhaust process- step for opening the air                guide assembly, the carbon dioxide gas in the                bottle is discharged to the external environment                through the exhaust side of the air guide assembly,                so that the air pressure in the bottle is equal to                the external atmospheric pressure. (S5)
   Step for starting the bottle support                assembly, driving the bottle to move downwards, so                that the bottle is far away from the valve body.                (S6)
EA    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a bottle on a                bottle support assembly. A bottle support assembly                is started, The bottle is driven to move downward                to the proper position(S1). Opening the air guide                assembly filling carbon dioxide gas into the bottle                to discharge the gas in the bottle through the                exhaust side making the inner part of the bottle                form a deoxidizing environment (S2). In secondary                inflating process (S3) carbon dioxide gas is rushed                into the bottle, so that bottle and wine cylinder                form pressure difference. In filling process (S4)                making the fresh beer in the cylinder flow into the                bottle through the guide assembly, and controlling                the speed of filling fresh beer. The carbon dioxide                gas in the bottle is discharged (S5) to the                external environment, so that air pressure in the                bottle is equal to the external atmospheric                pressure. Starting the bottle support assembly,                driving the bottle to move downwards, so that the                bottle is far away from the valve body (S6).
   USE - Precision brewing fresh beer pressure                difference type filling process for use in beer                production technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing an adjustable                filling speed, improving content of carbon dioxide                in fresh beer, reducing oxidation degree of fresh                beer in the bottle, improving taste of the fresh                beer and reducing device failure rate, simple                structure and easy to realize precise brewing fresh                beer pressure difference type filling                process.
