A                monitoring module monitors an end-expiratory carbon                dioxide and an arterial carbon dioxide partial                pressure curve of a patient in the first                concentration. A second setting module monitors a                second concentration of the carbon dioxide in the                end-extension carbon dioxide. The latter setting                module sets a concentration gradient of a carbon                dioxide air introduced into the breathing machine                on the basis of the former setting module.
   USE - Respirator for mixing carbon dioxide used in                emergency treatment of hospital, intensive care and                bedside breath first aid and treatment of each                department. Can also be used in treatment of                respiratory diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The respirator sets concentration of carbon                dioxide ventilating by the respirator and                concentration gradient for ventilation, so that the                patient sucks carbon dioxide, thus stimulating the                respiratory nerve center, stimulating and                recovering patient respiratory muscle function, and                making the patient to self-breathe, and hence                quitting dependence of respirator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for solving respiratory failure by mixing carbon                dioxide.

PN CN108280789-A; CN108280789-B
TI Fine-scale carbon dioxide emission factor            influence space analysis method involves determining            degree of influence between carbon dioxide emission            factor and impact on carbon dioxide flux at different            time resolutions and time scales.
AU LI Y
   YUAN L
   DAN G
   SHI G
AE CAS GEOGRAPHIC SCI & NATURAL RESOURCES (CAGN-C)
GA 201855992P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a remote sensing                image of a target area, and setting a carbon                dioxide flux observation point of the target area.                The carbon dioxide flux within the set time                resolution and time scale within the carbon dioxide                emission space are calculated. The factors                affecting the carbon dioxide flux in different time                resolutions and time scales are calculated through                a multivariate regression analysis process. Buffer                analysis is performed with the same main attribute                in all grid cells as an independent buffer center,                and the area of intersection of each buffer and                area of uncrossed portion are calculated. The                degree of influence between each carbon dioxide                emission factor and the impact on carbon dioxide                flux throughout the target area at different time                resolutions and time scales is determined,                according to the mutual positional relationship                between buffers and buffer cross relationship, and                the quantized ratio between the intersected and                uncrossed areas.
   USE - Fine-scale carbon dioxide emission factor                influence space analysis method.
   ADVANTAGE - The accounting process for different carbon                dioxide emission sources is clarified, so that                uniformity and the accuracy of the accounting                process are high.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the fine-scale carbon dioxide emission factor                influence space analysis process. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN113418999-A
TI Analyzing aroma components of tobacco flavors, by preparing absorbent cotton roll to cylindrical shape, placing in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction kettle, feeding carbon dioxide to extraction kettle, and analyzing mixed solvent containing tobacco flavor and fragrance components.
AU CHEN W
   HE A
   MA R
   FENG W
   WANG X
   JIANG L
   WANG L
   LI Y
   JIA X
   ZHANG Y
   KOU J
AE CHINA TOBACCO HEBEI IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2021B23320
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-processing method for analyzing aroma components of tobacco flavors, involves preparing absorbent cotton roll into a cylindrical shape, placing in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction kettle, after uniformly spreading the tobacco flavor on the surface of the absorbent cotton, feeding carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle, performing dynamic extraction, decompressing carbon dioxide fluid carrying the extracted fragrance components to 0.1 MPa, heating to 60-80 degrees C, passing into a mixed solvent of n-hexane and anhydrous ethanol in the mixed solvent of n-hexane and anhydrous ethanol, where volume ratio of n-hexane and anhydrous ethanol is 3:7, and the mass volume ratio of the tobacco flavor and the n-hexane-absolute ethanol mixed solvent is 1:1-5, and analyzing mixed solvent containing tobacco flavor and fragrance components collected by dynamic extraction immediately by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS).
   USE - The pre-processing method is useful for analyzing aroma components of tobacco flavors (claimed) and pretreatment of analysis of flavor and fragrance component.
   ADVANTAGE - The method analyzes aroma components simply and cost-effectively with single separating steps and good repeatability, achieves extraction of volatile and semi-volatile flavor substances in different types of tobacco flavors by adjusting parameters such as pressure, temperature and time, provides high purity, which is conducive to chromatographic separation and accurate qualitative and quantitative, obtains fragrance component information, provides high enrichment efficiency and no fragrance material loss, avoids usage of a large amount of organic solvents, effectively purifies impurities in the fragrance raw materials, and pollution of the chromatographic column and the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pre-processing method for analyzing aroma components of tobacco flavors, involves preparing absorbent cotton roll into a cylindrical shape, placing in a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction kettle, after uniformly spreading the tobacco flavor on the surface of the absorbent cotton, where the tobacco flavor uses lotus seed extract, sea buckthorn extract, Peru extract, Sophora japonica or tobacco extract, feeding carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle at a flow rate of 5 l/minute, after static extraction at 15-40 MPa and 35-60 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, performing dynamic extraction at a flow rate of 2.5-5 l/minute for 1-2 hours, decompressing carbon dioxide fluid carrying the extracted fragrance components to 0.1 MPa, heating to 60-80 degrees C, passing into a mixed solvent of n-hexane and anhydrous ethanol in the mixed solvent of n-hexane and anhydrous ethanol, where volume ratio of n-hexane and anhydrous ethanol is 3:7, and the mass volume ratio of the tobacco flavor and the n-hexane-absolute ethanol mixed solvent is 1:1-5, and analyzing mixed solvent containing tobacco flavor and fragrance components collected by dynamic extraction immediately by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS).
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

TI Flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor preparation involves pretreating flexible piezoelectric substrate layer after ultrasonic cleaning of flexible piezoelectric substrate layer to remove organic impurities.
AU XU S
   ZHANG R
   HAN J
   ZHAO X
   LI Y
   CUI J
   HU X
AE UNIV TIANJIN SINO-GERMAN APPLIED SCI (UTSG-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN SINO-GERMAN APPLIED SCI (UTSG-C)
GA 202031292M
AB    NOVELTY - Flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor preparation involves pretreating flexible piezoelectric substrate layer (1) after ultrasonic cleaning of flexible piezoelectric substrate layer to remove organic impurities and inorganic impurities on the surface by using ethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride-trifluoroethylene copolymer, polyacrylonitrile or polyethylene naphthalate. A tantalum zinc oxide piezoelectric buffer layer (2) is prepared on a flexible piezoelectric substrate by radio frequency magnetron sputtering method to obtain a substrate prepared with a piezoelectric buffer layer. The surface acoustic wave converter composed of interdigitated electrode (3) or reflective grid is prepared on the piezoelectric buffer layer side of the piezoelectric buffer layer substrate to obtain a prepared acoustic substrate of the surface wave converter.
   USE - Method for preparing flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor used in carbon dioxide detection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with great flexibility in application environment and working conditions, strong corrosion resistance and good interference and easy promotion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor preparation involves pretreating flexible piezoelectric substrate layer (1) after ultrasonic cleaning of flexible piezoelectric substrate layer to remove organic impurities and inorganic impurities on the surface by using ethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride-trifluoroethylene copolymer, polyacrylonitrile or polyethylene naphthalate. A tantalum zinc oxide piezoelectric buffer layer (2) is prepared on a flexible piezoelectric substrate by radio frequency magnetron sputtering method to obtain a substrate prepared with a piezoelectric buffer layer. The surface acoustic wave converter composed of interdigitated electrode (3) or reflective grid is prepared on the piezoelectric buffer layer side of the piezoelectric buffer layer substrate to obtain a prepared acoustic substrate of the surface wave converter. The interdigital transducer or the material of the reflective grid is a conductive metal or a conductive polymer. The conductive metal is aluminum, copper, and platinum metal or an alloy of any two metals among aluminum, copper, and platinum metal. The conductive polymer is polyaniline, polypyrrole, polythiophene, polyacetylene, organic polymer or polymer conductive silver paste or sintered conductive silver paste. The weakly basic phosphate buffer solution of alanine is used as electrolyte, where acryl is electrochemically deposited on the substrate on which the converter is prepared. The alanine adsorption layer (4) is located outside the surface acoustic wave converter. A weakly alkaline phosphate buffer solution of alanine-graphene mixture is used as an electrolyte to charge the alanine adsorption layer chemical deposition of alanine-graphene adsorption layer (5). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor, which includes a sensitive adsorption layer, an interdigitated electrode or reflective grid layer, a piezoelectric buffer layer and a flexible piezoelectric substrate layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the flexible surface acoustic wave carbon dioxide gas sensor.
   Flexible piezoelectric substrate layer (1)
   Tantalum zinc oxide piezoelectric buffer layer (2)
   Interdigitated electrode (3)
   Alanine adsorption layer (4)
   Alanine-graphene adsorption layer (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flexible piezoelectric substrate layer is sequentially used with absolute ethanol, deionized water, and sodium hydroxide solution 1. After ultrasonic cleaning with deionized water to remove organic and inorganic impurities on the surface, blow dry with nitrogen for use.

GA 2022E1929L
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical water                hydrogen production device, comprising a                supercritical boiler, an air floating bed reaction                kettle, a reaction material feeding device, a                supercritical boiler providing supercritical water;                the reaction material feeding device receives the                reaction material, and discharging the reaction                material; the reaction material discharged by the                reaction material feeding device is mixed with the                supercritical water into the gas floating bed                reaction kettle, in the air floating bed reaction                kettle, the reaction material reacts with                supercritical water to generate supercritical                mixture, the supercritical mixture comprises                supercritical carbon dioxide, supercritical                hydrogen, unreacted supercritical water, the                generated supercritical mixture is discharged from                the air floating bed reaction kettle. TI Manufacture of extruded polypropylene resin foam            particles e.g. automobile interior component, involves            melt-kneading resin mixture containing and composition            containing carbon dioxide gas, granulating the extruded            foamed particles by hot cut method.
AU TOBIMATSU Y
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 2022C9303J
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of extruded polypropylene resin                foam particles involves melt-kneading a resin                mixture containing a polypropylene-based resin                having a branched structure and a composition                containing carbon dioxide gas as a foaming agent,                granulating the extruded polypropylene resin foamed                particles by hot cut method. The hot cut method is                carried out by extruding the composition into the                air through a die hole, cutting in the air using a                cutter, foaming the composition, and cooling in the                air while or after foaming. The discharge rate of                the composition per hole of the die is 0.350                kg/hour or more. The pressure of the composition at                the inlet of the die is greater than the saturation                pressure of the carbon dioxide gas and equal to or                less than the saturation pressure of the carbon                dioxide gas of 4 MPa.
   USE - Manufacture of extruded polypropylene resin                foam particles. Uses include but are not limited to                automobile interior component, cushioning material,                packaging material, and heat insulating                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides extruded polypropylene                resin foam particles having excellent moldability                with high productivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The die hole has a                  diameter of 0.5-2 mm. The extruded foamed                  polypropylene resin particles have an expansion                  ratio of 9-45 times.

PT P
PN WO2020252557-A1; US2022248681-A1
TI Controlling metabolite production in crop of            plants of species, comprises e.g. delivering carbon            dioxide-infused water to leaves by foliar spraying, and            delivering oxygen-infused water to roots by root            feeding.
AU ARCHIBALD J
   JULIUS M
   ARCHIBALD J H
AE CO2 GRO INC (COTW-Non-standard)
   CO2 GRO INC (COTW-Non-standard)
GA 2020C96411
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling metabolite production in a crop of                plants of a species, comprises: (a) delivering                carbon dioxide-infused water to leaves of all                plants within the crop by foliar spraying; (b)                delivering oxygen-infused water to roots of all                plants within the crop by root feeding the plants;                (c) measuring an amount of a particular metabolite                within a subset of at least one plant of the crop;                (d) determining whether the amount of the                particular metabolite is within a target range; (e)                if the amount of the particular metabolite is not                within the target range, adjusting the amount of                carbon dioxide is infused into the carbon                dioxide-infused water, and adjusting the amount of                oxygen being infused into the oxygen-infused water;                and (f) repeating steps (a)-(e) intermittently                until the crop is harvested or until the plants in                the crop reach the end of their life cycle.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling                metabolite production in a crop of plants of a                species (claimed), which is used to reduce                spoilage, improve plant yield, and/or reduce growth                time. No biological data given.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) of measuring                  an amount of a particular metabolite within a                  subset of at least one plant of the crop                  comprises: measuring the amount of metabolite                  present directly; and measuring the amount of                  metabolite present indirectly by measuring an                  amount of mRNA associated with production of the                  metabolite. Preferred Components: The subset of                  at least one plant comprises more than one plant,                  where the step of determining whether the amount                  of the particular metabolite is within the target                  range comprises determining whether a statistical                  measure of all measured amounts of the metabolite                  is within the target range. The particular                  metabolite is ethylene, where step (e) comprises:                  (e)(i) if the amount of ethylene is below the                  target range, increasing the amount of carbon                  dioxide being infused into the carbon                  dioxide-infused water, and decreasing the amount                  of oxygen being infused into the oxygen-infused                  water; and (e)(ii) if the amount of ethylene is                  above the target range, decreasing the amount of                  carbon dioxide being infused into the carbon                  dioxide-infused water, and increasing the amount                  of oxygen being infused into the oxygen-infused                  water. The particular metabolite is vitamin C,                  where step (e) comprises (e)(i) if the amount of                  vitamin C is below the target range increasing                  the amount of carbon dioxide being infused into                  the carbon dioxide-infused water, and decreasing                  the amount of oxygen being infused into the                  oxygen-infused water.
   ACTIVITY - Plant Growth Regulant.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
DC C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN CN112108162-A
TI One-dimensional/two-dimensional composite nano material used for catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises bismuth tungstate nanosheet and cesium bismuth iodide nanoparticles located in nano sheet crystal lattice.
AU ZHANG M
   LIU Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 2020C99822
AB    NOVELTY - A one-dimensional/two-dimensional composite nano material comprises bismuth tungstate nanosheet and cesium bismuth iodide nanoparticles located in bismuth tungsten oxide nano sheet crystal lattice.
   USE - One-dimensional/two-dimensional composite nano material used for catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material contains shared bismuth atoms which provide bridge for electronic transmission between the nanosheet and nanoparticles and can reduce the composite rate of photogenerated electrons and holes of the nanosheet and nanoparticles. The composite material has improved photocatalytic property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A one-dimensional/two-dimensional composite nano material comprises bismuth tungstate nanosheet and cesium bismuth iodide (Cs3Bi2I9) nanoparticles located in bismuth tungsten oxide nano sheet crystal lattice. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the composite nanomaterial, which involves mixing bismuth tungstate (Bi2WO6) nanosheet with coordination unsaturated bismuth atom and cesium iodide in liquid phase, and reacting; and
   (2) catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, which involves carrying carbon dioxide reduction using the composite material as catalyst under illumination condition to obtain carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The nanosheet and the nanoparticles share a bismuth atom. The nanosheet has (001) crystal face. The composite nanomaterial has transverse size of 30-100 nm. Preferred Process: The process involves preparing bismuth tungstate nanosheet with coordinated unsaturated bismuth atom by mixing bismuth nitrate aqueous solution, sodium tungstate aqueous solution and potassium iodide aqueous solution and reacting at 100-160 degrees C, mixing the nanosheet with cesium iodide solution and reacting. Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of bismuth nitrate, sodium tungstate, potassium iodide and cesium iodide is 0.5-2.5:0.25-1.25:0.025-0.125:0.006-0.031.

PN US2020063275-A1
TI Electrochemical reduction used for forming syngas, involves providing metal substrate, forming self-assembled monolayer comprising organic ligand containing surface and opposing end, and contacting carbon dioxide and water with electrode.
AU JIA H
   MCDONALD K J
AE TOYOTA MOTOR ENG & MFG NORTH AMERICA INC (TOYT-C)
GA 202017539P
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and water involves providing a metal substrate (15) formed of a metal having a low carbon monoxide bonding strength, forming a self-assembled monolayer (20) bonded to the metal substrate, contacting carbon dioxide and water with the electrode (10) comprising the self assembled monolayer to form carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The selectivity of reaction products of carbon monoxide and hydrogen produced by the electrode is regulated to a bare metal substrate. The self-assembled monolayer comprises an organic ligand (25) comprising a surface end (30) containing a reactive group bonded to the metal substrate and an opposing end containing an organic functional group regulating a ratio of reaction products.
   USE - Electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and water used for forming syngas (mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide) used as feedstock in Fischer-Tropsch process for producing liquid hydrocarbons.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the self-assembled monolayer attached to the metal substrate.
   Electrode (10)
   Metal substrate (15)
   Self-assembled monolayer (20)
   Organic ligand (25)
   Surface end (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal substrate is chosen from gold, silver, zinc, palladium and gallium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand comprises 2-20 carbon atoms. The reactive group forms a covalent bond with the metal substrate. The reactive group is a thio group. The organic functional group is chosen from methyl group, hydroxyl group, carboxylic acid group, and amino group. Preferred Composition: The 11C organic ligand comprises methyl as functional group comprising a reaction product containing 77% hydrogen and 23% carbon monoxide, hydroxyl as functional group comprising reaction product containing 61% hydrogen and 39% carbon monoxide, carboxylic acid as functional group comprising reaction product containing 50% hydrogen and 50% carbon monoxide, and amino group as a functional group comprising reaction product containing 87% hydrogen and 13% carbon monoxide. Preferred Properties: The 2C organic ligand comprises hydroxyl as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 3.1-1, preferably 1.7-1, carboxylic acid as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 3.6-1, preferably 1.2-1, and amine as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 1.2-1, preferably 0.9-1. The 11C organic ligand comprises methyl as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 1.4-1, hydroxy as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 1.5-1, carboxylic acid as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 1.3-1, and amine as functional group comprising reaction product having a ratio (CO/H2) of carbon monoxide to hydrogen of 0.6-1.

PN CN110078046-A; CN110078046-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon material useful for ensuring good adsorption effect on carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. taking urea as nitrogen source, and filter paper as carbon source and dissolving urea in deionized water.
AU SHI J
   CUI H
   XU J
   YAN N
   LIU Y
AE JIANGXI ACAD SCI APPLIED CHEM INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201969525E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a nitrogen-doped porous carbon material comprises taking urea as a nitrogen source and using filter paper as a carbon source, and the specific method is: (i) dissolving urea in deionized water, adding 5 g filter paper, soaking the filter paper in aqueous solution at room temperature, taking out the filter paper and drying; and (ii) placing the obtained filter paper in a tube furnace, heating to 500 degrees C under an inert atmosphere for 1 hour, after which the furnace temperature continues to increase, then the gas flow is switched to carbon dioxide and thermostatically treating for 1 hour, then switching the gas to nitrogen, and naturally cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped porous carbon material is useful for ensuring good adsorption effect on carbon dioxide at 298 K (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous carbon material has large specific surface area and suitable porous structure, has good carbon dioxide adsorption performance; and method: has simple process, ensures to doping of carbon material with nitrogen element, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The added amount of urea is 10-80 g. The added amount of de-ionized water is 100 ml. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is physically activated by a carbon material, where the treatment temperature is 800-950 degrees C, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20-80 ml/minute. The soaking time of filter paper is 6 hours, and the drying temperature of filter paper is 100 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Separation of gas components involves                providing a carbon molecular sieve membrane                prepared by pyrolysis of a polymer precursor, and                contacting a gas stream comprising at least a gas                component (C1) and a gas component (C2) with the                carbon molecular sieve membrane to produce a                retentate stream having a reduced concentration of                the gas component (C1) and a permeate stream having                an increased concentration of the gas component                (C1). The partial pressure of the gas component                (C1) in the gas stream is at least 20 bar.

   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently separates gas                components using carbon molecular sieve membrane                with high permeability that can be suitable for                large scale and high pressure applications, and                prevents formation of liquids on the membrane                surface.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The gas component (C1)                  preferably has partial pressure of at least 50                  bar in the gas stream. The selectivity of the                  sieve membrane during the contacting step is at                  least 80%, preferably at least 90% of the                  selectivity of the sieve membrane when contacted                  with a gas stream in which the partial pressure                  of the gas component (C1) is 10 bar or less under                  the same conditions. Preferred Conditions: The                  gas stream and the retentate stream are at                  supercritical conditions. The contacting step is                  carried out at a temperature of between -50 and                  100 degrees C. Preferred Components: The gas                  component (C1) comprises carbon dioxide and/or                  hydrogen sulfide, or olefin or mixture of                  olefins, and the gas component (C2) comprises                  methane, nitrogen or paraffin or mixture of                  paraffins.

TI Carbon fixation of polluted acid neutralized            residue involves mixing polluted acid neutralized            residue with phosphogypsum, calcining, mixing with            water, hydrolysis reacting, filtering and fixing carbon            dioxide in flue gas.
AU NING P
   DAI Q
   MA L
   SUN X
   ZHANG W
   TIAN S
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021996389
AB    NOVELTY - Method for carbon fixation of polluted acid                neutralized residue involves mixing polluted acid                neutralized residue with phosphogypsum, sending                into calcining furnace, calcining at 700-900                degrees C for 1-3 hours, cooling to room                temperature to obtain calcined product, mixing with                water, hydrolysis reacting, filtering solid-liquid                mixture after reacting for 0.5-1 hour, using                filtrate for fixing carbon dioxide in flue gas,                returning separated impurity as heavy metal fixing                agent for calcining process of polluted acid                neutralized slag and phosphogypsum, introducing                flue gas containing carbon dioxide into filtrate,                fully reacting calcium ion and carbon dioxide in                filtrate, and filtering solid-liquid mixture after                reacting for 0.5-1 hour to obtain solid phase                product high-purity calcium carbonate finished                product.
   USE - The method is used for carbon fixing polluted                acid neutralized residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate, has                low production cost and high carbon fixing effect,                fully uses metallurgical solid waste acid                neutralized and industrial waste phosphogypsum,                reduces content of toxic element in neutralized                residue, and promotes both reduction and                harmlessness.

   USE - (A) are useful as catalyst for converting                carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (A) exhibit large carbon monoxide production                rates and/or low carbon monoxide production onset                temperatures as compared to existing                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Perovskite oxides (A) of formulae                (A1xA2(1-x)B1O3) (I), (A1B1yB2(1-y)O3) (II) or                (A1B1yB2zB3(1-y-z)O3) (III),                (A1xA2(1-x)B1yB2(1-y)O3) (IV), are new.
   x, y, z = 0.2-0.8;
   A1, A2 = La, Sr, Ca, or Ba; and
   B1-B3 = transition metals.
   Provided that, A1 and A2 are not the same, and                B1, B2, and B3 are not the same, and B1 and B2 in                (IV) are not Co.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (a) a catalyst comprising the perovskite                oxides (A), where the perovskite oxide has been                packed into at least one structure for packing a                chemical reactor, and the structure is beads,                pellets, and fluidized bed powders; and
   (b) converting carbon dioxide to carbon                monoxide, comprising contacting the perovskite with                hydrogen gas at a first elevated temperature to                produce an oxygen-deficient perovskite oxide, and                contacting the oxygen-deficient perovskite oxide                with the carbon dioxide at a second elevated                temperature to produce the carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is                  given.Preferred Components: (I)-(III) comprises                  La0.6Ca0.4MnO3, LaCo0.33Fe0.33Mn0.33O3,                  LaCo0.5Fe0.25Mn0.25O3, LaCo0.25Fe0.5Mn0.25O3, and                  LaCo0.25Fe0.25Mn0.5O3. The perovskite oxide is                  supported on a surface of the substrate. The                  substrate comprises a monolith having the                  perovskite oxide deposited on at least a surface                  of the monolith. The monolith comprises platinum,                  a cordierite, a mullite, and silicon carbide. The                  monolith has a Brunauer, Emmet, and Teller (BET)                  specific surface area of 5-100 m2/g. (I)                  comprises La0.6Ca0.4Fe0.4Mn0.6O3,                  La0.6Ca0.4Al0.4Mn0.6O3, La0.6Ba0.4Fe0.6Al0.4O3,                  La0.6Ca0.4Cr0.4Mn0.6O3, La0.6Ca0.4Cr0.6Al0.4O3,                  La0.6Ca0.4Cr0.6Fe0.4O3, La0.6Ba0.4Mn0.6Fe0.4O3,                  La0.6Ba0.4MnO3, La0.6Ba0.4Mn0.6Cr0.4O3,                  La0.6Ba0.4Cr0.8Co0.2O3, La0.6 Ca0.4Cr0.8Co0.2O3,                  and La0.6Ba0.4Cr0.6Fe0.4O3.Preferred Method: The first elevated                  temperature is 350-600degrees Celsius. The second                  elevated temperature is 400-800degrees Celsius,                  preferably 440-550degrees Celsius. The carbon                  monoxide is produced at a rate of 140-275                  (preferably 140-275) micronsoles g-1minutes-1                  based upon the mass of the perovskite                  oxide.
   Preferred Definitions:
   B1-B3 = Al, Fe, Mn, Cr, or Co;
   A1 = La;
   A2 = Sr, Ba, or Ca;
   x = 0.3-0.7; and
   y = 0.4-0.6.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Device for prolonging the refreshing time of food,            has carbon dioxide sensor, which is installed in inner            box body, and controller installed at outer side of            outer box body, where output end of controller            connected with fan and exhaust fan.
AU GAO R
   GAO X
AE TIANJIN R BOX ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021138335
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an inner box body (2), which is                fixed in an outer box body (1). A bottom unit of                the inner box body is supported on the outer box                body through a supporting leg. The inner box body                is provided with a shelf (21). An exhaust fan (12)                is fixed in an exhaust pipe (11). A carbon dioxide                sensor (13) is installed in the inner box body. A                controller (15) is installed outside the outer box                body. An output end of the carbon dioxide sensor is                connected with an input end of the controller. An                output end of the controller is connected with the                fan and the exhaust fan.
   USE - Device for prolonging the refreshing time of                food.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can open the exhaust fan to exhaust                outside, and ensure oxygen supply needed by food                breathing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural schematic view                of the device.
   Outer box body (1)
   Inner box body (2)
   Exhaust pipe (11)
   Exhaust fan (12)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (13)
   Controller (15)
   Shelf (21)

PT P
PN CN112209418-A
TI Preparing colloidal precipitated calcium carbonate by adding quicklime to warm water for calcium hydroxide slurry, adding to jacketed reaction vessel with dispersion paddle, opening dispersion slurry, cooling and filling carbon dioxide.
AU ZHENG Y
   HUANG Y
   WANG Z
   LI Z
   HE C
   YAO M
   CHEN G
AE SHANGHAI CALCIUM CARBONATE FACTORY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202109277B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing colloidal precipitated calcium carbonate involves (1) adding quicklime to warm water and stirring by a stirring paddle to obtain a calcium hydroxide slurry and (2) adding the calcium hydroxide slurry to a jacketed reaction vessel equipped with a dispersion paddle, opening the dispersion slurry, filling the jacket with cooling circulating water, cooling the calcium hydroxide slurry in the vessel to less than 22 degrees C, filling carbon dioxide and determining rate of filling carbon dioxide according to the concentration of the calcium hydroxide slurry, controlling the temperature of the slurry in the vessel until the pH value of the slurry in the vessel is less than or equal to 7 and ending the reaction to obtain an aqueous slurry of colloidal precipitated calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing colloidal precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method lowers the temperature of the calcium hydroxide slurry, controls the temperature of the carbonization reaction, determines the filling rate of carbon dioxide gas according to the concentration of the calcium hydroxide slurry, adopts simple process and is cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) involves adopting warm water with the temperature of 40-60 degrees C. The step (1) involves adopting the stirring paddle with a linear velocity of 2-3 m/second. The step (2) involves adopting the dispersing paddle with the linear velocity of 5-8 m/second, where the filling rate of carbon dioxide in step (2) is 0.0025 l/min per gram of dry calcium hydroxide added. The step (2) involves controlling the temperature of the slurry in the vessel at 20-30 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - (A): has excellent foaming performance; and is environmentally friendly; can be used as zero ODP foaming agent to replace the existing CFCs and HCFCs foaming agents; and produces polyurethane foam material with wide adjustable range of physical and chemical properties and uniform and stable structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of reversible carbon dioxide protection amine compounds (A), which are of formulae (I)-(V) as chemical blowing agent in preparing polyurethane foams, is claimed.
   n, p, q = 1-20;
   m = 1-10;
   R = CH2 or CH2CH2; and
   R1 = CH2CH(CH3) or CH2CH2.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the use method, the stirring speed of the uniform mixing is 1000-5000 revolutions/minute, the mixing time of (A) and polyurethane prepolymer is 5-30 minutes, and the mixing time of the foam stabilizer is 1-10 minutes, the mixing time of adding small molecule diol chain extender is 1-5 minutes, and the mixing time of adding catalyst is 1-5 minutes. Preferred Components: The mass ratio between (A) and polyurethane prepolymer is 2.925-7.822:200. The foam stabilizer is B8 (silicone foam stabilizer) or L64 (polyethylene-polypropylene glycol), and the mass ratio between foam stabilizer and polyurethane prepolymer is 6-10:200. The small molecule diol chain extender is 1,4 butanediol, and the mass ratio between small molecule diol chain extender and polyurethane prepolymer is 4.671-8.41:200. The catalyst is DB40 (polyurethane amine catalysts), triethylamine or dibutyltin dilaurate, and the mass ratio between catalyst and polyurethane prepolymer is 0.2-0.6:200.

PN WO2018051504-A1; KR2019039983-A; CN109688803-A; EP3494776-A1; JP2018539481-X; EP3494776-A4; JP6791973-B2; KR2196642-B1; CN109688803-B; EP3494776-B1
TI Purification apparatus such as carbon-dioxide supply apparatus for purifying waste gas which arose when heating greenhouse for growing plant, has cooling unit which cools waste gas that flows down cooling area.
AU KATO H
   SAITO T
   SAITO Y
   QI T
AE FUTABA IND CO LTD (FUTS-C)
   FUTABA IND CO LTD (FUTS-C)
   FUTABA SANGYO KK (FUTS-C)
   FUTABA IND CO LTD (FUTS-C)
GA 2018232235
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a flow path which flows down the waste gas along a predetermined flow direction. The waste gas flow path (10) has a cooling area which goes below as heading to the flow direction. The flowing-down part makes the waste gas which arosed according to the combustion to flow into the waste gas flow path, and flows down the waste gas flow path for the waste gas along the flow direction. The cooling unit cools the waste gas that flows down the cooling area.
   USE - Purification apparatus such as carbon-dioxide supply apparatus for purifying waste gas which arose when heating greenhouse for growing plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion by the condensed water which arose with cooling of waste gas is suppressed. The carbon dioxide adsorbs to an adsorbent suitably by reducing the temperature of waste gas using a heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon-dioxide supply apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Purification apparatus (1)
   Heating machine (3)
   Discharge flow path (4)
   Waste gas flow path (10)
   Heat exchanger (20)

PN JP6292563-B1; JP2018071795-A
TI Building e.g. detached house or apartment, has fan            which is provided with blowout port so that gas is sent            into living or activity space, where target carbon            dioxide concentration is set to predetermined            value.
AU ISHIBASHI A
AE ISHIBASHI A (ISHI-Individual)
   SHIMIZU K (SHIM-Individual)
   CSTEC CORP (CSTE-Non-standard)
   HIEI KENSETSU KK (HIEI-Non-standard)
   ISHIBASHI ARCHITECTS & ASSOC (ISHI-Non-standard)
   KINDAI SETSUBI SEKKEI JIMUSHO YG (KIND-Non-standard)
   ISHIBASHI A (ISHI-Individual)
   SHIMIZU K (SHIM-Individual)
   SEEDSTEC KK (SEED-Non-standard)
   HIEI KENSETSU KK (HIEI-Non-standard)
   ISHIBASHI ARCHITECTS & ASSOC (ISHI-Non-standard)
   KINDAI SETSUBI SEKKEI JIMUSHO YG (KIND-Non-standard)
GA 2018211841
AB    NOVELTY - The building has a fan which is provided with                a blowout port so that gas is sent into the living                or activity space. All of the gas flows out from                the blowout opening into the inside of the living                and activity space. The carbon dioxide is not                generated in the living or activity space when the                carbon dioxide concentration is in equilibrium with                the outside. The target carbon dioxide                concentration inside the above living or activity                space is set to predetermined value.
   USE - Building e.g. detached house, apartment,                hospital, movie theater, nursing institution,                school, nursery school, kindergarten, gymnasium or                factory. Uses included but are not limited to                coating room or lacquered chamber used in motor                vehicle such as ambulance, aeroplane, passenger                train, passenger bus, yacht cabin, and passenger                boat.
   ADVANTAGE - The relief and safety of the people is                ensured. The clogging arising is prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                building.
   Air supplying opening (10)
   Exhaust port (20)
   Chamber (100)
   Roof (103)
   Ceiling wall (104)

PT P
PN US2021363087-A1
TI Chemical process for reacting carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrocarbon, comprises natural gas, methane, ethane, propane, butane or pentane in presence of transition metal oxide catalyst e.g. manganese oxide, iron oxide or cobalt oxide, forming solvents e.g. acetone, pentanone, heptanol and nonane.
AU CARTER M K
AE CARTER M K (CART-Individual)
GA 2021D4992C
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical process for reacting carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrocarbon, comprises natural gas, methane, ethane, propane, butane or pentane in presence of transition metal oxide catalyst comprising manganese oxide, iron oxide or cobalt oxide, forming solvents comprising acetone, butanol, pentane, pentanone, heptanol and nonane.
   USE - The method is useful for chemical process for reacting carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: converts oxidized carbon compounds to organic compounds; and reduces or eliminates carbon footprint of industrial processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The chemical process comprises natural gas, methane, ethane, propane, butane or pentane in presence of transition metal oxide catalyst at 200-460degrees Celsius, preferably 200-500degrees Celsius under pressures of 1-7 atmospheres forming solvents comprising acetone, butanol, pentane, pentanone, heptanol and nonane.
   EXAMPLE - The reactor zone was heated to 280degrees Celsius as carbon dioxide and natural gas were supplied at 1.4 atmospheres (20 pounds per square inch) pressure with a total flow rate of 214 ml/minute. Carbon dioxide was set to 70 ml/minute while natural gas was set to 144 ml/minute. Gases were allowed to flow for 30 minutes. A colorless liquid, weight was 6.64 g was collected in the cold trap immersed in ice water bath. The liquid was dried over anhydrous sodium sulfate then filtered to collect 4.8 g liquid product. A Fourier transform Infrared spectroscopy spectrum of the liquid produced useful spectral information. Relatively intense absorption bands were observed at 2966, 2937 and 2875 wave numbers with weaker bands at 1467, 1379, 1276, 905, 886, 798 and 754 wave numbers. In addition, a relatively intense band was observed at 1715 wave numbers identified with ketones with a weak band at 1199 wave numbers indicating the presence of alcohol. This was identified as acetone.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E11-F02B; E11-F03; E35-V; E35-U02; E35-S; E10-F02B; E10-E04E3; E10-E04E4; E10-J02D3; J04-E01; J04-E04; N07-D02B; N07-D03; N03-E; N02-A01; N02-B01

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon sandwich copper nano-sheet electro-catalyst with sandwich structure comprises middle layer copper nano sheet with average thickness of 3-4 nm, and graphene-like layers on two sides of copper nano sheet with average thickness of 0.8-4.3 nm.
   USE - The carbon interlayer copper nanosheet electrocatalyst with sandwich structure is used for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide into methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-interlayer copper nanosheet electrocatalyst can prevent oxidation of copper nanosheet with high reaction activity in the atmosphere, and exhibits excellent catalytic performance in the process of selective electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide as methane.

AU POPONCZYK A S
AE POPONCZYK A S (POPO-Individual)
GA 202087518B
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions per obtained unit of electric power of coal-fired power units involves adding to fuel and changing the method of its administration. The dosing device intended for the power unit of a coal-fired power plant enables the enrichment of the fuel supplied to the furnace with an appropriate amount of ammonia, and used waste heat from the steam coming from the turbogenerator units for the regasification of liquid ammonia.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide emissions per obtained unit of electric power of coal-fired power units.

PN PL423904-A1
TI Preparing activated carbon for carbon dioxide adsorption comprises mixing dried activated carbon source with aqueous activator solution or wetted activated carbon source with solid activator using potassium hydroxide and carbonizing.
AU MICHALKIEWICZ B
   LEWICKA K
AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
GA 201954094C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of activated carbon for carbon dioxide adsorption comprises mixing in a weight ratio of 1: 0.1-5 of a dried activated carbon source with an aqueous activator solution or a wetted activated carbon source with a solid activator, using potassium hydroxide as the activator or sodium hydroxide and/or sodium carbonate and/or potassium hydroxide, allowing the mixture to stand for 0-24 hours, then drying the resulting mixture, carbonizing it under a chemically inert gas, washing and drying. The active carbon source is lemon balm leaves (Melissa officinalis).
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of activated carbon for carbon dioxide adsorption.

PN CN108570088-A
TI Extraction of cucurbitacin E involves cutting            muskmelon base, crushing, sieving, introducing            resultant into raw material mixing tank, adding            pressurized carbon dioxide, mixing, rapidly spraying            resultant mixed fluid, and separating.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 201878639G
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of cucurbitacin E involves cutting                muskmelon base into a small section of 1-2 cm,                adding to a crusher, crushing, sieving using a                80-mesh sieve, introducing resultant into a raw                material mixing tank, gradually pressurizing carbon                dioxide to 150-250 degrees C from an inverted steel                cylinder through a pneumatic pump, adding to the                raw material mixing tank, mixing at 50-60 degrees                C, rapidly spraying resultant mixed fluid from                nozzle, and separating finished product from carbon                dioxide in a separation tank.
   USE - Extraction of cucurbitacin E.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of cucurbitacin                E by multi-effect wall breaking method.

PT P
PN KR2022077984-A
TI Montmorillonite catalyst in which cation contained            in the montmorillonite is extracted and substituted            with a transition metal cation useful for carbon            dioxide capture.
AU NAMSUNGCHAN
   BAEK I H
   JANG J T
   UMAIR H B
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202280865V
AB    NOVELTY - Montmorillonite catalyst in which cation                contained in the montmorillonite is extracted and                substituted with a transition metal cation.
   USE - The montmorillonite catalyst is useful for                carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The montmorillonite catalyst: has high carbon                dioxide desorption rate; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing a montmorillonite catalyst comprising (1)                adding montmorillonite to the hydrated inorganic                compound, (2) stirring the added montmorillonite                with the hydrated inorganic compound, (3) filtering                the clay containing montmorillonite after the                stirring step, (4) mixing the filtered clay                containing montmorillonite with a separate hydrated                inorganic compound, (5) filtering the clay                containing montmorillonite, (6) washing the                filtered filtrate with deionized water, and (7)                drying the washed clay.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal                  is chromium, iron, or cobalt. The sum of the                  ratio of silicon and aluminum elements is 0-30%.                  The montmorillonite catalyst has a surface area                  of 30-70 m2/g. The montmorillonite catalyst has a                  pore volume of 0.1-0.2 cm3/g. The montmorillonite                  catalyst has an average pore diameter of 10-15.5                  nm. The montmorillonite catalyst has a surface                  acidity of greater than or equal to 2 mmol/g and                  total acid site of 0.2 mmol/g. The hydrated                  inorganic compound of the step (1) contains a                  transition metal. Preferred Method: In the step                  (2), stirring at 50-100 degrees C. In the step                  (4), mixing for 2-6 hours. In the step (6),                  washing is performed until the pH of the filtrate                  is 6-7. In the step (7), drying at 100-150                  degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - Montmorillonite 5 g was slowly added to an                aqueous solution of ferric chloride hexahydrate (80                ml, 0.5 mol/l) at 80 degrees C with vigorous                stirring. After 12 hours, the ion exchange process                was stopped and the clay was filtered. The                collected clay was mixed with a fresh metal                solution (80 ml, 0.5 mol/l) and the ion exchange                process was repeated for 4 hours. Then collected                clay was filtered and washed repeatedly with                deionized water until the filtrate became neutral                (pH = 6-7). The collected clay was dried in an oven                at 120 degrees C overnight and then pulverized                using an agate mortar.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113354828-A
TI New stable metal organic framework material used for high carbon dioxide selective capture ability at room temperature after solvent exchange with anhydrous methanol and removal of solvent molecules in vacuum.
AU CHEN C
   LI X
AE UNIV SHENYANG (UNHE-C)
GA 2021A7745A
AB    NOVELTY - Stable metal organic framework material (I)-(II) is new.
   USE - The stable metal organic framework material is used for high carbon dioxide selective capture ability at room temperature after solvent exchange with anhydrous methanol and removal of solvent molecules in vacuum (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic framework material is three-dimensional structure with large porosity and has good stability and carbon dioxide selective capture performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Stable metal organic framework material of formula (C148H110Fe15O82) (I) and Fe15O5(C22H10O8)6(CH3COO)8(H2O)13 (II) is new. The C22H10O8 is organic ligand, (1,1':4',1''-terphenyl)-3,3'',5,5''-tetracarboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The synthesis of organic ligand (1,1': 4',1''-terphenyl)-3,3'',5,5''-tetracarboxylic acid comprises mixing p-dibromobenzene, 3,5-bis (methoxycarbonyl) phenylboric acid, potassium phosphate and tetratriphenylphosphine palladium in deoxidized 1,4-dioxane solvent in nitrogen atmosphere at 80 degrees C for 48 hours, cooling to room temperature, spin drying solvent, extracting with chloroform, drying organic layer with magnesium sulfate, spin drying, purifying with silica chromatographic column to obtain white product, hydrolyzing product with alkaline aqueous solution and acidifying acid to precipitate. Preferred Components: Every 2.36 g p-dibromobenzene corresponds to 2.38 g 3,5-bis (methoxycarbonyl) phenylboric acid, 21 g potassium phosphate, 0.5 g tetratriphenylphosphine palladium and 300 ml 1,4-dioxane. The mass ratio of ferric chloride to organic ligand is 3:1. Each milligram of ligand corresponds to 2 ml of N,N-dimethylformamide solution and 0.09 ml acetic acid.

PN CN111592024-A; CN111592024-B
TI Preparation of submicron solid spherical calcium            carbonate involves preparing glycine-calcium complex            solution by performing electrochemical complex reaction            of calcium chloride solution, carbonizing with carbon            dioxide, and separating.
AU HUANG K
   HUANG S
   HUANG Z
   GU C
   YANG H
   SHI D
AE GUANGXI ACAD SCI (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI ACAD SCI (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202085781N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of submicron solid spherical                calcium carbonate involves preparing                glycine-calcium complex solution by regulating                direct current voltage of electrochemical complex                reaction, pH value, and complexing temperature                reaction conditions of electrochemical complex                reaction of calcium chloride solution as raw                material under the auxiliary action of glycine and                accelerating agent, adjusting carbonization carbon                dioxide flow rate, carbonization conductivity, and                carbonization temperature reaction conditions,                carbonizing glycine-calcium complex solution with                carbon dioxide, separating, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of submicron solid spherical                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and                environmentally-friendly preparation of submicron                solid spherical calcium carbonate using clean                energy without carrying out high-temperature                calcination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The calcium chloride                  solution is obtained by treating residue of                  calcium-based materials by hydrometallurgical                  process. Preferred Composition: The calcium-based                  material residue is high-quality marble residue,                  high-quality limestone residue and/or leftover                  seafood shell material. The electrolyte of the                  cathode chamber is 10-15% potassium hydroxide or                  sodium hydroxide solution. The electrolyte of                  anode chamber is calcium chloride solution with                  concentration of 2.2-6 mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The accelerating                  agent is potassium glycinate and/or sodium                  glycinate. The electrochemical complex reaction                  is carried out using device comprising an                  anion-cation exchange membrane, a cathode                  chamber, an anode chamber, and a complex chamber,                  in which the anion exchange membrane separates                  the cathode chamber and the complex chamber, the                  cation exchange membrane separates the anode                  chamber and the complex chamber, and the complex                  chamber is located between the cathode chamber                  and the anode chamber. The molar ratio of glycine                  and accelerating agent is 1:0.1-0.5. Preferred                  Conditions: The control conditions of                  electrochemical complex reaction include direct                  current voltage of 12-36 V, pH value of 12-13 and                  the complexing temperature of 25-40 degrees C.                  The control conditions of carbonization reaction                  include carbon dioxide flow rate of 500-1500                  ml/minute, carbonization conductivity of 200-400                  mu S/cm and carbonization temperature of 20 plus                  minus 5 degrees C.

PN CN111454455-A; CN111454455-B
TI New type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups used in the catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG S
   CHEN G
   ZHANG Y
   XU Q
   LIU K
   HUANG R
   ZHANG K
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 202072716M
AB    NOVELTY - Type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups (I), are new.
   USE - Type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups used in the catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups can achieve high-efficiency catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide under mild conditions to prepare high value-added cyclic carbonates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups of formula (I and II), are new. and. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups of formula (I and II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The type of porous hybrid polymer rich in polyoctahedral silsesquioxanes-derived silanol groups of formula (I and II) is prepared by combining the vinyl polyhedral silsesquioxane of formula (III), tri (4-bromobenzene) amine of formula (IV) and tetrakis(4-bromobenzene)methane of formula (V) in an organic solvent, and the porous hybrid polymer rich in polyhedral silsesquioxane-derived silyl hydroxyl groups is prepared by heck reaction. Preferred Process: The method specifically involves weighing vinyl polyhedral silsesquioxane (III), tri (4-bromobenzene) amine (IV) or tetrakis(4-bromobenzene)methane (V) in an organic solvent and then dissolved in an organic solvent to obtain a homogeneous solution, transferring the uniform solution to the reaction kettle, adding alkaline acid absorbent and catalyst, heating for a period of time to react. After the reaction, filtering, washing and drying to obtain the porous hybrid polymer rich in polyhedral silsesquioxane-derived silanols. The application specifically comprises using an epoxy compound as a substrate, using the porous hybrid polymer rich in polyhedral silsesquioxane-derived silanol groups as a catalyst, performing cycloaddition reaction under heating in a normal pressure carbon dioxide atmopshere. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of vinyl polyhedral silsesquioxane, tri (4-bromobenzene) amine or tetrakis(4-bromobenzene)methane is 1:1-3. The reaction temperature is 120 degrees C. The reaction time is 72h. Preferrred Components: The alkaline acid-absorbing agent is potassium carbonate. The catalyst is triphenylphosphine palladium. The epoxy compound is represented by formula (VI).R=chloromethyl, bromomethyl, phenyl, n-butyl, n-hexyl or benzyloxy.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   ZHOU Y
   LU X
   YE R
   LIU W
   PENG M
   SHE L
   LI P
   LUAN Z
   CHEN B
AE NORTHWEST INST PLATEAU BIOLOGY CHINESE A (CAPB-C)
GA 2019233458
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Iris lactea volatile oil comprises (a) taking Iris lactea material, crushing and sieving to obtain crude powder, and (b) adding step (a) crude powder into extracting kettle, and introducing supercritical carbon dioxide gas, where extraction pressure is 3000-4500 Psi, the extraction temperature is 30-45 degrees C, the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 9-18 ml/minute, and extraction time is 2 hours, and collecting the volatile oil to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Iris lactea volatile oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has better yield than other extracting technique.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Iris lactea volatile oil, extracted by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the raw material is Iris lactea seed. The chromatogram contains 31 main characteristic peaks when the volatile oil is detected by the gas chromatography-mass method as given in the specification. Preferred Method: In the step (a), the sieving refers to passing through a 80 mesh sieve. In the step (b), the extraction conditions are the extraction pressure is 3500-4500 Psi, preferably 3500 Psi, the extraction temperature is 40-45 degrees C, preferably 45 degrees C, the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is 12-15 ml/minute, preferably 15 ml/minute, and extraction time is 2 hours. The volatile oil when subjected to GC/MS is detection, the chromatographic conditions were, the inlet temperature is 230 degrees C, and the column was HP-INNOwax (30 mx 0.25 mm, 0.25 mu m), the temperature program was kept at 50 degrees C for 5 minutes, 5 degrees C/minute was raised to 230 degrees C and kept for 5 minutes, the carrier gas was high purity helium, and the flow rate is 1 ml/minute, the mass spectrometry conditions are, electron bombardment (EI) ion source, ion source temperature 200 degrees C, electron energy 70 ev, acquisition mode is full scan mode, and scan quality range is 35-500 amu.

PT P
PN CN109355928-A; WO2020077665-A1; US2021324577-A1; CN109355928-B
TI Processing anhydrous fiber dyeing method of mixing            fluid medium comprises e.g. mixing supercritical carbon            dioxide fluid medium with certain proportion of steam            under certain conditions to form mixed fluid.
AU LONG J
   CAI C
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201919164U
AB    NOVELTY - Processing anhydrous fiber dyeing method of                mixing fluid medium comprises mixing supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid medium with certain proportion                of steam under certain conditions to form mixed                fluid, and using as processing medium for waterless                dyeing.
   USE - The method is useful for processing anhydrous                fiber dyeing method of mixing fluid medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the dyeability and                staining behavior of hydrophilic fibers in                supercritical carbon dioxide fluids; has simple                process; is convenient operation; has low energy                consumption; avoids the consumption of large amount                of water resources and pollutants in the                traditional process; is economical and                environmentally friendly; and has high                efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises (i) mechanically compacting the fiber                  in a dry state, and coating certain degree of                  tightness to the cotton or the filling in layered                  form in a special porous cage, (ii) placing the                  porous yarn cage that has been stepped on cotton                  or filled in the above step (i) in high-pressure                  dyeing tank, sealing the dyeing tank, and                  introducing suitable non-carbon dioxide medium to                  pre-treat the dry fiber layer under pressure and                  time, (iii) after the pretreatment in the step                  (ii), introducing the supercritical carbon                  dioxide medium and the special dye for                  dissolution into the high pressure dyeing tank,                  and carrying out supercharging, heating and heat                  preservation dyeing according to predetermined                  dyeing process, and (iv) after finishing the                  dyeing, simultaneously subjecting the dyeing                  system to online floating color cleaning and                  cooling treatment under certain conditions by                  using clean supercritical carbon dioxide medium,                  recovering the fluid medium in the dyeing system                  to normal pressure, and opening the cylinder to                  complete the waterless dyeing process in the                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid medium. In the                  step (i), the mechanical pressing method is                  uniform and uniform layer-by-layer extrusion                  processing of the fluffy fibers by mechanical                  external force, so that the flat packing can be                  performed according to certain tightness, the                  layered form refers to a dry fiber filled with                  certain thickness as layer when mechanically                  treading or filling, carrying out the next layer                  of squeezing, repeatedly layering the layers, and                  completing the predetermined amount of treading                  cotton in the special cotton. In the step (iii),                  in the predetermined dyeing process, the                  temperature is 50-160 degrees C, the pressure is                  7-35 Mpa, the dynamic and static cycle time ratio                  of the fluid is 1:5-10:1, and the processing for                  10-180 minutes. In the step (iv), the online                  floating color cleaning process has temperature                  of 30-100 degrees C, pressure of 8-35 MPa,                  dynamic cycle time ratio of the fluid of                  1:5-10:1, and processing for 10-120 minutes. In                  the step (iv), after completing the dyeing,                  recovering the carbon dioxide through the                  circulation system for the next recycling, and                  recovering the carbon dioxide gas in the dyeing                  system to atmospheric pressure to realize direct                  opening of the dyeing cylinder. Preferred                  Components: The fibers in the dry state are short                  fibers cotton in natural fibers, or processed                  hemp fibers, or synthetic fibers e.g. viscose,                  polyester, nylon, and acrylic fibers. The special                  porous yarn cage in the step (i) is made of an                  outer covering Teflon or other non-conductive hot                  surface material, and a series of small holes are                  arranged around the saddle and the central hollow                  tube. In the step (i), the dry fiber is treaded                  in cotton in the porous saddle in layered form,                  and the tightness is 50-300 kg/m3. The suitable                  non-carbon dioxide medium in the step (ii) is                  saturated steam, superheated steam and/or other                  polar solvents. The pretreatment of a certain                  pressure and time in the step (ii) has pressure                  of 0-1 MPa and a time of 5-180 minutes. In the                  step (iii), the specific dye for dissolution is a                  reactive dispersing dye, and the active group is                  vinyl sulfone, vinyl group, s-triazine type,                  nicotinic acid structure and/or derivative                  compounds, the solvent for dissolving the                  specific dye is supercritical carbon dioxide,                  ethanol, acetone, methanol and/or deionized                  water, the ratio of the solvent used to the two                  mixed solvents is 1:5-5:1.

PN US10065174-B1
TI Pelletized sorbent used for separating carbon dioxide from gaseous mixture, comprises first component comprising basic immobilized amine sorbent, second component comprising fly ash and third component comprising polymer binder.
AU WIFONG W C
   GRAY M L
   SOONG Y
   KAIL B W
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
GA 201868721G
AB    NOVELTY - Pelletized sorbent comprises: first component comprising a basic immobilized amine sorbent; second component comprising fly ash; and a third component comprising a polymer binder, where the basic immobilized amine sorbent and fly ash are interconnected by the polymer binder.
   USE - The sorbent is useful for separation of carbon dioxide from a gaseous mixture (claimed) such as a post combustion gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The pelletized sorbent exhibits a carbon dioxide sorbent capacity greater than 1.7 mmol/g, a crush strength equal to or greater than 1.0 MPa or attrition value less than 1% after 24 hours and a (PCR) value greater than 50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The basic immobilized amine sorbent is present at 63-92% of the total dry pelletized sorbent weight. The polymer binder is PVC, poly(chloroprene) (both preferred), poly(vinyl alcohol), poly(vinyl acetate) or polystyrene. The pelletized sorbent is hydrophobic. The sorbent further comprises: a fourth component comprising a crosslinker having at least two functional groups, where the at least two functional groups are bound to the polymer binder. The functional groups are aldehyde, amine, carboxylic acid, epoxy, aminosilane, alkoxysilane, cyanate, isocyanate, acrylate and/or cyanoacrylate. The crosslinker is a polyamine. The pelletized sorbent further comprises crush strength equal to or greater than 0.3 MPa preferably 1.3 MPa and carbon dioxide sorbent capacity equal to or greater than 1 mmol/g preferably greater than or equal to 2 mmol/g, percentage of carbon dioxide capture retained (PCR) value of greater than or equal to 20% preferably greater than or equal to 50%. The first component comprises basic immobilized amine sorbent that is Class 1, Class 2, or hybrid Class 1/Class 2 BIAS. The second component comprises inorganic strength additive. The inorganic strength additive is present at 5-37% of the total dry pelletized sorbent weight.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Metal-organic frameworks-derived carbon-based            photonic crystal-porphyrin-containing covalent organic            frameworks composite material useful for adsorbing            carbon dioxide, comprises e.g.            tetra(p-aminophenyl)porphyrin and            terephthalaldehyde.
AU YU D
   CHEN X
   YANG M
   JIAN Z
   ZHENG M
   HU X
   JIAN J
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201869229R
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic frameworks (MOF)-derived                carbon-based photonic crystal-porphyrin-containing                covalent organic frameworks (COFs) composite                material comprises MOF-derived carbon-based                photonic crystal, tetra(p-aminophenyl)porphyrin and                terephthalaldehyde, where the raw materials are                reacted together.
   USE - The material is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high adsorption capacity for                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the material, comprising dispersing                MOF-derived carbon-based photonic crystal,                tetra(p-aminophenyl)porphyrin and                terephthalaldehyde and organic acid catalyst in a                first solvent, ultrasonicating the obtained                reaction mixture, deoxygenating, deoxidizing the                reaction mixture and heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of heating                  reaction is 110-130 degrees C, and the heating                  reaction is carried out for 40-56 hours. The                  method for preparing MOF-derived carbon-based                  photonic crystal comprises dissolving zinc                  nitrate and 2-methylimidazole in a second solvent                  to obtain ZIF-8 precursor solution, adding the                  ZIF-8 precursor solution to a template of the                  silica photonic crystal, allowing to stand at                  room temperature for a certain period of time,                  passing the ZIF-8 precursor solution into the                  silica photonic crystal, evaporating the second                  solvent under heating to obtain ZIF-8 wrapped                  with silica photonic crystal, subjecting                  MOF-derived carbon-based photonic crystal to                  carbonization and annealing silica photonic                  crystal-encapsulated ZIF-8 and removing the                  silica photonic crystal. The method comprises                  placing period is 8-16 hours, conditions for                  evaporating the solvent include temperature of                  85-95 degrees C, time of 20-28 hours,                  carbonization-annealing temperature is 800-1000                  degrees C, the reagent used to remove the silica                  photonic crystal includes hydrofluoric acid.                  Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  MOF-derived carbon-based photonic crystal,                  tetra(p-aminophenyl)porphyrin and                  terephthalaldehyde is 1:0.7-1:0.2-0.3. The mass                  ratio of zinc nitrate hexahydrate to                  2-methylimidazole is 1:1.8-2.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid                  catalyst is acetic acid. The first solvent is a                  mixed solvent of o-dichlorobenzene and butanol.                  The second solvent is a mixed solvent of methanol                  and N,N-dimethylformamide.

PN JP2018100242-A
TI Skin pack material useful for applying carbon dioxide gas to skin, comprises internal sheet to be adhered to skin and external sheet arranged outside internal sheet.
AU KANAZAWA H
AE BOULTON PARTY KK (BOUL-Non-standard)
GA 2018524829
AB    NOVELTY - Skin pack material comprises an internal sheet to be adhered to skin and an external sheet arranged outside the internal sheet, the internal sheet is in a wet state by a liquid containing water, the external sheet is in a dry state, the internal sheet and external sheet are wrapped in a non-contact state, the internal sheet is arranged against skin and the external sheet is arranged so as to be in contact with arranged internal sheet to generate carbon dioxide gas by contact between both sheets.
   USE - The skin pack material is useful for applying carbon dioxide gas to skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The skin pack material is convenient to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The internal sheet comprises a carbonate. The external sheet comprises an acidic substance that generates carbon dioxide by reaction with the carbonate. The carbonate and acidic substance reacts with each other by contact between the both sheets to generate carbon dioxide gas. The skin pack material further comprises a steaming sheet for suppressing permeation of the carbon dioxide gas. The steaming sheet is formed separately from internal sheet and external sheet, and has a three-dimensional shape conforming to shape of face in a deployed state, and hooks engaged with ears on both left and right sides.
