PN JP2018039696-A; WO2018047749-A1; CN109661370-A; EP3511296-A1; EP3511296-A4; JP6742867-B2; US2021276878-A1
TI Manufacture of inorganic carbonate e.g. calcium carbonate or magnesium carbonate, involves depositing inorganic carbonate having preset average primary particle, in presence of ultra-fine bubbles containing carbon dioxide gas.
AU OISHI M
   NAKATANI T
   FUKUOKA M
   GOTO S
AE NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
   NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
   NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
   JUJO PAPER CO LTD (SEJJ-C)
   NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
GA 201821220C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of inorganic carbonate involves preparing ultra-fine bubbles containing carbon dioxide gas by injecting gas and liquid containing carbon dioxide gas into a reaction container from a nozzle, and depositing inorganic carbonate having an average primary particle of 300 nm or less in presence of the ultra-fine bubbles.
   USE - Manufacture of inorganic carbonate e.g. calcium carbonate or magnesium carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and quick manufacture of inorganic carbonate with reduced electric power consumption. The inorganic carbonate is in the form of fine inorganic particles having excellent shape uniformity and miscibility with resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The average particle diameter of ultra-fine bubbles is 1-800 nm. The average time after generating ultra-fine bubbles until ultra-fine bubbles disappear is 10 seconds or more. Preferred Process: The manufacture of inorganic carbonate involves depositing inorganic carbonate on fibers in presence of fibers.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Composition: The fibers are cellulose fibers.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN EP3928926-A1; DE102020003866-A1
TI Device for cooling components using carbon dioxide-particle jet, has supply channel for supplying liquid carbon dioxide, expansion section for generating carbon-dioxide-particles, and nozzle connected to expansion section.
AU AMMANN T
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2021F0635E
AB    NOVELTY - Cooling device comprises a supply channel for supplying liquid carbon dioxide, an expansion section for generating carbon dioxide particles from the liquid carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide expands in the expansion section and thus carbon dioxide particles and gaseous carbon dioxide is converted, and a nozzle for dispensing the carbon dioxide-particles in one direction of flow. A suction device is provided, where a volume flow of the device is designed to remove a large part of the gas phase in the section against the direction of the flow of carbon dioxide-particles to suck off.
   USE - Device for cooling components using carbon dioxide-particle jet for use in gas-shielded welding or in generative manufacturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The additive manufacturing with arc and plasma welding technology are that no complex tools and systems are required, which means that the investment costs are low, but the results are absolutely convincing. The protective gases are used to protect the electrode, weld pool and heat-affected zone from reactions with the ambient air and prevent the impairment caused by the chemical reaction. By using protective gases, the metallurgical properties of the components are retained or, in some cases, even improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) device for welding or generative manufacturing; and
   (2) process for cooling a component surface using carbon dioxide-particles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the front view of the device for cooling components.

PT P
PN US2018252684-A1; WO2018158730-A1; US10371673-B2
TI Carbon dioxide-based chromatography system, has second liquid modifier pump which is disposed to deliver liquid modifier according to reverse gradient compared to gradient entering column through first liquid modifier pump.
AU FOGWILL M O
   JARRELL J A
   MICHIENZI J D
   MCDONALD T S
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 2018693434
AB    NOVELTY - The system (500) has a first carbon dioxide pump (503) which is disposed to deliver carbon dioxide to a chromatography column. A first liquid modifier pump is disposed to deliver a liquid modifier to the chromatography column. A second carbon dioxide pump (529) is located downstream of the column and disposed to operate independently of the first carbon dioxide pump to introduce carbon dioxide into a flow stream at an output of the column, and operates in constant pressure mode in order to maintain a target pressure. A second liquid modifier pump is located downstream of the column and disposed to operate independently of the first liquid modifier pump to introduce liquid modifier into the flow stream at the output of the column, and is disposed to deliver liquid modifier according to a reverse gradient compared to a gradient entering the column via the first liquid modifier pump.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based chromatography system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides increased stability and controls of temperature and pressure levels, and provides increased accuracy and uniformity of response at the detector by the consistency in co-solvent flow rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method of maintaining pressure in a carbon dioxide-based chromatography system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the carbon dioxide-based chromatography system with two carbon dioxide pumps.
   Carbon dioxide-based chromatography system (500)
   First modifier pump (501)
   First carbon dioxide pump (503)
   Detector (523)
   Second carbon dioxide pump (529)

PT P
PN CN108236829-A; CN108236829-B
TI Method for separating high-purity carbon dioxide from raw material gas i.e. coal synthesis gas, involves recycling carbon dioxide, and introducing raw material gas or carbon dioxide lean gas into adsorption tower for lifting tower pressure.
AU ZHANG J
   LIU M
AE DEMMEL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DEMM-Non-standard)
GA 201855396T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves entering a raw material gas to a first adsorption tower through a tower bottom. Carbon dioxide is absorbed by using an adsorbent in the first adsorption tower. The raw material gas is converted into carbon dioxide lean gas. The carbon dioxide lean gas in the first adsorption tower is discharged to a second adsorption tower. Pressure of the first and second adsorption towers are balanced. The carbon dioxide is recycled by using a vacuum pump connected with a bottom end of the first adsorption tower. The raw material gas or carbon dioxide lean gas is introduced into the first adsorption tower for lifting tower pressure.
   USE - Method for separating high-purity carbon dioxide from a raw material gas i.e. coal synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficiently separating and purifying the carbon dioxide with high purity and high recovery rate, reducing energy consumption rate, pressure drop of a device, vacuum requirements, height of the adsorption towers and separating and recycling cycle period and saving cost for purifying carbon dioxide for industrial production so as to ensure better economic value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for separating high-purity carbon dioxide from a raw material gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a device for separating high-purity carbon dioxide from a raw material gas. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

TI High-impact carbon dioxide random copolymer useful            in degradable products, comprises carbon dioxide,            catalyst and epoxy monomer composed of epoxy            cyclohexane and other epoxy monomers other than the            epoxy cyclohexane.
AU TANG J
   CUI Y
   WANG J
   HU H
AE SHANGHAI HUAFON NEW MATERIALS RES & DEV (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022706861
AB    NOVELTY - High-impact carbon dioxide random copolymer                comprises carbon dioxide, epoxy monomers and                catalyst. The epoxy monomer includes combination of                epoxy cyclohexane and other epoxy monomers other                than the epoxy cyclohexane.
   USE - The high impact carbon dioxide random                copolymer is useful in degradable products                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high impact carbon dioxide random                copolymer has excellent biological degradation                performance, relatively high tensile strength and                high impact strength, and excellent mechanical                property and transparency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                high-impact carbon dioxide random copolymer,                comprising carrying out random copolymerization of                carbon dioxide, epoxy monomer and catalyst in a                solvent to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The number-average                  molecular weight of the high-impact carbon                  dioxide random copolymer is 40-800 kDa,                  preferably 50-600 kDa. The moisture of the epoxy                  cyclohexane is lower than 200 ppm, preferably                  lower than 100 ppm, the other epoxy monomers                  include ethylene oxide, 1,2-propylene oxide,                  1,2-butylene oxide, 1,2-pentene oxide, and                  1,2-hexane oxide and/or styrene oxide, preferred                  1,2 propylene oxide, 1,2-ethylene oxide,                  1,2-butylene oxide and/or styrene oxide, the mass                  percentage of the other epoxy monomers is 4-92                  wt.%, preferably 6-88 wt.%, and the moisture                  content of the other epoxy monomers is less than                  200 ppm, preferably less than 100 ppm. The                  catalyst comprises ternary rare earth catalyst,                  organic zinc carboxylate catalyst, double metal                  cyanide catalyst and/or modified double metal                  cyanide catalyst, preferably, the organic                  carboxylate zinc catalyst comprises zinc adipate                  catalyst, and the mass percentage of the catalyst                  is 0.005-1.8 wt.%, more preferably 0.010-1.2 wt.%                  (based on the total mass of the epoxy monomers of                  100%). The high-impact carbon dioxide random                  copolymer is compound of formula (I). The solvent                  includes tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane,                  chloroform, dioxolane, dioxane, ethyl acetate,                  dimethyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, acetone                  and/or butanone, preferably dichloromethane,                  tetrahydrofuran and/or dioxolane, the volume                  ratio between solvent and epoxy monomer is                  0.3-3:1, preferably 0.7-2.5:1, the moisture                  content of the solvent is less than 80 ppm,                  preferably less than 50 ppm.Preferred Method: The dew point temperature                  of the carbon dioxide is lower than                  -40&#8451;, preferably lower than                  -50&#8451;, the mass percentage content of                  the carbon dioxide is 30-500 wt.%, preferably                  40-450 wt.%.(based on the total mass of the epoxy                  monomers of 100%). The random copolymerization is                  carried out in a reactor, the temperature of the                  random copolymerization is 40-120&#8451;,                  preferably 50-110&#8451;, and the random                  copolymerization time is 4-30 hours, preferably                  6-24 hours.R = ethane-1,2-diyl, propane-1,2-diyl,                  butane-1,2-diyl, pentane-1,2-diyl, or                  (propyl)benzene-2,3-diyl;x:y = 9-99:1, preferably 10-98:1;m:n = 3-99:1, preferably 4-98:1;(x+y):(m+n) = 0.1-10:1, preferably 0.12-9:1;                  and* = attachment site.

PT P
PN RU2018143195-A
TI Production of carbon dioxide for soda production by ammonia method, involves burning calcium-containing raw material containing calcium carbonate, and concentrating cement waste gases containing carbon dioxide by absorption-desorption.
AU ZAGIDULLIN R N
   VORONIN A V
   ZAGIDULLIN R I
   AMINOVA E K
   MUKHAMETOV A A
AE UNIV BASHKIR STATE (UYBA-Non-standard)
GA 2020652453
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon dioxide, involves burning a calcium-containing raw material containing calcium carbonate, concentrating cement waste gases containing 16-22 vol.% carbon dioxide by absorption-desorption with an aqueous solution of amino alcohol and/or diamine at 0.1-1.6 MPa and 50-132 degrees C, and obtaining carbon dioxide with a content of at least 50 vol.%, which is mixed with a gas containing 35-38 vol.% carbon dioxide obtained by calcining limestone containing at least 90 vol.% calcium carbonate to obtain 40-44 vol.% carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of carbon dioxide for soda production by ammonia method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent is a mixture of monoethanolamine and monoethanolbenzylamine in a molar ratio of 1-3:1, or monoethanoldiamine and ethylenediamine in a molar ratio of 3:1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN FR3081882-A1; WO2019234344-A1; CA3102223-A1; FR3081882-B1; CN112384628-A; EP3788157-A1; US2021222212-A1
TI Recovering carbon dioxide by biological reduction,            comprises contacting liquid phase containing            Stenotrophomonas maltophilia and gaseous phase            containing carbon dioxide under conditions to allow            production of formate from carbon dioxide.
AU SOUSSAN L
   RUIZ-VALENCIA A
   BENMEZIANE D
   SANCHEZ- M J
   BELLEVILLE M
   PAOLUCCI-JEANJEAN D
   SANCHEZ-MARCANO J
AE UNIV MONTPELLIER (UMON-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM MONTPELLIER (ECOL-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIMIES MONTPELLIER (ECOL-Non-standard)
   UNIV MONTPELLIER (UMON-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM MONTPELLIER (ECOL-Non-standard)
   ECOLE NAT SUPERIEURE CHIM MONTPELLIER (ECOL-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV MONTPELLIER (UMON-C)
GA 2019A2090R
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbon dioxide by biological                reduction, comprises contacting a liquid phase                containing Stenotrophomonas maltophilia with a                gaseous phase containing carbon dioxide under                conditions allowing the production of formate from                the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering carbon                dioxide by biological reduction, and also for                producing organic compounds of interest, e.g.                methane, lactate or alcohols, preferably 1-5C                (claimed) including methanol, isobutanol or                methyl-1-butanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes the Stenotrophomonas                maltophilia, which is capable of producing formate                by reduction of carbon dioxide under mild operating                conditions, while eliminating the use of costly                cofactors or growth factors; and provides the                formic acid without impurities.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The step of contacting is                  carried out in: a closed reactor; or a                  semi-closed reactor, in which the gaseous phase                  containing carbon dioxide is provided                  continuously, preferably by bubbling, inside the                  reactor. The step of contacting is carried out in                  the presence of an electron donor, intra or                  extracellular. The process comprises a                  preliminary phase of co-cultivation of                  Stenotrophomonas maltophilia with at least one                  methanotrophic bacterium before the step of                  contacting the liquid phase with the gas phase.                  The process further comprises for recovering the                  formate produced in the form of formate and/or                  formic acid and/or organic compounds of interest                  derived from the use of the formate by a                  microorganism capable of using the formate to                  produce organic compounds of interest. The carbon                  dioxide-containing gas phase is injected at the                  center of the reactor by a gas distributor. The                  air present in a head space of the reactor is                  removed, and the gaseous phase containing carbon                  dioxide is injected into the head space of the                  reactor, to place the reaction for the production                  of formate in an atmosphere consisting solely of                  the gas phase. Preferred Components: The reactor                  contains an electrochemical assistance,                  preferably of the bio-electrolysis type. The                  liquid phase also contains at least one                  microorganism adapted to use the formate to                  produce organic compounds of interest, e.g.                  methane, lactate or alcohols, preferably 1C/5C.                  The gaseous phase is a biogas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Carbon dioxide conversion device used for reducing carbon dioxide electrochemical conversion, consists of cathode, anode, and electrolyte membrane including anion and cation exchange membranes, which contact to form junction structure.
AU LEE J
   PARK K
   SUJIK H
AE GWANGJU INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (GWAN-C)
GA 2019950868
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide conversion device consists of a cathode, an anode facing the cathode, and an electrolyte membrane positioned between the cathode and the anode. The electrolyte membrane includes an anion exchange membrane in contact with the cathode, and a cation exchange membrane in contact with the anode. The anion exchange membrane and the cation exchange membrane are contacted to form a junction structure.
   USE - Carbon dioxide conversion device used for electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction conversion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide conversion device has improved carbon dioxide conversion efficiency by minimizing the hydrogen reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction method.

   USE - Foam casting of low-pressure gaseous carbon                dioxide foamed rigid polyurethane.

measuring a low-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide by                a metering device and then transporting to the                static mixer in a constant ratio, and introducing a                isocyanate premix tank, (3) passing the polymerized                methylene diphenyl diisocyanate (MDI) into the                isocyanate premix tank through a static mixer in                proportion, and recycling the polymerized MDI and                low pressure gaseous carbon dioxide entering the                isocyanate premix tank to the static mixer under                normal temperature and pressure, and then                circulating through the static mixer to the                isocyanate premix tank, after mixing, opening the                valve of the isocyanate premix tank and                transporting the uniformly mixed polymer MDI and                low-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide to the                isocyanate storage tank of the high-pressure                casting machine, and (4) after measuring the                pouring ratio, starting the equipment for foaming,                adding the raw material of the isocyanate storage                tank and the polyol storage tank through the rotary                axial plunger metering pump by the high pressure                casting machine to the mixing head, injecting the                two components into the mixing chamber at high                pressure and flow rate, colliding with each other,                achieving the purpose of mixing through the                conversion of kinetic energy, and flowing out of                the mixing head into the mold cavity between the                insulating tube working tube and high density                polyethylene outer protective tube, forming a foam                insulation layer between the insulating tube                working tube and the high-density polyethylene                outer protective tube, after completing the                injection time, forwarding the mixing head piston                rod, and pushing out all the materials of the                mixing chamber at the same time, cleaning the inner                wall of the mixing chamber by the piston rod                without leaving any residual materials, returning                the remaining raw materials to the polyol storage                tank and the isocyanate storage tank containing                gaseous carbon dioxide through the reflux tanks on                both sides of the piston rod. tank, an                isocyanate premix tank, a combined polyether                metering pump, and a polymeric MDI barrel, in which                the polymeric MDI barrel is connected by a delivery                pipe, a feeding pump and the isocyanate premix                tank, and the isocyanate premix tank is connected                to the carbon dioxide cylinder through the static                mixer, the stirring paddle is installed inside the                isocyanate premix tank, the stirring paddle is                connected to the stirring motor, the static mixer                is connected to a metering pump, the static mixer                and the carbon dioxide cylinder are connected with                a pressure reducer, a one-way valve and a flow                meter, the metering pump is connected to an                isocyanate storage tank through a conversion valve,                and the isocyanate storage tank is connected                through the PN CN110330408-A
TI Preparation of gas circulation decomposing coking crude phenol to produce light calcium, involves distilling high temperature coal tar, dephenolizing, feeding causticized mother liquor to filtration centrifuge and drying.
AU SU G
   LIU J
   FAN Y
   YAN M
AE SHANXI YONGDONG CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201986832S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gas circulation decomposing coking crude phenol involves distilling high temperature coal tar to obtain phenolic oil naphthalene oil washing oil mixed fraction, washing and dephenolizing with sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, reacting phenol with sodium hydroxide, decomposing sodium phenolate by carbon dioxide in circulating gas, decomposing phenol sodium salt by carbon dioxide in circulating gas, carrying out static separation, establishing gas circulation system for carbon dioxide decomposition, feeding lower purified sodium carbonate solution into causticizer and reacting with calcium hydroxide, feeding causticized mother liquor to filtration centrifuge, separating solid calcium carbonate and liquid aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, centrifugally separating solid calcium carbonate and aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide, feeding solid calcium was fed into tumble dryer with belt conveyor and drying.
   USE - Preparation of gas circulation decomposing coking crude phenol to produce light calcium.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation of gas circulation decomposing coking crude phenol effectively reduces equipment corrosion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of gas circulation decomposing coking crude phenol involves distilling high temperature coal tar to obtain phenolic oil naphthalene oil washing oil mixed fraction with phenol content of 4-6%, washing and dephenolizing with sodium hydroxide aqueous solution in vertical washing tower, reacting phenol with sodium hydroxide to form 20-40 %mass phenol sodium salt solution, transporting phenol sodium salt solution heated to 80-90 degrees C to upper portion of primary decomposition column having two layers of packing with flow through packing layer from top to bottom and maintaining reverse contact with mixed gas having 16-22% rising carbon dioxide in column, decomposing sodium phenolate by carbon dioxide in circulating gas to form crude phenol and sodium carbonate with flow into bottom of decomposition tower, separating crude phenol from sodium carbonate solution by static separation at temperature difference of 70-80 degrees C for 6-8 hours depending on specific gravity difference between crude phenol and sodium carbonate solution, feeding separated sodium carbonate solution to extraction column using centrifugal pump, where crude phenol flows from intermediate tank of crude phenol, transporting primary crude phenol obtained by static separation to upper part of secondary decomposition tower having three layers of packing through secondary decomposition tower raw material pump under pressure of 0.4 MPa or more, flowing through packing layer from top to bottom having reverse contact with air and carbon dioxide mixed gas with 16-22 vol.% carbon dioxide, decomposing phenol sodium salt by carbon dioxide in circulating gas and flowing into bottom of secondary decomposition tower, carrying out static separation for 6-8 hours to complete separation of crude phenol and sodium carbonate solution, transporting separated sodium carbonate solution using centrifugal pump, increasing decomposition rate of phenol sodium salt to 99.5% or more by secondary decomposition, separating carbon dioxide gas and air that does not participate in reaction by primary decomposition tower, feeding top product of secondary decomposition tower into inlet of circulation fan to be mixed with supplemented carbon dioxide gas, establishing gas circulation system for carbon dioxide decomposition, mixing sodium bicarbonate solution and dephenolated phenol oil in mass ratio of 1:0.2 during extraction, statically layering to obtain purified sodium bicarbonate solution containing less than 0.3% phenol, returning upper phenol oil to washing tower, mixing with aqueous sodium hydroxide solution to wash dephenol, feeding lower purified sodium carbonate solution into causticizer and reacting with calcium hydroxide having mass percentage greater than 95% at temperature greater than 90 degrees C to form an aqueous sodium hydroxide solution and calcium precipitate, feeding causticized mother liquor to filtration centrifuge, separating solid calcium carbonate and liquid aqueous sodium hydroxide solution by centrifugation, returning liquid aqueous sodium hydroxide solution to washing tower for washing and dephenolization of phenol oil naphthalene oil washing mixed fraction, raising solid calcium carbonate to scrubber with vertical hoist, repeatedly washing with water two times, centrifugally separating solid calcium carbonate and 0.016% aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide, adding aqueous sodium hydroxide to caustic soda and returning to washing tower, and mixing with 10% sodium hydroxide solution produced by causticization with concentration of 8% or more, feeding solid calcium into tumble dryer with belt conveyor and drying at temperature of 300-350 degrees C for 20 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The light calcium carbonate semi-finished product is pulverized and sieved by gas stream sorting machine to prepare 800-1200 mesh light calcium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The solid calcium carbonate is fed into drum dryer by belt conveyor, and drying process is in closed negative pressure state controlled between 0.5-5 KPa to prevent dust from escaping, and exhaust gas is evacuated through pulse bag filter. The causticizing and washing processes are carried out in closed state of 0.5-5 KPa under negative pressure, and incinerating tail gas by washing oil in two stages. Waste water, waste residue and waste gas are not produced throughout the process. Preferred Composition: The decomposition gas is mixed gas having 16-22 vol.% carbon dioxide and in order to maintain balance of system carbon dioxide gas having volume concentration greater than 95% is added at inlet of the circulation fan. The sodium carbonate solution formed by decomposition and dephenolized phenol oil are mixed in mass ratio of 1:0.2, and statically layered to obtain purified sodium carbonate solution containing less than 0.3% phenol. Preferred Process: The process involves returning upper phenol oil to washing tower, and mixing again with aqueous sodium hydroxide solution to wash phenol oil and transferring lower purified sodium carbonate solution from causticizer to obtain a purified sodium carbonate solution.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. TI Preparing spherical silica comprises passing sodium silicate and carbon dioxide-containing gas into a microreactor for carbonization.
AU LUO G
   HAN C
   WANG K
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201965140W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing spherical silica comprises passing sodium silicate and carbon dioxide-containing gas into a microreactor for carbonization.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing spherical silica.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves specific surface area, dispersibility, uniform mesoporous structure and quality; and is economical and easy to process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The obtained reaction liquid is aged, separated, precipitated, washed, and spray-dried to obtain spherical silica after the carbonization reaction is completed. Preferred Components: The sodium silicate is an aqueous solution of sodium silicate having a concentration of 10-150 g/L, carbon dioxide-containing gas is carbon dioxide and an inert gas or an industrial waste gas containing carbon dioxide, and the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas or industrial exhaust gas is 10-100%. The microreactor is a micro-mesh reactor or a membrane-dispersed micro-reactor, where the micro-mesh has a diameter of 0.2-1.0 mm, the number of mesh holes is at least 1, and the average pore diameter of the dispersed film is 0.5-100 mu m. The volume flow ratio of the carbon dioxide-containing gas and sodium silicate is 1:1-50:1. The carbonization reaction is a single feed or a recycle feed. The spray drying spray dryer has a nozzle diameter of 0.7-2 mm, an inlet temperature of 120-160 degrees C, a spray solution concentration of 50-500 g/l, and a spray solution feed rate of 20-90%. The spherical silica has a uniform mesoporous structure, an average particle diameter of 0.5-3 mu m, a specific surface area of 290-1150 m2 /g, and a pore volume of 0.35-1.85 cm3/g. Preferred Method: The carbonization reaction temperature is 0-50 degrees C, and when the pH of the reaction solution is 7-9, the carbonization reaction ends. The aging reaction is carried out in an aging reaction vessel, the aging temperature is 0-100 degrees C, the stirring speed is 0-1000 revolutions/minute, and the aging time is 0.01-12 hours and the separated precipitate is washed with water, dispersed in water, and spray-dried.

TI Coal-to-natural gas co-production of methanol system comprises air separation unit and coal gasification unit connected to acid gas removal unit provided with cryogenic separation unit and methanol synthesis and rectification unit.
AU YANG S
   LIU Z
   ZHENG J
   QIAN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020209082
AB    NOVELTY - Coal-to-natural gas co-production of methanol of system comprises air separation unit, coal gasification unit, acid gas removal unit, cryogenic separation unit, methanation unit, methanol synthesis unit and methanol rectification unit. The air separation unit is provided with an air inlet, a nitrogen outlet and an oxygen outlet, and the oxygen outlet is connected to the oxygen inlet of the coal gasification unit. The coal gasification unit is provided with an oxygen inlet, a raw coal inlet, a medium pressure steam inlet, a waste residue outlet, a low pressure steam outlet and a gasification crude synthesis gas outlet. The refined methanol outlet outputs methanol products and the waste water system is discharged outside the system. The methanation unit is provided with a purified synthetic gas inlet, a purge gas inlet, a medium pressure steam outlet and a synthetic natural gas outlet and the synthetic natural gas outlet outputs synthetic natural gas products.
   USE - Used as coal-to-natural gas co-production of methanol system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces natural gas and methanol production can be adjusted to changes in market prices.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coal-to-natural gas co-production of methanol system comprises air separation unit, coal gasification unit, acid gas removal unit, cryogenic separation unit, methanation unit, methanol synthesis unit and methanol rectification unit. The air separation unit is provided with an air inlet, a nitrogen outlet and an oxygen outlet, and the oxygen outlet is connected to the oxygen inlet of the coal gasification unit. The coal gasification unit is provided with an oxygen inlet, a raw coal inlet, a medium pressure steam inlet, a waste residue outlet, a low pressure steam outlet and a gasification crude synthesis gas outlet. The gasification crude synthesis gas outlet is connected to the gasification crude synthesis gas inlet of the acid gas removal unit. The acid gas removal unit is provided with a coal gasification crude synthesis gas inlet, a sulfide outlet, a purified synthesis gas outlet and a carbon dioxide outlet. The purified synthesis gas outlet is divided into two channels, one channel is connected to the cryogenic separation unit and the other channel connected to the methanation unit. The carbon dioxide outlet is divided into two channels, one channel is connected to the methanol synthesis unit and the other channel is discharged outside the system. The cryogenic separation unit is provided with a purified synthesis gas inlet, a methanol synthesis feed gas outlet and a synthetic natural gas outlet. The methanol synthesis feed gas outlet is connected to the methanol synthesis feed gas inlet of the methanol synthesis unit and the synthetic natural gas output outputs a synthetic natural gas product. The methanol synthesis unit is provided with a methanol synthesis feed gas inlet, a purge gas outlet, a medium pressure steam outlet, a flash steam outlet and a crude methanol outlet. The purge gas outlet is connected to the purge gas inlet of the methanation unit and the flash steam is discharged outside the system. The crude methanol outlet is connected to the crude methanol inlet of the methanol rectification unit. The methanol rectification unit is provided with a crude methanol inlet, a refined methanol outlet and a waste water outlet. The refined methanol outlet outputs methanol products and the waste water system is discharged outside the system. The methanation unit is provided with a purified synthetic gas inlet, a purge gas inlet, a medium pressure steam outlet and a synthetic natural gas outlet and the synthetic natural gas outlet outputs synthetic natural gas products. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for co-producing methanol from natural gas using the system comprising entering the air separation unit, separating the air into nitrogen and oxygen, entering oxygen directly to the coal gasification unit and reacting with high temperature gasification of raw coal under medium pressure steam to produce coal gasified crude syngas and remaining waste residue, discharging the synthesis gas as a by-product of low-pressure steam in the waste heat boiler of the gasification furnace in the gasification unit, removing the gasified crude synthesis gas the acid gas removal unit to remove sulfides and high-concentration carbon dioxide to obtain purified synthesis gas, entering the sulfide subsequent Claus sulfur recovery unit, dividing the removed carbon dioxide into two parts, one part of the carbon dioxide entering the methane synthesis unit and the other part of the carbon dioxide is discharging outside the system for storage, dividing the purified synthesis gas into two parts, entering one part of the purified synthesis gas, entering the other part of the purified synthesis gas the methanation unit, entering the methane separated after the purified synthesis gas in cryogenic separation unit by natural gas, removing remaining synthesis gas and carbon dioxide gas from the acid gas removal unit, adjusting the flow distribution after the comparison, entering the methanol synthesis feed gas is sequentially into the methanol synthesis unit obtaining the methanol rectification unit obtains the methanol product after reaction and purification, passing the hydrogen-rich purge gas from the methanol synthesis unit to the methanation unit, adjusting the purified synthesis gas from the acid gas removal unit and the purge gas from the methanol synthesis unit, using as the feed gas of the methanation unit to enter the methanation unit to synthesize methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the coal-to-natural gas co-production of methanol system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of methane in the coal gasified crude synthesis gas is 8-18% and the hydrogen-carbon ratio of the coal gasified crude synthesis gas is 2.3-2.8. The total amount of sulfur in the purified synthesis gas is less than or equal to 0.1 ppm and carbon dioxide is less than or equal to 0.1 ppm. The flow ratio of the purified synthesis gas to the cryogenic separation unit and the methanation unit is 0.5: 1-5: 1. The amount of methane in the cryogenic separation unit is greater than or equal to 98.0 mol.%. The amount methane of the methanation unit is greater than or equal to 96.5 mol.%, where the reactor temperature of the methanation unit is 330-680 degrees C and the pressure is 25-50 bar. The amount of methanol of the methanol synthesis unit is greater than or equal to 80.0 m%, where the reactor temperature of the methanol synthesis unit is 220-280 degrees C and the pressure is 50-80 bar. The purity of the methanol of the methanol rectification unit is greater than or equal to 99.0 m%, where the temperature of the rectification column of the methanol rectification unit is 60-150 degrees C and the pressure is 1-10 bar. Preferred Method: The gasification furnace of the coal gasification unit has a temperature of 800-1100 degrees C and a pressure of 30-50 bar.

PT P
PN CN112410123-A
TI Preparing menthol spice liquid comprises e.g. mixing menthol, N-alkylimidazole and water to prepare mixed solution, and mixing with carbon dioxide to form aggregate solution system.
AU SHI Q
   HU J
   HUA C
   CHAI G
   XU X
   FAN W
   ZHANG Q
   MAO J
   SONG Y
   LIU J
   QU Z
   ZONG Y
AE ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
GA 2021274957
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing menthol spice liquid comprises (i) mixing menthol, N-alkylimidazole and water to prepare mixed solution, where the N-alkylimidazole contains 8-12C alkyl, and (ii) mixing the obtained mixed solution with carbon dioxide to form aggregate solution system.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing menthol spice liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: forms stable menthol-embedded spice by introducing carbon dioxide into the aggregate solution system; has high embedding efficiency; utilizes carbon dioxide as control releasing switch of the spice; destroys the embedding system when non-carbon dioxide or low-concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas is introduced into the system; and realizes controllable release of the spice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for controlled releasing processing menthol spice, comprising (i) mixing menthol, N-alkylimidazole and water to prepare mixed solution, where the N-alkylimidazole contains 8-12C alkyl, (ii) mixing the obtained mixed solution with carbon dioxide to form aggregate solution system, 3) introducing carbon dioxide-lean gas into the aggregate solution system to destroy the aggregate solution system and releasing menthol, where the carbon dioxide-lean gas does not contain carbon dioxide or contains carbon dioxide of not greater than 20 vol.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the mixing process of the mixed liquid and carbon dioxide comprises introducing carbon dioxide gas or high-concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas having carbon dioxide concentration of more than 80% into the mixed liquid. The time for introducing carbon dioxide gas or high-concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas is 10-60 minutes/100 ml mixed solution. In step (ii): the mixing process of the mixed liquid and carbon dioxide includes adding dry ice into the mixed liquid. In step (i), 0.1-0.2 g menthol and 0.1-0.5 g N-alkylimidazole are added for every 50 ml of water when preparing the mixed solution. Preferred Components: The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas or high-concentration carbon dioxide mixed gas is 0.05-0.5 l/minutes. In step (i), the N-alkylimidazole is N-dodecylimidazole. The particle size of the aggregate solution system is 100-300 nm. In step (iii), the flow rate of the carbon dioxide-lean gas is 0.05-0.5 l/minutes.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); P15 (Tobacco (A24).)
MC D10-A05; P15-L05
IP C11B-009/00; A24B-003/12

PN CN212483517-U
TI Air distribution type carbon dioxide correction fixture, has computer connected with relay small plate, and carbon dioxide input adapter connected with carbon dioxide input adapter.
AU XIAO Y
   GU D
   HE Y
   DONG C
   WANG Y
   ZANG Y
AE KUNSHAN RADIAN PHOTOELECTRIC CO LTD (KUNS-Non-standard)
GA 202115773E
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a gas distribution type carbon dioxide correction fixture, composed of a gas distribution box and a gas bottle set connected on the gas distribution box and a correction box; the front end of the air distribution box is provided with a computer, a power switch, a pure nitrogen flow meter and a pure carbon dioxide flow meter; the rear end is provided with a data joint, a power supply socket, a pure nitrogen input adapter, a carbon dioxide input adapter and an output adapter; the air bottle group is composed of a pure nitrogen bottle and a carbon dioxide gas bottle; the pure nitrogen bottle and the carbon dioxide bottle are respectively connected with the pure nitrogen input adapter and the carbon dioxide input adapter. The air distribution carbon dioxide correction fixture has high automation degree, saves the labour, the method of gas distribution becomes pure nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide, reduces the cost, gold finger; the assembling way of the guide rail and the guide strip makes the device assembly simpler; the sealing way of the rubber magnetic strip makes the switch sheet metal front cover and the bottom plate more easy; the operation is convenient; the production efficiency is greatly improved.

PT P
PN CN114773152-A
TI Preparing bio-based butanediol, comprises e.g.            placing bio-based succinic acid into absolute ethanol,            and dispersing uniformly, contacting obtained            pretreatment solution with pretreated adsorbent,            performing primary modification by placing pretreated            adsorbent matrix and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU LI F
   SUN L
   GAO W
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG J
AE SHANDONG YUANLI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022988415
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bio-based butanediol, comprises                placing bio-based succinic acid into an absolute                ethanol, and dispersing uniformly to prepare the                pretreatment solution, contacting the pretreatment                solution with the pretreated adsorbent, performing                primary modification by placing the pretreated                adsorbent matrix in a closed environment,                introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the                temperature and pressure to carbon dioxide                supercritical state, maintaining carbon dioxide                supercritical state, grinding the pretreated                adsorbent matrix, and releasing the pressure and                performing secondary modification by adding                pretreated adsorbent obtained in the forming step                into the modification solution for modification                treatment, where the pretreated adsorbent is                prepared by placing magnesium nitrate and aluminum                nitrate into deionized water, heating, maintaining                pH value, heating, cooling, separating solids,                washing with water, drying to obtain pretreated                adsorbent matrix.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bio-based                butanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the targeted adsorption                of impurities in bio-based succinic acid; can                control the content of trace impurities in                bio-based succinic acid to very low level, which                efficiently ensures the smooth progress of                subsequent reactions; efficiently reduces the                occurrence of side reactions, and the prepares                1,4-butanediol, which can be efficiently purified                and removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing bio-based butanediol, comprises                placing bio-based succinic acid into a absolute                ethanol, and dispersing uniformly to prepare the                pretreatment solution, contacting the pretreatment                solution with the pretreated adsorbent for 10-15                minutes to complete the pretreatment step,                performing primary modification by placing the                pretreated adsorbent matrix in a closed                environment, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing                the temperature and pressure to carbon dioxide                supercritical state, maintaining the carbon dioxide                supercritical state, grinding the pretreated                adsorbent matrix to the particle size of 60-120 nm,                and releasing the pressure to normal pressure and                performing secondary modification by adding                pretreated adsorbent obtained in the forming step                into the modification solution for modification                treatment, where the modification solution is                composed of citric acid, N,N-dimethylacetamide,                diethanolamine, and deionized water, where the                pretreated adsorbent is prepared by placing                magnesium nitrate and aluminum nitrate into                deionized water, heating to 100&#8451;,                maintaining the pH value of the solution at 10-11,                heating for 7-9 hours, and then cooling to room                temperature, separating the solids, washing with                water until the pH is neutral, and drying at low                temperature for 18-20 hours to obtain the                pretreated adsorbent matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for contacting                  the pretreatment liquid with pretreatment                  adsorbent includes passing the pretreatment                  solution through an adsorption column filled with                  a pretreatment adsorbent at a flow rate of 3-5                  ml/minute and pressure of 6-8 MPa. In the matrix                  preparation step, the ratio by weight of                  magnesium nitrate, aluminum nitrate and deionized                  water is 4-6:3-5:80-90. In the first modification                  step, the temperature of carbon dioxide                  supercritical state is 70-80&#8451;, and the                  pressure is 15-20 MPa, and the forming method                  includes subjecting the primary modified                  pretreated adsorbent matrix obtained in the                  primary modification step to tableting treatment,                  and then crushing into particles with a particle                  size of 14-32 meshes. The secondary modification                  includes adding the pretreated adsorbent obtained                  in the forming step into the modification                  solution at 70-80&#8451; under stirring                  conditions at a feeding speed of 3-5 g/minute,                  stirring at 200-300 revolutions/minute for 20-30                  minutes, allowing to stand for 15-20 hours,                  separating the solid matter, drying at                  110-120&#8451; under nitrogen atmosphere for                  10-12 hours to complete the secondary                  modification step. The post-processing method                  includes placing the modified pretreated                  adsorbent obtained in the second modification                  step in a closed environment under nitrogen                  atmosphere conditions, performing irradiation                  treatment using &#947;-rays for 10-15                  minutes, and the post-treatment steps are                  completed, where in the irradiation treatment,                  the radiation dose is 60-120 kGy. The                  esterification method includes mixing                  pretreatment solution prepared in the                  pretreatment step with esterification catalysts                  under the temperature condition of                  120-140&#8451;, carrying out esterification                  reaction until the acid value is not more than                  0.5 mg potassium hydroxide/g to obtain                  esterification product. The purification method                  includes distilling and purifying the                  esterification product obtained in the                  esterification step, where the distillation                  temperature is 120-130&#8451;, the                  distillation pressure is 20-30 kPa, and the acid                  value is not more than 0.15 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g. The hydrogenation method includes                  transferring the product obtained in the                  purification step to a fixed bed reactor equipped                  with a copper-chromium hydrogenation catalyst for                  hydrogenation reaction, where in the                  hydrogenation reaction, the catalytic                  hydrogenation temperature is 160-170&#8451;,                  the catalytic hydrogenation pressure is 16-17                  MPa, and the hydrogen feed flow rate is 0.3-0.5                  m3/h.Preferred Components: In the modification                  solution, the weight ratio of citric acid,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, diethanolamine and                  deionized water is 12-15:3-5:3-5:60-70.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-F06; E11-D02; E10-E04B; J01-G; N07-D07; N07-B02; N05-E02; N02-D; N03-D01

PT P
PN CN114686528-A
TI Synergistic anaerobic reaction method of kitchen            and kitchen waste comprises e.g. carrying out anaerobic            fermentation reaction to organic slurry, converting            organic volatile fatty acids into carbon dioxide and            methane, or reducing carbon dioxide with hydrogen to            produce methane.
AU YANG J
   XU J
   WEN J
   XU F
   LAN Q
   RAN M
   YU C
   ZHAO S
   WANG Y
AE CHENGDU XINGRONG RENEWABLE ENERGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022897984
AB    NOVELTY - Synergistic anaerobic reaction method of                kitchen and kitchen waste comprises mixing the                organic slurry to be treated, mixing the organic                slurry to be treated again, performing the                homogeneous hydrolysis reaction of the organic                slurry to be treated, under the action of                fermenting bacteria, firstly degrading the                macromolecular organic matter and refractory                organic matter in the organic slurry into small                molecules and easily biodegraded into organic                matter, carrying out anaerobic fermentation                reaction to the organic slurry that has undergone                the above-mentioned treatment, so that under the                action of methanogens, converting organic volatile                fatty acids into carbon dioxide and methane, or                reducing carbon dioxide with hydrogen to produce                methane, setting a return pipeline so that the                biogas slurry after the anaerobic fermentation                reaction is returned in a certain proportion, and                forming the organic slurry to be treated again to                repeat the above process.
   USE - The method is useful for synergistic anaerobic                reaction of kitchen and kitchen waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can avoid the disadvantages of                acid inhibition in the early stage of anaerobic                fermentation and inhibition of methane production                in the later stage due to different degradation                rates of different organic materials, e.g. kitchen                waste, and the mismatch of mixed fermentation                efficiency, improves the synergistic anaerobic                reaction efficiency of kitchen and kitchen waste,                and improves the economic benefits of the operation                of the cooperative treatment plant for kitchen                waste and kitchen waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synergistic anaerobic reaction method of                kitchen and kitchen waste comprises mixing the                organic slurry to be treated, mixing the organic                slurry to be treated again, performing the                homogeneous hydrolysis reaction of the organic                slurry to be treated, under the action of                fermenting bacteria, firstly degrading the                macromolecular organic matter and refractory                organic matter in the organic slurry into small                molecules and easily biodegraded into organic                matter, then hydrolyzing into monomers under the                action of hydrolase secreted by microorganisms,                then the hydrogen-producing acid-producing bacteria                convert the above products into organic volatile                fatty acids, while producing carbon dioxide and                hydrogen, carrying out anaerobic fermentation                reaction to the organic slurry that has undergone                the above-mentioned treatment, so that under the                action of methanogens, converting organic volatile                fatty acids into carbon dioxide and methane, or                reducing carbon dioxide with hydrogen to produce                methane, setting a return pipeline so that the                biogas slurry after the anaerobic fermentation                reaction is returned in a certain proportion, and                forming the organic slurry to be treated again to                repeat the above process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The organic slurry to                  be treated is kitchen waste organic slurry,                  kitchen waste organic slurry, a small amount of                  backflow biogas slurry after anaerobic                  fermentation. The designed hydraulic retention                  time of the homogeneous hydrolysis reactor is 5                  days. The designed hydraulic retention time of                  the anaerobic fermentation reactor is 30 days.                  The return pipeline includes a biogas slurry                  return pipe, a regulating valve, a return pump                  and/or a flow meter connected in series.Preferred Method: The first mixing treatment                  of the organic slurry to be treated is                  specifically: initially uniformly mixing the                  kitchen waste organic slurry, the kitchen waste                  organic slurry and a small amount of backflow                  biogas slurry after anaerobic fermentation in a                  pipeline mixer. The organic slurry to be treated                  is mixed again, and the homogeneous hydrolysis                  reaction of the organic slurry to be treated is                  specifically: subjecting the organic slurry of                  kitchen waste, organic slurry of kitchen waste                  and a small amount of backflow biogas slurry                  after anaerobic fermentation to a homogeneous                  hydrolysis reaction in a homogeneous hydrolysis                  reactor. The anaerobic fermentation reaction of                  the organic slurry after the above treatment is                  specifically: performing an anaerobic                  fermentation reaction on the organic slurry after                  the homogeneous hydrolysis reaction in an                  anaerobic fermentation reactor. The backflow of                  the backflow pipeline can be adjusted within the                  range of the backflow ratio of 0-35%, in order to                  make the biogas slurry with pH 7-7.5 produced by                  anaerobic fermentation reaction to be adjusted                  back to the homogeneous hydrolysis reactor                  according to the reflux ratio of 0-35%, has                  buffering effect on the pH reduction of the                  homogeneous hydrolysis reactor, and enables the                  organic slurry to stably transition to the                  anaerobic fermentation reactor for the reaction                  in the methane production stage.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); 
MC D04-A01J; D05-A04A; E10-J02D1; H09-F03; H06-A04; H06-P
IP C02F-011/04; C12M-001/00; C12M-001/107; C12P-005/02

PT P
PN CN111411004-A; CN111411004-B
TI Purification of biogas by pressure swing adsorption principle involves passing biogas produced after anaerobic fermentation into desulfurization tower, drying and passing to pressure swing adsorption tower to adsorb carbon dioxide in biogas.
AU NIU X
   WANG Z
   LV M
   GUO H
   TANG Z
   LIU M
   LIN Z
   ZHANG D
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
   UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 202068938W
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of biogas by pressure swing adsorption principle involves passing biogas produced after anaerobic fermentation into desulfurization tower containing desulfurization agent, passing desulfurized biogas into drying tower, passing dry biogas into filter tower to remove insoluble impurities in the dry biogas, adjusting pressure of the outlet gas from the filter tower to obtain compressed biogas, passing compressed biogas into pressure swing adsorption tower (A) containing purification adsorbent to adsorb carbon dioxide in compressed biogas to obtain purified biogas (A), passing purified biogas (B) into adsorption tower (B) containing purification adsorbent to obtain purified biogas, stop passing compressed biogas into adsorption tower (A) when carbon dioxide adsorbed in adsorption tower (A) reaches saturation, simultaneously evacuating adsorption tower (A) and adsorption tower (B) to desorb carbon dioxide gas adsorbed on adsorbent in adsorption tower (A) and (B) and releasing.
   USE - Purification of biogas by pressure swing adsorption principle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively purifies biogas with high purification efficiency and is suitable for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of biogas by pressure swing adsorption principle involves passing biogas produced after anaerobic fermentation into the desulfurization tower containing desulfurization agent to remove sulfide in the biogas, passing desulfurized biogas into drying tower to remove the moisture in the desulfurized biogas so that the moisture content of the desulfurized biogas is 2-9 mg/kg to obtain dry biogas, passing dry biogas into filter tower to remove insoluble impurities in the dry biogas, adjusting pressure of the outlet gas from the filter tower to 0.5-1.0 MPa to obtain compressed biogas, passing compressed biogas into pressure swing adsorption tower (A) containing purification adsorbent at flow rate of 1-3 m3/hour to adsorb carbon dioxide in compressed biogas to obtain purified biogas (A) where intermittent time of the intake air is 3-5 minutes and temperature of intake air is 50~150 degrees C, adjusting pressure of purified biogas (A) to 1.0-1.5 MPa, passing purified biogas (B) into adsorption tower (B) containing purification adsorbent at flow rate of 2-5 m3/hour to obtain purified biogas where intermittent time of the intake air is 1-3 minutes and temperature of intake air is 150-250 degrees C, stop passing compressed biogas into adsorption tower (A) when carbon dioxide adsorbed in adsorption tower (A) reaches saturation, simultaneously evacuating adsorption tower (A) and adsorption tower (B) to desorb the carbon dioxide gas adsorbed on purification adsorbent in adsorption tower (A) and adsorption tower (B) and releasing to realize the regeneration of purified adsorbent. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for purification device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The desiccant used in drying tower is composite desiccant. Preferred Composition: The composite desiccant comprises 16-33 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 12-21 pts. wt. sodium carbonate, 10-20 pts. wt. calcium chloride, 8-23 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 7-19 pts. wt. calcium hydride and 10-25 pts. wt. deionized water. The purification adsorbent comprises 15-26 pts. wt. alumina, 5-18 pts. wt. carbon molecular sieve, 8-17 pts. wt. sodium sulfate, 10-23 pts. wt. zeolite and 18-20 pts. wt. deionized water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The desulfurization agent comprises 15-30 pts. wt. organic amine desulfurizer, 8-15 pts. wt. sulfoxide, 1-8 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol, 5-16 pts. wt. propylene carbonate and 20-35 pts. wt. ionized water. Preferred Component: The filter membrane in filter tower is one of polyamide fiber membrane, polyimide hollow fiber membrane and polyvinylidene fluoride hollow fiber membrane. Preferred Process: The desulfurization agent is prepared by reacting organic amine desulfurizer with sulfoxide and polyethylene glycol, controlling pH value of the reaction to be 6-8 to generate colloidal precipitate, oxidizing colloidal precipitate with oxygen-enriched gas, filtering and separating, washing filtered sediment with water for 2-5 times to obtain solid, mixing solid with propylene carbonate and deionized water, extruding on molding machine and naturally drying.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E06-A02E; E07-A04; E11-Q02C; E31-A04; E31-F01A; E31-N04C; E31-N05C; E31-P02B; E33-C; E33-D; E34-B03; E34-C02; E34-D02; E34-D03A; H06-A04; H06-X01; J01-E02B; J01-E03C; J01-E03D; J01-G03; A04-E10B; A05-F01E; A05-H03; A05-J01B; A12-T03A; A12-W11A; A12-W11F
IP C10L-003/10

PT P
PN CN109019642-A; CN109019642-B
TI Extracting lithium carbonate from salt lake brine by introducing carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas into heating device, supplying lithium-rich brine, precipitating lithium carbonate particles from brine, filtering and discharging.
AU WEI F
   ZHANG C
   ZHU C
   LIU C
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2018A46340
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium carbonate from salt lake brine involves: (a) introducing carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas into a heating device; (b) supplying a lithium-rich brine to the heating device; (c) carrying out convection on the carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas and the lithium-rich brine in the heating device; (d) ensuring the carbon dioxide to enter the brine to form a carbonate ion, and absorbing the heat of combustion gas by the lithium-rich brine; (e) precipitating lithium carbonate particles from the brine to form a suspension containing lithium carbonate particles; (f) discharging the suspension into a filtration device, and trapping the lithium carbonate particles by a filter screen in the filtration device; and (g) when the amount of the lithium carbonate particles trapped by the filter screen reaches a predetermined threshold value, discharging the lithium carbonate concentrated salt slurry through a particle discharge port.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lithium carbonate from salt lake brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of extracting the lithium carbonate from salt lake brine with high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting lithium carbonate from salt lake brine involves: (a) introducing carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas having a temperature of not less than 500 degrees C into a heating device through a gas inlet arranged at a lower end of the heating device; (b) supplying a lithium-rich brine having a pH value of 7-9 to the heating device through a liquid inlet provided at an upper end of the heating device, where the lithium-rich brine is one of a carbonate-type lithium-rich brine, a sulfate-type lithium-rich brine for removing magnesium ions, and a chloride-type lithium-rich brine for removing magnesium ions; (c) carrying out convection on the carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas and the lithium-rich brine in the heating device; (d) ensuring the carbon dioxide to enter the brine to form carbonate ion, absorbing the heat of combustion gas by the lithium-rich brine and heating; (e) precipitating lithium carbonate particles from the lithium-rich brine after heating to form a suspension containing lithium carbonate particles; (f) discharging the suspension containing lithium carbonate particles into a filtration device, and trapping the lithium carbonate particles by a filter screen in the filtration device; and (g) when the amount of the lithium carbonate particles trapped by the filter screen reaches a predetermined threshold value, discharging the lithium carbonate concentrated salt slurry through a particle discharge port.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves heating each of the filtration devices, where the temperature in each of the filtration devices is not lower than the temperature of the lithium-rich brine after heating, and the temperature the lithium-rich brine is not higher than the local boiling point. Preferred Condition: In the hot water apparatus, the contact time of the carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas and the lithium-rich brine is not less than 2 seconds and not greater than 10 seconds. Preferred Properties: The linear velocity of the carbon dioxide-containing combustion gas at the gas inlet is not greater than 1.5 m/hour. The discharged lithium carbonate concentrated salt slurry has a flow rate of 5-500 kg/hour, a solid density of 1500-3000 kg/m3, and a pore diameter of 3-5 mu m.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC E11-Q01C; E11-Q02C; E33-G; E34-B04

PT P
PN CN108380180-A; CN108380180-B
TI Preparing mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide-carbon dioxide absorbing material involves adding flake graphite and sodium nitrate to three-necked flask, then adding concentrated sulfuric acid and potassium permanganate and stirring.
AU XU J
   LIU H
   LI J
   ZHAO L
   XING W
   ZHU H
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2018643409
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide-carbon dioxide absorbing material involves (a) adding flake graphite and sodium nitrate to three-necked flask, then adding concentrated sulfuric acid under ice water bath and stirring, then adding potassium permanganate and continuously reacting, heating and continuously stirring, adding deionized water and heating in water bath, stirring, adding deionized water and heating, then adding hydrogen peroxide and stirring, washing the product and vacuum drying to obtain graphene oxide, (b) then adding graphene oxide solution, ultrasonically processing the graphene oxide solution, adding mineral acid solution to beaker and continuously stirring in magnetic stirrer, then filtering and vacuum drying to obtain mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide-carbon dioxide absorbing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enable simple and economical preparation of mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide-carbon dioxide absorbing material, and is suitable for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide-carbon dioxide absorbing material involves (a) adding 2 g flake graphite and 1 g sodium nitrate to three-necked flask, then adding 46 ml concentrated sulfuric acid under ice water bath and stirring, then adding 6 g potassium permanganate and continuously reacting for 3 hours, heating at 35 degrees C and continuously stirring for 30 minutes, adding 92 ml deionized water and heating in water bath at 98 degrees C, stirring for 20 minutes, adding 280 ml deionized water and heating at 70 degrees C, then adding 20 ml hydrogen peroxide and stirring for 15 minutes, washing the product and vacuum drying to obtain graphene oxide, (b) then adding 6.06 mg ml-1 graphene oxide solution, ultrasonically processing the graphene oxide solution for 2-3 hours, adding 2.4 ml mineral acid solution to beaker and continuously stirring in magnetic stirrer for 24 hours, then filtering and vacuum drying at 40 degrees C to obtain mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide material, and (c) characterizing structure and carbon dioxide adsorption properties of mineral acid intercalated graphene oxide material by X-ray powder diffraction, determining the mineral acid intercalation of interlayer distance by Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy analysis to determine functional groups (such as sulfate, phosphate and hydroxyl group) containing mineral acid intercalation graphene oxide material by nitrogen assay of mineral acid intercalation graphene oxide material of Brunauer-Emmett-Teller specific surface area, determining by carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of mineral acid intercalation oxide graphene material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of mineral acid is 0.5 mol.l-1. The mineral acid is 1 mol.l-1 sulfuric acid or 5 mol.l-1 and phosphoric acid. The content of hydrogen peroxide is 30%.

PT P
PN CN109621925-A; CN109621925-B
TI Carbon dioxide trapping agent useful in flue gas of large coal-fired enterprises including power generation, metallurgy and cement.
AU LIU D
   CHEN L
   ZHOU L
   YU Y
   SONG J
AE UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
   UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
GA 201938733F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide trapping agent is claimed. The trapping agent is prepared by taking waste concrete as raw material, crushing, calcining, pre-sintering, vibrating and separating through 60-325 mesh sieve, collecting sieved fine particles, subjecting fine particles to grinding treatment, controlling specific surface area of milled powder at greater than or equal to 400 m(2)/kg, grinding and magnetically separating dry iron, where discharge size after crushing is less than or equal to 10 mm.
   USE - The carbon dioxide trapping agent is useful in flue gas of large coal-fired enterprises including power generation, metallurgy and cement.
   ADVANTAGE - The trapping agent: has simple operation, fast absorption rate, short reaction time and improves gel hardness. The method utilizes easily available raw materials and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of carbon dioxide trapping agent comprises either (i) mixing carbon dioxide trapping agent with water, reacting under normal temperature conditions at speed of 100-400 revolutions/minute for 5-30 minutes, passing through 100-325 mesh stainless steel filter and filtering suspension to obtain slurry, (ii) adding obtained slurry into fixed bed carbon trap, introducing carbon dioxide gas to be treated from the inlet of fixed bed carbon trap, stirring slurry at a speed of 100-400 revolutions/minute, contacting with with carbon dioxide, heating at 20-95 degrees C and stopping the reaction when the concentration of carbon dioxide gas at the outlet of fixed bed carbon trap differs from the concentration of carbon dioxide at the inlet of the fixed bed carbon trap by less than 0.5% (or) loading carbon dioxide trapping agent into Catlab reactor, heating at 600-1200 degrees C at a rate of 2-20 degrees C/minute, introducing carbon dioxide gas to be treated into inlet of Catlab reactor, contacting carbon dioxide trapping agent with carbon dioxide and stopping the reaction when the concentration of carbon dioxide gas at the outlet of fixed bed carbon trap differs from the concentration of carbon dioxide at the inlet of the fixed bed carbon trap by less than 0.5%, where flow rate of carbon dioxide gas to be treated is 5 ml/minute to 5 l/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises magnetically separating dry iron, stirring dry iron powder and carbon powder uniformly to obtain mixture, transferring mixture into ball milling machine, adding water and aqueous sodium silicate solution for rotation into ball milling machine, ensuring diameter of the ball for less than or equal to 5 mm, discharging ball outside the disk, drying pellets, placing in muffle furnace for calcination, cooling to room temperature to obtain porous particles and crushing. The method comprises calcining at 500-800 degrees C for 30-40 minutes. The method comprises heating at constant temperature of 700-900 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute for 10-30 minutes. Preferred Components: The mass ratio between dry iron powder and carbon powder is 10-20:1. The mass ratio between ball milled mixture, water and aqueous sodium silicate solution is 90-96:2-5:2-5. The mass concentration of aqueous sodium silicate solution is 30-40%. The mass ratio between carbon dioxide trapping agent and water is 1-10:100 and flow rate of carbon dioxide gas to be treated is 5-20 ml/minute.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)

TI Combined device comprises heating device used to            heat carbon dioxide concentrated gas to generate carbon            dioxide fluidized gas, and dryer arranged downstream of            heating device, where bottom of dryer is passed into            fluidized gas.
AU LI L
