PN GB2583142-A; WO2020211983-A1
TI Treating starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide and water, being produced including Claus process, comprises forming fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide.
AU GUZMANN M
   MANIUT C
   MERCER J
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2020A26936
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide and water, being produced including a Claus process, comprises forming a fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide, where the starting gas mixture is enriched in carbon dioxide and depleted in sulfur dioxide and water in a first group of method steps forming an intermediate gas mixture, the first group of method steps includes compressing, cooling, condensing and drying steps, and in that the intermediate gas mixture is at least partially submitted to a second group of method steps, the second group of method steps includes absorbing steps in which liquid carbon dioxide is used to absorb sulfur dioxide from the intermediate gas mixture, the liquid carbon dioxide is formed from carbon dioxide contained in the intermediate gas mixture after treatment in the absorbing steps.
   USE - The method is useful for treating starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide and water, being produced including Claus process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for treating a starting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide and water, comprising means adapted for forming a fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide and to enrich the starting gas mixture in carbon dioxide and deplete the starting gas mixture in sulfur dioxide and water forming an intermediate gas mixture in the first group of method steps.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The starting gas mixture comprises 15-25 mole% carbon dioxide, 1-5 mole% sulfur dioxide and 60-70 mole% water, where the intermediate gas mixture comprises 65-75 mole% carbon dioxide and less sulfur dioxide and water than the starting gas mixture. The first group of method steps includes subjecting the starting gas mixture to a first cooling step at a first pressure level and from a first temperature level to a second temperature level, forming a first condensate and a first gaseous remainder, subjecting at least a part of the first gaseous remainder to a compression step from the first pressure level to a second pressure level, forming a second condensate and a second gaseous remainder, subjecting at least a part of the second gaseous remainder to a further cooling step to a third temperature level, forming a third condensate and a third gaseous remainder, subjecting at least a part of the third gaseous remainder to a drying step obtaining a dried gas mixture, and using at least a part of the dried gas mixture as the intermediate gas mixture which is subjected to the second group of method steps. The first gaseous remainder comprises 50-60 mole% carbon dioxide, where the second gaseous remainder, the third gaseous remainder and the dried gas mixture each comprise 65-75 mole% carbon dioxide, the third gaseous remainder comprises less than 5000 vppm water, and the dried gas mixture comprises less than 20 vppm water. The first temperature level is 200-300 degrees C and the second temperature level and the third temperature level each are 0-60 degrees C. The first pressure level is 0-1 bar (g) and the second pressure level is 20-30 bar (g). Absorbing steps used in the second group of method steps include a first absorbing step using a first absorption column in which a first liquid bottom product and a first gaseous overhead product is formed, and a second absorbing step using a second absorption column in which a second liquid bottom product and a second gaseous overhead product is formed, at least a part of the intermediate gas mixture being supplied to the first absorbing step at least a part of the first gaseous overhead product being supplied to the second absorbing step, at least a part of the second gaseous overhead product being liquefied, and the liquid carbon dioxide used in the absorbing steps being formed from a part of the liquefied second gaseous overhead product. At least a part of the first liquid bottom product and the second liquid bottom product is treated in a waste handling step, forming a fraction which is recycled to the Claus process. A part of the second liquid bottom product and/or a further fraction which is formed in the waste handling step is recycled to the second absorbing step. Heat is transferred in the first group of method steps to a part of the liquefied second gaseous overhead product, forming an evaporated carbon dioxide product. The evaporated carbon dioxide product is compressed to provide a compressed carbon dioxide product. The fraction predominantly or exclusively containing carbon dioxide is at least in part produced as or processed to a liquid fraction.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110668970-A; CN110668970-B
TI Separating catechin, theaflavin, theanine, and tea polysaccharides from instant tea comprises e.g. injecting powdered instant tea into dissolver, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction of caffeine and purifying.
AU ZHANG S
AE ZHANG S (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202006186M
AB    NOVELTY - Separating catechin, theaflavin, theanine, and tea polysaccharides from instant tea comprises (i) injecting the powdered instant tea into the dissolver under vacuum, and dissolving in deionized water at a constant temperature to obtain tea soup; (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction of caffeine using petroleum ether and purifying and recrystallizing; (iii) introducing first raffinate phase into the second supercritical extraction kettle, extracting catechins and precipitating catechin and ethyl acetate from the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (iv) introducing second raffinate phase into the third supercritical extraction kettle, extracting theaflavin and precipitating theaflavin and ethanol; (v) introducing third raffinate phase into the fourth supercritical extraction kettle, extracting theanine and precipitating theanine and ammonium hydroxide; and (vi) introducing fourth raffinate phase into the alcohol decanter and precipitating tea polysaccharides.
   USE - The method is useful for separating catechin, theaflavin, theanine, and tea polysaccharides from instant tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures caffeine purity by greater than or equal to 99%, catechin purity by greater than or equal to 92%, theaflavin purity by greater than or equal to 80%, theanine purity by greater than or equal to 95%, and tea polysaccharide purity by greater than or equal to 42, preferably greater than or equal to 62% and ensures product good color, good solubility, and high active ingredients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating catechin, theaflavin, theanine, and tea polysaccharides from instant tea comprises (i) injecting the powdered instant tea into the dissolver under vacuum, and dissolving in deionized water at a constant temperature to obtain tea soup; (ii) pouring tea soup into the first supercritical extraction kettle, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction of caffeine using petroleum ether as a carrier for 15-30 minutes at 35-45 degrees C with pressure of 35-40 MPa to obtain the first extraction phase and the first extractive phase, continuously and quantitatively precipitating of caffeine and petroleum ether from first extraction phase at a separation pressure of 6-6.5 MPa, washing, purifying and recrystallizing in deionized water and drying in vacuum to obtain caffeine; (iii) introducing first raffinate phase into the second supercritical extraction kettle, extracting catechins with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid using ethyl acetate as a carrier for 20-30 minutes at 55-65 degrees C with pressure of 12-25 MPa to obtain a second extraction phase and a second extractive phase, precipitating catechin and ethyl acetate from the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid at the second extraction phase at a separation pressure of 6-6.5 MPa, washing and purifying with deionized water, recovering and freeze-drying ethyl acetate to obtain catechins; (iv) introducing second raffinate phase into the third supercritical extraction kettle, extracting theaflavin with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid using ethanol as carrier for 20-30 minutes at 35-42 degrees C with pressure of 15-25 MPa to obtain a third extraction phase and a third extractive phase, precipitating theaflavin and ethanol at a separation pressure of 6-6.5 MPa from the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and freeze-drying after enzymatic oxidation to obtain theaflavin; (v) introducing third raffinate phase into the fourth supercritical extraction kettle, extracting theanine with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid using ammonium hydroxide as a carrier for 20-30 minutes at 40-50 degrees C with pressure of 30-38 MPa to obtain a fourth extraction phase and a fourth extractive phase, precipitating theanine and ammonium hydroxide from the fourth extraction phase at a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid at a separation pressure of 6-6.5 MPa, purifying and recrystallizing and drying under vacuum to obtain theanine; and (vi) introducing fourth raffinate phase into the alcohol decanter, continuously quantitatively precipitating tea polysaccharides with 90% ethanol for 30-40 minutes, recovering and concentrating the alcohol solution by vacuum ethanol and vacuum drying at 50-60 degrees C to obtain tea polysaccharides.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the amount of carrier is 5% of the volume of carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid with a volume ratio of 1:10. In the step (iii), the amount of carrier is 5% carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid with a volume ratio of 1:8-12. In the step (iv), the amount of carrier is 10% of the volume of carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid with a volume ratio of 1:8-12. In the step (v), the amount of carrier is 10% of the volume of carbon dioxide, and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid with a volume ratio of 1:10-15. In the step (vi), the volume ratio of the fourth raffinate phase to ethanol is 1:1-1.5 and the residual liquid is continuously entered into a vacuum low-temperature atomization concentrator, dehydrated at 50-60 degrees C to obtain a thick slurry, and dried in vacuum to obtain tea polyphenols. Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises vacuuming the powdered instant tea into a continuous dissolver rotating tangentially, dissolving in a constant temperature range of 50-80 degrees C to prepare tea soup using 2-6 times non-ionized water, adjusting the pH value of tea soup using citric acid solution to 5.5-6.8 and filtering through a microfilter with a pore size of 0.1-5 mu m to obtain a refined tea soup.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN CN108975355-A; CN209567818-U
TI Pre-carbonizing device used to increase the            particle size of baking soda includes mixed gas intake            pipe formed by low- and high- concentration carbon            dioxide pipe set to carbonization tower, and alkali            liquor pipe set to feed port.
AU PANG S
   MEI S
   XIE Z
   LI M
   LI Z
   NIE Z
   MA L
   DING J
   WANG S
AE TIANJIN BOHUA YONGLI CHEM CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN BOHUA YONGLI CHEM CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A26436
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-carbonizing device comprises an exhaust                gas discharge port set at the top of each of                carbonization tower (1), and mixed gas intake pipe                (3) connected with the mixed air inlet of each                carbonization tower. The mixed gas intake pipe                formed by confluence of low concentration carbon                dioxide pipe (4) and high concentration carbon                dioxide pipe (5), carbonized liquid outlet pipe                connected with the liquid outlet of each                carbonization tower, carbonized crystal slurry pipe                and pre-carbonized crystal slurry pipe set with                carbonized liquid outlet pipe, thickener via slurry                pump connected to the carbonized crystal slurry                pipe, centrifugal drying system connected to the                thickener, alkali liquor pipe (2) connected with                feed port of each carbonization tower, an exhaust                gas venting pipe and carbonized tail gas recycling                pipe connected with the exhaust gas discharge port                of each carbonization tower.
   USE - The device is useful for increasing the                particle size of baking soda.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has less production cost, simple                operating process, and is convenient for                popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pre-carbonizing device comprises an exhaust                gas discharge port set at the top of each of                carbonization tower (1), the top side set with a                feed port, while the bottom side set with a mixed                air inlet and a liquid outlet, a mixed gas intake                pipe (3) connected with the mixed air inlet of each                carbonization tower. The mixed gas intake pipe                formed by confluence of a low concentration carbon                dioxide pipe (4) and a high concentration carbon                dioxide pipe (5), a carbonized liquid outlet pipe                connected with the liquid outlet of each                carbonization tower, a carbonized crystal slurry                pipe and a pre-carbonized crystal slurry pipe set                with carbonized liquid outlet pipe, a thickener via                a slurry pump connected to the carbonized crystal                slurry pipe, a centrifugal drying system connected                to the thickener, an alkali liquor pipe (2)                connected with feed port of each carbonization                tower, an exhaust gas venting pipe and a carbonized                tail gas recycling pipe connected with the exhaust                gas discharge port of each carbonization tower. The                pre-carbonized crystal slurry pipe connected to the                feed port of each carbonization tower via an alkali                pump, the carbonized tail gas recycle pipe                connected to the low concentration carbon dioxide                pipe. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                pre-carbonization process comprising (a) passing                the alkali solution obtained by dissolving and                purifying the raw material to a feed port of a                carbonization tower which is a washing tower,                carrying out pre-carbonization reaction with a low                concentration of carbon dioxide in the                carbonization tower, prolonging the reaction                residence time after obtaining the supersaturated                solution at the corresponding temperature, and                sampling and testing the liquid in the                carbonization tower until the crystals are                generated in the carbonization tower, where the                solid-liquid ratio is not greater than 10%, (b)                turning on the alkali liquor pipe to deliver the                pre-carbonized crystal slurry in the cleaning tower                to the feed port on the top side of each                carbonization tower which is the alkali tower,                where the total amount of liquid returned is equal                to the total amount of crystal slurry flowing out                of the liquid outlet of the cleaning tower, and is                further carbonized with a high concentration of                carbon dioxide in a carbonization tower, and (c)                pumping the carbonized crystal slurry flowing out                from the bottom of the alkali column to the                thickener through crystal slurry, continuously                conveying the thick sodium bicarbonate crystals to                a centrifugal drying system to obtain finished                baking soda.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the pre-carbonizing device.
   Carbonization tower (1)
   Alkali liquor pipe (2)
   Mixed gas intake pipe (3)
   Low concentration carbon dioxide pipe                (4)
   High concentration carbon dioxide pipe                (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The low-concentration                  carbon dioxide pipe, high-concentration carbon                  dioxide pipe, carbonized crystal slurry pipe,                  pre-carbonized crystal slurry pipe, alkali liquor                  pipe, exhaust gas venting pipe and carbonized                  tail gas recycling pipe are respectively set with                  a valve, when carbonization tower is used as a                  cleaning tower, the high concentration carbon                  dioxide pipe connected to the carbonization tower                  and valve on the carbonized crystal slurry line                  are closed, when carbonization tower is used as                  the alkali tower, the low-concentration carbon                  dioxide pipe connected to the carbonization tower                  and the valve on the pre-carbonized crystal                  slurry line are closed. The specifications of the                  carbonization tower are same, determining the                  number of the cleaning tower and the alkali tower                  according to actual production conditions, and                  the number of cleaning towers is less than or                  equal to 50% of the total number of carbonization                  towers. The carbonization column is preferably                  set with four, where 1-2 are cleaning towers, and                  2-3 are alkali-making towers. The concentration                  of carbon dioxide in the low-concentration carbon                  dioxide pipe is 15-35%, and the concentration of                  carbon dioxide in high-concentration carbon                  dioxide pipe is 45-65%. The concentration of the                  low concentration carbon dioxide in the step (a)                  is 15-35%, and the preferred concentration is 35%                  in the initial stage of driving. The carbon                  dioxide concentration after the normal production                  is 25%, and the solid-liquid ratio in the step                  (a) is 5%. The low-concentration carbon dioxide                  is initially configured with high concentration                  of carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and the low                  concentration of carbon dioxide mainly uses                  carbonized tail gas to assist a small amount of                  high concentration carbon dioxide after the                  normal production of device. The size parameters                  of the carbonization towers in step (a) and (b)                  are same, the distinction is made according to                  the different roles of the carbonization tower in                  the production process, and part of the                  carbonization towers are selectively made                  according to actual production conditions,                  selecting the part of carbonizing tower as alkali                  tower and the remaining part as cleaning tower,                  and the cleaning tower and the alkali tower can                  be flexibly switched. Preferred Method: The                  concentration of the high concentration carbon                  dioxide in the step (b) is 45-65%, which is                  prepared by mixing carbon dioxide gas from a pipe                  network concentration of 90% with pipe network                  nitrogen.

PN CN108125986-A
TI Extracting active components of ants comprises e.g. selecting an artificially cultivated or wild complete ant body, rinsing the ant body with clean water, placing the treated ant body in a drying device, passing through hot air and feeding.
AU LU Z
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201846517D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting active components of ants comprises (i) selecting an artificially cultivated or wild complete ant body and rinsing the ant body with clean water, (ii) placing the ant body treated in the step (i) in a drying device, passing through hot air, feeding, sending to the normal temperature wind for drying and recovering the ant body to room temperature, (iii) placing the ant body processed in step (ii) into a carbon dioxide extractor and performing cell wall wall-breaking twice, performing second cell wall breaking treatment, (iv) placing the ants after two cell wall breaking treatments in a carbon dioxide extractor for extraction, (v) sending the extract fluid prepared in step (iv) to the separator for separation after the extraction is complete, (vi) cooling the temperature in the separator to room temperature, taking out the extracted product from the separator and (vii) filling the extracted product obtained in step (vi) into the container, sealing the container and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting active components of ants (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the yield of the active component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting active components of ants comprises (i) selecting an artificially cultivated or wild complete ant body and rinsing the ant body with clean water, (ii) placing the ant body treated in the step (i) in a drying device, passing through hot air at 90-100 degrees C, feeding continuously for 3-4 hours, sending to the normal temperature wind for drying and recovering the ant body to room temperature, (iii) placing the ant body processed in step (ii) into a carbon dioxide extractor and performing cell wall wall-breaking twice, where during the first cell wall disruption process, the cell wall breaking pressure is 10-12 MPa, the wall breaking temperature is 25-30 degrees C, the time is 5-10 minutes, the pressure in the carbon dioxide extractor is reduced to 5 MPa by a pressure reducer and allowing to stand for 20-30 minutes, performing second cell wall breaking treatment, where during the second cell wall disruption treatment, the cell wall breaking pressure is 35-40 MPa, the wall breaking temperature is 45-55 degrees C and the time is 60-80 minutes, (iv) placing the ants after two cell wall breaking treatments continuously in a carbon dioxide extractor for extraction, where the extraction pressure is 38-40 Mpa, the extraction temperature is 50-55 degrees C, the whole extraction process is divided into 4 stages from the first stage to the last stage, the flow of carbon dioxide increases sequentially, the specific process includes: in the first phase, the flow of carbon dioxide is 5-7 m3/hour, the duration is 30-40 minutes, in the second phase, the flow of carbon dioxide is 10-13 m3/hour , the duration is 1-2 hours, in the third stage, the flow of carbon dioxide is 18-20 m3/hour, the duration is 1-2 hours and in the fourth stage, the flow of carbon dioxide is 40-50 m3/h and the duration is 3-4 hours, (v) sending the extract fluid prepared in step (iv) to the separator for separation after the extraction is complete, where the first stage separation pressure of the separator is 20-25 MPa, the separation temperature is 50-55 degrees C, the separation time is 5-10 minutes, the secondary pressure of the separator is 10-15MPa, the separation temperature is 50-55 degrees C, the separation time is 5-10 minutes, the separator three-stage separation pressure is 5-8 MPa, the separation temperature is 50-55 degrees C and the separation time is 5-10 minutes, (vi) cooling the temperature in the separator to room temperature at a cooling rate of 0.5-1 degrees C/minute after the separation is completed, taking out the extracted product from the separator and (vii) filling the extracted product obtained in step (vi) into the container, sealing the container and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The species of the ant body is polyrhachis ant, red ants, insect body or brown sarin. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), during the first cell wall disruption process, the cell wall breaking pressure is 10 MPa, the wall breaking temperature is 25 degrees C, the time is 5 minutes, the pressure in the carbon dioxide extractor is reduced to 5 MPa by a pressure reducer and allowing to stand for 20 minutes. In the step (ii), the second cell wall disruption process is performed, during the second cell wall disruption process, the cell wall breaking pressure is 35 MPa, the wall breaking temperature is 45 degrees C and the time is 60 minutes. In the step (iii), the primary separation pressure of the separator is 20 MPa, the separation temperature is 50 degrees C, the separation time is 5 minutes, the secondary separation pressure of the separator is 10 Mpa, the separation temperature is 50 degrees C, the separation time is 5 minutes, the separator's three-stage separation pressure is 5 MPa, the separation temperature is 50 degrees C and the separation time is 5 minutes. In the step (iii), the temperature inside the separator is controlled to fall to room temperature at a rate of 0.5 degrees C/minute after the separation is completed.

TI Preparing hydrogen and food-grade dry ice for fuel cells from liquefied natural gas, involves entering the pressurized liquefied natural gas to the carbon dioxide production dry ice production section.
AU WANG X
   YAO H
   MU X
   SUI Y
   ZHOU C
   HOU J
   WANG S
   SONG P
   HOU H
   ZHANG Y
AE CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL CORP (CNOO-C)

GA 202112316H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen and food-grade dry ice involves entering the pressurized liquefied natural gas (LNG) to the carbon dioxide production dry ice production section, where it exchanges heat with the purified carbon dioxide, or exchanges heat with nitrogen or mixed refrigerant and raising the temperature. The heated natural gas enters the natural gas conversion section after being heated. The natural gas is enriched by conversion, conversion and pressure swing adsorption, and the enriched gas is a hydrogen-rich gas that meets the requirements of hydrogen for fuel cells. The purge gas outlet of the pressure swing adsorption section in the natural gas conversion section is a carbon dioxide-rich gas mixture, and purified carbon dioxide is obtained after the carbon dioxide-rich gas mixture passes through the carbon dioxide purification section.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrogen and food-grade dry ice for fuel cells from liquefied natural gas (LNG).
   ADVANTAGE - The method organically combines the dry ice process of LNG gasification, hydrogen preparation, carbon dioxide purification and preparation, and the energy is mutually utilized, and the obtained hydrogen can be conveniently transported and utilized, and has good economic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrogen and food-grade dry ice involves entering the pressurized liquefied natural gas (LNG) to the carbon dioxide production dry ice production section, where it exchanges heat with the purified carbon dioxide, or exchanges heat with nitrogen or mixed refrigerant and raising the temperature. The heated natural gas enters the natural gas conversion section after being heated. The natural gas is enriched by conversion, conversion and pressure swing adsorption, and the enriched gas is a hydrogen-rich gas that meets the requirements of hydrogen for fuel cells. The purge gas outlet of the pressure swing adsorption section in the natural gas conversion section is a carbon dioxide-rich gas mixture, and purified carbon dioxide is obtained after the carbon dioxide-rich gas mixture passes through the carbon dioxide purification section. The total sulfur content in the purified gas is less than 0.1 parts per million, the benzene content is less than 0.02 parts per million, the total hydrocarbon content is less than 50 parts per million, and the carbon dioxide concentration is greater than 99.99%. The purified carbon dioxide enters the carbon dioxide production dry ice production section, exchanges heat with the LNG for cooling, and prepares food-grade dry ice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The LNG exchanges heat with the purified carbon dioxide, and after the heat exchange, the carbon dioxide is cooled to (-80)-(-40) degrees C.

TI Liquefied gas regasification system of regasification vessel has e.g. vaporizer for vaporizing liquefied gas compressed to high pressure by high pressure pump, and carbon dioxide heater heats compressed carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide pump.
AU NAMBYUNGTAK
   KONGBYUNGHOON
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 202054759N
AB    NOVELTY - Liquefied gas regasification system of regasification vessel comprises a high pressure pump (310) compresses the liquefied gas to the regasification gas pressure required by the gas demand of the land. A vaporizer (320) is used for vaporizing liquefied gas compressed to a high pressure by the high pressure pump. A carbon dioxide cycle using carbon dioxide as a working fluid as a high temperature heat source for vaporizing liquefied gas in the vaporizer. A carbon dioxide pump (410) is compressing carbon dioxide discharged by being cooled by heat exchange in the vaporizer. A carbon dioxide heater (420) heats the compressed carbon dioxide by the carbon dioxide pump. A carbon dioxide turbine (430) is used for converting the rotational motion of the carbon dioxide heated in the carbon dioxide heater to electric power.
   USE - Used as liquefied gas regasification system of regasification vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The system applies carbon dioxide cycles, which additionally produces electric power in the process of regasifying liquefied gas, so that the capacity of the power generation engine can provided as small, which reduces fuel consumption and use less installation space, improves the regasification efficiency by using characteristics of high exhaust gas temperature with low efficiency, has lower efficiency than power generation engine, carbon dioxide cycle using high-temperature exhaust gas and low-temperature liquefied gas as high-temperature heat source and low-temperature heat source, respectively, since carbon dioxide having low critical point is used as cycle working fluid for supplying heat source for regasifying liquefied gas, compared to conventional working fluid i.e. steam, has excellent thermodynamic performance and the same power, reduces the size of the power generation device, and device, thus reduces the cost of power generation, is eco-friendly and safe because uses non-toxic, non-flammable, and non-corrosive carbon dioxide as the working fluid, excellent heat exchange performance since operates in the supercritical region of the working fluid, and reduces the consumption of fossil fuels for generating electricity, which reports the atmospheric environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of liquefied gas regasification system of regasification vessel (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Performing methanation method by using methane and            carbon dioxide involves contacting the combustion            exhaust gas with a carbon dioxide absorbent to absorb            carbon dioxide in the combustion exhaust gas, and            heating carbon dioxide absorbent.
AU OTSUKA H
AE OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
GA 201984663U
AB    NOVELTY - Performing methanation (100) method by using                methane and carbon dioxide contained in combustion                exhaust gas (101) of carbon-containing fuel as raw                materials to obtain methane by a methanation                reaction using a catalyst involves contacting the                combustion exhaust gas with a carbon dioxide                absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide in the                combustion exhaust gas. The carbon dioxide                absorbent is heated that has absorbed carbon                dioxide to taken out the first gas mainly composed                of carbon dioxide. The first hydrogen (202) is                added, which is a first amount of hydrogen, to the                first gas to form a second gas. The second gas is                passed through a desulfurizer (23) filled with a                desulfurizing agent and sulfur in the second gas.                The second amount of second hydrogen is added,                which is a second amount of hydrogen, to the third                gas that has undergone the step of removed the                sulfur compound, and converted it to methane by a                methanation reaction through a methanation                catalyst.
   USE - Method for performing methanation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform methanation by                providing high-purity carbon dioxide, and                purification cost becomes low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a methane                production facilities, which includes hydrogen and                carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas                of carbon-containing fuel as raw materials, and                obtains methane by a methanation reaction using a                catalyst, an absorption tower for contacting the                combustion exhaust gas with a carbon dioxide                absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide in the                combustion exhaust gas, a regeneration tower for                heating the carbon dioxide absorbent that has                absorbed carbon dioxide in the absorption tower and                taking out a first gas mainly composed of carbon                dioxide, a second gas comprising the first gas from                the regeneration tower and a first hydrogen that is                a first amount of hydrogen, a desulfurizer for                treating with a desulfurizing agent to remove                sulfur compounds in the second gas, and adding                second hydrogen, which is a second amount of                hydrogen, to the third gas that has passing through                the desulfurizer, and a methanation reactor for                converting to methane by a methanation reaction                through a methanation catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of                performing methanation.
   23Desulfurizer
   32, 34, 36Heat exchange
   100Methanation
   101Combustion exhaust gas

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The first amount is                  less than a total hydrogen amount necessary to                  complete a methanation reaction in which the                  first gas and hydrogen are reacted to generate                  methane. The second amount is equal to or greater                  than an amount obtained by subtracting the first                  amount from the total hydrogen amount. The vol.%                  of the first hydrogen with respect to the second                  gas is 0.1 vol.% or more and 5 vol.% or less. The                  methanation method involves using the reaction                  heat due to the methanation reaction, heating the                  carbon dioxide absorbent to take out the first                  gas. The first hydrogen and the second hydrogen                  are obtained by electrolyzing water using surplus                  power from a power generation facility. Preferred                  Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent is an                  aqueous solution of an amine compound. The                  desulfurizing agent includes one component metal                  or oxide composed of iron, nickel, cobalt, and                  copper and zinc oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110608022-A
TI Landfilling carbon dioxide to increase production            of coalbed methane reservoir by selecting coal seam,            installing ground injection system and carbon dioxide            injection pipe string, and pumping carbon dioxide to            underground gas layer.
AU ZHANG B
AE CHINA UNITED COALBED METHANE CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   SINOPEC OIL & GAS TECHNOLOGY SERVICE CO (SNPC-C)
GA 202000903N
AB    NOVELTY - Landfilling carbon dioxide to increase the                production of coalbed methane reservoir involves:                (S1) selecting coal seam parameters to satisfy a                Young's modulus of coal rock measured at 104 MPa of                0.6-4.39, a Poisson's ratio of 0.22-0.39, a coal                seam thickness of larger than 4 m, a coal seam                depth of less than 1200 m, a permeability of                greater than 0.1 mD and a coal seam fracture                pressure of greater than 16 MPa, injecting coalbed                methane (CBM) production wells with a distance of                less than 1000 m from adjacent wells as liquid                carbon dioxide; and (S2) installing a ground                injection system and carbon dioxide injection pipe                string, pumping the liquid carbon dioxide to                underground gas layer in an intermittent injection                mode and continuously operating until the target                amount of liquid carbon dioxide is injected,                performing intermittent injection by pumping for 8                hours and stopping for 16 hours, and controlling                the wellhead pressure to be lower than the coal                seam rupture pressure.
   USE - The method is useful landfilling carbon                dioxide to increase the production of coalbed                methane reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the purpose of effective                carbon dioxide landfilling by injecting carbon                dioxide to displace the coalbed methane and                increase the production of coalbed methane                wells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Landfilling carbon dioxide to increase the                production of coalbed methane reservoir involves:                (S1) selecting coal seam parameters to satisfy a                Young's modulus of coal rock measured at 104 MPa of                0.6-4.39, a Poisson's ratio of 0.22-0.39, a coal                seam thickness of larger than 4 m, a coal seam                depth of less than 1200 m, a permeability of                greater than 0.1 mD and a coal seam fracture                pressure of greater than 16 MPa, injecting coalbed                methane (CBM) production wells with a distance of                less than 1000 m from adjacent wells as liquid                carbon dioxide; (S2) installing a ground injection                system and carbon dioxide injection pipe string,                pumping the liquid carbon dioxide to underground                gas layer in an intermittent injection mode and                continuously operating until the target amount of                liquid carbon dioxide is injected, performing                intermittent injection by pumping for 8 hours and                stopping for 16 hours, where the pumping speed of                liquid carbon dioxide is 1-2 time/hour, and                controlling the wellhead pressure to be lower than                the coal seam rupture pressure; and (S3) When the                target amount of liquid carbon dioxide is                completely injected, expanding the well for 1                month.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide                  landfill method further involves: pre-film and                  anti-corrosion treatment steps, which                  specifically involve injecting a corrosion                  inhibitor aqueous solution compatible with the                  formation of water component into the oil pipe                  and the casing pipe; and reverse circulation                  well-killing process, which specifically involves                  taking 1 wt.% potassium chloride aqueous solution                  to serve as an active liquid with a discharge                  capacity of 400 l/minute, using a pump pressure                  of 2 MPa to perform reverse circulation killing,                  stopping the pump and observing for 2 hours to                  determine whether the aqueous solution has no                  overflow phenomenon. Preferred Device: In step                  (S2), the ground injection system includes a                  storage tank, a carbon dioxide booster pump, a                  carbon dioxide injection pump, a carbon dioxide                  gasifier and a diesel generator. The diesel                  generator is connected to the storage tank, the                  carbon dioxide booster pump, the carbon dioxide                  injection pump and the carbon dioxide gasifier,                  respectively. The carbon dioxide injection pipe                  string includes an oil pipe, a perforated sieve                  pipe, a downhole pressure gauge holder with a                  stored electronic pressure gauge, and a downhole                  direct-reading electronic pressure gauge                  connected in order from top to bottom. The oil                  pipe is connected to the liquid outlet of the                  carbon dioxide gasifier through a pipeline. The                  length of the oil pipe satisfies the position,                  where the direct-reading electronic pressure                  gauge of the downhole is located above the gas                  layer and adjacent to the gas layer. The                  perforated sieve pipe is a sieve pipe having the                  same diameter as the oil pipe and a length of 2                  m, and 50 sieve holes are evenly distributed on                  the sieve pipe, where the pore diameter of each                  sieve hole is 8 mm.

The fluid containing coffee tincture flows into the coffee tincture collecting tank for dehydrating and concentrating according to the subsequent process requirement, and the coffee fine particles or coffee powder is discharged from the material outlet of the coffee tincture extraction tank.

TI Producing carbamic acid ester by absorbing carbon dioxide using carbon dioxide absorber containing primary amine or secondary amine and tertiary amino alcohol, and reacting with olefin compound in presence of acid catalyst.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202250867Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a carbamic acid ester, involves absorbing carbon dioxide using an carbon dioxide absorber containing a primary amine and/or a secondary amine and a tertiary amino alcohol, and reacting with an olefin compound in the presence of an acid catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a carbamic acid ester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces carbamic acid ester from carbon dioxide with low energy, reduces the energy for separating and recovering carbon dioxide and purifying, and converts carbon dioxide into useful substance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid catalysts are metal halides comprising aluminum chloride, zinc chloride and boron fluoride, mineral acids comprising sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid and heteropolyacids, methanesulfonic acid, trifluoromethanesulfonic acid and p-toluenesulfonic acid. The organic acids comprise trifluoroacetic acid, ion exchange resins, zeolites, and solid acids such as silica-alumina.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The primary amine and/or secondary amine comprises an alkylamine, an alkyleneamine, or an ethanolamine. The tertiary amino alcohol comprises triethanolamine, N-alkyldiethanolamine, and/or N, N-dialkylethanolamine. The olefin compound comprises ethylene, propylene, isobutene, cyclohexene, ethylhexene and/or styrene.

TI Electrolytic reduction useful for electrochemical            reduction carbon dioxide electrolytic cell system,            comprises according to certain frequency, alternately            applying different potentials to working            electrodes.
AU ZHANG H
   YAO P
   LI X
   QIU Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2022842788
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolytic reduction for electrochemical                reduction carbon dioxide electrolytic cell system                comprises according to a certain frequency,                alternately applying different potentials to the                working electrodes, where the alternating frequency                is 10-60 second/cycle, and the potential is 0.2-1.3                V lower than the equilibrium potential.
   USE - The method of electrolytic reduction useful                for electrochemical reduction carbon dioxide                electrolytic cell system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the characteristics of                improving the selectivity of carbon dioxide                product, the operation is simple and convenient,                alternately applying a cathode electrolytic                potential, causing different concentrations of                carbon dioxide near the working electrode. At the                high potential, the ERC reaction rate is slow,                carbon dioxide consumption is low, the carbon                dioxide concentration near the copper electrode is                high, and the pH is low. At this time, the high                concentration of hydrogen will accelerating the                occurrence of the HER side reaction. When working                electrode potential is quickly converted into low                potential, carbon dioxide reduction with high                concentration will promote more intermediate                product carbon dioxide generation, the coverage of                the copper electrode surface carbon dioxide is                greatly improved.

PN WO2019021141-A1
TI Producing methanol, comprises e.g. dry-reforming natural gas to produce a first syngas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide, and flowing at least some of first syngas stream to first methanol synthesis reactor.
AU DHUMAL S S
   CHOWDHURY D R
   NAIR V S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201912273E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) methanol, comprises: e.g. dry-reforming natural gas to produce a first syngas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide; flowing at least some of the first syngas stream to a first methanol synthesis reactor; separating at least some of the first syngas stream in a hydrogen/carbon monoxide separation unit to form a first hydrogen stream and a first carbon monoxide stream; flowing the first hydrogen stream to the first methanol synthesis reactor; reacting the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide of the first syngas stream with hydrogen of the first hydrogen stream and the hydrogen of the first syngas stream in the first methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions sufficient to form methanol; separating an effluent from the first methanol synthesis reactor to form a first methanol stream and a first byproduct stream; and splitting at least some of the first byproduct stream to form a first purge gas stream and a first recycle stream.
   USE - The methods are useful for producing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods utilize the carbon monoxide separated from the syngas stream produced by dry reforming combined with the purge gas from methanol synthesis to produce additional methanol, thus increasing utilization rate of the syngas and maximizing the methanol synthesis efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing (M1) methanol, comprises: dry-reforming natural gas to produce a first syngas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide; flowing at least some of the first syngas stream to a first methanol synthesis reactor; separating at least some of the first syngas stream in a hydrogen/carbon monoxide separation unit to form a first hydrogen stream and a first carbon monoxide stream; flowing the first hydrogen stream to the first methanol synthesis reactor; reacting the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide of the first syngas stream with hydrogen of the first hydrogen stream and the hydrogen of the first syngas stream in the first methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions sufficient to form methanol; separating an effluent from the first methanol synthesis reactor to form a first methanol stream and a first byproduct stream; splitting at least some of the first byproduct stream to form a first purge gas stream and a first recycle stream; steam reforming natural gas to produce a second syngas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; flowing the second syngas stream through a second methanol synthesis reactor; reacting the hydrogen, the carbon monoxide, and the carbon dioxide in the second syngas stream in the second methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions sufficient to form methanol; separating an effluent from the second methanol synthesis reactor to form a second methanol stream and a second byproduct stream; splitting at least some of the second byproduct stream to form a second purge gas stream and a second recycle stream; flowing a combined stream of the first purge gas stream and the second purge gas stream through a third methanol synthesis reactor, where the combined stream comprises primarily hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon monoxide; flowing the first carbon monoxide stream to the third methanol synthesis reactor; and reacting the hydrogen in the third methanol synthesis reactor with carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the third methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions sufficient to form methanol and a third purge gas stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (M2) methanol, comprising dry-reforming natural gas to produce a first syngas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide, flowing at least some of the first syngas stream to a first methanol synthesis reactor, separating at least some of the first syngas stream via a hydrogen/carbon monoxide separation unit to form a first hydrogen stream and a first carbon monoxide stream, flowing the first hydrogen stream to the first methanol synthesis reactor, reacting the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide of the first syngas stream with the hydrogen of the first hydrogen stream and the hydrogen of the first syngas stream in the first methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions sufficient to form methanol, separating an effluent from the first methanol synthesis reactor to form a first methanol stream and a first byproduct stream, splitting at least some of the first byproduct stream to form a first purge gas stream and a first recycle stream, steam reforming natural gas to produce a second syngas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, flowing the second syngas stream through a second methanol synthesis reactor, injecting carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to the second syngas stream to ensure a stoichiometric number of hydrogen to carbon monoxide flowing into the second methanol synthesis of 2-2.1, reacting the hydrogen, the carbon monoxide and the carbon dioxide in the second syngas stream in the second methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions to form methanol, separating an effluent from the second methanol synthesis reactor to form a second methanol stream and a second byproduct stream, splitting at least some of the second byproduct stream to form a second purge gas stream and a second recycle stream, flowing at least some of a combined stream of the first purge gas stream and the second purge gas stream through a first hydrogen separation unit to form a second hydrogen stream and a first residue gas stream, flowing the second hydrogen stream to the first syngas stream, flowing at least some of the combined stream of the first purge gas stream and the second purge gas stream through a third methanol synthesis reactor, where the combined stream comprises primarily hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and/or carbon monoxide, flowing the first carbon monoxide stream to the third methanol synthesis reactor, injecting carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide to the third methanol synthesis reactor, reacting the hydrogen in the third methanol synthesis reactor with carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the third methanol synthesis reactor under reaction conditions sufficient to form methanol and a third purge gas stream, flowing the third purge gas stream to a second hydrogen separation unit, separating the third purge gas stream in the second hydrogen separation unit to form a third hydrogen stream and a second residue stream, and flowing the third hydrogen stream to the first syngas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method (M1): the hydrogen, the carbon monoxide and the carbon dioxide flowing in each of the first methanol synthesis reactor, the second methanol synthesis reactor and the third methanol synthesis reactor has a stoichiometric number of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of greater than or equal to 2; the reaction conditions in each of the methanol synthesis reactors include a reaction temperature of 200-300 degrees C; the reaction conditions in each of the methanol synthesis reactors include a reaction pressure of 50-100 bar; the reacting in each of the methanol synthesis reactors is performed in the presence of a catalyst of copper and zinc supported on alumina and/or chromium and manganese oxide; and the reaction conditions in each of the methanol synthesis reactors comprise a gas hourly space velocity of 8000-10000 per hour. The method (M1) further comprises: injecting carbon dioxide into the second syngas stream; injecting carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide to the third methanol synthesis reactor to maintain the stoichiometric number of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of no less than 2; flowing at least some of the combined stream of the first purge gas stream and the second purge gas stream to a first hydrogen separation unit, separating the combined stream to form a second hydrogen stream and a first residue gas stream via the first hydrogen separation unit and flowing the second hydrogen stream to the first syngas stream; flowing the third purge gas stream to a second hydrogen separation unit, separating the third purge gas stream to form a third hydrogen stream and a second residue gas stream, and flowing the third hydrogen stream to the first syngas stream; recycling the first recycle stream to the first methanol synthesis reactor, where the first recycle stream comprises unreacted hydrogen, unreacted carbon monoxide and unreacted carbon dioxide; and recycling the second recycle stream to the second methanol synthesis reactor, where the second recycle stream comprises unreacted hydrogen, unreacted carbon monoxide and unreacted carbon dioxide. In the method (M2), the first syngas stream flowing in the first methanol synthesis reactor and the second syngas stream flowing in the second methanol synthesis reactor each has a stoichiometric number of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of greater than or equal to 2. Preferred Components: The first, second and third purge gas streams comprise carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and/or methane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Production of ethylene in integrated            configuration, involves subjecting stream comprising            saturated hydrocarbons to steam cracking conditions in            steam cracker unit, resulting in stream containing            water, and ethylene, and removing water from stream by            condensation in water condensation unit.
AU VAN ROSSUM G
   SCHOONEBEEK R J
   ESPOSITO CASSIBBA I D
   BOS A N R
   ESPOSITO C I D
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2022216807
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ethylene in integrated                configuration involves subjecting a stream                comprising saturated hydrocarbons to steam cracking                conditions in the steam cracker unit, resulting in                a stream comprising water, unconverted ethane,                ethylene, acetylene, hydrogen, methane, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide and 3C hydrocarbon,                feeding at least a portion of the stream coming                from the steam cracker unit to the water                condensation unit of the steam cracker                configuration, and removing water and a portion of                the 3C hydrocarbon from the stream by condensation                in the water condensation unit, resulting in a                stream, subjecting a stream comprising ethane and                oxygen to oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH)                conditions in the ODH unit, resulting in the                stream, feeding at least a portion of the stream                coming from the ODH unit to the water condensation                unit of the ODH configuration, and removing water                from the stream by condensation in the water                condensation unit.
   USE - Production of ethylene in integrated                configuration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of ethylene in                integrated configuration by using downstream                section of a steam cracker configuration having                high capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of ethylene in integrated                configuration involves subjecting a stream                comprising saturated hydrocarbons to steam cracking                conditions in the steam cracker unit, resulting in                a stream comprising water, unconverted ethane,                ethylene, acetylene, hydrogen, methane, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide and 3C hydrocarbon,                feeding at least a portion of the stream coming                from the steam cracker unit to the water                condensation unit of the steam cracker                configuration, and removing water and a portion of                the 3C hydrocarbon from the stream by condensation                in the water condensation unit, resulting in a                stream, subjecting a stream comprising ethane and                oxygen to oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH)                conditions in the ODH unit, resulting in the                stream, feeding at least a portion of the stream                coming from the ODH unit to the water condensation                unit of the ODH configuration, and removing water                from the stream by condensation in the water                condensation unit, resulting in the stream, feeding                an effluent coming from the ODH configuration,                which effluent comprises unconverted ethane and                ethylene, to the steam cracker configuration at a                position which is downstream of the steam cracker                unit, preferably downstream of the water                condensation unit of the steam cracker                configuration, removing unconverted oxygen, carbon                monoxide and acetylene from at least a portion of                the stream coming from the ODH unit by oxidation of                carbon monoxide and acetylene into carbon dioxide                in an oxidation unit which is located at a position                (a) which is downstream of the ODH unit, preferably                downstream of the water condensation unit of the                ODH configuration, and (b) which is downstream of                the steam cracker unit, preferably downstream of                the water condensation unit of the steam cracker                configuration, and upstream of the carbon dioxide                removal unit of the steam cracker configuration,                feeding stream to the carbon dioxide removal unit                of the steam cracker configuration, and removing                carbon dioxide from the stream in the carbon                dioxide removal unit of the steam cracker                configuration, resulting in a stream, and                recovering unconverted ethane and ethylene from at                least a portion of the stream coming from the                carbon dioxide removal unit of the steam cracker                configuration and optionally from at least a                portion of the effluent coming from the ODH                configuration. The integrated configuration                comprises steam cracker configuration and an                oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) configuration. The                steam cracker configuration comprises a steam                cracker unit, a water condensation unit and a                carbon dioxide removal unit. The ODH configuration                comprises an ODH unit and a water condensation                unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The saturated                  hydrocarbons are fed to the steam cracker unit                  comprising one or more of ethane, propane,                  butane, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), naphtha,                  hydrowax and recycled waste plastics oil,                  preferably ethane and/or naphtha. The ethane is                  fed to the ODH unit which originates from one or                  more sources chosen from a source of fresh ethane                  that is used to feed ethane to the steam cracker                  unit, another source of fresh ethane, other than                  first source mentioned under, and unconverted                  ethane. The steam cracker configuration                  additionally comprises a separation unit (u1), a                  separation unit (u2), and an acetylene removal                  unit. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves feeding at least a portion of the stream                  coming from the carbon dioxide removal unit of                  the steam cracker configuration and optionally at                  least a portion of the effluent coming from the                  ODH configuration, to the separation unit (u1),                  and separating the stream or streams in the                  separation unit (u1) into a stream comprising                  hydrogen, methane and optionally carbon monoxide                  and a stream comprising 3C hydrocarbons, feeding                  unconverted ethane and ethylene from the stream                  comprising unconverted ethane, ethylene,                  optionally acetylene, hydrogen, methane,                  optionally carbon monoxide and 3C hydrocarbons to                  the separation unit (u2), and separating the                  stream in the separation unit (u2) into the                  stream comprising ethylene and the unconverted                  ethane, and removing the acetylene by using the                  acetylene removal unit removed from the stream                  comprising acetylene, ethylene and optionally                  unconverted ethane.

TI Preparation of sulbactam involves adding concentrated sulbactam sodium as raw material to co-solvent, and passing carbon dioxide.
AU TIAN H
   JIA Q
   ZHANG S
   SHI C
   LI Q
   LIU S
   REN F
   JIA J
AE NCPC HEBEI HUAMIN PHARM CO LTD (NCPC-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3577H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of sulbactam involves adding concentrated sulbactam sodium as raw material to co-solvent, and passing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Preparation of sulbactam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, energy saving, environmentally-friendly, and is suitable for industrial production. The prepared sulbactam has less impurities, low color grade, excellent fluidity and stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent is activated carbon or white clay. Preferred Composition: The added amount of adsorbent is 1-10 %mass based mass of sulbactam sodium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The concentrated sulbactam sodium is obtained by dissolving sulbactam sodium, separating, decolorizing and concentrating sulbactam sodium. The preparation of the sulbactam preferably involves dissolving sulbactam sodium in water, adding dissolved sulbactam sodium to organic solvent (i), adjusting pH to 0.5-2.5 using hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or phosphoric acid, stirring, leaving still, separating into aqueous phase (a) and solvent phase (b), adding an organic solvent (ii) to aqueous phase (a), stirring, leaving still, separating aqueous phase (a1) and solvent phase (b1), combining solvent phase (b) and solvent phase (b1), adding adsorbent, stirring for 0.5-1 hour, collecting the filtrate by filtration, washing the filtrate with an organic solvent, collecting the washing liquid, combining filtrate and washing liquid, concentrating to obtain concentrated liquid, adding co-solvent to concentrated liquid in drops for 0-10 minutes, crystallizing to 30-50 degrees C and -0.09 to -0.07 MPa, passing 5-10 L/minute carbon dioxide to cool crystal at -15 degrees C to 5 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, filtering crystal, washing, and drying at 30-50 degrees C and -0.09 MPa to -0.07 MPa. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of sulbactam sodium to water is 1:2-10. The added amount of organic solvent (i) is 2-12 times based on the mass of the sulbactam sodium. The added amount of organic solvent (ii) is 3-5 times based on the mass of the sulbactam sodium. The concentrated liquid is 1.0-2.5 times based mass of the sulbactam sodium. The added amount of cosolvent is 0.5-1 times the mass of the sulbactam sodium. The 50% co-solvent is added for 10-18 minutes. The 20% co-solvent is added for 18-24 minutes. Preferred Components: The organic solvent (i) and (ii) is methyl acetate, ethyl acetate or butyl acetate. The cosolvent comprises methyl isobutyl ketone, methyl ethyl ketone and/or cyclohexanone. Preferred Properties: The sulbactam sodium has quality index of more than 1.0%, largest single impurity of more than 0.5% or color grade of 3.

MC B02-C04; B14-Y
IP C07D-499/04; C07D-499/18; C07D-499/86

PN CN112852491-A; CN112852491-B
TI Producing coal-based synthetic oil and co-producing liquefied natural gas using double-head gasification comprises e.g. subjecting raw materials and oxygen to coal gasification treatment, purifying and performing synthesis reaction.
AU CHEN L
   DONG G
   HAO X
   LIU L
   LI Y
   YANG Y
   CAO L
AE ZHONGKE SYNTHETIC OIL TECH CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202162056B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing coal-based synthetic oil and co-producing liquefied natural gas using double-head gasification comprises e.g. (a) subjecting the raw materials and oxygen to coal water slurry gasification treatment, (b) purifying the crude synthesis gas, (c) subjecting the raw material block coal, oxygen, medium-pressure steam and part of the carbon dioxide and coal gasified for crushing and coal gasification, (d) processing the crushed coal purified gas and the remaining part of the carbon dioxide, (e) performing a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction on the purified gas and the purified synthesis gas, (f) sending the light oil, heavy oil, qualified wax and synthetic water as well as the naphtha and part of the methanol to the oil processing unit, (g) processing the naphtha and liquefied petroleum gas, (h) processing the decarburization tail gas, the processed dry gas and the reformed dry gas back, and (i) returning the hydrogen to the gasoline plant for reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for producing coal-based synthetic oil and co-producing liquefied natural gas using double-head gasification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses by-product coke or biochemical sludge to replace part of the raw material pulverized coal, and carbon dioxide to replace part of water vapor in the crushed coal gasification, reduces the consumption of raw materials and the output of gasification wastewater, and rationally treat the heavy oil, qualified wax and tail gas produced in the middle, increases the output of oil products while the amount of raw materials is reduced, and reduces the generation of pollutants, which brings higher economic and environmental benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing coal-based synthetic oil and co-producing liquefied natural gas using double-head gasification comprises (a) subjecting the raw materials and oxygen to coal water slurry gasification treatment to obtain crude synthesis gas, acid gas, wastewater, waste residue and filter cake, where the raw materials include pulverized coal and optionally coke, dust-containing tar, green coke and biochemical sludge, (b) purifying the crude synthesis gas to obtain purified synthesis gas, process condensate, carbon dioxide, acid gas and exhaust gas, (c) subjecting the raw material block coal, oxygen, medium-pressure steam and part of the carbon dioxide and coal gasified for crushing and coal gasification to obtain crushed coal purified gas, naphtha, liquefied petroleum gas, diesel, acid gas, expansion gas, polyphenols, ammonia, waste residue and wastewater, (d) sending the crushed coal purified gas and the remaining part of the carbon dioxide into the natural gas and methanol plant for processing to obtain purified gas, liquefied natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, methanol, hydrogen, naphtha and tail gas, (e) performing a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction on the purified gas and the purified synthesis gas to obtain light oil, heavy oil, qualified wax, synthetic water and decarbonized tail gas, (f) sending the light oil, heavy oil, qualified wax and synthetic water as well as the naphtha and part of the methanol to the oil processing unit for processing to obtain blended diesel, gasoline, heavy diesel, naphtha, liquefied petroleum gas, liquid paraffin, processing dry gas, mixed alcohol and synthetic wastewater, (g) sending the naphtha and liquefied petroleum gas in step (f), the naphtha and liquefied petroleum gas in step (c), and the liquefied petroleum gas in step (d) and the remaining part of methanol into the gasoline plant for processing to obtain gasoline, polypropylene, reformed dry gas and hydrogen, (h) sending the decarburization tail gas, the processed dry gas and the reformed dry gas back into the natural gas and methanol plant described in step (d) for processing, and (i) returning the hydrogen to the gasoline plant in step (g), the oil processing plant in step (f), and the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction in step (e) for reaction. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system, comprising a coal water slurry gasification device and a crushed coal gasification device arranged in parallel. A purification device, which is connected to the coal-water slurry gasification device in a fluid communication manner. An oil synthesis device, the oil synthesis device is connected to the purification device in a fluid communication manner. An oil product processing device, which is connected to the oil product synthesis device and the natural gas and methanol device in a fluid communication manner. A gasoline device, which is connected to the oil processing device, the coal gasification device, and the natural gas and methanol device in a fluid communication manner. A natural gas and methanol plant, the natural gas and methanol plant is connected to the coal gasification unit, the purification unit, and the oil synthesis unit in a fluid communication manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further includes a sulfur recovery and sulfuric acid device, and the sulfur recovery and sulfuric acid device is connected to the coal water slurry gasification device, the purification device, and the crushed coal gasification device in a fluid communication manner.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (e), the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is performed using Fe-based catalysts and/or cobalt-based catalysts, preferably, the Fe-based catalyst includes an active ingredient and an electronic assistant, the active ingredient is alpha -ferric oxide and/or ferrous oxide, and the electronic assistant is at least one of alkali metals, transition metals and/or rare earth metals. The Fe-based catalyst further includes structural assistant, and the structural assistant is at least one of silicon oxide, aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide, titanium oxide and activated carbon. The cobalt-based catalyst is active component cobalt, a carrier and an auxiliary agent, and the carrier is at least one of silicon oxide, gamma -aluminum oxide, activated carbon and titanium oxide. The auxiliary agent is at least one of nickel, zirconium, magnesium, manganese, ruthenium, platinum, rhenium, cerium, lanthanum and thorium. Preferred Method: The method further includes returning the process condensate in step (b) to step (a) to participate in the gasification of the coal water slurry, preferably, the method further comprises the following steps: the oxygen, the acid gas in step (a) and step (b), and the acid gas and expansion gas in step (c) are processed by sulfur recovery and sulfuric acid plant to obtain sulfur products and concentrated sulfuric acid and returning the heavy diesel oil in step (f) to step (e) to participate in the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction. In the step (a), the coal water slurry gasification treatment is performed under the following conditions: the temperature is 1250-1400 degrees C, and the pressure is 5-8 MpaG. In the step (c), the temperature of the crushed coal gasification is 1000-1300 degrees C, and the pressure is 2-5 MpaG, preferably, the molar percentage of methane in the purified gas from the crushed coal is 5-50% and the mass ratio of the carbon dioxide to the medium pressure steam is less than 1:1, preferably (3-4):(7-6). In the step (d), the following processing is performed in the natural gas and methanol plant: cryogenic separation is carried out at a pressure of 1-5 MpaG and a temperature of -200 to -150 degrees C and the methanol synthesis is carried out at a pressure of 2.5-10 MpaG and a temperature of 200-400 degrees C. In the step (e), the temperature of the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is 180-380 degrees C, and the pressure is 0.5-6 MpaG and the temperature of the decarburization tail gas is 20-100 degrees C and the pressure is 1.5-5 MpaG. In the step (f), the following processing is performed in the oil processing device: hydrorefining is carried out under the conditions of a temperature of 220-350 degrees C and a pressure of 7-8.5 MpaG, under the conditions of temperature of 300-400 degrees C and pressure of 7.1-8.5 MPG, the hydro-upgrading is carried out and the catalytic cracking is carried out under the conditions of a temperature of 300-750 degrees C and a pressure of 0.1-0.5 MpaG. In the step (g), the following treatment is carried out in the gasoline plant: the isomerization reaction is carried out at 2-6 MpaG, 80-150 degrees C, and the polymerization reaction is carried out at 2-5.5 MpaG, 50-90 degrees C.
