PT P
PN CN111440645-A; CN111440645-B
TI Purifying biogas based on vacuum freezing process comprises e.g. filtering untreated crude biogas to remove dust and solid impurities in the crude biogas to obtain Pretreatment of biogas, and pumping the pretreated biogas into a compressor.
AU NIU X
   XIAO H
   GUO H
   TANG Z
   LIU M
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG D
   LI X
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOG (UNGP-C)
   UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 202072454R
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying biogas based on vacuum freezing process comprises e.g. filtering untreated crude biogas to remove dust and solid impurities in the crude biogas to obtain Pretreatment of biogas, pumping the pretreated biogas into a compressor, freeze-drying to remove the moisture in the biogas, then pumping the dried biogas into a heat exchanger, condensing and separating the liquid H2S and NH3 from the biogas through a gas-liquid separator, recycling the precipitated liquid H2S and NH3, pumping the purified biogas is into the compressor, drying the biogas, then pumping the dried biogas into a heat exchanger, reducing the temperature, and separating the liquid carbon dioxide from the biogas through a gas-liquid separator to obtain the final purified biogas, and recycling the precipitated liquid carbon dioxide and storing the biogas.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying biogas based on vacuum freezing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can send the detection results to the remote monitoring module to facilitate timely detection of leaks and deal with them as soon as possible to avoid accidents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying biogas based on vacuum freezing process comprises (i) filtering untreated crude biogas to remove dust and solid impurities in the crude biogas to obtain pretreatment of biogas; (ii) pumping the pretreated biogas obtained in step (i) into a compressor, pressurizing to 1013 bar, and the pressurized biogas is pumped into a vacuum freeze dryer, freeze-drying at a freezing temperature of 15-25 minutes under the condition of -8 to 3 degrees C to remove the moisture in the biogas, then pumping the dried biogas into a heat exchanger, reduce the temperature to -20 to -15 degrees C, and incubating for 25-30 minutes, where the part of the hydrogen sulfide gas and ammonia in the biogas are condensed and separated, and condensing and separating the liquid H2S and NH3 from the biogas through a gas-liquid separator, recycling the precipitated liquid H2S and NH3; (iii) pumping the purified biogas is into the compressor, pressurizing to 20-25 bar, and the pressurized biogas is pumped into the vacuum freeze dryer at a freezing temperature of -20 to -15 degrees C under the conditions, freeze-drying for 15-25 minutes, drying the biogas again, then pumping the dried biogas into a heat exchanger, reducing the temperature to -55 to -55 degrees C, and keeping for 35-45 minutes to condense the carbon dioxide in the biogas, and separating the liquid carbon dioxide from the biogas through a gas-liquid separator to obtain the final purified biogas, and recycling the precipitated liquid carbon dioxide; and (iv) storing the biogas obtained in step (iii), and testing the purity of the final biogas with a testing instrument, if the purity of the biogas after treatment is qualified, it will be used as a backup, if it is not qualified, the above-mentioned biogas will be pumped to in the compressor in step (ii), reprocessing until the biogas purity is qualified. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for purifying biogas based on vacuum freezing process, comprising a temporary storage tank (1) for storing the untreated crude biogas and internally provided with a filter element (10), a compressor (2) for pressurizing the marsh gas after pre-treatment of the temporary storage tank, a vacuum freeze drier (3) for freezing and drying the pressurized marsh gas and removing water, a heat exchanger (4) for heat exchanging and cooling the gas processed by the vacuum freeze drier, gas-liquid separator is connected with the heat exchanger, a buffer detection box connected with the gas-liquid separator and the compressor, an intelligent control device, and a power supply electrically connected with each electric element between the temporary storage box and the compressor between the compressor and the vacuum freeze drier between the vacuum freeze drier and the heat exchanger between the heat exchanger and the gas-liquid separator, one-way electromagnetic valve is arranged between the gas-liquid separator and the buffer detection box and between the buffer detection box and the compressor, the buffer detection box is provided with a hydrogen sulfide concentration detector for detecting the concentration of H2S in the marsh gas, a dissolved oxygen tester for detecting the dissolved oxygen content in the marsh gas, and a carbon dioxide detector for detecting the CO2 in the marsh gas, the intelligent control device comprises a controller for controlling the normal operation of each electric element, an electronic pressure gauge for detecting the pressure of the biogas, a thermometer for detecting the temperature of the biogas, and a humidity detection sensor for detecting the liquid in the biogas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of device.
   Temporary storage tank (1)
   Compressor (2)
   Vacuum freeze drier (3)
   Heat exchanger (4)
   Filter element (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), when the purity of the biogas is detected, the H2S in the biogas is detected by a hydrogen sulfide concentration detector concentration, the dissolved oxygen content in the biogas is detected by a dissolved oxygen analyzer, and the CO2 in the biogas is detected by a carbon dioxide detector. When the dissolved oxygen content is 0.05-0.10 mg/l, the H2S concentration is less than 10mg/m3, and the CO2 concentration is 8 g/m3 can be discharged, otherwise, re-pump to the compressor in step (ii) and re-process until the biogas purity is qualified.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooling water pipeline whose                outlet is connected with a boiler water supply                pipeline in a power plant. A top unit of a                circulating fluidized bed (14) is provided with a                flue gas outlet. A first heat exchanger is provided                with a gas outlet. The system reduces energy consumption                under improving condition of carbon capture                efficiency.

AU LI K
   DONG S
   ZHENG X
   GUO P
   LIU B
   KONG L
   HE C
   FANG L
   LI N
   ZHANG S
   HAN Y
   ZHANG L
   WEN H
   XU S
   GAO W
   HUANG B
AE BEIJING CHINA COAL MINE ENG CO LTD (CHLY-C)
GA 2022564313
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a tunnel whose axis is arranged with a central cracking hole (1). A hollow hole (2) is formed around the center cracking hole. A first ring of a first blast hole (3) is arranged on an outer ring of the hollow hole, where the first ring hole is provided with a first groove cracking hole and a second ring hole (4). A second ring of a second blast hole is provided on the outer ring. The first ring holes are provided with first groove cracking holes (31) and a second groove cracking holes (41). The hollow hole is opened at the middle point of the central connecting line of two adjacent first slotting cracking holes.
   USE - Cutting structure for a carbon dioxide induced cracking device for use in a home.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure provides a stone tunnel carbon dioxide cracking structure and blasting method with reasonable blast hole arrangement, thus improving blasting effect of hard rock tunnel. The structure allows the carbon dioxide cracker in the central cracking hole to be detonated at the same time, under the action of multi-directional co-blasting inside and outside, so that the rock is fully squeezed and crushed, and the rock crushing effect is better than that with the central hole simply being an empty hole. The triangular arrangement of the cracking hole makes the rock more easily damaged by shearing, when the blasting stress wave reaches the free surface, so as to. form a groove cavity of the expected volume, thus creating a new free surface for the blasting of the lower rock, and hence greatly reducing the blasting resistance of a lower rock to facilitate the crushing and throwing of the rock
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide induced cracking blasting cutting structure usage method.

PN CN111175818-A; CN111175818-B
TI Model training method for judging position of            carbon dioxide gas drive front edge, involves inputting            vertical seismic profile data and full waveform            inversion result into preset neural network model for            training.
AU LI D
   PENG S
   GUO Y
   LU Y
   CUI X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 202045279N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S101) reservoir                longitudinal and shear wave velocity and density                parameters. The reservoir longitudinal and                transverse wave velocity and density parameters are                input (S102) by pre-built initial geological                structure model and vertical seismic profile data                to perform full waveform inversion. The initial                geological formation model is updated in real time,                and the obtained longitudinal wave velocity, the                transverse wave speed and the density parameter are                recorded as full waveform inversion results. The                vertical seismic profile data and the full waveform                inversion result is input (S103) into a preset                neural network model for training. The training of                the model is stopped (S104) to obtain the model of                the carbon dioxide gas-driven leading edge position                determination when the output result in the preset                neural network model meets a preset expected                threshold.
   USE - Model training method for judging position of                carbon dioxide gas drive front edge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method makes full use of ground seismic                and vertical seismic profile (VSP) data, which                ensures a large observation range of ground seismic                data without reducing the resolution, and reduces                the amount of calculation of full waveform                inversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a model training device for judging                position of carbon dioxide gas drive front edge;                and
   (2) an electronic device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating of                the model training method for judging the position                of carbon dioxide gas drive front edge. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining the reservoir longitudinal                and shear wave velocity and density parameters                (S101)
   Step for inputting the reservoir longitudinal                and transverse wave velocity and density parameters                by pre-built initial geological structure model and                vertical seismic profile data (S102)
   Step for inputting the vertical seismic                profile data and the full waveform inversion result                into a preset neural network model for training                (S103)
   Step for stopping the training of the model                to obtain the model of the carbon dioxide                gas-driven leading edge position determination                (S104)

PN JP2018162184-A
TI Gas production system has separation device that            is capable of taking out water from product gas, where            hydrogen or carbon dioxide selectively passed through            separation membrane is taken out as product gas.
AU WATANABE S
   TANAKA Y
AE MITSUBISHI JUKOGYO KK (MITO-C)
GA 2018805859
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first separation membrane                (16) that selectively passes hydrogen or carbon                dioxide from the product gas to a catalyst layer                (15) filled with a catalyst that generates a                product gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide                from a raw material gas containing carbon monoxide                and water. A catalyst unit (13) is provided in the                vicinity of first separation membrane. A cooling                unit (18) is provided for cooling the catalyst                layer with a coolant. A separation device (20)                having a second separation membrane is set                downstream of a gas production device (10), and is                capable of taking out water from the product gas,                where the hydrogen or carbon dioxide selectively                passed through the first separation membrane is                taken out as the product gas. The water taken out                by the second separation membrane is supplied to                the source gas.
   USE - Gas production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The purity of the product gas such as hydrogen                and carbon dioxide is increased, and the product                gas is efficiently produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system has first separation membrane that                is set in a storage pipe. The gap between the                storage tube and the first separation membrane is                filled with the catalyst to form the catalyst                layer. The first separation membrane is provided                for selectively allowing hydrogen to pass through.                The second separation membrane and the separation                device are set to selectively pass water in the                product gas on the primary side of the second                separation membrane to the secondary side of the                second separation membrane. The hydrogen that is                passed through the first separation membrane as                product gas is taken out, and the carbon dioxide on                the primary side of the second separation membrane                as product gas is taken out. The water on the                secondary side of the second separation membrane is                supplied to the source gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a gas production method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view                demonstrating the structure and principle of                operation of the gas production system and gas                production process. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)

   Catalyst unit (13)
   Catalyst layer (15)

   Cooling unit (18)
   Separation device (20)

   ZHU Y
   ZHU W
AE JIANGSU RENGUAN MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B47775
AB    NOVELTY - The filter has an air pipe assembly provided                with an air inlet pipe, a smoke exhaust pipe and a                pressure measuring pipe. The air pipe is provided                with a heating component for heating carbon dioxide                flowing through the air pipe. A temperature sensor                detects the temperature of the carbon dioxide flow                through air pipe and the air outlet of a smoke                cavity through a second air in-let interface. An                airflow inlet of the air intake pipe is connected                with an airflow outlet of an air intake cavity                through the second air intake interface. A heating                component is electrically connected to an energy                transmission module. The temperature sensor is                connected to a signal transmission module through                the signal transmission terminal. An end of the                pressure measuring tube communicates an air                pressure detection port of a puncture outfit                through a third air pressure measuring                interface.
   USE - Filter between pneumoperitoneon machine and                puncture outfit used for minimally invasive                laparoscopic surgery. Can also be used in                minimally-invasive surgery of internal organs of                patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The filter reduces the temperature difference                of the carbon dioxide entering the abdominal cavity                and the environment in abdominal cavity, preventing                forming water mist at the lens position. The carbon                dioxide gas can be recycled, and the problem that                the operation time is prolonged due to low                temperature carbon dioxide is effectively solved to                make the cavity mirror lens fogging in minimally                invasive surgery, so as to effectively shorten                operation time on a certain degree, to reduce the                adverse effect caused by operation to the internal                organs of the patient. The air inlet cavity and                smoke discharging cavity is skilfully designed, the                structure is compact, saving space. The temperature                sensor is set in the third air-inlet interface, the                largest degree close to abdominal cavity to detect                the true temperature in the abdominal cavities. The                heating wire passes through the whole air- Inlet                pipe.

PT P
PN WO2022079693-A1
TI Making carbon black, comprises immersing anode and cathode in molten carbonate electrolyte that includes dissolved carbon dioxide, applying electric current to cathode and anode and forming carbon black on cathode.
AU DOUGLAS A E
   PINT C L
AE AEROBORN BV (AERO-Non-standard)
GA 2022533452
AB    NOVELTY - Making carbon black comprises: immersing an anode and a cathode in a molten carbonate electrolyte that includes dissolved carbon dioxide; applying an electric current having a current density of 50 mA/cm2 to 10 A/cm2 to the cathode and the anode; and forming the carbon black on the cathode.
   USE - The method is useful for making carbon black used in product comprising rubber tire, rubber hose, or rubber layer that includes blend of carbon black and rubber, electrostatic coating, pigment or pigmented coating, abrasion resistant coating, or energy storage device comprising super capacitor, electrochemical cell, or thermal mass (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces carbon black rather than other carbon allotropes, high current density of at least 100 mA/Cm2 is beneficial, by combining this reactor construction with specified current densities, the reactor during operation would produce solid carbon black particles having a diameter of 50 nm to 100 mu m; utilizes molten carbonate electrolyte comprising different components to form eutectic mixture, that is beneficial because it permits operation at lower temperatures than a single, pure or nearly pure molten metal carbonate electrolyte, is binary eutectic with two different molten carbonate electolytes and is a tertiary eutectic with three different molten carbonate electrolytes; and improves often associated with carbon capture and conversion/storage by transformation of the captured carbon into functional material useful for applications in variety of sectors and possible application for this technology is direct integration of this system to exhaust pipe on passenger car, which would utilize hot carbon dioxide exhaust as carbon dioxide source as well as thermal energy to heat the electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for forming carbon black comprising a chamber configured to immerse the anode and the cathode in the molten carbonate electrolyte that includes dissolved carbon dioxide, an inlet for providing carbon dioxide to be dissolved in the molten carbonate electrolyte, at least one electrical connection that is configured to provide an electric current having a current density of 100 mA/cm2 to 10 A/cm2 to the cathode and the anode, and an outlet for collecting the carbon black.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises a power source that is in electrical contact with the anode and the cathode. The current density is 1-10 A/cm2. The cathode comprises a conductive substrate coated with a passivating layer. The conductive substrate includes at least one metal, metal oxide, ceramic, or carbon material. The passivating layer includes aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, magnesium oxide, titanium-nitride, or vanadium nitride. The passivating layer is 2-100 nm, preferably 45-55 nm thick. The passivating layer is formed by on the surface of the conductive substrate by at least one of atomic layer deposition, physical vapor deposition, or electroplating. The anode and cathode comprise at least one metal, metal oxide, ceramic, or carbon material. The anode and cathode are a metal and the metal is steel. The molten carbonate electrolyte has a temperature of 400-850 degrees C. The molten carbonate electrolyte includes at least one lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, rubidium carbonate, cesium carbonate, francium carbonate, beryllium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, strontium carbonate, barium carbonate, and radium carbonate. The molten carbonate electrolyte includes lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, or potassium carbonate. The molten carbonate electrolyte is a eutectic mixture of at last two lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, and potassium carbonate. The carbon black has an average particle diameter of 50 nm to 100 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: injecting the carbon dioxide into the molten carbonate electrolyte; and collecting the carbon black from the cathode. The carbon dioxide is obtained from air, seawater, exhaust from industrial process, or exhaust from internal combustion engine. The step of collecting the carbon black includes: immersing the cathode in a fluid; permitting the carbon black to slough from the cathode so that the carbon black falls to the bottom of the reactor; collecting the fluid and the carbon black from the bottom of the reactor; and filtering the fluid; centrifuging the fluid; and evaporating the fluid or applying an electric field to the fluid to separate the carbon black from the fluid. The step of collecting the carbon black includes: immersing the cathode in a fluid; sonicating or scraping the cathode to separate the carbon black from the cathode, thus dispersing the carbon black into the fluid, and filtering the fluid, and centrifuging the fluid; and evaporating the fluid or applying an electric field to the fluid to separate the carbon black from the fluid.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Industrial supercritical composite extraction            system, has collecting pipe provided with anti-return            valve of carbon dioxide flowing extraction device, and            condensing device provided with cooling collection tube            that is provided with collecting container.

AE SEASON FARM BIO-ELECTROMECHANICAL CO LTD (SEAS-Non-standard)
   SEASON FARM BIO-ELECTROMECHANICAL CO LTD (SEAS-Non-standard)

PT P
PN CN110125153-A
TI Green recycling method of heavy metal polluted soil by forming leaching agent by oxygen and carbon dioxide, distributing to each of injection drilling hole, leaching heavy metal in soil and removing solid particles through filtering process.
AU NA P
   LI M
   QIAO B
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019728175
AB    NOVELTY - Green recycling method of heavy metal polluted soil involves passing oxygen and carbon dioxide into filling pipeline to form leaching agent, distributing to each of injection drilling holes after the flow rate is adjusted by well site, flowing leaching solution using polluted soil layer for leaching heavy metal in soil, lifting leachate from polluted soil layer along the pumping hole using diving pump to surface, collecting leaching solution in each of liquid collecting branches, removing solid particles through filtering process, and entering filtered leachate into heavy metal recovery process. The heavy metal recovery process uses ion exchange resin adsorption and activated carbon adsorption processes for removing heavy metal ions. Preparation of carbon dioxide involves air drying activated carbon adsorbed by activated carbon adsorption process, oxygen-enriched combustion processing, collecting exhaust gas, and washing and purifying.
   USE - The method is used for green recycling heavy metal polluted soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is highly operational, has no secondary pollution, improves crop utilization efficiency, and forms process of carbon recycling and environmental protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Green recycling method of heavy metal polluted soil involves passing carbon dioxide and oxygen into filling pipeline to form leaching agent, distributing to each of injection drilling holes after the flow rate is adjusted by well site, flowing leaching solution through polluted soil layer for leaching heavy metal in soil, lifting leachate from polluted soil layer along the pumping hole through diving pump to surface, collecting leaching solution in each of liquid collecting branches, removing solid particles by filtering process, and entering filtered leachate into heavy metal recovery process. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen is 3:1. The heavy metal recovery process uses ion exchange resin adsorption and activated carbon adsorption processes to remove heavy metal ions. The activated carbon adsorption material is prepared by modifying and carbonizing agricultural straw. The carbon dioxide is prepared by air drying activated carbon adsorbed by activated carbon adsorption process, oxygen-enriched combustion processing, collecting exhaust gas, and washing and purifying. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for green recycling device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of dissolved carbon dioxide in leaching solution is 200-400 mg/L. The residual oxygen content in leaching solution is 30-60 mg/L. Preferred Method: The activated carbon adsorption material is prepared by crushing agricultural straw into particles having length of 1-10 cm, leaching in 1-5 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution for 7-72 hours, taking out, washing with water until neutral, drying, dipping in solution of 0.01-0.5 g/mL diammonium phosphate for 24-36 hours, drying particles at 100-120 degrees C, oxidizing in air at 120-180 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and activating under nitrogen protection at 500-800 degrees C for 50-60 minutes at rate of 20-30 degrees C/minute. The method further involves ion exchange resin elution process and ion exchange resin regenerating process.

PT P
PN CN109251943-A; CN109251943-B
TI Preparing OPO structural grease by enzyme catalysis under supercritical carbon dioxide condition comprises dissolving the halogenated carboxylic acid, extracting, mixing, dialyzing, reacting and separating.
AU ZOU B
   ZHANG L
   XIA J
   CHU Y
   HUO S
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201910423B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,3-Dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride (OPO) structural grease by enzyme catalysis under supercritical carbon dioxide condition comprises (i) dissolving the halogenated carboxylic acid in acetonitrile, adding imidazole compound, first magnetically stirring reaction, extracting the reaction product with anhydrous diethyl ether to obtain lower layer of oily substance and vacuum drying to obtain a modifier, mixing the modifier with carbonyl diimidazole, adding into anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide; (ii) adding zinc nitrate into deionized water to obtain solution-A, dissolving 2-methylimidazole, acylated lipase solution and polyvinylpyrrolidone in deionized water to obtain solution-B, mixing solution-A with solution-B, stirring, centrifuging; and (iii) adding immobilized lipase using tripalmitin and oleic acid as reaction substrates, passing through the medium carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions, reacting and separating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing OPO structural grease by enzyme catalysis under supercritical carbon dioxide condition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, has high conversion rate, high purity, high selectivity and the enzyme catalyst can be used repeatedly, creates less pollution to the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 1,3-Dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride (OPO) structural grease by enzyme catalysis under supercritical carbon dioxide condition comprises (i) dissolving the halogenated carboxylic acid in acetonitrile, adding imidazole compound, first magnetically stirring reaction, extracting the reaction product with anhydrous diethyl ether to obtain lower layer of oily substance and vacuum drying to obtain a modifier, mixing the modifier with carbonyl diimidazole, adding into anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide, second magnetically stirring and reacting at room temperature to obtain active body, adding the active body into the lipase solution, third magnetic stirring reaction and dialyzing to obtain acylated lipase solution; (ii) adding zinc nitrate into deionized water to obtain solution-A, dissolving 2-methylimidazole, acylated lipase solution and polyvinylpyrrolidone in deionized water to obtain solution-B, mixing solution-A with solution-B, stirring, centrifuging and washing to obtain immobilized lipase; and (iii) adding immobilized lipase using tripalmitin and oleic acid as reaction substrates, placing in a reaction kettle, passing through the medium carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions, reacting and separating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halogenated carboxylic acid is chloroacetic acid or bromopropionic acid. The imidazole compound is 1,2-dimethylimidazole or 1,butyl-2-methylimidazole. The lipase in the lipase solution comprises 1,3-specific transesterified lipase, specifically, Aspergillus oryzae lipase, Aspergillus niger lipase, black mucor lipase, Pseudomonas cepacia lipase, thermophilic lipase, Candida antarctica lipase, Candida rugosa lipase or porcine pancreatic lipase. The molar ratio of the halogenated carboxylic acid and imidazole compound is 10:1.1. The molar ratio of the modifier and carbonyl diimidazole is 1:1. The volume ratio of the active body and the lipase solution is 90 mu l :5 ml. The concentration of the lipase solution is 150-350 mu M. The dosage ratio of zinc nitrate, deionized water and 2-methylimidazole is 10-400 mg :1-5 ml :410 mg. The ratio of the amount of the 2-methylimidazole, acylated lipase solution and polyvinylpyrrolidone is 410 mg :5 ml :50 mg. The molar ratio of glyceryl tripalmitate to oleic acid is 1:1-1:8. The amount of immobilized lipase is 0.5-12 wt.% the total amount of the reaction substrate. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the first magnetic stirring reaction is carried out at 60-100 degrees C for 5-10 hours, the room temperature reaction is carried out for 2-8 hours and the third magnetic stirring reaction is carried out at 0-4 degrees C for 8-24 hours. In the step (ii), the dissolution is carried out at 42 degrees C and stirring for 5-10 minutes. In the step (iii), the reaction is carried out at 20-90 degrees C for 5-24 hours at a pressure of 5-40 Mpa.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN111017967-A
TI Preparing ultra-high-purity magnesium oxide involves preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate and high-purity magnesium oxide by using lightly burned magnesium oxide, sulfuric acid, carbon dioxide, and water as raw materials.
AU CHEN S
   LI L
   XING X
   BI L
   FENG C
   SUN M
AE HEBEI MEISHEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
   HEBEI XINGTAI METALLURGICAL MAGNESIUM (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202034504P
AB    NOVELTY - Preapring ultra-high-purity magnesium oxide involves preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate and high-purity magnesium oxide by using lightly burned magnesium oxide, sulfuric acid, carbon dioxide, and water as raw materials. The ammonium sulfate mother liquor is recovered from the synthesis process and lightly burned magnesium oxide are used as raw materials, mixed and heated to produce magnesium oxide, other sulfates and ammonia gas, where the molar ratio of ammonium sulfate to lightly burned magnesium oxide is controlled at 1:1-1:1.1, the ammonium sulfate mother liquor is 0.8-1.2 mole/Litre, the reaction temperature is 85-100 degrees C, the reaction time is 4-7 hours, and the stirring speed is 30-60 rpm. The ammonia gas and dilute magnesium slurry are recovered. The concentration of magnesium sulfate in the dilute magnesium slurry is 1.2-1.8 mole/Litre.
   USE - Method for preparing ultra-high-purity magnesium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare ultra-high-purity magnesium oxide has high-purity magnesium oxide purity reaches 99.9% in eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultra-high-purity magnesium oxide involves preparing high-purity magnesium carbonate and high-purity magnesium oxide by using lightly burned magnesium oxide, sulfuric acid, carbon dioxide, and water as raw materials. The ammonium sulfate mother liquor is recovered from the synthesis process and lightly burned magnesium oxide are used as raw materials, mixed and heated to produce magnesium oxide, other sulfates and ammonia gas, where the molar ratio of ammonium sulfate to lightly burned magnesium oxide is controlled at 1:1-1:1.1, the ammonium sulfate mother liquor is 0.8-1.2 mole/Litre, the reaction temperature is 85-100 degrees C, the reaction time is 4-7 hours, and the stirring speed is 30-60 rpm. The ammonia gas and dilute magnesium slurry are recovered. The concentration of magnesium sulfate in the dilute magnesium slurry is 1.2-1.8 mole/Litre. The sulfuric acid and lightly burned magnesium oxide are added to increase the magnesium sulfate content in the dilute magnesium slurry and removed other metal impurities, calculated based on the concentration of magnesium sulfate in the reaction solution after the process is 2.5 mole/Litre, weighed a certain amount of sulfuric acid and lightly burned magnesium oxide to react. The added molar amount of lightly burned magnesium oxide is 1.05-1.3 times of sulfuric acid, added sulfuric acid to the mixture of lightly burned magnesia and dilute magnesium slurry to stabilize the pH of the reaction solution at 8-10, the stirring speed of 30-60 rpm, and the reaction temperature of 70-100 degrees C, added the precipitant, warmed to boiling, kept the temperature for 1-2 houyrs, and stirred at 20-30 rpm. The hetero ions are reacted with free OH- to form a precipitate, but to achieve the technical requirements, a second purification is required. The carbon dioxide is passed into the ammonia water recovered in the distillation process to form an aqueous solution of ammonia with an appropriate concentration, and then a certain amount of ammonium bicarbonate is added to make a precipitating agent. The moles of carbon dixoide in the precipitant account for 45-55 wt.% of the total moles of ammonium, the concentration of the precipitant in terms of ammonium is 4.0-5.5 mole/Litre, and the reaction temperature is normal temperature. The magnesium sulfate solution is heated to a certain temperature, and quickly added to the precipitant that has been heated to carry out the metathesis reaction. The molar concentration of magnesium sulfate solution and precipitant is controlled at 1.8-2.2 mole/Litre, the reaction molar ratio of magnesium sulfate and precipitant is 1:0.9-1:1.1, the feed rate is 60-100 Litre/second, and the stirring time is 120-300 second, stirring speed is 30-45 rpm, reaction temperature is 46-60 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours. The aged material is centrifuged to obtain basic magnesium carbonate filter material, and at the same time recover the mother liquor. The sulfate is attached to the surface of the basic magnesium carbonate crystals should be washed away. The washed basic magnesium carbonate is heated to 90-100 degrees C, constant temperature for 15-30 minutes, stirring speed of 20-40 rmp. The basic magnesium carbonate crystal form is converted from the 4:1:8 form to the more stable 4:1:5 form, and the sulfates wrapped between the crystals are released into the pyrolysis solution. The second washing should washed out the sulfates attached to the surface of the basic magnesium carbonate crystals. The washed basic magnesium carbonate material at 140 degrees C to obtain high-purity basic magnesium carbonate. The dried basic magnesium carbonate is sent to the roasting kiln, and roasted at 850-900 degrees C for 6-8 hours to produce ultra-high purity magnesium oxide with magneisum oxide content of 99.9%.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN CN114753887-A
TI Method for sealing carbon dioxide geology based on            mixed fluid self-separation, involves injecting carbon            dioxide of liquid into underground sealed space, and            sealing carbon dioxide by using water body dissolving            capacity and space gap of salt water layer.
AU MIAO Q
   REN S
   TIAN L
   XU Q
AE GEN PROSPECTING INST CHINA NAT ADMINISTR (GEPR-Non-standard)
GA 2022949875
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a gas injection                position and a monitoring position. The gas                injection hole and the monitoring hole are                vertically drilled. The communication injection                hole is arranged in a pipe. The industrial carbon                dioxide waste gas is compressed into high pressure                liquid carbon dioxide. The high pressure carbon                dioxide is transported to a sealing position by a                transport tank truck. The air outlet end of the                transport tank car is connected with the pipeline                in the air injection. The carbon dioxide of the                liquid is injected into the underground sealed                space by a pressure pump in the transportation tank                car. A sealing purpose is realized by using water                body dissolving capacity and a space gap of the                salt water layer.
   USE - Method for sealing carbon dioxide geology                based on mixed fluid self-separation. Uses include                but are not limited to forest, ocean, bottom layer                and chemical reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables monitoring the carbon                dioxide concentration in the geology for a long                time, so that automatic pre-warning prompt working                staff can prevent and treat in advance, thus                avoiding carbon dioxide leakage, protecting                ecological environment, and improving safety of                carbon dioxide geology sequeetion.

AB    NOVELTY - The arterial carbon dioxide monitoring device                (10) includes a housing (12), an earpiece (14)                coupled to the housing, and a carbon dioxide sensor                located within the housing. The earpiece is                configured to provide a hermetic seal enclosing a                patient's external auditory canal. The carbon                dioxide sensor measures arterial carbon dioxide                levels in arterial blood vessels of a patient's                tympanic membrane and external auditory canal. The                hermetic seal creates equilibration between                airspace contained in the external auditory canal                and the arterial blood vessels.
   USE - Arterial carbon dioxide monitoring device for                monitoring and measuring carbon dioxide levels in                arterial blood vessels of patient's tympanic                membrane and external auditory canal.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can measure carbon dioxide levels                non-invasively by quantifying gas diffusion through                the tympanic membrane and surrounding structures                via a space that is hermetically sealed from the                environment within the external auditory canal. The device provides real-time accurate                measurements, while providing information in a                manner that is familiar to healthcare                providers.

   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for light flame retardant insulating plate for building external wall.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of high density polyethylene, coupling agent, foaming agent, aramid fiber and phenolic resin is 100-150:35-40:8-10:15-20:10-30. The mass ratio of high density polyethylene and refractory is 100-150:50-70. The coupling agent is chromium complex coupling agent. The refractory is clay refractory mud and nano-silicon dioxide in a mass ratio of 2.3:1. The powder particle size of the refractory is 90-100 microm. The inside of the flame retardant bag is sodium bicarbonate. The pressure in the drying step is 20-25 Pa, the temperature is 5-10degrees Celsius, and the time is 10-15 minutes.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The reaction                  resistance of metal oxide (A1O) to carbon dioxide                  is higher than reaction resistance of barium                  oxide to carbon dioxide. The material having                  perovskite structure has lattice parameter of                  plus minus20% of lattice parameter of metal (A1)                  oxide. The driving pressure and a luminescence                  intensity of the piezoelectric structure are                  adjusted by a number and/or a content of                  interlayers. The                  interlayer is a monolayer of metal (A1) oxide or                  has a structure in which the monolayer is stacked                  several times within ten layers. The                  piezoelectric structure includes metal oxide                  compound of formula: (AA1)n+1B'nO3n+1. The                  piezoelectric structure further comprises a                  dopant included in at least one of the perovskite                  material layer and the interlayer. The dopant                  includes a rare earth element or a transition                  metal element. The transition                  metal element comprises at least one of                  manganese, copper, niobium, and cobalt. Preferred                  Process: The formation of the perovskite material                  layer and formation of the interlayer are                  performed by atomic layer deposition method,                  chemical vapor deposition method, or physical                  vapor deposition method, preferably atomic layer                  deposition method.

PT P
PN CN111003859-A; CN211921154-U
TI Performing zero-emissions treatment method for reclaimed water, involves homogenizing the reclaimed water, and using the multi-media filter to remove the suspended matter in the wastewater after the treatment.
AU PENG W
   LUO X
   WU Z
   ZHANG H
   SHI J
   ZHANG Q
   ZHOU M
   WANG L
   XIA W
   FAN K
   DANG J
AE JIANGSU JIUWU HI TECH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020323118
AB    NOVELTY - Performing zero-emissions treatment method involves homogenizing the reclaimed water, and using the multi-media filter to remove the suspended matter in the wastewater after the treatment. The ultrafiltration is used to further remove the suspended matter in the wastewater after the treatment. The resin treatment is used to remove the hardness in the wastewater after ultrafiltration filtration. The treated wastewater treated is treated with a period of reverse osmosis to concentrate the wastewater, the reverse osmosis clear liquid is reused, and the concentrated liquid is further processed. The carbon dioxide in the concentrated liquid is reduced by using a carbon remover for the reverse osmosis concentrated liquid. The carbon dioxide-removed concentrated water is subjected to nanofiltration to separate monovalent and divalent salts. The obtained nanofiltration concentrate is frozen and crystallized to obtain thenardite.
   USE - Method for performing zero-emissions treatment method used for reclaimed water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing zero-emissions treatment method involves homogenizing the reclaimed water, and using the multi-media filter to remove the suspended matter in the wastewater after the treatment. The ultrafiltration is used to further remove the suspended matter in the wastewater after the treatment. The resin treatment is used to remove the hardness in the wastewater after ultrafiltration filtration. The treated wastewater treated is treated with a period of reverse osmosis to concentrate the wastewater, the reverse osmosis clear liquid is reused, and the concentrated liquid is further processed. The carbon dioxide in the concentrated liquid is reduced by using a carbon remover for the reverse osmosis concentrated liquid. The carbon dioxide-removed concentrated water is subjected to nanofiltration to separate monovalent and divalent salts. The obtained nanofiltration concentrate is frozen and crystallized to obtain thenardite. The second stage reverse osmosis treatment is applied to the nanofiltration liquid, the wastewater is concentrated, the reverse osmosis liquid is reused, and the concentrated liquid is further processed. The obtained reverse osmosis concentrate is treated with high-pressure reverse osmosis, and the clear liquid is reused. The obtained high-pressure reverse osmosis concentrate is used for power plant ash treatment. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a zero-discharge treatment device for reclaimed water, which includes a waste water tank for storing reclaimed water obtained after printing and dyeing wastewater treatment, and ultrafiltration membrane is connected to waste water tank, and used for ultrafiltration treatment of reclaimed water, and ion exchange resin column is connected to ultrafiltration membrane, and used for ion exchange hardening treatment of permeate of ultrafiltration membrane, and first reverse osmosis membrane is connected to ion exchange resin column, and is used to concentrate the product water of the ion exchange resin column, and carbon remover is connected to the interception side of the first reverse osmosis membrane, and is used to remove carbon from the concentrated liquid obtained by the first reverse osmosis membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The media filtration refers to using one or a combination of manganese sand, activated carbon, or fly ash as the filter medium. The particle diameter of the medium gradually increases from top to bottom. The particle diameter of the uppermost layer is 0.4-0.6 mm, the particle diameter of the middle layer is 0.6-1.6 mm, and the particle diameter of the lowermost layer is 2-4 mm. The ultrafiltration uses a ceramic ultrafiltration membrane with a membrane pore size range of 20-50 nanometer. The first filtration aid and/or the second filtration aid are also added to the ultrafiltration influent, and the addition amount may be 1-3 wt.%. The first filter aid is aminated magnetic nano-iron oxide (Fe3O4) particles, and the second filter aid is amidated magnetic nano-Fe3O4 particles. The ion exchange resin refers to a sodium-type cation exchange resin. The flow rate of the upper column liquid is 2-5 bohrvolt/hour. The method of resin regeneration is hydrochloric acid and sodium hydroxide regeneration. The first-stage reverse osmosis and the second-stage reverse osmosis are cellulose acetate membranes or polyamide-based materials. The recovery rate of the first-stage reverse osmosis filtration treatment water is 75-80 wt.%, and the operating pressure ranges from 2-4 megapascal. The recovery rate of nanofiltration membrane is 75-80%. The operating pressure ranges from 4-8 megapascal. The sodium sulfate in the concentrated solution of nanofiltration is not less than 12 wt.%.The temperature of freezing crystallization is (-5)-5 degrees C, the purity of the obtained sodium sulfate is between 97% and 99%, and the yield of sodium sulfate is not less than 97 wt.%. The recovery rate of reverse osmosis is 80-85%, the operating pressure range is 2-4 megapascal, and the salt content of the dope is between 3-5 wt.%. The high-pressure reverse osmosis concentrated liquid salt content is between 8% and 10%, and the concentrated liquid is used for power plant ashing treatment.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
MC A12-M; A12-W11A; A12-W11J; A12-W14; D04-A01; D04-B10A; Q42-D
IP C01D-005/00; C01D-005/16; C02F-103/30; C02F-009/08; C02F-009/12

PN CN110927151-A
TI Quality-preservation status indicator comprises first permeable membrane, a composite indicating layer, a porous substrate, an adhesive layer, a protective film, and a pressure-sensitive adhesive layer that are sequentially contacted.
AU TENG C
AE LUAN YICHENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202026462U
AB    NOVELTY - Quality-preservation status indicator comprises first permeable membrane, a composite indicating layer, a porous substrate, an adhesive layer, a protective film, and a pressure-sensitive adhesive layer that are sequentially contacted, where components of the composite indicator layer include adhesives and indicators. The indicator includes one or more of carbon dioxide indicator, oxygen indicator and water vapor indicator. The adhesive in the composite indicator layer includes one or more of polyester adhesive and polyurethane adhesive. The material of the porous substrate is expanded polytetrafluoroethylene. The quality indicator includes the first permeable membrane, composite indicator layer, porous substrate, adhesive layer and second permeable membrane in sequence. The components of the composite indicator layer include adhesives and indicators. The indicator includes one or more of carbon dioxide indicator, oxygen indicator and water vapor indicator.
   USE - Quality-preservation status indicator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Quality-preservation status indicator comprises first permeable membrane, a composite indicating layer, a porous substrate, an adhesive layer, a protective film, and a pressure-sensitive adhesive layer that are sequentially contacted, where components of the composite indicator layer include adhesives and indicators. The indicator includes one or more of carbon dioxide indicator, oxygen indicator and water vapor indicator. The adhesive in the composite indicator layer includes one or more of polyester adhesive and polyurethane adhesive. The material of the porous substrate is expanded polytetrafluoroethylene. The quality indicator includes the first permeable membrane, composite indicator layer, porous substrate, adhesive layer and second permeable membrane in sequence. The components of the composite indicator layer include adhesives and indicators. The indicator includes one or more of carbon dioxide indicator, oxygen indicator and water vapor indicator. The adhesive in the composite indicator layer includes one or more of polyester adhesive and polyurethane adhesive. The porous substrate is made of expanded polytetrafluoroethylene. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing quality-preservation status indicator, which involves:
   (A) coating mixed reagent of an adhesive and an indicator on the surface of the first permeable membrane, dry-composite the coated first permeable membrane and the porous substrate to obtain first compound membrane, where material of the porous substrate is expanded polytetrafluoroethylene;
   (B) coating adhesive on the porous substrate of the first composite film, subjecting coated porous substrate and the protective film to dry-composite to obtain second composite film;
   (C) coating pressure-sensitive adhesive on the protective film of the second composite film, and drying to obtain quality indicator;
   (D) applying mixed reagent of adhesive and indicator on the surface of the first permeable membrane, subjecting the coated first permeable membrane and the porous substrate to dry-composite to obtain first compound membrane; and
   (E) coating adhesive on the porous substrate of the first multi-layer film, and drying coated porous substrate and the second permeable membrane to obtain quality indicator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide indicator includes a carbon dioxide sensitive reactive dye, an alkaline substance and a solvent. The carbon dioxide sensitive reactive dyes include m-cresol purple, thymolphthalein, o-cresolphthalein, acryloyl fluorescent yellow, beta-methyl umbellate, bromothymolphthalein blue, cresol red, cetyltrimethyl ammonium hydroxide, neutral red, phenol red, rhodamine 6G, sulforhodamine 101, thymol blue and thymol phthalocyanine blue. The alkaline substance includes sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide. The oxygen indicator includes oxygen-sensitive reactive dyes and solvents. The oxygen-sensitive reactive dyes include one or more of methylene blue, azure B, Nile blue, ruthenium terpyridine, ruthenium perchlorate, octaethylporphyrinone platinum and protoflavin. The water vapor indicator includes water-soluble ink. The adhesive in the adhesive layer comprises polyester adhesive and polyurethane adhesive. The protective film comprises a polyethylene film, polypropylene film, polystyrene film, polyvinyl chloride film, polyvinylidene chloride film, polyethylene terephthalate film and nylon film. The component of the pressure-sensitive adhesive layer comprises a rubber-based pressure sensitive adhesive, polyacrylate pressure-sensitive adhesive and organic silicon pressure-sensitive adhesive. The first transparent film comprises a polyethylene film, polytetrafluoroethylene film, polypropylene film, polystyrene film, polyvinyl chloride film, polyvinylidene chloride film, polyethylene terephthalate film and nylon film. The second transparent film comprises a polyethylene film, polytetrafluoroethylene film, polypropylene film, polystyrene film, polyvinyl chloride film, polyvinylidene chloride film, polyethylene terephthalate film and nylon film. Preferred Condition: The drying temperature of the drying tunnel in the dry compound is 10-45 degrees C, and the drying time of the drying tunnel is 1-24 hours.

PN CN109557225-A; CN109557225-B
TI Method for determining carbon and oxygen isotope contained in carbonate sample, involves loading powder of carbonate sample into bottom of sample vial, evacuating bottle followed by injecting phosphoric acid, and collecting gas.
AU CUI L
   WANG X
   FENG L
AE INST GEOLOGY & GEOPHYSICS CHINESE ACAD S (CAGG-C)
GA 2019324055
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon and oxygen isotope contained in a carbonate sample determining method involves loading a powder of a carbonate sample into Y a bottom of the sample vial, where the sample vial comprises a first branch and a second branch, the first branch and the second branch are in communication with each other, the powder of the carbonate sample is passed through the first a branch pipe is loaded into the bottom of the sample vial, sealing a first open end of the first branch pipe and a second open end of the second branch pipe, and connecting the second branch pipe to a vacuum line of vacuum system. The sample bottle is evacuated to drain the air in sample bottle. The vacuum line from second open end of second branch is separated, and phosphoric acid is injecting into the vial through the second branch with a syringe to cause the phosphoric acid and the carbonate and powder of the sample is reacted followed by collecting carbon dioxide gas in the sample vial through first branch pipe.
   USE - Method for determining carbon and oxygen isotope contained in a carbonate sample.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon and oxygen isotope contained in a carbonate sample determining method involves loading a powder of a carbonate sample into Y a bottom of the sample vial, where the sample vial comprises a first branch and a second branch, the first branch and the second branch are in communication with each other, the powder of the carbonate sample is passed through the first a branch pipe is loaded into the bottom of the sample vial, sealing a first open end of the first branch pipe and a second open end of the second branch pipe, and connecting the second branch pipe to a vacuum line of a vacuum system. The sample bottle is evacuated to drain the air in the sample bottle. The vacuum line from the second open end of the second branch is separated, and phosphoric acid is injecting into the vial through the second branch with a syringe to cause the phosphoric acid and the carbonate and powder of the sample is reacted followed by collecting carbon dioxide gas in the sample vial through the first branch pipe by using a double-hole needle, where the double-hole needle includes a first pipeline and a second pipeline located inside, and the first pipeline is connected to helium gas such that the carbon dioxide gas flows out of the sample vial through the second line, and the collected carbon dioxide gas is introduced into a mass spectrometer to determine carbon isotope and oxygen isotope contained in the carbonate sample. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon and oxygen isotope determination system.

PN CN111484014-A
TI Carbon dioxide production device comprises e.g.            compressor, liquefier, reaction kettle, leaching tower,            dehydrating tower, absorption tower and rectifying            tower, and side surface of reaction kettle set with            material inlet and acid inlet.
AU JIANG J
AE BIFU CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BIFU-Non-standard)
GA 202076899Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide production device comprises                e.g. compressor, a cooler, a liquefier, a reaction                kettle, a leaching tower, a dehydrating tower, an                absorption tower and a rectifying tower. The side                surface of the reaction kettle is provided with                material inlet, and acid inlet. The bottom of the                reaction kettle is provided with liquid discharging                pipe. The top of the reaction kettle is provided                with exhaust pipe. The exhaust pipe of the reaction                kettle is connected with the gas inlet of the                leaching tower. The gas outlet at the top of the                leaching tower is connected with gas inlet of the                compressor. The gas outlet of the compressor is                connected with inlet of the cooling machine. The                outlet of the cooling machine is connected with the                inlet at the bottom of the dehydrating tower. The                outlet of the top of the dehydrating tower is                connected with the inlet of the top of the                absorbing tower. And the outlet at the bottom of                the absorption tower is connected with the inlet of                the liquefier.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide production                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple process and convenient                operation, recycles carbon dioxide produced                generated in calcium nitrate production process,                obtains pure carbon dioxide, avoids influence of                carbon dioxide on the environment, and recycles                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide production device comprises a                compressor, a cooler, a liquefier, a reaction                kettle, a leaching tower, a dehydrating tower, an                absorption tower and a rectifying tower. The side                surface of the reaction kettle is provided with                material inlet, and acid inlet. The bottom of the                reaction kettle is provided with liquid discharging                pipe. The top of the reaction kettle is provided                with exhaust pipe. The exhaust pipe of the reaction                kettle is connected with the gas inlet of the                leaching tower. The gas outlet at the top of the                leaching tower is connected with gas inlet of the                compressor. The gas outlet of the compressor is                connected with inlet of the cooling machine. The                outlet of the cooling machine is connected with the                inlet at the bottom of the dehydrating tower. The                outlet of the top of the dehydrating tower is                connected with the inlet of the top of the                absorbing tower. The outlet at the bottom of the                absorption tower is connected with the inlet of the                liquefier. And the outlet of the liquefier is                connected with the inlet of the rectifying tower.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing                carbon dioxide using device, comprising (i) feeding                calcium carbonate and dilute nitric acid from                material inlet and acid liquid inlet of the reactor                into reactor for reaction, discharging the carbon                dioxide gas generated by the reaction from the                exhaust pipe of the reactor, discharging the                reaction liquid after the reaction of calcium                carbonate and dilute nitric acid from the drain                pipe, (ii) carbon dioxide gas enters the leaching                tower from gas inlet of the leaching tower,                washing, and discharging from the outlet of the                leaching tower, (iii) the washed carbon dioxide gas                enters compressor to obtain compressed carbon                dioxide gas, and the compressed carbon dioxide gas                enters cooler to obtain cooled carbon dioxide gas,                (iv) cooling carbon dioxide gas, introducing into                the dehydration tower for dehydration, introducing                the adsorption tower for adsorption treatment, and                obtaining the purified carbon dioxide gas, and (v)                the purified carbon dioxide gas enters the                liquefier for separating carbon dioxide liquid                phase to obtain carbon dioxide liquid, the carbon                dioxide gas enters the rectification tower for                rectification, and obtaining pure carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of                the carbon dioxide production device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: There is two spraying                  layers in the leaching tower, the leaching liquid                  at the bottom of the leaching tower is introduced                  to the spraying layer through circulating pump,                  there is circulating liquid inlet pipes and waste                  liquid drain pipes on the bottom side of the                  leaching tower. The dehydration tower uses                  molecular sieve for dehydration, and the                  adsorption tower uses activated carbon for                  adsorption. In the step (ii), the leaching                  solution in the leaching tower is sodium                  bicarbonate solution, and the spraying density is                  15-20 L/m2x hours. Preferred Method: In the step                  (iii), in the compression process, the pressure                  of the carbon dioxide gas is 1-1.5 MPa, and the                  temperature of the cooled carbon dioxide gas is                  20-35 degrees C. In the step (iv), the pressure                  in the dehydration and adsorption process is                  1-1.5 MPa, and the temperature is 20-35 degrees                  C.

TI Accelerated dissolution of pH-buffered carbonate,            by providing apparatus for accelerated dissolution of            pH-buffered carbonates, supplying water, carbonic gas            and carbonate, dissolving any carbonates in lean            mixture, defining desired pH of buffered ionic mixture,            mixing hydroxide and releasing to sea.
AU CAPPELLO G
AE CAPPELLO G (CAPP-Individual)
GA 2022A85424
AB    NOVELTY - Method for accelerated dissolution of                pH-buffered carbonate, involves providing an                apparatus for accelerated dissolution of                pH-buffered carbonates comprising a mixer, a                dissolution reactor, and a pH correction reactor,                supplying the mixer with water, carbonic gas and                carbonate in order to obtain a design flow rate of                lean mixture, conveying the lean mixture to the                dissolution reactor, maintaining the lean mixture                in the dissolution reactor for the minimum time                necessary to dissolve any carbonates in the lean                mixture to obtain an ionic mixture, maintaining the                mixture inside the dissolution reactor in a                turbulent regime, releasing the ionic mixture from                the dissolution reactor to the pH correction                reactor, defining a desired pH of the buffered                ionic mixture to be released to the sea, supplying                the pH correction reactor with hydroxide, mixing                the ionic mixture with hydroxide to obtain a                buffered ionic mixture, and releasing the buffered                ionic mixture with the desired pH to sea.
   USE - The method is used for accelerated dissolution                of pH-buffered carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for accelerated dissolution of                pH-buffered carbonate, involves providing an                apparatus for accelerated dissolution of                pH-buffered carbonates comprising a mixer, a                dissolution reactor, and a pH correction reactor,                supplying the mixer with predetermined flow rates                of water, carbonic gas and carbonate in order to                obtain a design flow rate of lean mixture,                conveying the lean mixture to the dissolution                reactor, maintaining the lean mixture in the                dissolution reactor for the minimum time necessary                to dissolve any carbonates in the lean mixture                according to the reaction of calcium carbonate                (solution) and carbon dioxide (aqueous) and water                to obtain calcium bicarbonate (aqueous), calculated                at the design flow rate, so as to obtain an ionic                mixture, maintaining the mixture inside the                dissolution reactor in a turbulent regime with a Re                of greater than 4000, releasing the ionic mixture                from the dissolution reactor to the pH correction                reactor, defining a desired pH of the buffered                ionic mixture to be released to the sea, supplying                the pH correction reactor with hydroxide, mixing                the ionic mixture with a predetermined amount of                hydroxide to obtain a buffered ionic mixture having                the desired pH by the reaction of calcium hydroxide                (aqueous) and carbon dioxide (aqueous) to obtain                calcium bicarbonate (aqueous), and releasing the                buffered ionic mixture with the desired pH to                sea.

PN CN114871268-A
TI Repairing soil polluted by tetracycline            antibiotics, involves utilizing tetracycline antibiotic            in activated persulfate oxidative degradation soil of            hydrothermal charcoal, grinding waste biomass into            powdery particles, and adding soluble transition salt            by achieving remediation of polluted soil.
AU SONG M
   TANG Z
   CHEN R
   ZHANG C
   ZHANG X
   LI F
   FANG Z
   RUAN X
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 2022A4958D
AB    NOVELTY - Repairing soil involves utilizing the                tetracycline antibiotic in the activated persulfate                oxidative degradation soil of hydrothermal charcoal                and grinding the waste biomass into powdery                particles with a particle size of less than 100                meshes. The soluble transition metal iron salt is                added in a mass ratio of 1:5-1:10 to the biomass.                The water is mixed with the powdery particles. The                substances are uniformly mixed in a solid-liquid                ratio of 1:5-1:10 in carbon dioxide under the                protection of atmosphere, 180-240 &#176;C,                hydrothermal carbonization reaction for 3-6h to                obtain hydrothermal carbon. The persulfate solution                is configured according to the solid-to-liquid                ratio of persulfate to water of 6:1-10:1 mmol/L.                The three substances are fully mixed and uniform.                The tetracycline antibiotics in the soil are fully                degraded under normal temperature and pressure                after 6-12 hours of reaction followed by achieving                the goal of remediation of polluted soil.
   USE - Method for repairing soil polluted by                tetracycline antibiotics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple technique, mild reaction                condition, easy control and simple operation,                environment-friendly material, high efficiency and                low energy consumption, improves the physical and                chemical properties of the soil, which is good for                microbial community recovery, and realizes the                purpose of recycling the waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Repairing soil involves utilizing the                tetracycline antibiotic in the activated persulfate                oxidative degradation soil of hydrothermal charcoal                and grinding the waste biomass into powdery                particles with a particle size of less than 100                meshes. The soluble transition metal iron salt is                added in a mass ratio of 1:5-1:10 to the biomass.                The water is mixed with the powdery particles. The                substances are uniformly mixed in a solid-liquid                ratio of 1:5-1:10 in carbon dioxide under the                protection of atmosphere, 180-240 &#176;C,                hydrothermal carbonization reaction for 3-6h to                obtain hydrothermal carbon. The persulfate solution                is configured according to the solid-to-liquid                ratio of persulfate to water of 6:1-10:1 mmol/L.                The mass ratio of soil contaminated with                tetracycline antibiotics to hydrothermal carbon is                5:1-100:1. The solid-liquid ratio of tetracycline                antibiotic-contaminated soil and persulfate                solution is 5:1-10:1. The three substances are                fully mixed and uniform. The tetracycline                antibiotics in the soil are fully degraded under                normal temperature and pressure after 6-12 hours of                reaction followed by achieving the goal of                remediation of polluted soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The waste biomass                  mainly comes from agricultural waste, forestry                  waste and municipal waste. The sulfate is mainly                  sodium persulfate, hydrogen persulfate, sodium                  potassium persulfate or potassium hydrogen                  persulfate. The total content of the antibiotics                  is 10-100 mg/kg soil.

TI Slurry fuel used for diesel engine, gas turbine, diesel power generation, gas turbine power generation, and boilers, and used as fuel for internal combustion, comprises solid biomass comprising semi-carbonized biomass, liquid components and additives.
AU KAKIZAKI N
   SUTO Y
   UENO Y
AE JGC CORP (JAGA-C)
GA 202257591J
AB    NOVELTY - Slurry fuel comprises solid biomass comprising semi-carbonized biomass, liquid components and additives, where the maximum particle size of the semi-carbonized biomass is 500 mu m or less, and the fuel ratio of the semi-carbonized biomass is 0.15 or more, and the solid substance concentration in the slurry fuel is 10-70 %mass.
   USE - The slurry fuel is useful for a diesel engine, a gas turbine, a diesel power generation, a gas turbine power generation, and boilers, and used as fuel for internal combustion engines such diesel engines and gas turbines used in remote areas such as ships or remote islands.
   ADVANTAGE - The slurry fuel has high energy yield, reduces greenhouse gases, reduces the fuel transportation cost, reduces energy consumption and carbon dioxide emissions from the transportation of semi-carbonized biomass, promotes more advanced carbon dioxide-free, and effectively converts the energy of biomass into valuable products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing the slurry fuel, involving crushing semi-carbonized biomass, and mixing liquid components and additives;
   (2) a method for using the slurry fuel for power generation, involving using an internal combustion engine or a boiler, or for power generation or power for a ship;
   (3) a method of using power generation or power, involving using the slurry fuel immediately before being supplied to the internal combustion engine or the boiler, when the slurry fuel is used as a fuel for an internal combustion engine or a boiler or a method of using power generation or power, involving using the slurry fuel, and using the semi-carbonized biomass stored in advance is supplied to a power generation device or a power device;
   (4) a method for producing a slurry fuel and bio-oil, involving performing high-temperature semi-carbonization treatment of raw material biomass, producing semi-carbonized biomass and volatile substances, crushing the semi-carbonized biomass, mixing liquid components and additives to obtain slurry fuel, and liquefying the volatiles to obtain bio-oil; and
   (5) a method for producing a chemical product derived from slurry fuel and bio-oil, involving producing semi-carbonized biomass and volatiles by high-temperature semi-carbonization of raw material biomass, crushing the semi-carbonized biomass, mixing liquid components and additives to obtain the slurry fuel, liquefying the volatiles to obtain bio-oil, and co-treating the bio-oil with a petroleum distillate or crude oil to obtain a bio-oil-derived chemical product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The semi-carbonized biomass has a carbon concentration of 15-85 %mass. The content of the semi-carbonized biomass in the slurry fuel is 10 %mass or more. The content of the additive in the slurry fuel is 0.1-10 %mass, where the additive is a biomass-derived additive. The temperature of the high temperature semi-carbonization treatment is 300-500 degrees C. The liquefaction temperature of the volatile substance is 10-200 degrees C. Preferred Method: The method involves crushing the raw material biomass before high temperature semi-carbonization treatment of the raw material biomass, and using the gas separated when liquefying the volatile substance as a heat source for the high temperature semi-carbonization treatment of the raw material biomass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component comprises water, fossil-derived oil, or biomass-derived oil. The bio-oil contains an aromatic compound.
