TI Preparing copper-zinc-based catalyst for preparing methyl formate by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, involves weighing water-soluble copper salt, zinc salt and third transition metal salt, mixing with deionized water, and absolute ethanol.
AU LIANG P
   LIU J
   LIU Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (SDST-C)
GA 202247081A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-zinc-based catalyst involves weighing water-soluble copper salt, zinc salt and third transition metal salt, mixing with deionized water and absolute ethanol in the solution, stirring and dissolving in a three-necked flask, adding ammonia water dropwise, after stirring for 30 minutes, slowly adding the solution made of sodium silicate nonahydrate dropwise, stirring for 30 minutes, transferring the above mixture to an autoclave at 160-220 degreesC., water, thermal reaction for 12-48 hours, after cooling, suction filtrating, washing with deionized water, drying in a 60 degreesC., oven overnight, and then heating to 400-500 degreesC., for 2-4 hours, in an air atmosphere at a heating rate of 2 degreesC/minutes, obtaining copper-zinc-based poly metallic, poly metallic catalyst heating to 500-750 degreesC., at a heating rate of 5 degreesC/minutes, under hydrogen atmosphere for 1-2 hours, and soaking in 0.001-0.01 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution for 0.5-5 hours/minutes.
   USE - Method for preparing copper-zinc-based catalyst for preparing methyl formate by carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the catalyst, which is cheap and easy to obtain, and has high catalytic activity, high selectivity, strong metal copper anti-sintering ability, and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing copper-zinc-based catalyst involves weighing water-soluble copper salt, zinc salt and third transition metal salt, mixing with deionized water and absolute ethanol in the solution, stirring and dissolving in a three-necked flask, adding ammonia water dropwise, after stirring for 30 minutes, slowly adding the solution made of sodium silicate nonahydrate dropwise, stirring for 30 minutes, transferring the above mixture to an autoclave at 160-220 degreesC., water, thermal reaction for 12-48 hours, after cooling, suction filtrating, washing with deionized water, drying in a 60 degreesC., oven overnight, and then heating to 400-500 degreesC., for 2-4 hours, in an air atmosphere at a heating rate of 2 degreesC/minutes, obtaining copper-zinc-based poly metallic, poly metallic catalyst heating to 500-750 degreesC., at a heating rate of 5 degreesC/minutes, under hydrogen atmosphere for 1-2 hours, and soaking in 0.001-0.01 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution for 0.5-5 hours/minutes, washing with deionized water, and vacuum drying at room temperature to obtain copper-zinc-based catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a scanting electron microscopic image of the non-reduced copper-zinc-based poly metallic phyllo silicate catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water-soluble copper salt, zinc salt and the third transition metal salt are nitrate, chloride or acetate. The water-soluble third transition metal is one of iron, cobalt or nickel.Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of copper salt, zinc salt and the third transition metal salt are 6:1: 0.3-15:1:3.75. The mol ratio of ammoniacal liquor and the added water-soluble salt total mass is 10:1-20:1.Preferred Method: The method further involves operating condition of de alloying is to soak 0.001-0.01 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution for 0.5-5 minutes. The method further involves transferring the mixture to an autoclave and hydrothermally heating at 160-220 degreesC for 12-48 hours, after cooling, it is washed with deionized water by suction filtration, dried overnight in a 60 degreesC in oven, and then heating at 400-500 degreesC for 2-4 hours, at a heating rate of 2 degreesC/minutes, in an air atmosphere to obtain copper-zinc-based poly metallics, the phyllo silicate catalyst heating to 500-750 degreesC at a heating rate of 5 degreesC/minutes, under hydrogen atmosphere for 1-2 hours, soaking in 0.001-0.01 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution for 0.5-5 minutes, and washing with deionized water, and obtaining the copper-zinc-based catalyst, after vacuum drying at room temperature.

PN CN113880050-A
TI Preparing sodium hypochlorite by using waste brine            involves mixing the melamine sponge and polyamic acid            solution, dipping treatment, taking out and using            liquid nitrogen for freezing treatment, finally putting            in freezing drying box.
AU WEI G
   FU W
   CHEN J
   ZHOU M
   JIN P
   YUAN Y
AE GUANGXI HUANMIAO IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202216261G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sodium hypochlorite by using waste                brine involves mixing the melamine sponge and                polyamic acid solution, dipping treatment, taking                out and using liquid nitrogen for freezing                treatment, finally putting in the freezing drying                box for freezing and drying, performing imidization                treatment to obtain the polyimide/melamine sponge                aerogel, using carbon dioxide laser engraving                machine to etch the polyimide/melamine sponge                aerogel, preparing polyimide/melamine sponge                aerogel with multiple micro-groove array, putting                the prepared polyimide/melamine sponge gas gel with                multiple micro-groove array in pyrrole monomer                solution, then adding p-toluene sulfonic acid,                violent oscillation to remove bubbles, then slowly                dropping ferric chloride solution, stirring and                polymerizing, then filtering.
   USE - Method for preparing sodium hypochlorite by                using waste brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method hashigh production safety, in                simple and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sodium hypochlorite by using waste                brine involves mixing the melamine sponge and                polyamic acid solution, dipping treatment, taking                out and using liquid nitrogen for freezing                treatment, finally putting in the freezing drying                box for freezing and drying, performing imidization                treatment to obtain the polyimide/melamine sponge                aerogel, using carbon dioxide laser engraving                machine to etch the polyimide/melamine sponge                aerogel, preparing polyimide/melamine sponge                aerogel with multiple micro-groove array, putting                the prepared polyimide/melamine sponge gas gel with                multiple micro-groove array in pyrrole monomer                solution, then adding p-toluene sulfonic acid,                violent oscillation to remove bubbles, then slowly                dropping ferric chloride solution, stirring and                polymerizing, then filtering, and using de-ionized                water to wash the precipitate, finally drying to                obtain the polypyrrole coated polyimide/melamine                sponge aerogel, adding the polypyrrole coated                polyimide/melamine sponge aerogel into the                Tris-hydrochloric acid buffer solution of dopamine,                reacting, after finishing reaction, washing and                drying the precipitate, obtaining the aerogel with                multi-stage water transmission network. The upper                surface of the polystyrene foam is provided with a                dust collecting paper, the dust collecting paper is                provided with multiple aerogel of water                transmission network, the lower surface of the                polystyrene foam is provided with multiple cotton                cores, performing primary impurity removing                treatment to the waste salt water to obtain the                pre-treated waste brine, carrying out ion exchange                treatment to the pre-treated waste water through                chelate resin, placing the water evaporation system                in the waste salt water after ion exchange                treatment, performing evaporation concentration                treatment to obtain concentrated salt water,                putting the prepared concentrated brine in the                electrolytic tank, performing electrolysis, the                generated chlorine and sodium hydroxide after                cooling, mixing and reacting to obtain                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The melamine sponge is                  cylindrical structure with diameter of 20 mm and                  height of 1 millimeter and the mass concentration                  of the polyamic acid solution is 0.5 wt.%.                  Preferred Conditions: The freezing treatment time                  is 30-60 minutes, the freezing and drying                  temperature is -20degrees Celsius, the freezing                  and drying time is 20-30 minutes. The imidization                  treatment time is 200degrees Celsius and the time                  is 1-2 hours. The concentration of                  dopamine in the dopamine Tris-hydrochloric acid                  buffer solution is 0.1 mole/L, the reaction                  temperature is room temperature, the reaction                  time is 10-20 hours.

PN CN113651495-A
TI Wastewater treatment process of steel barrel paint            spraying line involves adjusting pH of paint spray            wastewater with sodium hydroxide, coagulating,            separating, and passing pretreated wastewater to            biochemical treatment to degrade macromolecular organic            pollutant in sewage.
AU ZHOU Q
AE JIAXING ZHONGLIANG BARREL MAKING CO LTD (JIAX-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9697M
AB    NOVELTY - Wastewater treatment process of steel barrel                paint spraying line involves passing paint spray                wastewater, pretreatment wastewater and net washing                wastewater to adjustment tank for homogenization                and water volume adjustment, adding sodium                hydroxide to adjust pH, adding coagulant to                coagulation tank (A), adding coagulant aid,                reacting to form large-particle flocculants, then                passing to sedimentation tank (A) for solid-liquid                separation to obtain pretreated wastewater, passing                pretreated wastewater to pH adjustment tank (A),                passing to biochemical treatment section after                adjusting the pH value, then passing pretreated                wastewater to biochemical reaction tank and                facultative contact biochemical tank to degrade the                macromolecular organic pollutants in sewage into                small molecular organics matter, and then passing                treated water to aerobic tank (A) and aerobic tank                (B) so that small-molecule organic matter is                converted into water, carbon dioxide, other                inorganic matter and microbial oxygen.
   USE - Wastewater treatment process of steel barrel                paint spraying line.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and efficient, and                reduces wastewater treatment costs, and effectively                treats wastewater and discharges water meeting                effluent index requirements, which ensures                environmental protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wastewater treatment process of steel barrel                paint spraying line involves passing paint spray                wastewater, pretreatment wastewater and net washing                wastewater to adjustment tank for homogenization                and water volume adjustment, and using pump to                quantitatively increase to the pH adjustment tank                after adjusting the water quality and quantity,                adding sodium hydroxide, stirring, adjusting pH,                adding coagulant to coagulation tank (A) to enhance                the coagulation effect, adding coagulant aid to                coagulation tank (A), stirring and reacting to form                large-particle flocculants which are easy to                settle, then passing to sedimentation tank (A) for                solid-liquid separation to obtain pretreated                wastewater, passing pretreated wastewater to pH                adjustment tank (A), passing to biochemical                treatment section after adjusting the pH value,                then passing pretreated wastewater to biochemical                reaction tank and facultative contact biochemical                tank to degrade the macromolecular organic                pollutants in the sewage into small molecular                organics matter, then passing treated water to                aerobic tank (A) and aerobic tank (B) so that                small-molecule organic matter is converted into                water, carbon dioxide, other inorganic matter and                microbial oxygen, adding wastewater obtained after                biochemical treatment to coagulation tank (B) for                coagulation reaction and precipitation, adding                coagulation aid to coagulation tank (B) to perform                chemical coagulation reaction, then passing to                sedimentation tank (B) for solid-liquid separation,                passing treated wastewater to pH adjustment tank                (B), adjusting pH value and discharging into                municipal sewage pipe network through the discharge                tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The coagulant (A) is                  calcium hydroxide and/or ferrous sulfate. The                  coagulant (B) is sodium hydroxide and/or                  polyaluminum chloride.Preferred Composition: The amount of                  coagulant (A) added in coagulation tank (A) is 7                  g/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH adjuster used                  to adjust the pH value in pH adjustment (A) is                  oxalic acid. The facultative contact biochemical                  tank comprises biological carriers with                  three-dimensional network structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The coagulant aid is                  polyacrylamide flocculant.

PT P
PN CN113620331-A; CN113620331-B
TI Method for preparing nano vaterite calcium carbonate from carbon dioxide (CO2) mineralized calcium carbide slag, involves adding dispersant to continue stirring to ventilate to terminate carbonization reaction, and to carry out solid-liquid separation.
AU FANG M
   WANG T
   LI W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021D1816B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves leaching a calcium component in calcium carbide slag. The calcium carbide slag is mixed with the ammonium sulfate solution and the reaction and filter are stirred to obtain the calcium-containing intermediate product and the filtrate. The calcium-containing intermediate product is mixed with water and added with a flotation agent to perform flotation, and the calcium-containing product after the flotation is filtered to obtain solid phase calcium sulfate dihydrate. The calcium sulfate dihydrate is mixed with water and fully stirred and mixed to obtain a calcium-containing slurry. The filtrate is added to the calcium-containing slurry and the CO2 gas is passed for carbonization to obtain a calcium carbonate product. The dispersant is added to continue stirring to ventilate to terminate the carbonization reaction, and to carry out solid-liquid separation. The solid phase is dried and ground to obtain nano-vaterite-type calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing nano vaterite calcium carbonate from carbon dioxide (CO2) mineralized calcium carbide slag applied to plastic, rubber, coating, adhesive, cigarette paper, ink, and medicine, and used for road bed material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses ammonium sulfate as the leaching agent of calcium carbide slag to obtain nano vaterite calcium carbonate through a stepwise gentle mineralization process, and can obtain nano vaterite type calcium carbonate with higher purity and whiteness. The calcium conversion rate of not less than 85% can be obtained within 15-30 minutes, and the purity of the calcium-containing product is not less than 85% such that the formed filtrate can be used for subsequent carbonization reactions to save the costs. The combining flotation technology with flotation agent can effectively separate the impurities in the leached calcium-containing product such that the purity and whiteness of the later carbonized product can be improved. The intermediate calcium-containing product after CO2 mineralization and purification combined with the dispersant can obtain nano vaterite-type calcium carbonate with uniform particle size distribution and high purity, and the filtrate formed by filtration is cyclically leached from calcium carbide slag by adjusting the concentration. The process flow is simplified, the equipment investment and operation cost is lowered, the reagents can be recycled, the secondary pollution is avoided, the production energy consumption is lowered, the product quality is good, the carbon emission reduction can be achieved to a certain extent, and the environmentally friendly engineering process can be achieved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The flotation agent is selected from an oil phase emulsion consists of several oleic acid, sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate, glycerol, kerosene and pine oil. The dispersant is selected from aminophosphoric acid composite salt consists of mixture of inorganic phosphate, ethylenediamine, glycerol, and ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid.

PN CN113429288-A
TI Synchronously extracting burdock leaves essential oil and chlorogenic acid useful in preparing e.g. tobacco flavoring agent, comprises e.g. mixing burdock leaves slurry and water, adding complex enzyme to mix, and performing ultrasonic and enzymatic hydrolysis.
AU ZHANG J
   YU N
   CHEN S
   CHEN F
   LIU E
AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 2021B3886Q
AB    NOVELTY - Synchronously extracting burdock leaves essential oil and chlorogenic acid comprises (1) washing and pulping burdock leaves to obtain burdock leaves slurry, (2) uniformly mixing burdock leaves slurry and water, placing it in an ultrasonic instrument, then adding complex enzyme to uniformly mix, and synchronously performing ultrasonic and enzymatic hydrolysis, (3) using carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction, where the collected extract is burdock leaves essential oil, (4) adding the liquid extracted in the step (3) to the D101B macroporous resin adsorption column for adsorption, and collecting the eluent of the target product, and (5) adding the concentrated solution in step (4) again to the D101B macroporous resin adsorption column for adsorption, eluting the adsorption column with the eluent, collecting the eluent of the target product, concentrating under reduced pressure, and recrystallizing the concentrated solution to obtain the chlorogenic acid product.
   USE - The burdock leaves essential oil is useful in preparing tobacco flavoring agent, cosmetic additive, food additive and medical health product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: obtains relatively high yield of the essential oil and chlorogenic acid; and maximizes the resource utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synchronously extracting burdock leaves essential oil and chlorogenic acid comprises (1) washing and pulping burdock leaves to obtain burdock leaves slurry, (2) uniformly mixing burdock leaves slurry and water, placing it in an ultrasonic instrument, then adding complex enzyme to uniformly mix, and synchronously performing ultrasonic and enzymatic hydrolysis, where the complex enzyme is composed of cellulase, hemicellulase, maltogenic amylase, acid alpha-amylase, and acid lipase, (3) after ultrasonic enzymatic hydrolysis, using carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction, where the cosolvent is ethane, the content of cosolvent is 2-5 mol.%, the extraction temperature is 25-50degrees Celsius, the extraction time is 1-3 hours, the extraction pressure is 15-25 MPa, and the collected extract is burdock leaves essential oil, (4) adding the liquid extracted in the step (3) to the D101B macroporous resin adsorption column for adsorption, eluting the adsorption column with the eluent, and collecting the eluent of the target product, and (5) adding the concentrated solution in step (4) again to the D101B macroporous resin adsorption column for adsorption, eluting the adsorption column with the eluent, collecting the eluent of the target product, concentrating under reduced pressure, and recrystallizing the concentrated solution to obtain the chlorogenic acid product.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for burdock leaves essential oil or chlorogenic acid product prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the mass ratio of the burdock leaves and water is 1:(2-4). In the step (2), the pH of the ultrasonic-enzymatic hydrolysis reaction system is 5-6. In the step (2), the dosage of the complex enzyme is 0.5-2 wt.% of the mass of the material. In the step (2), the complex enzyme is composed of 30-50 wt.% cellulase, 10-20 wt.% hemicellulase, 5-10 wt.% maltogenic amylase, 5-10 wt.% acid alpha-amylase, and acid lipase (remaining amount), the enzymatic activity of cellulase is 500,000 U/g, and the enzymatic activity of hemicellulase is 500,000 U/g, The enzyme activity of maltogenic amylase is 100,000 U/g, the enzyme activity of acid alpha-amylase is 100,000 U/g, and the enzyme activity of acid lipase is 100,000 U/g. In the steps (4) and (5), the adsorption parameters are: the pH of the adsorption solution is 4-5, and the flow rate is 2-4 ml/minutes. The analytical parameters are: the desorbent is 50-60 vol./vol.% ethanol solution, the elution speed is 1.5-3 ml/minutes, and the pH is 2.5-3.5.Preferred Method: In the step (2), the ultrasonic conditions are: ultrasonic frequency is 20-30 kHz, power is 100-300 W, temperature is 30-50degrees Celsius, and ultrasonic time is 30-90 minutes. In the step (3), carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction is used, the cosolvent is ethane, the content of the cosolvent is 3 mol.%, the extraction temperature is 35degrees Celsius, the extraction time is 2 hours, and the extraction pressure is 20 MPa.

PT P
PN CN113372497-A
TI Extracting chitosan and graft copolymer from squid fan-shaped bone comprises e.g. adding hydrogen peroxide into squid fan-shaped bone powder, heating, enzymatically hydrolyzing, centrifuging, purifying crude liquefied chitosan, and drying.
AU WU W
   YANG J
   BI J
AE RONGCHENG WANYING AQUATIC TECHNOLOGY CO (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8101M
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting chitosan and graft copolymer from squid fan-shaped bone comprises (i) (ia) taking squid fan-shaped bone, crushing to obtain squid fan fan-shaped bone powder; and (ib) adding hydrogen peroxide into squid fan-shaped bone powder, heating to obtain oxidized chitosan; (ii) adding complex protease into oxidized chitosan, enzymatically hydrolyzing, centrifuging and taking clear solution to obtain crude liquefied chitosan; (iii) purifying crude liquefied chitosan, placing dried crude chitosan into supercritical carbon dioxide extractor and extracting; (iv) taking chitosan, adding acrylic acid monomer, dissolving acrylic acid and chitosan, and curing to obtain chitin-acrylic acid copolymer; and (v) adding deionized water into chitin-acrylic acid copolymer, introducing nitrogen, adding natamycin, reacting, cooling, adjusting pH, and centrifuging to obtain precipitate, freeze-drying, and adding the supercritical fluid added with carrying agent to remove impurity.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting chitosan and graft copolymer from squid fan-shaped bone.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared chitosan has a large molecular weight and good copolymerization grafting effect. The method is simple and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting chitosan and graft copolymer from squid fan-shaped bone comprises (i) (ia) taking squid fan-shaped bone, crushing using ultra-micro crusher to obtain squid fan-shaped bone powder; and (ib) adding hydrogen peroxide into squid fan-shaped bone powder, heating to 100-150 degrees C to obtain oxidized chitosan; (ii) adding complex protease into oxidized chitosan, enzymatically hydrolyzing for 10-12 hours under the action of ultrasonic wave, centrifuging and taking clear solution to obtain crude liquefied chitosan; (iii) purifying crude liquefied chitosan in vacuum drying, placing dried crude chitosan into supercritical carbon dioxide extractor and extracting for 2-5 hours, where the residual material is chitosan; (iv) taking chitosan, adding acrylic acid monomer, maintaining at 35-50 degrees C and pH is less than 3, continuously stirring, completely dissolving acrylic acid and chitosan, stirring and reacting for 2-3 hours, and curing for 5-8 hours to obtain chitin-acrylic acid copolymer; and (v) adding deionized water into chitin-acrylic acid copolymer, adjusting pH to 9.1-9.8, continuously introducing nitrogen, adding natamycin, stirring and reacting at 55-60 degrees C for 6-8 hours, cooling at room temperature, adjusting pH to neutrality, and centrifuging to obtain precipitate, freeze-drying, and adding the supercritical fluid added with carrying agent to remove impurity, where the residue is chitosan graft compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide is 60-70%. The complex protease comprises alkaline protease and deacetylase at a mass ratio of 1:1 3-5. The mass ratio of chitosan and acrylic acid is 1:3-5. In the step (v), the mass ratio of the chitin-acrylic acid copolymer to deionized water is 1:6-8. In the step (v), the mass ratio of the chitin-acrylic acid copolymer aqueous solution to natalycin is 1:0.3-0.5. The natalmycin is an aqueous solution with a concentration of 3-10%. Preferred Method: In the step (ib), the hydrogen peroxide is soaked in the squid fan-shaped bone powder for 2-3 hours before heating. In the step (ii), the ultrasonic treatment is carried out at ultrasonic frequency of 0.5-1.2 KHz, ultrasonic power of 0.1-0.3 W/cm2 ultrasonic applying period of 5-8 minute/hour. In the step (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide treatment conditions are as follows: the pressure is 15-20 MPa, the temperature is 20-30 degrees C, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-15 kg/hour, and time 10-20 minutes. In the step (iv), the acrylic acid monomer is added with ammonium cerium ammonium sulfate and sodium metabisulfite at the same time.

TI Promoting protein synthesis with high content and high purity comprises extracting acid slurry of calyx, and medium containing beta-cryptoxanthin and bile salt.
AU HAN C
AE FOSHAN SHANXI COSMETICS CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 202158715J
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting protein synthesis comprises extracting acid slurry of calyx, medium containing beta - cryptoxanthin and bile salt, where the medium is prepared by (i) taking acid slurry of calyx, cleaning the raw material, and avoiding the impurity and dust, (ii) using hot air to dry, avoiding sunlight drying, and crushing the raw material by a grinder after finishing drying, (iii) mixing the raw materials uniformly by a mixer, then sieving the uniformly mixed raw material by a medicine sieving machine, sieving and filtering to obtain the acid slurry of calyx powder, (iv) preparing enzymolysis solution under the inert gas atmosphere by taking the acid slurry of calyx powder and water, (v) preparing enzymatic hydrolysis solution by uniformly mixing the enzymolysis solution by a mixer, then using inorganic acid or inorganic alkali to adjust the pH, (vi) taking the dried enzyme processing acid slurry calyx powder, and using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction supercritical extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting protein synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses above raw material, which contains high content, and high purity of beta -cryptoxanthin, uses bile salt and other substances for compounding, which increases the protein formation; prevents the protein loss; improves the medicine utilization effect of beta -cryptoxanthin; improves the extracting efficiency; uses the raw material, which is easy to obtain; is simple; has high quality product; and is economical and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Promoting protein synthesis comprises extracting acid slurry of calyx, medium containing beta - cryptoxanthin and bile salt, where the medium is prepared by (i) taking acid slurry of calyx, cleaning the raw material, and avoiding the impurity and dust, (ii) using hot air to dry, avoiding sunlight drying, and crushing the raw material by a grinder after finishing drying, (iii) mixing the raw materials uniformly by a mixer, then sieving the uniformly mixed raw material by a medicine sieving machine, sieving and filtering to obtain the acid slurry of calyx powder, (iv) preparing enzymolysis solution under the inert gas atmosphere by taking the acid slurry of calyx powder and water in a mass ratio of 1:10-60, ensuring the dosage of the biological enzyme is 0.1-5 wt.%, (v) preparing enzymatic hydrolysis solution by uniformly mixing the enzymolysis solution by a mixer, then using inorganic acid or inorganic alkali to adjust the pH of the enzymolysis solution to 4-6, (vi) taking the dried enzyme processing acid slurry calyx powder, and using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction supercritical extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bile salt comprises sodium glycocholate, potassium cholate, sodium glycodeoxycholate, sodium glycodeoxycholate and bezoar sodium taurocholate, where the mass ratio of beta - cryptoxanthin and bile salt is 1: (3-6). Preferred Method: The step (vi) comprises performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 40-70 degrees C, at extracting pressure of 20-40 MPa for 1.5-5 hours. The step (v) comprises adjusting the pH of the enzymolysis solution, then adding the enzymolysis solution into the constant temperature shaking table, shaking at a rate of 90-130 revolutions/minute, carrying out heat preservation enzymolysis for 80-160 minutes at 40-80 degrees C.

TI Preparation of divalent copper            tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)porphyrin film for gas            separation, involves soaking polysulfone substrate in            polyvinyl alcohol-copper nitrate solution, and in            solution of polyvinylpyrrolidone and porphyrin            compound.

   YU C
   LIANG Y
   ZHONG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 202147223X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of divalent copper                tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)porphyrin film involves                adding polyvinyl alcohol with degree of                polymerization of 1600-1700 and alcoholysis degree                of 87-89% and copper nitrate trihydrate to                deionized water, mixing, reacting at 90-95 degrees                C for 4-6 hours to obtain polyvinyl alcohol-copper                nitrate complex aqueous solution, soaking modified                polysulfone substrate in polyvinyl alcohol-copper                nitrate complex aqueous solution, drying to form                polyvinyl alcohol organic interface layer                containing small amount of divalent copper ions on                the surface of substrate to obtain divalent copper                ion-containing polyvinyl alcohol organic interface                layer deposited modified polysulfone substrate,                immersing the divalent copper ion-containing                deposited modified polysulfone substrate in mixed                solution of polyvinylpyrrolidone, copper nitrate                trihydrate, trifluoroacetic acid,                tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)porphyrin ligand and                organic solvent for self-assembly, drying, washing                and drying.

PN CN112456522-A
TI Comprehensively utilizing spice production waste residue and synthetic ammonia purge gas by moving basic magnesium chloride waste residue from reactor, adding water, stirring, beating, distilling to recover organic solvent and hydrolyzing.
AU GENG Q
   SHEN J
   LI R
   SHI J
AE ANHUI JINHE IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202125164N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for comprehensively utilizing spice production waste residue and synthetic ammonia purge gas by moving basic magnesium chloride waste residue from reactor, adding process water, stirring, beating, distilling to recover the organic solvent and hydrolyzing to obtain turbid liquid containing magnesium chloride and magnesium hydroxide, adding hydrogen peroxide to turbid liquid, stirring, performing pressure filtration separation to obtain magnesium hydroxide filter cake and magnesium chloride mother liquor, placing magnesium hydroxide filter cake in reactor, adding water, stirring, beating to form magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid, passing the synthetic ammonia purge gas into the magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid to carry out the carbonization reaction, and pressure filtering to obtain heavy magnesium water containing magnesium bicarbonate, steam heating and separating the basic magnesium carbonate filter cake using hydraulic filtration, washing, drying and calcining.
   USE - Method for comprehensively utilizing spice production waste residue and synthetic ammonia purge gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of magnesium resource recycling and reuse, avoids the impact of waste residue on soil and surface water pollution and the direct emission of carbon dioxide gas into the atmosphere on the ecological environment, reduces the carbonization time, increases the utilization rate of carbon dioxide, saves energy consumption, reduces the equipment investment and provides economic and ecological benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for comprehensively utilizing spice production waste residue and synthetic ammonia purge gas involves (i) moving the basic magnesium chloride waste residue from the production of spices into a reactor, adding process water, stirring and beating the slurry, heating to boil, and distilling to recover the organic solvent, hydrolyzing the basic magnesium chloride to obtain a turbid liquid containing magnesium chloride, magnesium hydroxide and some impurities, (ii) adding hydrogen peroxide to the turbid liquid, stirring the reaction, and performing pressure filtration separation to obtain magnesium hydroxide filter cake and magnesium chloride mother liquor, (iii) placing the magnesium hydroxide filter cake into the reaction kettle, adding appropriate amount of process water, stirring and beating to a uniform magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid, (iv) passing the synthetic ammonia purge gas into the magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid to carry out the carbonization reaction, and separating by pressure filtration to obtain heavy magnesium water containing magnesium bicarbonate, (v) steam heating and pyrolyzing of the heavy magnesium water to obtain basic magnesium carbonate suspension and (vi) separating the basic magnesium carbonate filter cake using hydraulic filtration of the basic magnesium carbonate suspension, washing, drying, and calcining to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves adding 2-3 times process water based on mass of the basic magnesium chloride waste residue, stirring and beating the slurry, heating at 100-105 degrees C, maintaining it for 2-2.5 hours and recovering the organic solvent for recycling. The step (i) involves hydrolyzing the basic magnesium chloride to generate a turbid solution of magnesium hydroxide and magnesium chloride during the process of heating and recovering the solvent. The step (ii) involves adding 3-5 vol.% hydrogen peroxide having concentration of 27.5% to the turbid liquid, maintaining the temperature at 85-100 degrees C for 10-20 minutes to destroy the digested organic matter and achieving the purpose of decolorization. The step (iii) involves adjusting the magnesium hydroxide turbid liquid to contain the magnesium hydroxide in a concentration of 1-2% at 15-30 degrees C. The method further involves purifying purge gas with special activated carbon adsorbent to remove trace hydrogen sulfide gas. The step (vi) involves after pyrolyzing, decreasing the temperature to 20-35 degrees C, filter pressing to obtain basic magnesium carbonate filter cake, washing, drying and calcining to obtain high-activity magnesium oxide, where the content of magnesium oxide is greater than or equal to 95.5 and the activity (citric acid value) 5-10 s, classifying, packaging and producing high-activity magnesium oxide. Preferred Conditions: In step (iv), the pressure at which the synthetic ammonia purge gas is introduced for carbonization is 0.2-0.25 MPa, and the pH value of the solution is 6.5-7.5. In step (v), the heating pyrolysis temperature is 60-80 degrees C, and the negative pressure pyrolysis is adopted to generate a basic magnesium carbonate suspension.

AU ZHOU Z
   CUI H
   DONG H
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202118240H
AB    NOVELTY - A bifunctional alkali metal nitrate modified                cadmium oxide-magnesium carbonate (CdO-MgCO3)                material comprises three kinds of alkali metal                nitrates and CdO-MgCO3 (I).
   USE - Bifunctional alkali metal nitrate modified                cadmium oxide-magnesium carbonate material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material is suitable for two different                fields of medium temperature carbon dioxide capture                and energy storage and utilization, and has simple                preparation method, low cost, good cycle stability,                and excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A bifunctional alkali metal nitrate modified                cadmium oxide-magnesium carbonate (CdO-MgCO3)                material comprises three kinds of alkali metal                nitrates and CdO-MgCO3 of formula                (LiaNabKc)m-(CdxMgy)(CdxMgy) (I).
   Li, Na, K=LiNO3, NaNO3 and KNO3;
   x=35-65;
   y=100-x;
   a=30-40;
   b=20-40;
   c=30-40; and
   m=15-25.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the material comprising:
   (A) weighing magnesium salt and cadmium salt,                dissolving in 150 mL deionized water, and                magnetically stirring for 10 minutes until solution                is transparent and clear;
   (B) weighing citric acid and polyethylene                glycol (PEG)-400, adding to prepared clear                solution, mechanically stirring at speed of 500                revolutions/minute, water bath heating to 80                degrees C for 4 hours, taking out sol, placing in                evaporating dish, and oven drying to 150 degrees C                for 5 hours to obtain fluffy gel structure;
   (C) grinding into white powder, adding to                muffle furnace, and calcining to 500 degrees C at                heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute for 4 hours to                obtain granular cadmium oxide-magnesium                oxide;
   (D) weighing lithium nitrate, sodium nitrate                and potassium nitrate, mixing, adding 60-100 mL                anhydrous methanol, magnetically stirring, adding                granular cadmium oxide-magnesium oxide,                magnetically stirring for 2 hours to obtain                suspension, vacuum rotary evaporating to 60 degrees                C to remove solvent and obtain alkali metal                salt-loaded cadmium oxide-magnesium oxide;                and
   (E) adding to fixed bed reactor, heating to                300 degrees C at heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute                under nitrogen atmosphere, changing atmosphere to                carbon dioxide at constant temperature for 1 hour,                heating to 400 degrees C at rate of 5 degrees                C/minute for 30 minutes, changing atmosphere to                nitrogen, and quickly cooling to room                temperature.
   The magnesium salt is magnesium nitrate and/or                magnesium acetate. The cadmium salt is cadmium                nitrate and/or cadmium acetate. The number of moles                of magnesium nitrate or cadmium nitrate is between                0.005-0.015. The molar ratio of citric acid and sum                of magnesium ions and cadmium ions is 1:1.2. The                volume ratio of PEG-400 and deionized water is                0.05:0.10.

PN CN112237248-A
TI Comprehensively preserving cold and fresh bento by sterilizing container, adding cooked staple rice, cooked meat product side dishes and vegetable side dishes into container, spraying food additives, cooling and packing in bento box.
AU LU L
   CAI J
AE JIANGSU BESTA FRESH FOOD CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202109740N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for comprehensively preserving cold and fresh bento involves (a) sterilizing a lidded container with bento units using high-temperature steam and leaving in a sterile environment to dry, (b) adding the cooked staple rice, cooked meat product side dishes and vegetable side dishes into different bento components in the container, spraying food additives in the meat product side dish container and covering with sealing covers, (c) leaving the container containing the bento components in a pre-cooler and removing the sealing cover on the top of the container to cool the food, (d) adding the cooled bento components into the bento box, (e) sending the bento box containing the bento components into the packing machine for packing, extracting the air in the box using air pump, filling with mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen and then sealing and (f) performing cold chain transportation of the packed bento box in a low temperature environment.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensively preserving cold and fresh bento.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces growth of the bacteria, effectively prolongs storage shelf life of bento and is suitable for widespread promotion and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for comprehensively preserving cold and fresh bento involves (a) sterilizing a lidded container with bento units using high-temperature steam and leaving in a sterile environment to dry, (b) adding the cooked staple rice, cooked meat product side dishes and vegetable side dishes into different bento components in the container, spraying suitable amount of food additives in the meat product side dish container and covering with sealing covers respectively, (c) leaving the container containing the bento components in a pre-cooler and removing the sealing cover on the top of the container to cool the food until surface temperature is 5-6 degrees C and internal temperature is 7-8 degrees C, (d) adding the cooled bento components into the bento box and ensuring that the height of the bento components is lower than the top surface of the bento box by 0.2-0.5 cm, (e) sending the bento box containing the bento components into the packing machine for packing, extracting the air in the box using air pump, filling with mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen and then sealing and (f) performing cold chain transportation of the packed bento box in a low temperature environment of 4-6 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves when cooking side dishes, meat products and vegetables, adding suitable amount of bacteriostatic seasoning, which comprises 2-5 g shredded scallion, 3-4 g minced garlic, 2-4 g shredded ginger and 1-2 g lemon juice. In step (b), the food additive comprises 0.1-0.2 g/kg water-soluble chitosan oligosaccharide, 0.05-0.12 g/kg phytic acid, 0.12-0.15 g/kg theaflavins and 0.02-0.18 g/kg rosemary. In step (d), the bento box contains 360-385 g rice, 105-115 g meat product side dishes and 90-100 g vegetable side dishes, where the total weight of the components is not greater than 580 g. nbIn step (e), the mixed gas comprises 25-28% carbon dioxide and remainder of nitrogen. Preferred Device: The bento box includes a box structure, where the top surface of the box structure is provided with a rectangular limit slot that limits the position of the food placing plate. The bottom of the box structure is provided with a rectangular support frame supporting the food placing plate and a heating pack is also placed at the inner bottom end of the box structure. The distance between the lower surface of the food placing plate and the inner bottom surface of the box structure is 4-6 cm. The box structure and the food placing plate are made of polyethylene, polypropene or polyamide materials.

PT P
PN WO2020243320-A1; CA3141127-A1; CN113924269-A; EP3976531-A1; BR112021022641-A2; IN202117054259-A
TI Preparing graphene involves performing            electrolysis between an electrolysis anode and an            electrolysis cathode in a molten carbonate electrolyte            to generate carbon nanomaterial on the cathode.
AU LICHT S
AE C2CNT LLC (CTWO-Non-standard)
   C2CNT LLC (CTWO-Non-standard)
GA 2020C0744H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene involves performing                electrolysis between an electrolysis anode and an                electrolysis cathode in a molten carbonate                electrolyte to generate carbon nanomaterial on the                cathode. The carbon nanomaterial is                electrochemically exfoliated from a second anode to                produce graphene.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepared graphene, where                exfoliating step produces graphene in high yield                than thicker, conventional graphite exfoliation                reactions, and carbon dioxide can be the sole                reactant used to produce the valuable product as                graphene, and this can incentivize utilization of                carbon dioxide (CO2), and unlike alternative                products made from CO2 such as carbon monoxide or                other fuels such as methane, use of the graphene                product does not release this greenhouse gas back                into the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for producing carbon                nano-platelets, which involves heating a carbonate                electrolyte to obtain a molten carbonate                electrolyte; disposing the molten carbonate                electrolyte between an electrolysis anode and an                electrolysis cathode in a cell, where the                electrolysis anode and the molten carbonate                electrolyte; and applying an electrical current to                the electrolysis cathode and the electrolysis anode                in the cell to electrolyze the carbonate and                generate carbon nano-platelets on the electrolysis                cathode without the formation of transition metal                nucleation sites on the cathode;
   (2) a method for producing carbon nano-onions                involves heating a carbonate electrolyte comprising                an oxide additive to obtain a molten carbonate                electrolyte; disposing the molten carbonate                electrolyte between an electrolysis anode and an                electrolysis cathode in a cell, where the                electrolysis anode and the molten carbonate                electrolyte do not include a transition metal                nucleating agent; and applying an electrical                current to the electrolysis cathode and the                electrolysis anode in the cell to electrolyze the                carbonate and generate carbon nano-onions on the                electrolysis cathode without the formation of                transition metal nucleation sites on the cathode;                and
   (3) a method for producing graphene carbon                nano-scaffolds which involves heating a carbonate                salt to obtain a molten carbonate electrolyte                enriched in non-lithium carbonates; disposing the                molten carbonate electrolyte between an                electrolysis anode and an electrolysis cathode in a                cell, where the electrolysis anode and/or the                molten carbonate electrolyte optionally further                comprises a transition metal nucleation agent; and                applying an electrical current to the electrolysis                cathode and the electrolysis anode in the cell to                electrolyze the carbonate and generate carbon                nano-scaffolds, where if a transition metal                nucleation agent is present, inhibiting activation                of the transition metal nucleation agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The preparation of                  graphene is performed without a transition metal                  on or adjacent to the surface of the cathode. The                  electrolysis anode and molten carbonate                  electrolyte do not include a transition metal.                  The electrolysis is performed in the absence of a                  transition metal, lithium oxide, or both. The                  electrolysis cathode is having the carbon                  nanomaterial is used as an anode to produce                  graphene. The electrolysis cathode having the                  carbon nanomaterial is cooled prior to performing                  the exfoliation. The electrolyzed carbonate is                  replenished by addition of carbon dioxide. The                  source of the added carbon dioxide is one of air,                  pressurized carbon dioxide (CO2), concentrated                  CO2, a power generating industrial process, an                  iron generating industrial process, a steel                  generating industrial process, a cement formation                  process, an ammonia formation industrial process,                  an aluminum formation industrial process, a                  manufacturing process, an oven, a smokestack, or                  an internal combustion engines. The electrolysis                  cathode stainless steel, cast iron, a nickel                  alloy, or a material that resists corrosion in                  the presence of the molten carbonate electrolyte,                  or any combination of the foregoing. The                  coulombic efficiency is greater than about 80%.                  Preferred Process: The electrolysis is performed                  by heating a carbonate electrolyte to obtain a                  molten carbonate electrolyte, and disposing the                  molten carbonate electrolyte between an                  electrolysis anode and an electrolysis cathode in                  a cell. An electrical current is applied to the                  electrolysis cathode and the electrolysis anode                  in the cell to electrolyze the carbonate and                  generated carbon nano-platelets on the                  electrolysis cathode. Preferred Components: The                  electrolysis cathode is coated with zinc. The                  carbon nanomaterial comprises carbon                  nanoplatelets. The carbon nanoplatelets comprise                  less than about 125 graphene layers. The molten                  carbonate electrolyte comprises an alkali metal                  carbonate, an alkali earth metal carbonate, or                  any combination. The alkali metal carbonate or                  alkali earth metal carbonate is lithium                  carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate,                  rubidium carbonate, cesium carbonate, francium                  carbonate, beryllium carbonate, magnesium                  carbonate, calcium carbonate, strontium                  carbonate, barium carbonate, radium carbonate, or                  any mixture. The molten carbonate electrolyte                  comprises lithium carbonate. The exfoliation                  electrolyte comprises an aqueous solution of                  ammonium sulfate. The graphene produced comprises                  less than 10 graphene layers.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E05-U05C; E05-U05D; E11-A01; E11-D02; E11-E02; E11-F03; E11-N

PT P
PN CN111763694-A
TI Coupling production capacity of high-temperature hydrogen production and microalgae oil production by performing continuous high-temperature hydrogen production reaction, transporting microalgae, and collecting microalgae.
AU LIU B
   YIN T
   DU J
   CAO G
   XIE G
   XING D
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2020A1923N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for coupling production capacity of high-temperature hydrogen production and microalgae oil production involves (i) using the continuously stirred tank reactors (CSTR) anaerobic reactor as the high-temperature hydrogen production reactor, and performing continuous high-temperature hydrogen production reaction, (ii) during the high-temperature hydrogen production reaction, separating and treating the fermentation broth by CSTR anaerobic reactor continuously transported to the microalgae growth reactor, (iii) transporting the microalgae and its culture liquid in the microalgae growth reactor and circulating by the circulation pipeline, and (iv) collecting the microalgae and hydrogen in the microalgae oil-producing reactor of circulating oil-producing culture, and extracting the oil in the microalgae.
   USE - The method is useful for coupling production capacity of high-temperature hydrogen production and microalgae oil production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for coupling production capacity of high-temperature hydrogen production and microalgae oil production involves (i) using continuously stirred tank reactor (CSTR) anaerobic reactor as high-temperature hydrogen production reactor, passing wastewater into high-temperature hydrogen production reactor, inoculating sludge from secondary sedimentation tank after high-temperature acclimation, cultivating at 50-60 degrees C without light, and performing continuous high-temperature hydrogen production reaction under airtight and anaerobic conditions, (ii) during high-temperature hydrogen production reaction, separating and treating the fermentation broth by CSTR anaerobic reactor continuously transported to the microalgae growth reactor, and continuously transporting the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen produced in the high-temperature hydrogen production reactor below the liquid level of the fermentation broth in the microalgae growth reactor, continuously passing the fermentation product produced in the high-temperature hydrogen production reactor into the microalgae growth reactor for one day, inoculating the microalgae growth reactor with microalgae, and culturing the microalgae, (iii) after culturing the microalgae for 3-5 days, transporting the microalgae and its culture liquid in the microalgae growth reactor to the microalgae oil-producing reactor for cyclic oil-producing culture, transporting the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen above the liquid surface in the algae growth reactor below the liquid surface in the microalgae oil-producing reactor, and communicating the microalgae oil-producing reactor which is provided outside the microalgae oil-producing reactor to the inner cavity of the microalgae oil-producing reactor, circulating by the circulation pipeline makes the microalgae fermentation liquid, and (iv) collecting the microalgae and hydrogen in the microalgae oil-producing reactor after 5-7 days, and extracting the oil in the microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The high-temperature acclimation culture in step (i) is performed at 50 degrees C for 5-8 days. The pH value of the fermentation liquid is adjusted to 5-7 in the process of continuously conveying the fermentation liquid separated and processed by the CSTR anaerobic reactor to the micro-algae growth reactor, and then the UV sterilization is performed for 5-8 minutes. The step (ii) of culturing microalgae is carried out under the condition of 25-45 degrees C, illuminating and stirring. The oil-producing culture conditions in step (iii) has 15-40 degrees C temperature, illuminating, and sealing. The microalgae in step (ii) have the seed liquid in the last stage of the logarithmic growth stage or the early stage of the stationary phase. The inoculating amount of the microalgae in the step (ii) is 5-10% of the total volume of the fermentation liquid. The microalga is collected by centrifuging method or filtering method. The extraction in step (iv) adopts chloroform-methanol method, Soxhlet extraction method, normal hexane-isopropanol method, normal hexane-ethanol method or ether-petroleum ether method.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - The solar power supply system supplies a stable output voltage of 220V during the process of coupling high-temperature hydrogen production and microalgae oil production, the solar power supply system has solar battery module, solar controller, inverter and storage battery, the solar cell components, solar controller, inverter and storage battery are connected in sequence.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN EP3705170-A1; WO2020182684-A1; AU2020233957-A1; CN113766962-A; EP3934792-A1; US2022177827-A1; JP2022523592-W
TI Biogas plant for obtaining biomethane from raw biogas, comprises fermenter for production of raw biogas from biomass, thermal engine, and biogas treatment plant including compression unit and gas separation unit.
AU BECKER J
   WIEGERS J
AE HITACHI ZOSEN INOVA AG (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN INOVA AG (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN INOVA AG (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN INOVA AG (HITF-C)
GA 202087555T
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas plant (10) comprises a fermenter (12) for the production of raw biogas (26) from biomass (21), a thermal engine (14) for the production of mechanical and/or thermal energy (39), and a biogas treatment plant (16) including a compression unit (33) and a gas separation unit (32) for the separation of the raw biogas into a 1st and 2nd gas stream (27,29). The 1st gas stream consists of a product gas (28) that is enriched in biomethane (17) with respect to the composition of the raw biogas and the 2nd gas stream consists of a residual gas (30) that is enriched in carbon dioxide with respect to the composition of the raw biogas. The thermal engine includes a combustion unit (19), which is provided with a combustion gas (35) for combustion, and an exhaust gas treatment unit (18), which is provided with an exhaust gas (31) for treatment. At least one of the combustion gas and the exhaust gas includes fraction of the residual gas.
   USE - The biogas plant is useful for obtaining biomethane from raw biogas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biogas plant has an improved energy and economical balance and at the same time meets the requirements with respect to purity of the gas streams obtained from the separation of the raw biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for obtaining biomethane from raw biogas, which involves: (a) compressing raw biogas from a fermenter with the aid of a compression unit; (b) separating the compressed raw biogas with the aid of a gas separation unit into a product gas that is enriched in biomethane in relation to the raw biogas and into a residual gas that is enriched in carbon dioxide in relation to the raw biogas; (c) delivering fraction of the residual gas as portion of a combustion gas to a combustion unit and/or as portion of an exhaust gas to an exhaust gas treatment unit, which are portions of a thermal engine and (d) combustion of the combustion gas in the combustion unit and/or treatment of the exhaust gas in the exhaust gas treatment unit produces energy that is at least partly used for operating the compression unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the biogas plant.
   Biogas plant (10)
   Fermenter (12)
   Thermal engine (14)
   Biogas treatment plant (16)
   Biomethane (17)
   Exhaust gas treatment unit (18)
   Combustion unit (19)
   Biomass (21)
   Raw biogas (26)
   Gas streams (27,29)
   Product gas (28)
   Residual gas (30)
   Exhaust gas (31)
   Gas separation unit (32)
   Compression unit (33)
   Combustion gas (35)
   Thermal energy (39)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The combustion gas includes a variable amount of natural gas (36) and/or raw biogas of the fermenter and/or product gas. The exhaust gas includes exhaust gasses from the combustion unit. At least one of the combustion gas and the exhaust gas consists of gasses from the biogas plant. Preferred Parameter: The gas separation unit has a methane slip of greater than or equal to 0.25%, preferably greater than or equal to 1.5%, more preferably greater than or equal to 3%. Preferred Composition: The combustion gas includes a biomethane concentration of greater than or equal to 10%, preferably greater than or equal to 20%, more preferably greater than or equal to 30%. Preferred Process: All residual gas from the gas separation unit is delivered directly or indirectly to the thermal engine, preferably without prior treatment. Thermal energy produced during the compression of the raw biogas and/or by the thermal engine is used for operating the fermenter. At least portion of the energy produced within the thermal engine and/or the compression unit is used for operating the fermenter.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC D05-A04A; D05-C14; H06-A02; H06-A04; H06-P; H09-F03; J01-E02B

PT P
PN CN111208173-A
TI Method for calculating conductivity of saturated leachate of saline soil in southern Xinjiang, comprises calculating the saturated conductivity of the saline soil extract in the southern Xinjiang area with specific ratio of soil water.
AU LI X
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 202049726M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for calculating conductivity of saturated leachate of saline soil in southern Xinjiang, comprises calculating the saturated conductivity EC0 of the saline soil extract in the southern Xinjiang area with ratio of soil water to water 1:1, 1:2.5, 1:5, EC1:1, EC1:2.5, EC1:5 respectively.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating conductivity of saturated leachate of saline soil in southern Xinjiang.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the disadvantages of the preparation of saturated soil leaching solution, e.g. the difficulty of mastering the saturation standard, the cumbersome preparation process, the small amount of solution, and the high analysis cost; has simple preparation process, time and labor saving, cost savings, and sufficient solution volume.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for calculating the conductivity of saturated leachate of saline soil in southern Xinjiang, comprising (i) taking 250 g soil sample, placing in a 500 ml plastic cup, slowly adding carbon dioxide-free distilled water, stirring while adding water and constantly shaking the plastic cup on the experimental table until the soil is completely saturated, judging standard for saturated mud with the mud shining when reflecting light, tilting the mud flows slightly when the plastic cup, allowing to stand saturated mud for 16 hours, filtering with a Buchner funnel to obtain a saturated extract, preparing a leachate with a soil to water ratio of 1:5, taking 10 g of soil sample in a 200 ml Erlenmeyer flask and adding 50 ml of carbon dioxide-free distilled water, shaking it on a reciprocating shaker for 15 minutes (150-180 times/minute), allowing to stand for 1 hour, shaking it for 5 minutes, and utilizing a Buchner funnel for suction filtration to obtain an extract; (ii) determine the saturated water content of the soil using the drying method, taking aluminum box mass, taking a small amount of prepared saturated mud into the aluminum box and weighing, placing in an oven to dry to constant mass at 105 degrees C and calculating the soil saturated water content as given in the specifications; (iii) measuring the EC value of soil saturated leachate and 1:5 leachate by DDS-307 conductivity meter to determine the type of soil salinity, determining the anions of the 1:5 extract, determining by dual indicator-neutralization titration, determining chloride ion by silver nitrate titration and determining by indirect titration with EDTA; and (iv) calculating the conductivity of leachate with different soil-water ratios as given in the specifications.

PN CN110931762-A; CN110931762-B
TI Preparing nano-copper oxalate three-dimensional graphene anode material used in lithium ion battery, by heating lithium sheet in tube furnace with gas to obtain graphene product, pulverizing, soaking in nitric acid and adding copper powder.
AU WANG L
   DENG J
   LIU P
   XU H
   CHEN H
   CHANG K
   GAO R
   WAN W
AE SHANGHAI YULING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202025685C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nano-copper oxalate composite three-dimensional graphene anode material, involves (a) putting the lithium sheet in a ceramic boat in the inert gas glove box, putting the ceramic boat in a tube furnace, and evacuating the tube furnace until the pressure in the tube furnace is reduced to 10 Pa, (b) filling the tube furnace with carbon dioxide gas, and heating the tube furnace to obtain a crude three-dimensional graphene product, (c) pulverizing the crude three-dimensional graphene product to obtain a powdery crude three-dimensional graphene product, (d) soaking the powdery three-dimensional graphene crude product in concentrated nitric acid, (e) adding metal copper powder or copper salt into the suspension and fully stirring, and (f) continuously magnetically stirring the suspension and slowly dripping absolute ethyl alcohol.
   USE - The method is used for preparing nano-copper oxalate three-dimensional graphene anode material, which is used in a lithium ion battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared nano-copper oxalate three-dimensional graphene anode material improves the coulombic efficiency of the obtained three-dimensional graphene anode material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing nano-copper oxalate composite three-dimensional graphene anode material, involves (a) putting the lithium sheet in a ceramic boat in an inert gas glove box, putting the ceramic boat in a tube furnace, and evacuating the tube furnace until the pressure in the tube furnace is reduced to 10 Pa, (b) filling the tube furnace with carbon dioxide gas, and heating the tube furnace to 550-600 degrees C for 24-48 hours to obtain a crude three-dimensional graphene product, (c) pulverizing the crude three-dimensional graphene product to obtain a powdery crude three-dimensional graphene product, (d) soaking the powdery three-dimensional graphene crude product into concentrated nitric acid for more than 2 hours, where the mass ratio of the three-dimensional graphene crude product to concentrated nitric acid is more than 1:2, (e) adding metal copper powder or copper salt into the suspension and fully stirring, so that the mass ratio of the crude product of the three-dimensional graphene to the copper in the suspension reaches 100:(1-5) and (f) continuously magnetically stirring the suspension and slowly dripping absolute ethyl alcohol until the molecular weight ratio of copper to ethanol in the suspension reaches 1:(1-2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (b), carbon dioxide gas is charged into the tubular furnace until the air pressure value in the tubular furnace reaches 0.3-0.5 MPa, and the tubular furnace is heated to 550-600 degrees C at a heating speed of 5-10 degrees C/minute, and the temperature in the tubular furnace is kept at 550-600 degrees C for 24-48 hours to obtain the three-dimensional graphene crude product. The crude powdered three-dimensional graphene product in step (c) is a crude three-dimensional graphene product with a mesh number of 100 mesh or more. In step (d), the powdered three-dimensional graphene crude product obtained in (c) is put in concentrated nitric acid with a concentration of 68% for 12 hours, and the mass ratio of the three-dimensional graphene crude product to concentrated nitric acid is 1:(4-5). The copper salt in step (e) comprises copper nitrate, copper carbonate, copper sulfate, and/or copper chloride.

TI Foot bath effervescent tablet comprises folium artemisiae argyi, orange peel, Arnebia euchroma, Chia Pee, transdermal absorbent, sodium bicarbonate, citric acid, microcrystalline cellulose and hydroxypropyl cellulose.
AU ZHENG Y
AE ZHENG Y (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 2020195209
AB    NOVELTY - Foot bath effervescent tablet comprises 1-5 pts. wt. folium artemisiae argyi, 3-8 pts. wt. orange peel, 4-6 pts. wt. Arnebia euchroma, 5-6 pts. wt. Wu Chia Pee, 1-2 pts. wt. transdermal absorbent, 30-60 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, 10-40 pts. wt. citric acid, 3-9 pts. wt. microcrystalline cellulose, 2-6 pts. wt. low-substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose, 1-5 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 1-2 pts. wt. magnesium stearate, 0.5-2 pts. wt. essence, 0.5-3 pts. wt. sodium lauryl sulfate, 0.5-2 pts. wt. pigment, 3-10 pts. wt. beta -cyclodextrin.
   USE - Foot bath effervescent tablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The foot bath effervescent tablet can not only play a role of bacteriostasis and antibacterial, but also can make aroma during use, thus improves the comfort in the foot bath, and pigments can play a role in protecting the privacy of users, uses a dry granulation process to process effervescent tablets, which can prevent the problem of moisture absorption, thus improves the industrial production efficiency, and can be widely used in the field of health care medicine technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the foot bath effervescent tablet, which involves:
   (A) taking folium artemisiae argyi and orange peel, extracting volatile oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under 20-35 MPa pressure, and 15-25 MPa separator pressure, and 15-45 L/hour carbon dioxide flow rate, at 25-50 degrees C temperature for 3-6 hours, collecting volatile oil;
   (B) combining the medicine residue with remaining traditional Chinese medicine and adding water for extracting for 2 times, adding 10 times amount of water for the first time, and adding 8 times amount of water for the second time, each time for 2.0 hours, filtering through a 200-mesh sieve, and concentrating the filtrate at 60 degrees C temperature under reduced pressure, and maintaining 1.10 relative density, then drying to obtain the extract powder;
   (C) embedding the volatile oil with beta -cyclodextrin to obtain the volatile oil inclusion compound;
   (D) sorting, purifying, washing, drying and breaking the wall into a fine powder, adding 6 times amount of 75% ethanol for 24 hours, filtering, drying under reduced pressure, and crushing to obtain a dry powder; and
   (E) mixing the volatile oil, dry cream powder, alcohol-extracted dry cream powder, sodium bicarbonate, citric acid, microcrystalline cellulose, low-substituted hydroxypropyl base cellulose, white sugar, magnesium stearate, flavor, sodium lauryl sulfate and color mixture, dry granulation, and tableting to make effervescent tablets.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Foot bath effervescent tablet comprises transdermal absorbent containing azone, borneol, menthol and Eucalyptus oil. Preferred Conditions: The dry granulation is applied in process. The volatile oils of folium artemisiae argyi and orange peel are extracted by supercritical extraction method, and the volatile oil is encapsulated by using an inclusion technique. The Xinjiang radix arnebiae and cortex periplocae are crushed into fine powder by a wall-breaking crushing technique. The product specification is 10 g/pieces.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

PN CN110833842-A
TI Micro-nano-copper-ruthenium composite phosphide catalyst used for carbon dioxide nitrogen formylation comprises copper-ruthenium composite phosphorus oxide as active component highly dispersed on surface of hydroxyapatite as carrier.
AU CUI D
   ZHAO K
   TONG Q
   CAI T
   YUAN J
   JIN C
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 202018858M
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-nano-copper-ruthenium composite phosphide catalyst comprises hydroxyapatite as carrier and copper-ruthenium composite phosphorus oxide as active component, which is highly dispersed on hydroxyapatite surface.
   USE - The micro-nano-copper-ruthenium composite phosphide catalyst is used for carbon dioxide nitrogen formylation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high loading quantity, uniform dispersion, single active component, and simple preparation, and is easy for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the catalyst, where the electronegativity of the surface of the carrier is controlled by adjusting the pH of the solution, so that the carrier and a target copper ruthenium ion form weak repulsion, the weak repulsion action ensures that copper and ruthenium completely enter carrier lattices in the ion exchange process and realize uniform and single dispersion, removing possible weakly adsorbed copper and ruthenium ions on the surface by ultrasonic treatment, and under a specific condition, forming a monodisperse copper-ruthenium composite phosphide catalyst in situ by a temperature programming reduction method, and optimizing the copper ruthenium ratio promotes the synergistic effect between the copper ruthenium ratio, comprising preparing hydroxyapatite carrier by weighing a calcium source and a phosphorus source, and respectively dissolving the calcium source and phosphorus source in deionized water to ensure that the concentrations of the calcium source and the phosphorus source are 0.2-0.5 mol/L, adjusting pH at 2-4 with an acid solution, mixing a calcium source and a phosphorus source according to a molar ratio of 5:3 under the condition of vigorous stirring, adjusting pH at 10-12 with ammonia water under stirring, reacting in water bath at 40-90 degrees C for 4-10 hours, standing and aging for 18-24 hours, filtering, washing with deionized water, and freeze-drying to obtain hydroxyapatite carrier; and preparing a micro-nano-copper-ruthenium composite phosphide catalyst by adjusting the molar ratio of ruthenium to copper at 1:1-3, dispersing hydroxyapatite in the ruthenium-copper mixed solution at pH 7, where the mass ratio of ruthenium to hydroxyapatite is 0.1-5%, stirring at room temperature for 2-5 hours, centrifuging, dispersing in water solution again, ultrasonically treating for 15 minutes, repeating for 3-5 times, drying at 50-100 degrees C, carrying out temperature programming at 400 degrees C for reduction for 2 hours in hydrogen atmosphere, and continuously heating at 600-800 degrees C for reduction for 4-6 hours to obtain the micro-nano-copper-ruthenium composite phosphide catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The calcium source is calcium nitrate and/or calcium chloride. The phosphorus source is ammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium phosphate and/or phosphoric acid. The acid solution is nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, and/or phosphoric acid. The ruthenium-copper mixed solution is a mixed solution of ruthenium chloride and copper nitrate and/or copper chloride. The hydrogen atmosphere is 5-10% hydrogen and argon mixed gas. The ruthenium-copper mixed solution has total metal concentration of 0.2-30 mmol/L.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
