TI Tumor-triggered targeted liposome comprises phospholipid, cholesterol, long-chain pegylation phospholipids containing pH-sensitive imine bonds, folate-functionalized pegylation lipids, and ammonium bicarbonate.
AU ZOU T
   CHEN S
   LI L
   XIA T
   LI Y
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 2019A3071K
AB    NOVELTY - Tumor-triggered targeted liposome comprises phospholipid, cholesterol, long-chain pegylation phospholipids (PE-imine-PEG) containing pH-sensitive imine bonds, folate-functionalized pegylation lipids (1,2-distearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine-polyethylene glycol (PEG)-folate), and ammonium bicarbonate (ABC).
   USE - Tumor-triggered targeted liposome.
   ADVANTAGE - The tumor-triggered targeted liposome has the characteristics of long circulation in the blood, and open its targeting characteristics in the tumor microenvironment. Through the action of the folate ligand-receptor, it can enter tumor cells and generate carbon dioxide (CO2) bubbles in elevated temperatures or acidic endosomes/lysosomes. The generated carbon dioxide bubbles create permeable defects in the phospholipid bilayer, quickly release the drug in the cells, and destroy the tumor cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for preparing the PE-imine-PEG, which involves dissolving phosphatidylethanolamine and methoxypolyglycol aldehyde (mPEG-CHO) in a solvent of 0.5-5000 mL, adding volume ratio of methanol and water as 40-80:20-60:1-15 ratio in order to obtain solution A, adding 0.01-50 mL of triethylamine into the solution A, then stirring at 20-60 degrees C temperature for at least 1 hour, filling the solution into a dialysis bag with a cut-off molecular weight more than 1000 Da, and using the solvent for dialysis for more than 1 hour, and then the continuing dialysis for more than 1 hour with a 0.001-1 mol/L sodium bicarbonate solution to obtain solution B, and freeze-drying the solution B for at least 5 hours to obtain PE-imine-PEG; and
   (2) a method for preparing the tumor-triggered targeted liposome, which involves adding the phospholipid, cholesterol, PE-imine-PEG and DSPE-PEG-folate, dissolving in 0.5-1000 mL chloroform, and evaporating chloroform to generate a lipid film, adding ABC aqueous solution into the lipid membrane, and performing ultrasonic treatment at 5-37 degrees C temperature for more than 1 minute to hydrate the lipid membrane to form a suspension, extruding the suspension through a 800-nm, 400-nm, 200-nm, and 100-nm polycarbonate filter membrane in sequence at 5-37 degrees C temperature, charging the extruded suspension into a dialysis bag with a cut-off molecular weight more than 1000 Da, and performing the dialysis in a buffer solution having a pH of 7.0-8.5 at 5.0 /asterisk 10-3 to 1.0 mol/L for more than 1 hour to remove ABC.

TI Multi-type air cleaner for use in indoor spaces e.g. houses, has water tank installed on inner bracket, handle formed on upper portion of water tank, and spay nozzle installed on water tank to supply humidification spray and phytoncide.
AU JANG B
AE JANG B (JANG-Individual)
GA 2019A2389W
AB    NOVELTY - The cleaner has a top cover (116) installed at an upper portion of an upper housing. A clean air supply grill (G) supplies clean air supplied through a three-way duct (150). A humidification combined phytoncide generating unit (180) is installed in a humidifier unit mounting unit (116a) to generate humidification and phytoncide. An anion generating unit (190) is installed at a side of the grill for supplying the clean air. An inner bracket is detachably installed in an outer bracket (181) for storing a humidifier generator and a phytoncide generator. A water tank is detachably installed on the inner bracket and stores water. A handle (184) is formed on an upper portion of the water tank. An angle control spray nozzle is installed on the water tank to supply humidification spray and phytoncide.
   USE - Multi-type air cleaner for use in indoor spaces e.g. houses, offices, and underground spaces.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaner detects air pollution degree, humidity, fine dust or carbon dioxide in room in real-time be displayed on a display window to inform indoor air condition to a user clearly and quickly, so that the humidification combined phytoncide generating unit can be operated, and negative ions can be generated to keep room clean at times. The cleaner can improve filtration efficiency without air leakage by filtering fine dust or carbon dioxide contained in air using a dual-filter. The cleaner utilizes an anion emission sensor so as to ensure that anion release is normal by confirming whether or not anion release normal. The cleaner can supply a large amount of clean air at same time by blowing the clean air to three discharge portions through the three-way duct with a turbo fan so as to quickly implement indoor air purifying. The cleaner ensures that a guide bracket is installed at the discharge portion formed upwardly from the discharge portions, so that air can be smoothly discharged by guiding the clean air upward.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for controlling operation of a multi-type air cleaner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view of a multi-type air cleaner.

GA 201990284B
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene having a polynitrogen coordination                structure, is claimed comprises 1-5 atomic layers                comprising e.g. carbon, oxygen, nitrogen, hydrogen                and other elements a catalytic active site                comprising metal element of iron, cobalt, nickel,                and the graphene has multi-nitrogen coordination                transition metal and the nitrogen-to-metal                coordination is 4-5.
   USE - The graphene is useful for electrocatalyst in                the electrocatalytic reaction of CO2, O2 and                formaldehyde (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene has high electrocatalytic                performance for carbon dioxide, oxygen and                formaldehyde, easy dispersibility, easy processing                and easy functioning, and is easy to realize                functions of electric conduction, heat dissipation,                infrared heat release and electromagnetic shielding                and has comparable catalytic activity to precious                metal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing graphene having a polynitrogen                coordination structure comprising (i) providing a                slat and a liquid containing a graphite component,                where the mass of the liquid is 30-100 times the                mass of the slab containing the graphite component                and liquid refers to the electrolyte in each of the                following steps, (ii) sequentially placing the                graphite-containing slats in different electrolytes                for electrolysis and electrolysis, (iii) taking out                the electrolysis system, high-speed emulsification                shearing, ultrasonic dispersion, sanding machine                sanding treatment ro obtain a uniform slurry, (iv)                drying slurry, calcining, where the calcination                atmosphere is argon, and calcininf at 800 degrees                C, naturally cooling with the furnace; and # Use                method of graphene having a polynitrogen                coordination structure comprises dispersing                graphene in a solvent, mechanically treating by                shearing and sanding, coating, spraying, printing                on the substrate to obtain a functional film or                coating. The resulting functional film or coating                has excellent acid and alkali resistance, resistant                to 5M hydrochloric acid, 5 M nitric acid, no                significant change after 36 hours of immersion,                withstands 5M soadium hydroxide , 5M aqueous                potassium hydroxide solution, no significant change                after 180 hours of immersion to obtained functional                film has a thickness of 10 mu m to 1 mm and surface                resistance is between 1 ohm and 10 kilo ohms and                resulting functional film has excellent thermal                conductivity, heating to 100-120 degrees C in 30                seconds, resulting functional film has a                significant electromagnetic shielding effect in the                frequency range of 300-20 GHz, the effectiveness of                electromagnetic shielding is 40-90dB, the thickness                of the film is 40-100 microns, the functional film                has a elimination property against formaldehyde,                power on 20V, time 12 hours and indoor formaldehyde                concentration of 12 square meters was reduced by                50%.

TI Use of Magnolia officinalis oil for preparing a medicine for treating or preventing brain function and its disorder comprising a health product for improving brain function and its disorder.
AU XU M
   HAN J
   CHEN S
   CAO H
   LUO D
   ZHENG S
   LI Y
   GU P
   SONG H
AE DONGGUAN HUAIPU PLANT ESSENTIAL OIL CO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201984202S
AB    NOVELTY - Use of Magnolia officinalis oil for preparing a medicine for treating or preventing brain function and its disorder comprising a health product for improving brain function and its disorder, is claimed.
   USE - The oil is useful for preparing a medicine for treating or preventing brain function and its disorder, preferably brain function and its disorders includes anxiety, depression, insomnia, epilepsy, mania, schizophrenia, spasticity, bipolar disorder, stroke, Huntington ' s disease, brain trauma, spinal cord injury and neurodegenerative disease, preferably neurodegenerative diseases are intended to include Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil regulates mechanisms by which multiple molecules, e.g. neurotransmitters, act synergistically on multiple targets, has good medicinal parameters, which can pass through the blood-brain barrier, has toxicological safety, metabolic stability, alternative half-life and small substitution characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the Magnolia officinalis oil for (i) selecting the dried Magnolia officinalis rhizome, placing the pile for 1-2 days and pulverizing to 30-60 mesh coarse particles; (ii) immersing the coarse particles in 7-9 times by mass of hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid aqueous solution having a pH of 5-6, and extracting the formic acid to obtain leaching mixture; (iii) adding cellulase and pectinase into the leaching mixture obtained in step (ii), the mass ratio of cellulase, pectinase and extract mixture is 0.5-1:0.1-0.5:100 and digesting at 35-37 degrees C for 2-3 hours; and (iv) (iv.a) adding the mixture obtained by the step (iii) into a steam distillation extraction apparatus, refluxing at 100-120 degrees C for 5-7 hours to collect distillate, cooling to room temperature, separating oil and water using oil and water separator and drying the oil layer with anhydrous sodium sulfate and separating to obtain Magnolia officinalis oil; (iv.b) adding the mixed solution after the hydrolysis in step (iii) into a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus and extracting at a temperature of 45-48 degrees C, pressure is 13.70-17.80 MPa, time is 50-100 minutes and the rotation speed of carbon dioxide is 25-35 kg/hour, collecting Magnolia officinalis oil and refining in a molecular distillation apparatus, the feed temperature is 40-45 degrees C, the injection speed is 480-530 ml/hour and the evaporating temperature is 110-115 degrees C, the two-stage evaporation temperature is 60-65 degrees C, and collecting the second-stage distillation portion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The Magnolia officinalis is Magnolia officinalis or Magnolia family Magnolia plant concave leaf Magnolia officinalis. The Magnolia officinalis oil is analyzed by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry and the ratio of the components includes alpha -Eucalyptus oil of greater than 10 wt.%, beta -Eucalyptus oil of greater than 9 wt.%, anisene of greater than 5 wt.% and borneol acetate of greater than 5 wt.%. The medicine includes oral preparation and non-oral administration preparation. The oral preparation includes tablets, pills, capsules, granules, microcapsule tablets, suspensions, dropping pills and oral liquids. The parenteral administration preparation includes an inhalant, an injection, an aerosol, a suppository, and a subcutaneous administration dosage form. The health products includes cosmetics, baths, and aromatizers.
   ACTIVITY - Tranquilizer; Antidepressant; Hypnotic; Anticonvulsant; Antimanic; Neuroleptic; Musclerelaxant; Vasotropic; Nootropic; Vulnerary; Neuroprotective; Antiparkinsonian.

AU ZHENG H
   QIU P
   CHEN Z
AE ACCURATE INT BIOTECHNOLOGY GUANGZHOU CO (ACCU-Non-standard)
GA 201973598K
AB    NOVELTY - Culture medium comprises 40-60X dilution B27, 1-5 mM N-acetylysteine, 1-100 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 50-200 ng/ml Noggin, 200-1000 ng/ml R-spondin 1 or 10-50% (v/v) conditioned medium, 200-1000 nM A83-01, 50-200 ng/ml fibroblast growth factor 10, 10-50 ng/ml fibroblast growth factor 2, 1-20 mM nicotinamide, 1-20 mu M Y-27632, 0.1-2 mu M prostaglandin E2, 1-20 mu M SB202190 and 1-5 mu g/ml heparin.
   USE - The culture medium is useful for pleural and ascites organs (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medium maintains tissue cell specificity, stem cell characteristics, high genotyping, and highly similar tissue morphology, meets the needs of scientific research in vitro drug-related organ guidance and provides a good and beneficial option for patients with drug medication guidance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) culturing cancerous pleural and ascites organ comprising collecting pleural fluids, adding preservation solution, shaking the shaker, filtering cells, centrifuging, removing supernatant, adding Dulbecco's modified eagle medium-F-12, centrifuging, and removing supernatant, cell counting, adding glue, dropping in middle of well of well plate, placing culture dish, solidifying glue, where the medium is added to each well and culturing in a cell culture incubator; and
   (2) testing drug sensitivity of pleural fluids comprising aspirating culture medium in well plate of organ-type, adding 200 mu l of phosphate-buffered saline, rinsing, adding 200 mu l digest, digesting at 37 degrees C for 5 minutes, and terminating digestion with Dulbecco's modified eagle medium-F-12 comprising 5% fetal bovine serum, centrifuging (4 degrees C, 200 g, 2 minutes), removing supernatant, re-suspending cells by adding 10 ml of Dulbecco's modified eagle medium-F-12, and filtering cells through 40 mu m cell sieve, counting cells, centrifuging (4 degrees C, 200 g, 2 minutes), and removing supernatant, adding glue (comprising 10% matrigel and 40% type I collagen), 5000 cells per 30 mu l, dropping in middle of well of 96-well plate, placing the culture dish to 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 10 minutes, coagulating glue, adding 80 mu l medium to each well, and culturing in a cell culture incubator at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 3 days, adding drug to be tested, continuously culturing for 5 days and performing cell viability test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The culture medium comprises 50X dilution B27, 2.5 mM N-acetylysteine, 10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 100 ng/ml Noggin, 1500 ng/ml R-spondin 1 or 30% (v/v) conditioned medium, 500 nM A83-01, 100 ng/ml fibroblast growth factor 10, 25 ng/ml fibroblast growth factor 2, 10 mM nicotinamide, 10 mu M Y-27632, 1 mu M prostaglandin E2, 10 mu M SB202190 and 2.5 mu g/ml heparin. The preservation solution comprises 0.5X B27, 5 mM hepes, 0.1% dispaseII, 10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 0.5X nitrogen, 5 mM nicotinamide, 5 mu M Y-27632, 0.5 mu M A83-01 and 5 ~Mm SB202190. The composition of the above digestive solutions is Dulbecco's modified eagle medium-F-12 comprises 0.1% trypsin, 0.2% dispase II, 0.1% Collagenase III, 2.5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.5 M hepes and 10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor. Preferred Method: In the method, collecting 100 ml pleural fluids from the tumor patients in the aseptic environment, adding 20 ml of preservation solution, transferring to 37 degrees C, shaking at 200 revolutions/minute for 20 minute, filtering cells with 100 mu m cell sieve, centrifuging (4 degrees C, 200 g, 5 minutes), removing supernatant, re-suspending in 10 ml Dulbecco's modified eagle medium-F-12, centrifuging (4 degrees C, 200 g, 5 minutes), removing the supernatant, cell counting, adding glue (comprising 20% matrigel and 80% type I collagen), 5000 cells per 30 mu l, dropping in middle of 48-well plate well, placing culture dish to 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 10 minutes, coagulating glue liquid, adding 150 mu l of medium to each well, culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in a cell culture incubator and changing medium every 3-4 days.
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen.

PN CN109354044-A; CN109354044-B
TI Extracting lithium from lithium by-product sodium            sulfate from spodumene sulfuric acid process comprises            e.g. mixing lithium-containing lithium by-product            sodium sulfate with the coal powder, calcining, and            extracting calcined material.
AU DENG H
   YAN X
   CHEN G
AE TIANQI LITHIUM SHEHONG CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201919208K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium from lithium by-product                sodium sulfate from spodumene sulfuric acid process                comprises (i) mixing lithium-containing lithium                by-product sodium sulfate with the coal powder, and                calcining at 800-1200 degrees C under an                oxygen-containing condition to obtain a calcined                material, (ii) extracting the calcined material                with water, and filtering to obtain a filter                residue, and a filtrate, (iii) extracting the                filter residue with dilute sulfuric acid,                separating the solid solution to obtain a lithium                sulfate solution, and a solid residue, returning                the lithium sulfate solution to a lithium salt                production line, and the solid residue is coal,                (iv) subjecting the filtrate to evaporation,                concentration, when the solid is precipitated,                carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a                solid product, and a primary mother liquid, the                solid product is sodium sulfide hydrate, and (v)                introducing carbon dioxide into the primary mother                liquid, and carrying out solid-liquid                separation.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lithium                from lithium by-product sodium sulfate from                spodumene sulfuric acid process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the difficulty of                lithium recovery; lithium recovery rate reaches                more than 95%; converts low-value                lithium-containing lithium by-product sodium                sulfate to higher value sodium sulfide; has iron                content is less than 0.0010% and low impurity                content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting lithium from lithium by-product                sodium sulfate from spodumene sulfuric acid process                comprises (i) uniformly mixing the                lithium-containing lithium by-product sodium                sulfate with the coal powder, and calcining at                800-1200 degrees C under an oxygen-containing                condition to obtain a calcined material, (ii)                extracting the calcined material with water, and                filtering to obtain a filter residue, and a                filtrate, (iii) extracting the filter residue with                dilute sulfuric acid, separating the solid solution                to obtain a lithium sulfate solution, and a solid                residue, returning the lithium sulfate solution to                a lithium salt production line, and the solid                residue is coal, (iv) subjecting the filtrate                obtained in the step (ii) to evaporation,                concentration, when the solid is precipitated,                carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a                solid product, and a primary mother liquid, the                solid product is sodium sulfide hydrate, and (v)                introducing carbon dioxide into the primary mother                liquid obtained in the step (iv) according to the                lithium content, carrying out solid-liquid                separation to obtain secondary mother liquid, and                lithium carbonate, returning the secondary mother                liquid to the step (iv) for evaporating, and                concentrating, and returning the lithium carbonate                is to the lithium salt production line.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the                  mass ratio of lithium-containing lithium                  by-product sodium sulfate, and coal powder is                  100:20-30. Preferred Method: In the step (i)                  calcining for 20-60 minutes. In the step (ii),                  controlling the temperature to 40-80 degrees C                  when extracted with water. In the step (iv)                  evaporating, and concentrating at 75-105 degrees                  C.

TI Producing synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides by partial oxidation of carbonaceous fuel, comprises supplying carbonaceous fuel, oxidant and moderator, mixing, discharging synthesis gas and reacting soot particles.
AU WALTER S
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201913238S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides by partial oxidation of carbonaceous fuel, comprises: (a) supplying the carbonaceous fuel, the oxygen-containing oxidant and the moderator to a gasification device, (b) mixing, and reacting the mixture under gasification conditions to a raw-material synthesis gas containing soot particles, (c) discharging the soot particle-containing raw synthesis gas; (d) separating the soot particles from the raw synthesis gas, (e) optionally supplying the raw synthesis, (f) discharging the soot particles from the filtration device and feeding the soot particles to a combustion device, (g) reacting the soot particles in the combustor under combustion conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides by partial oxidation of carbonaceous fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables gasification of carbonaceous fuels and produces carbon black as a by-product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides by partial oxidation of carbonaceous fuel in the presence of an oxygen-containing oxidant and a moderator containing water vapor and/or carbon dioxide, comprises: (a) supplying the carbonaceous fuel, the oxygen-containing oxidant and the moderator to a gasification device, (b) mixing the carbonaceous fuel, the oxygen-containing oxidant and the moderator in the gasification apparatus, and reacting the mixture under gasification conditions to a raw-material synthesis gas containing soot particles, (c) discharging the soot particle-containing raw synthesis gas from the gasification apparatus, cooling the raw synthesis gas in a cooling device, and supplying the raw synthesis gas to a filtration device, the cooling capacity of the cooling device is adjusted not to fall below the dew point of the raw synthesis gas upstream of and in the filtration device; (d) separating the soot particles from the raw synthesis gas and discharging a raw synthesis gas at least partially freed of soot particles from the filtration device, (e) optionally supplying the raw synthesis gas to further conditioning or processing steps, (f) discharging the soot particles from the filtration device and feeding the soot particles to a combustion device, (g) reacting the soot particles in the combustor under combustion conditions by means of a gaseous oxygen-containing oxidant supplied to the combustor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for the producing a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides by partial oxidation of carbonaceous fuel in the presence of an oxygen-containing oxidizing agent and a moderator containing water vapor and/or carbon dioxide.

PN CN109161524-A
TI Promoting proliferation of human adipose stem cells comprises e.g. taking fresh human adipose tissue, rinsing with phosphate saline buffer, removing adipose tissue membrane and blood vessels, cutting, digesting and adding complete medium.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN M
AE HUAIAN KANGYILAI STEM CELLS BIOTECHNOLOG (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 201906436X
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting proliferation of human adipose stem cells comprises taking fresh human adipose tissue, rinsing with phosphate saline buffer, removing adipose tissue membrane and blood vessels, cutting, digesting, adding complete medium, filtering, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant to remove suspended fat cells and lipid droplets and resuspending cell pellet in complete medium, adding red blood cell lysate, mixing, disintegrating, centrifuging and removing supernatant, re-suspending cell pellet in complete medium, inoculating into culture flask, and culturing, changing the culture solution to remove unattached cells and changing, fusing the cells, digesting, completely digesting the medium and passing, taking the passage cells, digesting to prepare cell suspension, inoculating into a culture flask, culturing in complete medium, then replacing with complete medium containing drug to continue culture and complete drug-containing medium comprises concentration of arachidol or agarose.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting proliferation of human adipose stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Promoting proliferation of human adipose stem cells comprises taking fresh human adipose tissue, rinsing with phosphate saline buffer, removing adipose tissue membrane and blood vessels, cutting into pieces, digesting with 0.1 wt.% type I collagenase and 0.25 wt.% trypsin at 37 degrees C, adding complete medium during the period of repeated shaking after the end of digestion, and filtering using 200 mesh filter to remove undigested fibrous connective tissue, centrifuging at 1200x g for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant to remove suspended fat cells and lipid droplets and resuspending the cell pellet in complete medium, adding 6 times the volume of red blood cell lysate, mixing, disintegrating at room temperature for 6 minutes, centrifuging at 1200x g for 5 minutes and removing the supernatant, re-suspending the cell pellet in complete medium, inoculating into a culture flask, and culturing at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, changing the culture solution to remove unattached cells and changing every 3 days, fusing the cells, digesting with 0.25% trypsin and 1 mmol/l EDTA at 37 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, completely digesting the medium and passing, taking the passage cells, digesting to prepare cell suspension, inoculating into a culture flask, culturing in a complete medium at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, then replacing with a complete medium containing the drug to continue the culture and the complete drug-containing medium comprises concentration of arachidol or agarose. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of arachidol or agarose in preparing medium for promoting proliferation of human adipose stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The complete medium is a high sugar Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum. The passage has a ratio of 1:2.

AU HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NINGMING XINGYU BIOLOGICAL RESOU (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201905027T

   USE - Method for processing nuts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has less nutrient loss. The nuts is rich in protein, fat and vitamin E.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing nuts comprises: (A) preparing spice by taking 3 pts. wt. fructus amomi, 2 pts. wt. Tsaoko amomum fruit, 0.3 pts. wt. sticktight and 0.3 pts. wt. peppermint leaf, crushing, mixing, adding 8 pts. wt. water, boiling for 1 hours, adding 3.5 pts. wt. hawthorn, cooling to room temperature, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain extract liquid at 23-26 MPa and 43-46.5 degrees C for 175 minutes, and separating at 45-50 degrees C; putting 2 pts. wt. pear, 3 pts. wt. lilac and 2.5 pts. wt. Milan flower, drying until water content is zero, putting in crushung machine at 44 degrees C for 55 minutes, taking out, stir-frying for 3 minutes, and grinding uniformly to obtain primary powder; taking Agastache rugosus, drying to water content of 5%, crushing and sieving by 40 meshes sieve, putting into - 45 degrees C to freeze for 5 minutes, carrying out breaking treatment using high pressure cutting crusher to obtain Agastache rugosus powder, mixing milk at mass ratio of 1:2, using ultrasonic cell crushing instrument to leach in water bath for 55 minutes, and obtaining Agastache rugosus extract; and mixing extraction liquid, primary powder and Agastacherugosus extract uniformly to form mixed liquid, bleaching mixed liquid by activated carbon to obtain fragrance material at 65 degrees C for 4 hours; (B) sunlight drying, adding salt, mixing prepared fragrance material uniformly, putting on steam drawer, and steaming to obtain primary product, where mass ratio of fruit, salt and spice is 75:8: 11; and (C) drying primary product to obtain nuts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The steaming at 38 degrees C for 30 minutes.

PN CN108726488-A
TI Producing acid-co-production building insulation            board comprises e.g. milling Phosphogypsum, fly ash,            additives and modifiers into raw meal, delivering into            the kiln for roasting to make clinker and subjecting to            water mill dissolution.
AU CHEN X
   WANG L
   ZHENG K
   LI Z
   WANG Q
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018872665
AB    NOVELTY - Producing acid-co-production building                insulation board comprises e.g. (i) mixing and                milling Phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and                modifiers into raw meal, delivering into the kiln                for roasting to make clinker, and subjecting the                clinker to water mill dissolution and solid-liquid                separation, (ii) subjecting the resulting                precipitate to flotation, separating sulfides,                calcining the separated sulfide, catalyzing the                calcination of the flue gas generated by the                calcination by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing                with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric                acid, (iii) adding calcium oxide to the liquid                obtained by solid-liquid separation in the step                (i), heating and stirring, filtering to obtain a                sodium meta-aluminate solution, (iv) introducing                the carbon dioxide gas into the resulting sodium                meta-aluminate solution until no precipitation                occurs, washing the precipitate with distilled                water, drying the washed precipitate, and grinding                to obtain aluminum hydroxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                acid-co-production building insulation board.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has high utilization                rate and added value; produces the aluminum                hydroxide is excellent raw material for building                insulation boardsl; and produces the board for                building has low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing an acid-co-production building                insulation board comprises (i) mixing and milling                Phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifiers                into raw meal, delivering into the kiln for                roasting to make clinker, and subjecting the                clinker to water mill dissolution and solid-liquid                separation, (ii) subjecting the resulting                precipitate to flotation, separating sulfides,                calcining the separated sulfide, catalyzing the                calcination of the flue gas generated by the                calcination by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing                with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric                acid, (iii) adding calcium oxide to the liquid                obtained by solid-liquid separation in the step                (i), heating and stirring, filtering to obtain a                sodium meta-aluminate solution, (iv) introducing                the carbon dioxide gas into the resulting sodium                meta-aluminate solution until no precipitation                occurs, washing the precipitate with distilled                water, drying the washed precipitate, and grinding                to obtain aluminum hydroxide, and (v) taking the                resulting aluminum hydroxide and placing into a                high-speed mixer, adding vinyl resin, inorganic                fiber, initiator, curing agent, and color paste to                obtain a mixture, then passing the mixture through                the injection molding machine into the wallboard                mold, heating the mold, curing the mixture to                obtain a wallboard shell, insulating material into                the wallboard shell, cooling and solidifying to                room temperature to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or                  caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon                  or coal vermiculite. The kiln is an industrial                  rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an                  industrial vertical kiln. In the raw material,                  the phosphogypsum and the fly ash are mixed at                  the weight ratio of 1-1.2:1-1.7. The additive is                  added at the weight ratio of 1:1 based on the                  molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide, and                  aluminum oxide and ferric oxide contained in the                  raw meal. The mixing ratio of the modifier is                  7-23% of the total weight of the raw meal. The                  calcination is carried out at 1150-1350 degrees C                  for 0.5-3.5h. The liquid-solid ratio at the time                  of elution of the water mill is 3-8:1. In the                  step (v), the initiator is benzoyl peroxide or                  tert-butyl peroxybenzoate, the curing agent is                  methyl ethyl ketone peroxide, and the inorganic                  fibers are glass fibers or ceramic fibers. The                  building insulation board comprises 50-60 pts.                  wt. aluminum hydroxide, 20-40 pts. wt. vinyl                  resin, 10-20 pts. wt. inorganic fiber, 2-3 pts.                  wt. initiator, 2-3 pts. wt. curing agent and 1-2                  pts. wt. color paste. Preferred Method: In the                  step (ii), calcination is carried out at                  1150-1250 degrees C for 3-5 hours, where sulfide                  is placed in an oxygen-rich environment of                  40-50%. In the step (iii), the calcium oxide is                  analytically pure, and the amount is 7-10 g/l,                  and heating is carried out at 75-95 degrees C for                  1-2 hours. In the step (iv), the concentration of                  carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide gas is                  greater than 38%, and drying is carried out at                  95-105 degrees C. In the step (v), stirring is                  carried out at the speed of 150-200                  rotations/minute for 20-40 min. The mold is                  carried out at 150-200 degrees C.

PN CN108726489-A
TI Co-producing acid phosphogypsum and fly ash train brake material comprises mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifier into raw materials and adding into a kiln for heating to obtain clinker and dissolving.
AU CHEN X
   LI M
   LI J
   WANG S
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018872664
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing acid phosphogypsum and fly ash train brake material comprises (i) mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifier into raw materials and adding into a kiln for heating to obtain clinker; (ii) dissolving and subjecting to solid-liquid separation; (iii) adding carbon dioxide into the product for the white precipitate no longer occurs, then filtering out the white precipitate, washing, drying and burning to obtain alumina powder; (iv) mixing alumina powder with copper powder, iron powder, manganese iron ore powder, silica, molybdenum disulfide, boron nitride, graphite and polyacrylonitrile fibers, pressing the green body, fixing the solid body on the back of the supporting steel and sintering the train brake pad material; (v) subjecting the residue to flotation and separating the sulfide; and (vi) placing the separated sulfide in oxygen-rich environment, calcining, catalyzing the flue gas and using concentrated sulfuric acid and subjecting to absorption.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing acid phosphogypsum and fly ash train brake material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high utilization rate of the waste residue, high quality, high hardness and high compressive strength and has simple acid making process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing acid phosphogypsum and fly ash train brake material comprises (i) mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifier into raw materials and adding them into a kiln for heating to obtain clinker; (ii) dissolving the clinker prepared in step (i) and subjecting to solid-liquid separation; (iii) adding carbon dioxide into the product for the white precipitate no longer occurs, then filtering out the white precipitate, washing, drying and burning to obtain alumina powder; (iv) mixing the alumina powder prepared in step (iii) with copper powder, iron powder, manganese iron ore powder, silica, molybdenum disulfide, boron nitride, graphite and polyacrylonitrile fibers by a high-speed mixer, pressing the green body, fixing the solid body on the back of the supporting steel and sintering the train brake pad material by high temperature; (v) subjecting the residue obtained in the separation of step (ii) to flotation and separating the sulfide; and (vi) placing the separated sulfide in step (v) in 30-50 wt.% oxygen-rich environment, calcining at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing the flue gas generated by the calcination by vanadium pentoxide and using the concentrated sulfuric acid and subjecting to absorption to obtain sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i) the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The mass ratio of phosphogypsum and the fly ash is 1:1-1.9. The additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide+ferric oxide contained in the raw meal. The mixing ratio of the modifier is 10-25 wt.% of the total weight of the raw meal. The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. In step (iv) the train brake pad material comprises 8-15 pts. wt. alumina powder, 30-40 pts. wt. copper powder, 10-15 pts. wt. iron powder, 10-13 pts. wt. ferromanganese powder, 2-4 pts. wt. silica, 5-8 pts. wt. molybdenum disulfide, 2-5 pts. wt. boron nitride, 15- 20 pts. wt. graphite and 0.4-0.8 pts. wt. polyacrylonitrile fiber. Preferred Method: In step (i) the baking time is 1-2 hours at a temperature of 1000-1350 degrees C. In step (ii) the clinker is firstly ground and then dissolved.The liquid to solid ratio at the time of elution is 4-6:1. In step (iii) the burning is performed at a temperature of 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours. In step (iv) the pressure of the pressed green body is 600-700 MPa. The high temperature sintering temperature is 850-1000 degrees C, the sintering pressure is controlled at 2-6 MPa, and the high temperature sintering is performed under the protection of hydrogen gas for 3-4 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN108387068-A
TI Oxygen-enriched pressurized backflow expansion nitrogen generator, comprises self-cleaning air filter, where raw air is compressed by self-cleaning air filter to be compressed by air compressor to desired pressure, cooled and enter purifier.
AU XIA J
AE JIANG M (JIAN-Individual)
GA 201864905Q
AB    NOVELTY - An oxygen-enriched pressurized backflow expansion nitrogen generator comprises self-cleaning air filter, where the raw air is compressed by a self-cleaning air filter to be compressed by an air compressor to a desired pressure, pre-cooled and enter the purifier, adsorbing moisture, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons in the raw air by purifier, enters the fractionation column, the air is cooled in the fractionation column by the main heat exchanger and the reflux rich oxygen and nitrogen gas to cool to the saturation temperature, entering the rectification column for rectification and obtaining high purity nitrogen gas at the top of the rectification column, high-purity nitrogen is used as product nitrogen, which is reheated by the main heat exchanger and sent out to the fractionation tower for delivery to the user, the other part is condensed and liquefied by a condensing evaporator.
   USE - Oxygen-enriched pressurized backflow expansion nitrogen generator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An oxygen-enriched pressurized backflow expansion nitrogen generator comprises self-cleaning air filter, where the raw air is compressed by a self-cleaning air filter to be compressed by an air compressor to a desired pressure, pre-cooled and enter the purifier, adsorbing moisture, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons in the raw air by purifier, enters the fractionation column, the air is cooled in the fractionation column by the main heat exchanger and the reflux rich oxygen and nitrogen gas to cool to the saturation temperature, entering the rectification column for rectification and obtaining high purity nitrogen gas at the top of the rectification column, high-purity nitrogen is used as product nitrogen, which is reheated by the main heat exchanger and sent out to the fractionation tower for delivery to the user, the other part is condensed and liquefied by a condensing evaporator, a part of the liquid nitrogen is output as a product, and the other part is reintroduced into the column as a reflux liquid, the cooling source of the condensing evaporator is obtained by extracting a part of the oxygen-rich liquid from the bottom of the rectification tower, cooling through a cooler, evaporating in a condensing evaporator after throttling, evaporating oxygen-rich liquid space in the condensing evaporator and reheated by the main heat exchanger and expanded to near atmospheric pressure in the pressurized turboexpander to generate the required cooling capacity of the device.
DC J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PN CN108207528-A
TI Method for managing wild rhizoma paridis seedling in Dabie mountain, by adding e.g. northeast grass charcoal, preventing bacteria, reducing moisture and nutrient substance, sterilizing, and mixing nutrient substrate and carbendazim.
AU AN F
   MENG J
   YANG B
   YANG Y
   LU B
   LIU P
   CHEN Q
AE UNIV BOZHOU (UYBO-Non-standard)
GA 2018536180
AB    NOVELTY - Method for managing wild rhizoma paridis seedling in Dabie mountain involves humus-rich northeast grass charcoal and fermented pine bark, adding e.g. northeast grass charcoal, Podocarpus pine needles, placing plastic film on the lower layer, preventing bacteria and pest from entering, reducing moisture and nutrient substance, sterilizing the substrate, mixing nutrient substrate and carbendazim, habitat of wild rhizoma paridis is high altitude, rhizoma paridis seedling period cannot be illuminated by strong light, shading with two layers of 30% shading net for weak light supplementation, low light supplement, and the total photosynthetic time is extended, and performing carbon dioxide supplementation together with low light supplementation.
   USE - The method is useful for managing wild rhizoma paridis seedling in Dabie mountain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has rapid and strong seedling growth without any disease and shortened growth time of 6 months.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for managing wild rhizoma paridis seedling in Dabie mountain involves (a) total thickness of substrate is 12 cm, thickness of lower layer is 8 cm, humus-rich northeast grass charcoal and fermented pine bark (crushing below 0.5mm), adding northeast grass charcoal, pine bark and chicken manure to the volume ratio of 6:3:1, upper layer thickness is 4 cm, Podocarpus pine needles, placing plastic film on the lower layer for preventing bacteria and pest from entering, reducing the loss of water and nutrient substance, performing sterilization of the substrate at 100 degrees C or more for 50 minutes, mixing nutrient substrate and carbendazim to the volume ratio of 1000:5, (b) habitat of wild rhizoma paridis is high altitude, low temperature on the mountain slope, large temperature difference and less light, growth cycle of heavy building is less and fast rhythm due to adverse environmental conditions, maintaining temperature at 25 degrees C in daytime and 15 degrees C at night, meeting the high temperature difference and low temperature at high altitude, during daytime the daily temperature of 25 degrees C increase photosynthesis, and the lower night temperature of 15 degrees C reduces the consumption of respiration due to less visible under forest, rhizoma paridis seedling period cannot be illuminated by strong light, shading with two layers of 30% shading net for weak light supplementation, low light supplement for 4 hours, and the total photosynthetic time is extended to 16 hours, performing carbon dioxide supplementation together with low light supplementation with supplementation of 3000 ppm for 4 hours.

PN CN107926316-A
TI Improving cold tolerance grafting cultivation of Cinnamomum camphora comprises e.g. immersing Cinnamomum camphora seeds and apricot seeds in solution, mixing soil organic fertilizer and fine sand, mixing seeds, cutting, and planting.
AU XIANG X
   MU X
AE CHAOHU NIANSHENG AGRIC ECOLOGY CO LTD (CHAO-Non-standard)
GA 201832131T
AB    NOVELTY - Improving cold tolerance grafting cultivation of Cinnamomum camphora comprises (i) immersing Cinnamomum camphora seeds and apricot seeds in a solution and mixing with the organic fertilizer, (ii) immersing Cinnamomum camphora seeds and apricot seeds in the nutrient solution, (iii) mixing soil organic fertilizer and fine sand, mixing seeds, (iv) cutting, spraying with potassium permanganate solution and (v) planting grafted apricot in greenhouses.
   USE - The method is useful for improving cold tolerance grafting cultivation of Cinnamomum camphora.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving cold tolerance grafting cultivation of Cinnamomum camphora comprises (i) immersing Cinnamomum camphora seeds and apricot seeds in a solution of potassium permanganate for sterilization and mixing with the organic fertilizer at -5 to +3?C for 17-20 days, under an intensity of 210-240 lux after 3-4 hours of exposure every day and preserving Cinnamomum camphora seeds and the apricot seeds, (ii) immersing Cinnamomum camphora seeds in a nutrient solution for mutagenesis at 37-40 degrees C for 13-16 minutes, reducing temperature to 3-1 degrees C for 24 minutes, irradiating using 60-70 Rem gamma -ray irradiation at low temperature for 6-8 minutes/times, providing alternating high and low temperature for 14-16 hours and immersing apricot seeds in the nutrient solution for constant temperature germination of seeds, (iii) mixing soil organic fertilizer and fine sand and laying on seedbeds, mixing seeds of Cinnamomum camphora and apricot after the germination and sowing for greenhouse cultivation, where the seed germination stage temperature is 14-17 degrees C and the carbon dioxide volume concentration is 0.03-0.04%, the seedling growth period temperature is 2-5 degrees C, the carbon dioxide volume concentration is 0.06-0.08% and the seedling height is 12-15 cm, (iv) cutting 6-8 cm from the upper part of Cinnamomum camphora seedlings into scions, retaining 2-3 leaf shoots, cutting both sides at 15 degrees diagonally, spraying with potassium permanganate solution to obtain spruce shoots and intercepted at apricot seedlings of 4-6 cm above the ground, cutting the middle region and spraying potassium permanganate to obtain the rootstock shoots, combining scion and the rootstock branches, coating a protective liquid and wrapping two layers of cling film to obtain grafted Cinnamomum camphora, and (v) planting grafted apricot in greenhouses for 20-30 days, transferring to outdoor cultures during the day and transferring to the greenhouse at night for external adaptive cultivation for 30 days and transplanting grafted apricot.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the bactericidal treatment has a mass concentration of potassium permanganate solution of 0.3-0.5% and a soaking time of 6-8 minutes. In the step (i), the organic fertilizer comprises 42-46 pts. wt. rapeseed, 24-27 pts. wt. soybean straw, 17-21 pts. wt. sesame, 12-15 pts. wt. humic acid, 8-11 pts. wt. weathered coal, 7-10 pts. wt. plant ash, 6-8 pts. wt. cassava residue and 4-7 pts. wt. diatomite. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the preparation of nutrient solution comprises crushing 14-18 pts. wt. corn powder, 5-8 pts. wt. peanut powder, 10-13 pts. wt. mulberry leaf, 8-10 pts. wt. balsam pear, 6-8 pts. wt. purslane, 5-8 pts. wt. hyacinth, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. ferrous sulfate, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. sodium molybdate, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. sodium selenate, adding 6-8 times of total mass of water, boiling for 20-25 minutes, adding water to 4-5% mass of EM bacteria (effective micro-organisms) to ferment for 36-40 hours, filtering and concentrating to 1/4 of the original volume. In the step (ii), the thermostatic germination described temperature is 22-24 degrees C and the is 20-22 hours. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of loess, organic fertilizer and fine sand is 10-14:6-8:3. In the step (iv), the preparation protective liquid comprises mixing and stirring 53-56 pts. wt. nutrient solution , 12-14 pts. wt. gypsum powder, 6-8 pts. wt. guar gum and 1-2 pts. wt. potassium permanganate and diluting colloid.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer; Bactericide.

PN RU2648737-C1
TI System of integrated zero waste processing of solid domestic and industrial wastes.
AU SARIEV V N
   VERETENNIKOV V A
   TROYACHENKO V V
AE VALTRON CO LTD (VALT-Non-standard)
GA 2019878379
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to treatment of solid domestic and industrial wastes (SDIW). Technical result is achieved by that the SDIW processing system includes technologically interconnected: a shaft-type counterflow reactor 1, which receives SDIW through preparation units 2, loading 3 and loading locks 4 and 5; high temperature heater 6 from which steam is supplied counter-current to the reactor via a supply lance with annular flue 7, heated to a temperature of not less than 1,700 degrees C. Apparatus 8 and 9 for releasing melts of minerals and metals, respectively. Two outlet annular gas outlets: first, 10 - for drawing off crude synthesis gas, second, 11 - for removal of raw pyrogas. Crude synthesis gas is sequentially sent through solids removal filter 12 to heat recovery device 13, dehydrating unit 14, gas separator 15, after which the purified and dried synthesis gas is supplied to receiver 16. Raw pyrogas is sent to purification unit 17 to remove solid particles, fat and resin contaminants, as well as chlorine, fluorine and sulfur contaminants, after which purified pyrogas enters drying unit 18, then vessel 19. Solid particles from the filters, fat and resin contaminants are returned to the reactor through loading unit 27. Mixer 20 into which purified and dried synthesis gas and pyrogas are supplied to produce a normalised combustible mixture which is stored in gas tank 21. Gas-turbine power generating unit 22 for power supply of claimed system. Steam generator 23 for feeding high temperature heater 6 with steam with a temperature of up to 500 degrees C; a water treatment unit 24 for the steam generator, fed from an external source and using water from recycled water purification unit 25; a low-grade heat recovery device 26 for meeting utility needs using the heat of the combustion products from high temperature heater 6 and gas turbine power generating unit 22. To optimise the combustion process in combustion chambers, oxygen separated from nitrogen is used as the oxidising agent with increased oxygen content. Exhaust gases from high temperature heater 6 and gas turbine power generating unit 22 after heat recovery and carbon dioxide from gas separator 15 are sent to a greenhouse to increase carbon dioxide content in the air in order to enhance photosynthetic activity of plants in industrial greenhouses; low-grade heat from heat exchanger 26 is sent to heat the greenhouse building, soil or hydroponic systems.
   USE - Processing and recycling of waste.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is higher productivity of the treatment process, efficiency and simultaneous environmental safety by eliminating formation of dioxins.
3 cl, 1 dwg

PN CN113514580-A
TI Analyzing oxygen, argon, nitrogen, methane, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in high-purity hydrogen for e.g. hydrogen fuel automobile, by adopting gas chromatography method by deoxygenating high-purity hydrogen sample, where deoxygenation step is carried out using deoxyhydrazine.
AU ZHANG X
   NIU Z
   WANG C
   WANG S
   LIU L
   NIU Y
AE BEIJING GAOMAIKE INSTR S & T CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2474F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for analyzing oxygen, argon, nitrogen, methane, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in high-purity hydrogen involves adopting gas chromatography method by deoxygenating high-purity hydrogen sample, where the deoxygenation step is carried out using deoxyhydrazine.
   USE - The method is useful for analyzing oxygen, argon, nitrogen, methane, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in high purity hydrogen for hydrogen fuel automobile, petrochemical industry, aerospace industry, electronic industry, metallurgy industry, food processing industry, float glass industry and aerospace industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to use, completely reacts 99.9999% of oxygen in oxygen impurities, and has high accuracy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The deoxyhydrazine contains elemental copper.Preferred Conditions: The step of deoxidizing involves contacting the elemental copper with impurity oxygen in the high-purity hydrogen sample and performing an oxidation reaction. In the gas chromatography, the column temperature is 65-75degreesC, preferably 68-72degreesC, and the detector is 22-28degreesC, preferably 24-26degreesC. In the gas chromatography method, the carrier gas is high-purity nitrogen, the carrier gas flow rate is 25-35 ml/minute, preferably 28-32 ml/minute and the discharge gas flow rate is 8-12 ml/minute, preferably 8-12 ml/minute.Preferred Device: The detector in the gas chromatography comprises discharge ionization detector (DID). The chromatograph in the gas chromatography is a GM592 gas chromatograph. The chromatographic column in the gas chromatography comprises PRECOLUMN1 comprising 8 feet 1/8VCR SC-ST 80/100, COLUMN1 comprising 10 feet 1/8VCR 13X80/100, PRECOLUMN2 comprising 15 feet 1/8VCR HATESEP-Q80/100, and COLUMN2 comprising 15 feet 1/8VCR HATESEP-Q80/100.Preferred Method: The gas chromatography method involves performinge switching and passage of the valve by introducing the standard gas/sample gas, placing the valve (I) in the characterized control valves (CCV) position, allowing carrier gas (I) to carry the standard gas/sample gas through the loop (I) to the pre-column (I), and venting the main component gas hydrogen through valve (II) in the clockwise (CW) position, switching the valve (II) back to the counterclockwise (CCW) state when the remaining impurities are about to flow out from the pre-column (I), and passing the column (I) to the DID detector to complete the analysis of oxygen and argon, nitrogen, methane and carbon monoxide, introducing the standard gas/sample gas, placing the valve (I) in the CW position, allowing the carrier gas (II) to carry the standard gas/sample gas through the loop (I) to the pre-column (I), venting the main component gas hydrogen through valve (IV) in the CW position, switching the valve (IV) back to CCW state when the remaining impurities are about to flow out from the pre-column (II), and passing the column (II) to the DID detector to complete the carbon dioxide analysis, when analyzing argon impurities, placing valve (I) in CCW position, allowing carrier gas (I) to carry standard gas/sample gas through quantitative loop (I) to deoxyhydrazine, connecting to the pre-column (I) through deoxyhydrazine, venting the main component gas of hydrogen gas through the valve (II) in the CW position, switching the valve (II) back to the CCW state when the remaining impurities are about to flow out from the pre-column (I), and passing the column (I) to the DID detector to complete the analysis of argon, nitrogen, and methane.

PN CN113372604-A
TI Ultrasonic-assisted preparation method of thermosetting epoxy resin microporous foam, involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide saturation adsorption to thermosetting epoxy resin sheet, and foaming saturated thermosetting epoxy resin under ultrasonic condition.
AU SHI X
   MA Y
   ZHANG G
   DI W
   FAN X
   GAO Q
   QIN J
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 2021A8162F
AB    NOVELTY - An ultrasonic-assisted preparation method of thermosetting epoxy resin microporous foam, involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide saturation adsorption to a thermosetting epoxy resin sheet, and foaming the saturated thermosetting epoxy resin under an ultrasonic condition.
   USE - Ultrasonic-assisted preparation method of thermosetting epoxy resin microporous foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluidity of carbon dioxide fluid in the epoxy resin is promoted through the energy released by the cavitation effect of ultrasonic waves, and the uniform distribution of the gas in the thermosetting resin system is promoted, so that the foaming performance of the epoxy resin is significantly improved, and a foam structure with small pores and uniform density can be formed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The polyetheramine-M2070-silane coupling agent KH560 (RTM: 3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane) is prepared by mixing polyetheramine M2070 and silane coupling agent KH560 (RTM: 3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane) in ethanol, reacting the resulting mixture at 60 degrees C for 12 hours, and drying. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the polyetheramine M2070 and the silane coupling agent KH560 (RTM: 3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane) is 10:(1-3).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The foaming temperature is 100-120 degrees C, and the foaming time is 6-30 seconds. The ultrasonic process is continuous ultrasonic process or pulse ultrasonic process. The power of the ultrasonic process is 75-450 W. When pulsed ultrasound is used, the duty cycle is 50%. The pulse width of the pulsed ultrasound is 1-5 seconds. The temperature of the saturated adsorption of supercritical carbon dioxide is 45-55 degrees C, the pressure is 15-20 MPa, and the time for keeping the temperature and pressure is 48-72 hours. Preferred Process: The thermosetting epoxy resin sheet is prepared by mixing bisphenol A epoxy resin, hyperbranched epoxy resin, 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole and modifier in dichloromethane, and vacuum drying to obtain modified epoxy resin powder, and pressing the modified epoxy resin powder to obtain a final product, where the modifier is polyetheramine or polyetheramine-M2070-silane coupling agent KH560 (RTM: 3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane), or subjecting the modified epoxy resin to rotary steaming and vacuum drying in sequence to obtain modified epoxy resin powder, and compressing the modified epoxy resin powder to obtain a final product. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the bisphenol A epoxy resin, hyperbranched epoxy resin, 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole and modifier is 18:2:1:0.98.

PN CN113181962-A; CN113181962-B
TI New cobalt porphyrin cross-linked polyquaternary            phosphonium salt ionic liquid useful in catalyzing            cycloaddition reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide to            prepare cyclocarbonate.
AU XIONG Y
   LIU Y
   WANG S
   DAI Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
GA 2021900790
AB    NOVELTY - Cobalt porphyrin cross-linked polyquaternary                phosphonium salt ionic liquid (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in catalyzing cycloaddition                reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide to prepare                cyclocarbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): does not needs solvents and co-catalysts                due to the synergy between the bifunctional active                center and the Lewis acid metal center and the                nucleophilic halide ion; exhibits high-efficiency                catalytic activity and high selectivity under                normal pressure and lower temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cobalt porphyrin cross-linked polyquaternary                phosphonium salt ionic liquid of formula (I) is                new.
   a = 50-320;
   b = 4-150; and
   X = Cl, Br or I.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use of (I) in catalyzing cycloaddition                reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide to prepare                cyclocarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I) comprises (i) adding 4-vinylbenzyl chloride                  and azobisisobutyronitrile into chloroform,                  refluxing at 60-80 degrees C for 40-50 hours                  under the protection of nitrogen, cooling to room                  temperature, removing the solvent by rotary                  evaporation, washing and filtering with suction,                  vacuum drying to obtain white powder                  poly-4-vinylbenzyl chloride, (ii) dissolving                  poly-4-vinylbenzyl chloride and                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis (4-pyridine) porphyrin in N,                  N-dimethylformamide reacting at 120-140 degrees C                  for 50-70 hours under nitrogen atmosphere,                  cooling to room temperature after reaction to                  obtain porphyrin cross-linked polyvinylbenzyl                  chloride, (iii) adding triphenylphosphine into                  porphyrin cross-linked polyvinylbenzyl chloride                  obtained in step (ii), reacting at 60-80 degrees                  C for 20-25 hours under nitrogen protection, then                  washing with chloroform and ether, carrying out                  suction filtration and vacuum drying to obtain a                  purple-black solid powder porphyrin cross-linked                  polyquaternary phosphonium salt ionic liquid,                  (iv) adding porphyrin cross-linked polyquaternary                  phosphonium salt ionic liquid obtained in step                  (iii) and cobalt (II) chloride hexahydrate into                  dimethylformamide, refluxing at 140-160 degrees C                  for 12-24 hours, cooling to room temperature,                  adding hydrochloric acid and oxidizing in air for                  5-6 hours, then adding deionized water for                  washing, carrying out suction filtration and                  vacuum drying to obtain cobalt porphyrin                  cross-linked polyquaternary phosphonium salt ion                  liquid of formula (CoTPyP-c-PVBnPPh3Cl)(II) and                  (iv) introducing (II) and alkali metal halide                  into deionized water, reacting at room                  temperature for 24-50 hours, filtering, washing                  and drying to obtain final product, where the                  alkali metal halide is sodium bromide or sodium                  iodide. Preferred Method: The use method                  comprises taking (I) as a catalyst, epoxide and                  carbon dioxide as reaction substrates to carry                  out a cycloaddition reaction to obtain a                  cyclocarbonate, where the reaction temperature is                  70-90 degrees C and the reaction time is 15-20                  hours; the epoxide is epichlorohydrin, allyl                  glycidyl ether, styrene oxide, butyl glycidyl                  ether, phenyl glycidyl ether; the mass to volume                  ratio of (I) to epoxide is 9-27 mg/ml; the                  pressure of the carbon dioxide is 1 atmosphere.                  Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar                  ratio of 4-vinylbenzyl chloride to                  azobisisobutyronitrile is 50:1-100:1. In step                  (ii), the molar ratio of poly-4-vinylbenzyl                  chloride to 5,10,15,20-tetrakis (4-pyridine)                  porphyrin is 1:1-80:1. In step (iii), the molar                  ratio of triphenylphosphine to                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis (4-pyridine) porphyrin is                  1:1-316:1. In step (iv), the molar ratio of                  cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate to porphyrin                  cross-linked polyquaternary phosphonium salt                  ionic liquid is 5:1-20:1; the molar concentration                  of hydrochloric acid is 1-2 M and the addition                  amount of hydrochloric acid is 2-10 times the                  molar amount of cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate.                  The molar amount of alkali metal halide is 10-20                  times that of (I).

PN CN112043731-A
TI Preparing whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil nanoemulsion rich in ganoderic acid comprises dry pressing Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and mixing with additives.
AU CHEN S
AE JIANGSU SHENGDIAN MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4733G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil nanoemulsion rich in ganoderic acid comprises (i) using whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder as raw material, dry-pressing one-step granulation, placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain light yellow whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil rich in ganoderic acid and (ii) mixing the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil with additives to prepare a whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil nano emulsion.
   USE - The nano-emulsion is useful for preparing anti-tumor medicine and improving rabbit-immunity medicine, and medicines and foods.
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion is stable and quality control is convenient. The method has simple process flow, convenient operation, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The whole Ganoderma lucidum is composed of 51-60% Ganoderma lucidum fruiting bodies and 40-49% Ganoderma lucidum spore powder. The whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder are made by crushing Ganoderma lucidum fruiting bodies to ultra-fine powder of greater than or equal to 150 mesh, and crushing into the wall broken ganoderma spore powder with a wall breaking rate greater than or equal to 96% and mixing the two evenly to obtain the raw material of whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder. The whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil and the auxiliary agent comprises 5-20% Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, 1-12% emulsifier, 1-8% solubilizer, 0-2.5% flavoring agent, 0-3% isotonic regulator, 0.0-0.2% flavor, 0-0.08% preservative and 0.01-0.04% antioxidant, and pH value adjuster and water (remaining amount). The emulsifier comprises soybean phospholipid, egg yolk lecithin phospholipid, liquid lecithin and/or cephalin. The solubilizer comprises glycerin and/or polyethylene glycol 400. The flavoring agent is steviol glycoside, aspartame, glucose or fructose. The isotonic regulator is glycerin for injection. The flavor comprises peppermint flavor, lemon flavor, rose flavor, milk flavor and/or peppermint flavor. The antioxidant is vitamin E. The preservative is potassium sorbate. The pH adjusting agent is sodium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate or sodium carbonate. Preferred Method: In the step (i) adding 0.5-1.2% starch or dextrin to the raw material of the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, and mixing evenly, using a granulator to produce granules with a particle size of 0.1-0.3 cm in one step by dry pressing method, selecting the main pressure of the granulator to be 5-9 MPa, and the side pressure to be 0.5-0.9 Mpa, and placing in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle for extraction. The supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide, the extraction temperature is 23-45 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 12-34 MPa, the extraction time is 2-5 hours and the extraction flow rate is 32-58 kg/h, adopting two-stage separation and the separation process conditions are: the pressure of the first-stage resolver is 9-13 MPa, and the separation temperature is 37-43 degrees C, the pressure of the secondary resolver is 4-6Mpa, and the separation temperature is 27-33 degrees C to obtain light yellow whole Ganoderma spore oil. The method specifically comprises (1) taking the Ganoderma lucidum spore oil in a 60-80 degrees C water bath and under the protection of nitrogen, adding emulsifiers and antioxidants, stirring to dissolve and mixing evenly, and cooling to room temperature to obtain the oil phase, (2) mixing the remaining emulsifier, solubilizer, and isotonic regulator under the protection of a water bath at 60-80 degrees C and nitrogen, and stirring, dissolving in water for injection, if necessary, shearing and stirring to dissolve and mixing uniformly and cooling to room temperature to obtain aqueous phase, and (3) under stirring conditions, dropping the oil phase to the aqueous phase while shearing and stirring for 15-60 minutes under the protection of nitrogen, and adjusting the pH to about 7 for preliminary homogenization, where the homogenization pressure is 150-300 bar, the homogenization time is 5-10 minutes and water for injection is added to 100% to obtain milky white colostrums, and (4) moving the prepared white colostrum into a high-pressure homogenizer, the high-pressure homogenization pressure is 800-1300bar, and the high-pressure homogenization is 10-15 times, filtering the microporous membrane, filling nitrogen, potting and sterilizing.

PN BR102018074541-A2
TI Welding GMAW-CW (gas metal arc welding-cold wire) protection gas torch in slave, involves double feeding main feed, energizing wire in the main torch (Torch A), protecting by a gaseous mixture which comprises argon and carbon dioxide.
AU DIMAS DE ABREU L
   SILVA GERALDI T
AE SOC MINEIRA CULTURA (MINE-Non-standard)
GA 2020600707
AB    NOVELTY - Welding GMAW-CW (gas metal arc welding-cold wire) protection gas torch involves double feeding main feed, energizing wire in the main torch (Torch A), protecting by a gaseous mixture which comprises argon and 15% carbon dioxide, feeding with non-energized wire in the slave torch (Torch B), and protecting by a gas mixture which comprises argon and 12-20% carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for welding GMAW-CW (gas metal arc welding-cold wire) protection gas torch in slave.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to weld GMAW-CW (gas metal arc welding-cold wire) protection gas torch that maintains the stability and quality of the welded line.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The angle (beta) between torches A and B is 55-65 degrees . The angle ( alpha ) between the main torch (torch A) and the workpiece surface is 55-65 degrees . The voltage and current values are used that allows the spray-type metallic transfer. The welding parameters refers to the voltage varying in the range of 30-32 volt. The welding parameters related to the current is 220-235 ampere. The welding parameters are used for referring to the energized wire feed speed is 5.0-6.0 m/minute. The welding parameters are used related to the cold wire feed speed is 3.0-3.5 m/minute. The welding parameters are used for referring to the gas flow in the energized torch and the slave torch is 16-20 l/minute. Preferred Process: The cold wire is introduced into the anode region of the electric arc (region E) in order to guarantee the stability of the process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); X24 (Electric Welding)

PT P
PN CN111100467-A; CN111100467-B
TI Preparing medium-density fiberboard by using fiber ethanol waste residue involves adding fiber ethanol waste residue, urea-formaldehyde resin adhesive and curing agent to reactor, passing carbon dioxide into the supercritical state.
AU LI X
   LI L
   CAO C
   WANG Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202041602V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medium-density fiberboard by using fiber ethanol waste residue involves adding fiber ethanol waste residue, urea-formaldehyde resin adhesive and curing agent to reactor, passing carbon dioxide into the supercritical state, and increasing temperature by 5-40 degrees C, preferably 20-30 degrees C, after the reaction is completed. The system is passed into an expansion separator to recover carbon dioxide. The collected residual products and wood fibers are mixed, compressed and molded to produce molded fiberboard.
   USE - Method for preparing medium-density fiberboard by using fiber ethanol waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare medium-density fiberboard by using fiber ethanol waste residue that has good physical mechanical performance, and saves the production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fiber ethanol waste residue is the residue of lignocellulosic biomass after pretreatment, enzymolysis, fermentation of ethanol and separation by distillation. The lignocellulosic biomass is straw, wood chips or energy plants containing cellulose, hemicellulose and lignin, preferably straw. The curing agent is ammonium chloride and ammonium persulfate. The auxiliary agent is at least one of methanol, ethanol, propyl alcohol, preferably ethanol. Preferred Composition: The dry slag after dehydration of cellulosic ethanol slag has 50-65% lignin content, 10-20% cellulose content, 0.5-3% hemicellulose content, 15-20% of soluble components content, and 10-15% ash content. The solid content of the urea-formaldehyde resin adhesive is 50-60%, and the free formaldehyde content is less than 0.1%. The dosage of urea-formaldehyde resin adhesive is 40-50% of the quality of fiber ethanol waste residue. The curing agent is 1-10% urea-formaldehyde resin adhesive. Preferred Condition: The reactor adopts a pressure-resistant reactor, after passing carbon dioxide, the control pressure is 10-15 megapascal, the temperature is 40-50 degrees C, so that the carbon dioxide is in a supercritical state. The temperature is raised to 45-80 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C, and the reaction is stirred for 30-90 minutes, preferably 60-90 minutes. The amount of the wood fiber is 0.8-1.2 times of mass of cellulosic ethanol residue. The collected remaining products and wood fiber are mixed, discharged into the mold frame, cold-pressed into a plate shape, and then under a pressure of 6-10 megapascal at 160-180 degrees C for 5-10 minutes.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P63 (Working, preserving wood (B27).)
