PN WO2020032300-A1
TI Carbonated drink useful for preventing adult diseases chosen from dental caries, diabetes and obesity, comprises carbon dioxide, allulose and high sweeteners.
AU HAN T
   LIM H J
   KIM B C
   KIM Y
   SEO I
   LEE S M
AE SAMYANG CORP (SAMY-C)
GA 202014040D
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated drink comprises carbon dioxide, allulose and high sweeteners.
   USE - The carbonated drink chosen from lemon-lime flavored carbonated drink, caffeine free carbonated drink and sugar-free carbonated drink (all claimed) is useful for preventing adult diseases chosen from dental caries, diabetes and obesity.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated drink reduces excessive intake of sugar by using allulose, has high quality, has low calorie or is calorie free drink, has sweetness similar to that of sugar, maintains carbonic acid content for a long time, has excellent sensory properties by enhancing refreshing and body feel and improving aftertaste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing carbonated drink which involves mixing the allulose and high sweetener and injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The drink comprises 0.1-20 wt.% allulose in syrup or powder form and 0.0001-0.5 wt.% high sweetener. The drink further comprises one or more components chosen from citric acid, 0.1-3 volume/volume% alcohol, 0.01-20 wt.% one or more sugars and components chosen from fruit juices, flavorings, pigments and acidity regulators. Preferred Components: The carbonate content in the carbonated drink is 2.8-4. The time taken for the carbonate of the carbonated drink to reach 2.6 when the carbonated drink is stirred at a temperature of 251:10 is 1300-2000 seconds. The pH calibration time is 3700-6000 seconds is calculated using a formula: P/(Q-2.6) (1), where P is time taken for reaching pH 2.6 under condition of stirring at 25 degrees C at 100 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 4 seconds and Q is pH of carbonated drinks before stirring. The high sweetener is chosen from aspartame, acesulfame potassium, sodium cyclate, sodium saccharin, sucralose, stevia sweetener, dulcin, thaumatin, neotame, rebaudioside and monelin. The sugar is chosen from rare sugars, fructose, syrup, glucose, oligosaccharides, sugar alcohols and dextrins excluding allulose. The fruit flavor is chosen from apple mint flavor, mojito flavor, lemon-lime flavor, mint flavor, apple flavor, grape flavor, raspberry flavor, blueberry flavor, mango flavor, kiwi flavor and strawberry flavor.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiabetic; Anorectic; Antibacterial. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN WO2020018740-A1; US2021284540-A1
TI Method of reforming hydrocarbon e.g. methane, involves exposing hydrocarbon to oxide and forming water, carbon dioxide and carbon as opposed to formation of hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
AU SCHEFFE J
   WARREN K J
AE UNIV FLORIDA RES FOUND (UYFL-C)
   UNIV FLORIDA RES FOUND INC (UYFL-C)
GA 202009035L
AB    NOVELTY - Method of reforming hydrocarbon involves exposing hydrocarbon to an oxide and forming water, carbon dioxide and carbon as opposed to the formation of hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
   USE - Method of reforming hydrocarbon such as methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves carbon-neutral utilization of methane and carbon sequestration by using renewable energy and coupled with zero-energy input product gas separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system, which consists of reforming reactor and parabolic trough. The reactor is operated at operating conditions to form water, carbon dioxide and carbon as opposed to formation of hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The parabolic trough is in electrical or thermal communication with the reactor and the energy derived from the trough is used to adjust the operating conditions in the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The exposing process is carried out at a temperature of less than 1000 degrees C, preferably 700-800 degrees C for 1 second-1 hour to form water, carbon dioxide and carbon over hydrogen and carbon monoxide. Preferred Component: The oxide having the characteristic of forming water, carbon dioxide and carbon over hydrogen and carbon monoxide is chosen from oxide of zinc, tin, iron, cobalt, copper, alumina and cerium. The oxide is optionally doped with one or more of strontium, lithium, gadolinium, samarium, praseodymium, zirconia or hafnium. Preferred Process: The method involves generating energy by parabolic trough at temperature of operating condition. The method involves forming less than 5%, preferably 1% hydrogen and carbon monoxide.

GA 2019A1304K
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of concentration of acid gas from                fluid gas stream involves injecting a solution                comprising at least one aminal compound into the                fluid gas stream. The aminal compound is an organic                compound containing two amino groups attached to                the same carbon atom that are not sterically                bonded.
   USE - Reduction of concentration of acid gas e.g.                carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, and mercaptan,                from fluid gas stream such as natural gas stream                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aminal compound containing two reactive                site enables high-efficiency of acid gas capture                than a simple alkanolamine even at dilute                circumstance, and diamine is capable of scavenging                the acid gases, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,                and/or mercaptan.

Preferred Process: The method                  further involves injecting aqueous solution                  comprising aminal compound into fluid gas stream                  comprising less than 10 wt.%, preferably 3 wt.%                  at least one acid gas to reduce the concentration                  of the 20 wt.% acid gas.
   Preferred Definitions:
   X1,X2 = group derived from ethanol or                methanol, methyl, or ethyl.

TI Improving water depth treatment based on controlling membrane pollution comprises pre-treating, performing ozone activated carbon treatment and ultrafiltration treatment, and using carbon dioxide for adjusting pH.
AU WANG G
   WEI Y
   ZHENG L
   WU Z
   YUE Z
   ZHANG Q
AE HUANENG JIAXIANG POWER GENERATION CO LTD (HUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201925388G
AB    NOVELTY - Improving water depth treatment based on controlling membrane pollution comprises pre-treating, performing ozone activated carbon treatment and ultrafiltration treatment, using carbon dioxide for adjusting pH, partially refluxing reverse osmosis concentrated water and refluxing reverse osmosis concentrated water portion by ozone oxidation.
   USE - The method is useful for improving water depth treatment based on controlling membrane pollution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the pH of organic matter and carbon dioxide by ozone; ensures the long-term stable operation of the water deep treatment process in the double membrane method; and improves the water recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for water depth treatment improvement device based on membrane pollution control with double membrane method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ozone activated carbon treatment has ozone dosage of 2-5 mg/l, carrying out carbon dioxide treatment, controlling the pH of the reverse osmosis water by 5.6-7 and adjusting pH by using carbon dioxide. The method comprises partially refluxing reverse osmosis concentrated water, and where the reflux ratio of the reverse osmosis concentrated water is 1:4-1:1, treating with ozone, mixing with raw water, where the mixing ratio of the reflux reverse osmosis concentrated water and the raw water is 1:5-1:2, in the ozone oxidation treatment, where the dosage of ozone is 2-5 mg/l. The method comprises performing pretreatment by lime coagulation and sand filtration for pretreatment of water containing flocculant/coagulant and lime, carrying out coagulation mixing process, precipitation or clarification process and sand filtration process. The specific operating parameters of the coagulation and sand filtration process are adjusted according to the quality of the water, where the flocculant is polymer substance, and solid-liquid separation tank is sedimentation tank, sloping plate sedimentation tank, mechanical acceleration clarification tank, and variable pore filtration tank. The pretreatment has lime dosage of 300-600 mg/l and flocculant dosage of 200-300 mg/l. Preferred Components: The flocculating agent is aluminum chloride, ferric chloride, polyaluminum chloride, polyaluminum sulfate, polyferric chloride, polyaluminum ferric chloride and/or chitosan. The coagulant is polyacrylamide. The activated carbon is industrial activated carbon, biochar, modified activated carbon and composite activated carbon. The ultrafiltration membrane used in the ultrafiltration is hollow fiber membrane, flat membrane or rolled membrane. The membrane material is cellulose acetate, polysulfone, polyethersulfone, polyvinylidene fluoride or polytetrafluoroethylene. The reverse osmosis is low pressure reverse osmosis membrane process and the membrane module is wound membrane module.

PN CN107778473-A; CN107778473-B
TI Preparing poly(cyclohexane carbonate) involves using carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide and subjecting to copolymerization using catalyst 2,5-di (2-aldehyde group benzene oxygen methyl) furan-3-amino thiophene zinc coordination compound.
AU ZHANG X
   WANG Y
   ZHAO K
   ZHANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
   HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 2018209354
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing poly(cyclohexane carbonate) involves using carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide and subjecting to copolymerization using catalyst 2,5-di (2-aldehyde group benzene oxygen methyl) furan-3-amino thiophene zinc coordination compound represented by I.
   USE - Method for preparing poly(cyclohexane carbonate).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare poly(cyclohexane carbonate) in a simple manner, which has high catalytic activity of 216 g polymer/g catalyst and shows yield of 95%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing poly(cyclohexane carbonate) involves using carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide and subjecting to copolymerization using catalyst 2,5-di (2-aldehyde group benzene oxygen methyl) furan-3-amino thiophene zinc coordination compound represented by formula (I)..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The 2,5-di (2-aldehyde group benzene oxygen methyl) furan-3-amino thiophene zinc coordination compound is prepared mixing 3-aminothiophene and 5-((Benzyloxy)methyl)furan-2-carbaldehyde in methanol to obtain mixed solution. The obtained mixed solution at room temperature and subjected to heat-stirring for 0.5-2 hours, then subjected to ether washing and drying to obtain 2,5-bis (2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl) furan-3-aminothiophene ligand. The obtained 2,5-bis (2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl) furan-3-aminothiophene ligand is placed in a container, under vacuum filled with nitrogen, and added with toluene for dissolving the ligand and cooling in an ice salt bath and reacted with diethylzinc at room temperature for 2 hours, then removing the unreacted diethylzinc under reduced pressure to obtain desired product. The poly(cyclohexane carbonate) is prepard by mixing 2,5-bis (2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl) furan-3-aminothiophene ligand and diethylzinc in the molar ratio of 1:1-1.3. The catalyst 2,5-di (2-aldehyde group benzene oxygen methyl) furan-3-amino thiophene zinc coordination compound is placed in a vacuum-dried reaction vessel, then filled with carbon dioxide to normal pressure, than ring is added to the reaction vessel and mixed with epoxycyclohexane at 60-200 degrees C, and using carbon dioxide to maintain the pressure of 0.5-5 megapascal under stirring reaction for 6-50 hours and cooled to room temperature to stop the reaction. The reaction solution is taken out, and unreacted epoxycyclohexane is distilled off, and the residue is subjected to post-treatment to obtain poly(cyclohexane carbonate). Preferred Conditions: The 3-aminothiophene and 5-((Benzyloxy)methyl)furan-2-carbaldehyde are mixed in the molar ratio of is 1:0.4-0.6. The 2,5-di (2-aldehyde group benzene oxygen methyl) furan-3-amino thiophene zinc coordination compound and cyclohexene oxide are mixed in the weight ratio of 0.0005-0.01:1. The post-treatment process involves dissolving the residue in methylene chloride, then filtering to obtain filtrate. The obtained filtrate is washed with dilute acid, dilute alkali, and distilled water in order stirred with methanol to obtain white solid precipitate. The obtained white solid precipitate is filtred, and vacuum dried to obtain poly(cyclohexane carbonate).
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A07A; A05-E06A; A10-D
IP C07D-409/14; C08G-064/02; C08G-064/34

PN WO2022187490-A1
TI Synthesis of polyester used for e.g. drug            delivery, involves hydrogenating lactone formed by            telomerization of 1,3-diene(s), and carbon dioxide, and            polymerizing obtained hydrogenated lactone in the            presence of catalyst in ring-opening process.
AU TONKS I A
   RAPAGNANI R M
AE UNIV MINNESOTA (MINU-C)
GA 2022B5959H
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of polyester involves hydrogenating                a lactone formed by telomerization of 1,3-diene                (d1), 1,3-diene (d2), and carbon dioxide to obtain                a hydrogenated lactone, and polymerizing the                obtained hydrogenated lactone in the presence of a                catalyst in a ring-opening process.
   USE - Synthesis of polyester (claimed) used as                rubbery midblocks of sustainable thermoplastic                elastomer, and for drug delivery, biomedical                application or degradation in aquatic                environments.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of polyester                having excellent biodegradability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   the polyester;
   a modified polyester; and
   ynthesis of lactone, which involves                telomerizing piperylene with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 1,3-diene (d1) and                  the 1,3-diene (d2) are the same. The 1,3-diene                  (d1) and the 1,3-diene (d2) are butadiene,                  isoprene, or piperylene. The hydrogenated lactone                  is partially hydrogenated. The catalyst is an                  acid catalyst or an organocatalyst. The catalyst                  comprises one or more of diphenyl phosphate,                  sodium methoxide, triazabicyclodecene,                  bistriflimide, methanesulfonic acid, and                  diaza-bicycloundecene. The lactone is one of                  structures of formulae (IA)-(ID).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyester                  comprises a pendent olefin. The polyester is                  chosen from 8 structures, e.g. structures of                  formulae (I)-(V).n=2-200.Preferred Process: The method further                  involves crosslinking the polyester through the                  pendent olefin to obtain a modified polyester,                  crosslinking the polymer through the pendent                  olefin by reacting the pendent olefin with a                  multi-mercapto coupling agent, modifying the                  pendent olefin with a thiol-ene click reaction by                  functionalizing the polyester with a carboxylic                  acid or functionalizing the polyester with a                  tertiary amine, quaternizing the amine to obtain                  a modified polyester with antibacterial property,                  and synthesizing the lactone by capturing carbon                  dioxide from the atmosphere. The polymerizing                  occurs at 0-166&#176; C.
   EXAMPLE - In an nitrogen glovebox, 203.6 mg                6-ethenyl-3-ethyloxan-2-one was added to a one-dram                vial equipped with a stir bar. This was followed by                the addition of 12.3 mg diphenyl phosphate and                0.037 mmol 3-phenyl-1-propanol. The sealed vial was                removed from the glovebox and allowed to stir at                room temperature for 55 days, with NMR aliquots                being removed on day 4, 11, 23, and 37. On day 55,                the polymerization was quenched with a few drops of                triethylamine, then using toluene the material was                transferred to a new vial. Concentrated under                reduced pressure to obtain 170 mg                3-ethyl-6-hydroxyoct-7-en-2-one as viscous and                colorless oil.

PN DE102019213501-A1
TI Producing isobutanol and/or 1-butanol starting from synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises e.g. performing catalytic conversion of synthesis gas to obtain first mixture.
AU TENHUMBERG N
   GEHRMANN S
   KLEIBER M
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
GA 2021234284
AB    NOVELTY - Producing isobutanol and/or 1-butanol starting from synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises (a) performing catalytic conversion of synthesis gas (11) to obtain a first mixture comprising at least one 2C alcohol, and (b) converting alcohols contained in this first mixture into isobutanol and/or 1-butanol in a Guerbet reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for producing isobutanol and/or 1-butanol starting from synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good yield.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the method for producing isobutanol and/or 1-butanol.
   Feed stream (10)
   Synthesis gas (11)
   Reactor (12)
   Separation facility (14)
   Separator (17)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The alkane and alkene are first separated from the alcohols from a first mixture of alcohols, alkenes and alkane obtained after the catalytic conversion of synthesis gas and then the alcohols are converted in the Guerbet reaction. The butanol and optionally water are separated off from the first mixture obtained after the catalytic conversion of synthesis gas and the Guerbet reaction is carried out with the remaining mixture of alcohols. The first mixture obtained after the catalytic conversion of synthesis gas, with which the Guerbet reaction is carried out, essentially contains methanol, ethanol and propanol. The Guerbet reaction, methanol is reacted with ethanol to form 1-propanol, 1-propanol is reacted with methanol to form isobutanol and / or ethanol is reacted with methanol directly to form isobutanol and/or ethanol is dimerized to 1-butanol. The catalytic synthesis of the higher alcohols from synthesis gas is carried out at 200-360 degrees C, preferably 260-300 degrees C under reaction pressure of 10-110 bar, preferably 50-70 bar. The alkenes contained in the mixture obtained after separation of the alkenes and alkane are converted by hydration to alcohols or hydroformylation to alcohols and/or aldehyde, which are then also subjected to the Guerbet reaction or an aldol condensation. The mixture of alcohols obtained after the catalytic conversion of synthesis gas in the first step, in particular after the separation of the alkenes and alkane, is separated into individual alcohols or fractions of alcohols before the Guerbet reaction is carried out. The product mixture obtained after the Guerbet reaction is purified from alcohol, preferably the water is separated off from the product mixture. The isobutanol and 1-butanol are separated off from the product mixture obtained after the Guerbet reaction. The separation of the alkenes and alkane from the alcohols takes place at a lower temperature and pressure than the preceding catalytic synthesis of higher alcohols from synthesis gas, preferably at 20-40 degrees C under pressure of less than 5 bar, preferably less than 2 bar. At least one step is provided in which the first product mixture obtained during the conversion of the synthesis gas is separated into a gas phase and a liquid Phase takes place, the liquid phase being used for the subsequent Guerbet reaction, before the Guerbet reaction is carried out and after the catalytic conversion of the synthesis gas. The gas phase obtained in the separation is at least partially returned to the step of catalytic conversion of the synthesis gas. The product mixture is processed after the catalytic conversion of the synthesis gas by at least partial absorption of the alcohols and any alkenes and/or alkane present in a high-boiling hydrocarbon or hydrocarbon mixture as the absorption medium, separating the gases not absorbed in the absorption medium as a gas phase, separating an aqueous phase from the organic phase of the absorption medium, preferably by decanting, optionally distilling out the alcohols from the aqueous phase, and performing desorption of the alcohols and any alkenes and/or alkane present from the absorption medium. The separation of the alcohols from the alkenes and alkanes or the separation of the alcohols from the alkanes prior to the Guerbet reaction comprises separating the alcohols in at least one distillation column at an elevated pressure of preferably at least 10 bar, and removing the water from the alcohol fraction, preferably by means of a molecular sieve, by extractive distillation, by pervaporation or by azeotropic distillation. Preferred Components: The catalytic conversion of the synthesis gas, a catalyst is used which comprises grains of non-graphitic carbon with cobalt nanoparticles dispersed therein, the cobalt nanoparticles having an average diameter of 1-20 nm and the mean distance between individual cobalt nanoparticles in the grains of non-graphitic carbon of 2-150 nm, the combined total mass fraction of the metal in the grains of non-graphitic carbon of 30-70 wt.% of the total mass of the grains is non-graphitic carbon. The catalyst material is doped with a metal including manganese and/or copper, the grains of non-graphitic carbon having a molar ratio of cobalt to doped metal of 2-15.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC B10-E04D; H04-E05; H04-F02E; J04-E04; N02-B01; N02-D01; N03-E; N06-F; N07-C03; N07-D04

PT P
PN EP3656892-A1; WO2020104569-A1; US2022127736-A1
TI Electrolysis device used for reducing carbon dioxide into hydrocarbons, consists of anode and cathode in which electrodes comprising electroconductive support of which portion(s) of surface is covered by metal deposit of copper.
AU FONTECAVE M
   MOUGEL V
   TRAN NGOC H
   ALVES DALLA CORTE D
   LAMAISON S
   LUTZ L
   ALVES D C D
   TRAN N H
AE PARIS SCI & LETTRES-QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV SORBONNE (USRB-C)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   UNIV SORBONNE (USRB-C)
   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES-QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
GA 2020450891
AB    NOVELTY - An electrolysis device consists of an anode and a cathode. The electrodes comprise an electroconductive support of which at least a portion of the surface is covered by a metal deposit of copper. The surface of the metal deposit is in an oxidized, sulfurated, selenated and/or tellurized form. The metal deposit has a specific surface area of 1 m2/g or more.
   USE - Electrolysis device used for reducing carbon dioxide into hydrocarbons (claimed) for renewable energy e.g. solar energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolysis device allows for a very short interelectrode distance for minimal resistance while allowing simple products separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reduction of carbon dioxide into hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electroconductive support comprises electroconductive material chosen from copper, steel, aluminum, zinc or titanium, a metal oxide chosen from fluorine-doped titanium Oxide or indium-tin Oxide, a metal sulfide such as cadmium sulfide or zinc sulfide, carbon in the form of carbon felt, graphite, vitreous carbon, or boron-doped diamond, and a semiconductor such as silicon. The anodic compartment and the cathodic compartment separated by a membrane, preferably an anion exchange membrane. The reduction of carbon dioxide involves exposing a cathode to carbon dioxide-containing aqueous catholyte solution. The catholyte solution comprises a salt of hydrogen carbonate, preferably cesium carbonate. Preferred Properties: The metal deposit has thickness of 10 mu m to 2 mm, preferably 70-300 mu m, and specific surface area of 3-50 m2/g (preferred).

PT P
PN US2020018547-A1; US10969169-B2
TI Separation of components of gas involves passing feed gas stream comprising methane, carbon dioxide and water into vessel, cooling feed gas stream in vessel, and resulting in product stream and depleted gas stream exiting vessel.
AU BAXTER L
   HOEGER C
   CHAMBERLAIN J
   STITT K
AE NEWVISTAS CAPITAL LLC (NEWV-Non-standard)
   SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 2020065246
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of components of gas involves passing a feed gas stream (120) comprising methane, carbon dioxide and water into a vessel, cooling the feed gas stream in the vessel in which the portion of methane, carbon dioxide and water condenses, and resulting in a product stream (128) and depleted gas stream (126) exiting the vessel.
   USE - Separation of components of gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical separation of components of gas with reduced energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the process for separating components of a gas.
   Exchanger (110)
   Feed gas stream (120)
   Slurry stream (122)
   Depleted gas stream (126)
   Product stream (128)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The feed gas stream further comprises a secondary component chosen from natural gas liquids, nitrogen, argon, hydrogen sulfide, mercaptans and hydrogen. The contact liquid stream comprises a mixture of solvent and an ionic compound, and mixture of solvent and soluble organic compound. The solvent is chosen from water, hydrocarbons, liquid ammonia, liquid carbon dioxide and cryogenic liquids. The ionic compound is chosen from potassium carbonate, potassium formate, potassium acetate, calcium magnesium acetate, magnesium chloride, sodium chloride, lithium chloride and calcium chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The natural gas liquids comprise ethane, propane, butane, isobutane, pentane, natural gasoline, cyclic hydrocarbons and aromatic hydrocarbons. The soluble organic compound is chosen from glycerol, ammonia, propylene glycol, ethylene glycol, ethanol and methanol. The contact liquid stream is chosen from ethers, alcohols, hydrocarbons, liquid ammonia, liquid carbon dioxide and cryogenic liquids. The alcohols are chosen from methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol and isobutanol.

TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent useful for capturing carbon dioxide comprises adsorbent matrix comprising metal-organic framework and carbon nanotubes that are dispersed within the adsorbent matrix, without forming chemical bonds with the matrix.
AU MANSOUR R B
   QASEM N
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2019A75129
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorbent comprises an adsorbent matrix comprising metal-organic framework (MIL-100(Fe)), and carbon nanotubes that are dispersed within the adsorbent matrix, where the carbon nanotubes are dispersed in the adsorbent matrix without forming chemical bonds with the adsorbent matrix.
   USE - The CO2 adsorbent is useful for capturing CO2.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO2 adsorbent has: a CO2 adsorption capacity of 0.5-20 mmol CO2 per one gram of the CO2 adsorbent, at a temperature of 10-40 degrees C; and a thermal conductivity of up to 500% higher than a thermal conductivity of a pristine adsorbent that comprises MIL-100(Fe) and does not include carbon nanotubes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A weight percent of the carbon nanotubes in the CO2 adsorbent is 0.05-1.5 wt.%. The adsorbent matrix further comprises zeolite 13X and/or Mg-MOF-74. The carbon nanotubes are: homogenously dispersed in the adsorbent matrix; or multi-walled carbon nanotubes.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110372862-A; CN110372862-B
TI New 2,6,9-triphenyl-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole-triazine polymer useful for gas adsorption, preferably carbon dioxide adsorption.
AU LI H
   WEN J
   CHEN H
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201990262S
AB    NOVELTY - 2,6,9-Triphenyl-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole-triazine polymer (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for gas adsorption, preferably carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, and low cost. (I) has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption performance under the condition of 273 K.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 2,6,9-Triphenyl-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole-triazine polymer of formula (I) is new.
   A = 9-(4-(4,6-dimethyl-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)phenyl)-6-(4-(4-methyl-6-(4-(6-phenyl-2-p-tolyl-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazol-9-yl)phenyl)-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)phenyl)-2-p-tolyl-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole of formula (II).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises carrying out bromination reaction of 9,10-phenanthrenequinone, then carrying out carbonyl protection, and carrying out substitution reaction with p-cyanophenylboronic acid, and then oxidizing to 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone, and then cyclizing 3,6-di(4-nitrophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone with p-cyanobenzaldehyde and imidazole to obtain 2,6,9-tri(4-cyanophenyl)-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole, then reacting 2,6,9-tri(4-cyanophenyl)-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole with zinc chloride to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The bromination reaction comprises of 9,10-phenanthrenequinone with bromine to obtain 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone. The carbonyl protection comprises reacting 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone with bromo-n-hexane to obtain 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene. The substitution reaction comprises reacting 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene with p-cyanophenylboronic acid to obtain 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene. The oxidation reaction comprises reacting 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene with oxidizing agent to obtain 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone, where the oxidizing agent is concentrated nitric acid, concentrated sulfuric acid, perchloric acid and/or potassium permanganate solution. The method comprises (a) dissolving 9,10-phenanthrenequinone and initiator dibenzoyl peroxide in solvent nitrobenzene, dropwise adding liquid bromine, heating the mixture to 80-200 degrees C, preferably 100-140 degrees C for 5-24 hours preferably 10-20 hours, washing with n-hexane, and separating, preferably filtration, suction filtration or column analysis to obtain brown product 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone, (b) dissolving 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone, catalyst preferably tetrabutylammonium bromide and/or sodium dithionite in solvent tetrahydrofuran solution, mixing, then adding bromo-n-hexane, adjusting the pH to alkaline, preferably using sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH to 10-14, mixing preferably shake for 1-5 minutes, cooling, (preferably adding ice water to the reaction system), washing (preferably adding deionized water to the reaction system), and extracting (preferably dichloro or ethyl acetate as solvent), and carrying out column chromatography (preferably using petroleum ether for column chromatography) to obtain product 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexylphenanthrene, (c) dissolving 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexylphenanthrene and p-cyanophenylboronic acid in a solvent preferably toluene, heating (preferably heating to 60-90 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere, refluxing for 8-48 hours), adding water and stirring, extracting (the solvent is preferably dichloro or ethyl acetate), subjecting the separated solid to column chromatography (preferably using a mixed solution of ethyl acetate and petroleum ether in a volume ratio of 1:2-20 for column chromatography) to obtain 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene, (d) dispersing 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene in oxidizing agent, stirring (preferably stirring time is 5-20 minutes), and separating (preferably by filtration or suction filtration) to obtain 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone, and (e) dissolving 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone and p-cyanobenzaldehyde in a solvent (preferably a mixed solution of ammonium acetate in acetic acid), heating and refluxing (preferably heating to 60-100 degrees C, refluxing for 6-48 hours), and separating (preferably filtration or suction filtration) and washing the isolated solid product (preferably with ethanol) to obtain 2,6,9-tri(4-cyanophenyl)-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole. The method comprises mixing 2,6,9-tri(4-cyanophenyl)-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole and zinc chloride, preferably grinding and mixing, adding into a closed reactor, evacuating the reactor to vacuum, heating (preferably using muffle furnace to 200-800 degrees C, more preferably, heating to 300-700 degrees C for 1-5 day, preferably 2-4 days to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The molar ratio between 9,10-phenanthrenequinone and liquid bromine is 1:1-3, preferably 1:1.2-2.5. The molar ratio of the 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone and bromo-n-hexane is 1:1.5-4, preferably 1:1.8-3. The molar ratio between 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene and p-cyanophenylboronic acid is 1:1.2-3, preferably 1:1.5-2.5. The molar ratio between 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone and p-cyanobenzaldehyde is 1:0.8-2.5, preferably 1:1-1.8. In step (a), the molar ratio between 9,10-phenanthrenequinone and liquid bromine is 1:1-3, preferably 1:1.2-2.5, the mass concentration of 9,10-phenanthrenequinone in the solvent is 0.10-0.20 g/ml, preferably 0.12-0.18 g/ml, the concentration of the initiator in the solvent is 0.01-0.10 mol/l, preferably 0.02-0.08 mol/l, in step (b), the molar ratio between 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone and bromo-n-hexane is 1:1.5-4, preferably 1:1.8-3, the addition amount of catalyst is 0.05-0.22 g/ml, preferably 0.08-0.15 g/ml, the mass concentration of 3,6-dibromo-9,10-phenanthrenequinone in the solvent is 0.02-0.12 g/ml, preferably 0.015-0.100 g/ml, in step (c), the molar ratio between 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexylphenanthrene and p-cyanophenylboronic acid is 1:1.2-3, preferably 1:1.5-2.5, the mass concentration of 3,6-dibromo-9,10-dihexylphenanthrene in the solvent is 0.02-0.22 g/ml, preferably 0.05-0.18 g/ml, in step (d), the mass concentration of 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-dihexyloxyphenanthrene in the oxidizing agent is 0.01-0.80 g/ml, preferably 0.02-0.5 g/ml, and in step (e), the molar ratio between 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone and p-cyanobenzaldehyde is 1:0.8-2.5, preferably 1:1:1-1.8, the mass concentration of 3,6-di(4-cyanophenyl)-9,10-phenanthrenequinone in the solvent is 0.001-0.120 g/ml, preferably 0.005-0.080 g/ml, the solvent is a mixed solution of ammonium acetate in acetic acid, where the mass concentration of ammonium acetate is 0.01-0.15 g/ml, preferably 0.02-0.10 g/ml. In step (ii), the molar ratio between 2,6,9-tri(4-cyanophenyl)-1H-phenanthro(9,10-d)imidazole and zinc chloride is 1:1-15, preferably 1:2-12, more preferably 1:3-10.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN110330202-A
TI Photocatalytic treatment of aquaculture waste            antibiotics based on titania involves adding titania to            mixed solution of antibiotic and aquaculture waste,            irradiating with UV light to form holes and electrons,            and oxidizing.
AU CHANG J
   LIU Y
   WEI Z
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 201986837L
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic treatment of aquaculture waste                antibiotics based on titania involves adding                titania as a catalyst to a mixed solution of                antibiotic and aquaculture waste under UV exposure                environment, placing mixed solution under xenon                lamp for UV light irradiation, forming holes and                electrons on the solid surface of titania which                oxidizes water and reduces oxygen in the air,                respectively, to form hydroxyl ion, and oxidizing                antibiotic waste using hydroxyl ion to form carbon                dioxide, and repeating the carbon dioxide                generation until the antibiotic waste is completely                converted to carbon dioxide by degrading carbon                atom.
   USE - Photocatalytic treatment of aquaculture waste                antibiotics based on titania.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective killing of                pathogenic bacteria, and is mild, fast, energy                saving and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titania is                  P25-type nano titania. The chemical reaction of                  the solid surface of titania under the                  irradiation of UV light is carried out by                  exposing titania to light to produce hole (h+)                  and electron (e-), reacting hole and water to                  produce hydroxide and hydrogen ion, reacting                  electron and oxygen to produce oxygen ion (O2-),                  reacting oxygen ion with hydrogen ion to produce                  water, converting 2 moles of water to hydrogen                  peroxide and oxygen, reacting hydrogen peroxide                  and oxygen ion to produce hydroxide, hydroxide                  ion and oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The chemical reaction of                  the hydroxyl ion on the oxidation of antibiotics                  is carried out by reacting ethyl compound of                  formula: R-CH2-CH3 with 2-hydroxy compound to                  form ethyl alcohol compound of formula:                  R-CH2-CH2-OH and water, oxidizing ethyl alcohol                  compound to form aldehyde compound of formula:                  RCH2CHO and hydrogen, oxidizing aldehyde compound                  to form acid compound of formula: R-CH2COOH, and                  reducing acid compound to form methyl compound of                  formula: RCH3 and carbon dioxide, where R is not                  defined.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

TI Hydrogenation catalyst for hydrogenating carbon            dioxide, comprises transition metal complex coordinated            to covalent organic framework, and two or more            identical anion ligands introduced into coordination            sphere of complex.
AU YOON S H
   PARK K H

   HARIYANANDAM G G
   YUNSUNGHO
   HO P K
AE UNIV KOOKMIN IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYKO-C)
GA 201948398P
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogenation catalyst comprises a                transition metal complex coordinated to a covalent                organic framework (COF), which is triazine or                heptazene, and two or more identical anion ligands                introduced into the coordination sphere of the                complex. The transition metal is iridium, platinum,                palladium, ruthenium, rhodium, nickel, iron,                copper, vanadium, cobalt, chromium, gold, rhenium,                tungsten, zirconium and/or molybdenum. The anion                ligand is an oxyanionic ligand chosen from                carboxylate, oxalate, carbonate, and                acetylacetonate, or an anion ligand, such as                chloride ion, bromide ion, iodide ion, sulfate ion,                trifluoromethanesulfonate ion, nitrate ion and                hexafluorophosphate ion.
   USE - Hydrogenation catalyst for hydrogenating                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogenation catalyst has high catalytic                activity, and exhibits excellent reusability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Two or more anion                  ligands are oxyanionic ligands, preferably                  acetylacetonate. The linkage of the organic                  framework is triazine. The ligand is chosen from                  phenyl, naphthalene, pyrene, thiophene, pyridine,                  pyrrole, phosphine, phosphinine, triphenylamine,                  porphyrin, biphenyl, bipyridine, biphosphine,                  bipyrrole, phenyl pyridine, bipyrimidine,                  biimidazole and bithiophene, preferably                  bipyridine. The transition metal is ruthenium.                  The transition metal complex is of formula (I).                  The hydrogenation is carried out in an aqueous                  solution, preferably a basic aqueous solution,                  more preferably aqueous triethylamine solution.                  Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  catalyst during hydrogenation is 0.1-30 wt.%.                  Preferred Conditions: The hydrogenation is                  carried out at temperature from room temperature                  to 200 degrees C, and 0.1-20 MPa. The catalyst is                  recycled continuously.

PN CN109704315-A
TI Preparing graphene, comprises e.g. placing graphite in fuming nitric acid, stirring, cooling, adding nitromethane under ice bath condition and configuring into liquid explosive.
AU MIAO Z
   TIAN H
   TIAN L
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 2019423233
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene, comprises (i) placing graphite in fuming nitric acid, and stirring to obtain graphite intercalation compound, then cooling, adding nitromethane under ice bath condition, and configuring into liquid explosive, (ii) adding the mixed liquid into plastic container, placing in the center of the detonation reactor with the electric detonator, and assembling booster column into a detonation sequence, (iii) pumping carbon dioxide gas into the explosion container as a protective medium, and (iv) entering the carbon dioxide gas in the vessel immediately the supercritical carbon dioxide gas fluid state, cooling, and collecting the product to obtain graphene.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids shortcoming of the impact time of the explosion impact force; and utilizes the heat generated by the explosion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): the amount ratio between graphite, fuming nitric acid and nitromethane is 3:3:4, and it can be properly adjusted and the stirring time is 1-24 hours. The step (iii) comprises taking carbon dioxide gas as a protective medium, and injecting carbon dioxide gas in sufficient amount to make the carbon dioxide gas pressure in the explosion reactor close to or greater than 7.38 MPa. In step (iv): the explosion time is extremely short, and the carbon dioxide gas absorbs heat into the supercritical fluid state and continuously peels off the graphite sheet.

PN CN108929298-A
TI Preparation of elecampane product by crushing elecampane, adding in extracting kettle with supercritical carbon dioxide, introducing methanol, extracting, analyzing, decoloring, concentrating, crystallizing and chromatography separating.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 201899495G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of elecampane product comprises crushing elecampane in 50-80 mesh, adding in extracting kettle with supercritical carbon dioxide, setting temperature at 50-60 degrees C and pressure at 15-30 MPa, introducing liquid carbon dioxide at flowrate of 2-3 mL/minute/g, introducing methanol as entrainer at flowrate of 0.1-0.4 mL/minute/g, extracting for 60-100 minutes, analyzing to obtain extract, dissolving extract in ethanol solution, adding activated carbon, decoloring under reflux, concentrating, extracting with ethyl acetate, crystallizing, dissolving using ethanol, high-speed counter-current chromatography separating, collecting according to chromatographic fraction, concentrating and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing elecampane product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process and small pollution, and is easy to implement for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Separation is done using acetonitrile, methanol and disodium hydrogen phosphate solution with pH of 6.5 at ratio of 3-5: 1-4: 3-7 as solvent system, where the upper phase as immobile phase and the lower phase is mobile phase.

PN DE102016218235-A1; WO2018054627-A1; AU2017329432-A1; CN109715859-A; EP3485065-A1; US2019249317-A1; AU2017329432-B2
TI Preparation of propanol, propionaldehyde and/or propionic acid involves performing electrolysis of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and ethylene, and conversion of carbon monoxide and ethylene with hydrogen to propanol and propionaldehyde.
AU SCHMID B
   SCHMID G
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 201822253H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of propanol, propionaldehyde and/or propionic acid involves carrying out electrolysis of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and ethylene, and performing conversion of carbon monoxide and ethylene with hydrogen to propanol and/or propionaldehyde, and/or conversion of carbon monoxide and ethylene with water to propionic acid.
   USE - Preparation of propanol, propionaldehyde and/or propionic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient, quick and economical preparation of propanol, propionaldehyde and/or propionic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for propanol, propionaldehyde and/or propionic acid preparation device, which comprises electrolysis device(s) (a1) for electrolysis of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and ethylene, and reactor(s) (r1) for the conversion process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The electrolysis of carbon dioxide is carried out to produce to obtain anodic oxygen groups e.g. oxygen and/or peroxide, and the anodic oxygen groups are reacted with propanol or propionaldehyde to produce propionic acid. Ethylene is produced from carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide by electrolysis on a copper-containing cathode. Carbon monoxide is produced from carbon dioxide by electrolysis on cathode comprising metal chosen from gold, silver and zinc. The conversion of carbon monoxide and ethylene with hydrogen is carried out by hydroformylation reaction and/or propane production reaction. The conversion of carbon monoxide and ethylene with water is carried out by hydrocarboxylation reaction. Waste heat obtained from electrolysis of carbon dioxide is used in hydroformylation reaction, propane production and/or hydrocarboxylation reaction. Preferred Component: The electrolysis device (a1) comprises electrolytic cell(s) having a cathode comprising copper, and electrolytic cell(s) having a cathode comprising metal chosen from gold, silver and zinc. The device comprises electrolysis device(s) (a2) for electrolysis of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. The electrolysis device (a1) comprises electrolytic cell(s) having a cathode comprising copper, and electrolytic cell(s) having a cathode comprising metal chosen from gold, silver and zinc, or electrolysis device (a1) comprises cathode comprising copper, and electrolysis device (a2) comprises cathode comprising the metal. The device further comprises electrolysis device(s) (a3) for preparing hydrogen by electrolysis of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and/or electrolysis of water, a heat pipe for supplying waste heat from electrolysis of carbon dioxide to the reactor (r1), and a reactor (r2) for conversion of propanol and/or propionaldehyde to propionic acid.

PN CN115183145-A
TI Carbon dioxide capturing and sealing system, has            storage unit that stores carbon dioxide captured by            trapping unit, and sealing unit connected with storage            unit, where sealing unit seals carbon dioxide            discharged by storage unit.
AU WANG S
   GUO D
   NIU H
   LIU L
   GAO S
   ZHENG Z
   LIU R
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2022D2378J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a trapping unit (1) that traps                carbon dioxide in an atmosphere. A floating frame                unit (2) is connected with the trapping unit such                that trapping unit floats on a sea. A storage unit                (3) is fixed on the floating unit. The storage unit                stores carbon dioxide captured by the collecting                unit. A sealing unit (4) is provided with a sealing                unit that is fixed with the storage unit for                sealing the carbon dioxide discharged by the                storing unit. An air inlet and an air outlet are                connected with each other.

AE JANHUNEN T (JANH-Individual)
GA 2022D24638
AB    NOVELTY - Producing engine fuel, where the base                materials is biocarbon comprises: (1) treating                carbon with water to form carbon monoxide and                hydrogen; (2) reacting carbon monoxide with water                to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen; (3) reacting                carbon with 0.5x oxygen to form carbon monoxide;                and (4) reacting carbon monoxide with hydrogen to                form methanol, where in step (1) water vapor is                introduced into glowing biocarbon ember, in step                (2) the gas mixture is directed through catalysts                in a temperature of 400&#176; C, in step (3)                biocarbon is combusted in oxygen-deficient                conditions and in step (4) biomethanol is formed by                means of catalysts, and in step (3) biocarbon is                combusted with oxygen, and the hydrogen formed in                steps (1) and (2) is used in step (4).
   USE - The method is useful for producing engine fuel                (claimed).

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite magnetized thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) material comprises (i) using carbon dioxide as foaming agent to dissolve it in polyester polyurethane in molten state under environment of 85degreesC, and foaming for 2 hours to obtain foamed polyester polyurethane, i.e. inner layer, (ii) attaching magnet particles to surface of polyester polyurethane to obtain magnetic chain polyester polyurethane. i.e. middle layer, and (iii) laminating inner layer, middle layer and outer layer together, and electromagnetizing magnetic chain polyester polyurethane of middle layer to obtain outer layer, where the outer layer is polyether polyurethane.

TI Controlling soft nitriding deformation of cylinder body of plunger pump comprises e.g. placing cylinder body semifinished product into vacuum furnace, heating, introducing ammonia gas, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, vacuumizing gas nitriding furnace, and cooling.
AU YANG L
   YE X
   MA Z
   ZHENG K
AE LONKING SHANGHAI PRECISION HYDRAULIC CO (LONK-Non-standard)
GA 2022316086
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling soft nitriding deformation of cylinder body of a plunger pump comprises (i) placing the cylinder body semifinished product into vacuum furnace, heating at heating rate of less than or equal to 100degrees Celsius/hour, and introducing nitrogen gas, (ii) heating to 600-650degrees Celsius, thermally insulating for 4-8 hours, introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and heating to 350-400degrees Celsius, (iii) maintaining the temperature of the cylinder for 30-60 minutes, and introducing air, (iv) vacuumizing the gas nitriding furnace, (v) heating the cylinder, (vi) introducing nitrogen, heating to 540-580degrees Celsius, and (vii) cooling the cylinder and at the same time introducing nitrogen until it cools to 110degrees Celsius.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling soft nitriding deformation of cylinder body of a plunger pump i.e., motor of an engineering machinery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains cylinder body surface with high hardness and high wear resistance at the same time without generating additional heat stress caused by deformation, greatly reduces deformation of the cylinder body plunger hole, ensures high precision requirement.

   USE - Method for removing mercury from fly ash and                purifying mercury removal solution.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for removing mercury from fly ash and                purifying mercury removal solution has the                advantages of high demercuration rate, simple                process, low cost and low energy consumption, and                can effectively improve the utilization rate of the                fly ash.

The                  temperature of step (2) described reaction is                  15-95 degrees Celsius, preferably, the reaction                  time of step (2) is 30-240 minutes. Method                  involves mixing fly ash and water and leaching                  according to the mass volume ratio of 1:2-10, the                  leaching temperature is 15-95degreesC, and the                  time is 30-240 minutes, and the mercury removal                  liquid and the low mercury fly ash are obtained                  after the solid-liquid separation, adding                  aluminate to the mercury removal liquid, the                  addition amount of the aluminate is 0.01-0.5 mole                  aluminate/liter mercury removal liquid, and                  feeding carbon dioxide gas into the mercury                  removal liquid after mixing and adjusting the pH                  to 7-11, after the reaction, solid-liquid                  separation is performed to obtain a purified                  solution, the reaction temperature is 15-95                  degreesC, and the time is 30-240 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Aluminate is sodium                  aluminate and/or potassium aluminate. Preferred                  Composition: Addition amount of the aluminate is                  0.01-0.5 mol aluminate/liter mercury removal                  solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The introduction rate                  of carbon dioxide in step (2) is 0.1-10                  liter/(minutes.liter mercury removal                  solution).

AU HONG H
   XU D
   ZHU N
   HAN L
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA TECHNOLOGY (UYIM-C)
GA 2021A7748J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of stable amorphous nano-calcium carbonate comprises subjecting calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2), polyvinylpyrrolidone and carbon dioxide (CO2) to liquid alcohol carbonization reaction to obtain amorphous calcium carbonate-containing alcohol suspension; centrifuging the alcohol suspension, taking the supernatant, and obtaining colloidal solution containing amorphous calcium carbonate; and drying.
   USE - The method is for preparation of stable amorphous nano-calcium carbonate used for preparation of carrier medicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product can continuously and stably exist in amorphous appearance, and has small particle size of 10 nm, large specific surface area of up to 334 m2/g, and high solubility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) stable amorphous nano-calcium carbonate; and
   (2) preparation of carrier medicine containing amorphous calcium carbonate (ACC)-PVP comprising subjecting Ca(OH)2, polyvinylpyrrolidone and CO2 to liquid alcohol carbonization reaction to obtain the amorphous calcium carbonate-containing alcohol suspension; centrifuging the alcohol suspension; taking the supernatant; obtaining colloid solution containing amorphous calcium carbonate; adding the to-be-dispersed active component in the colloid solution and dissolving; and drying to obtain medicine particle loaded by ACC-PVP.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The active component is fluorouracil or curcumin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The carbonization reaction is conducted at 20-60 degrees C. The method comprises uniformly dispersing Ca(OH)2 and polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) in liquid alcohol, and introducing CO2 to carry out carbonization reaction under stirring condition. The mol ratio of Ca(OH)2, CO2 and PVP is 1:1-3:3.8x 10-5-7.6x 10-4. The carbonizing reaction time is 1-4 hours.

PN CN113321813-A; CN113321813-B
TI Preparing cobalt-based metal-organic frameworks material as catalyst for photoreduction of carbon dioxide involves reacting anhydrous ethanol mixed solution containing cobalt-based bimetallic metal-organic frameworks and metal acetate.
AU REN J
   WANG Q
   ZHANG H
   TAN B
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 2021A4288H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cobalt-based metal-organic frameworks material comprises (1) reacting anhydrous ethanol mixed solution containing cobalt acetate and first metal acetate in a reaction container at 60-90 degrees C to obtain cobalt-based bimetallic metal-organic frameworks, where the first metal acetate is taken from magnesium acetate, nickel acetate or manganese acetate; and (2) reacting anhydrous ethanol mixed solution containing cobalt-based bimetallic metal-organic frameworks and second metal acetate in reaction container at 60-90 degrees C to obtain cobalt-based metal-organic frameworks material, where the second metal acetate is taken from magnesium acetate and nickel acetate; and manganese acetate or cobalt acetate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cobalt-based metal-organic frameworks material as a catalyst for photoreduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to add high-cost organic ligand; has low reaction temperature, short reaction time of main synthesis step and the preparation process does not need any organic solvent other than absolute ethyl alcohol. The material has various types, appearance, novel structure and structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (1), the formula of the absolute ethanol mixed solution containing cobalt acetate and the first metal acetate comprises: mixing 3-5 ml cobalt acetate absolute ethanol solution with a concentration of 0.02 mol/l and 3-5 ml first metal acetate absolute ethanol solution with a concentration of 0.01 mol/l and dilute with ethanol to 15 ml. In step (2), the formula of the absolute ethanol mixture containing the cobalt-based bimetal metal-organic frameworks and the second metal acetate comprises: dispersing 5-15 mg cobalt-based bimetal metal-organic frameworks at a concentration of 2-6 ml 0.03 mol/l of the second metal acetate in anhydrous ethanol solution, and diluted with anhydrous ethanol to 15 ml. In the step (1) and (2), the reaction time of is 1-3 hours. Preferred Components: The microscopic morphology of the cobalt-based metal-organic frameworks material is a submicron rod with a length of 0.6-3.0 mu m and a width of 100-400 nm. The microstructure of the cobalt-based metal-organic frameworks material is composed of a core and a shell outside the core, where the core is a cobalt-based bimetallic metal-organic frameworks, and the shell is magnesium-metal-organic frameworks, nickel-metal-organic frameworks, manganese-metal-organic frameworks or cobalt-metal-organic frameworks.

PT P
PN CN113120938-A; CN113120938-B
TI Preparing calcium fluoride comprises e.g. mixing            calcium-containing raw material with dissolution            medium, mixing calcium carbonate solids with            fluorine-containing wastewater and additives and            reacting and liquid-solid separating.
AU CAO S
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG F
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202183978X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium fluoride comprises (i)                mixing the calcium-containing raw material with the                dissolution medium to carry out a decomposition                reaction and liquid-solid separating of the                calcium-containing material liquid to obtain a                calcium-containing solution and insoluble residue;                (ii) contacting the calcium-containing solution                with a gas containing carbon dioxide to carry out a                carbonization conversion reaction and liquid-solid                separating of the carbonized feed liquid to obtain                calcium carbonate solids and a carbonized mother                liquor; and (iii) mixing calcium carbonate solids                with fluorine-containing wastewater and additives,                reacting, and subjecting the obtained reaction feed                liquid to liquid-solid separation to obtain calcium                fluoride solids and reaction mother liquor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild process conditions, no                special requirements for equipment materials and                types, strong industrial operability, and wide                application range and produces high-quality                artificial fluorite products with controllable                morphology, controllable particle size, and                controllable purity, increases the recovery rate of                fluorine resources to more than 90%, realizes the                efficient circulation of medium and carbon dioxide                and avoids the generation of fluorine-containing                sludge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  calcium-containing raw material is industrial                  lime, lime milk, calcium carbide slag, and/or                  calcium hydroxide, the dissolution medium                  includes ammonium chloride and/or ammonium                  nitrate, and the ratio of ammonium ion in the                  dissolution medium and calcium oxide in the                  calcium-containing raw material is 1.6-2.4:1. In                  the step (iii), the ratio of the amount of                  calcium carbonate in the solid calcium carbonate                  and fluorine in the fluorine-containing                  wastewater is 0.4-0.75:1 and the amount of the                  additive substance is 0-20%, the additive is                  alkali metal hydroxides, alkali metal carbonates                  and/or alkaline ammonium salts, the additive is                  sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium                  hydroxide, potassium carbonate, ammonia, ammonium                  carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the decomposition is                  carried out at 20-100 degrees C for 0.05-3 hours,                  where the washing water obtained from the                  insoluble residue is mixed with the                  calcium-containing solution. In the step (ii),                  the carbonization conversion reaction is carried                  out at 25-100 degrees C for 0.1-5 hours, where                  the washing water obtained by washing the calcium                  carbonate solid is mixed with the carbonization                  mother liquor and the carbonized mother liquor is                  evaporated and then returned to step (i) for                  recycling as the dissolution medium and the                  calcium carbonate solids are subjected to slurry                  treatment with water and/or the reaction mother                  liquor of step (iii), and the obtained calcium                  carbonate slurry is mixed and reacted with the                  fluorine-containing wastewater and additives,                  where the solid content of the material liquid                  after the chemical slurry treatment is 10-400                  g/l. In the step (iii), the reaction is carried                  out at 5-100 degrees C for 1-50 hours, the                  calcium fluoride solid is washed and dried,                  preferably, the washing water obtained by washing                  the calcium fluoride solid is mixed with the                  reaction mother liquor and the carbon dioxide gas                  produced by the reaction is collected,                  pressurized, and then returned to the                  carbonization conversion reaction in step (ii)                  for recycling.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E11-W; E34-D02

PT P
PN CN113044870-A
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate used for papermaking, involves adding calcium hydroxide slurry into carbonization reactor with stirring, adding sodium lauryl polyoxyethylene ether sulfate, carbonizing with carbon dioxide gas and filtering.
AU WANG Y
   XIA Z
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202178696P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light calcium carbonate involves adding calcium hydroxide slurry into a carbonization reactor with stirring. The sodium lauryl polyoxyethylene ether sulfate is added to the calcium hydroxide slurry. The adding amount of sodium lauryl polyoxyethylene ether sulfate is 0.3-0.6 wt.% of the dry weight of calcium hydroxide, stirred for 30 minutes, and carbonized with carbon dioxide gas. The carbonization ends when the pH is 7.0-7.5, the obtained calcium carbonate slurry is filtered and washed to form a filter cake, and then the filter cake is dispersed with water to form a slurry, where the solid content of the slurry is 40-60 wt.%. The 0.5-0.8 wt.% polypropylene oxide is added by the absolute dry weight of calcium carbonate to the slurry and stirred for 30 minutes. A light calcium carbonate slurry is obtained for papermaking. The slurry does not need to be dried and can be directly used as a papermaking filler.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate used for papermaking.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)

PN CN112647099-A; CN112647099-B
TI Preparation of transition metal nano-catalyst used in e.g. reduction of carbon dioxide, involves immersing carrier in solution prepared using potassium sulfate or bicarbonate, polyvinylpyrrolidone, and transition metal precursor.
AU SHI W
   HU B
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2021437344
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of transition metal nano-catalyst, involves (i) dissolving potassium sulfate or potassium bicarbonate in deionized water, preparing into 0.01-1 M solution, adding 0.5-5 g/l polyvinylpyrrolidone and 1-3 g/l ethanol, then adding transition metal electrodeposition precursor, ultrasonically dispersing into electrodeposition mixed solution, (ii) using pure copper sheet or carbon material as carrier to immerse in the electrodeposition mixed solution, using platinum sheet or graphite as the counter electrode, working electrode as the base, transparent conductive oxide, the substrate is a copper sheet or a carbon material, the reference electrode is silver/silver chloride or saturated calomel electrode, maintaining the deposition potential at -0.4 V to -3.5 V, electrodepositing at constant voltage, and maintaining the deposition time for 10-200 seconds, and (iii) collecting the transition metal catalyst after electrodeposition, and cleaning with deionized water.
   USE - Preparation of transition metal nano-catalyst used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed), electrocatalysis, and oxygen reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and only needs to add new electrodeposition precursor to continuously electrodeposit. The prepared catalyst has good performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal electrodeposition precursor in step (i) includes copper (II) carbonate, silver (I) oxide, silver carbonate, silver(I) chloride, gold trioxide, zinc carbonate or tin hydroxide, and its concentration is 100-3000 mg/L, commercially available or prepared by metathesis precipitation. The carbon material in step (ii) is carbon paper, carbon cloth or graphene. The transparent conductive oxide is fluorine-doped tin oxide, tin-doped indium oxide or aluminum-doped zinc oxide. Preferred Composition: The molar concentration of potassium sulfate or potassium bicarbonate in step (i) is 0.1M, 0.2M or 0.5M.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of polyvinylpyrrolidone in step (i) is 2 g/L, and the concentration of ethanol is 1.5 g/L.

TI Catalyst useful for addition reaction of alkylene            oxides, comprises nanocomposite ion exchange            resin.
AU YU F
   HE W
   JIN M
   HEO W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SHANGHAI PETRO-CHEM TECHNOLOGY RES IN (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SHANGHAI PETRO-CHEM TECHNOLOGY RES IN (SNPC-C)
   SHANGHAI PETRO-CHEM TECHNOLOGY RES IN (SNPC-C)
GA 202133042K
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises nanocomposite ion exchange                resin (I).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for addition reaction                of alkylene oxides (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high wear resistance,                swelling resistance, and activity, and can be                continuously used for multiple times. The product                after reaction is easy to separate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises nanocomposite ion exchange                resin of formula (P1-Im+-M-) (I).
   P1 = matrix of nanocomposite resin;
   Im(+) = 5-6 elemental heterocyclic cations                containing at least 1 nitrogen atom i.e. imidazole                cations, pyrazole cations, pyrrolidine cations,                piperidine cations, piperazine cations, pyrimidine                cations, pyrazine cations, pyridazine cations and                triazine cations or 5-6 membered heteroaromatic                cations containing at least 1 nitrogen atom i.e.                imidazole cations, pyrazole cations, pyrimidine                cations, pyrazine cations, pyridazine cations or                triazine cations; and
   M(-) = anion.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising adding                styrene monomer, crosslinking agent and cage                silsesquioxane in the presence of initiator,                polymerizing to obtain a nano composite resin                matrix, and performing chloromethylation reaction,                imidazole reaction and an ion exchange reaction of                the nano composite resin matrix; and
   (2) performing addition reaction of alkylene                oxide and carbon dioxide, comprising making epoxy                resin by reacting alkanes and carbon dioxide in the                presence of the catalyst or the catalyst prepared                by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nanocomposite                  resin matrix is substituted benzene based                  compound of formula (II) and (-CH(POSS)-CH2-)                  (III). The styrenic monomer is styrenic based                  compound of formula (IV). The cage silsesquioxane                  is vinyl silsesquioxane, hydrogen                  group-containing polysilsesquioxane, alkoxy                  group-containing polysilsesquioxane, and epoxy                  group-containing polysilsesquioxane, where the                  vinyl-containing silsesquioxane is                  octavinylsilsesquioxane; the crosslinking agent                  is ethylene glycol dimethacrylate,                  dipropenylbenzene, and divinylphenyl methane or                  divinylbenzene; the initiator is benzoyl                  peroxide, azobisisobutyronitrile,                  azobisisoheptonitrile, lauroyl peroxide or                  iso-propylbenzene hydrogen peroxide, and in the                  preparation of the catalyst comprises 85-95 wt.%                  styrene monomer, 1-6 wt.% crosslinking agent,                  0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 2-5 wt.% cage                  silsesquioxane and 0.1-5 wt.% initiator. The                  alkylene oxide is alkylene oxide based compound                  of formula (V). The mass ratio of the catalyst                  and alkylene oxide is (0.001-1):1, preferably                  (0.1-0.3):1.R1-R 8 = H or 1-6C alkyl, preferably methyl,                  ethyl, propyl or butyl;POSS = cage silsesquioxane of                  (-SiO1.5)m;m = 6, 8, 10 or 12, where the mass content                  of POSS in the nanocomposite resin matrix is                  0.1-10%, preferably 2-5%; andR9-R12 = H, 1-6C alkane or 6-10C aryl                  groups, preferably methyl, ethyl, propyl, butyl                  or phenyl.Preferred Method: The addition reaction of                  alkylene oxide is carried out at 60-180 degrees                  C, preferably 100-160 degrees C under pressure of                  0.1-10 MPa, preferably 2-5 MPa for 1-8 hours,                  preferably 3-6 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M(-) = halide ions or organic acid ions,                preferably F, Cl, Br, I, acetate, formate or                hydrogen oxalate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - A container-packed drink comprises edible                aqueous solution and hydrophobic droplet containing                hydrophobic aromatic component, where aqueous                solution and hydrophobic droplet are separated, and                edible aqueous solution contains emulsifier,                hydrophobic aromatic component, and carbon dioxide                gas. Hydrophobic aromatic component is an aromatic                component of characteristic incense of lemon, lime,                or grapefruit. Content of hydrophobic liquid                composition which forms the hydrophobic droplet to                total quantity of a drink is greater than or equal                to 0.1 g/L.
   USE - A container-packed drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The fragrance from the hydrophobic aromatic                component sensed at the time of opening of a                container and drinking is strong. The drink has                excellent palatability.

Aqueous solution contains fruit juice or fruit.                  The drink is a non-heated disinfection                  container-packed drink. Preferred Properties:                  Density of hydrophobic droplet is lower than                  density of aqueous solution. Areas of opening                  portion before the container main body is sealed                  constitute less than or equal to 70% of the areas                  of the liquid level of the edible aqueous                  solution filled by the container main body.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The drink is filled in                  a drink can which uses sealing compound which has                  fluororubber or nitrile rubber as a main                  component, or a bottle can using chain-shaped low                  density polyethylene as a cap liner.
