PN CN112474026-A
TI Pre-selection of magnesite acid triggering-spiral chute comprises crushing raw ore containing magnesite, desliming, and reacting with acidic solution containing surfactant and acid to generate characteristic of carbon dioxide.
AU DENG R
   DUAN W
   WANG Y
   XING D
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202130186V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves crushing raw ore containing magnesite. The raw ore containing magnesite is desliming. The raw ore containing magnesite is reacted with acidic solution containing surfactant. The raw ore containing magnesite is reacted with acid to generate characteristic of carbon dioxide. The apparent specific gravity is reduced so as to increase the proportion difference of magnesite and silicate mineral in the raw ore so as to realize effective separation of magnesite and silicate mineral by using conventional spiral chute pre-selecting device. The surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfonate, and dosage is 100-300 g/t raw ore. The acidic solution is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid or aqueous solution of nitric acid, and the concentration is 1-5 wt.%.
   USE - Magnesite acid triggering-spiral chute pre-selection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively solves the problem that the density difference of the magnesite and silicate mineral is small, the surface property is similar to that cause the problem of difficult separation, so as to improve the pre-selected efficiency of the ore dressing.

TI Preparing low-sodium carbon aluminum hydroxide,            comprises e.g. adding aluminum hydroxide slurry as a            seed crystal into sodium aluminate solution and passing            in carbon dioxide gas for carbonation decomposition of            seed crystals.
AU ZHENG H
   CHEN C
   HAN Z
   GUO C
   ZHANG Z
AE CHALCO SHANDONG ADVANCED MATERIAL CO LTD (CNAL-C)
GA 202125196N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-sodium carbon aluminum                hydroxide, comprises adding aluminum hydroxide                slurry as a seed crystal into the sodium aluminate                solution to obtain aluminum hydroxide slurry and                passing in carbon dioxide gas for carbonation                decomposition of seed crystals to obtain low-sodium                carbon aluminum hydroxide, where solid content of                the aluminum hydroxide slurry is 300-600 g/l, and                particle size of the aluminum hydroxide in the                aluminum hydroxide slurry is 90-120 mu m.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low-sodium                carbon aluminum hydroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum hydroxide simultaneously acts on                the decomposition of seed and carbon; has both pure                carbon-based aluminum hydroxide and pure-seed                aluminum hydroxide, physical and chemical                indicators and crystal nuclei that general aluminum                hydroxide does not have; sodium oxide content of                not greater than 0.200%, the particle size D50 of                90-110 mu m, and the particle size D90 is 160-185                mu m.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                low-sodium carbon aluminum hydroxide, obtained by                above method, where low-sodium carbon aluminum                hydroxide through solid-liquid separation and                solid-liquid drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The caustic ratio ak                  of the sodium aluminate solution is 1.4-1.7; and                  silicon content index A/S of the sodium aluminate                  solution is 500-900. The ratio of aluminum                  hydroxide slurry to aluminum oxide in the sodium                  aluminate solution is 1:9. The concentration of                  the carbon dioxide gas is 33-37%. Preferred                  Method: In the carbonation decomposition of the                  seed crystal, the control temperature is 75-90                  degrees C, and the control time is 8-16. In the                  carbonation decomposition of the seed crystals,                  the controlled decomposition rate is 88-92%. In                  the carbonation decomposition of the seed                  crystals, the super-saturation of the                  decomposition slurry is controlled to be 1.2-3.5.                  The inlet flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is                  2000-3000 m3/h, and the inlet pressure of the                  carbon dioxide gas is 70-120 KPa. The method                  comprises adding aluminum hydroxide slurry into                  sodium aluminate solution, and introducing carbon                  dioxide gas, and performing crystal seed                  carbonation decomposition to obtain low-sodium                  carbon aluminum hydroxide, adding aluminum                  hydroxide slurry into first groove sodium                  aluminate solution of continuous carbon                  separation process, and introducing carbon                  dioxide gas, performing seed carbonation                  decomposition on the first groove, and then                  performing continuous carbonation decomposition                  to obtain low-sodium carbon aluminum                  hydroxide.

PN KR2223072-B1
TI Manufacturing sleeve by mixing raw materials including alumina, silica sand, soda silicate, and lightweight raw material e.g., pearlite and/or cenosphere, placing in mold, spraying carbon dioxide gas, demolding cured mixture, and drying.
AU HONG Y F
AE TAE KWANG METECH CO LTD (TAEK-Non-standard)
GA 202123552Y
AB    NOVELTY - A sleeve is manufactured by mixing raw materials to prepare mixture; placing mixture into a mold with air pressure and molding; spraying carbon dioxide (CO2) gas to molded mixture for hardening; demolding cured mixture from the mold; and drying the demolded mixture, where raw material includes alumina, silica sand, soda silicate, and lightweight raw material, and lightweight raw material is rice husk ash (RHA), cenosphere, and/or pearlite.
   USE - Manufacture of sleeve (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - By manufacturing the sleeve through curing by gas injection, high productivity is achieved because products with precise dimensions can be produced. The resulting sleeve has high heat insulation or heat generation function through weight reduction, and low hygroscopicity. Since the demolded mixture is dried at a low temperature for a short time, drying time and cost are reduced. In addition, since the sleeve has high impact resistance, cracks are prevented from occurring inside the sleeve. Compared to existing wet process, since no wastewater is generated, the working environment can be improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Mixing step comprises mixing 5-15 pts. wt. alumina, 20-35 pts. wt. silica sand, 17-35 pts. wt. silicate soda, and 20-35 pts. wt. RHA. Drying step includes drying demolded mixture at 100-120 degrees C for 30 minutes to 2 hours. Mixing step includes further mixing 2.5-3.5 pts. wt. pitch-based carbon fibers to prepare a mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further comprises spraying urea mixture onto the surface of the demolded mixture after the demolding step. Urea mixture is prepared by mixing urea resin and water in a weight ratio of 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: Raw material further comprises an acrylic emulsion.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)

PT P
PN CN112391226-A
TI Preparing pepper oil comprises e.g. taking fresh pepper, washing, removing seeds and impurities, crushing de-impounded fresh pepper, subjecting pepper powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction and mixing with the volatile oil.
AU ZENG X
AE LUXIAN YONGQING FAMILY FARM (LUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021212084
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pepper oil, comprises (1) taking fresh pepper, washing, removing seeds and impurities to obtain fresh pepper, (2) crushing the de-impounded fresh pepper to 20-60 mesh to obtain pepper powder, (3) subjecting the pepper powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain the volatile oil (I) and the extracted residue (I), (4) centrifuging the volatile oil (I) to obtain the volatile oil (II), (5) Ultrasonic de-watering the slag (I) to obtain the dewatered slag (II), (6) soaking the residue (II) in vegetable oil to obtain the pepper oil (I) is obtained, and (7) mixing the pepper oil (I) with the volatile oil (II) to obtain the finished pepper oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pepper oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of volatile oil loss due to high oil immersion temperature, retains the volatile oil and hemp substances in the fresh pepper to the greatest extent, and enriches the taste layer of the pepper oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pepper oil, obtained by the method as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (3), the extraction pressure is 20-40MPa, the extraction temperature is 40-60 , and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 17-20 L/h. In the step (4), the centrifugal condition is 2000-3500 revolutions/minute. In the step (5), the ultrasonic de-watering power is 80-120W, the time is 3-5s, the temperature is 50-70, and the settling time is 1-1.5 hours. In the step (6), the oil immersion temperature is 40-60 degrees C and the time is 5 days. In the step (7), the solid-to-liquid ratio (g/ml) of the slag (II) to the vegetable oil is 1:20-30.

PN AU2020103774-A4
TI Recovering bioenergy from organic waste comprises            constructing biological chambers with good            airtightness, filling organic wastes, adjusting e.g.            temperature to obtain gases, and anaerobically treating            residual organic waste.
AU XIAO S
   WANG Y
   WANG W
   LIU C
   LI X
AE LINYI HENGYUAN INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LINY-Non-standard)
   UNIV LINYI (UNLY-C)
GA 202117920A
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering bioenergy from organic waste                comprises (i) constructing many biological chambers                with good airtightness, filling 85-99% organic                wastes, adjusting the temperature, humidity and air                quantity in the biological chambers to obtain gases                containing carbon, nitrogen or hydrogen, (ii)                carrying out anaerobic treatment on residual                organic waste to obtain biogas and carbon dioxide                gas, and (iii) introducing the gas containing                carbon, nitrogen or hydrogen obtained in the step                (i) and the biogas and carbon dioxide gas obtained                in the step (ii) into the combustion chamber,                igniting the combustion chamber to burn the gas,                and introducing the burned gas at 70-150 degrees C                into an ORC system evaporator as a working medium                heat source of an organic Rankine cycle to exchange                heat with the working medium.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering bioenergy                from organic waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the utilization rate of                organic waste by proper treatment of organic waste                to 20%, promotes aerobic and anaerobic reaction                treatment of organic waste, hence converts organic                waste energy into heat energy, and is                economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  temperature in the biomass chamber is 20-30                  degrees C, the relative humidity is 50-60%, and                  the air quantity is 0.05-0.15 m3/hour. In the                  step (ii), in the anaerobic treatment                  specifically comprises removing impurities and                  crushing organic wastes to obtain starting raw                  materials, and adding urea into the starting raw                  materials in the carbon and nitrogen ratio of                  30:1, placing the mixed materials into a biogas                  fermentation tank, adding biogas fermentation                  inoculum, which are stirred to obtain                  fermentation raw materials, adding water into the                  tank for fermentation, and controlling the                  fermentation temperature at 25 degrees C, and                  collecting the generated gas at any time after                  3-5 days of fermentation, when the pH of the                  fermentation liquor drops to 6, and introduced                  into the combustion chamber. In the step (iii),                  the working medium of the organic Rankine cycle                  is R123.

AU WANG W

   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
   FEI W
   PENG T
   GUO X
AE BEIJING FUALIE SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO L (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202109207L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medical glue material comprises (i) taking alpha -cyano-n-butyl acrylate and alpha -cyano-n-octyl acrylate, uniformly mixing to obtain medical adhesive matrix, (ii) taking carboxymethyl chitosan in a sealed container filled with ionic liquid, removing air and filling carbon dioxide into the container, controlling the temperature and pressure in the sealed container to make the carbon dioxide gas in the supercritical state, and under the condition that the carboxymethyl chitosan is not contacted with the ionic liquid, carrying the ionic liquid to modify the carboxymethyl chitosan, obtaining the needed modified carboxymethyl chitosan, drying for further use, and (iii) slowly adding the modified carboxymethyl chitosan into the medical glue matrix, fully stirring until the system is gel-shaped, sterilizing to obtain the product.
   USE - Preparing medical glue material useful for rapid hemostasis.

PN CN111530492-A; CN111530492-B
TI Composite electro-catalyst useful in cathode            material in electro-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction            to prepare synthesis gas comprises nitrogen-doped            carbon nanotube including metal nickel and molybdenum            carbide as carrier.
AU HOU Y
   ZENG Q
   LI Z
   YANG B
   LEI L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202080787C
AB    NOVELTY - Composite electro-catalyst comprises                nitrogen-doped carbon nanotube including 10-20 wt.%                metal nickel and 5-10 wt.% molybdenum carbide as                carrier, where the metal nickel is coated in                nitrogen-doped carbon nanotube, and the molybdenum                carbide is confined in carbon layer of                nitrogen-doped carbon nanotube.
   USE - The electro-catalyst is useful in cathode                material in electro-catalytic carbon dioxide                reduction to prepare synthesis gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electro-catalyst has high selectivity, low                over-potential, and wide hydrogen/carbon monoxide                ratio in narrow potential window.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation of the composite electro-catalyst                comprising (i) dissolving carbon nitride precursor                and nickel salt in solvent A, heating and drying                solid mixture A, calcining solid mixture A in                nitrogen atmosphere, and treating with acid to                obtain nitrogen-doped carbon nanotube coated with                metal nickel, (ii) dissolving nitrogen-doped carbon                nanotube coated with metal nickel in solvent B,                heating and drying to obtain solid mixture B,                calcining solid mixture B in nitrogen atmosphere to                obtain composite electro-catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The specific surface                  area of the composite electro-catalyst is 80-120                  m2/g with pore size distribution of 2-5 nm. The                  nickel salt is nickel chloride, nickel chloride                  hexahydrate, and/or nickel nitrate hexahydrate;                  the molybdenum salt is ammonium                  tetrathiomolybdate. Preferred Process: In the                  step (i), calcining is carried out at                  500-600&#176; C for 1-3 hours, and heating at                  800-1100&#176; C for 1-3 hours. In the step                  (ii), calcining is carried out at                  700-1000&#176; C for 1-3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nitride                  precursor includes dicyandiamide and/or melamine.                  In the step (i), the mass ratio of carbon nitride                  precursor and nickel salt is 5-15:1. In the step                  (i), the mass ratio of nitrogen-doped carbon                  nanotube-coated metal nickel and molybdenum salt                  is 5-30:1.

PN CN111514366-A; CN111514366-B
TI Medical suture thread comprises polylactic acid nanomaterial prepared using L-lactide, surfactant and antibacterial drug, deacetylated chitosan, polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether, tea tree oil, cinnamon oil, sorbitol, and mannitol.
AU ZHU X
   ZHU K
AE SHANDONG ZHUSHI PHARM GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202078772F
AB    NOVELTY - A medical suture thread comprises 110-130 parts mass polylactic acid nanomaterials, 20-25 parts mass deacetylated chitosan, 3-5 parts mass polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether, 1-2 parts mass tea tree oil, 2-3 parts mass cinnamon oil, 1-2 parts mass sorbitol, and 1-2 parts mass mannitol. The preparation of polylactic acid nanomaterial involves adding L-lactide, n-octadecylamine modified graphene oxide, surfactant and antibacterial drug into pre-dried reactor, using oil pump to vacuum dry for 2-3 hours at 65-85 degrees C, adding catalyst under protection of argon, heating to 130-150 degrees C, injecting 25-30 MPa carbon dioxide gas, reacting for 15-20 hours, stirring, lowering temperature to room temperature, discharging carbon dioxide gas, and opening reactor to collect sample.
   USE - Medical suture thread.
   ADVANTAGE - The medical suture thread has high tensile strength, firm knotting, excellent biocompatibility and strong antibacterial ability, and is prepared by a non-toxic, economical, and environmentally-friendly method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the medical suture thread, which involves using 250-350 mg/L ethylene oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred component: The graphene oxide is one or more of single-layer graphene oxide, double-layer graphene oxide and triple-layer graphene oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The n-octadecylamine modified graphene oxide is prepared using graphene oxide, N,N-dimethylformamide, oxalyl chloride, dichloromethane, acid chloride, triethylamine and n-octadecylamine. The catalyst is one or more of stannous lactate, aluminum lactate and zinc lactate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The antibacterial drug is one or two of penicillin and erythromycin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The deacetylated chitosan is 75-85% deacetylated chitosan. The mass ratio of L-lactide, n-octadecylamine modified graphene oxide, surfactant, antibacterial agent and catalyst is (100-120):(5-8):(2-3):(1-2):(2-3). Preferred component: The polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether is one or more of polyethylene glycol 8000 monomethyl ether, polyethylene glycol 9000 monomethyl ether and polyethylene glycol 10000 monomethyl ether. The surfactant is one or two of octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether and nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether.

PN CN111003704-A; CN111003704-B
TI Preparation of three-dimensional graphene lithium ion battery fast charge and negative electrode material includes heating metal sodium and metal potassium, introducing mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen-containing gas and crushing.
AU WANG L
   LIU P
   XU H
   CHEN H
   CHANG K
   GAO R
   WAN W
AE SHANGHAI YULING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202032314L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of three-dimensional graphene lithium ion battery fast charge and negative electrode material comprises heating metal sodium and metal potassium at 450-700 degrees C in argon atmosphere, introducing mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen-containing gas at gas flow ratio of 1:4-4:1, reacting for 3 hours, closing mixed air flow, keeping temperature and reaction space sealed for 6-48 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain black solid, crushing black solid, sieving to 100 meshes, removing sieved matters, adding deionized water or 1-5 mol/L acid solution at mass ratio of 1:2-5, soaking for 2-11 hours, filtering product, washing and drying.
   USE - The preparation of three-dimensional graphene lithium ion battery fast charge and negative electrode material is used for lithium ion battery cathode (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent fast charge discharge capacity and long cycle life. The method has simple operation, less steps, low cost, green and pollution-free and easy industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal raw material in is room temperature liquid alloy obtained by mass ratio of metal sodium and metal potassium of 1:3-4. The nitrogen-containing gas is nitrogen (N2), ammonia (NH3), nitric oxide (NO), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), nitrous oxide (N2O) and/or nitrous oxide (N2O3). The acid solution is hydrochloric acid solution, nitric acid solution and/or sulfuric acid solution. The three-dimensional graphene lithium ion battery fast charging and negative electrode material is nitrogen-doped three-dimensional graphene material.

AU KAKAD H M
   SHELAR A B
   CHAVHANKE V R
AE KAKAD H M (KAKA-Individual)
   SHELAR A B (SHEL-Individual)
   CHAVHANKE V R (CHAV-Individual)
GA 202023914M
AB    NOVELTY - Super critical vehicle comprises filter unit comprising soft spung filled with 1% benzene drop and lime water to absorb atmospheric dust and air pollutants. PVC pipes fitted at both sides of the supercritical chamber absorb the exhaust gases as well as environmental pollution through ID fan placed at the front of the vehicle. The top back side of the rectangular supercritical chamber having two exhaust pipes which helps in circulating the fresh air in the atmosphere. The solar panel installed at the back top of super critical vehicle structure provides free energy to drive the vehicle by storing solar energy in auxiliary batteries or lithium ion battery. The collected air pollutants can be heated using solar energy in backside rectangular chamber which will help in separating the oxygen from carbon dioxide. The stored solar energy in batteries helps in operating the vehicle and vehicle accessories such as head and tail lamps and horn.
   USE - Super critical vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The super critical vehicle have excellent environment features, reduces greenhouse gases and environmental pollution up to 95%, and converts the atmospheric carbon dioxide into useful biofuel to 50 gm/l per day.

TI Mobile air conditioning apparatus used to e.g. produce cool or heat, foam blowing agents, aerosol and propellants, contains composition comprising hydrofluorocarbon-1234yf and compound having e.g. trans-hydrofluorocarbon and carbon dioxide.
AU MINOR B H
   RAO V N M
AE CHEMOURS CO FC LLC (CMFC-C)
GA 202022518A
AB    NOVELTY - Mobile air conditioning apparatus contains a composition comprising hydrofluorocarbon (HFC)-1234yf and at least one compound comprising trans-HFC-1234ze, cis-HFC-1234ze, HFC-1243zf, HFC-32 or carbon dioxide (CO2).
   USE - The apparatus is useful for producing cooling or heat, as heat transfer fluids, foam blowing agents, aerosol propellants, and fire suppression and fire extinguishing agents e.g. refrigeration, air conditioning and heat pump systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus: improves solubility of hydrofluorocarbon refrigerants in conventional refrigeration lubricants; improves oil-return to compressor in a compression refrigeration, air conditioning or heat pump apparatus; suitable means for detecting at leak point or in vicinity of apparatus; capable of reacting and foaming under proper conditions to form foam or cellular structure; prevents fire or explosion; and utilizes composition in streaming e.g. using conventional portable (or fixed) fire extinguishing equipment, to increase rate of discharge of composition from streaming or flooding equipment utilized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing cooling in the mobile air conditioning apparatus comprising evaporating the composition and after condensing the composition in the apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises: lubricant comprising polyol ester, polyalkylene glycol, polyvinyl ether, mineral oil, alkylbenzene, synthetic paraffin, synthetic naphthalene or poly(alpha)olefins; stabilizer, water scavenger or odor masking agent; and at least one UV fluorescent dye comprising naphthalimide, perylene, coumarin, anthracene, phenanthracene, xanthenes, thioxanthene, naphthoxanthenes, fluorescein and/or derivatives of the dye. Preferred Components: The composition comprises HFC-1234yf and at least one compounds comprising trans-HFC-1234ze or CO2. The composition consists of HFC-1234yf, trans-HFC-1234ze or CO2. The composition comprises HFC-1234yf and the compound comprising trans-HFC-1234ze, cis-HFC-1234ze or HFC-1243zf. The composition comprises HFC-1234yf and the hydrofluorocarbon compound comprising trans-HFC-1234ze or HFC-32. The composition comprises HFC-1234yf and hydrofluorocarbon compound comprising HFC-32 or CO2. The composition consists of HFC-1234yf and HFC-32. The composition consists of 40-99 wt.% HFC-1234yf or 1-60 wt.% HFC-32. The stabilizer comprises nitromethane, hindered phenols, hydroxylamines, thiols, phosphites or lactone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE PHILLIPS S (PHIL-Individual)

GA 202021642M
AB    NOVELTY - Animal deterrent composition comprises (1) a mixture of extracted rosemary oil and extracted rosemary solids, where the extracted rosemary solids and extracted rosemary oil are the result of substantial removal of molecules with antioxidant properties by use of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, or (2) 1.5-2.5% beta -pinenes, 11-15% camphor, 1-1.5% camphene, 4-6% borneol, 6-9% caryophyllene, 4-6% alpha - terpineol, 1-1.5% linalool, 0.5-1% myrcene, 0.5-1% beta -cymene,4-5% gamma -cadinene, 5-7% heneicosane, 3-5% octacosane, and 0.2% or less alpha -pinene and 0.2% or less bornyl acetate.
   USE - The composition is useful for providing deterrent activity against cats and other animals such as dogs, deer, and rabbits.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides enhanced, and prolonged deterrent activity against cats and other animals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a sachet, which comprises an animal deterrent comprising a mixture of extracted rosemary oil and extracted rosemary solids, where the extracted rosemary solids and extracted rosemary oil are the result of substantial removal of molecules with antioxidant properties by use of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.

GA 2020B5853Y

   USE - Production of polysulfone-carbon molecular sieve mixed matrix membrane used for separation of carbon dioxide from natural gas.

TI Decomposing organic substance e.g. waste plastic, oil-impregnated sludge, waste oil, and biomass thermally, involves blowing organic substance into fluidized bed reactor and mixing with mixed gas comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU MURAI R
   ISHII J
   WASHIMI I
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 2019756508
AB    NOVELTY - Decomposing organic substance thermally involves blowing organic substance into a fluidized bed reactor through a blow pipe installed through a side wall, introducing a mixed gas containing at least hydrogen and carbon dioxide as a fluidizing gas, and thermally decomposing an organic substance by contacting with the mixed gas. Among the pyrolysis products of the organic substance extracted from the reactor, at least a portion of the pyrolysis product that is liquid at room temperature is in the liquid state in the reactor.
   USE - Method for decomposing organic substance e.g. waste plastic, oil-impregnated sludge, waste oil, and biomass thermally (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to increase the yield of a gaseous substance when the organic substance is pyrolyzed to obtain a pyrolysis product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a thermal decomposition installation ofthe organic substance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows block diagram of thermal decomposition installation of organic substance.
   Dispersing plate (1)
   Wind box (2)
   Gas supply line (3)
   Supply pipe/tube (4)
   Storage tank (5)

PN CN110127679-A
TI Preparing graphene by nitromethane intercalation graphite using ball milling process comprises e.g. dissolving anhydrous ferric chloride in nitromethane, adding graphite, heating under constant stirring state, and depressurizing.
AU MIAO Z
   GONG Y
   HU X
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 201972762Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene by nitromethane intercalation graphite using ball milling process comprises (i) dissolving anhydrous ferric chloride in nitromethane, adding graphite, heating under constant stirring state to obtain binary graphite intercalation compound, in which graphite ferric chloride and nitromethane are intercalated with graphite, (ii) placing binary graphite in a high pressure ball mill with dry ice seal, allowing to stand at room temperature, and where dry ice absorbs heat into carbon dioxide gas, (iii) starting a ball mill, decomposing nitromethane under impact of the steel ball, where the carbon dioxide gas absorbs the heat of the explosion and the heat of the steel ball, colliding rapidly into the supercritical state, and (iv) stopping operation of the ball mill, allowing to stand, depressurizing pressure-resistant high pressure ball mill and collecting product to obtain graphene.
   USE - The method is preparing graphene by nitromethane intercalation graphite using ball milling process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases binary graphite intercalation compound and the graphite interlayer distance; improves the peeling efficiency of ball milling; increases the interlayer distance in the ball milling process; and prevents the superposition of graphene sheets.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), anhydrous ferric chloride to graphite are taken in a mass ratio of 50:1-10:1, heating at constant temperature of 20-80 degrees C, and refluxing for 24-144 hours. In the step (ii), when all dry ice is converted into gas, then carbon dioxide pressure in the ball mill is higher than 7.38 MPa. In the step (ii), allowing to stand for 0.5-24 hours. In the step (iii), the ball milling process is carried out at speed of 100-1000 revolutions/minute for 0.5-24 hours.

AU GUO W
   CHEN W

AE INST APPLIED PHYSICS JIANGXI ACAD SCI (PHYS-Non-standard)
GA 201968113B
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-modified silicon-containing magnesium alloy comprises 0-6.5 wt.% aluminum, 0.5-2.0 wt.% silicon, 0.1-1.0 wt.% nickel, and magnesium and un-avoidable impurities (balance).
   USE - Used as nickel-modified silicon-containing magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method refines grain size of the alloy by adding nickel into the silicon-containing magnesium alloy, metamorphizes magnesium silicide strengthening phase in the silicon-containing magnesium alloy, and functions as a microstructure refining and alloy strengthening.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing nickel-modified silicon-containing magnesium alloy, comprising (i) preheating pure magnesium, pure aluminum, pure silicon, drying, preheating the refining agent and mold, (ii) melting pure magnesium under protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas, when the melt temperature is raised to 710-730 degrees C, adding the preheated pure aluminum, pure silicon, and nickel-containing intermediate alloy, stirring until the alloying elements completely dissolved in the melt, heating the melt to 740-760 degrees C, refining by adding refining agent, cooling to 710-740 degrees C, and maintaining at 740-760 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, and (iii) removing the dross, and carrying out the casting under the protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for adding the nickel-containing intermediate alloy is as follows: drying the nickel-containing intermediate alloy, wrapping in an aluminum foil, rapidly pressing into the melt liquid surface for 10-15 minutes with bell jar, maintaining the melt at 740-760 degrees Cfor 30-40 minutes, refining and casting the alloy liquid. The volume fraction of sulfur hexafluoride in the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride is 0.5%. In the method, the casting is formed by die casting, low pressure casting, metal casting or sand casting. In the method, the preheating temperature of pure magnesium, pure aluminum, and pure silicon is 160-210 degrees C, and the preheating time is 15-25 minutes, and the preheating temperature of refining agent and mold is 160-210 degrees C, and the preheating time is 25-55 minutes. Preferred Components: The nickel-containing intermediate alloy is in the form of magnesium-nickel intermediate alloy or aluminum-nickel intermediate alloy.

PN CN109880879-A
TI Detecting bacterial culture, comprises placing a photosenssitive chip at the bottom of the culture bottle.
AU YANG H
   ZHENG Y
   ZHAO W
AE ZHANGJIAGANG GAOPINCHENG MEDICAL EQUIP (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201954923E
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting bacterial culture, comprises placing a photosenssitive chip at the bottom of the culture bottle. The photosensitive chip comprises a carbon dioxide/pH sensor chip and an oxygen voltage-dividing sensor chip, a fluorescent glue layer wrapped on the surface of the carbondioxide/pH sensor chip and the oxygen voltage-dividing sensor chip. The carbon dioxide/pH sensor chip increases the fluorescence intensity of the first emitted light as the pH of the culture solution decreases. The O2 partial pressure sensing chip increases the fluorescence intensity of the excited second emitted light as the oxygen content in the culture solution decreases. The bacteria and culture solution are put into the culture bottle, and then sealing the growth gas. The temperature of the culture flask is adjusted as needed.
   USE - Method for detecting bacterial culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to detect bacterial culture, which has simple steps, low cost, low operation difficulty, good detection effect and high accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The fluorescence intensity of the first emitted light and the second emitted light at different time points are collected,a change in the content of the bacteria in the culture solution is determined according to the change in the fluorescence intensity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide/pH sensor chip is irradiated with the first excitation light by the light source, and the oxygen voltage-dividing sensor chip is irradiated with the second excitation light by the light source. The first excitation light and the second excitation light are collimated by a collimating lens. The first emitted light and the second emitted light are focused by using an optical focusing lens. The temperature of the culture flask is adjusted by an incubator, and the temperature of the incubator is 32-42 degrees C. The adjustment accuracy of the incubator is 0.5-1 degrees C. The growing gas introduced includes carbon dioxide and/or oxygen. The culture bottle is in a state of being slightly oscillated.

PN CN109851689-A; CN109851689-B
TI Preparation of levoglucosan, involves adding agricultural and forestry waste and water to hydrothermal reaction kettle, obtaining solid product, placing in pyrolysis reactor, and subjecting volatile matter to fractional condensation.
AU WANG X
   ZHENG D
   CHEN Y
   YANG H
   YANG Q
   LI J
   ZENG K
   HU J
   CHEN H
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019538598
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of levoglucosan involves (1) adding pulverized agricultural and forestry waste and water to the hydrothermal reaction kettle at a mass to volume ratio of 1:5-1:20, (2) filling the hydrothermal reaction vessel with 0-2 MPa carbon dioxide, sealing the hydrothermal reaction vessel, increasing the temperature to 150-250 degrees C under stirring, and maintaining for 10-50 minutes, (3) cooling hydrothermal reaction vessel, collecting reaction product, separating and drying by vacuum filtration to obtain solid product after hydrothermal pretreatment, (4) placing hydrothermally pretreated solid product in a pyrolysis reactor which is fluidized bed or fixed bed, and pyrolyzing in carbon dioxide atmosphere at 350-420 degrees C, and (5) subjecting volatile matter after pyrolysis to fractional condensation includes primary condensation and secondary condensation to obtain liquid product having a higher concentration of levoglucosan.
   USE - Preparation of levoglucosan by using agricultural and forestry waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The maximum conversion of agricultural and forestry waste to levoglucosan is promoted. The yield of levoglucosan in the bio-oil obtained by direct pyrolysis of biomass is improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The agricultural and forestry waste includes rice husks, straw, corn cobs, and wood chips.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (4), the fixed bed pyrolysis reaction time is 20-50 minutes. In step (5), the primary condensation temperature is 120-160 degrees C, and secondary condensation temperature is 20-40 degrees C. Preferred Properties: In step (1), the particle size of selected biomass particles is 60-120 mesh after the agricultural and forestry waste is pulverized and sieved.

PN RU2684932-C1
TI Method for repair and insulation works in well has limiting water inflows and improving efficiency of wells.
AU ANTONIADI D G
   KLIMOV V V
   USOV S V
   SAVENOK O V
   LESHKOVICH N M
   BURKOVA A A
AE UNIV KUBAN STATE TECHNOLOGICAL (UYKU-Non-standard)
GA 201940455B
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the oil and gas industry and can be used for repair and insulation works in wells for the elimination of interstratal flow of fluids, limiting water inflows and improving the efficiency of wells. According to the method, a working solution is prepared for pumping into an insulated interval with a concentration of (15.67-25.03)% density of (1031-1054)kg/m3. For this purpose, mix technical calcium oxide and industrial water. Get the milk of lime. Amount of milk of lime depends on the injectivity of the isolated interval. Annular space of the well is used as an insulated interval. After pumping, the working solution is bubbled with carbon dioxide up to formation of a carbonate crust on the outer surface of the column and a hardening effect due to mutual adhesion and coalescence of the formed submicrocrystalline calcium hydroxide particles. Required amount of carbon dioxide is determined stoichiometrically by a chemical equation.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is an increase in the sealing properties of the composition to eliminate fluid flows downstream of the production columns in oil and gas wells, reducing the formation time of the tamping agent when filling the flow channels in the well.

V-groove is provided with a groove angle of 40 to 45 degrees . The tube is welded to the plate by molten MAG welding. The shielding gas is a mixture of argon and carbon dioxide. The proportion of argon gas is 30% to 20%, and the proportion of carbon dioxide is 70% to 80%.
   USE - Method for single-sided welding and double-sided welding of molten MAG welded pipe sheet.
   ADVANTAGE - The construction cost is reduced, the construction efficiency is improved, and welding defects are avoided.

PN CN108849227-A
TI Method for cultivating bamboo fungus, involves            crushing bamboo stalk followed by preparing cultivation            material, laying in open container, cultivating bamboo            fungus, spraying aerosol and water and harvesting            bamboo fungus.
AU SHANG J
AE GUIZHOU ZHIJIN ZIMEI BAMBOO FUNGUS IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201895239X
AB    NOVELTY - A bamboo fungus cultivation method involves                crushing bamboo stalk, processing obtained bamboo                powder at 100-120 degrees C for 15-30 minutes,                cooling the bamboo powder to normal temperature,                weighing 500-1000 pts. wt. bamboo powder, spraying                bamboo sap nutrient solution on the bamboo powder                and uniformly mixing to obtain cultivation                material, laying the cultivation material in an                open container to a thickness of 8-16 cm,                cultivating bamboo fungus for every 6-10 cm,                placing the container with the bamboo fungus in                cultivation room, maintaining at 20-25 degrees C,                monitoring concentration of carbon dioxide in                cultivation room, ventilating the cultivation room                for 8 hours, when concentration of carbon dioxide                exceeds 0.04%, monitoring moisture content of the                cultivation room within 3 cm of surface of the                cultivation material in the cultivation room,                spraying aerosol, when moisture content is less                than 60% and water every time for 30-60 minutes and                harvesting bamboo fungus after 75-85 days of                cultivation.
   USE - Method for cultivating bamboo fungus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating bamboo fungus                with high yield and low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The bamboo sap                  nutrient solution comprises 25-35 pts. wt.                  soybean powder, 10-15 pts. wt. maize flour, 15-25                  pts. wt. glucose, 5-10 pts. wt. molasses, 12-24                  pts. wt. yeast extract, 7-14 pts. wt. calcium                  dihydrogen carbonate and 50-80 pts. wt. distilled                  water.

TI Extracting wild ginseng medicinal material by drying wild ginseng, crushing dried material, removing heavy metal, baking particles, crushing baked particle, adding auxiliary material, and extracting effective components.
AU 
AE TONGCHENG HEXI ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018865791
AB    NOVELTY - Process for extracting wild ginseng medicinal material involves (a) cleaning and washing wild ginseng, drying and crushing the dried material to obtain traditional Chinese material particles of 20-30 mesh sieve, (b) removing the heavy metal from traditional Chinese material particles by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, (c) baking the traditional Chinese material particles free of heavy metal at 45-50 degrees C for 50-60 minutes, until the water content is 0.3-0.5 wt.%, (d) ultra-finely crushing the baked particle to obtain traditional Chinese material particle size of 0.35-0.75 mu m, and (e) adding auxiliary material to the traditional Chinese material particle, mixing uniformly, and extracting effective components by low-temperature micro-nanometer wall breaking method to obtain precious plant traditional Chinese medicinal extract, where the auxiliary material is black rice syrup, tea polyphenol or sodium carboxymethyl cellulose.
   USE - The process is useful for extracting wild ginseng medicinal material.
   ADVANTAGE - The process avoids heavy metal pollution by removing the heavy metal from traditional Chinese material particles by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process.

TI Method for preparing de-sulfurized and de-nitrated composite material, involves mixing calcium-magnesium oxide and formaldehyde solution, followed by subjecting to carbonization reaction with carbon dioxide gas.

   CHEN J
AE BEIJING SILVER LINING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201871793H
AB    NOVELTY - A de-sulfurized and de-nitrated composite material preparing method involves mixing calcium-magnesium oxide or hydroxide and formaldehyde solution with a certain concentration for the complex to obtain compound slurry. The compound slurry is subjected to carbonization reaction with the carbon dioxide gas in the stator-rotor reactor. The reaction liquid after carbonization reaction with the ammonia water in the stator-rotor reactor is mixed and subjected to polycondensation, maintained the slurry temperature during polycondensation reaction process, utilized stator-rotor of reaction slurry reactor for circular reaction, added ferric nitrate solution according to a certain proportion, performed continuously crystallizing reaction for slurry circulating reaction, subjected the slurry to solid-liquid separation, recycled the liquid circulation for preparing calcium magnesium oxide slurry and dried the solid to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing de-sulfurized and de-nitrated composite material.

PT P
PN CN108251638-A; CN108251638-B
TI Extracting lithium from lithium-containing gold emerald-type beryllium ore by mixing ore and sulfuric acid, calcining, mixing water, extracting with diphosphorus tetraoxide, tributyl phosphate and oil, and introducing carbon dioxide gas.
AU TIAN X
   DENG C
   ZHANG X
   YANG S
   ZHOU X
   FANG Y
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
   UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201855322B
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium from lithium-containing gold emerald-type beryllium ore comprises crushing and grinding ore to less than 0.074 mm fineness; mixing industrial sulfuric acid with ore after grinding, and calcining under microwave irradiation at 125-150 degrees C for 1-4 hours; mixing ore and water at mass to volume ratio of 1:2-3 kg/L, heating slurry to 65-95 degrees C, leaching for 4-16 hours, adjusting pH of slurry to 4.5-5.5 with lime, standing for more than 1 hour, and liquid-solid separating to obtain lithium beryllium leachate; taking mixture of diphosphorus tetraoxide, tributyl phosphate and 260# solvent oil as extracting agent to extract leachate and back extracting with sodium hydroxide solution to obtain lithium beryllium back extract; heating and hydrolyzing at 55-100 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, standing for 2-6 hours, and filtering to obtain primary beryllium hydroxide and filtrate; and introducing carbon dioxide gas into filtrate to produce lithium carbonate white precipitate.
   USE - Method for extracting lithium from lithium-containing gold emerald-type beryllium ore.

GA 201844343R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves distilling a pre-treating agent after adding urine, monitoring the conductivity of a distillate in real time. The distillation is stopped, and the remaining concentrate is discarded. The distillate is sequentially filtered using a first fiber activated carbon filter element, a second fiber activated carbon filter element, and a polypropylene filter film. The catalyst is used for the catalytic oxidation of the filtrate to obtain gaseous water and carbon dioxide. The gaseous water is condensed into liquid water, and carbon dioxide. The liquid water is ion-exchanged with ions that are freely exchanged in ion exchange resin and water in the water to remove ions other than H+ and OH? in water to obtain effluent. The conductivity, pH value, total organic carbon TOC value, and total number of colonies is obtained from water.
   USE - Method for treating urine to obtain drinking water.

TI Preparing 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate and co-producing 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol comprises adding 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide in hydrogen halide solution and then reacting with carbon dioxide in presence of organic bases.
AU MA H
   LV J
   WANG B
   ZHAO B
   QIN Y
   GU Y
   ZENG J
   GU Z
   TU D
   LI C
   JIA Z
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
   XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 201839855A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate and co-producing 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol comprises adding 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide as raw material in hydrogen halide solution, reacting with carbon dioxide in presence of organic bases at 70-180 degrees C, at 0.1-5 MPa to obtain crude 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol and 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate, separating and then refining to obtain 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol and 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate, where amount of hydrogen halide is 0.5-20% the molar amount of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide; organic base is dialkylamine, trialkylamine, N,N-dimethylamide or nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compounds; and amount of organic base is 0.5-20% the molar amount of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate and co-producing 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate: has simple preparation process and mild reaction condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen halide solution is 55-58 wt.% hydroiodic acid, 48 wt.% hydrobromic acid, 36-38 wt.% hydrochloric acid or 40 wt.% hydrofluoric acid. The organic base is trialkylamine, N,N-dimethylacetamide, imidazole, alkyl imidazole, pyridine, 4-dimethylaminopyridine, 1,8-diazabicyclo undec-7-ene, 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)-5-nonene, 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene, 7-Methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene or triethylenediamine. The amount of hydrogen halide is 3-15% the molar amount of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide, preferably 5-10 wt.%; and amount of organic base is 3-15% the molar amount of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide, preferably 5-10 wt.%. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, and pressure is 0.5-2 MPa. The method comprises adding 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide as raw material in hydrogen halide solution, reacting with carbon dioxide in presence of triethylamine at 120 degrees C, at 0.5 MPa to obtain crude 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol and 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate, separating and then refining to obtain 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol and 4-trifluoromethyl ethylene carbonate, where amount of hydrogen halide is 10% the molar amount of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide; amount of water is 50% the molar mass of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide; and amount of triethylamine is 10% the molar amount of 3,3,3-trifluoropropylene oxide.

GA 2018345501

PN DE102016008150-A1
TI Method for operating high-efficiency powertrain of            carbon dioxide-minimized high-power engine in e.g.            passenger car, involves accelerating driving wheels by            working driving units according to driving conditions            and road guide.
AU ERDMANN C M
AE ERDMANN C M (ERDM-Individual)
GA 2018005654
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves accelerating driving                wheels by working driving units (1) according to                driving conditions and road guide. The driving                units are provided with a motor and a generator                that are equipped with each other without using a                coupling unit and/or a gear unit. The driving units                are arranged to a wheel hub. A driving battery                and/or an onboard network battery are supported in                a defined charging state, where acceleration and/or                torque of driven wheels can be changed during a                vehicle traveling process.
   USE - Method for operating high-efficiency                powertrain of a carbon dioxide-minimized high-power                engine in a vehicle e.g. motor vehicle such as                passenger car, and light truck.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accelerating the driving                wheels by the working drive units according to                driving conditions and road guide, so that                occupants and supplementary luggage can be                transported and protected during the traveling                process, and effective utilization of place and                energy can be enabled.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                passenger car.
   Working drive units (1)
   Inactive driving unit (2)

PT P
PN WO2022084995-A1
TI Producing hydrogen, by contacting water, iron-containing coal combustion product, and carbon dioxide source chosen from carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide precursor thereby producing hydrogen, where process is performed in reactor in absence of external heating.
AU VUKSAN S
AE TYKHE TECH PTE LTD (TYKH-Non-standard)
   SAAR R (SAAR-Individual)
GA 202257146K
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing hydrogen, involves contacting water, an iron-containing coal combustion product, and a carbon dioxide source chosen from carbon dioxide and a carbon dioxide precursor thereby producing hydrogen, where the process is performed in a reactor in the absence of external heating.
   USE - The process is used for producing hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process further involves collecting the produced hydrogen, and post-treating the produced hydrogen. The post-treating involves adopting gas separation, filtration, liquification and drying, and pretreating the iron-containing coal combustion product prior to the step of contacting water, an iron-containing coal combustion product, and a carbon dioxide source. The pretreating involves milling the iron-containing coal combustion product and enriching the iron content of the iron-containing coal combustion product. The process further involves adding an anti-caking agent to the reactor. The process involves (a) dispersing an iron-containing coal combustion product in water; and (b) adding a carbon dioxide source to the dispersion of step (a) for generating a reaction, or (a) supplementing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide source to the water, and (b) adding an iron-containing coal combustion product to the water supplemented with carbon dioxide of step (a) thereby generating a reaction. The process further involves adding an acid to the water. The process involves (a) dispersing the iron-containing coal combustion product in water, (b) adding hydrochloric acid to the dispersion of step (a), and (c) adding a carbon dioxide source to the dispersion of step (b) thereby producing hydrogen. The process further involves performing carbon dioxide capture and storage, and recycling of the coal combustion product. Preferred Conditions: The process is performed at less than or equal to 100 degrees C, preferably -5 to 50 degrees C with no addition of external electric energy. The process is performed at a pH of less than or equal to 6.5 or less, and 1-350 Bar, preferably 1-100 Bar. Preferred Components: The water is in a liquid phase. The water is chosen from tap water, sea water, partially purified water, deionized water, distilled water, brackish water, and waste water. The iron-containing coal combustion product comprises coal ash, fly ash, bottom ash, boiler residue, and/or heavy oil ash. The iron-containing coal combustion product originates from a power plant, a fuel boiler, or from cement production. The iron-containing coal combustion product comprises a divalent iron oxide, and/or a trivalent iron oxide, preferably a trivalent iron oxide. The iron-containing coal combustion product comprises iron(II) oxide, iron(II,III) oxide, and iron(III) oxide. The anti-caking agent comprises tricalcium phosphate, powdered cellulose, magnesium stearate, sodium ferrocyanide, potassium ferrocyanide, calcium ferrocyanide, calcium phosphate, sodium silicate, silicon dioxide, calcium silicate, magnesium trisilicate, talc powder, sodium aluminosilicate, potassium aluminum silicate, calcium aluminosilicate, bentonite, aluminum silicate, stearic acid, and/or polydimethylsiloxane. Preferred Composition: The iron-containing coal combustion product comprises 2-40 wt.%/wt.% iron oxide, and 25-75 wt.%/wt.% silicon dioxide. The carbon dioxide source is a carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide gas is originated from at least one of pure industrial carbon dioxide, flue gas, a carbon dioxide-producing plant, and atmospheric carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide gas is atmospheric carbon dioxide and the process further comprises atmospheric carbon dioxide sequestering. The carbon dioxide source is dry ice. The process carbon dioxide precursor comprises carbonic acid, a carbonate, and/or bicarbonate. Preferred Property: The produced hydrogen has purity of at least about 85%.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN JP2018176070-A; JP6681851-B2
TI Extracting carbon dioxide cultivating microalgae chosen from Cyanobacteria, diatom, yellowish green algae, dinoflagellate, red algae, brown algae and green algae, by extracting carbon dioxide from carbonated spring.
AU HIGASHIZONO Y
   FUJII T
AE TOYOTA BODY RES & DEV CO LTD (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA BODY RES & DEV CO LTD (TOYT-C)
GA 201889966C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting carbon dioxide, involves extracting carbon dioxide from carbonated spring.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting carbon dioxide, which is used for cultivating microalgae chosen from Cyanobacteria, diatom, yellowish green algae, dinoflagellate, red algae, brown algae and green algae (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts carbon dioxide for cultivating microalgae with mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for cultivating microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves adjusting the pH of the carbonated spring before the carbon dioxide extraction, performing physical stimulation process to carbonated spring, adjusting the temperature of the carbonated spring. The pH of the carbonated spring is adjusted to 8 or less, preferably 5.2 or less. The physical stimulation is performed by stirring at 150 revolutions per minute (rpm) or more. The temperature of the carbonated spring in the temperature adjustment is adjusted to 20 degrees or higher.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Environment-friendly greenhouse for controlling            carbon dioxide, has carbon dioxide input pipe provided            with automatic adjusting valve, and planting area            provided with carbon dioxide concentration detector            which is interlocked with automatic adjusting            valve.

AB    NOVELTY - The environment-friendly greenhouse has a drip                irrigation pipe (2011) that is provided above a                planting platform (2012), for irrigating the plant                on the planting platform. The drip irrigation pipe                is connected with the second purified water outlet.                One side of the greenhouse main portion (1) is                provided with a door (101). One side of the                greenhouse main portion opposite to the door is                provided with a first ventilation window (103). The                planting area (2) is provided with a carbon dioxide                input pipe (102) passing through the greenhouse                main portion. The carbon dioxide input pipe is                provided with an automatic adjusting valve (108).                The planting area is provided with a carbon dioxide                concentration detector (109). The carbon dioxide                concentration detector is interlocked with the                automatic adjusting valve.
   USE - Environment-friendly greenhouse for                controlling carbon dioxide used for cultivating                plant facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-controlled                environment-friendly greenhouse is integrated with                carbon dioxide absorption treatment, sewage                treatment and greenhouse planting, can absorb                carbon dioxide, and purify the sewage. The purified                sewage reaches the standard of recycling again,                energy saving and emission reduction are ensured,                and carbon emission is reduced, protecting                environment and water resource characteristics are                improved. The concentration of the carbon dioxide                in the greenhouse is real-time controlled without                manual operation of characteristics.

PN CN114515494-A
TI Direct air capturing carbon dioxide energy-saving            system for use in industrial application or biological            application, has acid-alkali swinging regeneration            device for concentrating alkali liquid to generate acid            liquid, and concentrated alkaline liquid whose outlet            is connected with spraying device.
AU FANG M
   XIA Z
   LIU W
   WANG T
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202273138A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an acid-alkali swinging                regeneration device for concentrating alkali liquid                to generate acid liquid. An outlet of the                concentrated alkaline liquid is connected with a                spraying device, where the generated acid liquid                enters into a carbon dioxide regeneration device                and rich in carbon dioxide-rich liquid reaction to                generate carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide                regeneration device partially enters rich in                carbonate ion-rich solution to generate carbon                dioxide gas, where acid liquid after carbon dioxide                regeneration enters into a valence ion sieving                device to receive an oxygen-chamber (13). A portion                of the acid liquid is entered into the valence ion                sieving device, or a portion enters an ion                screening device to receive an oxygen                chamber.
   USE - Direct air capturing carbon dioxide                energy-saving system for use in industrial                application or biological application. Uses include                but are not limited to carbon dioxide capture and                utilization (CCUS) technology for large-scale                reduction in greenhouse gas emissions in slowing                global warming and economical way.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can accurately control the                regeneration process, distribution lifting carbon                dioxide concentration, effectively reducing the                energy consumption of the direct air carbon dioxide                capture system. The system is featured with simple                structure, low cost and high energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a direct                air capturing carbon dioxide energy-saving                method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                direct air capturing carbon dioxide energy-saving                system with precise ion control.
   Air distribution device (1)
   Positive plate (8)
   Positive ion film (9)
   Different valence state ion sieving anion                film (10)
   Oh-chamber (11)
   Oh-chamber (12)
   Oxygen-chamber (13)

PT P
PN CN114046610-A; CN114046610-B
TI Carbon dioxide refrigeration system using            liquefied natural gas cold energy, has liquefied            natural gas cold energy ladder is transferred to the            cold spots for different temperature zones through            carbon dioxide, system uses carbon dioxide as the            refrigerant.
AU LAO L
   JIANG Y
   YAO L
   PENG X
   TAN F
   REN M
   HAN Y
AE HANGZHOU OXYGEN PLANT GROUP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGYANG GROUP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202233108S
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system using                liquefied natural gas cold energy, has liquefied                natural gas cold energy ladder is transferred to                the cold spots for different temperature zones                through carbon dioxide (CO2), system uses carbon                dioxide (CO2) as the refrigerant, the system is                equipped with a heat exchanger system, heat                exchanger system consists of low temperature                cooling heat exchanger (E01), medium and low                temperature cooling capacity heat exchanger (E02),                high temperature cooling capacity heat exchanger                (E03), refrigerant heat transfer heat exchanger                (E04), CO2 cooling capacity conversion heat                exchanger (E05), CO2 cooling heat recovery heat                exchanger (E06) and natural gas (NG) reheating heat                exchanger (E07) composition. Low temperature                circulating refrigerant is propane, propylene,                ethane, ethylene, R410A (RTM: difluoromethane and                pentafluoroethane), R404A (RTM: pentafluoroethane,                trifluoroethane, tetrafluoroethane), and/or                ethylene glycol.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system using                liquefied natural gas cold energy.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide refrigeration system utilizing                liquefied natural gas (LNG) cold energy cancels a                plurality of sets of electric equipment, namely                refrigeration compressors, and only a circulating                pump is arranged in the system, thus achieving the                purpose of saving energy compared with the                traditional carbon dioxide refrigeration system and                saving the electricity consumption by more than 2/3                compared with the conventional carbon dioxide                refrigeration system with the same scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                using a carbon dioxide refrigeration system                utilizing liquefied natural gas (LNG) cold                energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                first process variant of carbon dioxide                refrigeration system using liquefied natural gas                cold energy. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Low temperature cooling heat exchanger                (E01)
   High temperature cooling capacity heat                exchanger (E03)
   Refrigerant heat transfer heat exchanger                (E04)
   Carbon dioxide cooling capacity conversion                heat exchanger (E05)
   Natural gas reheating heat exchanger                (E07)

PT P
PN EP3685904-A1; WO2020152330-A1; CA3127683-A1; EP3914374-A1; US2022097004-A1
TI Separating and recovering carbon dioxide from            ambient air, comprises contacting ambient air with            aqueous solution of alkali metal or alkaline earth            metal cation, performing electrodialysis and thermal            desorption, separating water.
AU MAKARUK A
   SZIVACZ J
AE AXIOM ANGEWANDTE PROZESSTECHNIK GMBH (AXIO-Non-standard)
   MAKARUK A (MAKA-Individual)
   AXIOM ANGEWANDTE PROZESSTECHNIK GMBH (AXIO-Non-standard)
   MAKARUK A (MAKA-Individual)
   AXIOM ANGEWANDTE PROZESSTECHNIK GMBH (AXIO-Non-standard)
   MAKARUK A (MAKA-Individual)
GA 2020706719
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and recovering carbon dioxide from                the ambient air, comprises: (a) contacting ambient                air with an aqueous solution of at least one alkali                metal or alkaline earth metal cation to absorb the                carbon dioxide into the solution to form the                hydrogen carbonate or Carbonate of the at least one                metal; (b) performing electrodialysis of the                resulting solution to obtain a solution enriched in                (hydrogen) carbonate ions and a solution depleted;                (c) performing thermal desorption of the carbon                dioxide from the solution obtained in step (b) and                enriched in (hydrogen) carbonate ions by steam                stripping to obtain a carbon dioxide or water vapor                mixture; and (d) separating off water from the                resulting carbon dioxide or water vapor mixture and                optionally further drying the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and                recovering carbon dioxide from the ambient                air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant for the continuous implementation of a method                for separating and recovering carbon dioxide from                the ambient air comprising at least one absorber                for bringing ambient air into contact with an                aqueous solution an alkali or alkaline earth metal                cation for absorbing the carbon dioxide into the                solution to form the hydrogen carbonate or                carbonate of the at least one metal, an                electrodialysis separator comprising ion exchange                membranes (A, AK) for carrying out an ion exchange                to obtain a solution enriched in (hydrogen)                carbonate ions and a solution, a desorption column                for performing steam stripping of the solution                enriched in (hydrogen) carbonate ions to obtain a                carbon dioxide or water vapor mixture; and (d) a                condenser for separating water from the resulting                carbon dioxide or water vapor mixture by                condensation and optionally a dryer for the carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a) as a solution                  of the at least one alkali or alkaline earth                  metal cation, the water of a natural or                  artificial lake with a sufficiently high                  concentration of alkali or alkaline earth metal                  ions, e.g. a quarry pond or open pit lake, is                  used, the sufficiently high ion concentration is                  optionally preset by adding base. In step (a) the                  aqueous solution of the at least one alkali metal                  or alkaline earth metal cation is brought into                  contact with the ambient air in a spray scrubber                  or spray tower. The bipolar ion exchange                  membranes, anion-selective ion exchange membranes                  or ion exchange membranes selective for                  monovalent anions are used for the                  electrodialysis in step (b). In step (b)                  selective ion exchange membranes are used for                  monovalent anions and a solution enriched in                  hydrogen carbonate ions and a solution are                  obtained. The solution obtained in step (b) and                  depleted in (hydrogen) carbonate ions is recycled                  to step (a). The steam stripping in step (c) is                  carried out in a packed column, optionally under                  reduced pressure. The solution subjected to steam                  stripping in step (c) is recycled to step (b) to                  enrich it again in (hydrogen) carbonate ions. In                  step (d) the separation of water from the                  resulting carbon dioxide or water vapor mixture                  is carried out by cooling for condensation of the                  water vapor. The (hydrogen) carbonate                  ion-enriched, relatively cold solution obtained                  in step (b) is subjected to a heat exchange with                  (i) the relatively hot solution which has already                  been subjected to steam stripping before being                  recycled to step (b) to form the former solution                  to warm and cool the second solution; and/or ii)                  is subjected to the carbon dioxide or water vapor                  mixture obtained during steam stripping to heat                  it and to cool the carbon dioxide or water vapor                  mixture in order to condense the water vapo. The                  condensate obtained during the cooling of the                  carbon dioxide-water vapor mixture is recycled to                  step (b) and/or recycled to step (c) and water                  vapor for the steam stripping is generated.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The absorber is a                  spray washer. The electrodialysis separator is                  provided with (i) bipolar ion exchange membranes;                  (ii) anion-selective ion exchange membrane; or                  (iii) ion exchange membranes selective for                  monovalent anions. The electrodialysis separator                  and the absorber are connected via a line for                  recycling the solution depleted in (hydrogen)                  carbonate ions. A heating device is provided                  between the electrodialysis separator and the                  desorption column for heating the solution                  enriched in (hydrogen) carbonate ions before the                  steam stripping. The desorption column for                  introducing water vapor is connected to an                  evaporator and/or is connected to a vacuum pump                  in order to generate a negative pressure. The                  desorption column and the electrodialysis                  separator are connected via a line for recycling                  the solution subjected to steam stripping. The                  heating device is a heat exchanger in order to                  subject the relatively cold solution enriched in                  (hydrogen) carbonate ions in the electrodialysis                  separator to a heat exchange with (i) the                  relatively hot solution which has already been                  subjected to steam stripping before being                  recycled to the electrodialysis separator to heat                  the former solution and cool the second solution;                  and/or (ii) to subject the carbon dioxide or                  water vapor mixture obtained during steam                  stripping in order to heat it and to cool the                  carbon dioxide or water vapor mixture for                  condensation of the water vapor. The condenser                  for recycling the condensate obtained on cooling                  the carbon dioxide or water vapor mixture (i) is                  connected to the electrodialysis separator via a                  line; and/or (ii) is connected to the evaporator                  via a line to generate water vapor again from the                  condensate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
