PN EP3981485-A1; WO2022074174-A1
TI Bottom chamber of stripper device used to reduce the content in ammonia and carbon dioxide in liquid withdrawn involves e.g. guiding plate for guiding fluid falling from intermediate section walls towards lower cylindrical section side wall.
AU PORRO L G
   BYNES A
AE YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
GA 202250138S
AB    NOVELTY - Bottom chamber of stripper device, comprising container (1) comprising lower cylindrical section (2), intermediate section (3) and upper cylindrical section (4), many openings (6) located on top wall of upper cylindrical section, liquid outlet (7) located at the bottom of lower cylindrical section of the container and concentric with lower cylindrical section of the container, comprises: e.g. (i) vortex breaker joined to the bottom of lower cylindrical section of container, vortex breaker comprising top circular plate parallel with bottom of lower cylindrical section and concentric with liquid outlet; and (ii) guiding plate for guiding fluid falling from walls of intermediate section towards side wall of lower cylindrical section and the guiding plate is concentric with liquid outlet.
   USE - The bottom chamber is useful: in a stripper and a urea plant; and for reducing the content in ammonia and carbon dioxide in the liquid withdrawn from the bottom chamber of the stripper via the liquid outlet (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bottom chamber reduces the content in ammonia and carbon dioxide in the liquid withdrawn via the liquid outlet.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bottom chamber of a stripper device, comprising a container (1) comprising a lower cylindrical section (2), an intermediate section (3) and an upper cylindrical section (4), many openings (6) located on the top wall of the upper cylindrical section, a liquid outlet (7) located at the bottom of the lower cylindrical section of the container and concentric with the lower cylindrical section of the container, comprises: (i) a vortex breaker joined to the bottom of lower cylindrical section of the container, the vortex breaker comprising a top circular plate parallel with the bottom of the lower cylindrical section and concentric with the liquid outlet, where the diameter of the top plate is greater than the diameter of the liquid outlet to prevent fluid from falling directly from at least one opening directly into the liquid outlet; (ii) a blocking plate for preventing bubbles of gas entrained by fluid falling from the openings from reaching the liquid outlet, the blocking plate comprising a first cylindrical segment concentric with the liquid outlet and with a diameter greater than the diameter of the vortex breaker, and the height of the first cylindrical segment is greater than the height of the vortex breaker, the first cylindrical segment is joined to the bottom of the lower cylindrical section and a second segment joined to the top of the first cylindrical segment and having the shape of a truncated cone upwards, where the diameter of the bottom of the second segment is equal to the diameter of the first cylindrical segment and the first cylindrical segment comprises at least one opening, by allowing fluid to flow from the wall of the lower cylindrical section to the liquid outlet; and (iii) a guiding plate for guiding fluid falling from the walls of the intermediate section towards the side wall of the lower cylindrical section, the guiding plate is concentric with the liquid outlet 7, where the diameter of the guiding plate is equal to or greater than the diameter of the blocking plate, but smaller than the diameter of the lower cylindrical section of the container, and the guiding plate is located at the junction of the lower cylindrical section and the intermediate section, extending partially in the lower cylindrical section and partially in the intermediate section of the container. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a stripper comprising a top chamber comprising a liquid inlet, a gas outlet, an intermediate chamber comprising many tubes and the bottom chamber;
   (2) operating a urea high pressure (HP) stripper comprising directing a urea solution comprising urea, carbamate, ammonia and water to the liquid inlet of the urea HP stripper, and recovering from the liquid outlet of bottom chamber of the urea HP stripper a urea solution comprising urea and water, and depleted of carbamate, ammonia and optionally carbon dioxide; and
   (3) producing a solid, particulate urea based composition comprising (a) reacting a mixture of ammonia and carbon dioxide in a urea reactor, by producing a urea solution comprising urea, carbamate, ammonia, water and optionally carbon dioxide, (b) directing the urea solution in the urea HP stripper, (c) collecting the urea solution from the liquid outlet of the urea HP stripper, (d) concentrating the urea solution by obtaining a concentrated urea aqueous solution and (e) transforming the concentrated urea aqueous solution into a solid, particulate, urea-based composition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a cross-section of the bottom chamber of a urea high pressure stripper.

   Upper cylindrical section (4)

PT P
PN CN111594111-A
TI Processing double-injecting and diluting thick oil comprises e.g. placing the flocculent aluminum oxide on sodium meta aluminate in the solution dispenser, adding sodium hydroxide solution to the sodium meta aluminate solution dispenser.
AU CHENG Y
   WANG H
   YANG H
   SHA Z
   HAN Y
   XIANG F
   ZANG W
   FAN B
AE CHENG Y (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2020863943
AB    NOVELTY - Processing double-injecting and diluting thick oil comprises e.g. (i) placing the flocculent aluminum oxide on sodium meta aluminate in the solution dispenser, then adding sodium hydroxide solution to the sodium meta aluminate solution dispenser, stirring through the stirring shaft until the flocs disappear completely and a sodium meta aluminate solution is generated, (ii) connecting the high temperature packer, lower tail pipe, gas-liquid mixer, double-headed liquid injector and the oil pump barrel to the injection-production pipe string, (iii) starting the double injection of sodium meta aluminate solution and carbon dioxide gas by lifting the sucker rod so that the sucker pump plunger is slightly away from the sucker pump barrel i.e. to create a double injection string form on the one hand to make the injection and production string and (iv) stopping the injection and closing the valve for simmering.
   USE - The method is useful for processing double-injecting and diluting thick oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has strong physical fluidity and prolonged operation period by more than 2-3 times; and greatly improves the working effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing double-injecting and diluting thick oil comprises (i) placing the flocculent aluminum oxide on sodium meta aluminate in the solution dispenser, then adding sodium hydroxide solution to the sodium meta aluminate solution dispenser, stirring through the stirring shaft until the flocs disappear completely and a sodium meta aluminate solution is generated, where a condensation container is required on the outside of the sodium meta aluminate solution dispenser. The cold water enters through the condensate inlet and flows out through the condensate outlet to cool down the reactor, (ii) connecting the high temperature packer, lower tail pipe, gas-liquid mixer, double-headed liquid injector and the oil pump barrel to the injection-production pipe string. The lower end is then lowered into the oil well casing, first is seated, and the high temperature packer is seated by pressing and break the seat sealing shear nail so that the ball seat and the stern tube sealing steel ball fall on the limit seat after the seating is completed and the injection and production channels are opened the plunger and the sucker rod of the sucker pump are lowered, (iii) starting the double injection of sodium meta aluminate solution and carbon dioxide gas by lifting the sucker rod so that the sucker pump plunger is slightly away from the sucker pump barrel i.e. to create a double injection string form on the one hand to make the injection and production string, whree the gas-liquid mixer connecting the gas-liquid mixer on the other hand and make the cavity between the oil well casing and the injection-production string connected through the double-headed liquid injector after the construction of the double injection string is completed, a small amount of high-temperature carbon dioxide gas is injected into the injection-production string through the small four-way at the wellhead and open the big four-way valve at the wellhead and inject the sodium meta aluminate solution made on site into the oil well casing. The high temperature carbon dioxide gas enters the gas-liquid mixer along the injection-production pipe string . The sodium meta aluminate solution enters the double-headed liquid injector along the cavity between the oil well casing and the injection-production string. The double-headed liquid injector not only changes the flow direction of the liquid but also changes the longitudinal flow to the lateral flow and enters the gas-liquid mixer in multiple strands and the high temperature. The carbon dioxide gas is fully contacted to improve the reaction efficiency. The aluminum oxide produced by the reaction of the gas-liquid mixer enters the oil reservoir through the tail pipe of the pump, when the design volume is reached, stop the injection of sodium meta aluminate solution and continue to inject high-temperature carbon dioxide gas, and stop the gas injection when the design requirements are reached, (iv) stopping the injection, closing the valve for simmering for 1-2 days and the generated aluminum oxide is used as a catalyst to cracking the heavy oil at high temperature, where the part of the long chain of the heavy oil is cracked into short chains so that the heavy oil becomes diluent after the simmering is completed, when during oil production, the oil well pump plunger is lowered into the oil well pump barrel for trial pumping and oil production is started.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the gas-liquid mixer the high-temperature carbon dioxide gas first enters the gas-liquid homogenization chamber along the gas baffle. The sodium meta aluminate solution enters the gas-liquid mixer through the liquid inlet slotted pipe laterally and enters the gas-liquid homogenization chamber along the gas deflector at this time the sodium meta aluminate solution reacts with the high-temperature carbon dioxide gas and continue to flow down along the multi-stage capillary while reacting to carry out a sufficient combination reaction to generate aluminum oxide. The generated aluminum oxide is transported to the space under the tail pipe under the pump and then enters the oil reservoir to react with heavy oil. The gas-liquid mixer includes a liquid inlet slotted pipe, a carrier, a gas-liquid homogenization chamber, a gas deflector, gas-liquid homogenization plate, multi-stage capillary, lower chamber of the mixer, lower connector. The one end of the liquid inlet slitting pipe is connected to the bottom of the oil pump barrel and the side wall of the liquid inlet slitting pipe is connected to the outlet of the double-headed liquid injector. The liquid channel is connected. The other end of the liquid inlet slotted pipe is provided with a circular gas deflector. A gas-liquid homogenization chamber is formed between the gas baffle and the gas-liquid homogenization plate. The below the gas-liquid homogenization plate there is a multi-stage capillary connected to the lower cavity of the mixer. The bottom of the lower cavity of the mixer is a lower joint and is used to connect the lower tail pipe of the pump. The main body, seat sealing shears, ball seat, sealing steel ball, sealing rubber ring, limit seat. The inner cavity of the upper end of the tail pipe body is installed with the ball seat through the seat sealing shear nails and the tail pipe sealing steel ball is placed on the ball seat. The lower end of the tail pipe body is provided with a limit seat and the tail pipe body is provided with bare holes. The sodium metaaluminate solution configurator includes a reactor, a kettle cover and a stirrer. The upper part of the reactor is provided with a kettle cover. A stirrer is provided in the inner cavity of the reactor and the reactor includes a condensate outlet, a condensate inlet, a condensing vessel, reaction container, sodium metaaluminate solution outlet and sodium hydroxide solution inlet and outlet. The reaction container is sheathed with a condensation container. The condensate container is provided with a condensate inlet on the lower side and a condensate outlet on the upper side. The lower side of the reaction vessel is provided with an inlet and outlet of sodium hydroxide solution. A sodium metaaluminate solution outlet is provided in the middle of the reaction vessel.

TI Determining index for preventing spontaneous combustion and inerting of coal in goaf by injecting flue gas comprises e.g. preparing experimental gas, examining effect of dinitrogen on oxygen adsorption of coal, and analyzing gas composition.
AU DENG C
   GAO T
   WU Y
   JIN Z
   WANG X
   HAO C
   CHEN X
   WANG Y
   HAN Q
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2020099035
AB    NOVELTY - Determining index for preventing spontaneous combustion and inerting of coal in goaf by injecting flue gas comprises (i) preparing an experimental gas containing oxygen and dinitrogen and using for adsorption experiments to examine the effect of dinitrogen on the oxygen adsorption of coal, and (ii) collecting flue gas samples from the power plant for gas composition analysis, (iii) investigating the influence of power plant flue gas on coal's oxygen adsorption, (iv) measuring the balance number of each gas component in the cylinder, (v) taking the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorption phase x1, (vi) taking the mixed gas of the flue gas and oxygen, (vii) calculating the competition adsorption constant of the mixed gas system of nitrogen and oxygen, (viii) calculating the competitive adsorption constant of the mixed gas system of smoke and oxygen, (ix) taking the inertization index of the goaf, and (x) calculating the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorbed phase.
   USE - The method is useful for determining index for preventing spontaneous combustion and inerting of coal in goaf by injecting flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining index for preventing spontaneous combustion and inerting of coal in goaf by injecting flue gas comprises (i) preparing an experimental gas containing 20% oxygen and 80% dinitrogen and using for adsorption experiments to examine the effect of dinitrogen on the oxygen adsorption of coal, and (ii) collecting flue gas samples from the power plant for gas composition analysis and calculating the ratio of carbon dioxide and dinitogen in the flue gas, (iii) preparing an experimental gas containing 20% oxygen, 80% carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the same ratio as the flue gas, and using for adsorption experiments to investigate the influence of power plant flue gas on coal's oxygen adsorption, (iv) taking coal samples on site, sieving coal samples below 50 mesh after crushing, storing the sieved coal samples in a vacuum drying box, taking a coal sample, placing in an adsorption tank to perform isothermal adsorption experiments on the experimental gases in steps (i) and (iii), using a gas chromatograph to measure the initial volume fraction of each gas component in the cylinder, recording as the initial adsorption value, measuring the balance number of each gas component in the cylinder by a gas chromatograph after the adsorption equilibrium and recording as the adsorption equilibrium value, (v) taking the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorption phase x1 when the mixed gas of nitrogen and oxygen is in equilibrium and the volume fraction of oxygen in the free phase y1, where p is the initial equilibrium pressure value of the adsorption cylinder after injecting the mixed gas of nitrogen and oxygen, Mpa, p is the equilibrium pressure value of the adsorption cylinder when the mixed gas of coal adsorbing nitrogen and oxygen reaches the adsorption equilibrium, Mpa, the initial volume fraction of O2 in the adsorption cylinder after injection of a mixed gas of nitrogen and oxygen is dimensionless, the equilibrium fraction of N2 in the adsorption cylinder after injecting the mixed gas of nitrogen and oxygen, and the equilibrium volume fraction of dinitrogen in the adsorption tank when the mixed gas of nitrogen and oxygen adsorbed by coal reaches the adsorption equilibrium, dimensionless, (vi) taking the mixed gas of the flue gas and oxygen is adsorbed and balanced, the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorption phase x2 when the mixed gas of the gas and oxygen is adsorbed and balanced, the volume fraction of oxygen in the free phase y2, where the q is the initial equilibrium pressure value of the adsorption cylinder after injecting the mixed gas of flue gas and oxygen, Mpa, the q 'is the equilibrium pressure value of the adsorption cylinder during adsorption equilibrium, Mpa, it is the initial volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorption cylinder after injecting the mixed gas of flue gas and oxygen, which is dimensionless, the integral number of oxygen in the adsorption cylinder when the mixed gas of coal adsorbing flue gas and oxygen reaches the adsorption equilibrium, dimensionless, the initial volume fraction of dinitrogen in the cylinder after injecting the mixed gas of flue gas and oxygen is dimensionless, the equilibrium volume fraction of dinitrogen in the adsorption cylinder when the mixed gas of coal adsorbing flue gas and oxygen reaches the adsorption equilibrium, dimensionless, the initial volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the cylinder after the mixed gas of flue gas and oxygen is injected, dimensionless, and the equilibrium volume fraction of carbon of the mixed gas system of nitrogen and oxygen by taking k1 is the competitive adsorption constant of the mixed gas system of nitrogen and oxygen, dimensionless, (viii) calculating the competitive adsorption constant of the mixed gas system of smoke and oxygen by taking k2 is the competitive adsorption constant of the mixed gas system of smoke and oxygen, which is dimensionless, (ix) taking the inertization index of the goaf after nitrogen injection is that the oxygen content should not more than 7% according to regulations, i.e. in a mixed gas system of nitrogen and oxygen, where the volume fraction of oxygen in the free phase y1 /~ 7%, and the value of the competitive adsorption constant k1 of the mixed gas system of nitrogen and oxygen in step (vii) is used, and (x) calculating the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorbed phase in a mixed gas system of nitrogen and oxygen when the method of smoke injection is used to extinguish a heat, ensuring that the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorbed phase is equal to the volume fraction of oxygen in the adsorbed phase when extinguishing by nitrogen injection, and ensuring that x1 = x2, it is concluded that the use of flue gas to prevent coal spontaneous combustion and inertia in goafs.

PN DE102021100539-A1; WO2022152759-A2; WO2022152759-A3
TI Use of methanol as fuel in internal combustion            engine, preferably power unit, of watercraft powered by            hybrid electricity, and methanol is produced by e.g.            splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen in            electrolysis unit for producing hydrogen.
AU OBRIST F
AE OBRIST TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (OBRI-Non-standard)
GA 2022900047
AB    NOVELTY - Use of methanol as a fuel in an internal                combustion engine, preferably a power unit (120),                of a watercraft powered by hybrid electricity, is                claimed. The methanol is produced by: splitting                water into hydrogen and oxygen in an electrolysis                unit (11) for producing hydrogen; extracting carbon                dioxide from the ambient air using a carbon dioxide                sorption unit (12); and supplying hydrogen and                carbon dioxide to a methanol synthesis unit (34)                for producing methanol, where a photovoltaic unit                (24) absorbs solar energy and converts it into                electrical energy. The electrolysis unit, the                carbon dioxide sorption unit and the methanol                synthesis unit are operated by the electrical                energy generated in the photovoltaic unit.
   USE - The methanol is useful as a fuel in the                internal combustion engine, preferably a power                unit, of a watercraft (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is carried out in an economical                manner; ensure mobility of fuel in a most                environmentally friendly manner; and enables                operation of watercraft in a climate-friendly                manner and achieves climate-neutral goods                transport. The watercraft is provided with                sustainable propulsion system. The methanol: is                easy to transport and store in regions with high                annual solar radiation; has sufficient energy                density in such a manner that it can be used                sensibly for shipping; and enables refueling much                faster than charging a battery used to operate                light ferries or pleasure boats. The plant: has a                design in such a manner that it can be positioned                in a location-optimized manner and operated                independently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) operating the internal combustion engine                of the watercraft, preferably on inland waters,                comprising carrying out the above method, where the                methanol produced is transported using a                distribution system to at least one tank of the                watercraft and is fed from the tank to the internal                combustion engine as required and is consumed to                generate mechanical energy;
   (2) watercraft, preferably hybrid-electrically                powered watercraft, comprising a drive system                having the power unit, at least one drive battery                and at least one electric motor that draws                electrical energy from the drive battery, where the                power unit includes a two-cylinder piston engine                with two cylinders -piston units in a tandem                arrangement and at least one generator for                generating electrical energy, where each                cylinder-piston unit has a crankshaft. The                crankshafts of both cylinder-piston units are                mechanically coupled to one another, and at least                one crankshaft, preferably both crankshafts                respectively, is/are mechanically connected to at                least one generator; and
   (3) operating the watercraft, comprising                operating the two-cylinder reciprocating engine by                carrying out the above-mentioned method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of a plant for producing a globally usable energy                source.
   Electrolysis unit (11)
   Sorption unit (12)
   Photovoltaic unit (24)
   Synthesis unit (34)
   Power unit (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The water is desalinated                  in a seawater desalination unit and then fed to                  the electrolysis unit, where the seawater                  desalination unit is operated completely by the                  electrical energy generated in the photovoltaic                  unit. The method includes splitting part of the                  methanol in the splitting unit into a synthesis                  gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, and                  supplying the synthesis gas alone or together                  with the methanol into the reciprocating engine,                  where the splitting unit is arranged in the                  watercraft, preferably between a tank and the                  reciprocating engine. The electric motor acts via                  a gearbox or directly on a drive shaft of a                  propeller. The tank is fluidly connected to the                  two-cylinder reciprocating engine and is at least                  partially, preferably completely, filled with                  regeneratively produced methanol. Preferred                  Components: The internal combustion engine is                  part of a power unit of a hybrid-electrically                  powered watercraft, where the power unit converts                  the mechanical energy of the internal combustion                  engine into electrical drive energy for the                  propulsion of the watercraft. The internal                  combustion engine is a reciprocating piston                  engine operated with a compression ratio of at                  least 14:1, preferably at least 20:1.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. TI Operation method of oxy-combustion chamber exhaust of coal-fired power plant, comprises e.g. simultaneosuly separating and producing oxygen and nitrogen in air by liquefaction separation method and used as refrigerant.
AU YANG G W
   YANG S
AE YANG G W (YANG-Individual)
GA 2022517597
AB    NOVELTY - Operation method of oxy-combustion chamber exhaust of coal-fired power plant, comprises simultaneosuly separating and producing oxygen and nitrogen in the air by a liquefaction separation method and used as a refrigerant, injecting only pure oxygen into the combustion chamber of boiler, and allowing exhaust from this combustion chamber to flow into this exhaust cooler, cooling with refrigerant R-134a and using oxygen to steam, capturing carbon-dioxide at a low temperature below zero, and circulating water as a (purified) working fluid, preheating with superheated steam in the circulating water preheater by heat pump technology, further preheating in the circulating steam preheater of the refrigerant nitrogen liquefaction cooling system, preheating in circulating steam heater under the economizer and injecting into the combustion chamber and recirculated to realize pure oxygen combustion, liquefying R-134a in the refrigerant condenser with refrigerant nitrogen and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for operation method of oxy-combustion chamber exhaust of coal-fired power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases heat exchange efficiency and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Operation method of oxy-combustion chamber exhaust of coal-fired power plant, comprises simultaneosuly separating and producing oxygen and nitrogen in the air by a liquefaction separation method and used as a refrigerant, injecting only pure oxygen into the combustion chamber of boiler, and allowing exhaust from this combustion chamber to flow into this exhaust cooler, cooling with refrigerant R-134a and using oxygen to steam, capturing carbon-dioxide at a low temperature below zero, and circulating water as a (purified) working fluid, preheating with superheated steam in the circulating water preheater by heat pump technology, further preheating in the circulating steam preheater of the refrigerant nitrogen liquefaction cooling system, preheating in circulating steam heater under the economizer and injecting into the combustion chamber and recirculated to realize pure oxygen combustion, liquefying R-134a in the refrigerant condenser with refrigerant nitrogen, cooling the compressed air in the compressed air cooler with nitrogen, further preheating oxygen preheated in the steam cooler in the oxygen preheater, and in an oxygen heater under the economizer, injecting into the combustion chamber, recovering exhaust heat, and collecting coal dust, fine dust, and sulfur oxides from the inflow exhaust along with condensate, dissolving carbon-dioxide captured at sub-zero temperatures in the cooling water and seawater with carbonated water in the mixer, supplying carbonated water to a system where hydrogen is produced as carbon-dioxide to produce hydrogen, and producing ammonia from the produced hydrogen and nitrogen used as a refrigerant, where hydrogen is configured to be stored in this ammonia, and the seal trough at the bottom of the combustion chamber is replaced with an expansion joint type sealing device, before the combustion chamber exhaust system dust collector, a power generator is mounted, and the combustion chamber exhaust is configured so that the exhaust produces power and is discharged while a significant pressure is maintained.

PN CN212589256-U; CN113994836-A
TI Heat-cut air supply planting groove based greenhouse planting structure, has heat insulating plate inserted into two sides of outer frame groove, where planting groove is inserted into outer frame groove of heat insulating plate.
AU CHEN Y
   WANG X
AE ZHONGNONG SMART EQUIP SHENZHEN CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202126611Y
AB    (CN212589256-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a heat-cut air supply planting groove based on greenhouse planting, wherein The composition comprises a heat insulating plate, an outer frame groove, a planting groove; the connecting relation and the position relation are that the heat insulating plate is inserted into the two sides of the outer frame groove; and then the planting groove is inserted into the outer frame groove of the heat insulating plate. when the environment temperature of the plant growing in the planting groove is too low, the insulating plate can keep the temperature and the ventilation opening can be connected with the hot air to rise the temperature; when the environment temperature is too high, the ventilation opening can be filled with cold air for cooling; when the plant photosynthetic raw material of the planting groove is not enough, the ventilation opening can introduce carbon dioxide to increase photosynthesis, promote plant growth, provide proper temperature for plant in the cultivation groove in real time, cool environment and enough photosynthetic raw material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a greenhouse planting based heat-breaking air supply planting groove.
   Heat insulating plate (1)
   Outer frame groove (2)
   Planting groove (3)
   Concave-convex surface (5)
   Carbon dioxide (6)
   T-shaped drainage channel (7)
EA    NOVELTY - The groove has a composition provided with a heat insulating plate (1), an outer frame groove (2), and a planting groove (3). The heat insulating plate is inserted into two sides of the outer frame groove. The planting groove is inserted between the outer frame grooves of the heat insulating plate. The heat insulating plate is a cuboid made of polystyrene material, where a cross-section of the outer frame groove is inverted convex shape, a narrow head is downward, and wider head is upward. A bottom of the planting groove is provided with a T-shaped drainage channel (7).
   USE - Greenhouse planting based heat-cut air supply cultivating groove.
   ADVANTAGE - The groove provides the best growth environment condition for the plant in a certain small range, does not need large-scale greenhouse heating, cooling and fertilizing carbon dioxide, saves cost and increases the benefit of the plant planting. The insulating plate has good heat preservation effect, at the same time, the ventilation opening also can be filled with hot air to rise temperature, it avoids plant freezing, and provides proper growth temperature for the growth of plant. The ventilation opening can introduce carbon dioxide to increase photosynthesis, promote plant growth, provide proper temperature for plant in the cultivation groove in real time, cool environment and enough photosynthetic raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The outer frame groove is made of PVC material.

AB    NOVELTY - Production of syngas involves gasifying                pre-treated recovery plastic polymers by reacting                methylene and water to obtain carbon monoxide and 2                molar equivalent of dihydrogen, hydrogenating the                pre-treated polymers to higher hydrocarbons and                methane by reacting n molar equivalent of methylene                with the hydrogen, where n is 1-3, optionally                combining with oligomers and olefin formation                reaction, steam reforming the methane by reacting                methane and water to produce carbon monoxide and 3                molar equivalent of dihydrogen, and optionally                reforming the methane by reacting methane with                carbon dioxide to obtain 2 molar equivalent carbon                monoxide and 2 molar equivalent dihydrogen. The                process is carried out in a plant (10) having a                gasification section (11) and a reforming section                (12) having a tube bundle (13) provided with a                catalyst. The gasification and reforming sections                are a portion of a sole reactive unit or the                gasification and reforming sections are two                physically distinct reactive units.
   USE - Production of syngas used for producing                formaldehyde and/or dimethyl ether, fuel and/or                lubricant, and high added value chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces syngas by minimizing                unwanted products and using a minimum portion of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of syngas involves gasifying                pre-treated recovery plastic polymers by reacting                methylene and water to obtain carbon monoxide and 2                molar equivalent of dihydrogen, hydrogenating the                pre-treated polymers to higher hydrocarbons and                methane by reacting n molar equivalent of methylene                with the hydrogen, where n is 1-3, optionally                combining with oligomers and olefin formation                reaction, steam reforming the methane by reacting                methane and water to produce carbon monoxide and 3                molar equivalent of dihydrogen, and optionally                reforming the methane by reacting methane with                carbon dioxide to obtain 2 molar equivalent carbon                monoxide and 2 molar equivalent dihydrogen. The                process is carried out in a plant having a                gasification section and a reforming section having                a tube bundle provided with a catalyst. The                gasification and reforming sections are a portion                of a sole reactive unit or the gasification and                reforming sections are two physically distinct                reactive units. The gasification section or                reactive unit provides an energetical support to                the reforming section or reforming reactive unit by                performing exothermic combustion reaction by                reacting methylene with 1.5 molar equivalent oxygen                to produce carbon monoxide and water, or the                reforming section or reforming reactive unit                provides an energetic support to the corresponding                gasification section or gasification reactive unit                by performing exothermic combustion reaction by                reacting methane and 2 molar equivalent oxygen to                produce carbon dioxide and 2 molar equivalent                water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                reactive unit in which the reforming section and                gasification section are contained in sole reactive                unit of the plant.

The                  plant is combined with plants using syngas as a                  reagent for production of high added-value                  chemical products. The gasification section is                  fluid-bed type, trailed-bed type or fixed-bed                  type. AB    NOVELTY - Infrared filter comprises a substrate, a main film structure and a cut-off film structure, where the main film structure and the cut-off film structure are respectively arranged on both sides of the substrate. The main film structure is: Sub/LH4LHLHLHL2HLHLHLH4LH2L/Air, where Sub is substrate, Air is air, H is Ge film with quarter-wavelength optical thickness, L is ZnS film with quarter-wavelength optical thickness, and the number in the film structure is the film thickness coefficient. The design wavelength is 4260nm. The cut-off film structure is: Sub/0.54H0.47L0.47H0.56L0.39H0.17L0.66H0.47L0.39H0.51L0.10H0.47L0.64H0.65L0.43H1.03L0.36H0.88L0.59H0.67L0.47H0.51L1.63H1.88L1.22H1.89L1.25H1.82L1.44H1.79L1.61H0.91L/Air, where Sub stands for substrate, Air stands for air, and H stands for a quarter-wavelength optical thickness of Ge film.
   USE - Infrared filter for carbon dioxide gas detection.
   ADVANTAGE - The infrared filter has high performance and practical requirements of the sensor, and overcomes the shortcomings of bandwidth of the filter is too narrow, which leads to the low signal-to-noise ratio of the infrared sensor and the poor consistency of infrared carbon dioxide sensors using different batches of filters.

The L is a quarter-wavelength optical thickness of SiO film, the number in the film structure is the film thickness coefficient, and the design wavelength is 4260nm. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the infrared filter for carbon dioxide gas detection, which involves loading the substrate into the fixture and placing in the vacuum chamber of the coating machine, and evacuating, baking the substrate, ion bombarding the substrate, plating main film structure layer by layer according to the film layer required by the main film structure, plating cut-off film structure is plated layer by layer according to the film layer required by the cut-off film structure after plating is completed, breaking the gap, and picking up.

TI Urea production method involves exchanging heat            with another fluid to cool steam condensate, and            supplying steam condensate for generating low-pressure            steam.
AU SASAKI K
   KEIGO S
AE TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
   TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
GA 202091074B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves synthesizing urea from                ammonia and carbon dioxide to produce a urea                synthesis solution. Ammonium carbamate is                decomposed by heating the urea synthesis solution.                A mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide                from the urea synthesis solution, is separated. The                mixed gas is absorbed by an absorption medium. The                low pressure steam is generated from steam                condensate using the heat. The heat is exchanged                with another fluid to cool the steam condensate.                The steam condensate is heated by heat exchanging                the steam condensate with yet another fluid having                a temperature lower than the temperature of the low                pressure steam. The steam condensate is supplied                for generating the low-pressure steam.
   USE - Urea production method.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat is recovered from a relatively low                temperature fluid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The urea synthesis solution is heated at a                pressure lower than the pressure in the                high-pressure decomposition step and higher than                the atmospheric pressure. A gas phase and a liquid                phase are produced and the gas phase is separated                to obtain a urea synthetic solution having an                increased urea concentration, and to generate                low-pressure steam condensate. Low-pressure steam                is used as a heating source for heating the urea                synthesis solution. The urea synthesis solution is                heated at a pressure lower than the pressure in the                purification step and below atmospheric pressure to                form a gas phase and a liquid phase, and the gas                phase is separated. The low-pressure steam is                generated by reducing the pressure to above                atmospheric pressure. The fluid containing the low                pressure steam and the steam condensate is                separated. The urea synthetic solution having a                further increased urea concentration and producing                low-pressure steam condensate is obtained. The                granular solid urea is produced using air from the                urea synthesis solution. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a urea production apparatus has                synthesizer that synthesizes urea from ammonia and                carbon dioxide to produce a urea synthesis                solution. A high-pressure cracker separates the                mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide                from the urea synthesis solution. A condenser (A)                generates low pressure steam from steam condensate                using the heat. A first heat exchange structure is                configured to cool the steam condensate from the                first heat exchange structure. A second heat                exchange structure is configured to heat the steam                condensate from the first heat exchange structure.                A line is supplied to the condenser as a steam                condensate for generating the low pressure steam.                The amount of steam condensate supplied to the                condenser is greater than the amount of low                pressure steam generated by the condenser. A                gas-liquid separator separates low pressure steam                and steam condensate. The first heat exchange                structure is configured to use the steam                condensate. A heat exchange structure is configured                to heat by exchanging heat with the low pressure                steam generated by the condenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating the method of the steam condensate                system.
   Condenser (A)
   Refiner (B)
   Concentration apparatus (C)
   Low low pressure fluid generator (D)
   Heat exchanger (E)

PN CN111217597-A; CN111217597-B
TI Alkaline plate useful for special steel continuous            casting comprises e.g. magnesium aluminum spinel,            aluminum-rich spinel A90, scale graphite, carbon black,            metal magnesium powder, boron carbide, composite resin            and carbon dioxide gas.
AU CHEN S
   JIANG Z
   WANG J
   HU X
   LIU S
   YAN X
AE YIXING REFRACTORY MATERIAL CO LTD (CHNB-C)
   RUITAI MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHNB-C)
GA 202051458V
AB    NOVELTY - Alkaline plate comprises 25-50 wt.% magnesium                aluminum spinel having particle size of 3-1 mm,                22-33 wt.% magnesium aluminum spinel having                particle size of 1-0 mm, 13-18 wt.% magnesium                aluminum spinel having particle size less than or                equal to 0.045 mm, 7-11 wt.% aluminum-rich spinel                A90 having particle size of 0.002-0.005 mm, 1-2                wt.% scale graphite, 1-2 wt.% carbon black, 3-6                wt.% metal magnesium powder having particle size                less than or equal to 0.045 mm, 1-3 wt.% metal                magnesium aluminum alloy powder having particle                size less than or equal to 0.045 mm, 1 wt.% boron                carbide having particle size less than or equal to                0.045 mm, +4-6 wt.% additional composite resin and                carbon dioxide gas. The raw materials compound                resin is added separately, not included in the                total mass percentage of raw materials. The                composite resin is made by mixing silicone resin                and phenol resin in a ratio of 3-1. The raw                material carbon dioxide gas is used in the                atmosphere furnace during firing and is not                included in the total mass percentage of the raw                material.
   USE - The alkaline plate is useful for special steel                continuous casting (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The alkaline plate: has improved the corrosion                resistance and oxidation resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                alkaline plate comprising (i) taking magnesia                aluminum spinel having particle size less than or                equal to 0.045 mm, metal magnesium powder having                particle size less than or equal to 0.045 mm, metal                magnesium aluminum alloy powder having particle                size less than or equal to 0.045 mm, boron carbide                powder having particle size less than or equal to                0.045 mm, aluminum-rich spinel A90 having particle                size of 0.002-0.005 mm, and performing strong                premixing for 15-20 minutes, (ii) pre-mixing                silicone resin and phenolic resin at a ratio of 3-1                wt.% into prepare composite resin, (iii) taking                magnesia-alumina spinel having particle size of 3-1                mm, magnesia-alumina spinel having particle size of                1-0 mm, flake graphite and carbon black, dry mixing                for 3-5 minutes, adding the composite resin, mixing                for 2-3 minutes, adding the pre-mixed fine powder,                forcefully mixing and grinding for 15-30 minutes,                (iv) vigorously mixing and milling, sieving with a                5 mm sieve to remove large agglomerates, (v)                sealing the trapped material for 24-36 hours, (vi)                press molding, (vii) natural drying for 24-48                hours, and drying at 200-280 degrees C for 24-48                hours, (viii) loading into a atmosphere furnace for                sintering, passing carbon dioxide gas having                pressure of 0.2-0.3 MPa, and heat preventing at                1450-1550 degrees C for 5-8 hours, and (ix) natural                cooling after sintering, grinding and hooping to                obtain final product.

PT P
PN CN109354270-A; CN109354270-B
TI Non-discharge recycling of wastewater of mancozeb production comprises e.g. filtering the wastewater from the mancozeb production plant to obtain a first filter cake and a first filtrate and returning the first cake to the workshop for use.
AU SHI Y
   HU Z
   YANG N
   ZHANG L
   CHEN S
AE UNIV CHANGAN (UCHA-C)
   SHANNXI KEYUAN ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHANGAN (UCHA-C)
GA 201919203H
AB    NOVELTY - Non-discharge recycling of wastewater of mancozeb production comprises (i) filtering the wastewater from the mancozeb production plant to obtain a first filter cake and a first filtrate, returning the first cake to the workshop for use, adding a sodium carbonate solution into the first filtrate and obtaining a liquid containing the precipitate, (ii) obtaining a regenerated sodium hydroxide solution and a regenerated sulfuric acid solution, (iii) taking the regenerated sulfuric acid solution, adding the second filter cake for dissolution, releasing the carbon dioxide gas, adding the manganese oxide to carry out a reaction to obtain a manganese sulfate solution, and concentrating the manganese sulfate solution, (iv) returning the obtained zinc sulfate solution to the workshop, (v) obtaining the sodium carbonate solution by reacting the sodium hydroxide solution and (vi) concentrating the remaining sodium hydroxide solution remaining in step (v) and returning to the workshop.
   USE - The method is useful for non-discharge recycling of wastewater of mancozeb production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Non-discharge recycling of wastewater of mancozeb production comprises (i) filtering the wastewater from the mancozeb production plant to obtain a first filter cake and a first filtrate, returning the first cake to the workshop for use, adding a sodium carbonate solution into the first filtrate, where the carbonate and the heavy metal ions in the first filtrate form a precipitate until a precipitate is formed and obtaining a liquid containing the precipitate, (ii) filtering the liquid containing the precipitate to obtain a second filter cake and a second filtrate and electrolyzing the second filtrate to obtain a regenerated sodium hydroxide solution and a regenerated sulfuric acid solution, (iii) taking the regenerated sulfuric acid solution containing the corresponding mole of sulfuric acid according to the amount of manganese sulfate used in the mancozeb production process, adding the second filter cake for dissolution, releasing the carbon dioxide gas, adding the manganese oxide to carry out a reaction to obtain a manganese sulfate solution, and concentrating the manganese sulfate solution according to the mass concentration required on the production process of mancozeb and returned to the workshop, (iv) adding zinc oxide to the remaining regenerated sulfuric acid solution in step (iii) to obtain a zinc sulfate solution and returning the obtained zinc sulfate solution to the workshop, (v) using a part of the regenerated sodium hydroxide solution obtained by electrolysis in step (ii) to completely absorb the carbon dioxide gas generated in the second filter cake dissolving process of step (iii), and obtaining the sodium carbonate solution by reacting the sodium hydroxide solution with the carbon dioxide gas sent to the first step and (vi) concentrating the remaining sodium hydroxide solution remaining in step (v) according to the mass concentration required for the mancozeb production process and returning to the workshop.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the sodium carbonate solution is the sodium carbonate solution obtained in the step (v) after steps from (i to vi) form a cyclic system. In the step (iii), the manganese carbonate is substituted for manganese oxide. In the step (iv), the zinc oxide is substituted for zinc oxide.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

The carbon dioxide spray rod is connected with                a carbon dioxide spray head (12). The pesticide                spray rod is connected with a pesticide spray head                (13). A drive electric wheel (11) is set under                fixing frame and is connected to fixing frame                through a support wheel telescopic rod (20). One                end of a scissor mechanism (19) is connected to a                machine frame (2) of a rail car chassis (26) and                other end of scissor mechanism is connected to                fixing frame. The carbon dioxide spray rod is                connected to a carbon dioxide cylinder (6) through                a vent pipe (18). The pesticide spray rod is                connected to a medicine storage tank (8) through a                medicine passage pipe (10). A carbon dioxide supply                pump is arranged between cylinder and vent pipe. A                medicine pump is arranged between the storage tank                and passage pipe.
   USE - Multifunctional rail car for modern sunlight                greenhouse used in solar greenhouse industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The rail car can realize uniform spraying of                greenhouse crops in each ridge, supplement of                carbon dioxide and image transmission without                manual participation and effectively prevent the                occurrence of poisoning by operator.

PN JP2022136032-A
TI New fluoro-containing aromatic ester compound used            as gelling agent in gel composition used as electrolyte            for all-solid state lithium-ion battery and fuel cell,            is prepared by reacting sulfonyl compound with            fluoroalkyl alcohol in solvent such as anhydrous            pyridine or toluene.
AU OKAMOTO H
   MORITA Y
AE UNIV YAMAGUCHI (UYAM-C)
GA 2022C1074T
AB    NOVELTY - A fluoro-containing aromatic ester compound                (I), is new.
   USE - New fluoro-containing aromatic ester compound                used as gelling agent in gel composition (all                claimed) used as electrolyte for all-solid-state                lithium-ion battery and fuel cell. Uses include but                are not limited to cosmetics, medicine, foodstuff,                paints, adhesives, and sludge treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) has low molecular weight,                electrochemical stability, ionic conductivity,                mechanical strength, and provides gel having                selectively absorbs carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fluoro-containing aromatic ester compound of                formula (I), is new.
   Ar'=optionally substituted divalent                aryl;
   y=0-2;
   m,n=1-20;
   p,q=0-6;and
   r=1-3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gel                composition, which comprises a gelling agent and an                organic solvent.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (disclosed): The compound (I) is                  prepared by chlorinating a sulfonyl compound of                  formula (II) by using a chlorinating agent such                  as thionyl chloride and then reacting the                  compound (II) with a fluoroalkyl alcohol of                  formula: CmF2m+1(CH2)pOH (III) in a solvent such                  as anhydrous pyridine or toluene. Preferred                  Components: The organic solvent is an ionic                  liquid.                                                                                                                              
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ar'=phenylene, naphthylene or biphenylene (all                optionally substituted).
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 7 Compounds (I) are disclosed e.g.                  4-{[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)oxy]carbonyl}phenyl                  4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)sulfanyl]benzoate                  of formula (IA),                  4-{[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)oxy]carbonyl}phenyl                  4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctane)sulfonyl]benzoate,                  3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl                  4-{4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)sulfanyl]phenyl}benzoate,                  3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl                  4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctane)sulfonyl]benzoate,                  and 3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl                  4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)sulfanyl]benzoate.
                                                                                                                                 
   EXAMPLE - 0.52 g                3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl                4-hydroxybenzoate, 0.50 g                4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)sulfanyl]benzoic                acid, 0.30 g                1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide                hydrochloride, 0.5 g dimethylaminopyridine and 25                mL dimethylformamide were added to a 50 mL eggplant                flask. A calcium chloride tube was attached and                stirred overnight at room temperature. The                resulting reaction mixture was extracted twice with                toluene, and the organic layer was washed with                saturated brine. The resulting organic layer was                concentrated under reduced pressure using an                evaporator and purified by silica gel column                chromatography using chloroform as a developing                solvent to obtain 0.65 g                4-{[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)oxy]carbonyl}phenyl                4-[(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,8-tridecafluorooctyl)sulfanyl]benzoate                with yield of 67%. PN CN113826763-A
TI Preparing feed protein by combining coal with natural gas comprises adding natural gas and coal to coal gasification furnace, washing gas, subjecting gas for acidic gas removal to remove sulfur-containing gas, subjecting gas sequentially to anaerobic fermentation, aerobic fermentation.
AU ZHAO Y
   LI W
   HE Z
AE SHANGHAI JIANCAN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022103230
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing feed protein by combining coal with natural gas comprises (i) adjusting ratio of natural gas and coal then adding to coal gasification furnace, where mass ratio of hydrogen molecule to carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is 2-2.3, (ii) washing gas obtained in step (i) by using washing and dust removing device and maintaining gas at temperature 35-40degrees Celsius, (iii) subjecting the gas obtained in step (ii) for acidic gas removal to remove sulfur-containing gas, gas pressure after desulfurization is 1.3 MPa and temperature is 35degrees Celsius and (iv) subjecting the gas obtained in step (iii) sequentially to anaerobic fermentation, aerobic fermentation, concentrating and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing feed protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: Can reduce carbon emissions while co-producing high value-added products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the pressure of the coal gasifier is 2.0-6.0 MPa, preferably 2.4MPa. Heat recovery device is installed between step (i) and step (ii), and the temperature of the high-temperature gas obtained in step (i) is taken away by boiler water to form saturated steam for the coal gasifier. The gas pressure in step (ii) is 1.6-4.6 MPa, preferably 1.6 MPa. In step (iv), the anaerobic fermentation pressure ranges from 0.8-1.2 MPa, preferably 1.2 MPa. The exhaust gas produced by anaerobic fermentation in step (iv) is subjected to exhaust gas treatment and the steam formed is used for concentrating and drying in step (iv). The acetic acid solution with a concentration of 2.5-4% is obtained by anaerobic fermentation and the protein powder solution with concentration of 2.5-4% is obtained by aerobic fermentation. In step (iv) the remaining purified circulating clear liquid after concentrating and drying process is returned to the anaerobic fermentation.Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass ratio of natural gas to coal is 3-4:6-7, preferably 7:3.

   LI Z
   LI Y
   XUE T
   PENG L
AE SHIDIDI XIAMEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHID-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2021E4303U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silver nano-particle composite                antibacterial material based on metal organic                framework comprises (i) activating MIL-127 (metal                organic framework) under vacuum condition, cooling                at room temperature, dispersing into absolute ethyl                alcohol solution of o-phenylenediamine, placing the                mixture into an autoclave, sealing and heating,                introducing carbon dioxide into high pressure                kettle by supercritical setting, stirring and                reacting, releasing pressure, washing and drying                the obtained powder; and (ii) mixing and reacting                MIL-127/PoPD (polymer) with silver solution,                centrifuging, washing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silver                nano-particle composite antibacterial material                based on metal organic framework.

In the step (ii),                  the loading amount of silver is 0.1-20 wt.% and                  the concentration of silver solution is 10-1000                  ppm. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of                  MIL-127/PoPD-Ag and alkali is 0.05:1-1:0.2. In                  the step (iii), the alkali is an inorganic alkali                  or an organic alkali, and the concentration of                  the alkali solution is 0.01-1 M and the acid is                  an inorganic acid or an organic acid, and the pH                  of the solution after acid neutralization is 5-8.                  Preferred Method: The method further comprises                  (iii) adding MIL127/PoPD-Ag into an alkaline                  solution, stirring, reacting, and neutralizing                  with acid. In the step (iii), the activation                  under vacuum condition is carried out at                  80-200degrees Celsius, and sealing and heating                  temperature is carried out at 50-120degrees                  Celsius. In the step (i), the certain pressure is                  6-18 MPa, and the stirring reaction time is 1-30                  hours.

PT P
PN CN113679674-A
TI Preparing Huangpu Tongqiao granules comprise e.g.            preparing powder of Huangpu Tongqiao comprising            rhubarb, rhizoma Acori graminei, fructus Alpinia            oxyphylla, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, radix Polygoni            multiflori preparata and Ginseng, and extracting active            ingredients of Huangpu Tongqiao powder.
AU WANG G
   SONG H
   YE S
   ZHAO G
   CHEN W
   XIE D
   CAI B
AE UNIV ANHUI CHINESE MEDICINE (UYAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1165V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Huangpu Tongqiao granules comprise                preparing powder of Huangpu Tongqiao, where the                powder of Huangpu Tongqiao comprises 60 pts. wt.                rhubarb, 200 pts. wt. rhizoma Acori graminei, 200                pts. wt. fructus Alpinia oxyphylla, 200 pts. wt.                rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 60 pts. wt. radix                Polygoni multiflori preparata, and 100 pts. wt.                Ginseng, using carbon dioxide supercritical                extraction method to extract active ingredients of                Huangpu Tongqiao powder, and collecting the                extract, concentrating, drying and crushing the                extract in a vacuum to obtain a dry paste powder,                mixing dry paste powder with medicinal auxiliary                materials to obtain finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Huangpu                Tongqiao granules.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: greatly improves rate of main                active ingredients of Huangpu Tongqiao; The                granules: have excellent solubility, high forming                rate and small hygroscopicity; and can also be                packaged into 20 g/bag Huangpu Tongqiao granules,                which is easy for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the Huangpu Tongqiao powder is: taking 60 pts.                  wt. rhubarb, 200 pts. wt. rhizoma Acori graminei,                  200 pts. wt. fructus Alpinia oxyphylla, 200 pts.                  wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii and 60 pts. wt.                  radix Polygoni multiflori preparata to make                  coarse powder, and then adding 100 pts. wt.                  Ginseng to prepare into fine powder. The                  extraction conditions for extracting the active                  ingredients of Huangpu Tongqiao powder by carbon                  dioxide supercritical extraction method are:                  extracting at 40-60degrees Celsius at the                  extraction pressure is 10-14 Mpa for 2-3 hours,                  where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-30                  kg/hour, the entrainer is ethanol, and the ratio                  is 4-6%. The recycling process of ethanol is:                  pumping the extract into a vacuum concentration                  tank, and recycling ethanol at 40-50degrees                  Celsius and a vacuum of 0.07-0.1 Mpa, where the                  moisture content of recycled extract is 10-35% to                  obtain wet extract, which then enters the vacuum                  freeze-drying process. The vacuum freeze-drying                  procedure is: pre-freezing the wet extract at                  -80degrees Celsius to -40degrees Celsius for 2-6                  hours, then when the vacuum degree is below 100                  Pa, heating up the temperature to sublimate the                  water, freeze-drying time is 12-30 hours, vacuum                  freeze-drying when the moisture content of the                  wet extract drops below 10%, and obtaining a                  crude extract. The crude extract is crushed to                  obtain dry paste powder with particle diameter                  less than 50 m. The preparation steps of the                  Huangpu Tongqiao granules are: mixing dry paste                  powder with medicinal auxiliary materials, using                  60-80% ethanol as a wetting agent to prepare                  Huangpu Tongqiao soft material, passing Huangpu                  Tongqiao soft material through a coarse sieve to                  prepare Huangpu Tongqiao wet granules, drying                  Huangpu Tongqiao wet granules under reduced                  pressure and finely sieving to prepare Huangpu                  Tongqiao granules. The Huangpu Tongqiao soft                  material is passed through 14-meshes sieve to                  obtain Huangpu Tongqiao wet granules, drying                  Huangpu Tongqiao wet granules under reduced                  pressure below 60degrees Celsius, and then                  passing through 20-40 meshes fine sieve to                  prepare Huangpu Tongqiao granules.Preferred Components: The medicinal                  auxiliary materials comprise medicinal filling                  agent and binders. The mass ratio of the dry                  paste powder, filling agent and binder is                  1:4-5:0.4-0.6. The filling agent is starch syrup,                  lactose, dextrin, powdered sugar, calcium                  sulfate, sucrose, mannitol, microcrystalline                  cellulose, or glucose. The binder is starch                  slurry, dextrin, methyl cellulose, hydroxypropyl                  cellulose, hypromellose, sodium carboxymethyl                  cellulose, ethyl cellulose, povidone, gelatin, or                  polyethylene glycol.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B14-S18; B12-M11D
IP A61K-036/9062; A61K-047/26; A61K-047/36; A61K-009/16; A61P-025/28

PN CN214629212-U
TI Simple anesthesia device for orange planthopper, has freezing box that is detachably connected with anaesthesia cabin for cooling anesthesia cabin.
AU FAN Z
   LI Y
   ZHANG L
   QIU B
   ZHAO S
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 2021D1016K
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a simple anaesthetic device for orange planthopper, comprising a sealing cover, an anaesthesia chamber and a freezing box. the sealing cover is detachably connected on the anesthesia cabin; the sealing cover is provided with a ventilating structure with aperture less than orange planthopper; the anaesthesia chamber is upper end opening; the lower end is closed; the side wall is provided with a through wall body for connecting the joint of the carbon dioxide conduit; the freezing box is detachably connected with the anaesthesia cabin, for cooling the anaesthesia cabin. The anesthesia device of the invention has small volume, low cost, simple structure, convenient operation, which not only can realize the fast anesthesia of orange planthopper, but also can adjust the anaesthesia time, the odelphax is kept healthy, stable state; The device has small physiological influence to the orange planthopper, high safety, convenient for collecting the planthopper, anesthesia and observation, and performing other biological and genetic experiment operation.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PT P
PN CN113597953-A
TI Screening Lycium barbarum germplasm involves            selecting leaf of medlar germ plasm material to be            evaluated as sample to be tested, performing cluster            analysis, and selecting group with best evaluation            index data.
AU ZHAO R
   YANG L
   YAN Y
   MI J
   DUAN L
   YIN Y
   HE J
   ZHANG B
   HUANG T
   WANG Y
   CAO Y
   DAI G
   HE X
AE NINGXIA ACAD AGRIC & FORESTRY SCI GOJI (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9125D
AB    NOVELTY - Screening Lycium barbarum germplasm involves                selecting leaves of Lycium barbarum germplasm                material to be evaluated as samples to be tested,                and determining evaluation index data of samples to                be tested. The evaluation indexes comprises net                photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance,                intercellular carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration,                transpiration rate, leaf area, leaf fresh weight                and specific leaf weight. The cluster analysis is                carried out on evaluation index data to obtain                multiple clusters. The group with best evaluation                index data is selected from multiple groups, and                germplasm materials of Lycium barbarum in this                group are high-light-efficiency germplasm.
   USE - Method for screening Lycium barbarum                germplasm.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to have high                photosensitivity, provides medlar blade                light-combination property index and physical                property index of medlar high light effect filter                and powerful guarantee and theoretical basis for                medlar breeding, and evaluates photosynthetic                capacity of medlar variety.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The data of evaluation                  index is subjected to K-means clustering                  analysis, and clustering number is set to 3. The                  stepwise discriminant analysis is carried out on                  cluster analysis results to obtain discriminant                  functions and coordinates of center of gravity of                  various groups. The measured index data of Lycium                  barbarum germplasm material to be identified is                  substituted into discriminant function to obtain                  coordinates corresponding to Lycium barbarum                  germplasm material to be identified. The group to                  which germplasm material of Lycium barbarum to be                  identified belongs is determined according to                  principle of minimum distance. The leaf area is                  measured by violet scanner. The fresh weight of                  leaves is measured by electronic analytical                  balance. The specific leaf weight is determined                  according to dryness of leaves. The ratio of                  weight to leaf area is calculated. The dry leaf                  weight is determined by using electronic                  analytical balance after drying fresh leaves at                  70degreesC to constant weight.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC P13-A02; S03-E09F; S03-E14J
IP G01D-021/02; A01G-017/00
PD CN113597953-A   05 Nov 2021   A01G-017/00   202201      Chinese
AD CN113597953-A    CN10902300    06 Aug 2021
PI CN10902300    06 Aug 2021
