PT P
PN CN113583366-A
TI Flame-retardant thermoplastic elastomer comprises            fluororesin, flame-retardant fluororubber premix,            vulcanizing agent, crosslinking agent, lubricant,            antioxidant and ultraviolet light initiator.
AU LI R
   SUN G
   YAN B
AE ORINKO ADVANCED PLASTICS SHANGHAI CO LTD (ORIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C69604
AB    NOVELTY - Flame-retardant thermoplastic elastomer                comprises 20-60 pts. wt. fluororesin, 45-100 pts.                wt. flame-retardant fluororubber premix, 1-10 pts.                wt. vulcanizing agent, 1-10 pts. wt. crosslinking                agent, 3-10 pts. wt. lubricant, 1-3 pts. wt.                antioxidant and 0-2 pts. wt. ultraviolet light                initiator. The flame-retardant fluororubber premix                is obtained by mixing fluororubber and                fluorine-containing silane coupling agent modified                inorganic flame retardant, and undergoing                supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) treatment.
   USE - Flame-retardant thermoplastic elastomer.
   ADVANTAGE - The flame-retardant thermoplastic elastomer                has high temperature resistance, low compression                deformation, excellent resistance to chemical media                and radiation resistance and excellent properties                of flame retardancy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing high-temperature resistant and                low-compression-deformation flame-retardant                thermoplastic elastomer, which involves:
   a. mixing fluororesin, flame-retardant                fluororubber premix, vulcanizing agent,                crosslinking agent, lubricant, antioxidant and                ultraviolet light initiator, melting and extruding                to obtain, where temperature of melt extrusion is                200-320degreesC.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of                  flame-retardant fluorine rubber premix, quality                  of the fluorine rubber and fluorine-containing                  silane coupling agent modified inorganic flame                  retardant is (30-70):(15-30). The temperature of                  mixing is 70-80degreesC for 5-8 minutes. The mass                  of fluorine-containing silane coupling agent is                  2-5 wt.%. The inorganic flame retardant is                  composed of magnesium hydroxide, aluminum                  hydroxide and antimony trioxide in mass ratio of                  (10-20):(3-5):(2-5). The recrosslinking is                  ultraviolet light crosslinking, electron beam                  crosslinking, radiation crosslinking and cobalt                  source radiation crosslinking.Preferred Process: The flame-retardant                  fluororubber premix is prepared by modifying                  fluororubber and fluorine-containing silane                  coupling agent. The inorganic flame retardant is                  mixed in internal mixer, and placed in                  high-pressure container, and supercritical CO2 is                  introduced and treated at 10-15 megapascal and                  120-160degrees Celsius for 4-6 hours, and                  pressure is released quickly. The                  fluorine-containing silane coupling agent                  modified inorganic flame retardant is prepared by                  mixing coupling agent, ethanol and water to                  obtain fluorine-containing silane coupling agent,                  adding inorganic flame retardant, maintaining                  reaction at 70-90degreesC for 1-3 hours,                  filtering, and drying and crushing filter                  cake.Preferred Components: The fluororesin is                  perfluoroalkoxy alkanes (PFA), multi-functional                  acrylate (MFA) resin, fluorinated ethylene                  propylene (FEP) resin,                  polychlorotrifluoroethylene (PCTFE) resin,                  ethylene tetrafluoroethylene (ETFE) resin,                  ethylene chlorotrifluoroethylene (ECTFE) resin                  and polyvinylidene difluoride (PVDF) resins. The                  fluorine rubber is vinylidene                  fluoride-hexafluoropropylene binary rubber,                  vinylidene fluoride-trifluorochloride Ethylene                  binary rubber, vinylidene                  fluoride-tetrafluoroethylene binary rubber,                  vinylidene                  fluoride-tetrafluoroethylene-hexafluoropropylene                  ternary rubber, propylene-tetrafluoroethylene                  binary rubber, propylene-vinylidene                  fluoride-tetrafluoroethylene rubber,                  propylene-tetrafluoroethylene-chlorosulfonated                  ethylene, perfluoroether rubber and                  fluorosilicone rubber. The fluorine-containing                  silane coupling agent is                  trifluoropropyltriethoxysilane, trifluoropropyl                  trimethoxysilane, perfluorooctyltriethoxysilane,                  perfluorooctyltrimethoxysilane,                  perfluorodecyltriethoxysilane, and                  perfluorodecyltrimethoxysilane. The inorganic                  flame retardant is antimony trioxide, antimony                  pentoxide, magnesium hydroxide, aluminum                  hydroxide and zinc borate. The vulcanizing agent                  is hexamethylene diamine carbamate, ethylene                  diamine carbamate, N,N-secondary                  cinnamyl-1,6-hexamethylene diamine, alicyclic                  amine salt, hydroquinone, bisphenol AF, dicumyl                  peroxide, 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-dihexyl alkane and                  di-tert-butylperoxycumene. The crosslinking agent                  is triallyl isocyanurate, triallyl cyanurate,                  triallyl cyanurate, trimer thiocyanate,                  trimethylallyl isocyanate, trimethylol,                  trimethylolpropane triacrylate and                  trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate.
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC A04-E10; A08-C01; A08-C03; A08-M03; A08-A03; A08-A06; A08-M01D; A08-F01; A11-B07; A09-D02

PN CN214427795-U
TI Environment monitoring system used in open air or indoor environment, has display screen, switch and multiple sensors that are respectively electrically connected with single chip.
AU GAO Q
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Q
AE WEIFANG VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (WEIF-Non-standard)
GA 2021D27046
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of environment monitoring, especially to an environment monitoring system, comprising a box body; one side of the box body is provided with an air inlet; the other side of the box body is provided with an air outlet; the front surface of the box body is provided with a display screen and a switch; the inner part of the box body is provided with a single chip and a plurality of sensors, a display screen; the switch and multiple sensors respectively electrically connected with the single chip; a plurality of sensors comprises a temperature and humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, an atmospheric pressure sensor, a light intensity sensor, a rainfall intensity sensor, a solar radiation sensor and a PM2.5 sensor. The environment monitoring system is convenient to carry and use; it can monitor more data information and satisfy more requirements.

PT P
PN CN214422592-U

AU CHEN G

AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 202199775C
AB    NOVELTY - Gallium nitride series copper indium gallium selenide device comprises transparent and conductive oxide (TCO) layer, zinc oxide layer, cadmium sulfide layer, copper indium gallium selenide (CIGS) layer, molybdenum layer, indium layer and gallium nitride unit. The allium nitride unit comprises allium nitride layer, n+-allium nitride layer and sapphire layer. The gallium nitride layer is partially etched to expose n+-gallium nitride layer. The indium layer is in direct contact with n+-gallium nitride layer. The indium layer is not in contact with gallium nitride layer.
   USE - The gallium nitride series copper indium gallium selenide device is used for artificial photosynthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is energy-saving and environment-friendly, has simple process, large light current density, high cathode potential and strong light absorption coefficient and solves development and utilization of new energy and environment problem caused by carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The device is prepared by placing gallium nitride cell on clean glass sheet, etching gallium nitride layer to expose n+-gallium nitride layer underneath, placing CIGS solar cells on the surface of indium particles, placing cleaned glassware in vacuum drying oven, heating at 165-185 degrees C for 1-2 hours and cooling to room temperature.

PN WO2021162751-A1; AU2020428114-A1; CA3165199-A1; EP4078030-A1
TI Chemical looping system for converting fossil            fuels or biomass-based fuels to products such as            syngas, comprises reducer reactor including active            solid particles and inert solid particles, and            combustor reactor.
AU FAN L
   KONG F
   SHAH V
AE OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
GA 202195748T
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical looping system comprises reducer                reactor comprising active solid particles (A1) and                inert solid particles, and combustor reactor in                fluid communication with the reducer reactor. The                active solid particles (A1) comprise metal oxide,                and are capable of cycling between reduction                reaction and an oxidation reaction. The inert solid                particles comprise refractory material, and are not                reactants in either the reduction reaction or                oxidation reaction. The combustor reactor is                provided to receive active solid particles (A1) and                inert solid particles from the reducer                reactor.
   USE - Chemical looping system used for converting                fuels into products such as liquid fuels, hydrogen                and electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical looping system can achieve higher                energy efficiency and lower carbon dioxide                emissions via higher level of process                intensification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                operating the chemical looping system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                chemical looping system.
   Reactor system (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is                  iron oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, manganese                  oxide, cerium oxide, cobalt oxide, tungsten                  oxide, vanadium oxide, and/or calcium-iron                  pentoxide. The active solid particles (A1)                  further comprise support comprising lithium,                  beryllium, boron, sodium, magnesium, aluminum,                  silicon, potassium, calcium, scandium, titanium,                  vanadium, chromium, manganese, cobalt, zinc,                  gallium, germanium, rubidium, yttrium, zirconium,                  niobium, molybdenum, cadmium, indium, tin,                  antimony, cesium, barium, lanthanum, cerium,                  and/or thorium. The support is silicon dioxide,                  magnesium oxide, aluminum oxide, titanium oxide,                  and/or silicon carbide. The active solid                  particles (A1) further comprise one or more                  dopants chosen from nickel, cobalt, copper,                  scandium, titanium, vanadium, chromium,                  manganese, zinc, yttrium, zirconium, niobium,                  molybdenum, technetium, ruthenium, rhodium,                  palladium, silver, cadmium, lutetium, hafnium,                  tantalum, tungsten, rhenium, osmium, iridium,                  platinum and gold. The refractory material                  comprises silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide,                  calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, aluminosilicates,                  kaolin, mullite, alumina-zirconia-silica,                  monocalcium aluminate, or calcium-aluminum oxide                  of formula: CaAl4O7 (I). The reducer reactor                  further comprises active solid particles (A2).                  The carbonaceous feedstock and active solid                  particles are subjected to reduction reaction to                  obtain product gas stream (S1). The product gas                  stream (S1) is obtained by subjecting reduced                  active solid particles and oxidizing agent to                  oxidation reaction. The product gas stream (S1)                  comprises one or more of unconverted gaseous                  fuel, unconverted volatile components of solid                  feedstocks, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon                  dioxide and steam. The oxidizing agent comprises                  at least one of steam, carbon dioxide, and air.                  The product gas stream (S2) comprises one or more                  of hydrogen, steam, carbon monoxide, carbon                  dioxide, nitrogen, and oxygen. Preferred                  Composition: The amount of support material is                  1-99 wt.% of the active solid particles (A1).                  Preferred Properties: The ratio of inert solid                  particles to active solid particles (A1) is                  (0.25:1)-(1:1). The active solid particles (A1)                  and inert solid particles have a diameter of                  0.05-5 mm. The active solid particles (A1) have                  an active particle density of 1000-5000 kg/m3.                  The inert solid particles have an inert particle                  density of 1000-5000 kg/m3. The active particle                  density is 40% or more, or 40% or less than the                  inert particle density.

AB    NOVELTY - Double Z-type photocatalyst nickel/nickel iron oxide/iron oxide prepared by mixing nickel hydroxide suspension and iron oxide-hydroxide suspension, adjusting pH to 8-11, stirring thoroughly, filtering, drying, and grinding, performing calcination of the resulting powder in a muffle furnace at 400-600 degrees C for 1-3 hours, cooling, and grinding to obtain the double Z-type photocatalyst nickel oxide/nickel iron oxide/iron oxide, is claimed.

   ADVANTAGE - The organic dye is methylene blue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of double Z-type photocatalyst nickel/nickel iron oxide/iron oxide comprising adding the double-Z photocatalyst nickel/nickel iron oxide/iron oxide organic dye wastewater, degrading the organic dyes in the wastewater into carbon dioxide, water and inorganic salts under sunlight, and continuously producing hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The nickel hydroxide suspension prepared by adding the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution dropwisely to the nickel nitrate aqueous solution under stirring, continuously stirring for 30 minutes, washing the precipitate with deionized water many times, and finally ultrasonically dispersing the obtained precipitate in deionized water to obtain a nickel hydroxide suspension. The iron oxide-hydroxide suspension prepared by adding the iron oxide-hydroxide aqueous solution dropwise to the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution under stirring, continuously stirring for 30 minutes, washing the precipitate many times with deionized water, and finally ultrasonically dispersing the obtained precipitate in deionized water again to obtain iron oxide-hydroxide suspension. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of nickel oxide/nickel iron oxide/iron oxide is 1:1:1.

AU XIE H
AE QINHUANGDAO QINGFEN KEJIAN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QINH-Non-standard)

GA 202162777Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of roadbed soft soil curing mortar by saponified residue involves (1) sieving saponified residue, (2) mixing with slaked lime, (3) adding mixture of saponified residue and slaked lime into cement and sand or cement dry-mixed mortar and stirring uniformly.
   USE - Preparation of roadbed soft soil curing mortar by saponified residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The roadbed soft soil curing mortar solves the problem of collapse caused by poor bearing force after curing and soft soil solidification, breaking and condensed water quality problems, ensures air permeability and fusion performance, due to the addition of cement and sand or cement dry-mixed mortar. The adsorption of desulfurized ash reduces the moisture content in the saponification slag material to dry it, and absorbs carbon dioxide in the air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a screening device for preparing roadbed soft soil curing mortar by saponified residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The outer portion of fixed end comprises PTFE layer. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of saponified residue, white lime, cement and sand is 4-8: 1-3: 1-3: 1-3. The mass ratio of saponification residue, slaked lime and cement dry-mix mortar is 4-8: 1-3: 1-3. The mass ratio of saponified residue, slaked lime, desulfurized ash, cement and sand is 4-8: 1-3: 1-3: 1-3: 1- 3. The mass ratio of saponified residue, white lime, desulfurized ash and cement dry-mixed mortar is 4-8: 1-3: 1-3: 1-3. Preferred Process: The step (2) involves mixing the saponified residue with the slaked lime and adding desulfurized ash. Preferred Property: The saponification residue is sieved with an aperture of 1 cmx 1cm to 2.5 cmx 5 cm.

PN CN112126783-A; WO2022042228-A1; CN112126783-B; HU202200168-A1
TI Recycling of iron and aluminum recycling in nickel            cobalt manganese solution comprises leaching the            battery powder, removing copper, obtaining liquid after            removing copper, adjusting pH value to remove iron and            aluminum in sections,.
AU DENG H
   CHEN Z
   CHEN R
   RUAN D
   HE F
   LI C
   KAN Z
   DENG HAOZHEN
   KAN ZHIXIN
   CHEN RUOKUI
   RUAN DINGSHAN
   HE FANG
   LI CHANGDONG
AE HUNAN BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NDCT-C)
   GUANGDONG BRUNP CYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (NDCT-C)
   HUNAN BRUNP AUTOMOBILE CIRCULATION CO (NDCT-C)
   GUANGDONG BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (NDCT-C)
   HUNAN BRUNP VEHICLES RECYCLING CO LTD (NDCT-C)
   HUNAN BRUNP AUTOMOBILE CIRCULATION CO LTD (NDCT-C)
   GUANGDONG BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (NDCT-C)
GA 2021016427
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of iron and aluminum recycling in                nickel cobalt manganese solution comprises leaching                the battery powder, removing copper, obtaining the                liquid after removing copper, adjusting pH value to                remove iron and aluminum in sections, obtaining the                needle iron slag and iron aluminum slag, mixing the                iron-aluminum slag with alkali solution, heating                and stirring to obtain the aluminum-containing                solution and alkali residue, heating and stirring                the aluminum-containing solution, introducing                carbon dioxide, controlling the pH value to obtain                aluminum hydroxide and removing aluminum                liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling of iron and                aluminum recycling in nickel cobalt manganese                solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can remove the iron-aluminum in                the solution; improves the resource recycling                utilization rate; is economical; environmentally                friendly; and there is no hazardous waste residue                in the process system and has good economic benefit                and social benefit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The stepwise adjustment of                  pH to remove iron and aluminum comprises adding a                  neutralizer, controlling the pH to 2-4, heating                  the reaction to obtain goethite slag, adding                  harmonizing agent, adjust the pH to 4-5, heating                  to react to obtain iron-aluminum slag and                  iron-removed liquid. The control pH value is 2-4,                  the reaction temperature at 70-95 degrees C, the                  reaction time is 1-4 hours, when the pH value is                  stable, ending the reaction to obtain the needle                  iron slag. In the recycling method obtaining the                  aluminum-containing solution and the alkali slag,                  it further includes: returning the alkali slag to                  the acid leaching. In the method introducing                  carbon dioxide, controlling the pH value to be                  9.5-11.5, and terminating reaction when the pH                  value is stable, to obtain aluminum hydroxide and                  the dealuminized liquid. Preferred Components:                  The neutralizer comprises sodium carbonate,                  potassium carbonate, calcium carbonate, calcium                  hydroxide and magnesium hydroxide. The alkali                  liquor is solution of sodium hydroxide and                  potassium hydroxide. The iron-aluminum slag is                  mixed with the alkali liquor according to the                  caustic ratio of 2.5-7.5. The filling speed of                  the carbon dioxide is 2-8 l/minutes.

TI Preparation of lignin-based sunscreen cream by            extracting lignin in birch bark extract residue by            high-boiling alcohol solvent extraction method,            stirring with pure emulsion and ethanol, and            determining optimum lignin addition amount.
AU CHEN H
   LIU Z
   FENG Y
   ZHANG Q
   CUI P
   HU N
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYCY-C)
GA 2020A57141
AB    NOVELTY - A lignin-based sunscreen cream is prepared by                selecting birch bark powder for supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction, vacuum drying extract to                obtain birch bark extract residue, determining best                extraction process by orthogonal test, extracting                lignin in birch bark extract residue by                high-boiling alcohol solvent extraction method to                obtain lignin extract, passing through toxicity                test, judging toxicity to obtain qualified lignin                extract, mixing and stirring qualified lignin                extract and pure emulsion and ethanol according to                different proportions to obtain lignin-based                sunscreen cream with different gradient                concentration, UV measuring, and determining                optimum lignin addition amount.
   USE - The method is used for preparing lignin-based                sunscreen cream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses alkaline substance and                antioxidant plant oil, surfactant, humectant,                emulsifier and preservative to realize high                sunscreen performance of sunscreen cream, and                realizes high purity lignin extraction by                orthogonal test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pure emulsion is                  5-15 pts. wt. glycerol, 0-6 pts. wt. petroleum                  jelly, 1-3 pts. wt. cetyl alcohol, 0-6 pts. wt.                  sunflower seed oil, 1-10 pts. wt. tocopherol,                  1-10 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, 1-10 pts. wt.                  disodium EDTA, 0.1-1.5 pts. wt. phenoxyethanol,                  0.1-0.5 pt. wt. propylene glycol, 0-6 pts. wt.                  sweet almond oil, 1-3 pts. wt. tocopherol                  acetate, 1-3 pts. wt. octyl ether, 1-3 pts. wt.                  polydimethylsiloxane alcohol, 5-15 pts. wt.                  glycerol stearate, 5-15 pts. wt. polyethylene                  glycol 30 stearate, 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. acrylate/C-30                  alkanol acrylate crosslinked polymer, 0.1-0.5 pt.                  wt. benzyl alcohol, 1-10 pts. wt. butyl                  hydroxytoluene, 1-10 pts. wt. carbomer and                  balance water. The experimental gradient                  concentration is 10, 50 and 100 g/mL. The                  different gradient concentration is respectively                  0 wt.%, 0.5 wt.%%, 1.0 wt.%, 2.0 wt.% and 5.0                  wt.% lignin-based sunscreen cream. Preferred                  Method: The orthogonal test is to design                  orthogonal test by extracting temperature,                  extracting time, liquid-solid ratio and volume                  fraction of solvent. The toxicity test involves                  using zebrafish as experimental object, taking                  zebrafish wild-type embryo (n=30) as                  administration group, preparing lignin extract                  according to experiment gradient concentration,                  adding in oral way, recording abnormal phenotype                  and death rate of administration group, repeating                  lignin extract 3 times experiment of each                  gradient concentration, and judging qualified                  lignin-based sunscreen cream.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing degradable agricultural film comprises mixing raw materials to obtain mixture, adding the mixture into a twin-screw extruder to extrude a colloid, casting the colloid on a casting machine into a film, drying and cooling the film, where the raw material includes 100-120 pts. wt. thermoplastic starch modified polyvinyl chloride, 5-15 pts. wt. inorganic non-metallic and 0.8-2 pts. wt. plasticizer.

PN CN111617803-A
TI New silicate compound used as catalyst for synthesizing dimethyl carbonate by oxidative carbonylation.
AU WANG Y
AE SICHUAN HONGZHENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202087805C
AB    NOVELTY - Silicate compound (I) is new. The carrier of catalyst is composite carrier. The active component is copper.
   USE - The silicate compound is used as catalyst for synthesizing dimethyl carbonate by oxidative carbonylation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound maintains high activity of copper ion, improves stability and selectivity of dimethyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate from gas raw materials, avoids being physically entrained out of system by gas discharge in reaction process, has moderate coordination strength of heteroaryl group containing nitrogen with copper ion, high contact area with oxygen and carbon monoxide under the premise of good dispersion and reduces production of by-product carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Silicate compound of formula (I) is new. The carrier of catalyst is composite carrier. The active component is copper.
   R1=1-4C alkyl, methyl, ethyl, propyl, isopropyl or butyl;
   R2=pyridine, pyrrole, pyrazole, imidazole, pyrimidine, pyrazine, pyridazine, indole, quinoline, isoquinoline, purine, benzopyrrole, o-phenanthroline, substituted phenanthroline, biimidazole, N-methylimidazole, bipyridine, pyrimidine, N-methylpyrrolidone, imidazoline aminopyridine or 4-phenylpyridine; and
   X=Cl or Br.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is prepared by dissolving composite carrier in ice water bath in tetrahydrofuran solution, mixing at room temperature for 0.5-2 hours, adding copper compound, mixing for 10-30 minutes to obtain uniform and transparent composite gel, calcining composite gel for 3-5 hours at 400-600 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen and cooling. The composite carrier is prepared by dissolving ethyl orthosilicate in ethanol to prepare 0.5-1 mol/L solution, adding glacial acetic acid and water, mixing to obtain 0.1-0.3 mol/L silica sol, preparing alumina solution into 0.3-0.5 mol/l solution, adding silica based sol to obtain silica alumina composite sol, drying, aging at room temperature and calcining for 3-5 hours at 400-600 degrees C.

PN CN111454866-A
TI Culturing Helicobacter pylori, by mixing magnetic            particles, buffer and antibody solution, adding            cross-linking agent, reacting, spreading culture            medium, performing bacterial identification and            determining Helicobacter pylori.
AU HOU Z
   MARSHALL B
   ZHENG J
   CAI Y
   ZHANG W
AE SHENZHEN HONGMED-INFAGEN DIAGNOSTIC TECH (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202072707V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing Helicobacter pylori,                involves mixing 1-1000 mg magnetic particles, 1-100                ml buffer and 0.1-100 mg antibody solution, adding                cross-linking agent and reacting at room                temperature for 2-4 hours to obtain magnetic                particle solution coated with antibodies, adding                0.01-1 g sample carrying H.pylori to 0.01-100 ml                magnetic particle solution, mixing for 1-20                minutes, shaking and centrifuging, discarding the                supernatant to obtain a culture, uniformly                spreading the culture medium on the surface of the                culture medium, culturing at 35-38 degrees C and                carbon dioxide volume fraction of 1-15% for 2-7                days to obtain sample colonies, performing                bacterial identification on the sample colony, and                determining that the H.pylori culture is                successful, where the concentration of buffer is                10-100 mM.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing H.pylori                (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The magnetic particles                  carry carboxyl groups. The buffer is chosen from                  2-(N-morpholino)ethanesulfonic acid (MES),                  3-morpholino-2-hydroxypropanesulfonic acid                  (MOPSO), 3-(N-morpholino)propanesulfonic acid                  (MOPS),                  4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic                  acid (HEPES) and phosphate-buffered saline (PBS)                  buffer, and has pH of 3-10. The cross-linking                  agent is chosen from                  1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide                  (EDC), N-hydroxysuccinimide,                  N-hydroxysulfosuccinimide, hydrazide, and                  potassium isocyanate. The sample is chosen from                  gastric mucosal tissue, gastric juice or feces.                  The antibody solution recognizes antigen on the                  surface of H.pylori, and the antigen includes                  urease, catalase, and oxidase. The antibody is a                  monoclonal antibody or a polyclonal antibody. The                  culture medium is a solid culture medium. The                  method involves performing bacterial                  identification on the sample colony to determine                  that the H.pylori culture is successful,                  simultaneously performing urease test, oxidase                  test, and catalase test on the sample colony,                  where the three test results are positive,                  indicating that the sample colony is H.pylori,                  and the H.pylori culture is determined to be                  successful. The method involves performing                  bacterial identification on the sample colony to                  determine that the H.pylori culture is                  successful, and performing a test on the sample                  colony morphological identification.

PN CN111454569-A
TI Preparing nylon material with flame retardant performance comprises e.g. providing nylon, microcapsule mannitol, anhydrous magnesium carbonate and calcium chloride, mixing, and glass fiber, heating, and kneading with kneader.
AU WANG L
   LI Q
   LIU Z
   WENG Y
   DING X
AE SUZHOU RUNJIA ENG PLASTICS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020727141
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nylon material with flame retardant performance comprises (i) providing nylon, microcapsule mannitol, anhydrous magnesium carbonate and calcium chloride, mixing, and glass fiber, (ii) heating the mixture obtained in step (i) and kneading with kneader for at least 30 minutes, (iii) introducing the mixture and glass fiber obtained in step (ii) into extruder for melting and extruding, and (iv) cooling the extruded blank to obtain flame-retardant nylon material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nylon material with flame retardant performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces microcapsules of mannitol and anhydrous magnesium carbonate that do not contain phosphorus and halogen. The nylon material has flame retardancy. The microcapsule mannitol reduces temperature around the nylon through phase change heat absorption, and prevents occurrence of combustion. The anhydrous magnesium carbonate absorbs heat when burned, decomposes to produce carbon dioxide at the same time, reduces oxygen concentration around the nylon material, and plays flame retardant effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: In the step (i), the material comprises 100 pts. wt. nylon, 5-10 pts. wt. microcapsule mannitol, 6-12 pts. wt. anhydrous magnesium carbonate, 5-8 pts. wt. calcium chloride, and 10-20 pts. wt. glass fiber. In the step (i), the microcapsule mannitol is structure in which silicon dioxide encapsulates mannitol, and prepared by sol-gel method. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), heating the mixture to 210-220 degrees C. In the step (ii), the kneading time is 30-60 minutes, and the rotation speed of the kneader is 42 revolutions/minute. In the step (iii), melting and extruding using double-screw extruder, and the double-screw rotating speed is 250 revolutions/minute. In the step (iv), extruding the blank, and cooling in the room temperature.

PN CN111411277-A
TI Improving magnesium antioxidant capacity comprises e.g. mixing magnesium and calcium to obtain mixed material, introducing protective gas, smelting, again introducing protective gas, refining, die-casting and annealing.
AU ZHU Y
   HUO D
   LIU G
   WANG Y
   TIAN Z
   JIANG Q
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202067901C
AB    NOVELTY - Improving magnesium antioxidant capacity, comprises (i) mixing magnesium and calcium in a mass ratio of 95-99:1-5 to obtain mixed material, (ii) placing mixed material at 250-350 degrees C, introducing protective gas and smelting to obtain melt, where the protective gas is composed of sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide in a volume ratio of 1:150-250, (iii) introducing protective gas into the obtained melt, stirring, refining and die-casting to obtain alloy ingot, and (iv) placing obtained alloy ingot at 300-500 degrees C and annealing to obtain magnesium-calcium alloy.
   USE - The method is useful for improving magnesium antioxidant capacity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can form dense and stable magnesium-calcium composite film in magnesium-calcium alloy and thus the magnesium metal matrix cannot be easily oxidized under condition of high temperature and pure oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for magnesium-calcium alloy prepared by above mentioned process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: In step (ii): the intake flow rate of protective gas is 3000-6000 cm3/minute; and the pressure of protective gas is 0.8-1.2 atmosphere. In step (ii), the smelting temperature is 720-800 degrees C. In step (iii), the die-casting temperature is 650-700 degrees C. In step (iv), the mixed gas is introduced during annealing, where the mixed gas is composed of argon and hydrogen in a volume ratio of 7.5-8.5:1.5-2.5. The flow rate of mixed gas is 30-50 cm3/minute. The weight gain of magnesium-calcium alloy after oxidizing with pure oxygen for 120 minutes is not greater than 0.038%. Preferred Components: In step (iii), the protective gas is argon. The magnesium and calcium are blocky or granular structure.

PN CN111348888-A
TI Preparing high temperature resistant aerogel fiber            composite material involves pre-hydrolyzing tetramethyl            orthosilicate to form silica sol, dissolving zirconium            source in mixed solvent of ethanol deionized water,            adding formamide.
AU LI Z
   GE G
   WANG X
   ZHAO S
   LIU J
   WU D
AE TIANJIN NAKESHIJI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202063032R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high temperature resistant aerogel                fiber composite material involves pre-hydrolyzing                tetramethyl orthosilicate to form silica sol,                dissolving zirconium source in mixed solvent of                ethanol deionized water, adding formamide to                solution produced and stirring, adding silica sol                produced to solution to form mixed sol system. The                gel initiator is added to the mixed sol system                generated to cause sol-gel transition to form wet                gel mass, solvent replacement of the wet gel mass                generatedias performed with ethanol. The surface                silanization of the wet gel mass after solvent                replacement is performed in                ethanol/tetraethylorthosilicate solution. The                basalt fiber is immersed into the wet gel block                after surface silanization, leave to kept for                aging, and then wind up and placed in supercritical                reactor. The material in the supercritical reactor                is supercritically dried with carbon dioxide to                obtain aerogel composite fiber block.
   USE - Method for preparing high temperature                resistant aerogel fiber composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The zirconium source                  is zirconium oxychloride or zirconium                  polyacetylacetonate. The gel initiator is                  propylene oxide or ammonia. Preferred Conditions:                  The mass fraction of silica in the zirconium                  dioxide is 12.4-16.8 wt.%. Preferred Process: The                  method involves adding 1.86 mL of silica sol                  generated to the solution. The mass fraction of                  silica in zirconia is 14.6%. The 3.8 mL of                  propylene oxide or 200 microliter of ammonia are                  added to the mixed sol system generated. The                  numerical values of the above-mentioned                  components and process parameters represents                  range of 15% above and below the numerical                  value.

PN CN111337692-A
TI Adiponectin assay reagent comprises phosphate, sodium chloride, polyethylene glycol, sodium chloride, trehalose, glycerol, surfactant and latex particles, where latex particles are coated with adiponectin antibody.
AU WAN M
   ZHOU H
   CHEN Y
AE JIANGXI LECHENG BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202061061C
AB    NOVELTY - Adiponectin assay reagent comprises first reagent comprising first phosphate, sodium chloride and polyethylene glycol, and second reagent comprising second phosphate, sodium chloride, trehalose, glycerol, surfactant and latex particles, where latex particles are coated with adiponectin antibody.
   USE - As adiponectin assay reagent.
   ADVANTAGE - The reagent has improved sensitivity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing adiponectin assay reagent, involving (a) dissolving first phosphate, sodium chloride and polyethylene glycol in distilled water, and uniformly mixing to constant volume to obtain first reagent, (b) preparing latex coated with adiponectin antibody, dissolving second phosphate and sodium chloride in distilled water to obtain mixed solution, adjusting the temperature of mixed solution to 20-50 degrees C, continuously introducing carbon dioxide into mixed solution, uniformly stirring for 1-2 hours, and washing the latex using the mixed solution, and (c) adding trehalose, glycerol and surfactant to obtained mixed solution, uniformly mixing, distilling and concentrating to obtain second reagent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The latex coated with adiponectin antibody in step (b) is prepared by (i) preparing carboxyl-based latex microspheres with particle size of 50-300 nm, adding 2-morpholinoethanesulfonic acid, uniformly mixing, and repeatedly centrifuging and removing supernatant to obtain cleaning latex, (ii) adding 2-morpholinoethanesulfonic acid to cleaning latex, ultrasonically treating in water bath to make microspheres in monodisperse state, adding active agent for activating the latex, and oscillating the reaction for 10-20 minutes to obtain activated latex, (iii) centrifuging obtained activated latex, removing supernatant, adding third phosphate for resuspending the microsphere, centrifuging, removing the supernatant, adding third phosphate for resuspending microspheres, and ultrasonically treating the microspheres in monodisperse state to obtain latex microsphere solution, (iv) dripping adiponectin antibody solution to latex microsphere solution, oscillating the reaction for 2 hours, adding stop solution, continuously oscillating for 30 minutes, centrifuging, removing the supernatant, adding blocking solution, and continuously oscillating the reaction for 30 minutes to obtain coupled antibody latex, and (v) centrifuging obtained coupled antibody latex to remove the supernatant, adding third phosphate for resuspending the microsphere, and ultrasonically treating in water bath to obtain microspheres in monodisperse state to obtain the latex. Preferred Composition: The concentration of 2-morpholineethanesulfonic acid solution is 30-60 mmol/l, and the pH is 6. Preferred Conditions: The latex coated with adiponectin antibody preparation involves ultrasonically treating in water bath by cyclically performing condition for 2 seconds, stopping for 2 seconds, and ultrasonically treating for 2-5 minutes under frequency of 40-50 Hz.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentrations of first phosphate is 15-50 mmol/l, and second phosphate 30-100 mmol/l. Preferred Components: The first phosphate comprises sodium hydrogen phosphate and sodium bicarbonate in a mass ratio of 10:(1-3). The second phosphate comprises potassium dihydrogen phosphate and potassium hypochlorite in a mass ratio of 10:(0.5-2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant comprises 1-10 %mass sorbitol. The active agent comprises carbodiimide and N-hydroxysuccinimide in a mass ratio of 1:1. The stop solution comprises 50-100 mg/ml ethanolamine, lysine or glycine solution, and the blocking solution is 5-35 mg/ml bovine serum albumin. The third phosphate comprises potassium hydrogen phosphate, potassium sulfide, and Tween-20 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitol ester) in a mass ratio of 50:(1-5):(0.2-1). Preferred Composition: The concentration of sodium chloride of first reagent and second reagent is 1-20 g/l. The concentration of polyethylene glycol is 10-50 g/l. The concentration of trehalose is 5-10 g/l. The second reagent comprises 1-10 %mass glycerol and 0.1-1 %mass latex particles.

TI Preparing modified graphene aerogel useful in modification of polymer material, comprises e.g. adding hummers graphene oxide to de-ionized water by ultrasonic dispersion, adding double-ended epoxy base polyether, and stirring.

AE HONG Y (HONG-Individual)
GA 202056497J

   USE - The preparing modified graphene aerogel is useful in modification of polymer material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves compatibility of graphene aerogel and polymer materials of different polarities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for modified graphene aerogel.

The n/m is /~0.5, and n/m is /-10. The polyamine reducing agent is a diamine and a triamine having molar ratio of 2:1-20:1. The molar ratio of the diamine to the triamine is 5:1-15:1. Preferred Components: The diamine is diaminodiphenylmethane, diaminodiphenylsulfone, ethylenediamine, 1,3-propanediamine, 1,4-butanediamine, 1,5-pentanediamine, and/or 1,6-hexanediamine. PN CN111268714-A
TI Preparing black cerium oxide nanomaterials            involves mixing and stirring the cerium salt solution            with the alkaline solution, performing the first            reaction, and obtaining a precipitate, and placing            precipitate in liquid nitrogen.
AU ZHANG L
   LU B
   ZHANG Z
   LUO C
   LI X
   LI S
   ZHI X
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202055601J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing black cerium oxide nanomaterials                involves mixing and stirring the cerium salt                solution with the alkaline solution, performing the                first reaction, and obtaining a precipitate. The                molar concentration ratio of the cerium salt                solution to the alkaline solution is 1:100-200. The                precipitate is placed in liquid nitrogen for low                temperature treatment to obtain blue crystals, and                thawn and dried the blue crystals to obtain yellow                cerium oxide. The yellow cerium oxide is subjected                to a reduction reaction with a reducing agent at a                temperature of 500-800 degrees C to obtain a black                cerium oxide nanomaterial that is stable at room                temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing black cerium oxide                nanomaterials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare black cerium                oxide nanomaterials, that have excellent                application value of photothermal conversion                efficiency, exhaust gas catalytic treatment, carbon                dioxide catalytic reduction, etc.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution                  includes at least one of sodium hydroxide (NaOH)                  or potassium hydroxide (KOH). The cerium salt                  includes at least one of cerium nitrate                  hexahydrate and cerium acetate. Preferred                  Conditions: The molar concentration of the cerium                  nitrate hexahydrate is 0.11-0.12 mol/L. The                  rotation speed of the stirring is 400-600                  revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is                  10-30 minutes. The ratio of the volume (L) of the                  liquid nitrogen to the molar amount (mol) of the                  cerium salt in the cerium salt solution is                  100-120:1. The drying temperature is 80-100                  degrees C.

   ZHOU Z
AE SHENZHEN XINXING LIGHT ALLOY MATERIAL CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202044204S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of magnesium-aluminum-titanium alloy involves placing magnesium ingot in induction furnace, adding pre-prepared aluminum alloy refining agent to furnace to cover the magnesium ingot, heating to 800-900 degrees C for melting of magnesium ingot, adding pre-prepared aluminum-titanium alloy ingot to furnace, heating to 800-900 degrees C for 30 minutes to obtain magnesium aluminum titanium alloy liquid and casting magnesium-aluminum-titanium alloy liquid into a magnesium-aluminum-titanium alloy ingot or rolling. The magnesium-aluminum-titanium alloy comprises 75-92 %mass magnesium, 5-22 %mass aluminum and 1-3 %mass titanium.
   USE - Preparation of magnesium-aluminum-titanium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, avoids chemical reaction of magnesium with oxygen and carbon dioxide or spontaneous combustion in alloy preparation process, which enables the production of magnesium aluminum titanium alloy without the need for a closed container, and improves the production efficiency and quality of alloy.

Preferred Component: The aluminum alloy refining agent is potassium magnesium tetrachloride. The potassium magnesium tetrachloride comprises potassium chloride and magnesium dichloride in molar ratio of 2:1.
